d: Combine all ingredients and half can of noodles.
Mix well. Place in square baking dish.
Sprinkle remaining noodles on top. Bake at 350° for 50 minutes.

i: 1 (16 oz.) bottle Kraft Zesty salad dressing
d: Mix all ingredients together and chill.
Mix together.
Pour into large casserole (2-quart).
Bake at 375° for 45 minutes.

i: 1 lb. boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cubed
1 each small onion and green pepper, chopped
1 pkg. frozen sweet corn, thawed (10 oz. pkg.)
d: Heat oil in a large skillet on medium-high heat. Add chicken, onion and pepper. Cook and stir until chicken is cooked through. Add corn, broth and salsa. Bring to a boil.
Stir in rice. Cover. Remove from heat. Let stand 5 minutes.
Sprinkle with cheese. Cover.
Let stand 2 minutes or until cheese melts.
Makes 4 servings.
Mix salt, pepper and flour.
Dredge pieces of meat and shake off excess flour.
Heat oil in skillet.
Chop onion and garlic. Brown in skillet until transparent.
Remove with slotted spoon and place in Dutch oven or suitable stew pot.
Add meat to skillet and brown on all sides.
Combine first 8 ingredients.
Shape into 4 patties.
Coat grill rack with cooking spray, place on grill over medium-hot coals.
Place patties on rack and cook 8 minutes on each side or until done.
Serve on buns.
Makes 4 servings.
Brown hens in oil.
Add garlic.
Sprinkle with herbs.
Add 1/2 cup water or chicken stock.
Cover; reduce heat to low and cook until done, 40 to 60 minutes.

i: 1 (8 oz.) carton frozen whipped topping, thawed
d: In a saucepan, bring pineapple to a boil.
Remove from heat; add gelatin and stir to dissolve.
Add buttermilk and mix well. Cool to room temperature.
Fold in whipped topping.
Pour into an 11 x 7 x 2-inch dish.
Refrigerate several hours or overnight. Cut into squares.
Cream first 3 ingredients.
Add eggs and vanilla.
Add flour, then add coconut and pecans.
Mix well.
Bake in floured and greased 9 x 12-inch pan in 350° oven for 30 minutes.
Pour a jar of marshmallow cream over cake and top with frosting.
Place sugar, vinegar and oil in pan; let come to a boil.
Turn heat on low and cook for 10 minutes.
Cool and pour over other ingredients that have been mixed in large bowl.
Let stand in refrigerator overnight.
This will last for several days.

i: 2 small size pkg. Jell-O milk chocolate pudding
d: Bake brownies as on package; when done, mix coffee, sugar and water and pour over baked brownies.
Mix pudding as directed. Chop candy bars real fine.
In trifle bowl, put layer of brownies, layer of pudding, layer of candy and layer of Cool Whip, ending with Cool Whip and candy.
Mix all ingredients together.
Pour in the water and mix with hands until soft.
Pat a small amount back and forth between hands until flat and round.
Fry in 1 cup shortening until brown. Sprinkle warm bread with powdered sugar or serve warm with honey.
Mix all ingredients in pan; bring to boil.
Reduce heat; simmer for 20 minutes.
Put in sterilized jars and seal.
Makes approximately 9 pints.
Drain pineapple very well, then add the rest of the ingredients in order.
Mix very well and refrigerate until ready to use, at least 3 hours.
Delicious with poultry or serve as a dessert salad.
Drain fruit well.
Cut pear and peach halves in two.
Arrange in baking dish.
Blend butter, sugar and curry powder.
Cover fruit with sauce and chill several hours or overnight.
Bake 30 to 35 minutes at 375°.

i: 1/4 c. flour, seasoned with salt and pepper
d: Have lamb cut in 2-inch cubes; and the excess fat trimmed off. Roll each piece of lamb in seasoned flour; place in hot oil; add sliced onion.
Brown lamb well on all sides.
Pour off oil; add boiling water, cover and simmer slowly 1 1/2 hours.
Add vegetables and marjoram; cook until carrots are tender.
Remove to casserole or baking dish.
Pour thickened gravy over meat and vegetables.
Do not double or halve this recipe.
Measure flour and salt into a large bowl.
Add only 1 cup of water to the dry ingredients.
Mix thoroughly with your fingers, adding more water if necessary to make the dough the consistency of pie crust. Remove the dough from the bowl and knead for 4 or 5 minutes on wax paper.
Store in tight container until used.
Peel and dice kiwi; peel, core and dice apples. In a large bowl, thoroughly mix fruits, white sugar, brown sugar, and fruit preserves. Cover and place in refrigerator for at least 15 minutes.

i: 2 lb. spinach (fresh), rinsed, drained and chopped
d: Brown onion in olive oil.
Beat eggs and add to spinach. Break up Feta cheese in crumbled pieces and mix with cottage cheese.
Add to spinach.
Add browned onions; season with pepper. Butter a 9 x 13-inch pan and place 10 sheets phyllo into pan, brushing each with melted butter.
Spread spinach mixture on top. Cover with 10 sheets of phyllo, buttering each phyllo as you add it.
Butter top well and cut into squares about 2 x 3-inches.
Drain juice from green beans and reserve.
Cut bacon in half. In each slice of bacon, roll up 3 or 4 green beans.
Secure bacon with toothpicks.
Add part of green bean juice to the roll-ups after placing the roll-ups in a baking pan.
Season with salt and pepper.
Bake until bacon browns.
Combine all ingredients and blend until smooth in a food processor or with an electric mixer.
For a great sandwich, spread in a pita bread and fill with fresh vegetables.
Stir chocolate chips, condensed milk and vanilla.
Microwave 3 1/2 minutes.
Stir in nuts.
Put in square pan with wax paper on bottom.
Refrigerate 2 hours.

i: 1 (16 1/2 oz.) can whole kernel corn, drained
1 (8 1/2 oz.) pkg. Jiffy corn bread mix
d: In a bowl, mix corns, sour cream, corn bread mix and melted margarine.
Pour into a greased 8 x 8 x 2-inch pan.
Bake at 350° for 50 minutes.
Mix salt
and soda with flour.
Pour boiling water over bran and
set aside.
Mix other ingredients and add bran.
Mix all thoroughly
Cook onion, celery and pepper.
Add ketchup, sugar and vinegar.
Warm, then add to meatballs.

i: 1 (6 oz.) jar red maraschino cherries, cut in half
1 (6 oz.) jar green cherries, cut in 1/4
d: Melt chocolate in microwave on High 3 minutes.
Stir until all is melted.
Stir in marshmallows, cereal and peanuts.
Pour onto greased or Saran Wrap covered 12-inch pizza pan or smaller plates. Top with red cherries and 1 green for leaf.
Sprinkle with coconut. Melt remaining white bark and shortening.
Drizzle over coconut. Chill 15 minutes in freezer.
Use about 2 teaspoons per cup (according to taste) with hot water.

i: 2 gal. cucumbers, cut in small pieces
1 box pickling spices, tied in cloth
d: Pour 1 gallon boiling water over the cucumbers.
Add the salt. Let stand for 1 week, stirring each day.
Drain water off.
Add 1 gallon boiling water and the alum.
Let stand 24 hours.
Drain. Make a syrup of the sugar, vinegar and pickling spices.
Slice onions and separate into rings.
Combine water, sugar and vinegar; stir until sugar dissolves.
Pour over rings.
Cover and chill at least 3 hours; drain.
Stir in mayonnaise and celery seeds.
Serve on lettuce leaves.

i: 1 jar marinated artichoke hearts, chopped and drained
1/2 tsp. dry mustard or curry powder
d: Cook the Rice-A-Roni as directed in large skillet.
Add the green pepper, onions, celery and artichoke hearts to the skillet with the Rice-A-Roni.
Mix the mayonnaise, lemon juice and dry mustard or curry powder.
Mix this into the skillet and serve.
Dissolve yeast in warm water; let stand 5 minutes.
Combine yeast mixture with butter and flour in bowl; mix well.
Stir in egg and sugar.
Refrigerate overnight.
Spoon butter into greased muffin pans; fill 2/3 full.
Bake at 350° for 25 minutes.
Mix all ingredients.
Blend well.
Enough spread for 1 loaf of sandwich bread.
Boil red potatoes with skins on.
Peel after cooked and set aside.
Fry bacon until crisp.
Drain on paper towel.
Reserve 2 to 3 tablespoons drippings of bacon grease.
Crumble the bacon. Saute onion in bacon grease until tender.
Put pears, onions and peppers through food chopper.
Add all other.
Cook for 30 minutes after it reaches a hard boil.
Put in jars and seal while hot.
Cut eggs in half lengthwise. Remove the yolks; set whites aside. In a bowl mash yolks. add remaining ingredients; mix well. Spoon into egg whites. Refrigerate until serving.
Heat oven to 400°.
Grease bottoms and sides of 12 medium muffin tins.
Beat egg; stir in milk and oil.
Mix in remaining ingredients just until flour is moistened.
Batter should be lumpy.
Fill muffin cups
Preheat oven to 350°.
Soak bread in water.
Squeeze out excess water.
Combine bread, ground beef, egg, seasonings, onion and 1/2 cup tomato sauce.
Mix well with a fork.
Shape into a loaf in a shallow pan.

i: 1 (10 oz.) can Ro-Tel tomatoes and green chilies, chopped and drained (reserve liquid and add enough water to make 2 c.)
d: Brown ground beef in hot oil, separating with a fork as it cooks. Add green pepper and onion and continue to fry on medium heat for 5 minutes. Drain excess fat. Gently stir in seasonings, Ro-Tel and Ro-Tel liquid and rice.
Cover and reduce heat to simmer.
Cook for about 15 minutes. Do not uncover or stir during the last 15 minutes of cooking.
Brush both sides of the salmon steaks with the olive oil. Sprinkle on the salt and pepper.
Grill the salmon steaks with the flesh side down for 2 minutes.
Turn the steaks over and spread on a thick layer of the Spinach Pecan Pesto.
Grill fish for 2 to 4 minutes more, or until just done.
Serves 6.
Preheat oven to 325°.
Blend Crisco and 1/2 cup brown sugar. Add 1 cup flour and salt.
Stir in milk.
Pat evenly in greased pan.
Bake at 325° for 20 minutes or until light brown.
Beat eggs, 1 cup brown sugar and vanilla until thick.
Drain potatoes.
Add 1/2 cup potato juice to mixture.
Beat potatoes with egg yolks, sugar, vanilla and flour.
Add pecans and coconut (mix by hand).
Microwave on High for 8 minutes. Refrigerate to cool.
Bake pie crusts (let cool).
Beat first 6 ingredients for 2 minutes.
Then add cake mix and pudding.
Beat for 2 additional minutes.
Grease Bundt pan.
Pour in batter.
Dust top with cinnamon-sugar.
Mix all together ingredients and drop by spoonfuls in hot oil. Fry until golden brown.

i: 4 qt. long red, green or yellow peppers
d: Wash and drain peppers.
Cut 2 small slits in each pepper. Dissolve salt in 1 gallon water.
Pour over peppers and let stand 12 to 18 hours in a cool place.
Drain; rinse again and drain thoroughly.
Combine remaining ingredients.
Simmer 15 minutes. Remove garlic.
Cream margarine and sugar on medium speed for 5 minutes.
Add eggs, one at a time, and vanilla.
Mix on low speed until blended. Combine dry ingredients.
Add to creamed mixture.
Mix on low speed until blended.
Add nuts; mix to blend.
Put cherries in pan.
Add Jell-O and sugar.
Place on stove and let it get good and hot while stirring constantly.
Take off stove and add pineapple and nuts.
Pour into dish and refrigerate.
Add enough liquid to chicken soup and consomme to make 4 cups of water.
Season with salt and pepper.
Cook at 350° for 1 hour.
Soak cukes with salt in enough boiling water to cover.
Let soak overnight.
Next morning, drain off salty water.
After draining, add 2 tablespoons alum to enough cold water to cover. Let stand overnight and next morning, drain.
Make syrup of vinegar, sugar, 1 cup water and all of spices listed.
Bring to boil; add cukes.

i: 1 prepared chocolate or graham cracker crumb crust
2 (4 oz.) pkg. Jell-O chocolate cooking pudding
d: Spread caramel topping on bottom of crust; sprinkle with pecans.
Refrigerate.
Stir pudding mixes in medium saucepan. Stirring constantly, cook over medium heat until a full boil. Remove from heat.
Cool 5 minutes, stirring twice.
Pour into crust.
Place plastic wrap over pie.
Cream the butter and sugar; add egg, then flour, sifted, then nuts.
Drop on greased cookie sheet with a teaspoon.
Bake at 350° for about 20 minutes.
Combine
cucumbers,
tomatoes,
cabbage
and salt.
Let stand 3 hours.
In mixing bowl, put bananas and beat until smooth; add eggs, butter, salt, baking powder, soda, flour, sugar and milk.
Grease three 9-inch aluminum loaf pans.
Heat oven to 350°; cook about 45 minutes or until top of bread is brown.
Chop peppers and onions.
Cover with boiling water.
Let stand for 5 minutes.
Drain.
Add vinegar, sugar and salt; boil mixture 5 minutes.
Pour into clean hot jars and seal immediately.

t: Lime Salad Chiffon With Graham Crackers
d: Make the crust with butter and cracker crumbs.
Put into an 8-inch square pan.
Chill.

i: 1/3 c. green bell pepper, cut into strips
1/3 c. red bell pepper, cut into strips
4 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves, cut into small chunks
1 (12 oz.) jar pineapple ice cream topping (about 1 c.)
d: Saute the peppers in the butter and oil until tender but not mushy. Remove from pan and set aside.
Add the chicken to the skillet.
If needed, add a little more butter and oil. Cook until the chicken is lightly browned and tender.
Add peppers back to skillet along with the ice cream topping, sugar, ginger, and vinegar.
Stir to mix well.
Combine the soy sauce and cornstarch in a small bowl and stir until blended.

i: 1 large pkg. spinach noodles, cooked in broth
6 chicken breasts, boiled (save 1 c. broth for mixture)
d: Cook chicken. Saute celery, pepper and onion. Add cheese and soup. Add 1 cup chicken broth. Mix spinach with chicken mixture. Bake, uncovered, for 30 minutes at 350°.
Combine marinade and place pork chops in a glass dish; add marinade.
Cover and refrigerate overnight, turning occasionally. Grill or broil chops for 8 to 10 minutes; brush occasionally with reserved marinade.
Pound chicken a little, not to tenderize, but so you can work with it.
Put about 2 tablespoons of pineapple in the center. Wrap bacon around it and put a toothpick into it to hold the roll together.
Bake at 350° for 30 to 45 minutes, depending on how thick the breast is.

i: 1 can whole kernel corn and liquid
d: Brown ground beef with onion; drain off grease.
Add remaining ingredients; simmer until done or ready to serve.
Serve with tortilla chips or cornbread muffins.
Blend all ingredients and chill 1 hour.
Serve with crackers or as a dip.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Spray bottom and sides of an 8-inch spring-form pan with nonstick coating.
Lightly flour the pan.

i: 2 Tbsp. balsamic or red wine vinegar
6 boneless skinless chicken breast halves (about 2 lb.)
d: In shallow bowl, whisk together honey and vinegar. Pour bread crumbs into separate bowl; set bowls aside. Spread oil over foil-lined baking pan large enough to hold all chicken pieces in 1 layer. Roll chicken pieces in honey mixture, then in bread crumbs; place in pan. Bake at 375° for 30 minutes or until cooked through. Makes 4 servings.
Preheat oven to 350°. Mix all ingredients in order listed. Pour into lightly floured Bundt pan. Bake 1 hour. Remove from oven to rack. Cool completely.
Cream together peanut butter, sugar and corn syrup.
Add Rice Krispies and put in an 8 x 8-inch pan.
Press down.
Top with chocolate chip pieces and place in heated oven until chocolate melts.
Spread with knife.
Cool and cut into serving pieces.
Preheat oven and Pam spray two 9 x 13-inch casserole pans. Mix all ingredients together except oleo and corn flakes.
Pour into casserole pans and top with corn flake crumbs (rough crumbs) and drizzle melted oleo over the tops.
Bake about 45 minutes or until bubbly.
Yields two 9 x 13-inch pans.

i: 1 (4 oz.) can mushroom stems and pieces
d: Brown ground beef in skillet, stirring until crumbly.
Layer remaining ingredients, except cheese, in order given over ground beef.
Cook, covered, over low heat for 25 minutes.
Sprinkle with cheese.

i: 1 - 11 oz. can of corn, not creamed
d: Brown meat with onions, drain grease. Put in crockpot. Add all remaining ingredients, (do not drain juice). Cook on High 1 1/2 hours, stirring occasionally, then switch to low, to keep warm for serving. Can also be cooked in a large pot on stove.
Combine dry ingredients.
Add buttermilk, water, oleo and egg. Mix well.
Stir in onion and peppers.
Carefully drop batter by teaspoonful into hot oil.
Fry 3 to 5 minutes until golden brown, turning once.
Yield:
Preheat oven to 350°.
In a large bowl, combine first 7 ingredients.
In a medium bowl, whisk together remaining ingredients.
Add egg mixture to dry ingredients; stir until batter is moist.
Pour batter into 3 greased 3 x 5 1/2-inch loaf pans.
Bake 25 to 30 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in center comes out clean.

t: Mostaccioli Con Formaggio(Baked Macaroni And Cheese)
d: Cook mostaccioli according to package directions.
Melt butter in a large skillet.
Add onion, celery and garlic and cook over medium heat until golden.
Add tomato paste, water, seasonings and sugar.
Cover and cook about 20 minutes.
Mix all ingredients in large bowl except sunflower seeds. Toss with dressing and store covered in refrigerator.
Toss in sunflower seeds before serving.
Combine in cruet and shake vigorously.
Chill before using.

i: freshly ground black pepper to taste
d: Sprinkle or rub chicken with a paste of olive oil and garlic. Sprinkle with pepper.
Broil about 5 minutes per side.
Put into a covered casserole.
Bake at 350° about 40 minutes, until tender.
Marinate 3 hours.
Add one large head of lettuce.
Sprinkle on more Parmesan cheese.
Cook all ingredients in skillet for 3 minutes except beans; add beans.
Put in crock-pot and cook until ready or simmer for 25 to 30 minutes atop stove.
Peel onions.
Core and remove a section approximately the diameter of a quarter.
Do not cut through to the bottom.
Fill cavity with 1 tablespoon butter and 1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce.
Place onions in 9-inch glass pie plate.
Cover with plastic wrap, turning back one edge to vent.
If apples lack tartness, sprinkle with the 1 tablespoon lemon juice.
In mixing bowl, combine sugar, flour, cinnamon and nutmeg (for a very juicy pie, omit the flour).
Add sugar mixture to the sliced apples; toss to mix.
Fill pastry-lined pie plate with apple mixture; dot with butter or margarine.
Cut slits in top crust for escape of steam; place pastry atop filling.
Seal and flute edge.

i: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen, chopped spinach, cooked and drained
2 c. (8 oz.) shredded sharp Cheddar cheese
d: Drain spinach well on absorbent paper.
Toss cheese with flour. Add spinach, milk, eggs, bacon and seasonings.
Mix well. Pour into pastry shell. Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Garnish with additional bacon if desired.
Yields 6 servings.
Brown meat, onions and green pepper in skillet; pour off fat. Add remaining ingredients.
Pour into baking dish.
Bake at 350° for 15 to 20 minutes, or until mixture is bubbly.

i: 1 medium-size green pepper, cut into 1-inch cubes
d: Peel and devein shrimp; set aside.
Drain pineapple chunks, reserving 1/4 cup juice.
Set pineapple aside.
Place flour, cocoa and sugar in a large mixing bowl.
Stir to mix well.
Add eggs, margarine, salt and vanilla.
Beat with a whisk for about 4 minutes.
Stir in nuts.
Pour into a greased 8-inch pie pan and bake in preheated 325° oven until done, about 30 to 40 minutes.
Mix cream cheese, sour cream, and taco mix.
Spread on 8 inch pie plate.
Layer refried beans, then salsa.
Top with cheese, lettuce and olives.
Refrigerate up to 1 hour then serve with crackers or tortilla chips.

i: 1 lb. ground beef, cooked and drained
d: Combine all ingredients except pita bread in a large bowl, mixing to coat with oil.
Chill in refrigerator for 2 to 3 hours. Cut pita bread into halves with scissors.
Pull open to form pockets.
Fill with chilled mixture.
Place fish fillets in Pyrex dish; salt and pepper to taste. Add lemon juice and Worcestershire sauce; allow to marinate while preparing vegetables.
Saute onion in olive oil until golden.
Add tomatoes, pepper, garlic, capers, black olives, tomato paste and chopped parsley.
Cover; cook for 10 minutes.
Top fish with this mixture.
Bake at 350° for 10 minutes per inch of thickness of fish.

i: 1 small bag shredded Monterey Jack cheese
1 small bag shredded sharp Cheddar cheese
dried cilantro (in a jar) or fresh minced
d: Fry
each
tortilla
in 1/4 inch of hot oil until crisp and golden, turning once.
Drain and place tortillas on paper towels. Sprinkle
each

i: 10 3/4 oz. can condensed cream of mushroom soup
d: Brown meat slowly in skillet.
Combine soup, curry powder and water.
Place rice in bottom of buttered baking dish or shallow casserole.
Place browned pork chops on top of rice.
Pour sauce over pork chops and rice.
Bake at 350° until meat is tender, about 25 to 30 minutes.
Place the egg in a bowl and beat.
Add the fruit cocktail, then add dry ingredients (except brown sugar).
Mix well.
Put in ungreased pan.
Sprinkle with the brown sugar.
Bake at 325° for 50 minutes.
Mix all ingredients well.
Use nonstick skillet, well coated with spray.
Heat skillet.
Drop zucchini mixture by tablespoons into hot skillet.
Brown on both sides.

i: leg of lamb, boned and tied
d: Insert slivers of garlic in top side of lamb.
In heavy Dutch oven in vegetable oil, lightly brown meat.
Remove lamb from pot. Add chopped vegetables, cover pot and simmer vegetables for 10 minutes.
Return lamb to Dutch oven, add broth and wine.
Bring to a boil.
Cover pot and place in 325° oven for 2 to 2 1/2 hours, until lamb is tender.
Sprinkle salt over cucumbers and let stand about 15 minutes. Squeeze water from slices and combine with remaining ingredients. Chill.
Mix all ingredients together well.
Pour into ungreased 12-inch plate.
Bake at 350° for 40 minutes.
Cook to a soft ball and add the nuts.
Beat until creamy and drop onto wax paper.
Combine sweet potatoes, sugar, salt, eggs, margarine, milk and vanilla and pour into casserole dish.
Combine brown sugar, flour, pecans and margarine in a medium bowl and sprinkle over potatoes mixture. Bake at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes.
Preheat oven to 425°.
Peel potatoes or leave them unpeeled, depending on your preference.
Cut into two-inch strips.
Mix garlic powder and salt and set aside.
Place potatoes into large plastic container; pour oil and pepper over.
Shake vigorously.

i: 2 c. chicken, cooked and cut in chunks
d: Combine mayonnaise,
curry
powder, soy sauce and lemon juice. Add to chicken mixture.
Chill and serve.
Combine apple cider, vinegar, oil, sugar and broth packs from Ramen noodles.
Refrigerate.
Combine cabbage, green onions, crushed Ramen noodles (uncooked) and sunflower seeds.
Mix all together just a few minutes before serving.
Cube chicken and put in a casserole with chopped celery, uncooked rice and the seasonings.
Add the liquid which may be either milk or gravy and water.
Cover and bake at 375° for 1 1/4 hours.

i: 2 lb. ground turkey or ground chuck
d: Saute chopped onions, garlic and celery.
Add ground beef; saute until well done.
Add remaining ingredients.
Cover and simmer slowly for about 1 hour.
Stir occasionally.
Serve with a dollop of sour cream and grated Cheddar cheese.

i: 1 (7 oz.) can water-packed tuna, drained and flaked
6 thin white bread slices, crusts removed
d: In medium bowl, combine tuna, anchovies, green pepper, pimento, egg and 2 tablespoons Russian dressing.
Mix well and set aside.
In small bowl, mix remaining Russian dressing with cognac and set aside.
In hot butter in a chafing dish or larger pan, saute bread until well browned and butter is absorbed.
In same pan, spread tuna mixture over bread.
Cover with Russian dressing mixture.

i: 1 can (8 oz.) dark red kidney beans, rinsed and drained
d: Cut sausage in half lengthwise, cut crosswise into 1/4-inch slices.
In 10-inch skillet, combine rice, sausage, tomatoes, water, salsa and beans.
Mix well, breaking tomatoes with large spoon.
Bring to boil, reduce heat, cover and simmer 20 minutes. Remove from heat, let stand, covered, about 5 minutes.
Makes 4 servings.

i: 4 lb. beef short ribs, cut into pieces
d: Brown ribs and cook in Dutch oven for 2 hours or until tender. Drain and place in a shallow baking dish.
Combine catsup, molasses, lemon juice and dry ingredients.
Pour over ribs.
Place under broiler at medium heat for 15 to 20 minutes.
Cover whole chicken (or parts) with water in large pan and boil with onions and celery until tender.
Take out chicken and remove any bones.
Cut into bite-size pieces and return to pan. Combine flour, baking powder and salt; cut in shortening and add milk to make a stiff dough.
Roll out to 1/8-inch thick and sprinkle with flour.
Cut in diagonal pieces, about 1-inch and drop into pan with boiling stock.
Cover closely and boil gently, about 8 to 10 minutes.
Mix ingredients and chill.
Serve with shrimp.

i: 12 oz. fresh spinach, washed, trimmed and torn into bite-sized pieces
d: In small skillet, melt margarine over medium-low heat.
Add almonds.
Cook until light golden brown, stirring constantly. Remove from heat; cool.

t: Breadsticks With Rosemary And Sea Salt
i: 2 tsp. coarse salt (sea salt or Kosher salt)
d: Brush breadsticks with olive oil.
Sprinkle with rosemary and salt.
Bake in 375° oven for about 8 minutes.
Remove from oven. Brush with additional olive oil.
Add more rosemary and salt, if desired, to taste.
Serve immediately.
Sift together flour, baking powder, soda, salt, cloves, cinnamon and nutmeg.
Cream shortening and sugar; add eggs, bananas, and vanilla.
Mix well.
Add dry ingredients; mix 2 minutes at medium speed.
Pour into 2 greased and floured 8 inch cake pans; bake at 350° for about 30-35 minutes.
You can also use greased and floured tube pan and bake 1 hour.
In a large bowl, toss first 6 ingredients.
Toss lightly until well blended.
Shape into bite-sized meatballs.
Roll in flour.
Preheat pan to 320° or medium-hot.
Add oil.
Peel onions and cucumbers.
If cucumbers are large, remove the seeds.
Slice tomatoes thin.
Chop onions, cucumbers and peppers small.
Cut cauliflower into small pieces.
Soak vegetables overnight in brine made of 1 cup salt to 1 quart water. Stir and mix well.
Marinate lamb chunks in rest of ingredients for four hours or more. Skewer chunks onto bamboo skewers and bake over charcoal for 15 minutes.
Drain yams.
Melt butter in bottom of baking dish.
Put yams in bottom of baking dish.
You can mash or cut up in small pieces. Sprinkle brown sugar on top along with cinnamon.
Add pecans and marshmallows.
Bake about 20 minutes.

i: 1 lb. spinach, cooked and all water squeezed out
8 oz. raw chopped shrimp, scallops, crab, lobster meat or any combination
d: You will also need string and double thick cheesecloth and 2 roasting pans (same size).
Brown beef, onion and spices together.
Add tomato sauce and mushrooms.
Let cool.
Put on hamburger bun halves.
Sprinkle cheese on top and bake at 400° for about 15 minutes or until cheese melts.
Makes about 12 to 16 halves.
Mash tofu and let it drain until most of the water is gone. Mix all ingredients and make into patties and fry in bacon drippings or Wesson oil until browned.

i: 1/4 c. plus 1 Tbsp. nonfat sour cream
1 1/4 c. (5 oz.) shredded Monterey Jack cheese
d: Combine first 6 ingredients in a medium bowl; stir well.
Spoon crabmeat mixture evenly over 5 tortillas.
Spread to within 1/2-inch of edge.
Sprinkle evenly with cheese and top with remaining tortillas.

i: 1 (14 oz.) can Eagle Brand sweetened condensed milk (not evaporated milk)
d: Heat oven to 350°.
In a medium bowl, combine flour and sugar. Cut in margarine until crumbly.
Stir in 1/2 cup chips.
Press firmly on bottom of a 13 x 9-inch baking pan.
Bake 15 minutes.

t: Scrambled Eggs With Peppers And Zucchini
i: 1/2 medium sweet red pepper, seeded, deveined and finely chopped
d: Heat oil in a heavy skillet.
Add peppers, zucchini, garlic and marjoram or oregano (if using dried herbs).
Saute 4 to 5 minutes until peppers begin to soften.
Pour off any liquid in skillet. Beat eggs with marjoram or oregano (if using fresh herbs) until fluffy.
Pour into skillet.
Swirl eggs around skillet with wooden spoon, cooking over medium heat until eggs are barely set.
Cut off stem and "X" bottom of each sprout.
Over low heat, melt the butter with olive oil in a large pot.
Add garlic; cook 2 to 3 minutes.
Add sprouts; toss with butter mixture.
Add chicken broth, salt and pepper.
Cover; cook for 10 to 15 minutes over low.

i: 4 chicken breasts, slightly frozen and cut into bite-size pieces
d: Put all ingredients together in large kettle and simmer about 30 minutes.
Serve with crushed tortilla chips, grated cheese, sour cream, and avocado slices.
Cut cucumbers lengthwise and place in jars.
Boil solution for five minutes and pour over pickles in jars.
Seal tight and put in boiling water bath for 5 minutes.
Makes 6 pints.

i: 1 1/2 c. water or 1 c. water and 1/2 c. wine
d: Combine 1 1/2 cups water with spaghetti sauce in bowl.
Cover bottom of sprayed 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish with 1 1/2 cups of sauce.
Combine sugar, cornstarch and water in a heavy saucepan; cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until thickened.
Remove from heat; add gelatin, stirring until dissolved.
Gently stir in blackberries.
Pour into pastry shell.
Chill until firm.
Serve with Cool Whip or ice cream.
Put 2 cups flour in a deep mixing bowl.
Make a well in the middle of the flour and add the eggs and water.
Gradually mix the dry ingredients into the liquid ones with your hands or a wooden spoon until well blended.
Gather into a ball and knead until very smooth on a floured board, adding a little more of the remaining flour if the dough sticks to your hands.
Knead for 10 minutes. Cut dough into thirds and roll out one-third at a time.
Combine apples, pears and sugar.
Cook in sauce pan over medium heat about 1 hour or until mixture looks like honey.
Then add crushed pineapple and cook 10 more minutes.
Very good on a hot buttermilk biscuit.

i: 1 can frozen lemonade in 4 cans water
d: Mix ingredients together and chill.
Place chicken pieces in shallow baking dish, skin side up. Combine butter, honey, mustard, salt and curry powder.
Pour over chicken and bake at 350° for 1 1/4 hours, basting every 15 minutes until chicken is tender and golden brown.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.
Mix all ingredients, except egg roll skins.
Spoon 1 tablespoon onto egg roll skin; fold.
May use drop of water to seal edges.
Deep fat fry until brown on both sides.
May also use mixture for won tons.
Serve as a snack or as a side dish.
Mix and simmer 10 minutes.

i: 2 c. ground pecans or hickory nuts
d: Mix together ingredients.
Roll into balls.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Roll in powdered sugar while still warm.
Mix all ingredients
and
put in 15 molds or a 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan.
Refrigerate.
Mix
mayonnaise in hot water and it will dissolve better!
Grease an 8-inch square pan.
In a heavy 3-quart pan, combine sugar, milk, butter and salt.
Cook over medium heat until bubbling.
Stir constantly.
Reduce heat to low.
Cook for 5 minutes, until sugar is dissolved.

i: 1 medium yellow or green pepper, cut in 1-inch pieces
d: In a large bowl, mix beef, garlic powder, oregano and pepper. In a medium size bowl mix broth, tomato paste and sour cream. Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large deep skillet over high heat until hot but not smoking.
Add meat mixture and stir-fry, breaking up with a large spoon until no longer pink.
Spoon into a large bowl.
Heat remaining oil in the skillet.
Add eggplant and stir-fry 2 to 3 minutes until slightly softened.
Add bell peppers and stir-fry about 1 minute, until crisp-tender and eggplant is tender.

Cook rice in boiling salted water until tender. Fry 1/2 cup bacon until crisp. Add onion and green pepper; simmer until onion is soft and yellow. Add tomatoes, sugar, salt and pepper. Pour rice into strainer and rinse with hot running water. Add rice to onions, bacon and green pepper mixture. Mix well and pour into greased casserole dish.
Bake about 30 minutes at 350°.

i: 1 lb. ground beef, browned and drained
d: Mix first 3 ingredients and refrigerate.
Then mix next 3 ingredients.
Toss these two parts together; at the last minute add chips and Russian dressing.
Combine dry ingredients; mix well. Add margarine and milk. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until desired thickness. Stir in vanilla. Serve over hot biscuits.
Use portions as desired of the above items.
Marinate in Kraft's Italian olive oil dressing.
Refrigerate overnight.
Serve cold.

i: 1 loaf day old Italian bread cubed, remove crust
1/2 med. red onion sliced thin & diced
d: Combine bread, tomatoes, onion, garlic, olives, Feta, grated cheese & basil in lg. mixing bowl.
Pour oil over all and mix well to combine.
Best if made ahead to allow flavors to blend.
Add black pepper and salt if needed.
Serves 8.

Combine sugars and evaporated milk in heavy saucepan. Cook over medium heat. Stir in butter, pecans and coconut. Cook until candy reaches soft ball stage. (Mixture will form a soft ball when dropped in a cup of cold water.)
Cool 2 minutes. Beat until thick, but glossy.
Drop by tablespoon onto wax paper. Cool thoroughly.

i: 1 pkg. Hidden Valley Original Ranch or honey mustard
1/2 tsp. garlic powder (we add a little more)
d: Mix ingredients in order given.
Stir well in bowl.
Bake at 375° for 12 minutes.

i: 2 tbsp. dried basil (or 1 cup fresh, chopped)
d: Toss all vegetables in enough olive oil to coat; add herbs. Spread out onto 2 cookie sheets and roast in oven at 400° until well done and crispy, about 15-20 minutes, stirring vegetables at least once during baking.
Heat oven to 350°.
Brown ground beef and onion; drain.
Spoon into ungreased 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish.
Stir in seasoning mix (dry), tomato sauce and corn.
Sprinkle with cheese.
Stir remaining ingredients until blended.
Cream sugar and margarine.
Add eggs and flour, then the bananas; mix well.
Fold in nuts.
Pour into greased loaf pan. Bake at 300° for 1 hour on the middle shelf of oven.
Mix both together until smooth.
On a small platter or something flat, break up lettuce small; chop tomatoes small and sprinkle shredded cheese on top.
Dip Fritos chips into dip, then scoop up salad.
Good!
Cut into chunks the onions and pickles.
Mix with mayonnaise to make sauce.
Salt and pepper to taste.
Serve with fried fish.

i: 3 c. dry black-eyed peas (prepared as below)
4 ripe tomatoes (spaghetti sauce may be substituted)
few cloves minced or crushed garlic
d: Heat oil in a nonstick frying pan.
Add garlic, ginger and chopped onion and stir-fry for 5 minutes.
Add diced tomatoes and cook 10 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add curry powder and prepared beans.
Bring mixture to a boil and cook on medium heat for 10 more minutes.
Garnish with Garam Masala and finely chopped cilantro.

i: 1 1/2 c. snow peas, trimmed and blanched
d: In a small bowl, whisk together the oil, vinegar, mustard, chopped onion, chives and 1/2 cup of the raisins.
Season with salt.
Slice each chicken breast diagonally to 1-inch thick pieces.
Simmer the cider, brown sugar, cloves, allspice and cinnamon sticks.
Strain and add the lemonade and orange juice.
Melt together caramels and 1/3 cup evaporated milk.
Mix together 1/3 cup evaporated milk, cake mix, butter and nuts. Press 1/2 cake mixture into greased and floured pan.
Bake at 350° for 6 minutes (probably longer).
Remove from oven.

i: 1 lb ground beef, cooked and drained
d: Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Brown beef, drain and add taco seasoning mix along with the water, and cheese. Place crescent rolls in a ring around a large round stone with the pointed end facing right overlapping each other then place meat mixture in the center of the crescent rolls. Take the end of each crescent roll and wrap around the other end until circle so complete. Bake in the oven until the rolls become golden brown, about 10 minutes. Use the rest of the ingredients to garnish with.
Crumble enough Oreo cookies to line bottom and sides of pie plate (I make mine small chunks).
Fix the pudding according to directions on box.
Place cookies in pie pan.
Add pudding on top, then the cherry pie filling.
Top with Cool Whip.
Refrigerate.

i: 1 (8oz.) pkg. semi sweet Chocolate chips, melted and opt.
d: At medium speed beat sugar and butter.
Add 1 c. flour and then the rest of the ingredients.
Add the other 1 1/2 c. flour.
Pat dough into 2 (12x3) loaves.
Bake on cookie sheet for 25-30 minutes at 350°.
Remove and cool for 20 minutes.
Combine chicken, craisins, walnuts in one bowl.
Cut chicken into 1 inch cubes.
Marinate for 45 minutes in soy sauce, sesame oil and ginger.
Mix ketchup, water, vinegar and sugar and set aside.
Put marinated chicken and cornstarch in Ziploc bag and shake until well coated.
Preheat skillet or wok, add 1 tablespoon vegetable oil and add chicken breasts a few at a time.
Brown chicken, then remove from skillet.
Preheat oven to 325°.
Grease custard cup.
Crack egg into cup.
Sprinkle with cheese.
Place custard cup on tray.
Bake 18 to 20 minutes.
Place first 4 ingredients in a
x 9 x 2-inch baking dish. Sprinkle the fruit with almonds.
Combine butter, brown sugar and curry powder.
Top
fruit
In a stock pot, saute onions, garlic and green pepper in oil. Blend in the flour.
Cook and stir over low heat until vegetables are tender.
Add tomatoes, okra, tomato paste, broth, Worcestershire sauce and all seasonings (except fresh parsley). Simmer for 1 hour.
Add cooked chicken; simmer briefly.
Bring to boiling first 3 ingredients and pour over tea bags. Steam 5 minutes and strain.
Add the remaining ingredients and serve either hot or cold.

i: 2 1/2 c. cubed cooked turkey or chicken
d: Cook pasta according to package directions.
Drain and set aside.
Run water over broccoli to thaw.

i: 2/3 c. dark brown sugar, firmly packed
d: Mix tomatoes and salt.
Let stand about 12 hours.
Drain. Heat vinegar, sugars and spices to a boil; add sliced onions and boil gently 5 minutes.
Add the drained tomatoes and peppers; bring slowly to a boil.
Simmer 5 minutes, stirring occasionally with a wooden spoon.
Pack into hot jars.
Peel potatoes and slice.
Simmer onion in bacon drippings and add flour, vinegar, sugar, water and seasoning.
Mix well all ingredients together and serve warm.

i: 1 medium green pepper, cut in pieces
d: Cut chicken into 1/2-inch strips and then 1/2-inch squares. Place in bowl with cornstarch and toss.
Pour in sherry, soy and toss.
Heat wok; pour in 1 tablespoon oil and stir-fry peppers, water chestnuts, mushrooms and salt for 2 to 3 minutes.
Remove, set aside and add remaining 3 tablespoons oil and stir-fry chicken.
Add hoisin sauce and stir well with chicken.
Add vegetables back in and cook 1 minute.
Boil together vinegar, sugar, celery salt, mustard seed and turmeric.
Pour in cabbage, pepper and onions.
Bring to boil; can.

i: 1 pkg. chicken flavor Ramen noodles (3 oz. pkg.)
d: Heat water to boiling in 3-quart saucepan.
Break block of noodles; reserve seasoning packet.
Stir noodles, chicken, bok choy and carrot into water.
Heat to boiling; reduce heat. Simmer, uncovered, 3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in seasoning packet.

Place the cabbage in a greased 2 1/2-quart baking dish. In a skillet, cook beef and onion until done. Stir in rice, salt and pepper, if desired. Spoon over cabbage. Combine soup and water; pour over beef mixture. Cover and bake at 350° for 40 to 50 minutes or until rice and cabbage are tender. Uncover. Sprinkle with cheese. Bake 5 to 10 minutes longer or until cheese is melted. Makes 4 servings.
Place butter in 2 1/2-quart casserole.
Heat full power 20 to 30 seconds or until melted.
Stir in green pepper, onion and celery. Cook full power 6 to 8 minutes until vegetables are tender. Stir halfway through. Sprinkle vegetables with flour and stir to blend.
Mix in tomatoes. Cook full power until heated, 4 to 6 minutes. Stir halfway through cooking time. Blend in remaining ingredients. Cook full power about 6 minutes, or until shrimp are fully cooked.
Stir twice during time.
Remove bay leaf.

i: 1/2 c. leftover cooked taco ground beef
d: In a bowl, combine eggs, cheese, onion, green pepper and hot pepper sauce.
Cook and stir in a nonstick skillet until eggs begin to set.
Add taco meat.
Cook until eggs are completely set. Spoon onto a warmed tortilla and roll.
Top with salsa.
In a five-quart Dutch oven, heat 3 tablespoons of corn oil. Add onion, celery and garlic and saute for five minutes.
Add 1 cup chopped tomatoes and the carrots and cook for five minutes.
Add vegetable broth, can of whole tomatoes (chopped), cabbage, salt and pepper and simmer for 1 hour or until vegetables are tender.
Add zucchini and cook 10 minutes.
Add kidney beans and corn and heat.

i: salt, pepper and oregano to taste
d: Wash meat and place in roasting pan.
Make incisions in the lamb in several places and insert cloves of garlic.
Rub lemon juice over entire roast, then salt, pepper and oregano.
Bake at 350° for 2 1/2 hours.
After the meat has browned, turn over to brown back side.
Potatoes and carrots may be added, with a small amount of water after lamb has been cooking one hour.

i: 1 large box Mexican Velveeta cheese (mild or hot)
d: Brown ground beef and onion. Drain.
Place meat in crock-pot. Cube cheese and add to beef. When cheese has melted, add Tabasco sauce to taste.
Keep warm in crock-pot.
Serve with tortilla chips.

i: 1/2 slice bread, soaked and squeezed dry
1 lb. tomatoes, cut into salad size pieces
d: Separate egg yolks and whites.
Mash yolks and finely chop whites.
Mix yolks with garlic, oil and bread until smooth.
Add pepper, vinegar and salt.
This is the dressing.

i: 2 lb. beef brisket, plus extra beef bones (2 to 3)
3 c. fresh or canned tomatoes, cut up
d: Combine brisket, bones and water in deep pot.
Add onions and tomatoes and bring to boil.
Skim off any foam and reduce heat to low.
Simmer 1 hour uncovered.
Add cabbage, salt, pepper and garlic and cook another hour over low heat.
Stir in lemon juice and sugar; cook 20 minutes longer on low heat.

t: Walnut And Wild Rice Chicken Salad
i: 1 c. white and wild rice blend
d: Cook rice according to package directions. Cool. In a large bowl, combine yogurt, mayo, green onions, olive oil, lemon peel, salt, lemon juice and pepper. Stir in rice, chicken and walnuts. Refrigerate until serving. Makes 6 servings.

i: 4 c. grape juice or pineapple juice
d: Boil
sugar and water for 3 minutes and cool.
Add rest of the
juices
and
chill.

i: 1 (8 oz.) can stewed tomatoes, cut up
1 (10 3/4 oz.) can condensed Cheddar cheese soup
d: Cook broccoli in unsalted water 5 to 10 minutes until tender; drain. Preheat oven to 350°.
Combine eggs, soup and oregano. Stir in tomatoes and cooked broccoli.
Turn into a 10 x 6 x 2-inch baking dish. Sprinkle with Parmesan.
Bake, uncovered, 30 minutes.

i: 1 medium pkg. spaghetti, cooked in chicken broth
3 medium onions, cooked in butter until transparent
d: *If you leave these items out, I suggest that you increase the tomatoes.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Wash and dry chicken pieces.
Combine Bisquick, Parmesan, paprika, celery salt, salt and pepper.
Dip chicken in milk and roll in dry mixture.
Place chicken, skin side up, in large shallow baking pan.
Pour melted butter over chicken. Bake 55 to 60 minutes or until chicken is tender.

Cook noodles in enough water to cover them and cook until tender. Then add tuna, soup and milk. Pour into greased casserole dish. Melt 1/2 stick butter and crumble about 4 slices of bread; mix in the melted butter. Cover top of casserole with mixture. Bake at 400° until done.

i: 1 medium jar Old El Paso picante sauce
1 bag Mexican style white corn chips
d: Brown ground beef in a large skillet and drain.
Stir in picante sauce until heated.
Put on serving dish.
Drizzle melted cheese on top, then sprinkle lettuce and tomato on top.
Garnish with sour cream.
Serve along side of white corn chips.
Mix peanuts, Karo syrup, water and sugar together.
Cook on medium high, stirring constantly until syrup turns a caramel color, 15 to 20 minutes.
Remove from heat.
Add soda and stir vigorously for a few seconds.
Then pour on a greased cookie sheet.
When cool, break in desired pieces.

i: 1 pkg. spaghetti sauce mix with mushrooms
d: Brown meat and onions; drain. Add tomato sauce, tomatoes, oregano, Italian seasoning, soul seasoning, spaghetti sauce mix and mushroom soup and cook until hot. Cook spaghetti according to package directions. Layer half of spaghetti, sauce and cheese. Repeat layers. Bake in slow oven (250°) for about 1 hour or until bubbly.
Mix all ingredients together in a large bowl.
Pour into 2 greased loaf pans.
Bake at 350° for at least 1 1/4 hours.
Makes 2 loaves.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Layer sliced potatoes and onions. Sprinkle with salt and pepper in baking dish.
Spread Dijon mustard on pork chops.
Place pork chops on top of potatoes.
Now, pour ginger ale in baking dish, being careful not to get ginger ale on pork chops.
Bake for 1 hour or until done.
Cut shortening into flour until well mixed.
In another bowl, dissolve yeast in warm water; add sugar.
Let stand 2 or 3 minutes.
Add
milk
and
Beat together egg, oil and applesauce. Add flour, baking soda, baking powder and spices. Beat well. Stir in raisins.
Spoon into oiled and floured muffin wells. Bake at 375° for 20 to 25 minutes or until firm to the touch and browned.
Cool on wire racks.
Make a soft dough, using the first 3 ingredients and a little shortening.
Add enough milk to mix; cut this together.
Drop by spoonfuls into hot grease; let fry until brown and crisp.
Serve hot.
Flavor is enhanced when vegetables are chopped in food processor.
Salt the chicken and brown in hot oil for 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the carrots, celery, mushrooms, onion, bouillon, curry powder and ginger; cover and simmer for 20 minutes.
Make a smooth paste of the cornstarch, soy sauce and 1/4 cup cold water. Add to the vegetable mixture, stirring and simmering until it thickens.
Serve with rice.
Serves 4.
Cream sugar and oleo.
Add vanilla.
Mix in flour and pecans, then roll in small balls and place on cookie sheet.
Bake at 325° for 8 to 10 minutes, then roll in powdered sugar twice.
Combine cucumber, onion, vinegar, sugar, salt and pepper. Toss well.
Add yogurt and fresh mint.
Stir well to combine. Refrigerate.
Mix together.
Pour on dressing when ready to eat.

i: 1 (6 oz.) pkg. chips (semi-sweet chocolate, butterscotch or milk chocolate)
d: Cook in 2-quart pan, stirring constantly, over medium heat until mixture comes to a full boil.
Boil and stir 5 minutes. Take off heat; stir in 6 ounces chips, vanilla and nuts.
Pour in greased pan.

i: 2 (3 lb.) chickens, cut up or 16 chicken pieces (your favorite)
d: Combine dry ingredients in a plastic bag.
In a bowl, lightly beaten eggs and milk.
Dip chicken pieces in egg mixture and shake off excess.
Shake a few pieces in the bag at a time, coating well.
In an electric skillet, heat 1/4-inch oil to 350°.
Brown chicken on all sides.
Mix ingredients together to blend.
Pour over 2 pound cut of London broil.
Marinate 3 hours or overnight.
Broil over grill or in the oven.
For medium rare, broil about 15 minutes on each side in the oven.

If you are cooking on the outdoor grill, adjust the time according to the distance your rack is from the coals, 15 minutes on the first side and 10 minutes on the other side might produce the desired doneness.

i: 2 Tbsp. red cayenne pepper, dried and crushed
d: Grind all ingredients together, two, maybe three times.
Mix well.
Put in covered container in refrigerator for about three days so flavors can blend.
Divide into 10 (1-pound) packages and freeze.
Yields 10 pounds.

i: 1 lb. hot Italian link sausage, cut into 1-inch pieces
d: Brown sausage over low heat in a large, heavy skillet; drain sausage on paper towels, reserving 1/4 cup drippings.
Saute onion and garlic in drippings until crisp-tender.
Return sausage to skillet; stir in tomato paste, water, sugar, 1/2 teaspoon salt and bay leaf.
Simmer, uncovered, for 1 hour; stir occasionally. Discard bay leaf.

i: 1 (8 oz.) can water chestnuts, chopped in small pieces
2 lb. Polish sausage or any type hard pork sausage, cut in small cubes
d: Add water to texture desired.
Mix all together and cook until done.
Place layer of beans and thinly sliced onion rings alternately.
Pour leftover grease over beans.
Mix vinegar and sugar together and pour over beans.
Salt and pepper to taste. Crumble bacon over top and refrigerate overnight.
Bake at 350° for 40 minutes.
In 9 x 13-inch glass, combine margarine and onion.
Cook on High for 4 minutes.
Add chicken and cook 8 to 10 minutes or until meat is no longer pink.
Get
a
large
pot
and
put
Combine all ingredients except muffins; mix well.
Spread mixture on muffin halves; cut each in four wedges.
Place on cookie sheet and bake at 350° for about 10 minutes.
Serve warm.

i: 1 c. cooked white kidney beans, drained
1 (15 1/4 oz.) can red kidney beans, drained
1/2 green pepper, thinly sliced or part red pepper or pimiento
d: Put vegetables in large bowl.
Combine remaining ingredients. Pour over vegetables and mix gently.
Chill several hours or overnight.
Serve on Boston lettuce.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.

i: 24 Kraft jet-puffed marshmallows or 3 c. Kraft miniature marshmallows
2 cans (8 oz. each) crushed pineapple, drained, reserving 1/2 c. liquid
2 c. thawed Cool Whip nondairy whipped topping
1 prepared graham cracker crumb crust (6 oz.)
d: Microwave marshmallows and reserved 1/2 cup pineapple liquid in large microwave safe bowl on High 2 to 2 1/2 minutes, or until smooth when stirred, stirring halfway through heating time. Refrigerate 30 minutes or until slightly thickened; mix until well blended.
Fold in pineapple and whipped topping.
Pour into crust. Refrigerate several hours or overnight.
Garnish with pineapple chunks and maraschino cherries.
Makes 6 servings.
Brown and drain ground beef.
Brown and add onions.
Add chopped apple and tomatoes, jalapeno peppers, raisins, olives, cinnamon and cloves.
Salt and pepper and add ketchup.
Add almonds (optional).
Boil cucumbers and onions until tender.
Drain.
Then add vinegar, sugar, flour, salt, celery seed and turmeric.
Mix all together.
Boil 5 minutes.
Jar and seal.
Mix the lime with the 2 gallons water and pour over cucumbers. Let stand 24 hours.
Stir often, but gently.
Stir these several times so the lime mixes with the cucumbers.
Rinse well; cover with clean, cold water.
Let stand for 4 hours; drain well.
Clean chops; then sprinkle on powdered ingredients.
Add liquids.
Let set as long as possible, at least 30 minutes. Grill on grill and enjoy!!
Serves 6.

i: 1/2 c. toasted pecans, candied almonds or Almond Accents (honey roasted)
d: Tear lettuce; cut strawberries in fourths (if large, cut in sixths). Add cranberries; toss or layer in dish. Add nuts just before serving. Top with poppy seed dressing (page 37).
Mix all dry ingredients together.
Add beaten eggs and oil. Mix well.
Bake at 325° for 10 to 12 minutes.
Mix well.
Roll out thin; cut into squares and cook in broth until well done.
Mix Tahini, pepper, salt and garlic in blender for about 3 minutes.
Press chickpeas through sieve and add to blender.
Mix to thick paste.
Alternately add small quantities of oil and lemon juice.
When finished, place in small bowls.
Garnish with black olives and parsley.
Bring to boil raisins and water.
Add salad oil.
Cool mixture.
In large bowl, beat well the sugar and egg.
Soft together all dry ingredients 3 times.
Mix in raisin, water and oil mixture with dry ingredients.
Combine ingredients in large electric skillet.
Heat until just a boil.
Add 5 pounds thawed chicken drummettes (mini drumsticks).
Cook approximately 20 minutes on medium heat.
Dissolve the jello in the boiling water.
Add the cold water, pineapple juice and sugar.
Blend thoroughly.
Then chill or place in juice cans, cartons or freezer bags.
Freeze.
No ice needed.

i: 2 lb. lean round steak or chuck roast, cut in 1-inch cubes
d: Brown meat in hot shortening.
Add onion and applesauce; cook until onion is tender.
Blend in flour, curry powder and nutmeg. Add beef broth and tomato sauce, raisins and brown sugar.
Cover and simmer until meat is tender; about 1 1/4 hours or bake in oven at 325°.
Serve over hot rice.

i: 4 c. pinto beans, cooked and drained
d: In iron skillet, heat bacon grease and add pinto beans.
Mash with potato masher.
Add chili powder, garlic salt and onion salt and stir over medium heat until beans are smooth.
Top with grated cheese.
Melt margarine in bottom of baking dish.
Layer the vanilla wafer crumbs, chocolate pieces, nuts and coconut.
Dribble the Eagle Brand milk over the top.
Bake in a 350° oven for 30 minutes.
Cool and cut in desired size squares.

About 45 minutes before serving, in 8-quart Dutch oven or saucepot over medium heat, in hot olive or salad oil, cook onion, green pepper and garlic until tender, about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add tomatoes and their liquid, wine, parsley, salt, basil, pepper and bay leaf.
Heat to boiling. Reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer 15 minutes.

t: Gazpacho("Spanish Cold Soup That Is Delicious On A Hot Day")
d: Mix well; cover and refrigerate. Chill overnight and serve with croutons. Remove from the refrigerator about 30 minutes before serving.

i: 1/2 lb. spaghetti, cooked, drained and rinsed
d: Mix package of dressing according to instructions on package. Add remaining ingredients.
Chill until time to serve.
Mix all ingredients well.
Bake 1 hour at 350° in two loaf pans lined with waxed paper or foil.

i: 1/2 lb. Turkey or Italian sausage, sliced in 1/2 inch slices
d: Assemble all ingredients (except beans) in large kettle and cook till vegetables are tender.
Add the beans, undrained, and reheat.
Ready to serve.

i: flour seasoned with salt and pepper
d: Coat chicken with flour, fry on both sides until brown. Transfer to 9 x 13-inch shallow casserole.
Add chopped onion.
Mix chicken bouillon with apricot; pour over chicken.
Sprinkle with parsley and almonds.
Cook, covered, at 350° for 45 minutes, uncover and cook 20 minutes more.
Serve over rice.

Saute onion in oil. Add rice; stir constantly for 3 to 5 minutes. Add water, tomato, garlic powder and bouillon cubes (that have been dissolved in the water). Reduce heat and cover. Let simmer 15 minutes. Let set a few minutes before serving. Makes 3 to 4 (1 cup) servings.
Mix flour, sugar, salt and baking powder; cut in shortening until mixture is crumbly (set aside).
Dissolve yeast in warm water; add soda and buttermilk.
Stir yeast mixture into dry ingredients and mix well.
Chill.

i: 12 chicken thighs, skin and fat removed
d: In large frying pan, cook chicken parts in oil until lightly browned, turning once.
Add onion; stir until cooked.
Mix broth, curry powder, lemon juice and salt.
Pour over chicken; cover and simmer 25 minutes.
Turn chicken once.
Increase temperature; stir in pepper and pineapple.

i: 8 oz. shredded cheese (Cheddar or Monterey Jack)
d: Mix all ingredients together.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes. Serve with tortilla chips.
Combine shortening and sugar.
Blend in eggs, milk, vanilla, cherries and dates.
Sift together flour, soda, salt and baking powder.
Add to other mixture.
Shape into balls; roll in crushed corn flakes.
Bake at 375° for 12 to 15 minutes.
Beat eggs until foamy.
Add oil, sugar and vanilla.
Stir in zucchini.
Mix in dry ingredients.
Pour into greased and floured cupcake tin.
Bake at 325° for 1/2 hour.
Do not drain beans.
Fry hamburger, onions and bell pepper (green) until brown (drain).
Add beans and other ingredients. Cook in electric fry pan at 350° for 30 minutes or in slow cooker for 2 to 3 hours on low.

i: 1 clove garlic, minced in 2 Tbsp. shortening
d: Brown hamburger and drain grease.
Combine all ingredients in frying pan with meat and simmer 1 hour, uncovered.
Prepare peppers (remove white sections).
Mix ground beef, rice, egg and seasoning.
Fill peppers; place in deep cooking vessel.
Cover with V-8.
Cook for 1 hour at 350°.
Slice paper thin California oranges, limes and lemons.
Pour punch over mold of ice.
Put fruit in 1 hour before. Whoop-see-doo!
Combine all ingredients in a 10-inch skillet.
Cook over medium low heat.
Stir until well mixed.
Cook for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Cream together margarine and sugar; beat in eggs.
Dissolve soda in buttermilk and add alternately with flour and salt.
Mix in orange peel, nuts, dates and coconut.
Cook in a 300° oven until done.
Bake in a ring pan.

i: 1/2 c. prepared Italian salad dressing, divided
1 lb. boneless sirloin steak, cut into 1/4 inch strips
1 medium onion, sliced and separated into rings
d: In a large resealable plastic bag, combine 1/4 cup salad dressing and chili powder; add steak.
Seal and turn to coat; refrigerate for 30 minutes.
In a skillet, saute peppers and onion in remaining salad dressing until crisp and tender.
Remove and keep warm.	Drain steak, discarding marinade in skillet, saute steak for 6-10 minutes or until no longer pink.
Return vegetables to pan.
Heat through and spoon meat and vegetables down center of tortillas; fold in sides.
Drain beans. Form bundles by placing 10 beans on a piece of bacon, wrapping bacon around beans.
Cook sugars and milk until forms soft ball in cold water. Remove from heat.
Add butter and beat until creamy.
Add pecans and vanilla.
Drop on waxed paper.
Makes 30 pieces.

i: rice and saffron (vigo flavoring and coloring)
d: Brown chops and chicken until done or almost done; set aside. In large roaster, brown onions, peppers and garlic to soft state in 1/2 cup olive oil.
Wash 3 cups rice in strainer until water is clear.

i: 1 can black olives, drained and sliced
d: Layer in very large bowl the first 5 ingredients in order. Mix mayonnaise and sugar and spread on top, covering salad well. Sprinkle Parmesan cheese and bacon bits on top and refrigerate at least 8 hours.
Toss before serving.
Layer ingredients with beans on bottom into serving dish. Garnish with jalapeno slices.

t: Linda Twelves' Broccoli And Cauliflower Salad
i: 1 lb. bacon, fried crisp and chopped into small pieces
d: Chop together; cauliflower, broccoli and onions.
Mix sugar and vinegar.
Mix vegetables with sugar and vinegar sauce mixture. Marinate in refrigerator overnight.
Before serving add mayonnaise and bacon.
Mix.
Cook ground beef in a skillet over medium heat until browned, stirring to crumble. Drain off pan drippings.
Spoon beef into 8-inch square baking dish.
Top with pizza sauce and cheese. Combine biscuit mix, milk and eggs; beat until smooth.
Pour mixture over casserole, covering evenly.
Bake at 400° for 30 to 35 minutes.
Yields 6 servings.

i: 1 1/2 c. Pillsbury's Best all-purpose or unbleached flour
6 oz. pkg. (1 c.) semi-sweet chocolate chips
d: Heat oven to 375°.
In large bowl, combine sugar, brown sugar, margarine, peanut butter, vanilla and egg; blend well.
Lightly spoon flour into measuring cup; level off.
Stir in flour.
Press dough evenly over bottom of 12 or 14-inch pizza pan, forming rim along edge.
Bake at 375° for 10 minutes.

i: 1 can beef broth or chicken (8 oz.)
d: Melt margarine.
Add flour; mix well.
Cook over medium heat until hot.
Add broth.
Cook until boiling.
Add other soup and water.
Melt margarine in an 8 x 8-inch pan.
Place biscuits in pan. Cover with grated cheese.
Bake in preheated oven according to package directions.

i: 2 to 3 bananas, sliced just before serving
d: Do not drain liquid off fruit.
Mix all ingredients together. Chill and serve.

i: 1/3 c. vinegar (I use white but other may be used)
d: Boil potatoes with the skins on.
Peel and slice thinly.
Fry slowly in large skillet the bacon.
Drain on paper towel.
Saute in the bacon fat until golden the onion.
Blend in flour, sugar, salt, celery seed and dash of pepper.

i: 50 grams salt pork or bacon (about 1.76 oz.)
d: Rinse a long slice of meat and pat dry.
Pound it lightly with a mallet; salt and pepper it.
Put on it slices of salt pork or bacon and hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters.
Roll meat up and dredge in flour.
Fry, add some beef stock and bake in oven until done at 350°.
Just before the meat is done, add minced garlic. Serve with potatoes and vegetables.

i: 1 c. yellow or red bell pepper, chopped
2/3 c. kiwi fruit, peeled and chopped
d: Combine all ingredients.
Serve on Hawaiian Lavash crackers or serve over grilled chicken.
Can be made 3 hours ahead.
Shape hamburger into patty.
Place vegetables on top of hamburger (which is in the center of a 12-inch square piece of foil).
Season with salt, pepper and butter.
Fold ends of foil in a drugstore wrap.
Bake at 400° for 1 hour.
Serves 1 person.
Brown hamburger and onions.
Add macaroni and remaining ingredients and simmer for 5 minutes.

Combine milk, sugar and salt; slowly stir in water. Mix in eggs and vanilla. Pour mixture into 6 custard cups. Set pan containing 1-inch hot water. Bake in a 350° oven for 50 minutes or until knife inserted near edge of custard comes out clean. Serves 6.
Combine all ingredients and cook for 20 minutes.
Slowly pour into hot sterile jars and seal.
Step 1:
Add 4 cups canning or pickling salt to 2 gallons of fresh water.
Fill 4 gallon churn with whole cucumbers.
Pour water and salt over cucumbers and let stand for 7 days.
Cook fresh broccoli until tender or thaw 2 packages of frozen broccoli.
In a large skillet, heat olive oil; brown broccoli in oil with onion, parsley flakes, garlic powder, salt and pepper. Add chicken broth and cook until liquid reduces, about 3 to 4 minutes.

i: 1 lb. boneless breast, cut in strips
1 pkg. Hidden Valley Ranch dressing mix
d: Heat oil in large skillet.
Add chicken breast and stir-fry for 5 minutes.
Add Hidden Valley mix, vegetables and soy sauce. Stir-fry over medium heat about 5 minutes.
Serve over chow mein noodles or Chinese rice.
Brown beef in large skillet.
Drain off fat.
Add all remaining ingredients and simmer 2 1/2 hours or until veggies are tender.
Serves 6 to 8.
Mix together flour, sugar, cinnamon, salt, baking powder and soda.
Add eggs and oil; mix well.
Add zucchini and nuts; stir just enough to mix together.
Pour into a greased and floured loaf pan (8 1/2 x 4 1/2 x 3-inch).
Bake about 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
The top will split open and a tester comes out clean when done.
Mix pumpkin
and
dry ingredients thoroughly.
Add eggs and milk.
Pour
into 9-inch pie pan with unbaked crust.
Peel tomatoes.
Blend all ingredients and refrigerate.
Bake in casserole in a 375° oven for 1 hour, until potatoes are tender.
Makes 8 servings.
Mix all ingredients except chipped beef and parsley.
Chill for 1 hour.
Shape in ball.
Roll in chipped beef and parsley until well coated.
Keep in refrigerator.
Serve with cracker (any kind).
Oil pan and add onion, garlic and beans.
Dissolve the tomato paste in water and add seasonings, herbs and a dash of sugar. Pour over the beans and cook for 15 minutes.

i: 1 (13 1/2 oz.) can pineapple tidbits, drained (reserve syrup)
d: Mix thoroughly the beef, crumbs, onion, egg, salt, ginger and milk.
Shape mixture into balls.
Melt shortening in a large skillet; brown and cook meatballs.
Remove meatballs, but keep warm.
Pour off fat from skillet.

i: 1/2 lb. bacon, cooked and crumbled, drain off all grease
d: Wash broccoli well; pick off leaves and peel outer skin.
Cut florets and chop well.
Cut and chop stem of broccoli and mix with florets.
Wash cauliflower well; pick off leaves.
Use only florets of cauliflower; chop florets and add to broccoli.
Mix in onion and bacon.
Cream together sugar and butter; add remaining ingredients. Roll in shape of a pecan. Bake at 350° for 8 to 10 minutes. When cooled, roll in powdered sugar.

i: 3 slices Mozzarella cheese, cut into wedges
d: Combine first 9 ingredients in bowl and mix well.
Pat into rectangle on waxed paper.
Layer ham and shredded cheese on ground beef.
Roll like jelly roll, sealing edges and ends.
Place seam side down in baking dish (13 x 9-inch).
Bake at 350° for 1 hour and 15 minutes.

Heat 2 inches of oil in skillet, over medium heat. Wash and slice squash about 1/4 inch thick, keep moist. Place dry ingredients in paper bag, add handful of sliced squash and shake well. Fry in hot oil until golden brown. Drain well on paper towel and serve hot. Can use zucchini as well.
In large skillet, melt butter.
Add chicken, onion and garlic; cook until chicken and onion are tender.

Stir in spaghetti sauce. Reduce heat; simmer, covered, stirring frequently, 10 minutes. Meanwhile, cook pasta according to package directions; drain. Serve sauce over hot pasta; sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.
Sift together the flour, soda and salt; set aside.
Combine shortening and sugar in a bowl.
Beat until creamy.
Add egg yolks; beat well.
Combine cocoa, water and vanilla to make 1 cup; mix well.
Add to batter.

i: 1 large apple, peel and shred (coat with a little lemon juice)
1/2 tsp. dill seed (or enough to suit taste)
6 oz. cucumber and onion salad dressing
d: Combine first five ingredients in a mixing bowl.
Blend in salad dressing to moisten.
Cook noodles.
Brown meat and drain off fat.
Add tomato sauce to meat and simmer.
Blend cream cheese and onion in bowl; add sour cream.
Drain noodles.
Rinse in cold water and drain again.

Combine first 6 ingredients in a large bowl. Combine the honey, oil and vanilla together and add to dry ingredients. Spread on 2 large greased baking pans and bake 20 to 25 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add the apples.
Store in covered container in refrigerator.

t: Cranberry Jello(Given To Clo Fletcher By Clara Hollingsworth)
d: Mix jello, hot water and cold water.
Add cranberry sauce; stir until dissolved.
Refrigerate and chill, then add sour cream and pecans.
Refrigerate until serving time.

i: chicken pieces (enough to fill a 9 x 13-inch dish)
d: Baste skinless chicken pieces with dressing.
Pour 1/2 cup of dressing over chicken and bake for 30 minutes at 375°.
Turn chicken; sprinkle Parmesan and drained mushrooms over chicken. Bake an additional 30 minutes, basting twice.
Rinse and drain chickpeas.
Place all in food processor and process until blended.
Serve with oil, parsley and ground red pepper sprinkled on top.
Stir-fry veggies in chicken broth, no oil.
Light cooking is best.
You can season with onion flakes, garlic flakes or no-salt seasoning mix.
Serve hot.

i: 3 c. chopped pears or 4 c. chopped apples
d: Mix oil and sugar; blend in vanilla and eggs.
Sift all dry ingredients and add to mixture.
Add pears and nuts (optional). Mix well.
Bake in greased and floured 9 x 13-inch pan.
Cook 45 minutes in 350° oven.
Cool and ice with Cream Cheese Icing.
Grind first 5 ingredients and drain.
Sprinkle with salt and let stand overnight.
Rinse well and drain.
Combine remaining ingredients.
Pour over vegetable mixture.
Heat to a full boil and boil for 10 minutes.

i: 1 garlic clove, peeled and finely minced
d: Broil peppers until charred on all sides.
Remove and place them in paper bag (to steam) for 10 minutes; remove skins and seeds.
Slice peppers into thin strips.
Set aside.
Cut broccoli flowers in small pieces.
Cook bacon and crumble. Mix mayonnaise, sugar and vinegar.
Pour over all other ingredients.
Mix well and refrigerate.
Cut lamb legs in strips.
Add black pepper and salt to 3 tablespoons flour; mix good with flour.
Pour 1 cup oil into skillet or frying pan.
When oil is hot, add lamb.
Keep turning until meat is soft; then add rest of flour.
Keep turning until gravy thickens.
If desired, chop pecans.
Pinch marshmallows.
Crush crackers. Pinch cherries.
Using hands to mix and squeeze, combine all ingredients in a large bowl and add just enough evaporated milk to mix to stiff consistency.
Melt oleo.
Add sugar and egg yolk, then add remaining ingredients.
Spread on ungreased cookie sheet.
Bake at 350° for 10 to 12 minutes.
Combine
all ingredients, except nuts, in top of double boiler over medium heat.	Stir constantly until smooth.
Then add nuts. Spread in 9 x 5-inch buttered pan.	Cool and cut in squares.

Combine soups, sour cream, picante sauce and onion. Heat to almost boiling. Grease 9 x 9-inch baking pan. Break tortillas into pieces. Line pan with half of the tortillas. Spread half of chicken on tortillas and pour half of soup mixture over top. Repeat layers with remaining ingredients and top with cheese. Bake in 350° oven for 30 to 40 minutes.
Brown meat; add onions, seasoning, tomatoes, corn and bouillon.
Bring to a boil.
Stir in green peppers.
Boil again. Stir in rice and remove from heat.
Cover and let stand for 5 minutes.
Serves 4.
Dice and saute celery, onion, carrots, zucchini, and ham in dutch oven with
olive oil and seasoning.
Stir in tomatoes, chicken broth, spinach, 1 can beans (smashed), and 2 cups water. Heat to boil.
Reduce heat and simmer 15 minutes.
Add the rest of beans and sprinkle with parmesan cheese.

i: juice of 1 lemon or to your taste
d: Wash the fish in cold water
and pat dry.
Rub fish all over with some olive oil.
Broil both sides of fish until they are done.
(This may take 15 minutes or more, depending on the size of the fish.) Beat the lemon juice and olive oil together with a fork and pour over the
fish
Drain oysters, saving liquid.
Combine crumbs, butter, salt and pepper.
Spread 1/3 of butter and crumbs in greased casserole (2-quart).
Cover with 1/2 of the oysters.
Using another 1/3 of buttered crumbs, spread second layer and add remaining oysters. Finish with last 1/3 of buttered crumbs on top.
Combine liquid ingredients and pour over top.

i: 3 large tomatoes, peeled, seeded and cut into chunks or 1 (28 oz.) can tomatoes
d: Heat the olive oil in a saucepan.
Add the onion and garlic and cook gently, stirring for 4 to 5 minutes, taking care that they don't brown.
Stir in the sugar and cook for another minute, then add the tomatoes, bay leaf, allspice and 1/2 teaspoon salt.
Cook lima beans first by themselves until done.
Add all the other ingredients and let simmer 10 minutes.
Combine salad dressing mix, sour cream and mayonnaise.
Set aside.
Place half of crumbled cornbread in a large bowl.
Top with half of beans.
In a medium bowl, combine tomatoes, pepper and onions; layer half of this mixture over beans.
Layer half of cheese, bacon, corn and reserved salad dressing.

t: Fresh Apple Cake With Caramel Glaze
d: Combine cooking oil and sugar.
Add well beaten eggs and vanilla (or cinnamon).
Measure and sift together the flour, salt and soda.
Add dry ingredients to first mixture.
Stir in apples and nuts; combine thoroughly.
Pour into tube or loaf pan; bake at 350°.

i: 1 tsp. salt, sugar and soy sauce
d: Can make up your choice of vegetables.
Heat oil; add meat. Stir-fry for 3 to 4 minutes.
Add onion, mushrooms and carrots. Stir-fry until onion turns clear.
Add cornstarch mixture.
Bring to boil and stir until thickened.
Add bok choy and broccoli. Cook until desired doneness.
In 9 x 13-inch pan, pour pineapple (juice and all) in bottom. Spread evenly, then sprinkle coconut, nuts and brown sugar over the pineapple.
Add cake mix (dry) over that.
Top with slices of butter or magarine and bake at 350° for 35 to 45 minutes.
Mix together. Serve in bowl with an ice ring.
Grind ingredients.
Cook until thickened.
Put in jars and refrigerate after it cools.
Makes 1 1/2 quarts.

i: 2 lb. fillets of mackerel or other white fish, cut into 1/2-inch pieces
d: Combine fish and lemon juice in a glass bowl.
Marinate 2 hours.
Drain and discard marinade.
Simmer for about (or at least) 1 hour.
Making 1 day before is better.
Combine flour and oil in large Dutch oven.
Stir over medium heat until flour is medium brown.
Stir in cut-up bacon and cook 1 minute.
Add onion, green pepper and garlic and cook until onion is transparent.
Add next 8 ingredients (soy sauce through water). Simmer 45 minutes, or until chicken is tender.
Add okra and cook 10 minutes.
Melt butter; add sugar, cocoa and milk and cook for 3 minutes. Remove from heat and add peanut butter, vanilla and oats.
Stir well; work quickly.
Spoon out on wax paper or aluminum foil and cool.

i: 2 cooked, skinless chicken breasts, cut up
d: Combine all ingredients.
Top with almonds or Chinese noodles. Add Ranch salad dressing to taste.
Combine Jell-O and boiling water.
Add while Jell-O is still hot the pineapple and bananas.
Cover top with small marshmallows while hot and let set in refrigerator.
Before serving, cover with salad dressing and grated Longhorn cheese.
Fix in 9 x 13-inch baking dish.

i: 1/4 box crackers (1 pack of 4 pack box)
d: Cook chicken and cut in bite size pieces.
Add soups, sour cream and lemon juice; mix well.
Melt oleo, crumble crackers over chicken mixture which has been put in a baking pan.
Drizzle oleo over the crackers.
Sprinkle poppy seed over the mixture and bake 30 minutes at 350°.
Mix together all ingredients as you would for meat loaf.
Form into small balls; brown in frying pan. Drain grease and place meat balls in a baking dish. Cover with jar of spaghetti sauce. Cover with foil. Bake at 400° for 40 minutes.
Heat
oven to 350°.
Grease 9-inch pie plate.
Sprinkle bacon, cheese and onions evenly over pie pan.
Place remaining ingredients
in blender on high speed for 1 minute.
Soak cut up onion and garlic in vinegar for 20 minutes. Strain.
Add to all remaining ingredients.
Mix and shake well. Delicious with any green salad or pasta salad.
Combine ground beef, onions and water in a saucepan.
Simmer until beef turns brown.
Add tomatoes with liquid, vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, chili powder, cumin, allspice, salt, cayenne, cinnamon, garlic powder and bay leaves.
Cover; simmer 3 hours.
Fat will float.
If time, chill the chili and lift off fat layer or spoon off fat.

i: 1 (12 oz.) can whole kernel corn, drained
d: Saute onion; add ground beef and cook until color changes; drain off fat.
Stir in 1/2 can tomato sauce and remaining ingredients and set aside.
Cut green peppers in half lengthwise; remove core and seeds.
Place peppers, hollow side up, in shallow casserole dish.
Spoon beef mixture into pepper halves.
Spoon remaining tomato sauce over peppers.
Mix salmon, potatoes, onion, egg and pepper until blended. Form into 8 patties, each about 3/4-inch thick.
Spread crumbs on waxed paper.
Gently press patties in crumbs to coat.
Heat oil in a large nonstick skillet.
Add patties and cook over medium heat about 4 minutes per side until heated through, golden and crisp. Serves 4.

t: Sea Scallops With Lemon Butter Sauce
d: Coat 1 lb. sea scallops in flour. Season with salt and pepper. Saute scallops in olive oil & butter in a large skillet over high heat for 2 minutes on each side.	Remove scallops.
Add butter, lemon juice, parsley, salt & pepper and heat.
Serve scallops over rice and top with sauce from skillet.
Soak 30 (6-inch) bamboo skewers.
Combine teriyaki sauce, ginger, oil, garlic and red pepper.
Cut steak across grain into 1/4-inch thick slices.
Place meat in plastic bag.
Pour marinade over steak and marinate in bag up to 2 hours.
Thread slices on bamboo skewers.
Blend thoroughly.
Shape into a round loaf in an 8 x 8 x 2-inch baking pan.
With a spoon, make a crater like depression in top of loaf.
Combine applesauce, brown sugar, vinegar and mustard; pour into depression.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Serves 4 or 5.
Wash and cut up rhubarb.
Measure 6 cups of sugar.
Mix well and let stand 2 hours or more.
Wash and grind up 2 oranges and add to rhubarb and sugar.
Boil about 20 minutes.
In a medium dish, put chicken, veggies and fruit all together. Pour honey and ginger on top.
Put lid on dish.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Serve over rice.

i: 1 chocolate ready crust pie shell (6 oz.)
1/4 c. Eagle Brand sweetened condensed milk
2 pkg. (4 serving size) chocolate flavored instant pudding
1 (8 oz.) tub frozen non dairy whipped topping, thawed
d: Pour chocolate and sweetened condensed milk into bowl; stir until smooth and pour into crust.
Press nuts evenly onto chocolate in the crust and refrigerate 10 minutes.
Pour milk into large bowl and add pudding mixes.
Beat with wire whisk for 2 minutes or until smooth.
Mixture will be thick.
Spread 1 1/2 cups of the pudding over nuts and chocolate layer in crust. Immediately stir half of the whipped topping into remaining pudding.

i: 2 pkg. Jiffy cornbread mix, cooked, cooled and crumbled
8 to 10 slices bacon, cooked, drained and crumbled
d: Mix well and let set overnight.
Will make 2 quarts and 1 pint.
You can cut this in half.

i: 1 (28 oz.) can plum tomatoes in thick puree or add tomato sauce to tomatoes
2 lb. string beans, trimmed and halved or 50 oz. canned, drained
d: Heat oil in Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add onion and garlic. Cook, stirring occasionally, 8 minutes or until soft.
Add tomatoes, thyme, sugar, salt and pepper, breaking up tomatoes with back of spoon.
Stir in string beans; simmer, covered, stirring occasionally, 25 to 35 minutes or until tender.
(Can be made a day ahead and refrigerated.)
Serve hot or warm.
Use fresh or frozen berries.
Put all ingredients into blender container.
Blend until smooth.
Serves 4.
Cut cabbage into 6 wedges, removing core; place in large saucepan.
Add onion, sugar, salt, bay leaf and water.
Bring to boil.
Cover, reduce heat and simmer 20 minutes.
Add vinegar and apple; cover and cook 3 to 4 minutes or until apple is tender.
Wash and peel potatoes, slicing very thin.
Use 1 tablespoon of the butter and place in a shallow baking dish.
Evenly layer one half of the potatoes in the dish, sprinkling lightly with salt and add 1/2 of the cheese.
Layer remaining potatoes, lightly salt and place remaining cheese on top.
Sprinkle with chopped chives and bake at 450°
for 45 minutes until potatoes are tender and browned on top.
In a bowl, mix dry ingredients.
Slowly add cold water, a little at a time, until dough forms.
Divide into balls about the size of a softball.
On a floured surface, roll balls out into an even circle about 1/4-inch thick.
In a large frying pan, heat lard or oil on medium heat.
Oil should be about 1-inch deep. Place fry bread in pan.

t: Old Fashioned Pot Roast With Egg Noodles
d: Brown rump roast in oil in Dutch oven.
Remove meat; drain off fat.
Combine mushrooms, vegetables, garlic, 1 1/4 teaspoons salt, bouillon cubes, pepper, bay leaves, tomato paste and 2 cups water in Dutch oven; heat to boiling point.
Add roast.
Simmer, covered, for 4 hours.

i: 1 head lettuce, torn into bite size pieces
1 (6 oz. or larger) pkg. corn chips, crumbled
d: Brown meat well in a large skillet. Drain. Add green pepper, onion, salt and pepper. Brown a few more minutes.
Add tomatoes. Place in a large bowl in this order: lettuce, tomatoes, corn chips, hot meat mixture and hot cheese mixture.
Melt butter.
Add sugar, spices and nuts.
Grease Bundt pan. Cut up 1 can of biscuits into pan.
Cover with 1/3 sauce.
Repeat 2 more times.
Bake at 300° for 40 to 45 minutes.

i: 3 to 4 lb. leg of lamb, boned
d: Cut meat into 1-inch cubes and place in marinating dish.
Mix all other ingredients and pour over meat.
Cover and refrigerate overnight, stirring occasionally.

i: 1 bag tri-colored rotini noodles, cooked and drained
d: Boil the noodles, then rinse and put in a bowl which you will be using for serving the salad.
Saute scallops in the olive oil. Add the scallops along with the rest of the seafood to the noodles.
Dice the green onions (exclude the chive part) and boiled eggs.
Add to the salad.
Add mayonnaise; use more or less for taste.
Mix ingredients together.
Cook bacon until crispy, then crumble into small pieces.
Cut broccoli into small pieces; mix all ingredients together.
Chill and serve.
Combine ingredients and heat at 325° in oven until it bubbles. (Can be done in microwave, about 3 minutes.) Serve with king sized Fritos.
Combine flour, baking powder, cinnamon, soda and salt; sift. Beat together eggs, pumpkin, sugar and oil.
Add flour mixture; beat well and stir in pecans.
Put in ungreased 15 x 10 x 1 baking pan.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes or until toothpick comes out clean.

Preheat oven to 375°. Cut peppers in half lengthwise. Remove stems, rubs and seeds. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat, swirling to cover bottom. Saut crookneck squash, zucchini, onion and garlic until zucchini is tender-crisp (about 4 minutes). Don't overcook. In a medium bowl, combine rice and cheese. Gently stir into skillet.
