Stuff pepper halves with vegetable mixture. Place in a 9-inch casserole dish. Carefully pour tomato sauce around peppers. Bake, uncovered, for 30 minutes. Serves 8.
Drain liquid from beans.
Put all in a bowl with onion and pepper.
Mix together oil, vinegar, sugar, salt and pepper.
Pour over beans.
Marinate at least 4 hours.
Mix yeast and sugar with 1/4 cup water.
Set aside until bubbly.
Place flour and yeast mixture into a mixing bowl and blend to form a dough.
Knead on a floured surface for about 5 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
Place in a greased bowl. Cover and let rise 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
Punch down and shape the pretzels as follows:
Mix all together and bake at 350° in a greased 9 x 13-inch pan for about 1/2 hour.
Top will turn light brown.
Layer asparagus in a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Pour margarine or butter and oil over asparagus.
Sprinkle with parsley.
Cover with foil; bake at 375° for 15 minutes.

i: 3 c. peeled and cubed Winesap or Rome apples
d: Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Grease and flour a tube or Bundt pan.
Combine the oil, sugar and eggs in a large bowl.

Mix well. Combine the flour, cinnamon, salt, and baking soda. Add the flour mixture to the creamed mixture alternately with the apples. Stir in the pecans and vanilla. Pour into the prepared pan. Bake for 1 1/2 hours or until a toothpick inserted into center of cake comes out clean. Cool for about 10 minutes before removing from the pan. Frost with your favorite caramel icing, if desired.
Mix together ground beef with salt and pepper and enough flour to hold meat together.
Roll into meat balls. Roll meat balls in more flour to coat. Drop meat balls into boiling water and cook. It will make its own gravy.
Serve over rice or mashed potatoes. May add more seasoning to taste as these are very bland.
Mix zucchini, onion and salt; cover with water and allow to set a minimum of 5 hours or overnight.
When time is up, bring to a boil the remaining ingredients.
Combine boiled syrup with drained zucchini and allow to simmer 1/2 hour.
Put mixture in hot canning jars and seal.
Sprinkle chicken with salt and pepper.
Brown in oil in skillet.
Add onion, garlic and mushrooms.
Cook until onion is soft.
Combine remaining ingredients and pour over chicken.
Cover and simmer 30 minutes.

i: 10 oz. pkg. frozen chopped broccoli, thawed and drained
d: Mix together sour cream, soup mix, broccoli and half of cheese.
Sprinkle remaining cheese on top.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes, uncovered.
Serve hot with vegetable crackers.

i: 1 head lettuce, torn into bite size pieces
6 slices bacon, broken into small pieces
d: In order given, put veggies in a bowl or cake pan in layers. Sprinkle with the sugar.
Spread crumbled cheese over it.
Spread broken bacon pieces next (fried and cooled).
Cover all with mayonnaise.
Cover with plastic wrap; refrigerate.
Best if used the same day.

i: 1 (12 oz.) can frozen orange juice concentrate
d: Combine juices and mix well.
Pour over ice in punch bowl and add ginger ale.
Garnish with maraschino cherries.
Makes 25 punch cup servings.
Cut uncooked fish into small pieces.
Add lime juice to cover fish; cover and refrigerate 3 to 4 hours, or until fish become opaque.
Stir occasionally.

i: 3 or 4 whole chicken breasts, cut in halves
d: Place chicken breasts in one layer in a shallow baking pan. Mix together all other ingredients.
Pour sauce over chicken. Cover with foil. Refrigerate. Spoon sauce over chicken a couple of times.
Soak bulghur wheat in 2 cups of warm water for 1/2 hour.
Chop parsley, green onions and tomatoes.
Pour excess water from wheat. Combine all ingredients in large bowl.
Refrigerate several hours or overnight.
Serve on lettuce leaves or stuffed in pita pockets.
Mix box of jello with the cup of boiling water.
Mix until dissolved.
Add cold water.
Let jell slightly, then add all other ingredients.
Mix and jell in a flat, 1 1/2-quart dish.
Cut into squares to serve on a lettuce leaf.

i: 1 2/3 c. cooked kidney beans or pink beans
d: Mash drained beans.
Saute chopped tomato, green pepper, onion, chilies, garlic and salt in oil until thoroughly heated. Add mashed beans, cheese and hot pepper sauce to taste; mix well.

i: 8 green onions, sliced with stock and bulb used
d: Saute rice in margarine until light brown (rice).
Place in 3-quart casserole.
Add remaining ingredients; cover.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour or a little longer.

i: 1 1/2 c. graham cracker crumbs, finely crushed
d: Mix the first 3 ingredients and pat into a 9 x 13-inch dish.

i: 1 1/2 lb. lean ground beef or turkey
3/4 c. bread crumbs or crushed crackers or any flavor potato chips, crushed
1/4 c. finely chopped onions or green onions
d: Combine all ingredients and form into a loaf.
Bake in 350° oven for 45 minutes to 1 hour.
When done, top with:
Place chocolate in microwave-safe bowl; heat until melted. Dip each pretzel rod about halfway into chocolate.
Sprinkle with candy stars.
Place on waxed paper.
Yield: 2 dozen.
Cut dates into thirds, add coconut, nuts and cherries, sprinkled with flour.
Add milk and mix well.
Butter loaf pan and dust with flour.

i: 1 (6 oz.) pkg. dehydrated hash brown potatoes
d: Place potatoes	in
a
medium bowl.
Cover with boiling water. Let
stand
for
Soak raisins in boiling water for 20 minutes.
Saute almonds in margarine until crisp; drain and reserve.
Combine next 9 ingredients.
Stir hot rice into the dressing.
Fold in bell pepper, green onions, raisins and coconut.
Chill.

i: 1 can okra and tomatoes or frozen okra or fresh smothered okra (much better)
d: Put in buttered baking dish a layer of okra, layer of creamed corn, layer of onion, green pepper, bacon and layer of whole kernel corn.
Top with grated cheese.
Cover and bake for 1 hour at 375°. Serves 6 to 8.

i: 1 boneless pork loin roast (3 to 4 lb.)
d: Place roast in shallow Pyrex baking dish.
Sprinkle with salt, pepper and garlic powder.
Pour lemon juice over it and cover. Marinate for 2 to 3 hours or overnight (refrigerated).

Roast pork in 325° oven for 20 minutes per pound with marinade and uncovered. Heat cranberry sauce until melted, add brown sugar and orange juice; simmer until sugar dissolves and mixture thickens slightly. Brush glaze on roast 1/2 hour before it is ready.
Cut beans lengthwise.
Chop onions.
Combine all ingredients in a heavy saucepan.
Cook, covered, for 20 to 30 minutes.
Serves 6.
Bring to a rolling boil and pour over packed jars.
Hot water bath if necessary.

i: 2 1/2 jars Kraft Old English cheese spread
1 can cubed chunky ham or 1 lb. cooled drained pork sausage
d: Soften and mix together first 3 ingredients.
Spread mixture on one side of bread slices; lay in 9 x 13-inch baking dish.
Beat eggs with milk.
Pour over.
Can add cubed ham or sausage.
Bake at 325° for 1 hour and 15 minutes or until set.

i: 1 whole chopped onion or 1 tsp. dehydrated onion
1 (16 oz.) can tomatoes and green chilies, chopped
d: Brown meat with onions in skillet.
Add tomatoes and chilies, beans and salt.
Put in 2-quart oblong casserole.
Make corn bread according to directions on package.
Pour corn bread mix over meat and beans mix.
Bake at 350° for about 35 minutes.
Trim off green and pink parts of watermelon rind; cut to 1-inch cubes.
Parboil until tender, but not soft.
Drain. Combine sugar, vinegar, oil of cloves and oil of cinnamon; bring to boiling and pour over rind.
Let stand overnight.
In the morning, drain off syrup.
Heat and put over rind.
Melt shortening and margarine until it bubbles; add to sugar. Stir in the molasses.
Dissolve soda in boiling water and add to mixture.
Sift in approximately 4 pounds flour.
Add spices along with the flour.
The dough should be almost stiff enough to roll. Cover and let set overnight.
Do not refrigerate.
Drain pineapple, reserving 3 tablespoons liquid.
Mix reserved 3 tablespoons liquid, dressing, mustard, curry and salt in a large bowl.
Add pineapple and remaining ingredients; mix lightly. Refrigerate.
Spoon chicken mixture over lettuce-lined platter, if desired.
Makes 4 servings.
Mix well, pour into greased and floured tube pan and bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Mix all ingredients well.
Pour into pie shell.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes to an hour.

i: 1 pkg. chive and onion cream cheese
d: Spread cream cheese on tortillas, followed by a thick layer of turkey and a cucumber.
Roll up jelly roll style and cut into bite sized pieces.
Dip in salsa for an appetizer.
Soak bread crumbs in water.
In food processor or blender, combine and process remaining ingredients until almost smooth. Add bread crumbs; process until smooth. Chill for several hours to blend flavors.
Serve with toasted pita chips.

In a large bowl, cream butter and sugar. Add egg. Mix well. In separate bowl, combine flour and baking powder. Gradually add to butter mixture. Add coconut, pecans and vanilla. Mix thoroughly. Press 2/3 of dough into a greased 9 x 13 pan. Spread preserves. Crumble remaining dough over preserves. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes. Cool in pan. (Makes 36 bars.)
Mix oil, sugar, eggs, prunes, milk, salt, spices and most of flour. Stir well. Add nuts; stir and add soda last with flour last. Pour into well greased and floured loaf pan.
Bake 35 minutes at 325°.
Slice the squash into thin rounds. Dip in beaten egg then coat with the cornmeal. Fry in oil on medium heat on both sides. Drain on paper towel and salt lightly before serving.
Mix eggs, sugar, margarine and vanilla; add coconut, raisins and pecans. Pour into 2 unbaked pie shells and bake at 350° for 40 minutes.

i: 1 lb. carrots, sliced diagonally or 1 lb. pkg. frozen carrot slices
d: In a large skillet, bring salted water to a boil.
Add carrots to boiling water and cook over medium heat until tender, about 10 minutes.
Drain well.
Add butter, marmalade and nutmeg to carrots.
Saute over medium high heat until carrots are glazed. Serve immediately.
Serves 4.
Mix and chill.
Can use for salad or dessert.
May add 1 cup pineapple chunks and 1 cup coconut.

t: Grilled Leg Of Lamb With Garlic And Rosemary
i: 2 Tbsp. fresh chopped rosemary or 2 tsp. dried
1 (5 lb.) leg of lamb, boned, butterflied and trimmed of excess fat
d: Combine garlic, rosemary, salt and pepper in small bowl.
Mash with fork to form coarse paste.
Place lamb in shallow baking dish.
Spread half of garlic paste over each side.
Cover and refrigerate at least 2 hours or overnight.
Bring to room temperature before cooking.
Heat oven to 325°.
Drain and dry fruit.
Place in 1 1/2-quart casserole dish.
Mix butter with sugar and curry powder and spread over fruit.
Bake for 1 hour.
Combine flour, sugar and cocoa and add to other ingredients. (Add melted butter last.)
Mix well and pour into unbaked pie shells.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Serve with ice cream.
Mix all ingredients and use as a dip for raw vegetables.
Blend all ingredients together with electric mixer on medium speed for 3 minutes.
Bake in greased and floured tube pan for 40 minutes at 350°.
Soak carrots, onions, cauliflower, cucumbers and peppers individually in weak salt water overnight.
Cook individually in same water, just until tender.
Pour off water.

i: 2 to 4 Tbsp. regular or light mayonnaise
d: In a bowl, combine apples, chicken, mayonnaise, pepper, pimento, rosemary and lemon pepper.
Chill until ready to serve. Place lettuce leaf on a serving plate and top with the chilled chicken salad.
Garnish with alfalfa sprouts if desired.

i: 1 lb. skinless chicken, pieces or chunked
d: Drain pineapple.
Reserve juice.
Combine juice, ketchup, vinegar, brown sugar, cornstarch and soy sauce.
Blend well.
Heat oil in wok or skillet.
Add chicken and stir-fry 3 minutes. Remove chicken.
Mix yeast and water; stir well.
Mix flour, salt and sugar, then add oil.
Stir in liquid; mix well.
Let rise double.
Spread on buttered cookie sheet and top with sauce and your favorite toppings.
Bake at 425° for 20 to 30 minutes.
Mix well and cook over medium heat until thick.
Serve over buttered toast or biscuits.
Mix all ingredients together, chill well and serve.
Combine all ingredients.
Mix thoroughly.
Pour into casserole dish.
Bake at 350° for 20 to 25 minutes.
Melt oleo and cocoa over low heat.
Add flour, sugar and baking powder; stir to blend.
Add eggs and vanilla; blend.
Add nuts. Pour into a greased 9 x 13-inch pan.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Combine sugar and milk in saucepan.
Add vinegar.
Boil until it forms a soft ball in cold water.
Pour mixture over pecan halves.
Add grated rind of 2 oranges and stir until pecans are coated.
Spread on foil to dry.

i: 2 1/4 c. warm water (not hot, but very warm)
d: In mixing bowl, combine warm water, yeast, sugar, salt and olive oil.
Mix well.
Gradually work in enough flour to make a soft dough that leaves the sides of the bowl and does not stick to hands.
Turn onto a lightly floured board.
Knead until smooth and elastic.
Place in greased bowl.
Brown meat and drain.
Add taco seasoning and canned tomatoes. Heat through.
Cover the bottom of a 9 x 13-inch pan with tortillas.
Pour beef mixture over tortillas.
Add another layer of tortillas over beef.
In a small bowl combine cottage cheese, Monterey Jack and egg.
Preheat broiler.
Arrange fish in a single layer in greased shallow baking dish.
Sprinkle with salt, pepper and olive oil. Broil 5 inches from heat about 10 minutes or until fish flakes easily.
Drain liquids from pan (important!).
Prepare macaroni and cheese as directed.
Add drained tuna, pimento and onion.
Mix.
Serve hot or cold.
Mix and heat ingredients and add crab meat at the end.
Melt chips in the microwave; stir in noodles and nuts.
Drop on waxed paper or a cookie sheet by the spoonful.
Refrigerate to set.
Combine all ingredients in a small serving bowl and set aside for the flavors to meld, about 3 hours or overnight is best. Keeps about 1 week. Good on almost everything.
Makes about 2 cups of salsa.
Sift together flour, soda and spices.
Add sugar to shortening, creaming well.
Blend in egg and honey.
Add the dry ingredients; mixing thoroughly.
If desired, chill dough before shaping.
Mix ingredients and form in balls; put in Pyrex pan close together.
Brown hamburger; drain.
Cook onions, pepper, celery and garlic in a separate pot until tender.
Add to meat with the rest of the ingredients.
Simmer 1 hour.
Mix first 4 ingredients and set aside.
Then use sifter to mix next 5 ingredients.
Combine the two and add nuts.
Use Bundt cake pan and bake at 350° for 1 hour (may take longer; some ovens vary).
When cooled, cover with following icing.

i: 1 (16 oz.) pkg. frankfurters, cut into 1-inch chunks
d: Saute onion in oil until tender.
Stir in catsup, water, vinegar, mustard and pepper.
Bring to a boil, stirring occasionally.
Place frankfurters in a 1-quart baking dish.
Pour sauce over.
Bake, uncovered, at 375° for 30 minutes or until bubbly.

i: Hunt's country herb ready tomato sauce
d: In skillet with oil, cook chicken breasts with onion.
When chicken is cooked through, add tomato sauce, mushrooms and parsley flakes.
Simmer 5 to 10 minutes.
Serve over rice.
Saute onion and green pepper in small amount of margarine.
Add ground beef and brown.
Add tomato soup and pizza sauce.
Stir in water.
Simmer 15 minutes.
Layer cooked noodles in a large greased casserole dish with lid.

i: 1 c. chopped nuts and/or 1 c. raisins
d: Cream butter and sugars.
Add almond extract, eggs, vanilla and applesauce.
Mix well.
Blend in flour, oatmeal and baking soda.
If desired, add nuts and/or raisins.
Drop by teaspoonfuls onto nonstick cookie sheets.

i: 1 c. chicken broth (leftover from cooking chicken)
d: Mix the first 5 ingredients together.
Grease a 10 x 13-inch baking dish.
Line with chips.
Top with 1/3 meat mixture and 1/3 cheese.
Repeat layers, ending with cheese.
Pour chicken broth over casserole.

i: 1 each, green, yellow, red pepper chopped
d: Soak bulgar in the lemon juice. Set aside. Prepare chopped ingredients. Add to bulgar when lemon juice has been absorbed. Mix all together, add salt and pepper and add olive oil last. Adjust seasoning.
This is a nice vegetarian salad served with pita bread.
Dissolve yeast in 1 cup warm water and 1 tablespoon of honey. Set aside 10 minutes.
Combine the next 5 ingredients.
Blend lentils, oil, remaining honey and a small amount of water in a blender.
Place in a large mixing bowl with remaining water.
Stir in two cups of (mixed) flour.
Add the yeast mixture.

i: 1 c. green beans, cut into 1-inch pieces
d: In 10-inch skillet, over medium heat in hot oil, cook cauliflowerets, beans, mushrooms and carrots with garlic, curry and cumin until vegetables are tender-crisp, stirring often.
In small bowl, stir together vegetable juice, cornstarch and pepper; gradually stir into skillet.
Cook until mixture boils and thickens, stirring constantly.
Sprinkle with almonds.
Makes 4 cups or 8 servings.

i: 1/2 c. each: pecans or black walnuts, raisins and coconut
d: In large bowl, combine eggs, margarine and sugar until creamy. Add nuts, raisins, coconut, vanilla and vinegar.
Mix well.
Pour into unbaked pie crust.
Bake 5 minutes at 425°.
Reduce heat to 325° and bake about 30 to 40 minutes or until set.
Combine eggs, sugar, oil and vanilla.
Mix in dry ingredients, beating well.
Then add pineapple, pecans and bananas.
Bake at 350° about 30 minutes in 9 x 13-inch pan.
Cut and fry onion in vegetable oil until red.
Add meat and other ingredients.
Let it cook on high heat for about 5 minutes, then turn the heat down.
Let it cook until the meat is tender.
Sprinkle sugar over berries.
Pour cake mix over pineapple, blueberries and sugar.
Melt and pour margarine over cake mix. Sprinkle chopped pecans over top.
Bake in a 9 x 13-inch buttered dish or pan for 45 minutes at 350°.
About 30 minutes before serving:
In medium bowl, mix well corned beef, celery, green onions, one egg and salt.
With hands, firmly shape mixture into 4 patties.
Prepare cake mix according to package directions.
Grease 9 x 13-inch pan.
Combine pecans and coconut; sprinkle in bottom of pan.
Pour prepared cake mix over pecan and coconut mixture.

i: 2 medium eggplants, cut into small cubes
d: Fry onion.
Add salt, pepper, turmeric powder and cumin.
Stir. Add eggplants, tomatoes and garlic.
Stir and leave it on low fire. When eggplants are cooked, put green pepper.
Cover.
Leave it on fire for 5 minutes.
Generously grease one 2 qt. deep casserole dish or two 8 x 4 in. loaf pans.
Lightly spoon flour into measuring cup; level off. In large bowl combine 1 c. bread flour, sugar, salt and yeast - blend well.
In small sauce pan, heat milk and margarine until very warm (120 to 130 degrees).
Add warm liquid and egg to flour mixture.
Blend at low speed until moistened; beat 3 minutes at med. speed.
By hand, stir in whole wheat flour to form a stiff dough.
Preheat oven to 375°F.
Lightly oil baking pan.
Cut a narrow wedge 2/3 through each potato.
Insert a rosemary sprig and a sliver or two of garlic in each wedge.
Drizzle a bit of olive oil in the opening, too.
Place potatoes in baking dish, making sure none of them touch.

i: 1 1/2 lb. shrimp, cooked, peeled (best to use small shrimp)
d: Mix all ingredients together except bread crumbs and 1/4 cup margarine.
Pour into greased casserole.
Top with bead crumbs and remaining margarine.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

i: 1 (3 to 4 lb.) chicken, cut up into bite-size pieces
d: Place cut up
chicken
in
large bowl, add 1 tablespoon curry powder, garlic, salt, pepper and turmeric.	Mix well and let sit overnight
in refrigerator or for 2 hours minimum.	In heavy pot, add oil
making

i: 4 large, very ripe tomatoes, seeded and diced
1 large cucumber, peeled, seeded and small diced
1 small clove garlic, mashed or minced
d: Mix the tomatoes, cucumber, onion, green pepper, tomato juice, vinegar,
oil and garlic; add salt and pepper to taste. Puree if desired.
Chill
until cold.
Serve in small glasses or spoon into bowls.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Melt margarine in a 9 x 13-inch pan in oven.
Sprinkle crumbs over margarine.
Pour condensed milk evenly over crumbs.
Top with chips; press down firmly.
Bake 25 minutes or until lightly brown.

i: 1 lb. ground beef, fried and drained very well (pat dry with paper towel)
1 large bottle and 1 small bottle Catalina dressing *
d: *One bottle is probably enough, but some people might like more; mix in.

i: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen mixed vegetables (broccoli, carrots or any type frozen vegetables)
d: In a 1 1/2-quart casserole, combine gravy and poultry seasoning.
Stir in chicken and frozen vegetables.
Bake at 400° for 15 minutes, or until mixture begins to bubble.
Grind zucchini, celery and onion.
Combine and refrigerate; let stand overnight.
Next day, rinse zucchini, celery and onion with water.
Squeeze out very good.
Then put all the other ingredients together.
Cook; when zucchini comes to a boil, cook 30 minutes longer.
In blender container, place all ingredients except whipped cream.
Cover; blend until smooth.
Serve immediately over crushed ice.
Garnish with whipped cream.
Makes about 4 (6 ounce) servings.
Beat the egg with a fork.
Bring the broth to a simmering point.
Remove from heat and slowly drizzle in the beaten egg, stirring slowly.
The egg should become stringy-looking rather than making the soup appear milky.
Garnish with parsley.
Serve hot.
Combine the flour, sugar and juice.
Mix well with pineapple. Stir Cheddar cheese into pineapple.
Pour into buttered casserole dish.
Melt margarine and add crumbled crackers.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

i: 1 frozen whole boneless turkey breast (thaw only when ready to use)
d: Put turkey breast in pan, skin side down.
Combine garlic, pepper, salt and rosemary in blender.
Add oil; chop all together. Spread 1/2 of mixture over turkey.
Bake on rack in roasting pan (300°) for 1 hour.
Put remaining mixture on turkey roast.
Finish baking at 300° until done.
Mix dry ingredients (flour, sugar, soda, salt and cinnamon). Stir in apples, raisins and nuts by hand.
Batter will be stiff. Bake in 2 greased and sugared loaf pans.
You may also bake in a tube or Bundt cake pan.
Bake loaves for 45 minutes and cake for 1 hour.
Also, may be baked in greased muffin tins (either regular or miniature sizes).
Freezes beautifully.
Put zucchini, onions, carrots and peppers through food grinder using medium blade.
Sprinkle with salt.
Let stand overnight (I let stand about 3 to 4 hours).
In morning, drain and rinse well with cold water.
Mix together the remaining ingredients.
Bring to boil in a large kettle.
Crush wafers to fine crumbs.
Add to nuts and chopped cherries.
Mix well in milk.
Pour coconut on waxed paper.
Form rolls by rolling and shaping on coconut.
Wrap in foil. Refrigerate.
Place steak in shallow pan.
Combine oil, vinegar and garlic. Pour over steak.
Cover and let stand in refrigerator for 3 hours or better yet overnight, turning several times.
Preheat broiler. Broil steak 3-inches from heat, about 5 minutes.
Season.
Turn; broil 3 minutes.
Bring to a boil and boil, covered, 2 minutes.
Uncover and cook to a soft ball stage.
While cooking, beat 2 egg whites until stiff, but not dry.
Pour the syrup over the beaten egg whites slowly.
Beat with electric mixer until thick, almost stands in peaks.
Quickly add 2 cups pecans and 1 1/2 teaspoons vanilla. Drop on wax paper.
Place the meat in a large, wide dish.
Slice 2 medium onions and lightly crush the bay leaves and black peppercorns.
Put them around the filet, then add the bottle of red wine, olive oil and Italian parsley sprigs.
Marinate the filet at cool room temperature for several hours or overnight, turning it several times.
Put all ingredients in a covered jar.
Let stand in refrigerator 2 to 3 days.
Serve small portions on small cup-like pieces of lettuce or large corn chips.

i: 1 pkg. frozen artichoke hearts or 12 tiny fresh artichokes
8 oz. fresh fettuccine (white, green or mixed)
1/3 c. toasted cashews, pine nuts or almonds, chopped
d: Trim artichokes and prepare as for hearts, cutting in halves or quarters.
Or, if using frozen artichokes, thaw and use as they come in the package.
Saute onion in butter until translucent.
Add garlic, wine, lemon juice and artichokes.
Cover and cook over medium heat 1 to 2 minutes.
Add heavy cream and cook, uncovered, 2 to 3 minutes longer until mixture thickens slightly.
Cook and drain broccoli.
Combine soup, mayonnaise and onion. Blend in cooked broccoli.
Pour into greased 9 x 13-inch baking pan.
Sprinkle with cheese.
Melt margarine and add seasoned crumbs.
Mix well and sprinkle over top of cheese.
Wash seeds.
Steam seeds (colander inside a pot).
Put water into the bottom of the colander or strainer.
Cook covered at a fairly high heat about 30 minutes; add more water if needed.
Dump seeds on paper towel and pat dry.
Spread on cookie sheet.
Dice
bacon
and
fry
until
crisp.
Heat large iron pot or Dutch oven until very hot.
Add oil and heat.
When oil is just before smoking, add all of the flour and stir rapidly with a whisk.
Cook until the flour is a rich brown color.
You must stir constantly in order not to burn the roux.

i: 2 (15 oz.) cans white kidney beans, rinsed and drained thoroughly
freshly ground black pepper to taste
d: Place drained kidney beans in a medium bowl and mash thoroughly with a fork.
Saute garlic in olive oil until lightly golden, about 30 seconds.
Do not let it brown.
Mix the garlic and the beans and all other ingredients except 1 tablespoon parsley.
Most ingredients are approximate, depending on how many guests are to be served.
This recipe can be altered to stuff the chicken breasts depending on what you have in the fridge. Some suggestions:
chopped cooked spinach, parmesan cheese, blue cheese, scallions, baby shrimp, asparagus, dried cranberries and sage, stuffing, herbs and whatever else inspires you.

i: 1 2/3 c. beef stock or canned broth
d: Chop onion.
Cut zucchini into 1-inch pieces.
Combine beef, chili powder, 1/2 teaspoon salt and 1/4 teaspoon pepper.
Shape into 1-inch balls.
In a Dutch oven, heat oil over medium heat. Add meatballs; brown well.
Remove meatballs and reduce heat.
Boil water with salt, red pepper, chili powder, puree and ketchup in large pot.
Fry meat until well browned.
Fry onions; mix together.
Add meat to large pot.
Simmer 2 hours.
Add hot dog sauce, chili beans and cooked spaghetti.

i: 4 to 6 chicken breasts, deboned and cut into bite size pieces
d: Mix buttermilk and dressing mix.
Pour over chicken, cover and let stand overnight or several hours in refrigerator.
Mix flour, salt, pepper and turmeric.
Dip chicken in dry mixture and deep fry until golden brown.
Spread pecans and coconut on bottom of sheet cake pan.
Mix cake mix as directed on box.
Pour over coconut and pecans.
Mix cream cheese, butter and powdered sugar together.
Pour mixture over cake mix.
Don't spread all the way to the edge.
Mix all ingredients together; put in a greased casserole dish. Make sure rice is moist and coated.
Bake at 350° for 35 minutes.

i: 2 c. (15 1/2 oz. can) pineapple chunks, drained
d: In a large bowl, combine first 7 ingredients.
Toss lightly. Cover and chill at least 2 hours.
Before serving, slice banana. Add to turkey mixture with nuts.
Serve in lettuce cups.
Garnish with coconut and oranges.
Mix flour, milk and salt with mixer.
Stir in all other ingredients.
Grease cookie sheet.
Spoon out and bake at 350° for 10 to 12 minutes.
Rinse beans and drain (do kidney beans separately and drain). Mix and add onion, celery and green pepper.
Heat vinegar and sugar until sugar is dissolved.
Cool.
Add oil, salt and pepper. Mix thoroughly.
Pour over bean mixture.
Mix gently.
In a blender, process first 7 ingredients until smooth. Transfer to a small bowl; stir in cheese.
Cover and chill 8 hours or overnight to allow flavors to blend.
Store in airtight container in refrigerator.
Makes about 1 1/2 cups dressing.

i: 3 medium size carrots, finely shredded (1 1/2 c.)
1/2 c. thawed, frozen, no-fat, no-cholesterol egg substitute
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
Spray muffin pan cups with grease.
In medium bowl, combine flour, 1/2 cup sugar, cinnamon, salt, baking powder and ginger.
In a large bowl with whisk or fork, mix carrots, yogurt, egg substitute, applesauce, raisins, brown sugar and vanilla until well blended.
With spoon, stir together the 2 mixtures until moistened.
Spoon batter into muffin pans.

i: 1 medium carrot, cut lengthwise into fourths and thinly sliced
d: Mix cabbage and salt; let stand 1 hour.
Heat sugar, vinegar, water and celery seed to boil in 1-quart saucepan.
Boil and stir 1 minute.
Cool to lukewarm.
Drain cabbage; stir in celery, green pepper, carrots and onion.
Stir vinegar mixture into cabbage mixture.

i: 6 1/2 oz. can tuna, packed in water and well drained
1/2 tsp. curry powder or to taste
d: In medium bowl, combine tuna, apple, celery, green onion, almonds and currants.
Stir in mayonnaise, chutney, mustard and curry powder.
Refrigerate.
Great for summertime entertaining!
Spread refried beans in shallow dish.
Mix sour cream with taco seasoning and spread over beans.
Top with cheese, tomatoes and black olives.
Use tortilla chips for dipping.
Preheat oven to 350°. Coat bottom and sides of 8

x 12 inch baking dish with cooking spray. In separate bowl, blend eggs, milk, yogurt Feta and Swiss cheeses, parsley, basil, oregano and garlic. Spread half of pasta over bottom of baking dish. Cover with salsa. Add half of shrimp. Spread remaining pasta over shrimp. Pour and spread egg mixture over pasta. Add remaining shrimp and top with Mozzarella cheese. Bake 30 minutes. Remove from oven and let stand 10 minutes before serving.
Combine flour, salt, paprika, and mustard.
Roll meat in flour mixture.
Brown meat slowly in hot oil.
Sprinkle remaining flour mixture over meat.
Add remaining ingredients.
Cover and simmer for 1 1/2 to 2 hours.
Heat oven to 350°.
Prepare potato puffs for 4 servings as directed on package.
Stir in pickle relish, mayonnaise, onion and mustard.
Spoon into a 1-quart casserole.
Cut each hot dog in half.
Insert hot dog pieces upright around edge of mashed potatoes.
Preheat oven to 325°.
In large bowl, beat eggs until foamy. Add flour, sugar and brown sugar.
Beat until well blended.
Blend in melted butter.
Stir in chocolate chips and walnuts.
Pour into pie shell.
Mix ingredients; pour over crackers.
Stir until absorbed. Store in airtight container.
Melt margarine in large sauce pan.
Add flour, salt and basil.
Stir until thick and comes to a boil.
Add chicken. Cheese mixture consists of cottage cheese and egg.
In 9x13 pan, layer chicken mixture, then noodles, then cheese mixture, spinach, Mozzarella cheese.
Repeat layers.
First, grind and drain the tomatoes, cabbage, peppers, onions, celery, lemons and hot peppers.
Mix with other ingredients. Store at room temperature in jars, covered tightly enough to keep sour gnats out.
But do not seal as it will work like sauerkraut. It is good either before or after it works, though the flavor will change.
Place all dry ingredients in a plastic bag and shake well. Then add sherry and soy sauce.
Shake well.
Add chicken; close bag tightly and marinate overnight.
Turn bag several times.
Bake chicken for 45 minutes at 400°.

i: 1/4 c. bacon bits or shredded cheese
d: Coarsely dice potatoes and eggs with salt, pepper and celery seed.
Add diced cucumbers, onion, carrots, celery and pickles/relish.
Mix salad dressing/Miracle Whip with mustard and pour over mixture.
Toss until well coated, adding more salad dressing as needed.
Add bacon bits or shredded cheese and garnish with tomato wedges.

i: 1 green bell pepper, seeded and diced
1/2 red bell pepper, seeded and diced
1/2 yellow bell pepper, seeded and diced
1 (35 oz.) can whole tomatoes, pureed with liquid
d: Combine the scallions, fresh tomatoes and peppers in a large mixing bowl.
Stir in the pureed tomatoes, water and lime juice. Stir in the oil and parsley; season with salt and pepper. Refrigerate, covered, until ready to serve.
Serve with your favorite corn tortilla chips.
A great summer dip!
Yields 8 cups.
Boil potatoes until half done; peel, cut and place in baking dish.
Combine sugar, water, syrup and margarine; cook over low heat until it forms soft ball when dropped into cold water.
Add lemon juice; pour over potatoes and sprinkle with nutmeg.
Place in 450° oven; cook for about 1 hour, basting potatoes with syrup.

t: Medallions Of Pork With Wild Mushrooms And Berries
d: Melt 1 stick of butter with the chopped garlic.
Add the mushrooms; saute over medium heat until partially cooked.
Add the cranberries, blueberries and Port; continue to cook for another 5 minutes, or until mushrooms are soft.
With a slotted spoon, remove the mushrooms and berries; set aside on a warm platter.
Thinly slice green onions (including tops).
Combine with white rice, finely chopped tomatoes, olive oil and vinegar. Cover and chill.

i: 1 to 1 1/2 c. green grapes (or you may use the seeded red grapes)
d: Combine all ingredients and chill.
Serve on lettuce leaves. Serves 8.
In a large bowl, soften and cream Genesis 18.
Slowly beat in Isaiah.
In small bowl, beat Job well and add to creamed mixture. Sift together 1 Samuel, 1 Kings, Exodus and Luke.
Add to creamed mixture and mix thoroughly.
Fold in 2 Samuel and Genesis 43. Grease a 9-inch tube pan with Judges.
Line with waxed paper; pour in batter.

i: 1/4 lb. American sliced or Velveeta cheese
d: Cook hamburger, stirring, but not browned.
Add salt, pepper and garlic.
Add tomato sauce; simmer for 20 minutes.
Cook noodles; drain.
Put a layer of meat sauce in baking dish.
Top with noodles and corn (which has been drained) and cheese.
Brown meat; drain.
Add seasoning mix and simmer 15 to 20 minutes; set aside.

i: 1 1/2 lb. medium shrimp, peeled and deveined
3 garlic cloves, peeled and cut in three
d: Place all ingredients in a bowl.
Marinate 12 hours in refrigerator.

i: 1 (4 oz.) bar German sweet chocolate, shaved
d: Mix dry ingredients; whip eggs and oil together and add remaining ingredients and stir to mix well.
Pour into a greased and floured 9 x 13-inch pan and bake at 350° for 40 minutes.

i: 1 large box grape or black cherry Jell-O
d: Mix all of the ingredients. Let congeal.

i: salt and fresh ground pepper to taste
d: Heat a dry heavy skillet, preferably cast iron over medium heat.
Add garlic cloves and roast, turning occasionally with tongs until blackened in spots and soft (about 10 minutes).
Peel, removing tough ends.
In blender or food processor, combine garlic, apple cider, oil, vinegar and mustard.
Process until smooth.
Season with salt and pepper.
Heat vegetable oil in a large pot set over medium-high heat. Add the tortillas, chili powder, cayenne pepper, paprika, cumin, garlic and bay leaves and saute for 10 minutes.
Add onion, red bell pepper and cilantro; cook 5 minutes.
Add tomatoes and cook 5 minutes.
Add chicken stock and cook 45 minutes.
Add the lemon juice and season with salt and pepper.
Blend soup in blender set on medium.
Fry chicken in shortening for 1 hour on low heat.
Use electric skillet.
Do not coat chicken with flour.
Mix mushrooms, green pepper, tomatoes, onion and water.
Pour over chicken.
Simmer 1/2 hour, basting often.
In blender combine eggs, egg yolks, milk, flour, cornmeal, sugar and salt.
Blend until smooth.
Mixture should be consistency of unbeaten whipping cream.
Set aside in refrigerator at least 1 hour.
Remove batter from refrigerator and stir well with whisk. Heat 9-inch nonstick pan over medium-high heat.
Rub lightly with butter.
Mix as for meat loaf.
Bake at 300° for 45 to 50 minutes.
As it bakes, keep grease poured off from pan.
Trim stem from eggplant but don't peel.
Cut into 1/2-inch slices, then cut into cubes.
Place the eggplant slices in a bowl with salted water to cover.
Weight eggplant down with a plate. Let soak 15 minutes.
Remove seeds and membrane from pepper, then cut into strips and chop coarsely.
Cut zucchini ends, then slice crosswise thinly.

i: 1 can Campbell's cream of mushroom soup
1 (6 1/2 oz.) can Chicken of the Sea tuna, drained and broken in chunks
d: Reserve 1/2 cup Chinese noodles.
In 1 1/2-quart casserole, combine remaining noodles, soup, water, tuna, cashews, celery, onion and peas.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes or until hot.
Stir; sprinkle reserved noodles around edge.
Bake 5 minutes more. Serve with soy sauce.
Makes about 4 1/2 cups.
Combine first 4 ingredients; stir until sugar is dissolved. Chill; add ginger ale just before serving.
Yield:
30 servings.
Boiling fish is a favorite way
of preparing and serving fish in Denmark.
Usually only large fish are cooked this way, but slices of large fish, such as salmon, pike, halibut or carp are also excellent.
You should have a rack with 2 handles so the fish can be lifted out of the water without breaking it.
You can tie a cloth around it before it cooks.
Wash the fish well; sprinkle with salt and let stand a few minutes.
In heavy skillet, heat and stir sugar until it browns and melts, being careful not to let it burn.
Add chicken, turning to coat with melted sugar.
Brown slightly.
Add rice, onion and seasoning, stirring a little.
Add water to cover.
Simmer, uncovered, until rice is cooked, adding water as needed.
Wash and slice onions and cucumbers.
Mix remaining ingredients to make syrup.
Put onions and cucumbers in jug and pour syrup over top.
Mix well and put in refrigerator.
Next day, take out of refrigerator and shake well.
Repeat this for 6 days.
Wash venison.
Season and fry in bacon drippings and remove. Make roux with olive oil and flour.
Add tomato sauce and tomato paste.
Add all seasonings except garlic.
Simmer for 1 hour.
Add venison to soup and seasoning.

i: 1 (4 oz.) can chopped green chilies (with juice)
1 (48 oz.) jar Great Northern beans (with liquid)
3 to 4 dashes Tabasco or pinch of cayenne pepper (optional)
1 to 1 1/2 lb. boneless, skinless chicken breasts*
d: *Bake 1/2 hour at 350°. Cool. Cut into bite size pieces.

Cook macaroni; drain and rinse in cold water. In a large bowl, combine the macaroni, green onions, tomato and celery. In a small bowl, combine mayonnaise, vinegar, salt and pepper. Pour over macaroni mixture and toss to coat. Cover and chill for at least 2 hours. Just before serving, add bacon. Yield: 12 servings.
Combine egg, sugar and oil.
Mix flour and cinnamon.
Stir into egg mixture.
This is very stiff.
Add vanilla, apples and walnuts. Pour into greased and floured 9-inch cake pan.
Bake at 350° for 45 to 50 minutes.

i: 8 to 10 oz. Monterey Jack cheese, grated
d: Grate 10 ounces sharp Cheddar cheese into a 9 x 13-inch buttered Pyrex dish.
Sprinkle 5 tablespoons of finely chopped jalapeno peppers on top.
Grate 8 to 10 ounces Monterey Jack cheese on top of peppers.
Press down.
Pour 6 to 7 beaten eggs onto cheese.
Bake 45 minutes at 325° or until set and golden. Cut into 1-inch squares.
Chop onions, peppers and cucumbers; sprinkle with salt and let set 1 hour.
Drain.
Mix remaining ingredients and add to cucumber mixture.
Cook at rapid boil for 20 minutes.
Put in jars and seal.

i: 1 small can sauce (pizza, tomato or spaghetti)
d: Put 1/2 pizza dough on greased sheet.
Spread sauce to cover dough.
Saute onion and garlic in butter; mix in cheeses and spinach.
Spread on pizza dough.
Place second layer of dough on top.
Bake at 450° for 15 to 20 minutes.
Bring pineapple to boil.
Add jello; stir until completely dissolved.
Cool.
Add remaining ingredients.
Dissolve yeast in warm water.
Add sugar, oil and room temperature buttermilk.
Stir together well (be sure oil is well blended when adding flour).
Add 4 cups flour and mix well.
Then add 5 cups flour (more if needed), salt, baking powder and soda; mix well.
Dough is ready to bake.
Beat together oil, egg and skim milk.
Add sugar, whole wheat flour, unbleached flour, baking powder and salt and beat until smooth.
Spoon into an 8 x 8-inch Pam sprayed pan.
In a small bowl, mix the dry ingredients.
Stir in buttermilk, egg, Tabasco and onion.
In a heavy saucepan, heat enough oil for deep frying.
Form the batter into rounded tablespoonfuls and drop into the hot oil.
Fry only until light brown.
Drain on paper towels and serve hot.

Brown sausage, ground beef, onion and pepper in large skillet. Drain off excess grease. Put mixture in a large pot; add chili powder, salt and pepper. Stir in tomatoes and beans. Bring to a boil; reduce heat to simmer for 20 to 25 minutes.
Mix together butter, sugar, eggs and lemon extract.
Blend in flour, baking powder and salt.
Cover and chill at least 1 hour. Roll dough 1/8-inch thick on a lightly floured board.
Cut into desired shapes.
Cut a small hole in center with a sharp knife. Place cookies on foil covered baking sheet.
Place a Life Saver in the center of each cookie.
Mix in order of ingredient list.
Bake at 350° for 45 to 60 minutes.
Combine sugar, oil and egg; beat well. Add molasses and beat. Combine flour and other ingredients. Stir into sugar mixture. Shape dough into 1-inch balls.
Place 1-inch apart on cookie sheet and bake at 350° for 8 to 10 minutes.

Combine sugar, oil and sweet potatoes. Add eggs, water and vanilla; blend. Sift dry ingredients and add mix only until blended. Do not overmix. Pour into greased pan. Bake at 350° for 25 minutes. Cool and ice with powdered sugar.
Brown meat and onion; drain well.
Place in a 13 x 9-inch pan. Pour the mixed vegetables (drained) over the meat mixture.
Mix 2 envelopes of gravy mix; pour over the meat and vegetable layers.
Mash meat of 1 very, very, large avocado with a fork.
Add juice of lemon, salt and pepper.
Add grated onion, dash of garlic salt and Tabasco sauce.
Beat with hand mixer until smooth.
Serve with corn chips.
In 3-quart saucepan over high heat, heat broth, tomatoes, garlic and basil to boiling.
Add chicken, rice, peas and hot pepper sauce.
Return to boiling; remove from heat.
Cover.
Let stand 5 minutes or until most of the liquid is absorbed.
Fluff rice with fork before serving.
Bake chicken at 350° for 45 minutes.
While chicken is baking, butter tortillas, then microwave on High for 1 minute and 45 seconds.
Mix in cream cheese and onion together.
Cut chicken up and put in mixture.
(This is your filling.)
Fill up tortillas and wrap.

t: Neiman-Marcus White Meat Of Chicken Salad
i: 3 c. white meat of chicken, cut in bite size dice
d: Mix mayonnaise and vinegar. Combine with remaining ingredients.
Spread on choice of bread or garnish with almonds if serving as a stand alone salad.
Serves 4 to 6.
Cream the sugar and butter in a mixing bowl.
Add egg yolks and beat well.
Stir in vanilla, milk and jam.
Add all dry ingredients and beat with mixer.
Add fruit and nuts.
Fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites.
Boil water.
Add oats; stir and set aside to cool.
(Or, use the leftover oatmeal from breakfast instead of throwing it out.) Mix oleo, eggs, brown sugar, sugar and cinnamon.
Add flour, soda and salt.
Add cooled oatmeal and mix.
Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.
In a large bowl, layer 1 1/2 cups of lettuce, then chopped eggs, green peas, remainder of lettuce, bacon and cheese.
Seal top of salad completely with mayonnaise.
Sprinkle with green onions and paprika.
Cover and refrigerate 24 hours.

i: 6 to 8 pieces chicken or amount needed
1 can Campbell's cream of mushroom soup
d: Wash chicken thoroughly.
Dry with paper towel.
Lay chicken pieces in a casserole dish.
Sprinkle with salt and pepper. Combine half the onions with the soup and milk.
Pour over chicken. Bake in 350° oven for 45 minutes or until chicken is done. Sprinkle remaining onions over top and return to oven for 5 minutes.
Place chickpeas and green and yellow beans in mixing bowl. Season well.
Place shallots, mustard, garlic, vinegar and oil in small bowl.
Season and, using a wire whisk, mix well until thick. Pour dressing over beans.
Mix well.
Add parsley and tarragon; mix well.
Let sit 15 to 30 minutes at room temperature.

Cook and stir beef, onion, garlic and crushed red pepper in 10-inch skillet until beef is light brown. Drain. Add tomatoes, green pepper, raisins, salt, chili powder, cumin, cloves and cinnamon. Cover and simmer 10 minutes.
In separate pan, cook and stir almonds over medium heat until golden, 2 to 3 minutes. Stir almonds and olives into beef mixture. Serve with rice.
In medium saucepan bring dressing ingredients to a boil & boil for 2 minutes,
stirring occasionally.
Combine all other ingredients (vegetables).
Pour warm dressing over combined vegetables, stir together & refrigerate overnight or longer.
stir before serving (you may drain before serving.
Serves 12-18.

i: 1 cake yeast or 3 pkg. dry yeast
d: Can use dehydrated potato flakes.
Add flour as desired. Dough should be soft and elastic.
Bake as rolls or bread at 350°. Bread is done when it sounds hollow when tapped.

i: 1 egg plus 2 Tbsp. milk or water, beaten
d: Wash chicken; drain on paper towel.
Season chicken with salt and pepper.
Dip in egg mixture.
Add spices to flour; coat chicken.
Fry slowly in hot oil until browned on both sides (about 35 minutes), turning as needed.
Put all ingredients except salt and paprika in a blender or food processor and puree.
Season with salt.
Garnish with paprika.
Serve with pita bread.
Makes about 3 cups.
Combine together sugar, oil and eggs.
Beat well and set aside.

i: 1/2 medium red or green bell pepper, chopped
1 1/2 tsp. brown sugar (or more, if desired)
d: Soak the black beans in water overnight.
Heat oil in a Dutch oven.
Add onions and garlic and cook over low heat until vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes.
Drain the beans.
Add the beans to the Dutch oven.
Add the ham bone and the water.
Arrange in layers in a casserole.
Bake at 350 degrees for 2 hours or until pork chops are done.
Place artichoke hearts (with marinade) in shallow casserole or glass pie plate.
Combine corn flake crumbs and Mrs. Dash and sprinkle over artichokes.
Top with Mozzarella cheese and bake in a 350° oven until hot, about 12 to 15 minutes.
Serve immediately.

t: Hot Shrimp Salad Or Scallop Appetizer
i: 1 head leafy lettuce (romaine, bibb, red or green leaf)
d: Clean lettuce, dry and arrange on salad plates.
Heat saute pan, add olive oil and melt butter.
Saute shallots first, then add garlic and saute until wilted.
Saute shrimp or scallops until done.
Serve on bed of lettuce and dress with pinch of salt and ground black pepper.
For more intense flavor, brown 1/2 pound lean bacon, reserve bacon and substitute bacon drippings for butter.
Mix flour with hot roll mix flour.
Use yeast from mix plus extra yeast, dissolved in the warm water.
Mix yeast water into flour and mix until dough comes away from side of bowl.
Add maybe 1/2 cup flour more and knead 1 minute.
Cover and let rise 1/2 hour.
Roll 1/3 of dough to size of 10-inch pie shell.

i: 1 c. shredded Monterey Jack or Cheddar cheese
d: Heat oven to 400°.
Grease pie plate (10 x 1 1/2-inch).
Cook and stir beef and onions until brown; drain.
Stir in seasoning mix; spread into plate.
Top with chillies.
Beat milk, baking mix and eggs until smooth, 15 seconds, in blender or 1 minute with mixer.
Scald 6 pint jars. Bring vinegar, water and salt to a boil. Add garlic, pepper, dill and mustard.
Simmer 5 minutes.
Place small okra pods and spices evenly into scalded jars.
Pour liquid over okra and seal.
Let stand at least a week.

i: 1 lb. pkg. brown and serve sausages
d: Cook sausages according to package directions and cut into thirds.
Alternate pieces of pineapple, sausage, water chestnuts, green pepper and mushrooms on long toothpicks.
Combine remaining ingredients; heat skillet.
Add kabobs, cover and simmer 10 minutes.
Remove from skillet; serve warm as a dinner or appetizer.
Yields 40 to 50.
You may use a little cottage cheese and cereal in place of bread crumbs.
Add a little more lentils.
Add enough crumbs so it is not soupy.

i: 1 tsp each - parsley, thyme, sage, rosemary
d: Coat the chicken pieces with olive oil.
Lay the chicken in a casserole with tight fitting lid.
Add the garlic cloves and herbs and all the remaining olive oil.
Cover.
Bake at 325° for 1 1/2 hrs. without removing lid.
Serve with French bread.
Combine first three ingredients.
Stir in remaining ingredients.
Drop by tablespoon onto greased cookie sheet.
bake at 350° for 10 to 12 minutes.
Combine all ingredients in a bowl; stir potatoes to coat. Wrap potatoes in two 12-inch square foil pouches, sealing all edges.
Grill over medium flame for 15 to 18 minutes.
Mix all ingredients and chill.

i: 1 lb. red bell peppers, roasted and peeled (about 2 large)
1 lb. yellow bell peppers, roasted and peeled
32 (1/2-inch thick) diagonally cut slices French bread baguette, toasted
d: Cut bell peppers into 1x1/4-inch julienne strips.
Combine pepper strips and next 5 ingredients in a bowl.
Stir well.
Cover and let stand at room temperature for 2 hours.
Spoon about 1 tablespoon of mixture onto each bread slice.

i: 4 boneless butterfly pork chops 1 inch thick
d: In a large skillet on medium heat brown pork in oil for 5 minutes.
Remove; keep warm.
In the drippings saute onion and mushrooms until tender, stir in water, mustard and salt; bring to boil.
Return chops to pan; reduce heat, cover and simmer for 15 minutes, until chops are tender.
Remove and keep warm.
Combine flour and sour cream until smooth; add to skillet.

i: 3/4 lb. boneless chuck, cut in thin slices
1 c. green and/or red pepper strips
d: Saute beef, onion and garlic in oil until meat browns.
Add rest of ingredients except rice; bring to a boil.
Stir in rice. Cover; let stand 5 minutes.
In beater bowl, beat the whites with the sugar, salt, mustard, garlic powder and pepper until quite foamy.
Slowly begin to add 1/2 cup oil while beating on high.
You must add oil slowly or the mixture will not emulsify.
Now add 1 tablespoon of vinegar. Continue beating and adding oil on high until all of the oil is used.
Then beat in the last tablespoon vinegar.
Pour into covered jar and keep refrigerated.

i: cucumbers (enough to fill a gal. jar), slice and pack in jar
d: Mix good and pour over packed pickles in gallon jar.
No cooking, so make sure sugar is dissolved and solution is mixed well.
Sliced onion may be used, 2 or 3.
Pimentos can be added, if desired.
Store in refrigerator for at least 10 days before using.
Premix Kool-Aid according to directions on pack.
Makes 1 gallon.
Pour 1/2 Kool-Aid in punch bowl.
Add 1/2 ginger ale and 1/2 sherbet.
This will make 2 punch bowls full.
Cut potatoes in quarters lengthwise.
Mix with olive oil. Sprinkle with pepper, salt, garlic powder and cayenne pepper. Bake at 450° for 20 minutes.
Cut chicken into 1/4-inch strips, cutting across the grain. Heat oil and add chicken until they go white (about 3 minutes). Add green pepper, onion, celery, water chestnuts, broth, salt and ginger.
Cover and cook about 6 minutes.
Mix soy sauce with cornstarch.
Add to skillet with bean sprouts.
Turn heat down and simmer few minutes.
Serve over hot rice.
For Cake:
In a large bowl, beat eggs for 5 minutes at high speed.
Gradually add sugar, mixing well.
Stir in pumpkin and lemon juice.
In a separate bowl, combine flour, cinnamon, baking soda, ginger, nutmeg and salt.
Fold into the pumpkin mixture.

i: 2 lb. chili peppers, chopped (or other hot peppers)
d: Simmer ingredients until vegetables are well cooked. Then can as you would tomatoes.

i: 4 boned and skinned chicken breast pieces
d: Cut chicken into strips.
Marinate 5 to 10 minutes in mixture of oregano, pepper, lemon juice and salt.
Heat olive oil in skillet and saute minced garlic briefly.
Add chicken strips (and marinade if you want) and cook until just brown.
Serve over cooked rice with some steamed broccoli.
Cream powdered sugar, granulated sugar, oil and butter until light and fluffy.
Beat eggs and vanilla in a small bowl.
Add to mixture and blend well.
Make small round balls.
Press down on cookie sheet with a glass dipped in sugar.
Bake 10 to 15 minutes at 350°.
Place zucchini in small amount of water and cook for 6 minutes.
Add sugar, lemon juice and pineapple with juice and cook 6 more minutes.
Remove from heat and add the jello.
Stir until jello is dissolved.
Place in jars and freeze.
Butterfly shrimp; do not remove shell.
In a large skillet, melt butter, add shrimp.
Saute 3 to 4 minutes until shrimp begins to turn pink.
Add remaining ingredients; simmer over low heat 3 to 4 minutes stirring occasionally.
Cover and let stand about 5 minutes.
Spoon shrimp and sauce into a large serving bowl.

i: 1 lb. medium-sized carrots, peeled and cut in 1/4-inch rounds (2 1/4 c.)
2 Tbsp. finely chopped fresh parsley or 1 Tbsp. dried
d: Mix all ingredients in a 1-quart casserole.
Cover with lid or vented plastic wrap.
Microwave on High 6 to 8 minutes, stirring twice,
until carrots are almost tender.
Let stand 3 minutes or until
tender.
Spread rice in bottom of large casserole.
Put chicken on top of rice.
Mix soup and water and pour over chicken.
Add onions and other ingredients if desired.
Cover.
Bake at 350° about 1 to 1 1/4 hours or until chicken and rice are tender.

i: 1 (8 oz.) carton Cool Whip, partially thawed
d: Mix dry jello into Cool Whip. Stir in cottage cheese, drained pineapple and chopped pecans. Cover with lid and refrigerate at least 4 hours.
Cook beef with taco seasoning according to directions.
Shred head of lettuce into large bowl; add chopped onion and tomatoes. Add meat, when cool, and grated cheese.
Crush tortilla chips and add just before serving.
Add Catalina dressing to taste.
Cream butter and sugar together until light and fluffy.
Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
Add crushed wafers alternately with milk.
Add coconut and pecans and mix well. Pour into a 12 cup tube pan greased with nonstick spray.
Cook completely before removing from pan.
May be glazed with powdered sugar and topped with pecan halves and maraschino cherries. Freezes well.
Dip tomatoes in boiling water to remove skins.
Chop all vegetables and put in large pot or dishpan.
(I use food processor for peppers.)
Cook on medium heat for 20 to 30 minutes or until starts to thicken.
Dissolve 3 tablespoons cornstarch with 1/2 cup water.
Add to salsa; cook a few minutes longer to thicken.
Mix all together and store in airtight container.

i: 4 skinless, boneless chicken breast halves (about 3 oz. each)
d: Heat oven to 350°.
Rinse chicken with cold running water; blot dry with paper towels.
Coat nonstick baking sheet with vegetable cooking spray.
Preheat the oven to 350°.
Remove the skin and fat from the chicken and discard.
Cut the chicken into six or eight serving-size pieces, then debone the breast.
(Reserve the wings, back and bones from the breast to use for stock.)
In a small bowl, combine the next seven ingredients.
Mix well and reserve 1/2 teaspoon of the seasoning mix; sprinkle the remaining seasoning over the chicken and set aside.
Combine meat, garlic and mushrooms and saute until meat is browned and mushrooms are soft.
Add sherry (if desired), salt and pepper and continue to saute for 15 minutes, stirring frequently. Makes 4 servings.
Combine the preceding and shape into balls.
Place in roaster with 1 can tomato soup and 1 can tomato sauce well blended.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour or longer.
Mix crumbs and melted butter in a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Beat powdered sugar, vanilla and stick of butter together for 10 minutes.
Add eggs and continue to beat until it looks like pudding.
Spread over crumb layer, peel bananas and slice over cream mixture; drain all juice from pineapple and put over bananas.
Cover with Cool Whip and sprinkle nuts.
Chill for 4 hours.
Combine all in blender or food processor.
Mix at highest speed for 30 seconds.
Store in tightly covered jar.
Uses:
gumbo, jambalaya, barbecue sauce, soups, stews, chicken, fish or shrimp.

i: 4 cans (10 biscuits in each) buttermilk biscuits
d: Combine
the
brown sugar and the cinnamon.
Divide the mixture
in
half
Cut liver into 1/4-inch slices.
Melt margarine in 10-inch skillet.
Saute onion slices in margarine until tender.
(Do not brown.)
Add liver; season with salt, chili powder and pepper. Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes. Makes 4 servings.

t: Leg Of Lamb With Cucumber Sauce
i: 1 beef bouillon cube in 1 c. water
d: Preheat oven to 325°.
Cut slits in lamb and insert dill and garlic slivers.
Roast for 2 1/2 hours or 30 minutes per pound. Brush mint jelly over meat the last 10 minutes of roasting time. Remove meat from oven to rest.
Cook all ingredients, except pecans and vanilla, to soft ball stage (240°).
Wash and remove excess fat from meat, set aside.
In a pressure cooker or pot add oil and warm over medium-high heat. Add cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, and zeera.
When it sputters, add the meat, onion, ginger/garlic, cayenne, salt, and turmeric. Brown the meat.
Cover and cook for 15 minutes in pressure cooker or 1 hour in a regular pot.
The meat should not be fully done at this point.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Use 9 x 13-inch pan and 2-quart saucepan.
Mix all ingredients together.
Makes 1 1/2 quarts.

i: 1 (1 lb.) can pear slices, well drained
d: Combine all ingredients and blend well.
Cover and refrigerate several hours to blend flavors.
Serve with veal, turkey or ham.

i: 1 (4.5 oz.) can chopped green chiles, drained
d: Combine all ingredients; cover and chill at least 2 hours. Serve with vegetables, fish, chicken or tortilla chips.
Yield: 2 cups.

i: 1 to 2 Tbsp. oil or bacon grease
d: Cook rice and set aside.
Fry bacon and crumble.
Beat 3 eggs and add to them 1/8 teaspoon pepper.
In small amount of bacon grease, fry eggs (one at a time) very thinly.
Chop up fried eggs. Put 1 to 2 tablespoons oil (or the rest of bacon grease) in skillet.
Add rice, bacon, egg, green onion and soy sauce.
Bake at 350° to 375° for 1 hour.
Melt the butter in a shallow baking pan at 300°.
Remove pan from oven and increase the setting to 400°.
Mix the egg and milk in a bowl.
Shake together in a paper bag the flour, almonds, sesame seed, paprika and salt.
Dip the chicken in egg, then shake in the bag to coat with flour.
Lay chicken in the pan, and turn over to coat with melted butter.
Mix all ingredients thoroughly.
Chill.
Refrigerate 8 to 12 hours and serve.

i: 2 cans taco sauce (small) or 2 medium cans tomatoes and chilies
d: Brown meat.
Add onions, pepper, sauce, beans and Tabasco. Add water if it seems dry.
Simmer, for taste.
In a mixing bowl toss first 6 ingredients.
In a small bowl, combine all remaining ingredients, except lettuce leaves.
Pour over turkey mixture and mix gently.
Chill.
Serve on a bed of lettuce if desired.
Yield:
Drain pickles and slice; put into large glass container or crock (punch bowl can be used).
Add sugar, garlic, vinegar and pepper and mix together.
Fold the cheese cloth 3 or 4 times to make it dense enough to hold the pickling spices, celery seed and mustard seed.
Fold up the spices in the cheese cloth to make a small bundle; tie ends with cord.
Bury bundle of spices in middle of pickles.
Sprinkle turmeric over top.
Combine oil, vinegar and seasonings; mix thoroughly.
Cut up lettuce, tomatoes, cucumbers, onions and pour dressing over all. Garnish with black olives and Feta cheese.
Heat over medium heat the first 4 ingredients, stirring constantly.
Cook until mixture turns amber colored and begins to emit a light smoke.
(This usually takes between 10 or 15 minutes.)
Slice onions thin. Soak in cold water. Drain and soak in milk. Add to batter of remaining ingredients. Drop in deep fat and fry. Drain on paper towel. Serve.
Boil potato until well done.
Take peeling off and mash well. Mix powdered sugar with potato until it is thick enough to roll out (takes about 1 pound box).
Sprinkle dough board with powdered sugar.
Roll out and spread with peanut butter.
Roll up.
Wrap in wax paper and refrigerate until cool enough to slice and serve.
In a large skillet, saute ground chuck and onions until brown.
Add soup, water and soy sauce.
Bring to boil; stir in rice.
Cover to bring back to boil; remove from heat.
Let stand 5 to 7 minutes.
Pour into 2-quart bowl.

i: 1 lb. can spaghetti in tomato sauce
d: Combine meat, onion and pepper in skillet and brown; drain off grease.
Add other ingredients.
Put in a covered 1 1/2-quart casserole dish.
Bake at 350° for 40 minutes.
This is a delicious meal with bread and a salad!
So easy!

i: 1 whole jalapeno chile, stemmed and seeded
d: In a blender put half of tomato juice and jalapeno chili. Process until chili is pureed.
Fill a cocktail shaker with ice and add tomato-jalapeno mixture and remaining ingredients.
Shake well and strain into highball glasses.
Bring all ingredients together except corn and bring to a boil.
Add corn and cook 20 minutes.
Before canning, add 1/4 tablespoon turmeric and 1 tablespoon celery seed.

i: 1 1/2 lb. ground beef, browned and drained
d: Put all ingredients in a large pot and cook 1 to 2 hours until green pepper is tender.

i: 1 to 1 1/2 lb. smoked sausage, thinly sliced
d: Cook sausage in a 12-inch skillet over medium heat for 2 minutes.
Add onion; cook, stirring occasionally, until tender (about 3 minutes).
Add rice; mix well.
Stir in remaining ingredients except for green pepper.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover and simmer 20 minutes until liquid is absorbed.
Stir in green pepper; let stand 5 minutes.
Heat oil in skillet or wok and saute chicken until brown. Blend rest of ingredients and add to skillet.
Mix; cover and simmer for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Place chicken in platter and coat with sauce.

i: dry tandoori mix (available at Indian grocery stores)
d: Remove skin from chicken and place in casserole dish.
Place 1 teaspoon tandoori mix on each piece of chicken.
Pour yogurt into dish and mix chicken, spice and yogurt until there is a uniform orange color.
Place in refrigerator 2 hours up to 12 hours.
Bake in oven at 375° for 1 hour.
Grease and flour a Bundt pan.
Quarter cut biscuits and roll each quarter in 1/2 cup sugar and cinnamon mixture.
In a saucepan, melt butter.
Add 3/4 cup sugar and cinnamon.
Heat until sugar melts.
Pour over biscuits.

i: 6 small plum tomatoes, cut in wedges
d: Cut pepper in julienne strips. In hot oil, cook garlic briefly.
Add pepper, tomatoes, olives, capers and thyme.
Cook 5 minutes.
Melt butter; add scallops and cook about 3 minutes. Sprinkle lemon juice and basil over and stir briefly.
Spoon tomato mixture on heated plates with scallops over mixture.
Serves 4.

Preheat oven to 400°. Cut the potatoes in half or quarters and place in a bowl with the olive oil, salt, pepper and garlic; toss until the potatoes are well coated. Transfer the potatoes to a sheet pan and spread out into one layer. Roast in the oven for 45 minutes to 1 hour or until browned and crisp. Flip twice during cooking with a spatula in order to ensure even browning. Remove from the oven; toss with the parsley. Season to taste and serve hot.

i: lots of salt and pepper to taste
d: Finely chop the first 3 ingredients.
Add the remaining ingredients.
Better to make ahead and let marinate.
May need to drain off some of the liquid that forms.
Serve with Doritos chips.
Delicious!

i: 2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen strawberries with juice
d: Sift flour,
soda, salt and cinnamon together.	Stir in sugar. Beat eggs and add with remaining ingredients, stirring just to mix well.
Bake
in
two
greased
Prepare spaghetti as directed on the box.
Drain and cool. Add vegetables, Italian dressing and seasoning mix to taste.
Toss and refrigerate.

i: 1 (10 1/2 oz.) can cream of chicken soup
2 large firm, ripe tomatoes, sliced thinly
1 (6 to 8 oz.) pkg. corn chips or tortilla chips
2 (5 oz. each) cans boned chicken, chunked or 2 c. cooked and cubed chicken
d: In a mixing bowl, place soup, chiles, onion and water.
Stir to blend well.
Lay 1/2 corn chips in 2-quart casserole.
Top with 1 can or 1 cup chunked chicken, then half of the tomato slices. Pour half of the soup mixture over chicken mixture. Sprinkle with half of the cheese.
Repeat layers in same order, ending with cheese.
Bake, uncovered, on High in microwave or reheat 8 to 10 minutes with cover on.

i: 2 lb. lean veal, cubed (washed and patted dry)
1 c. water, stock or tomato juice
d: Measure soy sauce into large skillet and brown veal in oil with onions and garlic, about 15 minutes.
Add 1 cup water, stock or tomato juice and the sugar, pepper and rosemary or marjoram. Add salt. Cover and cook until veal is tender.
Approximately 1 hour and 15 minutes before cooked, add carrots, green peppers and mushrooms.
Just before serving, stir in lemon rind.
Add extra water, stock or tomato juice as necessary during cooking.
Makes 4 servings.

i: 1 1/4 c. chopped shallots or green onions
d: Brush steak with soy sauce and sprinkle well with salt, freshly ground black pepper and dried thyme, crumbled.
Let stand for an hour.
Brush again with soy sauce and grill over brisk fire 3 or 4 minutes on each side for rare steaks.
Carve with sharp knife in thin slices on the diagonal.
Grease a 9 x 12-inch cake pan.
Spread cherry pie mix.
Sift cake mix and spread it dry over the cherries.
Top with ground nuts.
Melt butter and pour over top.
Bake 45 minutes in a 350° oven.
Heat oil and wine together on medium heat. Add all other ingredients and mix well.
Roll out in strips about the size of your thumb. Cut in 4-inch lengths. Bake 12 to 15 minutes at 400° on an ungreased baking sheet.
Combine and mix well all ingredients.
Store in a covered container in the refrigerator.
Will keep 2 weeks.
Combine sesame seed, flour and pepper.
Dip chicken pieces into soy sauce; dredge in sesame seed mixture.
Arrange chicken, bone side down, in a large, shallow baking dish.
Melt margarine; drizzle over chicken.
Bake at 400° for 40 to 50 minutes, or until chicken is tender.
Combine sugar and water; heat and stir until sugar is dissolved.
Bring to a boil for 5 minutes without stirring. Chill.
Add juices and ginger ale; pour over ice.
A large jar of maraschino cherries could also be added.
(Makes 50 servings.)
Saute onions, bell peppers and celery.
Mix everything together in rice cooker and stir often.
"The hotter, the better."
Sausage or shrimp may be added for variety.

i: 1 yeast cake, dissolved in 1/4 c. warm water
d: Scald milk with butter.
Pour into large mixing bowl.
Add sugar and salt.
Let cool.
Add eggs (unbeaten), yeast cake and 1 1/2 cups flour.
Mix well for two minutes.
Combine the ingredients to make a pliable dough.
Divide in balls of desired size and roll between 2 pieces of plastic wrap into circles.
Cook each circle on both sides on a hot griddle.

i: 1 pkg. Uncle Ben's long grain and wild rice
4 c. chicken broth made with chicken bouillon cubes
d: Saute both rices in the 1/4 cup oleo until slightly brown. Chop and saute in 1/4 cup olive oil the onion, green onions and bell pepper.
Add sauteed greens to browned rice.
Add the chicken broth, olives, olive juice, sliced water chestnuts and mushrooms to the rice mixture.
Mix all together and bake, covered with foil, at 350° for 30 minutes or until done.
Use a 9 x 13-inch pan or a 3-quart oblong Pyrex baking dish.
Brown onion in hot butter; add potatoes, parsley and seasonings.
Barely cover with hot water; cook in covered pan until potatoes are tender.
Serves 6.
Mix eggs, sugar, syrup, salt and vanilla flavoring.
Make pie crust or use ready-made pie crusts.
Put pecans in bottom of crust.
Pour ingredients over pecans.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes to 1 hour.
Makes 2 pies.
Brown, salt and drain the ground chuck.
Put everything in a crock-pot.
Stir well.
Cook on low about 8 hours (4 hours on high if in a hurry).

t: Meadowbrook Farm Traditional New Year'S Day Dinner
i: 1 lb. fresh pork, cut into 1-inch cubes
d: Season pork cubes with salt and pepper.
Brown in fat in large heavy saucepan.
Combine water, sauerkraut, apple and onion; add to pork.
Simmer 40 minutes.
Place dumplings on top of sauerkraut.
Cover pan; steam until done.
Trim fat from chops.
Season with salt and pepper.
Broil 5 minutes on 1 side; turn and broil 4 minutes.
Mix together mustard and honey; spread over chops and broil 2 minutes longer.
Makes 4 servings.
Cook spaghetti and drain.
Cook broccoli according to package directions to tender-crisp.
Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a large, heavy skillet over moderate heat.
Stir in garlic, basil leaves, onions and parsley.
Cook 1 to 2 minutes.
Turn off heat and gently mix in with cooked broccoli.

i: 2 (16 oz.) cans Ranch Style beans or barbecue style beans
d: Brown meat and onion together.
In crock-pot, add all ingredients.
Bake at 300° for about 2 hours (low setting on crock-pot).
Can be baked in a large Corning Ware dish in oven.

i: minced parsley and/or snipped fresh chives to taste
d: Measure mustard into a bowl.
Whisk in vinegar, sugar, salt, pepper and herbs to taste.
Continue to whisk mixture while slowly dribbling in olive oil until mixture thickens.
Adjust seasoning to taste.
Cover until ready to use.
(Vinaigrette is best if made just before it is to be used.)
Dissolve yeast in warm water.
Add other ingredients, then add 1 to 1 1/2 cups whole wheat flour.
Blend with electric mixer.
Add slowly bread flour (3 1/2 to 4 cups; total wheat and bread flour should be 5 cups).
After 3 cups flour are mixed with electric mixer, fold the balance of flour with a wooden spoon.
Put on a floured board or wax paper and knead.

i: 1 large head broccoli, rinse and let dry
d: In a large bowl, cut broccoli into bite-size pieces.
Crumble bacon into broccoli and add chopped onion.
In a small bowl, mix mayonnaise, vinegar and sugar.
Stir well.
Stir into broccoli gently until broccoli and bacon are well coated.
Refrigerate until ready to serve.
Slice onions and separate into rings.
Combine water, sugar and vinegar.
Stir until sugar dissolves.
Pour over onion rings. Cover and chill at least 3 hours.
Drain.
Stir in mayo and celery seed.
In a large saucepan, melt butter and saute onion, celery and apples for 5 minutes.
Stir in curry and flour.
Gradually stir in chicken broth, cream of coconut and half and half.
Stir over moderate heat until sauce bubbles and thickens.
Stir in chicken and season to taste with salt and pepper.
Serve over rice.

Measure out ingredients. Pour cereal into bowl; set aside. Combine chips, peanut butter and margarine in microwave-safe bowl. Microwave on High 1 to 1 1/2 minutes. Stir until smooth. Stir in vanilla. Pour over cereal. Stir until well coated. Pour into a zippered bag with powdered sugar. Seal bag; shake until coated. Pour onto foil. Cool. Store in airtight container.

i: 2 pkg. Kool-Aid (choose the color and flavor you want)
d: Mix and chill the first three ingredients.
Add 7-Up just before serving.
Serves 50 to 60 punch cups.
Brown ribs.
Combine remaining ingredients in baking dish over ribs.
Cover and bake 2 1/2 hours at 350°, basting 2 or 3 times during baking.

Put crushed wafers in bowl and add cherries and nuts. Add vanilla and pour milk over mixture. Mix with hands. Roll and shape into 2 large rolls (helps to dust hands with powdered sugar before rolling). Roll each with coconut, then sugar. Refrigerate 1 hour before slicing or wrap in plastic wrap and freeze.

i: 10 c. chopped zucchini, do not peel
d: Mix zucchini, onions and salt together.
Cover with water and let stand overnight.
Drain and rinse under water.
Add the remaining ingredients and bring to a boil.
Boil 3 minutes and seal in hot, sterilized jars.
Makes 6 pints.

t: Cabbage And Rice Jambalaya In Rice Cooker
i: 1/2 lb. lean ground beef, browned and drained
d: Mix all ingredients and cook in large rice cooker.
Let stand about 15 minutes after bell rings.

i: 1 loaf crusty French or Italian bread
d: Slice bread diagonally but leave bottom crust unsliced. Soften butter.
Add garlic, herbs and cheese to butter and mix well.
Gently pull slices of bread open and spread with butter. Take care not to break bottom crust.
Spread any leftover butter on top of loaf.
Carefully place buttered
loaf on foil and wrap ends, leaving top of loaf exposed.
In iron skillet add olive oil, cabbage, salt, pepper, sugar and water. Cover and let cook for 5 minutes. Add soda and cook 15 to 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.

i: 1 (16 to 18 oz.) jar or can baked beans
1/4 c. diced or slivered red or sweet white onion
d: Combine beans, tomatoes, picante sauce, beer and brown sugar in large saucepan.
Simmer uncovered 20 minutes.
Stir in rice and salt; heat through.
Sprinkle with onion and cilantro.
Brown ground beef.
Drain fat.
On low heat, add cream cheese, salt and chili powder.
Simmer 5 minutes.
Cook pasta shells; drain.
Toss in butter.

Preheat oven to 350°. Brown hamburger. Cook noodles according to box directions. In large container, combine ingredients. Pour into 9 x 13 pan. Top with Parmesan cheese, if desired. Bake at 350° for 40 minutes.
Combine all ingredients in a large Ziploc, except chicken. Mix well.
Add chicken breasts and refrigerate for 2 to 3 hours. Turn bag frequently so that chicken is well marinated. Remove chicken from marinade and grill over hot coals until done. Discard marinade.
Cook vermicelli according to directions.
Drain well.
Set aside.
In same saucepan, melt butter.
Stir in remaining ingredients and vermicelli.
Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until heated through (3 to 5 minutes).

Mix the milk, honey, blueberries, baking powder, egg and shortening. Combine flour and salt; add to the first ingredients. Stir just to moisten. Fill greased muffin pans 1/2 full. Bake at 400° for 12 to 15 minutes. Makes 12 to 15 muffins.
Saute onion and green pepper in butter until onion is tender. Stir in curry powder, slightly beaten egg, salt, pepper and cayenne.
Mix into hot rice; sprinkle with chopped olives if available.

i: 2 pkg. (1/4 oz. each) active dry yeast
d: Combine the water and sugar in a small bowl.
Stir in the yeast. Set aside until the mixture becomes bubbly. In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour and baking soda.
Stir to mix. Cut in the shortening using a pastry blender, a fork or your fingertips until the mixture resembles cornmeal.
Add the yeast mixture and buttermilk. Stir with a large wooden spoon until well mixed.
This will not be a smooth dough.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate the dough until needed; it will keep about 1 week. Makes about 8 cups of dough.

i: 1 (No. 2) can and 1 small can crushed pineapple, drained
d: Scrape and cut carrots into small pieces or chunks.
Cook and drain; salt.
Drain pineapple.
Use 1/2 cup pineapple juice, cornstarch, oleo and sugar and cook until thick.
Add carrots and pineapple to sauce and simmer 10 minutes.
Better if made a day ahead.

i: 10 whole leeks, cleaned, chopped, blanched and drained
d: Drain peas and place in a bowl.
Whisk oils, lemon juice, vinegar and garlic together and pour over peas.
Add remaining ingredients and toss well.
Refrigerate overnight before serving. Serves 16.
Boil all ingredients, except eggs and beets in a saucepan. Add the beets and simmer for several minutes.
Cool to just warm and add shelled, boiled eggs.
When cool, keep in refrigerator.
Dice fruit and vegetables. Add other ingredients and chill before serving. Serve with tortilla chips.
Fry breaded chicken in garlic and olive oil.
After frying all chicken, saute and cover pan until chicken is fully cooked.
Put aside sliced mushrooms, red peppers and sliced onion and simmer. Take a little olive oil and garlic; simmer mushrooms, red peppers and onions until cooked.
When cooked, pour over chicken and then pour balsamic vinegar over entire food.
Simmer until hot.
Mix all ingredients together.
Bake at 350° for 40 minutes. Frost with cream cheese frosting.
Cream shortening and gradually add sugar; cream together until light and fluffy.
Add eggs.
Sift flour before measuring, then add baking powder, soda and salt.
Add the sour milk and flour mixture, alternating with the egg, sugar and butter mixture.
Beat after each addition.
Add banana pulp; beat well.

i: 1 box Stove Top stuffing or homemade stuffing
d: Preheat oven to 375°.
Cook stuffing as directed on package. Slice green pepper in small strips, then cut strips in half.
Mix water and soup in a bowl.
Cream Crisco and 1/2 cup of the sugar.
Stir in flour, salt, vanilla and nuts.
Mix well.
Make into 1-inch balls.
Place on cookie sheet, which has been rubbed with Crisco.
Bake in oven, 375°, for 12 to 15 minutes.

i: orange slices and sprigs of Italian parsley (to garnish)
d: Combine garlic salt and pepper. Rub over roast. Place roast on rack in roasting pan and roast in a 350° oven for 40 minutes per pound.
Mix well and refrigerate one hour.

i: 1 pkg. Duncan Hines fudge marble cake mix
1 (4 serving size) pkg. vanilla instant pudding mix
d: Preheat oven to 350°. Generously grease and then flour pan. Blend all ingredients except small packet in a large bowl; beat at medium speed for 2 minutes. Pour 3/4 of batter into Bundt pan. Blend contents of small packet into remaining batter. Spoon dark batter here and there over light batter. Pull knife through the batter in wide curves. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes. Cake is done when toothpick inserted in center comes out clean. Crust will be darker than regular layer cake.

i: 3 lb. beef roast, cooked or 2 cans beef, cooked and drained (two 28 oz. cans or 2 1/2 size can)
d: Slice beef roast; mix with sauce.
Heat thoroughly.
Make several hours ahead so the sauce can flavor the meat.
Reheat and serve.

i: 1 (18 oz.) bottle Kraft Original barbecue sauce
1/2 bottle hot and spicy barbecue sauce
d: Cut up onion and green pepper very fine.
Cut fresh lemons; remove seeds.
Squeeze juice; cut up rind.
Place all ingredients in boiler.
Combine all sauces and seasonings and cook slow for 25 minutes.
Makes a delicious sauce for ribs, chicken, etc.

i: oregano and garlic powder to taste
d: Dice potatoes, add rest of ingredients.
Toss lightly.
Mix ground meat, Season-All, eggs, bread crumbs, Romano cheese and 1 to 2 to sprigs of chopped parsley together.
Take part of the mixture and put into cupped palm of your hand.
Make an indentation in the meat mixture; put in 1/4 hard-boiled egg, 1 hunk Mozzarella cheese, a small piece of bacon and parsley.
Cover with some meat mixture.
Brown in a skillet with oil in it, then cook in your favorite tomato sauce.
Cream sugar and shortening.
Sift flour, baking powder and salt.
Add alternately with milk to creamed mixture.
Add oats, raisins and nuts.
Mix well.
Spread in greased 8 x 8 x 2-inch pan.

i: 1 pkg. trim brisket (8 to 10 lb., best size select meat with least amount of fat)
d: You may want to trim some fat from brisket, but leave a good amount for juice.
Soak meat in teriyaki sauce; sprinkle with Tony Chachere's seasoning, using a good amount.
Sprinkle a lot of black pepper on meat.
I do not think you can use too much.
Cover meat and put in fridge for 24 hours.
Take out and let warm up some. Re-season and marinate.
Peel and slice onions 1/4-inch thick.
Let soak in milk to cover for at least an hour.
Make a thin batter of beaten egg, milk and pancake mix.
Dip onions into batter and drop into hot oil.
They brown quickly and must be turned quickly but only once.
Drain on paper towel!

i: 2 c. grated Cheddar cheese, firmly packed
d: Mix all together. Put in small casserole or souffle dish. Bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes. Serve warm with Club crackers.
Saute ham lightly in margarine or butter about 5 minutes.
Add the onion, green pepper and garlic.
Cook 5 minutes; stir occasionally.
Stir in tomatoes, water, salt, thyme and hot sauce. Bring to a boil and stir in the rice.
Cover, lower heat and simmer about 30 minutes or until rice is done.
Makes 6 servings.

i: 1 (2 1/2 to 3 lb.) fryer, cut into seving pieces
1/2 lb. fresh mushrooms, cut into pieces
1 (6 oz.) jar marinated artichoke hearts, drained
d: Heat oil in frying pan.
Dredge chicken pieces in flour and lightly brown in oil.
Place in casserole with mushrooms and artichoke hearts.
Stir in garlic and spices (with tomatoes). Pour over chicken.
Bake at 350° for 1 to 1 1/2 hours or until tender, adding sherry during last few minutes of cooking time. Makes 4 servings.
Wash lentils under running water.
