Put them in a cooking pan with just one cup of water and bring them to boil over high heat, 5-7 minutes.
Lower heat and cook, covered for 15-20 minutes or until water is absorbed.

i: 2 baked sweet potatoes or can pumpkin
d: Beat with spoon.
Sift dry ingredients.
Bake in Bundt pan 1 hour and 5 minutes at 325° for pumpkin; 1 1/2 hours with potatoes or until done.
Bake cake according to package directions.
Crumble.
Mix remaining ingredients
and roll into small balls.
Roll in coconut.

i: 1 (16 oz.) can stewed tomatoes, cut up
d: Cook macaroni and drain.
In saucepan, melt butter; blend in flour.
Stir in undrained tomatoes, tomato sauce, onion soup mix, oregano, salt and pepper.
Cook until bubbly.
Brown ground beef; drain.
Stir in sauce.
Remove husks and silks from corn.
Place ears in boiling water to cover.
Return water to a boil; cook 5 minutes.
Drain and immediately cover ears with ice water.
Let stand until cool; drain.
Cut corn from cob, measuring 8 cups of kernels.
Beat shortening and 1 cup sugar until light and fluffy.
Add molasses and egg; beat well.
Combine flour, soda, cinnamon, cloves, ginger and salt.
Add to molasses mixture; mix well.
Stir in raisins.
Cover.

i: 2 (6 oz.) cans frozen pink lemonade concentrate
d: In punch bowl, combine lemonade, juice, strawberries and 3 cups cold water.
Refrigerate.

i: 1 (16 oz.) can Chinese vegetables without meat
d: Mix and let stand overnight in refrigerator.
Will keep for days.
Cook onions in salted water until tender.
Spread drained onions in a shallow baking dish.
Place fish on onions.
Combine remaining ingredients.
Spread over fish.
Bake 30 to 40 minutes at 350°.
Combine the flours, wheat germ, dried milk powder, sesame seed, brown sugar, baking powder, baking soda and salt.
Stir in the water well.
Butter four 1 pound shortening cans.
Pour in the batter.
Smooth tops.
Cover each tightly with foil.

i: 6 oz. grilled, skinless, boneless chicken breasts, sliced
1 kiwi fruit, pared, halved and thinly sliced
d: In a small bowl or jar with tight fitting top, combine lime juice, honey, soy sauce. Mix or shake well. Refrigerate until ready to use.

i: 4 large chicken breast halves (boneless and skinless)
2 red bell peppers, seeded and cut into 1-inch pieces
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
In a shallow plate, dredge chicken in flour.
Shake off excess.
In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil.
Quickly saute the chicken over medium-high heat, turning over once, until lightly browned.
Remove to a 2-quart covered baking dish.
Mix all together and put into greased baking dishes.
Brown beef and onion; drain.
Stir in sauce mix, tomato sauce and water.
Simmer 10 minutes.
Spoon into greased 13 x 9-inch pan.
Top with cheese slices.
Beat eggs, milk and oil until foamy.
Do
not
dilute juices.
Put partially thawed juices in bowl; stir in chilled Sprite.
Serves 25.
Heat vinegar and sugar until sugar is dissolved.
Pour over other ingredients while warm.
Cover and refrigerate.
*Add more if desired.

i: 1 c. frozen hash brown potatoes, thawed
d: Cook sausage until brown; drain.
Separate crescent dough into 8 triangles.
Place in an ungreased 12-inch pizza pan with points toward the center.
Press over bottom and up sides to form a crust; seal perforations.
Spoon sausage over crust; sprinkle with potatoes.
Top with Cheddar cheese.

i: 1/2 c. non-fat yogurt or sour cream
d: Preheat oven to 375°.
Cover bottom of shallow baking pan with oil.

Shake chicken in bag with flour and salt until coated. Place chicken in single layer in baking pan and bake 25 minutes. Combine apricot preserves, mustard and yogurt (or sour cream). Spread apricot mixture on chicken and bake for 30 minutes more or until done.

i: 1 (46 oz.) can unsweetened pineapple juice, chilled
d: Combine all ingredients in a punch bowl.
Makes 5 1/2 quarts.
Dissolve gelatin and salt in hot water.
Break up cranberry sauce with fork.
Add the gelatin mixture; chill until thickened. Fold in apples and nuts.

i: 1 1/2 lb. coarsely ground lamb (not too lean)
pepper, cinnamon and allspice to taste
d: Core cabbage and submerge into salted boiling water.
While boiling, loosen each cabbage leaf with a fork and when slightly tender, remove.
If the leaves are too large, trim heavy vein and cut in halves or thirds.
Brown meat and onions.
Add sauce and olives.
Line baking pan (9 x 13-inch) with tortillas.
Pour in half of meat mixture carefully.
Add half of cheese.
Repeat layers.

i: 1 (3 3/4 oz.) pkg. Jell-O vanilla instant pudding and pie filling
d: Preheat oven to 325°.
Grease and flour 10-inch tube or 12-cup Bundt pan.
Sprinkle nuts over bottom of pan.
Mix all cake ingredients together.
Pour batter over nuts.
Bake 1 hour.

i: 3 lb. chicken parts, skinned and boned (if desired)
d: Pierce sausages; simmer in water to cover for 15 minutes. Drain.
Brown sausages in Pam sprayed pot.
Remove and brown chicken pieces in pot.
Spray again if needed.
Remove meat and cook onions until tender, stirring frequently.
Do not brown onions.
Melt oleo and combine with other ingredients.
Roll into small balls and place on wax paper.
Then melt chocolate chips and paraffin wax.
Then dip balls into chocolate mixture, place on wax paper and cool.
Makes 120 pieces.
Heat applesauce and mix with dry Jell-O.
Measure juice of one orange.
Add enough 7-Up to make one cup.
Mix with Jell-O and applesauce.
Add grated orange rind.
Pour into mold and chill until firm.
Combine corn, 1 cup of water, salt and pepper in stove top casserole. Cook for 20 minutes.
Add sugar, margarine and milk. Cook for 10 minutes, stirring constantly.
Arrange cheese on top. Heat until cheese melts.
Yields 6 servings.
Peel pears and put in blender and grind pears, onions and peppers. Put in saucepan and add vinegar, turmeric, sugar and salt. Bring to boil. Simmer 20 minutes and put in jars and seal.
Prepare macaroni and cheese as directed; add green pepper, onion and tuna.
Mix well and let set for 5 or 10 minutes with the lid on.
Cut ribs into serving size (3 to 4 per person).
Place in single layer, meaty side up, in a shallow baking pan.
Add 1 slice of lemon and 1 slice onion on each piece.
Bake at 450° for 30 minutes.

i: 1 (12 oz.) can whole kernel corn, drained
d: Brown sausage in skillet; pour off fat.
Add meat and onion; cook and stir a few minutes.
Add seasonings, corn, tomato sauce and milk.
Simmer 20 minutes.
Stir corn meal into meat mixture. Add olives.
Pour into casserole.

i: fresh ground black pepper to taste
d: Slice tomatoes in half. Place in shallow baking pan, flat side up. Combine seasonings and cheese; sprinkle over tomatoes. Bake at 350° for about 30 minutes or until tender. Makes 4 servings.
Mix together and cover dish with plastic overnight.
Bake the next morning at 375° for 45 minutes.
Cut bread in shape of a triangle.
Place 1 slice of Mozzarella between 2 slices of bread.
Cover them with flour, then dip in egg, then in bread crumbs.
Meanwhile get oil ready and hot so you can fry the bread.
Mix Cool Whip, softened cream cheese and Eagle Brand milk. Beat for 1 minute.
Pour in pie crusts.
Melt margarine and mix with coconut and almonds.
Place on cookie sheet and cook for 10 minutes at 350°.
Watch closely and stir often.
(I like mine toasty.)
Fry bacon until golden brown and crisp.
Then add the can of beans and wienies.
Cook until piping hot.
Mix and serve at once.
Put your cucumbers and onions in a big jar or crock.
Mix in 2 tablespoons salt and let sit overnight in refrigerator.
Drain in the morning.

i: 1/2 c. buttermilk (add 2 tsp. lemon juice to milk to equal 1/2 c.)
d: Combine sugar, margarine and eggs.
Mix well.
Stir in bananas, buttermilk and vanilla.
Stir in remaining dry ingredients.
Bake at 350° for 20 to 25 minutes.
Saute curry in butter, stir in chicken broth and bring to boil.
Add rice and green pepper, cover and simmer 20 minutes. Remove from heat, add onion and lemon juice; chill.
Stir in mayonnaise, milk, peas and almonds.
Preheat oven to 350°. Grease 2 loaf pans.

i: 4 large, ripe tomatoes, diced or 1/4 c. tomato paste
d: Place onion and oil in saucepan.
Cook until onion is golden brown.
Add drained chickpeas and water.
Cook until peas are tender (approximately 30 minutes).
In 2 tablespoons of butter, saute onions, then remove.
Add remaining butter and brown the fish fillets.
Spread onions over fish and season with salt and pepper.
Combine 1/4 cup water with remaining ingredients.
Pour over fish and simmer about 20 minutes. Serves 4.
Cook ground beef and onions until done.
Put small can of beans and tomatoes in.
Add salt and pepper to taste.

i: 1 to 2 lb. medium or large shrimp, peeled and deveined
d: Note:
Will also need 6 to 7 skewers.

i: 1 (15 oz.) can pork and beans, not drained
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
Brown bacon in skillet.
Drain, crumble and set aside.
Add onion and garlic to bacon drippings in pan; saute until onion is soft.
Combine all ingredients in a casserole dish and bake for 1 1/2 hours.

i: 2 c. mashed sweet potatoes (fresh is best)
d: Combine potatoes, sugar, butter, vanilla, allspice and evaporated milk.
Pour into buttered pan.
Mix coconut, pecans and brown sugar in blender or processor.
Pour on top of souffle. Decorate with marshmallows.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

i: 2 lb. chicken breast, skinned and boned
d: Mix and pour over chicken breasts.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

i: 3 Tbsp. sliced green onion with tops
d: Peel strip around center of each potato.
Cook potatoes in boiling water.
Drain.
Halve any large potatoes.
In bowl, combine cooked potatoes, lettuce, curly endive, eggs, radishes and green onion.
Mix all ingredients, except oil and catsup.
Put small amount of filling inside each eggroll wrapper.
Roll up filled wrapper, but before you get to the end, brush wrapper end with beaten egg so that wrapper will stick together.
Deep fry until golden brown. Serve with catsup for dipping.
Makes approximately 30 rolls.
Scramble eggs in a bowl.
Add ham and cheese.
Mix.
Add a dash of wine and spice to taste.
Pour in a heated skillet.
Flip when lightly golden brown.
Cook onion and garlic in butter for 5 minutes.
Add squash. Salt and pepper, if desired.
Drain fruit and pineapple to make 1 c. of juice.
Toss fruit cocktail, pineapple, bananas and oranges; set aside.
Mix sugar, flour and small amount of juice.
Add egg and beat.
Add raisins and rest of juice.
Cook until raisins puff up and mixture is thick.

i: 2-3 Tbsp. chopped canned green chili peppers
d: Combine, cover, refrigerate over night or at least 3 hours.
1st bowl - Combine water and egg white and beat until frothy but not stiff. 2nd bowl- Mix together sugar, salt, and cinnamon; set aside.
Preheat oven to 300 degrees.
Pour pecans into egg white and stir with a large serving spoon until the nuts are evenly coated.
Add sugar mixture.
Spread on large cookie sheet that has been sprayed with Pam and bake at 300 degrees for 30 minutes, stirring every 15 minutes-including after the last 15 minutes. Pour onto waxed paper until cooled.
Store in an airtight container.
Wash the okra and eggplant.
Cut into small pieces and boil until tender.
Place whole garlic pod in foil.
Pour over this the oil. Roast in a 350° oven for about 30 to 45 minutes.
Keep in refrigerator until ready to use and take off as many cloves as needed.
This will squeeze out like a paste.
May be used with mashed potatoes.
Rub over roast beef before cooking or any other place that you need garlic or mix with oleo for garlic butter spread.
Combine butter or margarine and garlic powder.
Brush over warm biscuits before removing from cookie sheet.
Serve warm. Makes 10 to 12 biscuits.

i: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. shredded sharp Cheddar cheese
d: Combine beans, picante sauce and 1 cup cheese; stir over low heat until cheese is melted.
On serving platter, layer chips, bean mixture, lettuce, sour cream and tomatoes.
Sprinkle with remaining cheese.
Makes 8 servings.

i: 1 1/3 c. honey (you can substitute some molasses for honey)
1/4 c. each: chopped citron, candied orange and lemon peel
d: Heat honey and sugar slightly in a pan.
Add butter.
Add 2 cups flour and baking powder; beat well.
Add nuts, citron, peels and spices.
Add 1 1/2 to 2 more cups flour until you have a thick, pliable dough.
Pat it 1/4 inch thick into pans (three 8 x 8-inch cake pans and one 9 x 9-inch cake pan).

t: Refrigerator Bran Muffins From Cypress Inn
d: Preheat oven to 375° to 400°.
Mix dry ingredients in a large bowl.
Stir in remaining ingredients and mix well.
Fill muffin tins 2/3 full.
Bake for 15 minutes.
Melt butter in a 9 x 13-inch baking dish. Beat eggs. Add all other ingredients to the beaten eggs.
Pour excess butter into mixture and mix well.
Pour into buttered baking dish. Bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes.

t: Five Minute Chili (Meatless)(Quick And Tasty With Very Little Fat.)
i: 1 (15 oz.) can black beans, rinsed and drained
1 (11 oz.) can Mexican-style corn or regular corn
d: Put all together and heat 4 or 5 minutes.
Serve with corn bread.

i: juice and grated rind of 1 lemon
d: Combine all.
Marinate in refrigerator 2 hours or overnight. Garnish with watercress.

i: 2 mild jalapeno peppers or to taste (can use canned peppers)
d: In large saucepan, saute onion in margarine until slightly browned.
Blend in flour and milk; stir.
Add hominy, cheese and peppers.
Cook over low heat until cheese has melted and is thick. Serve with Mexican food or great at a fish fry.
Serves 6 to 8.
Preheat oven to 325°.
Combine meats, herbs and spices, egg and breadcrumbs in a large bowl.
Mix well with hands.
Form into 6 patties.
Roll each patty in cornflake crumbs, coating well. Fry in oil until golden brown.
Remove to a shallow baking pan and bake 20 minutes or until cooked through.
Mix ground beef lightly with bread crumbs, egg, milk, onion, salt and pepper in a bowl.
Pack mixture lightly in a 2-quart loaf dish.
Cover with waxed paper.
Cook in microwave 5 minutes at Medium-high.
Top with catsup.
Rotate dish 1/2 turn.

i: 1 medium yellow onion, peeled and thinly sliced
salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
d: Trim off the stems and remove any limp leaves from Brussels sprouts.
Blanch the sprouts in boiling water to cover for 5 minutes.
Drain and rinse under cold water to stop the cooking. Heat a large frying pan and add the olive oil, garlic and onion. Saute a few minutes until the onion just becomes tender.
Add the blanched drained Brussels sprouts.
Saute a few minutes until the Brussels sprouts are cooked to your liking.
Add the vinegar and toss so that all the sprouts are coated with vinegar.

i: 1 (20 oz.) can each peaches, Bing cherries, pineapple chunks
2 (10 oz.) mandarin oranges or prunes (if preferred)
juice of 1 lemon and 1/4 c. margarine
d: Drain fruits well in colander until all juice has run off (1 to 2 hours).
Prepare shallow casserole (8 x 12-inch) by greasing with margarine.
Add fruit.
Cover with mixture of brown sugar and curry powder.
Sprinkle with lemon juice and dot with margarine.
Cover and bake 1 hour at 300°.

i: herbs: seasonings, tomatoes, etc. as needed
d: Note:
Use whatever in whatever amounts in order to delight your palate!
Blend cornmeal and milk together, then mix in tomatoes, corn and eggs.
Saute in olive oil the chopped onions, garlic cloves, bell pepper, chili powder, pitted olives and salt and pepper to taste.
Add boned chicken, vinegar and sugar.
Bake 1/2 hour at 350°.
Serves 15.
Cook beef, onion and garlic until light brown and tender. Stir in tomato sauce, catsup, oregano and sugar.
Cover; simmer 10 minutes.
Spoon off fat.
Remove from heat and add cheeses.
Stir until melted and well mixed.
Saute onions and peppers in 2 tablespoons butter; add kielbasa cut in 1/4-inch slices and the drained sauerkraut.
Heat.
Mix in sour cream and mustard.
Heat through, but do not boil.
Serve in pita pockets.

i: 3 lb. cabbage (2 1/2 lb. green, 1/2 lb. purple)
d: Shred cabbage.
Chop carrots, celery, onion and pepper. Combine cabbage with chopped ingredients.
Mix with sugar.
Mix all ingredients together.
Place meat in a Ziploc bag and marinate overnight, turning over to be sure both sides have been done. This is not only excellent for beef but also turkey London broil.
Brown 1 clove of garlic in a tablespoon of oil.
Remove from flame and cool slightly.
Add 1 large can of tomato sauce or puree,
one can tomato paste and 4 cans water.
Add 1 teaspoon salt, 2 teaspoons granulated sugar, garlic powder (2 shakes), sprinkle of black pepper and 1 or 2 basil leaves.
Bring to a boil; lower flame and simmer for 2 hours.
Cook the pears and sugar until real thick, then add coconut and pineapple about 1/2 hour before you take it up.
Process in jars for long period of keeping.
Saute onions and garlic in butter until onions are tender. Add meat and brown. Drain well. Add the rest of the ingredients and simmer at least one hour.
Cream together sugar and margarine.
Beat eggs and vanilla. In a bowl (on the side) sift flour, baking powder and soda.
Add eggs to creamed sugar.
Stir in flour and then fold in other ingredients.
Use 1 tablespoon scoop for each Buffalo Chip on cookie sheet.
Bake at 350° for 10 to 15 minutes.

i: 6 c. sweet peppers, diced (red and green for color)
d: To prepare corn, remove husks and silk.
Place in boiling water.
Simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove; plunge into cold water. Drain; cut corn from cob.
Do not scrape the cobs.
Combine all ingredients, except mustard and flour.
Mix all ingredients and bring to a boil.
Tear spinach into bite size pieces.
Heat wok or large skillet until hot.
Add vegetable oil; rotate to coat side.
Add garlic; stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Stir in chicken broth, salt and pepper. Cover and cook 1 minute.
Yields 2 servings.
In a large skillet, heat oil over medium-high heat. Add zucchini slices and cook turning often until lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Transfer zucchini slices to paper towels to drain briefly.
Arrange overlapping slices in a microwave-safe (9-inch) square baking dish.
Beat first 5 ingredients and spread in pie plate.
Spread pizza sauce on top and add rest in layers.
Bake for 10 minutes at 350°, then add cheese.
Bake 5 minutes more.
Serve with tortilla chips (I added bread sticks).
Pound chicken to 1/2-inch thickness.
Place flour in plastic bag.
Beat eggs in shallow bowl or pie plate.
Combine cornmeal, cumin, salt and cayenne pepper in another pie plate.
Place chicken, 2 pieces at a time, in bag with flour and shake to coat. Remove chicken and dip in egg.
Roll in cornmeal mixture, pressing to coat.
In a small pan, cook 2 tablespoons sugar, cornstarch and water until clear and thick; set aside and cool.
Beat the egg whites until stiff.
Combine the salt, vanilla and 6 tablespoons sugar; add gradually to egg whites.
Add cornstarch mixture; mix until thick and stands in a peak.
Be sure to cover dessert completely so that meringue mixture touches and seals edge of crust.
Cook at 250° until slightly brown.
Mix all ingredients together and stir by hand.
Pour into unbaked pie shell.
Bake in preheated 325° oven until glossy and set (about 25 to 30 minutes).
Do not overbake.
Serve warm with sliced bananas and whipped cream.
Serves 8.

i: 1 lb. hot sausage, cooked, drained and crumbled
d: Combine all ingredients, except biscuits.
Separate each biscuit into 3 pizza rounds.
Spread with mixture.
Put pizza on cookie sheet and store in plastic bags.
Bake frozen pizzas at 425° for 10 minutes.
Great for a cocktail party.
You may cut this recipe in half, it makes quite a bit.
Also, you can omit the Kahlua!
Mix together Kool-Aid, sugar and water.
Stir until sugar dissolves.
Add pineapple juice. Chill.
When ready to serve, add ginger ale.
Pour in punch bowl over an ice ring.
Melt chocolate chips on stove at low heat until completely melted.
Add peanuts and Chinese noodles.
Mix well.
Drop by tablespoon into cupcake wrappers.
Sprinkle with powdered sugar. Let chill in refrigerator until you're ready to serve.
Heat oven to 350°.
Grease and flour loaf pan (9 x 5 x 3-inch).
Measure all ingredients into large mixing bowl; beat on medium speed 1/2 minute.
Pour into pan.
Bake 55 to 65 minutes or until wooden pick inserted in center comes out clean.
Cool before slicing.
Heat oil and fry onion until golden brown.
Add ginger and garlic, fry for a few more minutes.
Add ground turkey, tomatoes and all the spices; fry for a few more minutes.
Add 2 to 3 cups water and cook until turkey is tender and gravy is thick.

i: 11 oz. can mandarin oranges, with juice
d: Wash and dry chicken pieces.
Shake in a paper bag with flour to coat.
In a skillet, heat oil and margarine.
Add chicken and brown each piece.
Drain mandarin oranges and set aside.
Mix juice from the can with the lemon juice, orange juice, honey, soy sauce and ginger.
Cook bacon, remove from pan and crumble.
Cook onions in bacon fat until tender, then add red tomatoes, rice, broth, catsup, seasonings and crumbled bacon.
Cook on low heat until rice is done.
Stir several times during cooking.

i: 2 (10 oz.) cans frozen strawberry daiquiri mix
d: Combine daiquiri mix and orange juice in blender.
Add enough ice to bring mixture up to 5 cups.
Blend until slushy.

i: 4 to 6 skinless, boneless chicken breasts or thighs
d: Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
Dip each piece of chicken in a mixture of yogurt, ginger and garlic powder.
Then coat lightly with bread crumbs.
Place in a single layer in a non-stick baking pan.
Bate at 350 degrees for 35 to 45 minutes or until chicken is tender and coating is crisp.

i: 2 c. cubed, cooked chicken or turkey
d: Combine chicken and celery in a bowl.
Mix salad dressing, salt, poultry seasoning, onion salt and pepper and pour over the chicken and celery.
Toss.
Cover and chill.
Serves 3 to 4 and may easily be increased to serve more.

i: 2 Tbsp. plus 2 tsp. Oriental sesame oil
3 Tbsp. chopped green onions or 2 Tbsp. minced shallots
d: The fresh basil really gives great flavor to this old favorite!!

i: 1 lb. ground beef, browned and drained
d: Cut tortillas into 1-inch pieces.
Combine with meat, soup, salsa, milk and 1/2 cup cheese.
Pour into 9 x 12 pan.
Cover and bake 45 minutes at 400°.
Uncover; sprinkle with remaining 1/2 cup cheese.
Return to oven until cheese melts; serve.

i: mushrooms, celery, bell pepper or whatever you like (optional)
d: Preheat
oven to 375° or 400°.
Brown pork chops.
In a 2 quart casserole pan, place chops.
Put onion slices on pork chops. Pour
in

i: 1 (1 oz.) sugar-free instant butterscotch pudding mix
1 1/3 c. nonfat dry milk powder, divided
1 (1 oz.) sugar-free instant chocolate pudding mix
d: In medium bowl combine butterscotch pudding mix, 2/3 cup nonfat dry milk powder and 1 1/4 cups water.
Mix well using a wire whisk. Pour into pie crust.
In medium bowl combine chocolate pudding mix, remaining 2/3 cup nonfat dry milk and remaining 1 1/4 cups water. Mix well using wire whisk.
Pour over butterscotch layer.
Cover and chill in refrigerator until set, about one hour.

i: 2 or 3 pt. raspberry sherbet (can use lime)
d: Mix Kool-Aid, sugar, water and pineapple juice.
Add sherbet and lastly pour in ginger ale.
Will serve about 50.
Mix first three ingredients together first (so mustard doesn't clump).
Mix rest.
Pour over chuck steaks or Spencer steaks. Marinate 2 to 3 hours.
Barbecue or broil.

i: 3 ripe bananas (mashed or blend in blender with a little water)
d: Mix all of the ingredients and divide into 4 separate containers and freeze.
When serving, add 1 quart of 7-up to each container.
Rinse chicken and pat dry.
Combine honey, salt, butter, mustard and curry powder.
Mix well.
Dip chicken pieces in sauce and place chicken skin side up in a roasting pan.
Bake 1 hour at 375 degrees.
Baste occasionally with remaining sauce.

i: 1 c. cooked tomatoes and tomato sauce
1/4 c. washed, uncooked rice or 1 c. uncooked noodles
d: Heat oven to 350°.
Saute onions and green pepper in hot fat until yellow.
Add ground beef and fry until mixture falls apart. Stir in remaining ingredients.
Pour into greased 1 1/2-quart baking dish.
Cover and bake 1 hour.
Remove cover last 15 minutes.

Remove husks and silks from corn. Cut corn from cob, scraping cob to remove pulp; set aside. Cook bacon in a large skillet until crisp; remove bacon, reserving drippings. Crumble bacon and set aside. Saute corn in bacon drippings until golden brown. Add water to cover, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil; reduce heat, cover and simmer 30 to 45 minutes. Stir in crumbled bacon.

t: Rice Salad(Great Switch From Potato Salad)
d: Cook rice according to directions.
Chill.
Add mayo or salad dressing, celery, onion, mustard and salt; mix well.
Chill.
Stir in eggs, radishes and cucumber before serving.

i: 3 medium (1 lb.) cooking apples, peeled, quartered, cored and cut into 1/2-inch slices
2 Tbsp. frozen orange juice concentrate, thawed
d: Arrange apples in circles in greased 9-inch pie plate.
Stir together honey, orange concentrate and cardamom.
Pour over apples and dot with butter.
Bake in preheated 375° oven, basting once with pan juices, 25 to 30 minutes or until tender.
Serve warm as side dish with lamb or pork or as dessert.
Makes 4 servings.
Combine cornmeal, sugar, salt and cinnamon.
Scald milk and pour over mixture.
Stir well, then add molasses and raisins.
Pour into buttered casserole.
Stir twice during early baking.
Set oven at 375°.
Saute chopped onion in bacon drippings until golden brown. Shred cabbage and add to mixture.
Cook until tender.
Cook noodles then blanche and drain.
Combine with cabbage.
Add desired amount of cottage cheese and serve.
Mix sugar, syrup, water and peanuts in 2-quart saucepan.
Boil at 350° for 10 to 12 minutes until golden brown.
Stir in wax; stir in baking soda.
Pour out on well-greased cookie sheet and spread thin.
Break into pieces when cool.
Drain fruit and put in large mixing bowl.
Add pistachio pudding mix.
Gradually add Cool Whip.
Add flaked coconut and marshmallows.
Mix well.
Put in large dish and chill.

i: 8 oz. wide noodles, cooked and drained
2 medium tomatoes, diced (may use 1 can diced tomatoes)
d: In a 9 x 13-inch baking dish, layer buttered noodles, sauerkraut, corned beef (broken in pieces) and tomatoes.
Dot with Thousand Island dressing; sprinkle with Swiss cheese.
Bake, covered, at 350° for 40 minutes.
Uncover; sprinkle crushed rye crackers on top.
Bake 10 minutes more until bubbly.
Serves 6 to 8.

Combine first 6 ingredients, stirring until mixture is thoroughly blended. Shape mixture into 6 loaves. Place loaves on rack of a greased broiler pan. Spoon barbecue sauce over loaves. Makes 6 loaves.
Wipe roast with paper towels.
Place oil and butter in 5-quart Dutch oven or heavy kettle.
Over medium heat, brown roast with sliced onion, turning roast with 2 wooden spoons until brown on all sides.
Add seasonings to drippings and saute, stirring, 1/2 minute.
Add beef broth; bring to a boil.
Reduce to simmer and cook just below the boiling point, covered, 2 1/2 hours.
Dissolve jello in boiling water.
Add vinegar, salt and cayenne pepper.
Chill.
When slightly thickened, stir in cabbage, apples and olives.
Chill until firm.
Serve on lettuce with mayonnaise.
Combine margarine, vegetables and curry powder in a skillet. Cook over low heat for 8 to 10 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
Stir in sour cream and pepper.
Heat thoroughly, stirring often; do not allow to boil.
Yields 6 servings.
In a large crock-pot, add the ingredients.
Cook on low 2 hours before serving.
Serve with chips.
In a large bowl combine flour, salt and baking powder.
Mix well.
Stir in water, about 1/4 cup at a time, until mixture forms a stiff dough.
Knead about 5 minutes, until dough is thick and elastic.
Pinch off about 2 tablespoons dough and form very thin round patties, using a rolling pin or hands.
Fry in hot oil (365°), turning once, until brown on each side.
Mix 1 cup of mayonnaise and 1 cup of Zesty Italian dressing. Pour over all the ingredients and mix well.

i: mayonnaise, sour cream or Ranch dressing
d: Combine first 7 ingredients.
Add enough mayonnaise, sour cream or Ranch dressing to moisten.
Combine all ingredients except ham, stirring until well blended.
Spread 1 Tbsp. mixture on each slice of ham.
Roll up jelly roll fashion, starting at short end; wrap in plastic wrap (may be frozen up to 1 month if needed).
Put in refrigerator overnight.
Cut each roll into 3/4 inch slices.
Makes approximately 5 dozen.

i: 1 lb. fish fillets, fresh or frozen, thawed
d: In large skillet, cook onion in margarine until tender.
Add fish, lemon juice, oregano, zucchini and mushrooms; cover and simmer 10 minutes.
Top with tomato and cheese; cover and simmer 2 minutes or until cheese melts.
Serve immediately.
Serves 4.

i: 1/4 c. dried green onion flakes (or minced onion)
2 Tbsp. plus 2 tsp. chicken-flavored bouillon granules
d: Combine all ingredients; stir well.
Store in an airtight container.
Yields 7 cups mix.
To cook, see the next recipe.
Mix all ingredients and chill before serving with raw vegetables.
Combine all ingredients (except nuts) and chill for several hours.
Form into balls and roll in nuts.
Serve with crackers.
Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.

i: 1 brisket roast (4 to 6 lb. when trimmed)
d: Salt and pepper roast.
Using a couple layers of heavy-duty aluminum foil, securely wrap the roast, pouring barbecue sauce over roast before sealing the foil.
Bake at low heat (250° to 300°) for 3 to 4 hours.
Mix Jell-O and water regular way.
Add nuts, celery, cranberries, apple and sour cream.
Chill until ready.
Mix first 5 ingredients.
Add mayonnaise to moisten.
Form small balls and roll in chopped nuts.
Chill overnight.
Makes 50.
Beat the butter in large bowl until light.
Slowly add sugar. Beat in molasses, ginger, cinnamon, cloves, baking soda and salt. Add flour, 1/4 cup at a time.
Mix thoroughly.
Chill 4 hours. Heat oven to 325°.
Divide cookie dough in half.
Shape each half into 1 1/2-inch thick rolls.

i: 3 Tbsp. cold butter or stick margarine
d: Preheat oven to 450°.
In a bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, baking soda and salt; cut in butter until mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Stir in enough buttermilk just to moisten dough. Turn onto a lightly floured surface; knead 3 to 4 times.
Pat or roll to 3/4 inch thickness.
Cut with a floured 2 1/2 inch biscuit cutter.
Place on a baking sheet coated with nonstick cooking spray.

t: Jack'S Good Tastin' Pinto Beans And Ground Meat
i: 2 c. pinto beans, picked, washed and soaked overnight
d: Drain water from beans that soaked overnight.
In a large saucepan, combine water, salt pork, salt and pepper.
Bring to a boil.
Add beans, tomatoes, onions, bell peppers, garlic and celery.
Cook on medium heat, adding water as needed, until beans are tender or until placing a cooked bean in a spoon and checking tenderness by smashing in the spoon.
Beans are done when they smash with little pressure.

i: 1 large ripe avocado, peeled and sliced
d: Place buttermilk, chicken stock, sliced avocado, lemon juice, salsa and cilantro in a blender.
Puree until smooth.
Season with salt and pepper.
Cover and refrigerate 1 to 4 hours.
Stir diced avocado into soup.
Ladle into chilled bowls.

t: Norwegian Gluige(Holiday After-Dinner Drink Served In Cordial Glasses)
d: Combine wine and spices in large soup pot.
Heat on stove. Light wine with match.
Sprinkle sugar over flames.
Cover to kill flames.
Add rye.
Cool and bottle.
In medium saucepan, combine the corned beef, tomatoes, diced onion and baby limas.
Cook over low heat for 30 minutes.
Serve with creamed potatoes, salad and rolls for a quick and easy meal the kids will love.

i: 1 (16 oz.) can artichokes, drained and chopped
d: Mix well and bake in preheated oven at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.
Stir all ingredients together.
Pour over steaks.
Marinate at least 2 hours.
Grill or broil steaks as usual.
Boil codfish and rinse to take out extra salt.
Slice onion and the peppers in rings.
Cook beans.
Slice the eggs in rings.
Take a platter; place the boiled potatoes, cut in rings or rounds on bottom.
Add flaked bacolao (codfish), add French-cut green beans. Add the colored peppers and onions.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Beat eggs, sugars, dressing and vanilla at medium speed with electric mixer; add flour and baking soda. Set aside 1 cup of batter.
Spread remaining batter into lightly greased 13 x 9-inch pan.
Bake 12 to 15 minutes or until lightly browned.
Cool 10 minutes.
In a large bowl, mix together sausage, ground beef, eggs, onion, garlic, bread crumbs, parsley, wine, basil, thyme, oregano and salt.
Put mixture on a piece of parchment paper, pressing out to about 3/4-inch thick, forming a rectangle.
Layer on basil leaves, sundried tomatoes and cheese. Roll meat loaf, jelly roll fashion.
In large pot, heat oil & saute ham, onion & garlic until tender.
Add water, sauce, beans, parsley and pepper and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer, covered, 10 minutes to blend flavors.
Add pasta.
Serve with grated Romano or Parmesan cheese.
Mix together and freeze.
When ready to use, let thaw until mushy.
Add ginger ale.
Drain the first three ingredients.
Mix all together and heat.
Combine bulgur and stock.
Let soak approximately 45 minutes until liquid is absorbed.
Blend all together in blender until dip consistency.
Serve with pita bread or crackers.
Garnish with mint.
Saute mushrooms in hot oil.
Remove and set aside.
Flour meat lightly; brown in hot oil.
Add mushrooms, salt, pepper and wine. Cover and simmer 20 minutes.
Serves six.
Mix ingredients and press 2/3 mixture in bottom of square baking dish.
(A bowl that has the same diameter top and bottom may be used, 1 1/2 to 2-quart size.)
Cook ziti with salt.
Cut ham into little squares.
Drain ziti.
Grease a casserole dish.
Then put one layer ziti and one layer of ham into casserole dish.

t: Bagna Cauda(Garlic, Oil And Anchovy Sauce)
i: 8 Tbsp. butter, cut into small sq.
1 Tbsp. finely chopped anchovies or anchovy paste
d: Heat the oil in a saucepan and add the garlic.
Cook briefly, stirring, without browning.
Add the wine and cook until reduced by half.
Add the butter, a little at a time, beating rapidly with a wire whisk.
Stir in the anchovy, season with pepper and serve hot. Yield:
about 3/4 cup.
Cut chicken into chunks or slices.
In a large skillet, melt butter.
Saute chicken until golden brown; set aside.
Combine consomme, curry powder, onion, salt and pepper.
Pour over chicken. Cover and simmer for 45 minutes.
Drain oranges, reserving syrup. Combine syrup, lemon juice and cornstarch.
Saute onion in 2 tablespoons shortening.
Add carrots, water and bouillon cubes.
Simmer 5 minutes.
Combine next 6 ingredients.
Add to vegetable mixture and cook until clear.
Add pepper, pineapple and chicken.
Brown crumbled meat and drain.
Add salt, garlic, mushrooms and green chili.
Cook over high heat for 3 to 5 minutes until liquid has evaporated.
Brush 4 large tortillas with oil and place on cookie sheet.
Spread with meat mixture.
Mix together oleo, sugar, cocoa and eggs.
Add flour and pecans.
Bake at 350° for 20 to 30 minutes.
Remove from oven and immediately cover with miniature marshmallows.
Let cool.

i: 4 or 5 green and red bell peppers
d: Cut cabbages, onions, tomatoes and green and red bell peppers finely.
Add salt and mix.
Let set for 30 minutes or longer. Squeeze dry with hands or in a bag.
Add remaining ingredients. Keep in refrigerator.
Keeps several weeks.

i: 1 c. pepperoncini, cut in 1/2-inch pieces with stem removed
1 c. pitted sliced black olives, 1/4-inch wide
1 bottle of your favorite Caesar dressing
d: A large platter works better than a bowl.
This way you can display each ingredient and the presentation is excellent.
Remove core of lettuce and break in 3-inch pieces and place on bottom of platter.
Mix ingredients well. Let set overnight all but the vinegar. Pour vinegar over mixture the next day and cook until brown. Do not add water.
Fry ground chuck and drain.

Saute onions, bell pepper and celery. Add to ground chuck. Add picante sauce; bring to a boil. Add all remaining ingredients in large bean pot and cook low for 30 minutes. Serve hot and with cornbread or crackers.
Wash and cut small cucumbers into eighths.
Pour boiling water over them and leave overnight.
Next morning, drain and pack in jars.
Mix all ingredients, except Bisquick.
Add Bisquick and place on a 9 x 13-inch greased pan.
Bake at 350° until golden brown (25 to 40 minutes).
Cut into 1 x 2-inch squares for serving.

i: 1/2 c. plus 1 Tbsp. Aunt Jemima corn meal
1 medium green pepper, cut in rings
2 c. (8 oz.) shredded Monterey Jack cheese
d: Heat oven to 450°.
Grease 14-inch round pizza pan or 15 x 10-inch jelly roll pan.
Sprinkle 1 tablespoon corn meal evenly into pan.
Combine remaining 1/2 cup of meal, flour, baking powder and salt.
Add milk and oil and stir with fork until mixture forms a ball.
Press into the pan and shape edge to form a rim.
Combine bread and milk in a small bowl.
Beat egg in large bowl; add oregano, salt, pepper and garlic.
Add beef and cheese; mix well.
Bake at 350° for one hour.
Mix together sugar, shortening and molasses.
Add spices, then boiling water.
Stir together.
Sift, then measure flour and sift with soda and salt into the mixture.
Beat well, then add last the 2 well beaten eggs and beat thoroughly.
Pour into a long cake pan that has been greased on the bottom and lightly floured before pouring the batter into the pan.
Rinse off broccoli rabe; drain well.
Steam broccoli rabe in a covered pot or microwave on High for about 5 to 8 minutes.
Cover the bottom of a deep sided frypan or Dutch oven with about 1/8 inch of olive oil.
Cut garlic cloves into slivers.
When oil is warm, add garlic; saute until transparent.
Carefully add broccoli rabe (it may splatter when first put in pan).
Line pie plate with vanilla wafers. Mix together Eagle Brand milk, juice from artificial lemon and Cool Whip. Put in pie plate and let set overnight.

i: 1 or 2 large onions peeled and sliced
d: Cook tomatoes and onions until done.
Add spices and other ingredients and cook slowly until very thick, stirring often. Will mike 1 large dishpan 3/4 full.
In a 9 x 13-inch pan, combine honey, water, mustard, melted oleo, Cajun seasoning, curry powder, lemon juice and garlic; mix well.
Add chicken, turning to coat.
Arrange in single layer. Bake, uncovered, in a 350° oven for 30 minutes, or until chicken is tender and no longer pink.
Simmer 25 minutes.
Top with 1/2 pound velveeta cheese sliced and simmer 5 more minutes.

Saute onion, carrot and celery in olive oil for 15 to 20 minutes. Add meat, cook and crumble. Add white wine to mixture and continue to cook (with lid on loosely) for 1 to 1 1/2 hours. Add tomato sauce, salt to taste and basil. Lightly cover and cook for several hours more. Serve with pasta.
Dissolve gelatin in cool water.
Heat cream cheese in tomato soup to mix.
Cool.
Add balance of ingredients and congeal.
Brown together beef and onion; drain fat.
Add garlic powder, salt, oregano, basil, brown sugar, green pepper, tomatoes, water, tomato sauce and tomato paste.
Simmer for 1 hour or until condensed.
Cook and drain hamburg.
Set aside.
Simmer onion, celery, green pepper and zucchini in water until almost tender.
Cook and drain noodles.
Mix all ingredients (except chips) and put in refrigerator. Serve with the tortilla chips.
Sift all dry ingredients in a large mixing bowl.
Stir.
Add the rest and beat with electric mixer for 2 minutes.
Pour into greased and floured pans (2).
I use small tube pan.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Prepare Rice-A-Roni according to package.
Remove from heat; add finely chopped other ingredients.
Stir in mayonnaise and curry.
Serve cold or still warm.

t: Zucchini Parmesan(Can Substitute Eggplant For Zucchini)
d: On wax paper, combine bread crumbs with oregano.
Cut zucchini lengthwise.
Brush with mayo; coat with crumb mixture.
In 2 1/2-quart casserole dish, arrange zucchini, crumb side up.
Cook, covered with wax paper on High for 12 to 15 minutes, until tender, rotating halfway through cooking time.
In small bowl, cook spaghetti sauce on High for 1 to 3 minutes, until hot.

i: 2 lb. sweet potatoes, peeled and cut into 1/2-inch dice
d: In a large pot, cover sweet potatoes with water. Add salt and bring to a boil over moderately high heat. Cook until just tender, about 10 minutes; drain well. Refresh with cold water and drain again.
Combine and cook first 6 ingredients.
Bring to boil and boil 5 minutes; cool.
Add sifted, dry ingredients to apple mixture. Stir and blend only.
Add nuts.
Pour into greased and floured tube pan.
Bake 1 hour at 350°.
Mix cream cheese, Cheddar cheese, sherry, Worcestershire sauce, curry and salt. Blend well.
Line loaf pan with wax paper. Smooth mixture into pan.
Refrigerate 3 hours or overnight. Remove from pan. Layer chutney, green onion, cashews and coconut.
Serve at room temperature with Bagel Chips or Wheat Thins.
Mix the oil with the vinegar, water, lemon juice, garlic and seasonings.
In a large salad bowl with a tight-fitting lid, toss the mixture with the broccoli.
Let marinate all day or overnight, tossing the mixture several times before eating.
Melt fat in Dutch oven.
Add meat, onions and fry until brown. Add salt, pepper and water.
Cook 1 hour.
Add celery and cook 30 minutes more.
Make thickening of flour, sugar and soy sauce.
Add 2 tablespoons water.

i: 1 gal. sliced cucumber, do not peel
d: Layer cucumbers and onions.
Combine sugar, vinegar and spices.
Pour over cucumbers and onions.
Can be frozen.
Must be refrigerated or frozen.

i: 2 c. frozen strawberries, thawed and sliced
d: Combine dry ingredients.
Add eggs and oil.
Add strawberries and pecans.
Mix in lightly; do not beat.
Spoon batter into loaf pans.
Makes 3 medium loaves or 5 small ones.
Mix all together and drop by spoonfuls into very hot oil. Cook until brown.
Fry hamburger and onion until red disappears.
Add garlic, onion salt, chicken gumbo and catsup.
Simmer for 2 hours.
The longer it simmers, the tastier it gets.
Open, drain and rinse canned chickpeas.
Mash in food processor or blender.
Add mashed garlic, salt and tahini paste. Blend until smooth.
Add juice of 2 lemons and mix.
If consistency is too thick to spread, add 1 tablespoon water.
Serve in a flat dish.
Mix, roll and cut
in
small pieces.
Drop into boiling broth. Cook, covered, 12 to 15 minutes.
Mix all ingredients.
Pour into individual molds such as small custard dishes or very small souffle cups.
Set molds in a pan of hot water and bake, uncovered, at 350° for 40 minutes or until the blade of a table knife inserted in the center comes out clean. Serve warm or chilled.

i: 1 large can crushed pineapple with juice
d: In a saucepan, combine cherry pie filling with sugar, pineapple and juice, cornstarch and food coloring.
Cook until thick.
Remove from heat and add gelatin.
Allow to cool.
Add bananas and pecans. Pour into two baked pie shells.
Top with whipped topping.

i: 30 onions, cut up or 1 to 2 lb. pickling onions, very small ones
4 c. vinegar (definitely not Heinz, too strong)
d: Cut up vegetables coarsely and put in large container.
Put in pickling salt.
Add 2 trays of ice cubes, mix through.
Cover with cloth and let stand overnight in a very large canner (15 1/2-quart or larger).
In morning, make syrup of vinegar, sugar, turmeric and mustard.
Cook down until mixed throughly.
Place nuts in bottom of greased Bundt pan.
Arrange rolls on top.
Sprinkle dry pudding over rolls.
Cook butter, sugar and cinnamon over low heat until sugar is dissolved and mixture bubbles; pour over rolls.
Cover tightly with foil and let stand on counter overnight.
Next morning, bake at 350° for 30 minutes (take foil off before baking).
Cook sugar, oleo and lemon juice until sugar is dissolved and mixture is clear.
Beat eggs to a yellow foam.
Add to other mixture.
Cook until mixture coats spoon.
Cool.
Store in refrigerator for up to 6 months.
Mix all ingredients together.
Pour into a greased 8 x 8-inch pan. Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes. Cut into squares when cooled.
Combine ingredients, except barbecue sauce.
Shape into 1-inch balls.
Place on broiler rack (or place in oven on a cookie sheet) at 400°.
Turn once.
Cook until done.
Combine meatballs with barbecue sauce.

i: 1/2 c. (1 stick) butter or margarine, melted
d: Combine crumbs, butter and sugar in medium bowl; press onto bottom of ungreased 13 x 9 inch baking pan.
Brown meat and diced onions.
Drain.
Add remaining ingredients.
Cook 30 to 45 minutes. Check for taste.
Add more chili powder and a dash of red pepper for hotter chili.
Serves 6.
Cut or break up candy bars.
Melt candy and oleo together in the microwave.
Stir until smooth (small pieces may remain). Cool.
Sift flour and soda together.
In a mixing bowl, slowly add buttermilk to flour and beat well.
Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each.
Mix the first 5 ingredients until crumbly; press half of the crumb mixture into greased baking dish.
Cover with rhubarb. Combine sugar, cornstarch, water and vanilla.
Cook until thickened and clear.
Pour over rhubarb.
Top with remaining crumbs.
Bake 1 hour at 350°.
Squeeze sauerkraut dry.
Combine all ingredients.
Mix well. Shape into one-inch balls.
Bake in ungreased shallow pan at 375° for 35 to 40 minutes.
Break open to test.
Serves 5.
Brown meat.
Add taco seasoning according to directions. Spoon on roll.
Add sliced tomato, lettuce and cheese.
Serve with corn chips.
Preheat oven to 400°.
Brush salmon with olive oil. Sprinkle with salt, pepper and dill.
Squeeze lemon on top
of salmon and sprinkle with brown sugar.	Top salmon with the lemon slices. Bake 20 to 30 minutes.
This bakes really well in stoneware.

i: 1 large can baked beans (30 to 40 oz.)
d: Fry bacon until crisp.
Chop onion and garlic.
Fry in 2 tablespoons of bacon fat until soft.
Add vinegar, sugar and mustard.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Mix the drained kidney beans and butter beans in a large baking dish with the baked beans.
Bring to boil, pour over beets and boil 10 minutes.
Pack into sterilized jars and seal.
No salt.
Makes 12 pints.

i: 1/2 to 1 tsp. crushed red pepper flakes
d: In 4-quart saucepot over medium heat, cook onion, garlic, cumin and pepper flakes in oil until onion is tender (about 3 minutes). Remove from heat.
Break up and boil Ramen noodles, reserve spice packet.
Drain and rinse.
Toast almonds in butter.
Combine noodles, almonds, chopped cabbage, onions, green peppers and sesame seed.
You will need freezer paper and string.
Lift telephone receiver.
Push 633-6655 for Plus One Pizza. Turn T.V. on.
Sit in chair.
Wait 20 to 30 minutes.
The Clergy Pizza special will be a sure way to please the entire family!

i: 1/2 lb. fresh mushrooms, cleaned and diced
d: Preheat oven to 425°.
Place chicken in pan.
Season with salt and pepper.
Brush with lemon and butter.
Roast 30 minutes in oven.
Reduce heat to 350°.
Drain beans, saving liquid (there should be 1 cup liquid). Mix together meat, milk, rolled oats and 1/2 teaspoon salt.
Shape mixture into 16 balls and roll in flour.
Melt shortening in a skillet and brown meatballs on all sides.
Reduce heat and add onion; cook slowly until onion is lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add liquid and remaining ingredients, except the beans. Cover and cook slowly for 10 minutes or until potatoes are tender. Add drained beans; cover and heat thoroughly.

i: 12 ears of fresh corn, cut off the cob
1 bunch of celery, cut into small pieces
1 small head of cabbage, cut as for slaw
d: Put into large kettle and stir often, as it will stick easily.
Cook for 30 minutes after it starts to boil.
Put into jars and seal.
Beat egg white until stiff.
Add sugar and vanilla; mix well. Dip 2 or 3 pecans at a time.
Place on ungreased baking sheet. Bake at 250° for 30 minutes.
Leave in oven for 30 more minutes with the oven turned off.
Crumble Shredded Wheat into a greased baking dish.
Beat eggs, add milk.
Stir in molasses, salt and cinnamon.
Pour over biscuits.
Dot with butter.
Bake at 350° for 35 to 45 minutes. Serve plain or with whipped cream.
Coat a medium nonstick skillet with vegetable oil.
Place over medium-high heat until hot.
Add next 3 ingredients; saute 1 1/2 to 2 minutes or until crisp-tender.
Remove from heat and set aside. Keep warm.
Combine first 6 ingredients in glass bowl.
Let stand overnight; then drain.
Mix other 6 ingredients in pan and bring to a boil.
Boil 2 minutes.
Add pepper mix and boil 3 minutes. Pack in hot jars and seal.

t: Pennsylvania Dutch Hot Salad Or Dandelion Dressing
i: few strips of crisp bacon (some drippings can be added for flavor)
d: Mix eggs slightly.
Add sugar and vinegar.
Mix with some water; add remaining water.
Mix again slightly.
Cook over low burner so it does not curdle.
If it should curdle, beat.
Place chicken on a rack in a shallow baking dish.
Combine remaining ingredients and brush on chicken.
Bake at 370° for 1 hour.
Brush chicken with mixture once or twice during baking.
Mix yeast and water together. Set aside.
Mix all the rest of the ingredients together.
Combine both mixtures. Turn dough on floured board.
Knead. Spread on greased pizza pan.
Let stand 15 minutes. Spread spaghetti sauce on dough, then add cooked hamburger meat, mushrooms, cheese, onions, pepper or whatever you like. Preheat oven to 425°.
Cook 15 to 20 minutes. Eat and enjoy.
Mix all ingredients with a mixer.
Deep-fry shrimp.
In Dutch oven, brown beef.
Add all remaining ingredients. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, for 1 hour. Yield: 3 quarts.

i: 1 lb. ground beef (ground chuck is better)
3 to 4 Tbsp. chili powder (more, if desired)
d: Combine chopped ingredients; combine with at least a half cup of water in cooking vessel or pot.
Let cook over very low heat until tender.
Brown the ground beef and add all other ingredients listed in the pot with the vegetables and browned beef.
Add enough water for a desired consistency.
Let simmer over very low heat for at least 45 minutes to one hour.
Makes a serving for at least 6.
Divide the okra evenly between 3 (1 pint) jars.
Place one dried chile and one tsp. of dill into each jar.
In a small saucepan, combine the water, vinegar and salt.
Bring to a rolling boil.
Pour over the ingredients in the jars, and seal in a hot water bath for 10 minutes.
Refrigerate jars after opening.
Mix first 7 ingredients well, then add cheese.

i: 1 can or 1 pkg. frozen green lima beans
d: Scramble the beef and onion together.
Add other ingredients and bake at 350° for 45 minutes.
In bowl, mix sour cream with chopped or diced jalapenos, onions and avocados.

i: cherry or blueberry pie filling (1/2 can)
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
Line 12 muffin cups with cupcake papers.
In a small bowl, mix the graham cracker crumbs and melted margarine together.
Divide the cracker mixture among the 12 cups, then press into the cups.

i: 1 (7 oz.) pkg. Near East rice pilaf
d: Prepare rice pilaf following package directions.
Place in large bowl, cover and chill.
Mix well.
Roll into balls (small).
Bake at 350° for 20 to 25 minutes.
Toss the oranges and spinach.
Add desired rice vinegar and olive oil.
Sprinkle with chopped walnuts.

i: 1 1/2 c. chopped jalapeno pepper (about 6 to 8 peppers for mild-medium salsa)
d: Simmer all ingredients in large pot, uncovered, for 1 hour. Make paste of 6 tablespoons cornstarch and 2/3 cup water.
Add to sauce and simmer to thicken.
Combine mustard and vinegar in a small bowl.
Add olive oil in a thin stream, whisking constantly.
Season with pepper and add the garlic clove.
Mix to blend.
For best flavor, let dressing stand for 30 minutes.
Before serving, mix in parsley.

i: 1 lb. small fresh okra with heads removed
d: Mix all the spices and salt together; set aside.
Slit the okra lengthwise without separating all the way through.
Stuff them individually with spice mixture.
Heat up oil in a vessel. When hot, add stuffed okra.
Saute it for a few minutes, then cover the vessel with a lid and let cook on medium heat.
Stir the okra intermittently to prevent burning.

i: 2 Tbsp. white mustard seed (if mustard seed are not available celery seeds will do)
d: Cook syrup 5 minutes then add ground mixture.
Simmer together for 5 minutes.
Pour into hot jars, and seal.
Brown pork chops in deep skillet.
Chop onion and add to chops in pan, then stewed tomatoes and rest of ingredients.
Cover and simmer 30 minutes until done.
Drain the beans (drain the kidney beans after being rinsed with water).
Add other ingredients and let marinate at least overnight.
This salad may be made several days ahead.
A can of baby lima beans may be added; also a 4 ounce can of sliced mushrooms.
Cut chicken breasts in half.
Thaw frozen artichoke hearts and green beans.
Combine first 5 ingredients in a large bowl; make a well in center of mixture.
Set aside.
Combine half and half and remaining ingredients.
Add to dry ingredients, stirring just until moistened.
Pour batter into a well-greased loaf pan.
Bake at 375° for 45 to 50 minutes or until golden.
Combine onion soup and juice.
Mix seasonings well and add to juice.
Mix all liquids and add. Add vegetables or serve on the side.
Chill soup at least 4 to 8 hours.
Combine all ingredients together and marinate in the refrigerator for 6 to 10 hours.
When ready to dry, remove slices and drain on paper towels; may pat dry.
To dry, spread slices in single layer on oven racks.
Place a pan under racks to catch drippings.
Set temperature at 140° or lowest temperature on your stove.
Open door slightly to allow air to circulate and moisture to escape.
Brown
beef;
stir
in tomatoes and tomato paste, salt, basil, oregano, garlic
powder
and water.
Sift flour.
Measure and resift with dry ingredients.
Beat eggs well.
Add sugar gradually.
Using medium speed of mixer, add pumpkin.
Reduce to low speed and add oil, water, vanilla and dry ingredients.
Combine first 5 ingredients in large bowl and form into balls. Roll in flour and brown in hot oil.
Add remaining ingredients and enough of the tomato juice to cover meatballs.
Simmer about 45 minutes or until sauce thickens.
Spread refried beans in bottom of container.
Spread sour cream evenly over beans.
Spread taco sauce evenly over sour cream.
Chop green onions (amount to suit you) and green olives. Sprinkle over layers.
Sprinkle 1 cup or amount to suit you Cheddar cheese over top.
Refrigerate.

i: 4 (1 lb. 12 oz.) cans B&M baked beans
enough bacon to cover the top of baking dish
d: Put half of beans in a baking dish.
Add 3/4 cup sugar and ketchup.
Add remaining beans and ketchup and add mustard.
Break up bacon to cover the top of the baking dish.
Bake, uncovered, at 325° for 2 1/2 hours.

t: Pork Chop And Rice Casserole (Or Chicken)
i: 4 pork chops (3/4-inch thick) or 6 pieces chicken
d: Dredge meat in flour.
Cook in a little shortening until brown. Put in 2-quart or larger baking dish.
Brown pepper, celery and onion.
Put in dish with meat.
Add rice on top, then tomatoes. Bake in 350° oven until tender, about 45 minutes.
Might have to add 1/2 cup water when gets dry.

i: Large deep dish pie shell, unbaked
d: Melt margarine; pour over chocolate chips until dissolved. Combine eggs, sugar, vanilla, and nuts.
Mix with chocolate mixture.
Place in deep dish pie crust.
Bake at 350° for 40 minutes.
Combine sugar, water, cinnamon and nutmeg.
Bring to a boil and add oleo.
Sift together flour, baking powder and salt.
Cut in shortening.
Add milk, all at once.
Stir until flour is moistened.
Combine all ingredients except the spinach and strawberries. Toss with the spinach and strawberries just before serving.

i: 18 large mushrooms, cleaned, stems removed and chopped (but reserved)
d: Clean mushrooms and pat dry.
Then rub with oil.
Arrange them in a large 13 x 9-inch pan.
Add water.
Cook sausage until brown; remove to drain.
Reserve drippings to saute onion, garlic and stems.
Cook shrimp in lightly salted boiling water until pink; drain and cool.
Carefully remove the shell from each shrimp; devein shrimp.
In blender, combine vinegar, honey and curry powder; cover and blend 5 seconds.
Through the opening in the lid of blender, on slow speed, gradually add oil into a thin stream. (When necessary, stop blender and scrape sides.)
Cover and chill dressing.

i: 10 to 12 strips bacon, crisp and crumbled
d: Mix ingredients together and chill 4 to 5 hours or overnight. Serves 6 to 8.
Combine the squash, onions and salt together.
Let stand overnight.
Then drain.
Add vinegar, sugar, cornstarch, pimentos, peppers, black pepper, nutmeg and turmeric.
Simmer 30 to 40 minutes.
(Do not boil.)

i: 2 lb. chopped beef (part lamb or pork)
1 c. oil or butter for frying
3 Tbsp. chopped parsley and mint leaves
d: Brown onion in a little butter.
Place onions in a mixing bowl and add meat, bread crumbs, eggs, and seasonings.
Blend well and shape into size desired.
Roll lightly in flour and fry in very hot oil or butter.

i: 1 1/2 lb. hamburger, cooked with 1 small chopped onion
d: Combine all ingredients into a large pot.
Simmer until done. Garnish with tortilla chips, sour cream, shredded cheese and/or chopped olives.
Mix moderately and sprinkle with awareness.
Bake in moderate oven of everyday life, using as fuel all grudges and past unpleasantness.
Cool.
Turn out onto platter of cheerfulness. Garnish with tears and laughter in large helpings.
Serve God, country and community.
Mix butter, sugar and vanilla until creamy.
Add pecans.
Mix, then gradually add flour.
Knead for 5 minutes.
Refrigerate overnight.
Take out 1 hour before; using teaspoon, roll in balls and bake 30 minutes at 350°.
Cook macaroni following package directions.
Mix cooked chicken and Italian dressing with hot macaroni.
Cool.
Blend mayonnaise, lemon juice and mustard.
Stir in onion, cucumbers, celery and pepper.
Add to macaroni mixture.
Whisk Miracle Whip, lemon juice, salt and pepper.
Add to chicken with onion and celery.
Toss.
Chill.
Put all ingredients in bowl and mix well.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes. When done, poke holes on top and pour a mixture of pineapple juice and powdered sugar over hot cake.
Cut
bread
in half lengthwise.
Heat oleo, oil, garlic and dressing and spoon over bread.
Place bread on foil, cover and heat in 350° oven for 30 minutes.

i: 1 medium red bell pepper, thinly sliced
1 medium yellow bell pepper, thinly sliced
10 small Calamato olives, pitted and chopped
d: In medium bowl, pour boiling water over tomatoes to cover for 2 minutes.	Drain, reserving 1/4 cup liquid.
Thinly slice tomatoes and set aside.

Cook peas according to package directions; drain. While peas cool, break apart cauliflower and slice thin. Combine cauliflower and peas; add celery and onion. Mix together mayonnaise, salt and milk. Mix with the cauliflower mixture. Marinate in refrigerator 6 hours.
Combine wine, soy sauce, garlic, ginger and parsley in baking dish.
Marinate meat for 1 1/2 to 2 hours.
Broil for 30 to 35 minutes at 550°, turning every 5 minutes.
Baste with mixture at each turn.
Stir together berries and 1/2 cup sugar.
Melt margarine in casserole dish.
Mix flour, baking powder, salt and rest of sugar. Add milk and mix well.
Pour batter into dish with margarine.
Do not mix.
Put berries and sugar over batter; do not mix.
Sift flour, baking powder and salt together.
Cut or rub in shortening. Add milk to form a soft dough. Turn out on lightly floured board and knead 1/2 minute.
Pat or roll out onto an 8-inch square about 1/4-inch thick.
Roll sugar into dough.
Cut into strips about 3/8-inch wide. Double each strip over and twist. Place on baking sheet which has been buttered slightly.
Bake in hot oven at 450° for 10 to 12 minutes.
Mix the ingredients together very well and put in an airtight container.
Store in a cool, dry place, away from the stove.
Mix well and refrigerate overnight.
Serve cold!

i: 1 lb. lean pork, cut in strips
d: In a large saucepan or Dutch oven, heat oil over medium heat. Add onion, garlic, ginger and pork; cook and stir until tender. Stir in broth, soy sauce, pepper and sesame oil; bring to a boil. Add carrots; simmer 15 minutes.
Add all remaining ingredients except eggs; bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low.
Stir in eggs with a fork to separate into strands.

Mix sugar, eggs, evaporated milk, condensed milk and vanilla flavoring. Pour into freezer, then add whole milk to top. Freeze. (Two cups of fruit may be added into mixture to create your favorite flavor.)

i: 1 medium onion, thinly sliced and separated
d: Combine sauce ingredients.
Add salt and pepper to taste. Pour over vegetables.
Allow to sit (refrigerated) for 24 hours. Serve on cold platter as salad or relish.
Serves 8.

i: 1 pkg. savory herb with garlic soup mix
1 Tbsp. olive oil or vegetable oil
d: Preheat oven to 375° in 13 x 9-inch baking pan.
Arrange chicken.
In small bowl, combine remaining ingredients; brush on chicken.
Bake 45 minutes or until chicken is done.
Makes about 4 servings.
Saute vegetables in hot oil until golden brown.
Add tomatoes and spices.
Simmer 30 to 45 minutes.
Serve over cooked ravioli and serve with Parmesan cheese.
Make a syrup of the 1 cup of water and 2 cups of sugar. Cook until sugar is dissolved.
Add the remaining ingredients. Makes about 30 cups.
Slice potatoes; mix all ingredients.
Bake slowly until done.
Combine milk and lemon juice.
Let stand until thickened. Fold in thawed whipped topping.
Add remaining ingredients. Chill.
In a glass scoop in ice milk, then pour warm or hot coffee. Makes 1 serving.
Drain grape leaves.
Place in a medium bowl.
Cover with boiling water; let stand 20 minutes.
Drain and discard water.
In large skillet over medium heat, heat vegetable oil.
Add onion, stirring occasionally, about 5 minutes or until golden brown.
Brown meat and onion.
Add salsa, tomato sauce and bouillon cube.
Bring to a full boil.
Reduce heat to low.
Cover and simmer for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, prepare rice as directed on package.

i: 4 (6 oz.) Louis Kemp crab or other seafood
d: In one pan, prepare almond rice mix according to package instructions.

i: 2/3 c. seasoned fine Italian bread crumbs
d: Mix all ingredients together.
Bake at 375° in loaf pan until brown on top.
Mix all ingredients and spread lightly on tortilla.
Roll the tortilla tightly.
Place rolls on plate and cover with plastic wrap.
Chill in refrigerator overnight.
Slice and serve.
Cook sweet potatoes to just tender, about 30 minutes.
Drain, cool and peel.
In large bowl, mash potatoes.
Stir in oleo, sugar and raisins.
Mix everything in pot and bring to boil before adding chicken. Cook until tender.
Prepare Rice-A-Roni according to directions on box.
Put in a large bowl and add mayonnaise, olives, artichokes and curry powder.
Serve warm or cold.
Mix gelatin, water, sugar, pineapple, lemon juice and pecans. Set in refrigerator until as thick as egg whites.
Whip 2 packages Dream Whip and fold in this mixture.
Pour mixture over cake lined pan. Layer another layer of cake and mix 2 packages of Dream Whip and cover cake. Sprinkle coconut and decorate with cherries.
Beat eggs until light and fluffy; add sugar, orange juice, salt and oil and blend thoroughly.
Add enough flour to form medium dough, which leaves bowl clean.
Knead until smooth.
Cut into large pieces of dough; place on flat surface sprinkled with flour and roll as thin as possible.
Cut in strips 2-inches wide. Deep fry in hot vegetable oil, twirling strips immediately with fork to shape Diples.
(May also be shaped into bows.)
Mix flour, margarine and 1/4 cup powdered sugar.
Press into ungreased square 8 x 8 x 2-inch pan.
Bake 20 minutes.

i: 2 c. green peppers, ground in blender
d: Blend green peppers, sugar, white vinegar and turmeric; cook to boiling.
Boil 1 minute.
Remove from heat.
Add Certo.
Stir for 5 minutes.
Add 10 or less drops of green food coloring.
Rinse chickpeas with cold water.
Put in pan and cover with water; boil for a few minutes until soft and drain.
Put chickpeas, tahini, garlic, lemon juice and salt in a blender and blend until smooth.
You can put homos in a shallow dish and serve with wedges of flat bread.
You can sprinkle red pepper and cumin over top, if desired.
In a heavy pan over moderate heat, cook bacon until fat runs. Add garlic and mushrooms and cook until they begin to lose their juices.
Stir in scallions, spinach, tomatoes and parsley with salt and pepper.
Cover pan and simmer gently for 10 minutes.
Combine zucchini and seasonings in casserole.
Cover.
Place in oven and cook 15 minutes.
Add tomatoes and continue cooking for 10 minutes.
Stir cottage cheese together until well mixed; add and mix well the jam and ground ginger.
Add yogurt and blend well in blender. Serve over bite size chunks of banana, apple, pear and other fruit. Makes about 1 1/2 cups.
Let cheese soften; work together.
Mix in garlic powder, mustard and horseradish.
Makes 4 balls/logs.
Roll in nuts, paprika and chili powder.
Refrigerate.
(Also freezes well.)

i: 1 1/2 c. pitted black olives, cut into small pieces
d: Heat oil in small saucepan.
Add garlic, but do not brown. Add olives and parsley; saute for 2 to 3 minutes or until olives are heated.
Immediately toss with hot pasta or cool and add to cold pasta of your choice.
Season with salt and pepper.
Place margarine in a 2-quart rectangular dish and microwave on High 40 seconds.
Stir in honey, mustard, curry powder and salt. Roll chicken pieces in mixture and arrange in a single layer, with meat portions to the outside of the dish and the bony pieces in the center.
Cover with waxed paper and microwave on High 9 minutes. Turn each pieces over, baste with sauce and sprinkle with paprika. Re-cover and microwave on High 9 to 11 minutes.
Let stand 5 minutes.
Makes 4 to 5 servings, and it is good served over curried rice.
Boil giblets in salted water with bay leaf and beef bouillon cubes.
Brown ground beef and drain.
Saute green pepper, onion and celery in saucepan with a little butter.
Chop cooked giblets after they cook and cool a little.
Add giblets, ground beef and sauteed vegetables to rice.
Add a teaspoon of poultry seasoning and 1/2 teaspoon sage to rice mixture.
Soak beans 2 or 3 hours; drain.
Chop cooked bacon finely. Place in large saucepan with water; cover.
Boil 10 minutes.
Then add beans, tomato sauce, onion, pepper pods, garlic, bay leaf, cumin seeds, salt and pepper.
Cover and let simmer 3 hours or until beans are tender.
Stir often.

i: 1 lb. fresh asparagus, cooked and strained
d: Place asparagus in shallow baking dish.
Mix 2 tablespoons margarine with bread crumbs and set aside.
Blend salt, pepper, flour and curry with remaining margarine.
Add milk and cook.
Add onion and pour over asparagus.
Sprinkle with bread crumbs. Bake 15 minutes at 425°.
In microwave, mix all but catsup in microwave-safe 12 x 8-inch dish.
Shape firmly into 8 x 4-inch loaf.
Cover with waxed paper. Cook on High 15 minutes, or until center is 170°.
Rotate during cooking time.
Cool.
Pour 2 tablespoons liquid into cup from drippings.

Combine oil, salt and pepper (to taste) in a shallow dish. Add fish. Marinate for at least 1 hour. Turn once. Drain fish, reserving marinade. Put some garlic inside fish. Grill or broil on moderate heat, 6 to 8 minutes on each side. About three minutes before fish is cooked, spoon some marinade and bread crumbs over fish. Garnish with lemon wedges.

i: 1 (8 oz.) can of whole kennel corn, drained
1 (15 oz.) can of pork and beans
1 (15 oz.) can of chili with beans
d: Brown hamburger meat and onion. Drain.
Mix hamburger and all other ingredients in a large crock pot. Cook for 45 minutes on high. This is good for a meal with creamed potatoes and bread.
Blend together and refrigerate.
Combine all ingredients in a jar with a tight fitting lid. Shake well.
Mix together first 5 ingredients in a heavy saucepan.
Bring to a
boil.
Let cool for 2 minutes.
Take 4 cans of biscuits and cut each one into fourths.
Roll in some sugar and place in a greased Bundt pan.
Saute ground beef, onion and garlic. Stir frequently until meat is no longer pink (do not brown).
Drain fat off. Add remaining ingredients and cook slowly, uncovered, stirring occasionally, for 2 to 3 hours until sauce thickens. Spoon over hot dogs. Top with chopped onions, if desired.
In a 10-inch skillet over medium-high heat, cook onions in oil until golden.
Remove with slotted spoon to bowl.
On sheet of waxed paper, mix flour, salt, dried rosemary and pepper to coat chicken thighs.
In remaining oil in skillet over medium-high heat, cook chicken until golden brown on all sides.

i: 2 c. scalded milk, let cool till luke warm
1 pkg. dry yeast, dissolved in 1/4 c. warm water
d: Sift flour in large mixing bowl and add remainder of ingredients and stir well mixed, cover and let stand for 10 minutes.
Sift 1 cup or more of flour on kneading board.
Pour dough on board and knead until smooth with palm of the hand, add more flour if needed to handle easily.
Place in greased bowl in warm place for 1 hour or until doubled.
Punch down and let rise again.
Then make in little balls, place 2-inches apart in greased baking pan, let rise about 1 hour or until light, bake about 20 to 25 minutes or until well browned in a 400° oven.
Chop each carrot into three pieces, then cut the potatoes. Next cut the chicken.
Get a big pan, maybe to 1 foot high.
Put the three cups of water in.
Put in the carrots and then the potatoes and then the chicken.
Add a cup of hot sauce.
Boil for 3 to 5 minutes.
In a large pot over medium heat brown flour in oil until dark brown (not burned), stirring constantly.
Add onions, peppers and celery and saute until tender.
In a large bowl add tomatoes crushed up and water.
Season to taste.
Add to roux-vegetable mixture.
Add consomme and okra and bring to a boil.
Serve in chimney glass.
Heat oil in skillet; fry chicken pieces.
Add mushrooms; continue cooking.
Add bay leaf, thyme and garlic, stirring so ingredients mix.
Cook approximately 12 minutes.
Sprinkle ingredients with flour; stir to blend.
Add wine, tomatoes and broth; stir and bring to boil in separate pan.

i: 1 can Hunt's Special tomato sauce (16 oz.)
1 can Old El Paso tomatoes and green chilies
2 medium cut up or sliced bell peppers
2 Tbsp. "medium and chunky" Old El Paso salsa
d: *Your choice of variety: ham, salami, bologna, spiced luncheon meat.

i: 1/2 c. (2 oz.) shredded reduced fat Cheddar cheese
d: Place turkey in a nonstick skillet; cook over medium heat until browned, stirring to crumble.
Drain and pat dry with paper towel. Combine turkey and next 5 ingredients in a bowl; stir well.
Add buttermilk, stirring until just moistened.
Drop dough by heaping tablespoonful onto a baking sheet coated with vegetable cooking spray.
Bake at 450° for 11 minutes (68 calories each).
Makes 1 dozen.
Combine juices and sugar in a punch bowl.
Stir until well mixed.
Add ginger ale and ice cubes.
Garnish with orange and lemon slices, if desired.
Yields 4 1/2 quarts of punch.

i: 3 c. Captain Crunch peanut butter cereal
d: Melt candy wafers and Crisco together.
Mix all other ingredients in a large bowl.
Pour melted candy over cereal and cool well.
Drop by tablespoon onto wax paper.
When firm, store in airtight container.
Cook fruit until cranberries burst.
Cranberries on bottom. Blend dry ingredients and stir into fruit.
Cook until thickened. Cool slightly and pour into 9-inch uncooked pie shell and bake at 350°
for 30 minutes.

i: 1 (6 oz.) can frozen lime or lemonade
4 to 5 c. cold water or soda
d: Mix and pour over ice cubes.
Mix together well.
Let set overnight in sealed container.
Combine all ingredients in a large bowl.
Mix at low speed until well blended.
Chill until firm.
Shape into ball and roll in chopped pecans.
Wrap in foil and refrigerate overnight.

i: 6 pieces bread (buttered with either butter or margarine)
milk (as much as you would like; to fluff eggs)
d: Saute together first 5	ingredients.
In a separate 9 x 12-inch pan, mix bread, eggs
and
milk.
Put
pieces of bread into
Add all ingredients to heavy crock-pot.
Simmer 1 hour or until meat is falling off of bone.
Cool and strip meat from bone. Return to pot and bring to boil.
Mix Kool-Aid, juice and ginger ale together.
Add large spoonfuls of sherbet to liquid.

i: 1 c. raw wild rice (4 c. cooked)
d: Cook rice and toss with olive oil and raisins.
Season with 1 teaspoon salt and 1/2 teaspoon pepper.
Let cool to room temperature.
Add parsley, onion, tomato and mint.
Add vinegar and toss.
Serves 1.
Lightly brown onions and celery in hot oil; add flour and blend.
Add broth and cook until thick, stirring constantly.
Add tomato juice, seasoning and chicken.
Heat thoroughly and serve over rice.

i: 2 cans Ro-Tel tomatoes with green chilies
d: Combine all ingredients. Cook over low to medium heat until cheese is melted. This is hot and delicious.

i: 1 (6 oz.) can frozen orange juice concentrate, thawed
d: Combine sugar, shortening and eggs in mixer bowl.
Beat until smooth.
Add yams and orange juice concentrate; mix well.
Mix flour, baking soda, cinnamon, nutmeg, allspice, cloves and salt together.
Add to batter, 1/3 at a time, mixing well after each addition.
Stir in pecans.

i: 1 (28 oz.) can Las Palmas Green chili enchilada sauce
chopped black olives, chopped onions, sour cream (optional topping)
d: Mix shredded chicken and 1/2 cup green enchilada sauce in saucepan.
Fry tortillas in vegetable oil until medium fry.
Dip tortilla in green enchilada sauce.
Scoop chicken; sprinkle cheese inside and roll tortilla.
Repeat steps for each tortilla.
Put enchiladas in tray and spread leftover enchilada sauce on top. Sprinkle cheese on top.
Mix
cabbage, green pepper, onion and pimento together. Make a
sauce
of
vinegar, salt, sugar, celery seed, mustard seed
and turmeric.	Bring sauce to boil.
Mix dry ingredients together and add egg, milk and oil.
Stir in blueberries.
Spoon batter into muffin tins.
Bake at 375° for 15 to 20 minutes.
These muffins come out with a great crunchy top.

i: 1/4 tsp. garlic powder or 2 cloves, minced
2 Tbsp. fresh or 1 Tbsp. dried parsley
d: Combine water, parsley, pepper and seasoning in covered saucepan.
Bring to a boil.
Combine oil, butter, garlic and parsley in 10-inch skillet and bring to frying temperature.
Mix first 6 ingredients and shape into balls.
Brown meatballs in 1 tablespoon olive oil.
Place in baking dish and cover with spaghetti sauce.
Top each meatball with Parmesan and Mozzarella cheese.
Bake 20 minutes at 350°.
Blend all ingredients together.
Yields 1-1/2 cups of dressing.

i: 3 strips bacon, chopped (need about 2 Tbsp. drippings)
d: Cook kraut and water about 10 minutes.
Fry bacon.
Add onion to
drippings and cook until onion is glazed.
Add flour and stir well.
Pour
Soften gelatin with 2 tablespoons of cold water.
Dissolve gelatin and jello with 1 1/2 cups of boiling water.
Run raw cranberries and unpeeled, but seeded, apples through food chopper. Cut up nuts and orange.
Mix sugar with pineapple and combine with ground cranberries, nuts and orange.
Add to jello mixture and turn into mold.

i: 2 (4 oz.) cans green chili peppers, drained, seeded and chopped
1/3 chopped parsley or 2 Tbsp. dried parsley flakes
1/2 tsp. ground black pepper to taste
2 tsp. cumin seed or 1 Tbsp. ground cumin
d: Cut pork and beef into 1-inch cubes.
Combine all ingredients in crock-pot; stir thoroughly.
Cover and cook on low setting for 8 to 10 hours.
Before serving, taste for seasoning; add salt and cumin as needed.
Makes 6 to 8 servings (about 3 quarts).
Mix all ingredients thoroughly and bake in greased casserole dish at 350° for 30 minutes.
Serve with crackers.

i: 2 (10 1/2 oz.) bags fresh spinach, cooked and chopped or substitute frozen, chopped spinach
1 (10 oz.) can pitted ripe olives, chopped fine
4 to 5 cloves fresh garlic, minced fine
olive oil (the best you can get)
d: Combine all ingredients.

Add olive oil to moisten to taste. Use as filling for spinach turnovers (in roll mix dough) or excellent as: pizza topping, dip for raw vegetables, to spread on French bread or crackers, to stuff boneless chicken breasts or to toss with hot pasta.

i: 4 c. coarsely cut up cooked chicken
d: Combine mayonnaise and curry powder.

Set aside. Combine next seven ingredients. Mix enough mayonnaise into chicken mixture until moistened. Slice cantaloupe into 1/2-inch sliced rings. To serve chicken salad, place ring of cantaloupe on lettuce. Heap salad on cantaloupe. Garnish with chunks of pineapple and paprika. Serve with hot bread. Serves 6.
Grease or Pam a 15 x 10 x 1-inch jelly roll pan.
Beat all ingredients, except coconut and nuts, until well blended.
Spread in pan and sprinkle with coconut and nuts.
Bake until golden brown at 350° for 25 minutes.
While hot drizzle with glaze.
Combine cake mix, apricot nectar, oil and sugar.
Add eggs, 1 at a time.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes to 1 hour.
Peel and devein shrimp.
Saute onion and green pepper in butter until tender.
Combine flour and dry seasonings; blend into onion mixture.
Stir in Worcestershire sauce and vinegar until smooth.
Add tomatoes and okra, stirring constantly until thickened.
Add shrimp and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes.
In a buttered 2-quart casserole, mix together tomatoes and 2 cups of stuffing mix.
Stir in onion, sugar and seasonings.
Dot butter; sprinkle remaining stuffing mix on top.
Bake at 375° for 45 minutes.
Yields 8 servings.
Sift
flour
and salt together and work shortening into it with hands and fingertips, then add water until dough forms into firm ball.
Knead well and form into small balls the size of golf balls.
Let
stand for about 15 minutes and then roll into thin pancake

i: 1/2 c. finely diced sun-dried tomatoes in oil
d: Dry scallops in paper towels.
Season with pepper.
Heat oil in large skillet over medium-high heat until very hot.
Add scallops, then garlic to skillet.
Immediately add tomatoes and toss.
Add crackers, lemon juice, 2 tablespoons of parsley and butter.

i: 2 (3 oz. each) pkg. sliced, smoked chicken, cut up
1 orange, pared and cut into 6 wedges
d: Crumble 2 tostada shells on each of 6 salad plates.
Layer half each of the chicken pieces and Mexican Coleslaw on crumbled tostadas.
Top with cheese, remaining chicken and coleslaw, olives and orange wedges.
Sprinkle with chili powder.
Top with taco sauce.
Yields 6 servings.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Cream sugar, syrup and margarine.
Add salt, vanilla and eggs (one at a time).
Stir in pecans.
Pour into pie crust.
Bake for 1 hour.
Slit each shrimp down the back without removing the shell. Flatten and place cut side up in a shallow broiler pan.
Sprinkle with the garlic and olive oil and put under a preheated broiler. Broil 3 minutes, then turn the shrimp.
Broil an additional 4 minutes, or until the flesh is opaque and the shrimp are cooked through.
Transfer to a warmed platter.
Pour the cooking liquid over the shrimp and sprinkle with the parsley and lemon juice. Garnish with lemon wedges.
Makes 4 servings.
Drain pineapple and reserve juice.
Add water to juice to make 1 cup.
Stir boiling water in jello in a large bowl until dissolved.
Stir in reserved liquid.
Refrigerate for 1 1/4 hours. Take out and stir in Cool Whip with a wire whisk until smooth. Refrigerate again for 10 minutes.
Take out and stir in pineapple, marshmallows, oranges and coconut.

i: 1 big batch cookie mix pouch (any flavor)
finely chopped nuts or granulated sugar
1 Creamy Deluxe Ready To Spread Frosting (any)
d: Heat oven to 375°.
Knead flavor packet about 10 seconds.
Mix cookie mix, flavor packet, egg and flour in a large bowl until moist (dough will be stiff).
Shape into 1-inch balls; roll in nuts.
Place about 2 inches apart on ungreased cookie sheet.
Bake until set, 9 to 10 minutes.

i: 1 (5 oz.) jellied cranberries, crushed with fork
d: Spread mixture over cranberries.
Refrigerate in freezer.
In pan mix corn meal with 1 cup cold water.
Add 3 cups hot water and salt.
Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly for 7 to 8 minutes,
bringing
heat
down to low.
Brown rice in a little butter.
Add rest of butter and beef consomme.
Put in 350° oven for 1 hour or until rice is done.
May add chopped onions and pepper.

i: 8 milk chocolate bars without nuts (1.55 oz. each)
