d: In a saucepan, melt chocolate with water over low heat. Mixture will begin to harden.
Cream butter and sugar.
Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
Blend in vanilla and chocolate mixture.
Stir together flour, salt and soda; add alternately with buttermilk to batter.
Fold in nuts, if desired. Pour into a greased and floured 10-inch pan (tube).
Prepare biscuit mix as directed on package for biscuits. Roll out dough onto well floured board or wax paper.
Thinly sprinkle uncooked sausage onto dough.
Roll up like a jelly roll and cut into small slices.	Cook at 350° until golden brown.
Bake at 300° for 1 hour.
Melt butter; add cocoa and blend well.
Add sugar and then eggs (unbeaten), one at a time, beating each one well.
Add remaining ingredients.
Bake in a slow 300° oven for 20 to 25 minutes.
Remove from oven and cut in squares.
Let cool before removing from the 8 x 8-inch pan.

i: 1/2 c. (1 stick) softened butter or margarine
d: Combine softened butter or margarine, confectioners sugar and orange peel in a small bowl; blend well.
Sift dry ingredients together.
Cut in shortening.
Make a well in dry ingredients. Add milk.
Roll out or pat down on a floured board and cut.
Bake 10 to 12 minutes at 450°.
Mix well all ingredients.
Form into balls.
Brown on all sides.
Mix remaining soup and 3/4 can water to pan.
Bake in oven at 250° for 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
Sift flour, measure 7 cups and sift with soda, salt and spices.
Sift 1/2 cup flour and set aside to use only if needed. Measure sugar, butter and molasses.
Turn molasses into small pan and warm slightly; add brown sugar and stir until melted.
Add butter, blending in well.
Stir in eggs, one at a time.
Batter is smooth.
Place onion, celery, green pepper, red pepper, black pepper and curry powder with oil.
Cook over slow fire.
Add stew beef and cook until meat is brown, stirring.
Fill pan 2/3 full with water and let boil, adding as much rice as needed.
Cook slowly until rice is soft.
Combine ingredients.
Mix well.
Form into 4 to 6 patties. Broil, or place in greased shallow baking dish and bake at 375° for 30 minutes.

i: 12 oz. can whole kernel corn, drained
d: In saucepan, combine sugar, cornstarch, onion, turmeric, celery seed, vinegar, water and corn.
Cook and stir until mixture thickens and bubbles.
Stir in green pepper and pimiento. Chill.
Keeps well in refrigerator.
Makes 1 3/4 cups.

i: chicken flavored soup base to taste
d: Boil chicken, onion, carrots and parsley until done.
Add soup base enough to taste.
Remove chicken to cool, then pick meat off bones.
Mix together and store in covered plastic container.
When you want to use, put 1/3 cup mix in cup with hot water.

i: 1/4-inch olive oil (in bottom of large saucepan)
d: Brown garlic in olive oil. Add rest of ingredients and simmer for 20 minutes. Pour over linguine or your favorite pasta.
Put yams in 4 quarts of water to boil until done.
Peel after cooling.
Mash potatoes.
Add next 4 ingredients.
Spread in Pyrex dish.
Place marshmallows on top.
Combine oleo, sugar and syrup.
Cook over low heat, stirring constantly until sugar is dissolved (do not boil).
Let cool slightly.
Add to beaten eggs, vanilla, salt and pecans.
Pour in unbaked pie crust.
Bake in 325° oven for about 40 minutes or until brown.
Mix jello and boiling water.
Stir until jello melts.
Add all other ingredients.
Pour into mold and let set 24 hours for flavors to mix.
Preheat oven to 425°.
Mix salt, pepper and garlic with olive oil.
Cut potatoes
into 1/4 slices or 1/8 slices.
Coat 10-inch glass Pyrex dish
with oil or Pam spray.
Cook spaghetti, set aside.
Cook chicken breasts, cool and chop.
Cook in 2 tbsp oleo, onions, bell pepper, and celery.
Add tomatoes and 2 cups chicken broth, red peppers, and garlic salt. Cook 20 minutes.
Add spaghetti, boned chicken and 1/2 tbsp chopped cheese.
Cream shortening.
Gradually add sugar, beating at low speed of electric mixer until light and fluffy.
Add egg, chocolate and vanilla; beat well.
Combine flour, soda and salt; add to creamed mixture alternately with milk, stirring well.
Stir in pecans or walnuts.
Drop dough by teaspoonfuls, about 1 1/2 inches apart, onto greased cookie sheets.
Mix eggs, milk, vanilla and salt.
Dip hamburger or hot dog halves; fry until crispy.
Serve with blueberries.
Trim excess fat from beef; cut beef with grain into 2-inch strips.
Cut strips across grain into 1/8-inch slices.
Mix cornstarch and 1 tablespoon water.
Heat oil in 12-inch skillet or wok over medium-high heat until hot.
Add beef and onion; cook and stir until beef is brown, about 3 minutes.
Stir in pineapple, 1/2 cup water, the sugar, vinegar, instant bouillon and soy sauce. Heat to boiling.
Cream Crisco and sugar.
Sift flour and add salt and baking powder.
Separate eggs.
Add yolks to sugar and Crisco.
Add milk, flour, beaten egg whites and vanilla.
Separate mix.

t: Slimey Goop For Kids(Also Called Gak)
d: Mix together the glue and water.
Mix together the water, Borax and food coloring.
Stir mixtures together and watch Gak appear!
Great for Halloween parties.
For age 5 and up.
Brush mushrooms and remove stems.
Mix all ingredients together and stuff mushrooms. Coat with a little melted butter. Bake at 350° for 35 minutes or until done. Serve warm.

i: 1 (19 1/4 oz.) can Campbell's chunky old fashioned beans with ham soup
1 (12 oz.) can whole kernel golden corn with sweet peppers, drained
d: In saucepan, brown frankfurters in butter.
Add remaining ingredients.
Heat; stir occasionally.
Garnish with green pepper rings.
Makes about 5 cups.

i: 1 can chili beans or kidney beans
d: Cook ground beef and drain well in colander.
Spray hot water over beef to get all fat off.
Combine cabbage, beans and tomato juice and add salt, pepper and chili powder to taste.
Simmer until cabbage is well done.

i: 1 large and 1 small pkg. cream cheese
d: Beat together the cream cheese, eggs, vanilla and 1/2 cup of sugar.
Pour into a crumb crust.
Bake 15 minutes at 350°.
Cool for 15 minutes.
In a large saucepan, mix the cider and sugar. Add cinnamon and cloves tied together in cheesecloth.
Bring to a boil.
Simmer 5 minutes.
Remove cinnamon and cloves. Add juices and water.
Heat until piping hot.
Serve in mugs with lemon slices, if desired.
Brown hamburger meat.
Drain off grease.
Add chopped onion, celery and green pepper.
Saute for 5 minutes.
Add rest of ingredients and simmer for an hour or more.
Picante sauce can be added if desired.
Spray pan with Pam.
Put dry ingredients together; mix well and add the rest.
Bake 1 hour at 325°.
Let set in pan 5 minutes before dumping.
Bake in loaf pan.
Heat all ingredients except oranges.
Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain punch and pour into punch bowl. Float oranges on top.
Makes 32 (1/2-cup) servings, 85 calories per cup.
Combine all ingredients (except roast) in a bowl and mix.
Put chuck roast in large roasting pan and cover roast with the mix. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Put in a 275° oven, roast up to 8 to 12 hours.
It is done when meat is very tender and shreds easily.
Grind tomatoes and mix with other ground vegetables. Sprinkle with salt and let stand overnight.
Rinse and drain. Combine remaining ingredients and bring to a boil.
Add vegetables and simmer for 4 minutes.
Do not overcook. Pour into sterilized jars and seal.
Process in hot water bath (212°) for 5 minutes. Remove jars and complete seal.

i: 2 (19 oz.) cans cannellini beans or 4 c. fresh cooked beans
d: Wash the escarole and cut it into 1-inch strips.
In a large pot, heat the oil and garlic for 2 minutes.
Discard the garlic, add the tomatoes and salt, cover and cook 5 minutes.
Take the escarole out of the water and put it in the pan with the water that clings to the leaves.
Cover and cook about 15 minutes, stirring now and then.
Add the beans and black pepper and cook 15 minutes more.
Mix all ingredients together; put in a jello mold or just a bowl.
Refrigerate overnight.

t: Ronald Reagan'S Corned Beef Hash In Bell Peppers
d: Five minutes before serving, drop raw egg in top of each pepper.
Sprinkle with paprika, salt and pepper.
Return to oven until egg has set.
Combine all ingredients.
Spread the batter evenly in a well-buttered oblong baking pan and bake in a slow oven, about 325°, for half an hour.
Cut into small oblongs while still warm.
Beat butter at medium speed of an electric mixer until creamy.
Add 1/2 cup powdered sugar; beat well.
Combine flour and salt; gradually add flour mixture to butter mixture, beating at low speed just until blended after each addition.
Stir in vanilla and pecans.
Divide dough into fourths; cover and chill at least 2 hours.
Work with 1 portion of dough at a time, storing remainder in refrigerator.
Mix macaroni, oregano and mayonnaise together.
Fold in shrimp and diced tomatoes.
Sprinkle with barbecue spice, salt and pepper to taste.
Best if allowed to set in refrigerator overnight.
Mix Kool-Aid, sugar and pineapple juice. Add enough water to make 1 gallon of punch. Pour over sherbet (pineapple or orange sherbet). Add Sprite or 7-Up to punch.
Add small amount of oil to grated carrots.
Stir; add raspberry vinegar and small amount of pepper.
Chill and serve.

i: 1 pkg. sugar-free instant butterscotch pudding mix
1 pkg. sugar-free chocolate instant pudding mix
d: Mix in medium bowl the butterscotch mix, 2/3 cup dry milk and 1 1/4 cups of water.
Mix well with wire whisk.
Pour into pie crust.
Mix in bowl the chocolate pudding mix, remaining 2/3 cup dry milk and 1 1/4 cups of water.
Mix well with wire whisk.
Pour over butterscotch layer.

i: 4 tomatoes cut into eighths or 1 can (16 oz) tomatoes with liquid
2 large green peppers cut into strips
d: Cut beef into 3 inch x 1 inch strips; brown in oil in a skillet. Transfer to a slow cooker. Combine the next seven ingredients pouf over beef. Cover and cook on low for 5-6 hr or until meat is tender. Add tomatoes and green peppers; cook on low 1 hr longer. Combine the cold water and cornstarch to make a paste; cook on high until thickened. Serve over noodles or rice.
Mix sour cream and cream cheese together and spread over bottom of bowl or pan.
Top with tomatoes.
Pour on salsa.
Then add cheese and finally lettuce.
You're ready to dip.
In large bowl, combine first three ingredients.
In cup, whisk together remaining ingredients.
Pour over salad to coat.
Mix well the gelatin and boiling water.
Stir until slightly thickened.
Add remaining ingredients.
Pour into mold and chill.
Mix apples and sugar.
Let stand 10 minutes.
Sift flour, salt, soda and cinnamon together; set aside.
Beat the egg; add oil and blend into apple mixture, then add flour all at once. Beat with wooden spoon until blended.
Bake in a 13 x 9 x 2-inch well-greased pan at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes.
Mix confectioners sugar and milk and drizzle over squares.
Soften cream cheese; then add olives and enough salsa to make mixture pink.
Place 1 tortilla on plate (or server) and spread cheese mixture; repeat process ending with tortilla on top.
Cut into small slices like pie.
For garnish, fasten stuffed olive on each slice with toothpick.

i: 1 1/2 c. uncooked, unwashed rice (measuring cup, not rice cooker cup)
salt and pepper and Tony Chachere to taste
d: Combine all ingredients in a bowl.
Mix well.
Put in rice cooker and cook as usual.
Blend all ingredients together well.
Use for bean salad or cucumbers.

i: 2 eggs, beat the whites separately and beat in last
d: Pour in 9-inch unbaked pie shell and bake for 20 minutes at 400°.
Reduce to 350° and finish baking.
When it doesn't wiggle in the center, it is done or when a knife inserted in the center comes out clean (about 20 to 25 minutes).
Combine vinegar and onions in blender; add chili sauce and remaining ingredients and blend well.
Store in refrigerator.
Layer vegetables in large bowl.
Top with bacon bits.
Mix mayonnaise and sugar together and pour on top of salad, making sure top is covered completely.
Cover and place in refrigerator overnight.
Put shredded cheese on top just before serving.
Pull lettuce apart, rinse and drain beans.
Add grated cheese and sliced onion and toss well.
Add crushed Doritos, tomatoes and salad dressing at the last minute to avoid a soggy salad.
Preheat oven to 400°.
Fit 12 muffin cups with paper liners. Coat each with cooking spray.
Mix flour, walnuts, wheat germ, brown sugar, baking powder, cinnamon, salt and nutmeg in large bowl.
Stir in bananas, milk, butter and egg.
Mix just until blended.
Using an ice cream scoop, fill muffin cups evenly with batter.
Saute the onion (chopped fine).
Add the tomatoes and green chilies. Cook until most of the liquid is gone.
Add and heat the milk. Add the Velveeta (cubed) and heat until melted.
Mix well and serve.
Mix well and drop by spoonfuls on greased pan.
Bake at 450° until golden brown.
Mix together in microwave bowl.
Cover with Saran Wrap.
Cook on High temperature for 3 1/2 minutes.
Stir and repeat 3 1/2 minutes.
Stir again, recover and let cool.
Store in refrigerator.
Combine all ingredients until well blended.
Makes 1 1/2 cups sauce.

i: 1 (10 1/2 oz.) can condensed chicken with rice soup
d: Cook apple, pepper and curry in butter until pepper is tender. Add soup and water.
Heat.
Stir now and then.
Makes 2 to 3 servings.
Mash some dough together with your hands.
Then you smash it. Cook it in a pan on the stove 5 minutes.
Then you smash them to decorate them.
Then you eat them.

i: 6 large skinless boneless chicken breast halves (about 2 lb.)
d: About 40 minutes before serving, in small bowl with fork, mix Feta cheese, lemon juice and oregano until smooth. On work surface, with meat mallet or dull edge of French knife, pound each chicken breast half to 1/2-inch thickness. With knife or small metal spatula, spread cheese mixture over each breast half to within 1/2-inch of edge. Fold chicken breasts crosswise in half to enclose filling; secure with toothpick. On sheet of waxed paper, mix salt, pepper and 1 tablespoon flour; use to coat chicken. In 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat in hot olive or salad oil, cook chicken until golden brown on both sides, turning once.
Fry onion in oil.
Set aside.
Cook broccoli as directed. Drain and set aside.
Melt oleo.
Grease 8 x 8-inch pan.
Mix cornbread mix and eggs.

i: 2 chopped green sweet peppers (if you want peppers in it)
d: Let cucumbers, onion, peppers and salt stand 3 hours in salt water.
In large pan, put sugar, vinegar, mustard seed, celery seed and turmeric; let all this come to a hard boil.
Put all this, cucumber, onions and peppers in this.
Let cook until tender, then can in jars and seal.
Take out of salt water, then put it in mixture to boil.
Cream shortening and add sugar, egg and molasses.
Add dry ingredients and hot water alternately.
Bake at 350° for 45 to 50 minutes.
About 1 1/2 hours before serving on wax paper, combine flour, salt and pepper. Roll meat in it.

i: 1 c. instant tea (plain; no sugar or lemon added)
3 oz. Kool-Aid brand lemonade (packaged with sugar)
d: Mix together.
Adjust mixture to your taste.
To use, add 2 teaspoons of mixture to 6 ounces hot water.
Cook and drain asparagus; arrange in shallow dish.
Mix all other ingredients except egg and gherkins in jar; shake well to combine.
Pour over asparagus.
Refrigerate at least 1 hour, turning spears several times.
Arrange on platter, sprinkle with finely chopped egg and gherkins.
Serves 8.
Cut margarine into flour and powdered sugar until crumbly. Pack into a 13 x 9-inch pan. Bake at 350° for 15 minutes.
Melt oleo; slice bread in finger size slices.
Dip in melted oleo and set aside.
Drain cooked asparagus and place in a long casserole dish.
Cut water chestnuts and place on top.
(I use the juice too.)
Put cheese on that, then cover with mushroom soup. Cover with bread slices.

i: 2 c. toasted pecans, walnuts or peanuts
d: Melt chocolate and peanut butter in microwave or double boiler, stir often to mix well.
Add remaining ingredients; stir with wooden spoon to coat evenly.
Drop by teaspoons onto waxed paper. Yields 11 dozen.
Pour milk over shortening, sugar and salt.
Beat in 1 cup flour, softened yeast, eggs and 1 more cup flour.
Cover with greased wax paper.
Let rise 15 minutes.

i: 1 Tbsp. flour, cornstarch or cream potatoes (your choice)
d: Use 1 cup of stir-fry vegetables.
Heat until soft, then add flour, cornstarch or leftover cream potatoes (your choice).
Add chopped onion to vegetables.
Salt and pepper.
Serve as open-faced sandwich or closed sandwich using 2 slices of your favorite bread.
Preheat oven to 375°.
Place pastry on sugar sprinkled chopping board.
Sprinkle pastry with 1 tablespoon sugar and finely chopped nuts.
Roll pastry into 12 x 6-inch rectangle; add more sugar to keep pastry coated.
Cut rectangle crosswise into 1/4-inch thick strips.
Twist strips and arrange them on lightly greased baking sheet.

i: 1 box yellow or chocolate cake mix
d: Preheat oven at 350°.
Mix cake mix with 1 egg and butter, melted.
Press into a 9 x 12 cake pan that has been greased and floured.
Then mix 2 eggs, cream cheese, 1 box powdered sugar and vanilla together.
Use a blender until all mixed, then pour over cake in pan and bake about 35 to 40 minutes at 350° or until you can stick a toothpick in and it comes out clean.
Cool, then cut into squares and eat.

i: 1 tsp. each: cinnamon, cloves and nutmeg
d: Mix Crisco with applesauce, nuts and raisins.
Sift dry ingredients and add.
Mix well.
Put in a greased and floured loaf pan or tube pan.
Bake in loaf pan at 350° to 375° for 45 to 60 minutes, layer pan at 375° for 20 minutes or tube pan at 325° for 1 hour.
Boil and cool.
Pour over sliced onions and cucumbers.
Store in refrigerator.
Makes about 1 gallon.
Cream butter with sugar.
Add eggs, beat well.
Add honey, salt and vanilla.
Combine baking powder and flour.
Add to creamed mixture.
Drop by half teaspoons onto buttered cookie sheet.
Mix filling in order given.
Pour into pie shells.
Bake in preheated 425° oven for 15 minutes.
Reduce heat to 350° and continue baking for 30 to 35 minutes or until knife inserted in center of pie filling comes out clean.
Let cool.
Top with Cool Whip.
Pack cucumbers and onion in jars.
Combine and bring to boil while stirring the sugar, vinegar, salt, mustard seed, celery seed, and turmeric.
Pour over cucumbers and onions.
Seal and keep in refrigerator.
Melt Crisco shortening in 4-quart saucepan over low heat. Remove and let cool.
Add sugar, molasses and egg to cooled shortening; beat well.
Sift together flour, soda, ginger, cloves, cinnamon and salt.
Add to first mixture.
Mix well.
Chill 2 hours.

i: 1 lb. fresh or frozen fish fillets
d: Thaw fish if frozen.
Cut fish into 1-inch pieces.
In a large saucepan, cook green onion, onion and garlic in hot oil until tender.
Add tomatoes, tomato sauce, wine, parsley, oregano, basil, salt and pepper.
Bring to boiling, then reduce heat. Cover and simmer 20 minutes.
Add fish, shrimp and undrained clams.

i: 1 (2 1/2 lb.) ready to cook chicken, cut up
d: Mix salt, garlic, oregano, tomato sauce, tomatoes, peppers, onion, achiote, olives and capers.
Add the chicken and cook for a few minutes.
Add the rice and water.
Stir and cook until the water has been absorbed, then cover until the rice is done.
Serve hot.
Garnish with strips of pimentos and peas.

i: 2 pkg. Ramen noodles (do not use flavored)
d: Cut cabbage lengthwise and crosswise (slice thin).
Add onions and chill.
Brown noodles in oleo.
Add sunflower seeds and almonds.
Saute until light brown; drain on paper towel.
I do this ahead of time and store in plastic container.
Mix and serve over ice.
Melt butter in a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish; set aside. Combine flour and next 5 ingredients.
Combine egg and buttermilk. Dip chicken in egg mixture, coating well.
Place chicken in baking dish, turning once to coat with butter.
Sprinkle with chopped pecans.
Bake chicken at 350° for 30 minutes.
Serves 8.
Process first three ingredients in food processor.
Sprinkle sugar over mixture and let stand.
Combine remaining ingredients and bring to a boil on high heat.
Pour over cabbage mixture while still hot.
Cover and refrigerate.
Will stay good for several days.
Stir all ingredients together.
Pour in 9-inch unbaked pie shell.
Bake for 1 hour at 300°.
Chill before cutting.
This is good served with the main meal and/or as a dessert.
Brown ground chuck.
Add onion to meat when almost done and cook until onion is tender.
Drain grease.
In small bowl, combine flour, chili powder, salt and sugar, mixing well.
Add this to the meat and onion, stirring to coat meat.
Add tomato paste and enough water to desired consistency.
Cream together sugar and margarine.
Stir in egg and molasses. Add vinegar; beat well.
Stir in sifted dry ingredients.
Mix well.
Chill overnight.
Place one small ball on cookie sheet; flatten with palm to make head.
Mix ingredients.
Refrigerate several hours before serving.
Soak bread crumbs in milk; add meat, eggs, onion and seasonings.
Mix well.
Form in individual loaves; place in greased muffin pans.
Cover with Piquant Sauce.
Bake in a moderate 350° oven for 45 minutes.
Makes 8 servings.
Mix the mustard, flour, oil and turmeric together with 1 pint cold vinegar.
Stir into 6 pints of vinegar and boil.
Add vegetables and cook until tender.
Put in jars and seal well.
In large saucepan or kettle having a lid, brown meat in oil. Add salt, spices and onion soup mix.
Stir in boiling water, tomato sauce and soy sauce.
Cook and simmer about 15 minutes.

i: 1 piece fresh or dried grated ginger (1 1/2-inches long)
d: Combine all ingredients.
Good on spareribs or chicken.
Saute onion and uncooked rice in mayonnaise until onion is tender.
Blend in the curry powder and currants.
Stir in broth. Cover and cook about 20 minutes or until rice is tender.
Let cool.
Fry hamburger with onion.
Layer corn tortillas (cut in 1/4) in pan.
Put 1 teaspoon hamburger on each piece, 1 teaspoon green chili on top of hamburger, then 1 teaspoon tomato sauce, top with grated cheese.
Repeat layers until all ingredients are gone.
Pour can of milk over all.
Cover with foil.
Mix cucumbers, onion and green pepper slices with salt and let stand, covered, for 1 hour.
Blend together the sugar, vinegar and celery seed.
Drain the cucumber mixture and pour the sugar mixture over them.
Place in a covered container in the refrigerator.
Enjoy a very low calorie food.
Serves 10 to 15.
Put salt over cucumbers and onion.
Let set for 3 hours. Drain off.
Mix all ingredients together.
Put pickles in large jar and pour mixture over pickles.
Put in refrigerator.
Use as needed.
Mix in saucepan.
Let it simmer for about 2 hours.
Serve with pork.
Combine first 4 ingredients and cook until thick (about 12 minutes).
Add last 2 ingredients.

t: Fettucini With Tomatoes, Basil And Feta Cheese
d: Mix tomatoes, Prosciutto, basil, olive oil, vinegar and garlic in large bowl.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Set aside for 1/2 hour.
Cook pasta in boiling water until tender.
Drain.
Add pasta to tomato mixture.
First Layer:
Mix graham cracker crumbs with melted oleo and press into bottom of 9 x 13-inch pan.

i: 1 lb. chicken, cut in small pieces
1 lb. rabbit, cut in small pieces
1/2 lb. pork, chopped, boned and cut in small pieces
d: In a paella pan or large skillet, fry chicken and rabbit. Remove.
In same pan, cook pork.
Remove.
In pan, saute onion, green pepper and garlic.
Add to the vegetables and chopped tomatoes.
Add parsley.
Saute butter, shrimp and garlic on medium heat.
Add basil and lemon pepper.
When almost cooked, add mushrooms and soup.
Add 1/2 can of water.
Simmer for 10 minutes.
Pour over angel hair pasta.
Place Cheerios in soup bowl.
Add milk and sugar; stir and eat.
Makes 1 serving.
Slice squash and onion and let soak in salt water for 2 hours. Boil rest of ingredients and pour over squash and let set for another 2 hours.
Boil for 5 minutes and put in jars.

i: 1 cut up fryer or 12 chicken legs or wings
3 Tbsp. or more sugar to taste
d: Precook chicken in very little water.
Combine broth with soy sauce, sugar and garlic.
Simmer and mix.
Add chicken and baste often until sauce thickens to heavy syrup consistency.

i: 1 lb. boneless beef shoulder steak, about 1-inch thick
d: Combine onions, parsley, vinegar, oil, mustard, garlic and thyme.
Place beef chuck shoulder steak in plastic bag.
Add marinade, spreading evenly over both sides.
Close bag securely and marinate in refrigerator for 6 to 8 hours, turning at least once.
Pour off marinade.
Discard.
Mix all ingredients together and pour over chicken. Let set as little as 1 hour or overnight for really juicy chicken.
Blend all ingredients (except chicken).
Add chicken, soak for 20 minutes and deep fry.

i: 2 lb. beef tip, cut into 1-inch cubes
d: Note:
Marinate meat in wine, soy sauce and crushed garlic overnight.
Meat will be very tender.

i: 24 ears corn, blanched and cut off
d: Boil corn, sugar, vinegar, mustard, turmeric and celery seed for 20 minutes.
Grind
onions
and
peppers
and

i: about 2 c. chopped chicken or turkey
d: Mix first 5 ingredients together and add to chopped chicken or turkey.
Top with crushed Ritz crackers. Chip butter on top and bake at 300° for 30 to 40 minutes.

i: 1 qt. bottle Jero pina colada mix
d: Mix together and freeze in an empty ice cream pail.
To serve, place 2 to 3 scoops of slush in glass and fill with 7-Up.
Cut eggs in half length wise. Remove yolk-place in bowl. Add remaining ingredients and mix well. Fill eggs and enjoy.
I add 2 red peppers or 1 fresh jalapeno.
Slice cauliflower and broccoli into tiny bite size pieces. Dice celery.
Thaw peas and add.

i: 10 oz. boneless, skinless chicken breasts, sliced into 1/2 inch strips
1 c. undiluted lite evaporated skim milk
1/2 c. (2 oz.) shredded, reduced fat Swiss cheese
1 pkg. (10 oz.) frozen broccoli spears, thawed, drained and cut into bite size pieces
d: Sprinkle chicken with Italian seasoning. Pound between sheets of plastic wrap. Spray nonstick skillet with nonstick cooking spray. Saute chicken strips just until no longer pink. Keep warm. In small saucepan, whisk small amount of evaporated milk into flour. Stir in remaining milk, garlic, salt and pepper. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until mixture just comes to a boil and thickens. Add cheese; stir until melted. Spray 10 x 6 x 2 inch baking dish with nonstick cooking spray. Spread about 1/4 c. sauce in bottom of dish. Arrange broccoli over sauce; top with chicken pieces. Pour remaining sauce over top. Sprinkle with paprika. Cover. Bake in preheated 350° oven for 20 minutes or until heated through.
Boil chicken, peel off skin.
Remove from bone.
Mix soup, tomatoes, bell pepper, onion, garlic powder, chili powder and 1/2 cup of cheese.
Mix well.
Grease large Pyrex pan, line bottom with Doritos and then place chicken on top of Doritos.
Pour mixed ingredients over chicken.
Toss first 5 ingredients.
Combine remaining ingredients and chill.
Mix together before serving.
Makes 5 large servings.

i: 1/2 c. water or liquid from string beans
d: Brown ground beef and onion in dry skillet and drain grease. Add remaining ingredients.
Cover and simmer for 30 to 40 minutes, until vegetables are tender.
Add a can of cut green beans during last ten minutes.
Serves 6; freezes well.

i: 12 chicken pieces (6 breasts, 3 drumsticks and 3 thighs)
d: Wash and pat chicken parts with paper towel.
Sprinkle each piece with salt and pepper.
Dredge in flour, mixed with paprika. Melt butter in skillet and brown chicken pieces.
Arrange chicken in an oblong dish.

i: 1 lb. sea scallops, cut into quarters
d: Saute vegetables in olive oil.
Add sea scallops and cook until opaque (10 to 15 minutes).
Add lemon juice, vinegar, horseradish and hot sauce.
Mix with cooked rice.
Serves 2.

i: 1 large bottle Kraft Zesty Italian dressing
1 bottle Schilling or McCormick salad seasoning
d: Mix together and marinate overnight.

i: 16 whole cloves (if small, add a few more)
1 tsp. whole allspice or 1 tsp. powdered
d: Combine cider, cranberry juice, orange juice and sugar in a large cooking pan.
Tie spices in a cheesecloth bag.
Simmer all over low heat for about 1/2 hour; leave spices in until next morning.
This may be put into a crock-pot on low heat to keep warm while serving.

i: 1 1/2 lb. round steak (1-inch thick), cut into strips
1 can drained tomatoes, cut up (save liquid)
d: Roll steak strips in flour.
Brown in skillet.
Salt and pepper.
Combine tomato liquid, water, onions and browned steak. Cover and simmer for one and a quarter hours.
Uncover and stir in Worcestershire sauce.
Add tomatoes, green peppers and simmer for 5 minutes.
Brown sausage; drain.
Brown ground beef, onion and green pepper until brown and onion is tender.
Add garlic.
Add remaining ingredients; simmer, uncovered, until thickened.
Serve over hot, fluffy rice.

i: 1 c. Italian bread crumbs (may take more)
d: Mix ingredients together; roll into walnut size balls.
Bake on cookie sheet at 350° until done (approximately 30 to 45 minutes).
Prepare the sauce while meat balls are baking.

i: 3/4 c. plus 1 Tbsp. warm water (105~ to 115~)
d: Dissolve yeast in measuring cup with warm water.
Stir together flour and salt in large mixing bowl.
Cut shortening into flour with pastry blender or two knives until mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Stir in dissolved yeast.
Turn out onto lightly floured board or pastry cloth and knead just until smooth.
Place dough in large greased bowl; turn once to grease top.

i: 1(16 oz.) container Mascarpone cream (can be purchased at an Italian specialty store)
2 tsp. instant coffee (expresso coffee with a little sugar is best if you have it)
Savoiardi biscuits (can be found at the Food Emporium)
d: In a bowl, beat cream, confectioners sugar, coffee liqueur, vanilla extract and 2/3 of grated chocolate.
Set aside remaining chocolate for top of dessert.
In another bowl, beat whipping cream until peaks form.
With rubber spatula, fold cream into Mascarpone mixture.
In a third bowl, mix coffee, liqueur, half teaspoon vanilla extract.
Dip Savoiardi biscuits into this mixture and line a cake pan (glass) or trifle bowl.
In a large frying pan, brown ground beef with onion, garlic and bell pepper.
Drain excess grease.
In a large saucepan (4-quart), combine remaining ingredients.
Refill tomato paste and sauce cans with water and add.
Add ground beef mixture.
Simmer on low heat for about 45 minutes.
In a bowl, beat egg white and water until frothy, not stiff. Add pecans and mix until nuts are covered.
Mix sugar, salt and cinnamon in
a small bowl and pour over pecans. Stir well with wooden spoon until pecans are coated.

i: 1 can (8 1/2 oz.) water chestnuts, sliced and drained
d: Bring water to boiling in large saucepan; add celery and peas. Cover; cook 5 minutes or until celery is almost tender.
Drain and leave vegetables in saucepan.
Add salt, pepper, soy sauce, wine and water chestnuts to vegetables in saucepan.
Toss together.
Cook 2 minutes longer.

Brown hamburger; season with pepper, seasoning salt and garlic powder to taste. Set meat aside. Combine ingredients in a 5-quart stockpot. Bring to boiling. Reduce heat; cover and simmer for 20 minutes. Add meat and simmer for 20 minutes longer. Remove bay leaf.

i: 1/2 c. presweetened lemon flavored instant tea
d: Mix together thoroughly.
Use about 1 teaspoon for each serving or 1/4 cup for a quart.
Good hot or cold.
Chop onions, bell pepper and celery.
Brown in small amount of olive oil.
Add ground beef and stir until brown.
Drain well. Add remaining ingredients and simmer slowly until most of the liquid has been cooked out.
Sauce cannot be cooked too long.
Trim ends of cucumbers and scrape with fork to make a decorative pattern.
Quarter the cucumbers lengthwise and remove seeds.
Cut them crosswise into thin slices.
Put in bowl and add chili.
In small saucepan, combine sugar and vinegar; bring to boil.
Cook, stirring, until the sugar has dissolved.
Spray a 1-quart casserole dish with Pam.
Layer rice and meat, diced onion and grated cheese.
Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.
Brown ground beef and sausage in a large skillet.
Drain off fat.
Melt Velveeta slowly over low heat.
When cheese is melted, stir in salsa.
Serve warm with nacho chips.
Combine sherbet, orange juice and lemon juice in electric blender.
Blend until smooth.
Pour in glasses and garnish with rolled half-slice of orange, kiwi wedge and fresh raspberry on skewer.

i: Cut 1 each, medium eggplant, onion, red pepper and zucchini into chunks
1/4 c. Kraft Special Collections Sun-Dried Tomato Dressing
1 can (16 oz.) undrained low-sodium diced tomatoes
2 Tbsp. reduced fat Parmesan cheese, shredded
1/2 c. reduced fat Mozzarella cheese, shredded
d: Saute vegetables in sun-dried tomato dressing in large ovenproof skillet until tender.
Add diced tomatoes, cook about 15 minutes, allowing some of the liquid to evaporate.
Top with cheeses and bake in 350° oven for 15 minutes.
Brown beef and onions about 10 minutes.
Stir in tomato sauce, kidney beans, chili powder, salt and pepper.
Break taco shells in 1/2 at fold.
Arrange on bottom of 13 x 9-inch baking dish.
Spoon meat mix over tacos and sprinkle with cheese.
Bake at 350° for 15 to 20 minutes or until hot.
Combine mustard, honey, curry and lemon juice.
Brush onto scallops.
Broil under low heat for 8 to 10 minutes.
Turn, and brush scallops again.
Cook until lightly brown.
Place scrambled eggs in center of the tortillas.
Crumble bacon or sausage over the eggs.
Then top with remaining ingredients.
Fold up bottom of tortilla, then roll up from the side.
Heat oil in saucepan.
Add onion, green pepper, celery and garlic and saute over medium heat until tender; do not brown.
Add tomatoes, tomato puree, bay leaf, thyme, salt and pepper.
Bring to a boil; reduce heat and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours.
Taste for seasoning.
May need some sugar to cut acidity of tomato products.

i: 2 (16 oz.) can French style green beans
d: Fry bacon and saute onion. Drain and add next four ingredients. Simmer for 2 minutes. Pour over green beans. Crumble 1 slice of fried bacon over top of beans and bake 350 degrees for 30 minutes.

i: 1 (20 oz.) can crushed pineapple with juice
d: Beat by hand until blended.
Add 1 (10 ounce) jar maraschino cherries, cut in half and drained, and 1 cup chopped nuts.
Pour into greased and floured 9 x 13-inch pan.
Bake in a 350° oven for 45 minutes.
Cream cheese icing is optional.
In a greased casserole, make a layer of each ingredient in the order listed.
Season each layer.
Bake for 2 hours at 350°. Serves 4 to 6.
Combine first 8 ingredients in salad bowl until smooth.
Add remaining; mix thoroughly.
Chill.

i: 1 (8 oz.) can mushroom stems and pieces, drained
1 (10 3/4 oz.) can cream of chicken soup
1 (8 oz.) pkg. egg noodles, cooked and drained
d: Cook ground beef and onion until beef is brown.
Stir in flour, salt, garlic salt, pepper and mushrooms.
Cook over medium heat for 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
Stir soup into meat mixture.
Simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Serve over noodles.

i: 1 (8 oz.) can tomato sauce (no salt added)
d: Heat oil in skillet; working in batches, add steaks and brown 2 minutes, turning once.
Remove to plate.
Add green onion, garlic and cumin; cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato sauce and salsa. Bring to simmering; add meat to skillet and cook 1 to 3 minutes or just until heated.
Drain crabmeat.
Spread layer of croutons in buttered 9-inch square casserole.
Sprinkle with crabmeat, then cheese.
Layer 2 times, ending with cheese layer.
Beat eggs; add milk and seasonings.
Pour over casserole.
Melt butter in skillet, add onion and celery, slowly cooking for 10 minutes.
Add other ingredients and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
Combine dry ingredients in a medium size mixing bowl and cut in shortening.
Make a well in center of dry ingredients.
Add water to dry ingredients and work into a dough.
Knead dough until smooth; cover and set aside for 20 minutes.
Heat 2 inches of shortening in a heavy pan at medium-high heat (425°).
Roll dough to a 1/8-inch thickness on a lightly floured board.
Grind oatmeal in blender until fine.
Add honey, salt, flour and soda.
Stir. Cut in butter. Add buttermilk and stir.
Roll very thin.
Cut with animal cookie cutters.
Bake at 400° until brown (10 to 12 minutes).
Mix ingredients well and bake for 1 hour or until done at 350°.
Bake cake in two layers according to directions on box.
Prick holes in layers.
Mix jello according to directions on box and spoon jello over cake layers until moist.
Layer Cool Whip between layers and ice entire cake with Cool Whip.
Combine all ingredients except milk and salad oil.
Dip chicken in milk and coat with crumb mixture.
Place in shallow roasting pan, skin side up (do not grease pan).
Bake at 375° for 30 minutes.
Brush with salad oil; bake 30 more minutes, or until nicely browned and tender.

t: Basic Mexican Bean Recipe(Makes 6 To 8 Servings)
i: 1 lb. dried pink or red beans
1/2 c. very hot lard or bacon drippings (about)
d: Wash beans and soak overnight in water to cover.
The next morning, if necessary, add more water to cover.
Add salt and simmer for 1 1/2 to 2 hours or until the beans are very tender.
Boil potatoes until tender.
Drain and cut lengthwise into quarters.
Arrange potatoes on platter, sprinkle with salt and pepper, olive oil, vinegar, scallions and oregano.
Garnish with parsley and serve immediately.
Serves 6.
Cook butter, cinnamon, allspice, nutmeg and brown sugar. Put a spoonful in each apple; cover apples with syrup.

i: 1 to 2 Tbsp. sugar or 1 packet Sweet 'N Low (optional)
2 egg whites, slightly beaten or 1/4 c. egg substitute
d: Combine dry ingredients.
Combine milk, egg and oil.
Blend all ingredients.
Use 1/3 cup for each cake.
Bake on a hot, nonstick griddle, sprayed with olive oil cooking spray.
Dice and peel apples.
Add sugar, raisins and walnuts.
Let set until rest is mixed.
In bowl, mix eggs, oil and vanilla. Beat well, then add flour, soda, cinnamon and salt.
Stir egg mixture into apple mixture.
Stir well by hand.
Sift of sugar with flour 4 times.
Beat whites to a foam.
Add cream of tartar and salt and beat till it holds up peaks.
Add remaining sugar and flavoring.
Fold in flour.

i: 1 can milk (empty soup and use can to measure milk)
d: Cook spaghetti noodles as directed.
In frying pan, brown hamburger with onions. Add cream cheese, tomato soup, milk and olives.
Cook until thick and bubbly, then add spaghetti noodles. Put into large glass dish and cook in oven at 400° for 45 minutes.

i: 2 pkg. chopped, frozen spinach, cooked and drained completely
d: Mix all ingredients together.
Refrigerate at least one hour. Roll into balls and bake on a greased cookie sheet at 350° for 15 to 20 minutes.

Combine ground beef, onions and water in saucepan. Simmer until beef turns brown. Add tomatoes, vinegar and seasonings. Cover, simmer 3 hours, stirring occasionally. Fat will float. If time, chill chili and lift off fat layer; if not, spoon off fat.
Boil 1 1/2 cups boiling water, cloves, cinnamon, ginger and tea
bags,	then
simmer.
Let steep 15 minutes.
In large pan, boil
Boil chicken.
Debone.
Add 2 cans cream of chicken soup to broth.
Add noodle dumplings.
Cook until tender.
Add salt and pepper to taste.

i: 1/2 lb. Monterey Jack cheese, shredded (2 cups)
d: In 10-inch skillet over medium heat, in hot salad oil, fry 1 tortilla at a time, about 30 seconds on each side until lightly browned and blistered. With tongs, remove tortilla to paper towels to drain. Discard any remaining oil.

i: 1 Tbsp. dried thyme and basil, crumbled
d: Place all the ingredients in a 1-pint jar.
Cover tightly and shake well to mix.
Store in a cool, dark, dry place for up to 2 months.
Makes about 1 1/2 cups.
Slice steak in narrow strips.
Brown steak in 2 tablespoons oil.
Combine with garlic, salt, pepper, soy sauce and sugar for 6 to 8 hours in crock-pot on low.
Turn to high and add vegetables. Dissolve 1 tablespoon cornstarch in 2 tablespoons cold water. Stir into pot.
Cook on high 15 to 20 minutes or until thickened. Serve over noodles or rice.

i: 4 oz. can black Turtle beans, rinsed and drained
1 c. prepared thick and chunky mild salsa
d: In food processor combine beans and garlic and process, using on and off motion until smooth (do not puree).
Transfer to bowl and stir in salsa.
Serve this dip with vegetables or toasted tortilla pieces.
Spread crushed pineapple in the bottom of baking dish. Spread cherry pie filling over the pineapple.
Spread yellow cake mix over pineapple and cherry filling.
Melt margarine and drizzle over the other ingredients.
Bake at 350° until golden brown on top.

i: generous sprinkle of Mrs. Dash or Lawry's seasoning with no salt
d: Mix all ingredients.
Form into 4 patties.
Broil on each side until brown.
Less calories than frying.
Marinate water chestnuts in soy sauce 1 hour.
Cut chestnuts in half.
Roll in white sugar.
Cut bacon in thirds, wrap chestnuts in bacon, anchor with toothpick.

i: 4 lamb chops (3/4-inch thick), trimmed of fat
d: Prick the lamb chops with sharp fork, then place in shallow bowl.
In a small bowl, combine lemon juice, mint, paprika, cinnamon, nutmeg and garlic.
Pour over chops, turning once so entire surface is covered with the marinade.
Refrigerate at least 30 minutes.
Drain chops.
Grill or broil 5 minutes on each side. Serve over rice.

i: 1 (28 oz.) can whole tomatoes, cut up
d: Place pork in shallow roasting pan.
Combine undrained tomatoes with rest of ingredients.
Pour over meat.
Bake, covered, in a 350° oven for 4 to 5 hours, until meat is tender. Uncover.
Bake 1 hour more.
Combine flour, soda, salt, sugar and cinnamon.
Add eggs and oil, stirring until moistened.
Do not beat.
Stir in 1 1/2 teaspoons vanilla, pineapple, bananas and 1 cup pecans.
Pour into 3 round, greased cake pans.
Bake at 350° for 25 minutes.

i: 6 skinless, boneless chicken breast halves (about 1 1/2 lb.)
d: In a 3-quart rectangular baking dish, combine honey, water, mustard, melted margarine or butter, Cajun seasoning, curry powder, lemon juice and garlic powder; mix well.
Add chicken, turning to coat.
Arrange in a single layer.
Bake, uncovered, in a 350° oven for about 30 minutes or until chicken is tender and no longer pink.
Bring water and ruta to boil.
Add all ingredients except whiskey.
Squeeze orange, lemon and lime into water.
After it boils, let it simmer until the flavor is in the water.
Add whiskey and bring to a boil.
Remove immediately and let cool overnight. Pour in bottle.
Mix 1 cup boiling water, sugar and cornstarch.
Cook until thick.
Add Jell-O and mix well.
Let cool and pour over sliced strawberries in a baked pie shell.
Top with Cool Whip.

i: 1 Tbsp. La Choy brown gravy sauce
2 (16 oz.) cans La Choy bean sprouts, drained and rinsed
d: Brown meat and onion in butter.
Add salt, pepper, celery and water.
Cover and simmer 20 minutes.
Add bean sprouts.
Blend cornstarch with 2 tablespoons water, then add soy sauce, sugar and brown gravy sauce.
Stir into meat-vegetable mixture and heat until thickened.
Cook macaroni, onion and pepper until macaroni is yellow.
Add ground beef to skillet along with rest of ingredients and cover. Simmer 15 minutes until thick.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Combine graham cracker crumbs, sugar, cocoa and margarine; press firmly on bottom of a 13 x 9-inch baking pan.
Bake 10 minutes.

i: 1/2 c. finely chopped fresh chives or scallions
d: Break eggs in a large bowl.
Add 2 tablespoons of oil, salt and pepper.
Beat well.
Add chopped herbs and greens.
Mix well.

i: 1 c. thinly sliced blanched almonds, coarsely broken
1 tsp. anise flavoring or 1/4 tsp. oil of anise
d: Sift together the flour, baking powder and salt.
In a large mixing bowl, cream butter, sugar and anise flavoring; beat in eggs, one at a time.
Add flour mixture and almonds.
Turn out on a floured pastry cloth and knead gently until smooth.
Divide dough in half.
Shape each half into a roll about 1 1/2 inches in diameter.

i: 12 oz. penne or other small tubular pasta
1/4 c. packed fresh basil leaves, chopped
1/4 tsp. bottled red pepper flakes or up to 1 tsp.
d: For pasta, drop penne into kettle of boiling water; return to boil and boil gently for 8 to 10 minutes or until tender.
Drain; toss with 1 tablespoon olive oil.
Return to pan and keep warm. For sauce, saute garlic slowly in 3 tablespoons olive oil in skillet until softened, but not browned.
Add olives, basil, red pepper and tomato sauce.
Heat.
Stir in cheeses and heat, stirring until chunks begin to melt.
Whisk together the milk, cream and egg yolks.
Combine the sugar and cornstarch in heavy saucepan.
Whisk in the yolk mixture and cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes. Then stir the mixture constantly until it comes to a boil, about 7 minutes more.
Boil, stirring, 1 minute.
Remove the saucepan from heat and stir in the vanilla, butter and bananas.
Pour into baked pie shell and smooth top.

i: 1 (22 oz.) red pack whole tomatoes in puree
d: Combine the green beans, oil, salt, pepper and onion in large skillet.
Cook 10 minutes.
Add tomatoes.
Add 2 tablespoons sugar.
Simmer until tender.

i: 1/2 tsp. turmeric mixed with 1 Tbsp. flour
d: Mix first 4 ingredients.
Let set 1 hour.
Drain.
Mix together with rest of ingredients and cook until tender.
Drain off excess juice.
Pack in jars.
Slice or wedge tomatoes and arrange in shallow dish.
Combine remaining ingredients and mix well.
Pour over tomatoes.
Chill, covered, one hour.
Stir together the flour, baking powder, salt and nutmeg. Combine the lemon peel, eggs, honey and oil.
Stir in the strawberries and almonds.
Add mixture all at once to flour mixture and stir only until flour is moistened.
Pour into greased 8 x 4-inch loaf pan.
Bake at 350° for 50 to 55 minutes.
Cool in pan for 15 minutes before turning onto rack.

i: 1 oz. packet original Ranch party dip
d: Mix packet of dip and sour cream. Add cheese and bacon bits. Mix well. Chill 1 hour before serving.

i: 1 (10 oz.) can cream of mushroom soup
1 (10 oz.) can cream of chicken soup
1 to 2 c. cheese of your choice
d: Cook spaghetti and drain.
Mix all other ingredients together.
Add to spaghetti, place in a 13x9 casserole dish.
Cook for 30 minutes at 350 degrees.
In a pot, lightly brown onion in oil.
Add meat and sprinkle with paprika and salt.
Cover pot, stirring frequently.
Stew the meat until tender, then add rice and mix well.
Add hot water to cover.
Slowly boil in covered pot until soup boils away and rice becomes soft.
Combine all ingredients.
Mix well.
Cover batter and store in refrigerator. Use as needed.
Bake at 350° for 20 minutes.
Remove from muffin tin at once.

i: 10 c. Zucchini - grind and let stand overnight with
d: Drain the zucchini and rinse thoroughly with cold water.
Add the vinegar, sugar, turmeric, dry mustard, cornstarch, celery seed, pepper, and bell peppers.
Mix well and cook together for 30 minutes in a large pan.
Ladle into hot, sterilized jars and put the lids on.
Process in a hot water bath about 10 minutes.

i: tahini (can be purchased in natural food store)
d: Boil the garbanzo beans in a pot (water should cover the beans).
When the beans are cooked and soft, remove from the stove and let it cool down.
Grind the garbanzo beans in a blender with the water left after boiling; add lemon juice.
Add salt and 6 spoonfuls tahini.
Put in Pyrex.
Add red pepper, olive oil and parsley (chopped) on top.
Combine cider, ginger ale and juices.
Add ice and fruit slices.
Serve.

i: 1 chicken breast (for 2 people; skinless or skin on)
white or black pepper to taste
d: Rinse chicken with cold water; pat dry with paper towels. Place skin side down on broiler pan.
Squeeze about 1 teaspoon lemon juice on each piece of chicken.
Sprinkle on lightly the pepper, garlic powder, salt and paprika.
Place broiler pan 6 to 8-inches below broiler element or gas flame.
Broil chicken until crispy.
Turn pieces of chicken over; apply lemon and spices on skin side and broil until done next to bone and surface is crispy.

i: 2 (20 oz.)( cans chunk pineapple, drained but reserve liquid
d: Butter a 2-quart casserole.
Put 1 can of pineapple on bottom of dish.
Mix cheeses together and sprinkle half of cheese mixture on top of pineapple.
Mix flour and sugar and sprinkle on cheese. Follow with second can of pineapple and the remaining half of flour-sugar mixture, leaving remaining half of cheese mixture for top.
Pour 1/2 cup pineapple juice over entire dish and dot with butter.
Bake at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes until bubbly and brown.

t: B.B.  King'S Beale Street Blues Sauce
d: In a large saucepan, combine ingredients.
Bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 1 hour.
Remove bay leaves. Serve warm.
Refrigerate unused sauce for up to several weeks.

i: 1 (3 1/2 oz.) box instant pistachio pudding mix
d: Drain pineapple and reserve juice.
Place pudding mix in large bowl.
Add about 4 tablespoons of the pineapple juice to make a soft paste.
Mix well.
Add drained pineapple, whipped topping, marshmallows and chopped nuts.
Mix well until mixture is fluffy and increases in volume.
Mix bananas and butter together until creamy; add eggs.
Beat well.
Combine flour, baking powder, soda and cinnamon.
Add to banana mixture alternately with water, beating well after each addition.
Stir in nuts.
Spoon into a greased baking pan.
Mix last four ingredients.
Add sliced beets.
Put in can. If desired, hard-boiled eggs can be immersed in juice.
Combine 1/2 cup sugar and water.
Bring to a boil; stir until sugar dissolves.
Boil, covered, over medium heat, for 5 minutes. Beat egg whites until stiff; gradually add remaining 1/4 cup sugar. Slowly pour hot syrup into whites; beat constantly until mixture has cooled.
Pare and puree kiwi fruit to measure 3/4 cup. Gradually beat lemon-lime soda, kiwi fruit puree and lime juice into egg white mixture.
Partially freeze.
Spoon mixture into chilled mixer bowl and beat with chilled beaters until mixture is light and foamy.
Mix flour, salt and pepper in bag.
Rinse chicken and pat dry. Place in bag; shake to coat well.
Brown chicken on 1 side in 1 tablespoon olive oil in skillet for 5 minutes; turn chicken.
Add remaining olive oil.
Cook for 5 minutes until brown.
Remove chicken.

i: 2 ripe bananas (1 c.), peeled and mashed
d: Grease loaf pan.
Line bottom with wax paper.
Grease paper. Sift flour, baking soda, salt and baking powder onto another sheet of waxed paper.
Brown onion and ground beef.
Drain off fat.
Add soup, catsup and mustard.
Heat until thoroughly warm.
Season to taste.
Serve on buns or over rice.

i: 1 (15 1/4 oz.) can whole kernel corn, drained
1 (2 1/4 oz.) can sliced ripe olives, drained
d: In a skillet, cook beef over medium heat until no longer pink; drain. In a bowl, combine the egg, milk and cornmeal until smooth. Add corn, tomatoes, olives, chili seasoning, seasoned salt and beef. Transfer to a greased slow cooker. Cover and cook on high for 3 hours and 45 minutes. Sprinkle with cheese; cover and cook 15 minutes longer or until cheese is melted. Yield: 6 servings.
Mix Burgundy, cider and spices to taste.
Heat before serving. Float orange slices in wassail.
Melt butter and mix with sugar.
Add well beaten eggs.
Sift together cocoa and flour.
Mix well. Add vanilla and nuts. Cook at 300° for 45 minutes.
Do not overcook.

i: 1 1/2 c. wagon wheel or corkscrew macaroni
12 oz. Italian sausage links, sliced to 1/2-inch thick
d: Prepare pasta according to package; drain and set aside.
In large skillet, cook sausage until no pink remains; remove from skillet.
Drain off fat, reserving 1 tablespoon.
Cook green onion and garlic in reserved drippings until tender.
In 2-quart casserole, combine cooked pasta, sausage, onion mixture, tomato sauce, spaghetti sauce, half of cheese, Italian seasoning and pepper.
Toss gently to combine.
Spray microwave dish with Pam.
Combine all ingredients and stir to mix egg and flour into mixture.
Microwave on High for 3 minutes.
Remove and stir.
Microwave on High again for 3 minutes. Ready to serve.

i: 1 (8 1/2 oz.) can crushed pineapple in syrup
1 (8 oz.) carton frozen whipped topping, thawed
d: In large saucepan, bring pineapple and syrup to boil.
Remove from heat and stir in gelatin.
Cool to room temperature.
Stir in buttermilk; fold in whipped topping and pecans.
Pour into 8-cup molds.
Chill 4 hours or overnight.

i: lettuce, torn in bite size pieces
1 medium onion, chopped or sliced (purple or green)
d: Layer lettuce, onion, peas and eggs. Sprinkle with bacon.
Ice top with mayonnaise.
Sprinkle top with shredded cheese.

i: 2 lb. red new potatoes, scrubbed and quartered
1 Tbsp. chopped fresh rosemary or 1 tsp. dried
d: Preheat oven to 425°.
Place potatoes in a large shallow baking dish that will hold all of them in a single layer.
Add the remaining ingredients and stir to coat well.
Cook bacon in a large skillet until crisp.
Crumble and set aside.
Drain and reserve 2 tablespoons of drippings.
Add onion and celery.
Cook until tender, but not brown.
Stir in flour. Stir in Worcestershire sauce and shrimp.
Cook beans with salt and 2 jalapenos.
Cook beef.
When done, add chopped onions and garlic.
Chop tomatoes and add to meat with tomato sauce.
Then add remainder of spices.
Simmer for 10 minutes, then add to bean pot.
Cream shortening.
Add sugar gradually.
Add egg and molasses. Add sifted flour, baking soda, salt and ginger.
Roll into balls in sugar.
Bake at 350° for 12 to 15 minutes.
In a shallow bowl, combine the Parmesan cheese, oregano, parsley, garlic salt and pepper.
Dip the chicken breasts in melted butter, then coat with Parmesan mixture.
Place in a greased 9-inch square baking dish.
Drizzle with any remaining butter.
Bake, uncovered, at 425° for 15 to 20 minutes or until the chicken juices run clear.
Makes 4 servings.
Mix all but ginger ale and soda water.
Add ginger ale and soda water (chilled) just before serving.
Garnish with orange slices.
Add crushed ice.
The best I have ever served.
Fry bacon until crisp. Saut bell pepper and onion in bacon drippings. Mix all ingredients and bake at 350 degrees for one hour.
Cook and drain broccoli as directed on package.
Stir and mix butter, eggs, cheese, mayonnaise, onions, soup and broccoli. Place in long baking dish.
Cover with crushed crackers. Bake for 20 minutes at 400°.
Cream shortening and sugars.
Add eggs and sifted dry ingredients; mix well.
Add oatmeal, Rice Krispies, nuts and vanilla.
Drop by teaspoonfuls onto lightly greased cookie sheet. Batter will be stiff and hard to drop.
Bake at 300° for 25 to 30 minutes.
Makes about 5 dozen.
Combine beans, cheese, pimentos and onion in medium size bowl. Add lime juice, sugar, vegetable oil and pepper; toss gently. Cover and chill 4 hours.
Serve on lettuce leaves with a slotted spoon.
Recipe is from Hills and Pines Garden Club, Raleigh, North Carolina.
Mix all ingredients together and scoop by teaspoonfuls; fry in hot oil.
Mix cabbage, green peppers and tomatoes with salt and let stand overnight; drain.
Tie mixed whole spices in a cheesecloth bag and add to sugar and vinegar.
Simmer for 20 minutes.
Add all other ingredients and simmer until mixture is hot and well seasoned.
Remove spice bag.
Pack in hot canning jars and seal at once.

i: 1 1/4 to 1 1/2 lb. boneless turkey breast, roasted, skinned and chilled
1 (6 oz.) jar marinated artichoke hearts, drained
d: Measure 1/3 cup of chutney and drain the syrup into a large bowl.
Finely chop chutney pieces and combine with syrup.
Cut turkey into 1/2-inch cubes.
Add to bowl with mayonnaise, mustard, salt and pepper; mix until combined.
Divide salad among 4 individual serving plates lined with lettuce leaves.
Sprinkle each with chopped almonds and garnish with the artichoke hearts, cucumber and radishes, dividing evenly.
Cooking Time: 15 minutes (also can be microwaved). Temperature: 400°.
Mix together and
marinate 6 pork chops in sauce, 3 to 4 hours.
Can grill or bake chops.

i: 2 small cans frozen orange juice concentrate
d: Heat water and sugar until sugar dissolves, stirring constantly.
Cool.
Add all other ingredients in a large bowl; stir.
Freeze in containers.
Combine cranberry sauce and lemon juice in bowl; mix well. Spoon in lightly oiled mold.
Freeze until firm.
Bring sugar and water to a boil.
Cool.
Mix remaining ingredients; add syrup and mix.
Pour into a 9 x 13-inch pan. Freeze.
Serve with 7-Up as a slush.
Slice apples directly into pie plate.
Mix remaining ingredients to a crumbly consistency and spread on top of apples. Bake for 1 hour at 350°.
Marinade chicken overnight.
Bake 1 hour at 350°.
Can be used on a whole cut up chicken.
Saute until brown the oil, garlic, ground beef and onions. Drain fat.
Add tomato paste, tomato soup, tomatoes, water, coffee, salt, pepper, mushrooms and oregano.
Cut the corn from the cob and add the milk, salt and pepper. Melt the butter in a large skillet and add the corn mixture and thyme.
Cook, stirring, until tender.
Makes 8 servings.

i: 8 c. cooked popcorn rice or regular
1 c. frozen corn, cooked and drained
d: Combine first 7 ingredients in large bowl and set aside. Combine mayonnaise, creole seasoning and curry.
Mix all ingredients.
Use 3 to 4 teaspoons of mix for mug of hot water.
Mix brown sugar and flour.
Mix first four ingredients and shape into 2-inch balls.
Roll in brown sugar and flour mixture. Sprinkle with nuts, if desired, and dip in melted butter.
Put in greased casserole and bake 15 to 20 minutes at 350°.
Mix chicken, 1 tablespoon sugar, 1 tablespoon soy sauce and egg in mixing bowl.
Sprinkle on cornstarch and stir in well.
In a saucepan, combine the broth, remaining soy sauce, sugar and sherry.
Bring to a boil.
Form meatballs with hands or drop from spoon into boiling broth.
Poach 5 to 6 minutes.
Preheat oven to 450°.
Pour oil in a 13 x 9-inch baking pan. Add garlic, stirring to coat.
Heat pan in oven for 5 minutes. Add rosemary and potatoes to pan, stirring to coat.
Preheat oven to 375°.
Whisk egg yolks with honey for 5 minutes.
Add orange peel.
Sift whole-wheat flour with salt. Gradually stir in egg and honey.
Add wine and olive oil, stirring constantly, as small amounts are added.
Beat the egg whites until stiff and fold into batter.

i: 1/2 to 2 tsp. curry powder (depends on taste)
juice and grated peel of 1 lemon
d: Brown meat in large skillet; pour off all but 2 tablespoons of drippings. Push meat to one side. Add flour and brown slightly, stirring constantly. Add all remaining ingredients and mix in the meat. Cover and simmer about 15 minutes or until onions are tender. Remove cloves before serving. Serve over hot rice. Serves 4.

i: 2 pkg. taco mix (I use store brand)
d: Mix the first 5 ingredients and place in a large roaster or foil turkey pan.
Pour oleo and taco mix over mixture and bake in 250° oven for 45 to 55 minutes.
Stir every 15 minutes.
Cool and store in airtight containers.

Slice cucumbers & onions. Cover with salt & ice. Let stand 3 hours. Drain & put in gallon jar. Dissolve sugar, vinegar, celery seed, turmeric & alum. Pour over cucumbers & onion & put in refrigerator. They are ready to eat in 7-10 days. Can be kept in refrigerator for a long time. I have had them for a year.
Mix ground beef, bread crumbs, milk, 2 tablespoons onion, egg, salt and pepper.
Shape into 24 meatballs and brown in lard or drippings.
Pour off drippings.
Dissolve bouillon cubes in hot water; add bouillon, remaining onion, soy sauce and celery to meatballs.
Cover tightly and cook slowly for 15 minutes.
Remove meatballs.

i: 1/2 c. chicken/vegetable broth or 1 bouillon cube, dissolved in 1/2 c. water
d: *Choose any 1 vegetable or combine several.
Vegetables should total 1 pound before cooking.
Brown ground beef with onion and drain.
In large pot, combine all ingredients and bring to a boil.
Boil, covered, for 40 minutes.
Uncover for the last 10 minutes.
Skim off grease.
Mix sour cream and soups; add 1/2 the cheese and mushrooms, along with the chiles.
Divide the soup mixture in half and add to the shredded chicken.
Fill the flour tortilla shells and roll. Place in a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Pour the remaining soup mixture over the top of tortillas and sprinkle with green onions and remaining cheese.
Bake for 30 minutes at 350°.
Peel, cut and cube the eggplant; leave soaking in water with salt for 15 minutes.
Saute in pan 1/2 of the garlic in olive oil. Drain eggplant well and add to garlic.
Cook until eggplant is soft.
Dissolve the flour in 1/2 cup water, then add to eggplant. Stir until thickens.
Mash with fork.
Add the other 1/2 of garlic, salt and mayonnaise.

i: 1/2 c. flour, seasoned with salt and pepper
1 1/2 lb. sirloin steak, cut into 2-inch x 3-inch pieces
d: Saute onion and garlic in olive oil.
Dredge meat with seasoned flour and saute in skillet with onions.
Mix tomatoes and water; add Worcestershire sauce, mustard, sugar, salt, paprika and pepper. Pour mixture over meat in skillet; simmer 1 1/2 hours.
Place lettuce in a 3-quart glass dish. Top lettuce with tomatoes (make layers). Next green pepper, red cabbage, water chestnuts, broccoli, red onion, cauliflower.
Just arrange it colorfully.
Use your favorite salad dressing.
Creamy Italian is great.
Brown meat with onion, a little butter, salt and pepper.
Pour water to top of meat and simmer all day on low.

i: 1 (12 oz.) frozen orange juice concentrate, thawed
d: Mix punch ingredients and use as a base.
Add about 1 - 2 quarts water and crushed ice and place in punch bowl.
Then when ready to serve, add Ginger ale.
The base can be doubled and poured into punch bowl as it fits, leaving room to mix in the water and ginger ale.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Mix all dry ingredients together (if you are using self-rising flour, eliminate salt, baking soda, and baking powder).
Grease and flour pans.
Mix all wet ingredients together and combine with dry ingredients.
Bake for about 50 minutes for loaf pans and 30-40 minutes for muffins.
Pour all juices into a large goblet half filled with ice cubes.
Top with ginger ale.
Decorate with slices or oranges and lemons.

i: 2 c. Pepperidge Farm seasoned crumb stuffing
d: Cook spinach as directed; drain well.
Add bread crumbs and eggs.
Add rest of ingredients and refrigerate overnight.
Make small balls.
Bake on cookie sheet in 350° oven for 20 to 25 minutes.
Makes 60 balls.
Combine until just moistened.
Drop by spoonfuls onto bubbling stew, chicken and gravy, etc.
Cover and simmer 12 minutes.
Mix mustard, flour and spices.
Mix vinegar, warm water and honey.
Combine; bring to a boil.
Simmer for 2 minutes.
Pack into clean jars.
Press out any air pockets.
Combine the first 4 ingredients in bowl.
In saucepan mix together the vinegar, sugar, celery seed, mustard seed and turmeric.
Bring to a boil and cool.
Pour over the cabbage mix.
In microproof baking dish, arrange lamb chops in one layer. Sprinkle with onion and garlic.
Cook, covered, on High (maximum power) for 5 minutes.
Combine remaining ingredients and spread over lamb chops.
Cover with waxed paper and cook on 60 (Bake) for 15 minutes or until lamb chops are tender.
Brown meat in large skillet; drain.
Add taco sauce; simmer. Melt cheese with milk to make cheese sauce.
Add more milk if needed for desired consistency.
Bring sesame seed and water to a boil; simmer 10 minutes.
Add remaining ingredients; blend until smooth.
Chill and serve.
Cream oleo and sugar.
Add eggs, 1 at a time, and beat well after each addition.
Add vanilla wafer crumbs, milk and coconut. Fold in pecans.
Grease and flour tube pan or 2 shallow square cake pans.
Bake at 275° for 45 minutes.
Increase heat to 300° and bake another 30 minutes.
Mix all ingredients together thoroughly.
To serve, stir in 2 teaspoons to 1 cup boiling water.
Place in casserole, onion on bottom, soup, almonds, cornflake crumbs and butter on top.
Bake uncovered, at 350 degrees for 1 hour.
Mix together well. Refrigerate. Will be ready to serve within an hour.

i: 1 to 2 lb. beef flank steak, tenderized
d: Rub liberal amounts brown sugar into both sides of the flank steak.
Don't be stingy!
Lay the steak into an oblong container and cover with soy sauce.
Remember to lift the steak so the sauce gets underneath.
Cover and marinate for 1 hour.
Turn the steak over, re-cover and marinate for 1 hour more.
Shake or stir all ingredients together.
Chill.

i: 1/2 c. chicken fat (or part butter or margarine)
d: Cook broad noodles 5 minutes in boiling salted water.
Drain; heat broth and season with salt and pepper.
Blend fat, flour and cold water; add to broth, stirring.
Boil 3 minutes.
Pour boiling water over peas to separate.
Drain.
Combine all ingredients and simmer; serve hot.
Remove cloves and cinnamon sticks before serving.
Makes a wonderful holiday aroma.

i: 1 (20 oz.) can crushed pineapple with juice
d: Mix all ingredients well.
Pour into 9 x 13-inch cake pan. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes or until done.
For option, try broccoli.
Mix all together.
Combine oleo, honey, mustard and curry powder.
Roll chicken breasts in this sauce and place in a single layer in a casserole dish.
Pour remaining sauce over chicken and bake, uncovered, at 350° for about 1 hour.
Baste often and turn once.
Mix Jell-O in boiling water. add sugar and mix thoroughly. Add cold water, lemonade, pineapple juice and extract. Freeze in plastic container. Thaw, but not completely and add ginger ale.
In small bowl, combine all ingredients until smooth.
Chill about 2 hours.
Serve over fresh fruit salads.
Yield: 1 1/4 cups.
Soak the first 3 ingredients overnight. Rinse and drain good. Add the remaining ingredients. Cook 20 minutes and seal in jars.
Sift dry ingredients together.
Cream shortening until light. Add unbeaten eggs and beat until light and fluffy.
Add molasses and dry ingredients.
Beat until smooth.
Add hot water, stirring briskly.
Turn into a greased loaf pan and bake at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes.
In a Dutch oven, bring water to a boil.
After rinsing off sprouts, simmer for ten minutes.
Bias cut celery and onions into semi-large pieces.
Add to sprouts and simmer another five minutes.
While you are cooking the vegetables, cut meat into whatever size pieces you desire.
Cream butter, Crisco and sugar.
Add eggs, one at a time.
Add flour, a cup at a time.
Add milk, a third at a time, then mix good.
Beat 3 minutes.
Put in a tube pan (greased and floured). Place in cold oven.

i: 1 Tbsp. grated Romano or Parmesan cheese
1/2 tsp. dried basil or oregano, crushed
d: You will also need nonstick spray coating.
Chill all ingredients.
Combine each one finishing with ginger ale.
Makes 2 1/2 quarts.

i: 2 lb. large shrimp in their shells
36 oz. your favorite beer (more if needed)
salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
d: Thoroughly wash the shrimp in cold water.
Pour the beer in a large soup pot.
Add the kielbasa slices to the pot along with the bay leaves, coriander seed and salt and pepper.
Bring ingredients to a boil over high heat.
Lower the heat and simmer, uncovered, for about 10 minutes.
Add the shrimp to the pot and bring back to a boil.

i: 2 cans (one 15 oz. and one 29 oz.) tomato sauce
d: In a large Dutch oven or soup kettle, brown beef with onions; drain. Stir in spices and tomato sauce. Simmer, uncovered, for 1 hour, stirring occasionally. Combine flour and vinegar. Stir into chili. Simmer another 15 minutes or until thickened.
Coat bottom of pot with olive oil.
Saute chopped meat and drain fat.
Saute onion and mix with meat.
Add tomato paste, cinnamon and nutmeg together.
Fill pot 3/4 full of water.
Add chicken to cook on medium flame and cook for 2 hours or until chicken is ready.
Beat vigorously all but cherries and nuts for 1/2 minute. Stir in cherries and nuts.
Pour into greased 9 x 5 x 3-inch loaf pan. Bake at 350° for 55 to 60 minutes.
Mix together well. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

i: 1 lb. fresh or frozen Brussels sprouts
d: Cook bacon until crisp.
Remove from pan and drain on paper towel.
Stir soy sauce into the bacon drippings.
Add chestnuts, garlic and broth.
Bring to a boil and add Brussels sprouts. Return to a boil; cover and reduce heat, cooking about 8 minutes or until sprouts are just tender.
Combine cornstarch and water and add to the pan liquid.
Heat the oil in a small, deep casserole and saute the onion and garlic until onion is transparent.
Stir in tomato sauce, wine, water, cloves, saffron, salt and pepper.
Simmer, covered, for 45 minutes, adding more water, if necessary.
Add the mushrooms to the cooking liquid and simmer 5 minutes more.
Turn off heat, cover and let cool.
Refrigerate overnight.
Wash and clean the peppers and cut into 1 1/2 to 2-inch pieces.
Peel the potatoes if thick-skinned, or use unpeeled new potatoes.
Cut potatoes into 1-inch chunks.
Peel the onion and cut into chunks.
Place everything in a shallow ovenproof dish and pour over the oil.
Toss the vegetables to coat them with the oil. Sprinkle with salt and lots of pepper.

i: 1 pt. sour cream (can be light or fat-free)
d: Prepare grapes, walnuts and mushrooms and set to the side. Clean chicken and remove all fat.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Saute chicken breasts in olive oil until browned on both sides.
Place in casserole dish.
Sprinkle grapes, walnuts and mushrooms over chicken.
Drain excess oil from frying pan (but leave browned bits).

i: 2 to 3 lb. top round steak (1 to 1 1/2-inches thick) or cut of your choice
d: Combine ingredients in a small bowl. Score meat on both sides. Pour marinade over meat.
Marinate overnight. Broil steak 4 to 5 minutes per pound on each side. Slice thin and serve.

t: Spicy Bean Salsa With Tortilla Chips
i: 1 (15 oz.) can whole kernel corn, drained
d: In a large bowl, combine all ingredients except tortilla chips; mix well. Cover and refrigerate several hours or overnight to blend flavors. Serve with tortilla chips as an appetizer. May also serve as a side dish. Makes 12 servings.

i: 1 pkg. (4 serving size) sugar-free Jell-O brand strawberry flavor gelatin or any red flavor
1/2 c. thawed Cool Whip whipped topping
d: Pour boiling water into blender.
Add gelatin and blend at low speed until completely dissolved, about 3 seconds.
Add ice cubes and stir until ice is partially melted.
Add strawberries, reserving 6 halves for garnish.
If desired, add the whipped topping.
Blend at high speed 30 seconds or until smooth.

i: 1 sweet yellow pepper, seeded and chopped
3 Tbsp. black olives (kalamata or oil cured, if possible), pitted and chopped
d: Combine all ingredients. Season to taste with salt and pepper.

i: 1/4 tsp. each: salt, paprika, oregano and ground cumin
d: In large, heavy pot, brown beef with onions, green pepper and garlic.
Drain off fat.
Add remaining ingredients, except kidney beans.
Simmer, uncovered, at least 45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
When thickened, stir in drained beans.
Heat through and serve.

i: 1 (15 1/2 oz.) can red kidney beans, drained
1 (15 1/2 oz.) can Great Northern beans, drained
d: Combine kidney beans, Northern beans, undrained tomatoes, tomato sauce, pepper, onion, chili powder, sugar, basil, garlic and water in a large saucepan.
Bring to boiling and reduce heat. Simmer, covered, for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Top each serving with 1/2 cup rice.
Serves 4.
Cut broccoli into small pieces.
Mash garlic with salt; mix in vinegar and beat in olive oil.
Pour over broccoli.
Add radishes, olives and pignoli nuts and marinate for about 5 hours.
Just before serving, add tomatoes and Feta cheese.

i: 1 Tbsp. each: cinnamon, cloves, ginger and salt
d: Chop cored apples, tomatoes, onions, peppers and raisins. Combine with remaining ingredients.
Cook until thick.
Pour into sterilized jars and seal while hot.
This is an excellent meat relish and it makes about 4 quarts.

i: 1 chicken, cut in pieces and salted
d: Place chicken pieces in greased casserole and pour taco sauce over chicken according to taste.
Cover casserole and bake at 350° for 1 to 1 1/2 hours, uncovered.
Sprinkle cheese over top of chicken and cover with broken tortilla chips.
Bake, uncovered, until cheese is melted and chips are hot.
Serves 4 to 6.
Place chicken wings in single layer in shallow roasting pan. Mix remaining ingredients together.
Heat on top of stove until sugar is dissolved.
Pour sauce over chicken wings.
Place, uncovered, in a 350° oven and bake for approximately 2 hours. Turn wings occasionally.
After first hour, check frequently to prevent burning.
Wings are done when sauce is thick and bubbly.
Cover cooking dish with Reynolds Wrap.
Spread deboned chicken breast(s) across bottom of pan.
Pour Honey Mustard Dressing over all chicken breast(s).
Make sure all are coated.
Then sprinkle chopped pecans across top of chicken breast(s).
Bake for 45 to 55 minutes at 325°.
Warm tortillas for 5 minutes in a 300° oven. Spread each tortilla with 1 tablespoon chutney. Top each tortilla with 2 ounces turkey and roll up. Secure with a toothpick.
Sprinkle chicken with salt and pepper.
Set aside.
Trim off eggplant ends, cut in 1/2 lengthwise and each 1/2 in thirds lengthwise.
Then cut across in 1-inch sections (total 5 cups). Cut onion in 1/2.
Slice thin (total 1 cup).
Core tomatoes and cut into small cubes (total 1 1/2 cups).
Boil chicken until done and debone. Cut-up onion and bell pepper.
Dissolve Roux in chicken broth and add onions and bell pepper.
Put debone chicken into mixture and cook until onions and bell pepper is done.
Remove bone from pork shoulder.
Drain sauerkraut and stuff in the shoulder.
Roll shoulder in flour with various seasonings to taste.
Close opening with sewing or skewers.
Place roast in oven at 450°, uncovered, in a greased pan.
Reduce heat as it begins to brown to 325° and bake about 40 minutes per pound or until tender.
Combine cucumber, onion and canning salt; let stand 1 hour and drain. Add remaining ingredients. Boil 15 minutes and put in can. Seal.
Use a heavy pan with cover, heat oil and add uncooked rice. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until rice is browned. Add onion, bell peppers, garlic and ground beef.
Cook and stir frequently until meat is brown.
Add seasoning, soup and water. Cover tightly and cook over low heat, stirring occasionally, for about 40 minutes or until rice and meat are tender and liquid is absorbed.
If needed, add more water during cooking.
Mix Cool Whip and milk.
Add pineapple, coconut and nuts; mix well.
Add lemon juice; beat until thick.
Pour into pie shells and chill.
Chop all of the vegetables, wash and drain.
Slice beef with a sharp knife into thin slices.
Combine meat, pounded ginger, brown sugar, soy sauce, garlic powder and flour.
Marinate for 1 hour.

i: 1 c. cold cola (Pepsi or Coke) soft drink
d: Combine water and sugar; bring to a boil.
Add pie filling and return to a boil.
Pour over the Jell-O to dissolve.
Add rest of ingredients.
Pour into 13 x 9-inch pan or mold to set.
Prepare 1 box strawberry jello and refrigerate to jell.
After it has jelled, spread 1/2 of the strawberries over the jello. Over strawberries, spread the sour cream.
Spread the remaining strawberries over the sour cream.
Prepare the second box of jello and pour over the already prepared mixture.
Sprinkle the pecans over the jello and refrigerate until firm.

i: 5 slices American cheese, cut in fourths
3/4 c. pizza sauce or barbecue sauce
d: Leave biscuits out at room temperature while you are browning meat.
Drain grease after browning hamburger meat.
Add barbecue sauce or pizza sauce.
Mix together in bowl and knead.
Mix well.
Turn out on well floured board.
Roll out to about 1/2-inch thick.
Cut with 3 1/2-inch cutter.
Brush on melted butter.

i: 12 c. squash, hand cut in 1/4-inch cubes
2 jars pimento or 2 large red peppers
d: Mix squash and salt.
Soak overnight.
Wash and drain.
Cut peppers, onion and cucumbers in 1/4-inch cubes.
Mix all ingredients. Bring to a boil.
Pack and seal.

i: One 8 oz. can sliced water chestnuts, drained
12-14 slices bacon, cut in half crosswise
d: Cut the livers in half, quarter any extra large livers. Place the livers in a plastic bag set inside a deep bowl. Combine the sherry, soy sauce, brown sugar, oil, garlic powder, ginger, and water, pour over livers.
Cook the corn as you would dried beans or peas until tender (20 minutes in the pressure cooker is one way).
Don't use a lot of water.
After the corn is done, add other ingredients.
(You may need more milk, coconut and sugar, depending on how the corn comes out.)
Before serving, sprinkle with cinnamon.
The texture should be creamy, but lumpy and not too thick.

i: 1 (25 oz.) Package Frozen Roll Dough, Thawed
1 (3.4 oz.) Package Butterscotch instant Pudding Mix
d: Sprinkle nuts in the bottom of a buttered 12 cup bundt pan. Arrange dough in pan; sprinkle with dry pudding mix. Stir together butter, brown sugar and cinnamon; pour over rolls. Cover and chill 8 hours.
Mix all ingredients well.
Shape into patties.
Cook at 325° on cookie sheet for 1/2 hour.
Turn; cook 1/2 hour until brown.
Mix graham wafer crumbs with butter and sugar to make crust. Spread on cookie sheet or shallow cake pan.
Put layer of chocolate chips first, then butterscotch chips, coconut and nuts. Top with Eagle Brand evenly.
Bake at 350° for 10 to 15 minutes or until lightly browned.
Cool and cut into slices or squares.
Beat eggs on high for 5 minutes, gradually beat in sugar.
Stir in pumpkin and lemon juice.
Stir together flour, baking powder, cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg and salt.
Fold into pumpkin mixture. Spread in greased and floured 15x10x1-inch pan.
Top with finely chopped nuts.
Bake at 375° for 15 minutes.

i: 2 1/2 c. fresh, crushed strawberries or 2 (10 oz.) containers frozen strawberries, thawed
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
Grease and flour two 9 x 5-inch loaf pans.
In a medium bowl, stir berries, eggs and oil.
In a large bowl, combine flour, sugar, cinnamon, soda, salt and nuts.
Add berry mix to dry ingredients and stir until blended. Pour into greased pans.
Bake 1 hour or until toothpick inserted in center comes out clean.

i: 2 lb. thinly sliced deli roast beef
d: Combine in crock-pot and cook 1 1/2 hours on high or 3 hours on low. Serve on buns if desired.

i: 1 (3 oz.) pkg. peach or apricot jello
d: Heat pineapple, cream cheese and sugar until cheese melts. Add jello and stir well.
Add cold water.
Put in refrigerator to set.
When mixture is near set, fold in Cool Whip and nuts.
Put in a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Cook at 350° for 1 3/4 hours in a 2-quart casserole.
Melt butterscotch chips in bowl in microwave.
Stir in noodles.
Add peanuts.
Drop by teaspoon on wax paper.
Let stand until cool, about 20 minutes.
Yields 4 dozen.
Add all ingredients and mix well (except cheeses).
Cook in casserole dish for 1 hour at 325°.
Cover with shredded cheeses and cover to keep moist after cooking.
Combine ingredients in a 2-cup measuring cup.
Microwave on High 3 minutes.
Equals 1 can.

i: 2 whole chicken breasts, boned, skinned and split into 2
1 c. grated well-aged white Cheddar cheese
4 large thin slices Black Forest ham
d: Slice each piece of chicken breast open to form cavity.
Stuff with cheese and reshape meat with hands.
Wrap in slice of ham. Carefully dredge chicken in flour and then dip in beaten egg. Thoroughly coat with breadcrumbs.
Chicken must be well-coated or cheese may leak during cooking.

i: 5 or 6 large apples, peeled and quartered
d: Mix brown sugar, flour, cinnamon and salt until crumbly. Place apples and mixture alternately in greased baking dish.
Add 2 teaspoons of lemon juice to 1/2 cup water and pour over all. Cover and bake 30 minutes at 350°.
Mix together all ingredients; chill.
Roll into balls.
Bake at 350° for 15 to 18 minutes.
Roll in powdered sugar.
Cream sugar and butter together until all sugar is dissolved. Add unbeaten eggs, one at a time, and beat thoroughly.
Sift and measure flour.
Add salt and sift again.
Add flour to egg mixture; mix carefully.
Add vanilla.
Bake at 350° for 50 to 60 minutes (grease pan).
Mix together and carefully add 1 pound lump crab meat.
Mix ahead of time and refrigerate so they will hold together better.

i: 6 oz. long grain and wild rice
d: Brown meats and onion.
Drain fat.
