Cook rice as directed. Combine all ingredients into a 3-quart glass casserole dish.
Bake at 350° for 50 minutes.

i: 1 pkg. can biscuit, cut in half
d: Grease muffin pan and press biscuit half in pan cups up the side as far as you can.
Divide sausage and place on dough.
Mix cheese cottage cheese eggs and pepper; pour over sausage and dough.
Bake 375° 15 to 20 minutes.
Wipe muffin pan, grease and make the rest of the muffins.

i: 4 oz. drained, cooked pinto beans, mashed
brown sugar substitute (to equal 1 c. plus 3 Tbsp. regular sugar)
d: Mix all ingredients together and pour into pie crust.
Bake at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes until firm.
For festive occasions, add 1 ounce pecan halves to top of pie.
Prepare vegetables and salt (first 5 ingredients); mix well and let stand overnight.
In A.M. drain and rinse in cold water. Drain well.

i: 1 can Ragu Thick and Hearty sauce (plain)
d: Take the cubed steak and set it into the pan (with Crisco) on medium-low.
While steak is cooking, chop the veggies into fine pieces and place them in a bowl (with the cheese).
When the meat is done, put it in with the veggies.
Put the whole jar of Ragu into the pan (simmer with a little bit of water).
Chop vegetables.
Add salt and let set overnight.
Add sugar, vinegar, spices and turmeric.
Cook slowly for 1 hour and put in jars and seal.
Makes about 15 pints.
Dissolve gelatin and sugar in 1/2 gallon of boiling water. Cool.
Add lemon juice, pineapple juice and 2 gallons of water. Freeze overnight.
Add ginger ale just before serving.
Stir together zucchini, Bisquick, cheese and onion in one bowl.
Beat eggs with oil and salt in separate bowl.
Mix all together with spoon until blended.
Pour into a greased 2-quart casserole dish.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Boil potatoes with skin on.
When cool, peel off skins and cut potatoes into pieces.
Add eggs, pickles, onions and sweet relish to the potatoes.
Add mayonnaise and mustard to taste and to make the salad the way you like it. Sprinkle paprika over the salad.
Refrigerate until ready to serve.
Preheat oven to 375°.
Grease a 9 X 13-inch baking dish with olive oil.
Place the first 5 ingredients
in a large salad bowl.
Layer them; do not mix them together.
Spread over 2 cups salad dressing to seal in the 5 ingredients.
Sprinkle 2 tablespoons sugar over salad dressing.
Fry 1/2 pound bacon until very crisp, then cool, crumble and sprinkle over the top.
In separate skillets, brown the meats, drain and spoon into large stew pot.
Then, in no particular order, add everything else. Cover and simmer on medium heat until vegetables are tender.
Simmer hamburger meat, green chilies, soups and enchilada sauce for a few minutes.
Crush Doritos in bottom of dish.
Dish sauce on top.
Sprinkle cheeses on top.
Put in oven until cheeses melt.
Serve.
Combine zucchini, peppers and onions with salt.
Let stand overnight, then drain well.
Combine vegetables with all remaining ingredients, blending well.
In a large pot, bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
Seal in hot sterilized jars.
Makes about 4 pints.
Layer all items, beginning and ending with sauce.
Cover tightly with foil to allow the noodles to steam cook.
Bake at 350° for 75 minutes or until noodles are soft.
Fast and easy!
Combine hamburger, crumbs, onion, pepper, egg, Italian seasoning and 1/4 cup tomato sauce.
On a sheet of tinfoil, spread mixture into a 10 x 12-inch rectangle.
Sprinkle with cheeses and roll into a log.
Seal ends and bake at 375° for 1 hour.
At the end of the hour, pour remaining tomato sauce over meat loaf and bake for another 15 minutes.
Score cucumber by running tines of fork lengthwise down sides; slice.
Toss cucumber with remaining ingredients.
Combine all ingredients as given and bake in casserole for 40 minutes at 350°.

i: 1 broiler-fryer chicken, cut in serving pieces
d: Line a 13 x 9-inch baking pan with aluminum foil.
Arrange chicken pieces in pan.

i: 5 c. flour (bread flour or natural bleached)
d: Sprinkle yeast in warm water; let stand until dissolved.
Add sugar, oils and 2 1/2 cups of the flour, beating well.
Gradually add the remaining 2 1/2 cups of flour to form a soft dough.
Turn out onto a floured board and knead until smooth and elastic, adding flour as needed.
May take 1/2 to 1 cup.
Turn dough into greased bowl.

In a bowl mix turkey, egg, thyme, milk and a pinch of salt and pepper. Mix well. Fold in instant potatoes. Take a heaping spoonful of mixture and roll in the palms of your hands to make balls. Add enough olive oil to the bottom of a frying pan to coat it. Arrange meatballs in pan and brown on all sides, turning frequently. Add turkey balls to your favorite sauce.
Mix thoroughly.
Other seasoning may be added according to taste.
Saute zucchini in margarine with onion until tender.
Mix egg, 1/2 of cheese and spices in bowl.
Add zucchini and onion to egg mixture.
Spread crescent rolls (raw) in pie pan to make crust. Pour egg and vegetable mixture over crust.
Top with remaining cheese.
Bake at 350° until golden brown, approximately 20 minutes.
Mix all ingredients into a
bowl.
Pour onto floured surface. Cover with flour and shape into a ball.
Wash
cucumbers
and
put
in a 4 or 5 gallon
crock or granite

i: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen lima beans, cooked and drained
1 c. cooked and diced chicken or turkey
d: Combine all ingredients in a 5-quart Dutch oven. Bring to boiling over medium heat.
Reduce heat and simmer 30 minutes, stir occasionally.
Makes 6 servings.
Preheat oven to 400°.
Grease a 9-inch cake pan with margarine.
Cream sugars, butter and shortening.
Beat in eggs and vanilla.
Combine flour, salt and soda; beat into first mixture. Add remaining ingredients; knead mixture.

t: Barbeque Sauce For Spareribs(Yield: Serving For 4 People.)
d: 1. Before starting the sauce, place the ribs in a pot with water and pour in one beer.
Bring to a boil and then simmer for one hour.
This will make the meat tender.
Saute beef, onions, green pepper and garlic until tender. Add tomato paste, water and salt. Mix well.
Add beans and mushrooms.
Simmer until all ingredients are thoroughly heated. Pour into baking dish and cover with canned biscuits.
Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes or until biscuits are golden.
Beat egg white until foamy.
Add pecans and stir until coated, set aside.
Combine sugar, cinnamon and salt.
Stir gently onto pecans until coated.
Spread pecans in lightly greased pan.
Bake at 300° for 30 minutes.
Combine sugar, eggs, evaporated milk and vanilla in large mixing bowl.
Add enough milk to fill freezer 3/4 full.

i: 2 (8 oz.) cans refrigerated buttermilk can biscuits
d: Separate the biscuits into 20 pieces.
Combine sugar and cinnamon.
Dip biscuits into melted butter, then roll in sugar mixture.
Arrange biscuits around the outer edge of a deep dish baker pan using a total of 15 pieces and overlap the other 5 pieces in the center.
Peel, core and slice apples into thin pieces; place in the biscuit crust.
Mix chopped pecans with the remaining sugar mixture and pour over all.
Combine dry ingredients and mix well.
Cream margarine; add sugar gradually.
Blend eggs in creamed mixture and mix well.
Add dry ingredients and pumpkin.
Stir in morsels and walnuts.
Bake at 350° for 65 to 75 minutes.

i: 3/4 c. (1 small bunch) cilantro or parsley, washed, with stems removed and lightly packed
1 (8 oz.) bottle Lawry's classic creamy Caesar with cracked black pepper dressing
d: Combine ingredients in a food processor or blender and process until smooth. Serve the dip in a shallow bowl or small hollowed out bell pepper.
Makes 1 1/4 cups.

i: 1 1/2 to 2 Tbsp. liquid red pepper seasoning
d: Saute pepper, onions and celery in butter and oil in a large pot uncovered until tender.
Stir in chicken broth and tomato puree.
Bring to boiling, lower heat and simmer 15 minutes.
Mix together the first 6 ingredients.
Stir in enough cold water for a thick batter.
Mix in the onions and drop by teaspoonfuls in hot oil.
Flatten patties as you turn.
Fry to a golden brown.

i: 1 box chopped spinach, thawed with liquid squeezed out
d: Mix ingredients. Pour into greased casserole dish. Bake at 350° for 20 to 25 minutes. Serve with tortilla chips.

i: 1 (2 1/2 or 3 lb.) chicken, boiled in water
d: Remove chicken from bone.
Cook spaghetti in chicken broth. Remove spaghetti from broth.
Cook onions, pepper and celery until tender.
Pour in tomatoes and cheese.
Mix all ingredients together.
Put in large casserole dish.
Combine water and sugar; bring to boil; let cool.
Add lemon juice.
Add ginger ale and sparkling Catawba juice.
Makes about 1 to 1 1/2 gallons.

t: Light 'N Easy Chicken And Rice Casserole
i: 1 (10 1/4 oz.) can cream of mushroom soup
d: Cook rice according to package directions.
Combine cooked rice and next 5 ingredients.
Mix; place into a lightly greased baking dish.
Top with chicken breast.
Combine soup and yogurt; spoon over chicken breasts.
Bake, uncovered, at 350° for 30 minutes.
Blend together all ingredients and chill.
Serve with raw vegetables.

i: onions, garlic and 1 medium green bell pepper (additional ingredients according to individual taste)
d: Cook ground beef and drain grease.
Put ground beef and all remaining ingredients into a large boiler pan. Cook for 3 hours on medium heat until the texture becomes thick and ground beef is coated with the tomato base.
Serve with garlic bread or saltine crackers.
Cut eggplant into cubes.
Dust with salt; drain in colander. Saute celery and onion in olive oil for about 15 minutes.
Saute eggplant for 10 minutes.
Place all in a 2-quart kettle.
Add balance of ingredients, except salt and pepper.
Simmer for 20 minutes, or until liquid evaporates.
Wash chicken with cold water.
Place in a large saucepan.
Add water halfway up the chicken with 1/2 teaspoon salt for every quart of water.
Cover; simmer for 1 hour, turning once or twice. Cool chicken in the broth and refrigerate until needed.

i: fruit of your choice (I use peaches, strawberries, blackberries, cherries, etc.)
d: Melt margarine.
Put in 2-inch deep dish.
Add fruit of your choice.
Mix together sugar, milk and flour.
Mix well and pour over fruit.
Do not stir.
Cut skinned and boneless chicken breasts into bite-sized pieces.
Mix slightly beaten egg white, 1 teaspoon cornstarch, soy sauce and fresh ground pepper in a plastic bag.
Add chicken, tie bag and refrigerate for up to 1 hour, turning bag occasionally. Cut both peppers into thin strips.
Slice onion very thin and separate into rings.
Mix 1 tablespoon cornstarch, water, sugar and sesame oil.
Heat wok until very hot, pour oil down sides of wok and rotate to oil all sides.
Grind berries.
Add sugar and let stand overnight.
Dissolve Jell-O in 1 cup hot water.
Add 1/2 cup cold water.
Mix all together.
Put in dish; let set in refrigerator overnight.

t: Leg Of Lamb In Oven(Cosciotto D'Agnello Al Forno)
d: Preheat oven.
Wash
leg
of
lamb thoroughly; pat dry. Make 4 small slits
around lamb.

i: 1/2 c. light mayonnaise style salad dressing
d: In blender container, blend cottage cheese until smooth and creamy to make "mock sour cream."
Add lemon juice; blend well. Mix curry powder, garlic salt, sugar, horseradish, onion and vinegar.
Add cottage cheese mixture, salad dressing and yogurt; mix well.
Cover and chill several hours or overnight.
Serve with fresh vegetables or as topping for baked potatoes.

i: 1 (8 oz.) can water (use juice from cooked escarole
1 1/2 lb. escarole, washed, drained and cut into bite size pieces
d: Saute garlic in oil, add tomato sauce and water, and any salt, if desired.
Bring mixture to a boil, lower heat and add escarole, a little at a time.
Cook until tender, 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add water if needed.
Five minutes before you are ready to serve, add the can of beans (rinse beans) and heat through.
Serve with spaghetti and grated cheese.

t: Tomatoes Stuffed With Rice And Mint
d: Wash each tomato and cut a thin slice from the stem end. (Save stems for lids.)
Scoop out pulp with a teaspoon and reserve.
Sprinkle insides with sugar.
Heat olive oil in a skillet and cook onion until soft, approximately 5 minutes.
Add rice, stir well and cook for 3 minutes.
Add the chopped tomato pulp, 1/4 cup hot water, raisins, mint, salt and pepper.
In the early
the Labor Day celebration on the "green" was a
festive
affair on St. Helena Island.
Visitors joined the crowd
Mix all together thoroughly and let cure for 48 hours.
Stuff into jars and pressure can for 35 minutes or cold pack for 2 1/2 hours.

i: 1 can baby whole clams, drained (reserve juice)
d: In saucepan, heat oil and garlic; saute for 2 minutes.
Stir in flour and clam juice, wine, red pepper flakes and salt.
Bring to boil and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add minced clams, baby clams and parsley.
Return to boil and remove from heat.
Cook linguine according to package and then serve with sauce.

i: 8 skinned and boned chicken breast halves
d: Sprinkle chicken with salt, pepper and garlic powder.
Dip chicken into flour and coat.
Heat butter and oil in large skillet.
Brown chicken slowly on both sides until cooked.
Remove to warmed platter.
Stir lemon juice and wine in pan with drippings to deglaze.
Mix all ingredients thoroughly.
Bake in a greased loaf pan at 350° for 1 1/4 hours.
Serves 6 to 8.
Soften yeast in water.
Add sugar and beaten eggs.
Stir dry milk into 3 cups of flour.
Add flour mixture to first mixture and beat until smooth.
Add salt and soft shortening.
Add remaining flour until soft dough is formed.

Boil potatoes. Drain 1/2 water off (only boil until potatoes are tender). Brown ground beef with onions and drain. Add ground beef, 2 cups of fresh water, tomatoes and tomato paste. Heat until boiling. Turn down to simmer for 30 minutes.
Combine all
ingredients and mix well.	Pack in greased loaf pan and bake at 375° for 1 hour.
In saucepan, boil water; add raisins.
Boil until water is gone.
Cool.
Combine flour, soda and 1/2 teaspoon salt.
Beat together sugar and margarine until combined.
Add eggs and vanilla; beat well.

i: 1 eggplant, cut into cubes (you don't have to peel it)
d: Saute eggplant in oil, about 7 minutes.
Add onion, garlic and pepper.
Saute 3 additional minutes or until tender.
Combine tomatoes, tomato juice and herbs.
Add to the eggplant mixture. Cover and simmer for 1/2 hour.
Serve over spaghetti.
Grind or chop bell pepper and onions together.
Mix with ground chuck thoroughly.
Add Italian dressing and soy sauce to taste. Add salt and pepper and mix.
Hand roll meatballs.
Spray pan with Pam nonstick spray before cooking.
Heat frying pan.

i: 2 c. fresh shredded coconut, soaked for 1 hour in 3 c. water
d: Grind together the garlic, ginger, coriander, cumin and red pepper to a paste.
Heat oil in a pan; add onion.
Saute onions. Add ground paste and cook until oil separates about 15 minutes on a low temperature.
Strain coconut.
Add coconut milk to the onions and bring to a boil, then simmer over medium low heat 15 minutes.
Add shrimp and simmer until shrimp is done, about 8 to 10 minutes.
Combine eggs and milk to produce a French toast batter. Combine flour and Parmesan cheese.
Rinse the halibut, dip into French toast batter, then dip into cheese mixture.
Saute with lid about 20 minutes, adding little amounts of water, if needed.

i: 1/2 c. Key lime juice or lemon
d: Fold together milk and juice.
Fold in Cool Whip.
Pour into crust and cool for a few hours.

i: 1 to 2 c. cubed chicken, beef or pork
1 (16 oz.) pkg. frozen broccoli, cauliflower and carrots
1 (16 oz.) pkg. frozen Oriental vegetable mix
brown sugar and soy sauce to taste
d: Marinate meat in marinade and/or vinegar for about 1 hour. Preheat vegetable oil in wok or large pan on stove.
Add meat and marinade to pan and quickly brown outside of meat, stirring so all sides brown.
Cover and steam for about 3 minutes.
Add vegetables; stir until coated with liquid.
Cover and steam for 5 minutes or until vegetables are desired tenderness.
Add brown sugar and soy sauce to taste.
Mix ingredients and put in baking pan.
Top with following mixture.
Mix in large mixing bowl; add to cooled thickened jello.
Pour in molds and let set.
Place zucchini, onions and salt in a large bowl; mix well and cover.
Refrigerate overnight.
Next day, place mixture in a fine strainer; drain well.
Rinse under cold water and drain 1 hour.
Mix all ingredients together.
Roll into small balls and bake at 400° for approximately 20 minutes on cookie sheet.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Peel wrappers off tamales and chop in large bowl.
Drain hominy, olives and peppers.
Add them to tamales.
Mix well.
Place in 9 x 13 x 3-inch baking dish.
Dice and fry bacon until crisp.
Add to drippings, diced apple, diced green pepper and diced onion.
Saute until tender. Add 2 large cans of Hunt's small red beans (drain before adding). Add 1 large can of chopped up tomatoes.
Add curry powder and brown sugar.
Mix together.
Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese. Cover; set overnight.

i: 1/2 c. warm milk (to dilute baking soda)
d: Cream room temperature margarine and Crisco.
Add sugar.
Add eggs, spices, baking powder and baking soda-milk mixture, mixing well with mixer after each addition.
Add flour, 1 cup at a time, mixing after each, until consistency is pliable.
Chill dough.
Sift together twice, the flour, baking soda, salt and cinnamon.
In a separate bowl beat eggs and sugar until creamy; add oil and vanilla.
Beat until smooth.
Add dry ingredients and mix until a stiff dough forms.
Stir in apples and pecans.
Pour into a greased and floured 10-inch tube pan.
Cut very fine as for relish the cukes, peeled and with seeds removed.
Add 1/4 cup of salt and let stand overnight.
Drain in morning and add onions, chopped fine, vinegar, sugar, flour, turmeric, dry mustard, mustard and celery seed.
Cook 1 hour and put in hot jars.

i: coconut topping and vanilla cream filling
d: In large bowl, mix 1/2 cup flour, sugar, salt, lemon peel and yeast.
Combine milk, water and margarine in saucepan.
Heat liquid (120° to 130°).
Gradually add to dry mix.
Beat 2 minutes on medium speed.
Add egg and 1/2 cup flour.

i: 1 part wine vinegar or fresh lemon juice
Minced fresh herbs, such as tarragon, basil, oregano, chives
d: Whisk together all ingredients or shake in a covered jar. Adjust seasonings.
Use as a marinade for raw tomatoes, mushrooms, zucchini, jicama, onion rings, or blanched asparagus, or green beans.
Drain pineapple; reserve juice.
Combine reserved juice with garlic, cornstarch, Worcestershire sauce, mustard and rosemary. Arrange chicken in shallow baking dish on broiler-proof dish, skin side up.
Broil until browned.
Stir sauce; pour over chicken.
Bake at 400° for 30 minutes.
Bring beet juice, white vinegar and sugar to a boil.
Cool before adding beets and eggs.
Start eggs with cold water.
Bring to a rolling boil.
Turn stove off and let eggs set in water for 20 minutes.
Run cold water over eggs and peel.

i: salt, pepper and curry powder to taste
d: Peel onions and cut into even thin slices. Heat margarine in large skillet. Add onions; toss until coated with margarine. Do not brown. Add vinegar, brown sugar and curry powder. Blend well. Add parsley. Cook 2 to 3 minutes until glazed. Season with salt, pepper and curry powder. Serve hot as a side dish or cook as an appetizer. Can be used in greens or potato salad.
Beat egg and milk together.
Pour into warm nonstick pan.
As egg mixture sets, lift gently with spatula so uncooked mixture flows beneath.
Cover egg mixture with mushrooms, pimento, basil, parsley, salt and pepper.
Fold in half.
Serve on heated plate.
In a bowl, dissolve yeast in water (plus 1 teaspoon sugar). Mix ingredients.
Add rest of flour until smooth.
Cover.
Let rise in a warm place until double, 1 1/2 hours.
Divide.
Shape. Let rise until double, 1 hour.
Mix all ingredients and let set overnight.
Combine all ingredients and put on half loaves of French bread.
Wrap foil around crust side.
Bake uncovered on cookie sheet for 25 minutes at 350°.
Garnish with strips of cheese and bake 5 minutes longer.
Saute onion in butter until transparent.
Add the rice and stir lightly until beige in color.
Boil water with the bouillon cubes.
Add to rice.
Also stir in raisins and curry.
Cover and simmer 20 minutes on medium heat.
Brown meat, onion and green pepper in skillet.
Drain.
Add remaining ingredients except cheese.
Simmer for 10 minutes.
Pour into a 9 x 13-inch buttered casserole dish.
Bake for 20 minutes at 400°.
Toss sliced/shredded vegetables in bowl.
In small saucepan, bring to boiling vinegar, sugar, salt, celery seed and dry mustard.
Pour hot dressing over vegetables.
Toss thoroughly. Cover and chill for 4 hours.
Toss before serving.
Sift flour with next four dry ingredients.
Combine egg with milk, vanilla and salad oil.
Pour into dry ingredients.
Add diced apples, then stir just enough to moisten completely.
Spoon into greased muffin tins, filling about two-thirds full.
Combine 2 tablespoons sugar and 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon for topping.

Combine flour, baking powder and salt in large bowl. Add pineapple, cherries and dates. Mix with hands until well incorporated. Beat 4 eggs until frothy; gradually beat in 1 cup sugar. Add to fruit mixture; mix well. Add pecans and mix well. Grease loaf pans and pack mixture in very firmly.
Bake at 275° for 1 hour. Don't overbake. Turn out on rack and cool.
Mix all together right before serving. Serves 12 to 15.

i: 1/2 to 2 tsp. garlic or seasoned salt
1 pkg. dry Hidden Valley Ranch dressing
d: Mix crackers and oil until oil is well soaked into crackers. Add remaining ingredients.
Stir until evenly coated.
Heat in oven at 250° for 45 minutes or so, stirring every 15 minutes.
Place ham slices, one on top of the other, on cutting board. Cut 1/2-inch strips lengthwise; do not separate.
Combine cheese and onion.
Spread thin layer on top of first slice of ham.
Roll up first strips of slice and repeat next slice, etc.
Makes 30 roll-ups.
Mix all ingredients together and form into balls.
Bake.
Blend liquid ingredients into dry ingredients; do not overmix. Bake in greased muffin pans for 20 to 25 minutes at 425°.
You may add can crabmeat.

i: 3 lb deboned chicken meat cut into large chunks
d: To make sauce, mix cornstarch and water together.
Add garlic, ginger, sugar, soy sauce, vinegar and wine.
Then add chicken broth and M.S.G. and stir until sugar dissolves. Refrigerate until needed.
In separate bowl, mix chicken, soy sauce and pepper.
Stir in egg.
Add cornstarch until chicken is coated evenly.

i: 1 qt. pecans (or use 1/2 pecans and 1/2 black walnuts)
d: Mix all together and bake
in
greased	tube
pan for 4 hours at 250°.
Put a pan of
water over cake and one of water under cake.
Combine first 3 ingredients with salt; let stand overnight. Drain and rinse with 4 changes of water.
Peel and slice potatoes.
Dice onion.
Fry together until tender.
Once cooked, break potatoes in small pieces; set aside. Fry bacon and crumble into pieces.
Add to potatoes.
Scramble eggs; add to potatoes.
Cook meat; drain.
In a large pot, add additional ingredients for 30 minutes.
Serve with cornbread.

i: 1 whole chicken breast, skinned, boned and cut in 1 x 1/2-inch pieces
2 c. chicken stock or canned broth
d: In large pot, bring stock and water to boil; add zucchini. Simmer for 10 to 15 minutes until tender.
In skillet, cook onion in olive oil over moderately low heat, stirring until softened. Add curry powder; cook, stirring for 1 minute.
In blender or food processor, puree onion mixture with zucchini.
Return soup to pot. Add chickpeas, chicken and seasonings.
Simmer over moderately low heat for 15 to 20 minutes, until vegetables are desired tenderness. Adjust consistency by adding water, white wine or broth.
Serve hot, chilled or at room temperature, with croutons and dollops of nonfat yogurt or sour cream.

i: 1 head lettuce, broken in bite size pieces
d: Mix together the mayonnaise and sugar in a small bowl and set aside.
Layer the first 6 items in order listed, then spread mayonnaise mixture over top, sealing edges.
Sprinkle cheese and bacon bits over the top.
Refrigerate and let sit overnight.
Boil diced potatoes, with skins left on, in water until done. Drain and set aside.
In skillet, saute onion and garlic in olive oil.
Add drained potatoes.
Sprinkle generously with paprika. Cook until crisp on all sides.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Dissolve yeast in the milk.
Heap the flour and salt in a mound in a mixing bowl.
Make a well in the center and pour in the oil.
Mix while adding the yeast and milk.
Stir mixture thoroughly and pour on floured surface.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes and shape into a ball.

i: 3/4 lb. cooked, cooled London Broil, cut into 1-inch strips
d: In a small bowl, combine first 5 ingredients.
Mix well.
In a small bowl, combine London Broil, radishes and onion.
Pour dressing on top.
Toss well.
Spread cream cheese on 8 or 9-inch plate.
Add rest of ingredients as listed one by one in layers on plate.
Top with cheese.
Eat with nacho chips.
In medium saucepan, add cooking oil and elbow macaroni. Brown macaroni lightly. Add water and tomato sauce. Add seasonings.

t: Cubed Steaks With Smothered Onions(Weight Watchers)
d: Cut steak into 2-inch wide strips.
Cut the onions into very thin slices.
Cut the scallions into 2-inch sections.
In a shallow bowl, blend the flour, 1/4 teaspoon of the pepper and 1/8 teaspoon salt.
Toss the steak strips in the flour mixture.

i: 3 or 4 (8 oz.) cans tomato sauce or 3 cans tomato sauce and 1 c. spaghetti sauce
1 lb. small curd cottage cheese (regular or fat-free)
5 c. wide, cooked noodles (cholesterol-free, if desired)
d: Brown ground beef in oil; add onion and green pepper.
Saute 5 to 10 minutes; drain.
Brown chops quickly on both sides in skillet.
Put in casserole.
Saute onion in butter for 5 minutes.
Blend in flour and seasonings.
Gradually stir in milk and cook, stirring constantly, until thickened.
Add mushrooms.
Cut wings apart and clip ends off.
Dip wings into a mixture of cornstarch, eggs and water.
Place wings in a pan coated with oil over medium-high heat.
Allow wings to brown.
Combine remaining ingredients, mixing well.
Pour this over wings.
Combine all ingredients in 3-quart pot; cook until tender.

i: 1 to 1 1/2 lb. chicken, cut into slices or strips
d: Combine cornstarch, soy sauce and vinegar into paste.
Boil water and add bouillon, ginger, pineapple juice and brown sugar and stir until bouillon and sugar are dissolved.
Add cornstarch paste and boil until mixture is clear and thickens (stir to prevent sticking).
Pour over cucumbers, put on lids and cold pack for 5 minutes, after water comes to a boil.

i: 1 (3 oz.) pkg. strawberry Jello (can use sugar free)
1 envelope plain gelatin, dissolved in 3 Tbsp. cold water
juice of one lemon and one orange
d: Grind cranberries and apple.
Add the sugar.
Stir and let stand overnight in the refrigerator.
Add remaining ingredients and mix well.
Melt margarine in large saucepan over low heat; add marshmallows.
Heat until smooth and completely melted, stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat.
Add cereal; stir until well coated.
Mix all ingredients well.
Shape into ball and roll into flour.
Cook in hot oil (360°) until brown, about 10 to 15 minutes.

i: 4 Tbsp. bacon fat or cooking oil
d: Heat 1 tablespoon fat in a small skillet.
Break eggs into a bowl and beat well.
Add to skillet and cook omelet fashion.
Break eggs into small pieces and set aside.
In a large skillet heat remaining 3 tablespoons fat.
Add scallions and celery, stir-fry. Add pork and fry briefly.
Mix melted butter, soy sauce and hot pepper sauce.
Drizzle over noodles.
Toss lightly until noodles are well coated. Sprinkle with celery salt and onion powder, tossing to mix.
Toast at 275° for 12 to 15 minutes.
Cube cheese and put in crock-pot on low.
Brown meat.
Add meat and salsa to cheese.
When melted, serve with tostito chips.
Mix ingredients by hand.
Let mixture set overnight in refrigerator.
Make into patties and cook on slightly greased grill or frying pan.

i: 1/2 can Eagle Brand sweetened condensed milk
1/2 can Eagle Brand sweetened condensed milk
d: Melt the caramels with the remaining 1/2 can of Eagle Brand milk.
Add the pecans and spread this over the baked cake.
Pour remaining batter over this and bake for 20 minutes at 325°.
While the cake is still warm, top with the following.
These are very, very
good
and worth the effort.
Read the recipe and have what you need on hand before you start.

i: 4 (9 oz.) cans Awake frozen orange juice
d: Combine cold water with Awake and cranberry juice cocktail. Stir and chill.
Just before serving, add chilled ginger ale and ice cubes.
Makes 7 quarts punch.

t: Never Fail Pie Crust(Double Pie Crust)
d: Combine flour, sugar and salt.
Put shortening in a chilled bowl and add flour mixture.
Blend with pastry blender to a coarse meal.
Blend egg and ice cold water together, then add vinegar and blend.
Add to flour mixture and mix well.
Roll out on floured surface.

i: 1 can (8 oz.) sliced water chestnuts, drained
1/4 head lettuce, torn into bite-size pieces (about 3 c.)
d: Prepare Honey Dressing.
Toss chicken, grapes, onions, bell pepper, water chestnuts and dressing in large bowl.
Cover and refrigerate about 2 hours or until chilled.
Toss chicken mixture, oranges and lettuce.
Serve on lettuce leaves if desired. Sprinkle with almonds.
Yields 6 servings.
Mix all ingredients until thin.
Place cleaned shrimp, split up back and deveined, in the mixture.
Remove 1 at a time, roll in flour and drop in hot cooking oil.
Cook to light brown.

i: 1 pkg. Stove Top stuffing or similar
d: Peel and slice in half.
Place in dish and cover.
Microwave on High 10 minutes or until soft.
Prepare stuffing according to package directions.
Add mushrooms and crabmeat and mix well. Mound stuffing onto onion halves and microwave for 2 more minutes or until hot through.
Can be served as a vegetable or with salad as entree.

i: 1 (16 oz.) can pears, cut in quarters
1 can pitted Royal cherries, drained overnight (save juice)
d: Cook the oleo, brown sugar, curry powder, fruit juice and flour until thick.
Add pecans and cherries.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Super with ham, chicken, turkey and roast beef.
Prepare chicken by removing bones and steaming good size pieces (good idea to buy breasts and thighs).
Dip in tempura batter and fry in hot deep oil.
Slice each piece in 1/2-inch to 1-inch slices.
Arrange on a bed of shredded lettuce.
Cover with sauce.

i: 1 c. coconut milk or 1/2 coconut milk and 1/2 regular milk
d: Cream eggs, sugar and Crisco.
Add milk, baking powder, flour, vanilla and coconut.
Bake 30 minutes at 350°.

i: 2 (14 1/2 oz. each) cans stewed tomatoes (no salt)
d: Cook and stir onions and garlic in Crisco until tender.
Add ground beef and drain if necessary.
Add tomatoes and tomato paste, basil, oregano, sugar and pepper.
Stir until well blended. Simmer 30 minutes.
Combine cottage cheese, 1/4 cup Parmesan cheese and parsley; set aside.
Cook noodles 7 minutes in unsalted boiling water.
Mix all ingredients (except Sprite) and freeze 2 days.
Combine bread crumbs, milk, beef, egg, 1/4 cup onion and 1 1/2 teaspoons salt.
Gently form into 20 meatballs; brown in butter in large skillet.
Add rice and remaining onion; stir gently until rice is lightly browned.
Drain pineapple, measuring syrup.
Add water to syrup to make 1 1/3 cups liquid.
Cut each pineapple slice into 6 wedges; gradually add with the liquid, 1 teaspoon salt and remaining ingredients to rice mixture; stir thoroughly. Bring to boil.
Grind or grate zucchini and onion; sprinkle with salt and let stand overnight.
Drain and rinse with cold water; drain again. Mix all other ingredients and pour over zucchini and onion.
Bring to a boil and boil for 5 minutes.
Pack in sterilized jars and seal.
Using a large mixing bowl, cream together margarine, sugar, eggs and vanilla.
Sift together and add to the mixture the plain flour, cloves, allspice and cinnamon.
Add the pecans. Combine and add applesauce and soda.
Using a wooden spoon, mix well. Batter will be thick. Store, covered, in refrigerator.
Batter keeps indefinitely.
In a hurry?
Need a quick, low-fat dinner?
Try this.

i: 1 (6 oz.) or 2 (3 oz.) pkg. orange-flavored gelatin (I use sugar-free jello)
d: Combine pineapple and sugar in a saucepan; bring to a boil, stirring until dissolved.
Cool.
Add buttermilk and stir until combined.
Fold in whipped topping and pecans.
Spoon into a 12 x 8 x 2-inch Pyrex dish and chill until firm.
Cut salad into squares to serve.
Melt shortening then let cool.
Ad sugar, molasses and egg. Mix well with mixer.
Sift dry ingredients together; add to first mixture.
Chill well.
Form into balls and roll in sugar.
Place on cookie sheet and bake at 350 degrees until they start to crack. About 6 - 8 minutes.
Mix dry pudding mix with Cool Whip and add spices.
Stir in the Amaretto to taste.
Serve with fresh fruit dippers.

i: 3 c. sifted Swans Down cake flour
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
Cream butter; gradually add sugar, creaming until light and fluffy.
Sift flour with baking powder and salt.
Add eggs one at a time to creamed mixture, beating well after each addition.
Add flour mixture alternately with milk and flavorings, beating after each addition until smooth.
Pour batter into three greased and floured 9 x 2-inch cake pans.
Cover bottom of pan with meat strips.
Liberally salt, pepper and garlic.
Sprinkle with soy sauce.
Cover with Worcestershire sauce.
Repeat layers if necessary.
Make sure meat is submerged. Cover and refrigerate 24 to 48 hours.
In heavy skillet, brown beef and drain.
Add onion, salt and pepper, then stir.
Add sliced potatoes, soup and water.
Cover and simmer until potatoes are done.
Makes 4 servings.
Beat cream cheese and mayonnaise until smooth.
Stir in next 5 ingredients.
Cover; chill several hours.
Form into ball; roll in nuts to coat.
Serve with crackers.

i: 1 (14.5 oz.) can diced tomatoes with garlic and onion
1 c. cubed grilled chicken or turkey
d: Combine tomatoes, beans, jalapeno and cumin in skillet. Add cooked chicken. Heat 5 minutes or until thickened.

i: 2 (15 1/2 oz.) cans crushed pineapple in its own juice, drained (reserving juice for cake)
1 Betty Crocker SuperMoist yellow cake mix
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
Melt margarine in 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan in oven.

Sprinkle brown sugar over top of margarine until even. Spread crushed pineapple on top of margarine-brown sugar mixture. Decorate with pecans and cherries, pressed to bottom of pan. Prepare cake mix as directed but use pineapple juice instead of water.
Pour batter into pan over margarine, sugar and pineapple. Bake 45 to 55 minutes.
Invert cake immediately on heatproof serving plate or cake pan.

i: salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
thinly sliced black bread or crackers
d: Cook onions until soft.
Add mushrooms, coriander, salt and pepper.
Cook until mushrooms let off their juice.
Turn up heat and cook until most of liquid evaporates.
Refrigerate until chilled.
Add seasoning, if necessary.
In large bowl, combine pears, sugar and pecans.
Set aside for 1 hour.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Butter and flour large tube pan. Using wooden spoon, stir into pear mixture the oil, vanilla and eggs.
Add remaining ingredients.
Bake 1 hour.

i: 1/2 pkg. or more 1/4 inch wide egg noodles, cooked
d: Fry cabbage and onion together with soft margarine until vegetables are soft.
Pour boiling water on bouillon cubes.
Let set until dissolved.
Then pour over cabbage and onion.
Add salt, pepper and garlic salt.
Simmer until really soft.
Cook and cool
the vinegar, lemon juice and cornstarch. Put this cooled
mixture
into	the blender with the remaining ingredients. Blend only
until well mixed. Store in refrigerator. Pour over
romaine	lettuce

i: 2 lg. Green Peppers, halved and cut into 1/2 inch strips
d: In a large stock pot add 4 quarts of water and all of the ingredients except the tomatoes and bring to a boil for 40 minutes.
Remove the chicken with a large spoon and remove the chicken bones and skin.
Skim fat oils from the top of the broth, put the chicken meat back into the pot, add tomatoes and boil for 15 minutes.
Serve on flour tortillas, with salsa or hot sauce, rice or beans optional.
Serves 6 to 8.
Slice sausage.
Mix together sausage, onions and garlic. Brown, then add rosemary and thyme.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Drain and add tomatoes, sauce, paste, 1 1/2 cups water, sugar, salt and pepper to taste.
Simmer on low heat for 30 minutes.
Cook spaghetti noodles according to directions on package.
Grease and flour a 10 x 4-inch Bundt pan.
Thoroughly stir together flour, soda and salt.
In a large mixing bowl at medium speed, beat oil, granulated sugar, eggs and vanilla until combined. Gradually beat in flour mixture until smooth.
Fold in apples and pecans.
Turn into prepared pan.
Bake in preheated oven at 325° for 1 hour and 20 minutes.

i: 1 c. grated Cheddar cheese (medium or sharp, whichever you prefer)
d: While the Velveeta is still cold, cut into small cubes.
Place in the top of double boiler set over hot water and allow cheese to melt.
When it is melted, stir in evaporated milk and pimentos. Cook over medium heat until slightly thickened, about 30 minutes. Cool and add Cheddar cheese and enough mayonnaise to moisten and for taste.
Use for sandwiches, filling for celery sticks, crackers, etc.

i: 1 (8 to 10 lb.) fresh leg of lamb
d: Preheat oven for 15 minutes to 350°.
Trim fat from leg of lamb; rub it with juice from half lemon, then with 3 tablespoons salt on all sides.

Sift together dry ingredients. Add milk to beaten egg. Pour into dry ingredients and stir until smooth. Stir in molasses and shortening or oil. Pour batter into greased shallow pan (about 8 inches square). Bake at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes. Serve warm or cooled with sweetened whipped cream.

t: Renal And Diabetic Chicken Breast Diane(Special Diet)
d: Place chicken breasts between sheet of waxed paper.
Pound slightly with mallet.
Sprinkle with lemon pepper.
Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in large skillet.
Cook chicken over heat, browning on each side.
Add onions, lemon juice and wine and parsley with mustard.
Heat olive oil and butter in wok.
Chop onions and garlic and saute.
Add clam broth.
Add wine and simmer for 10 to 15 minutes. Add the mushrooms, oregano, basil and pepper.
Note:
Add seasonings according to your own personal preferance.

i: 3 grapefruit, peeled and cut in strips
d: Peel the grapefruit; cut in strips.
Cook in the water and sugar.
Keep adding water as it cooks.
When it looks transparent, you have to keep stirring, then use sugar to roll pieces in until it will absorb no more.
Lay on wax paper, then put in container. Eat as pieces of candy.
In a large saucepan, brown meat and onion in oil over high heat.
Drain off excess fat.
Add garlic, cinnamon, cumin and cloves.
Stir and cook 1 minute.
Add remaining ingredients and simmer, uncovered, 30 minutes.
Makes 6 servings.
Mix water and add sugar until sugar dissolves.
Add remaining ingredients.
Use 1 1/2-quarts of ginger ale to one gallon. Yields 100 people.
Mix well.
Roll to size of walnut or smaller.
Put on ungreased cookie sheet and bake at 275° for 30 to 45 minutes.

i: 1/2 lb. bacon, cooked crisp, drained and diced
1 lb. spaghetti, cooked tender according to pkg. directions
1/4 c. cream, light cream or milk
d: Mix cream, egg, Parmesan cheese and bacon together.
Toss with cooked spaghetti.
Sprinkle with salt, Parmesan cheese and plenty of freshly ground black pepper.
Serve immediately!

i: 3-1/2 lb. boneless lamb, cut into cubes
1 tsp. whole coriander seeds or (4 tsp. powdered)
d: Preheat oven to 325°. Place cubed lamb in 1-1/2 qt. casserole. Top with sliced onions and raisins; sprinkle with spices. Cove and bake until lamb is very tender 1 to 1-1/2 hours. Serves 6.

i: 2 (16 oz.) cans Phillip's pork and beans
d: Combine all ingredients in baking pan and cook for 35 minutes at 300°.

t: Green Chile(Soup Or Smothering For Burritos, Chile Rellenos Or Enchiladas)
i: 1 medium onion, cut into large chunks
salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
8 to 10 Anaheim chiles, roasted, peeled, seeded and chopped
d: Heat oil in large stockpot.
Add pork, onion, garlic, salt and pepper.
Saute until pork is browned.
Drain juice from tomatoes into stockpot.
Chop tomatoes and add to pork with chiles and 2 cups water.
Stir well and simmer, covered, for 45 minutes.
Mix shortening, honey, egg and molasses.
In another bowl, sift soda, salt, ginger, cinnamon, cloves and flour.
Mix wet and dry ingredients to form dough. Roll 1/4-inch thick on floured surface.
Cut with cookie cutter. Decorate with raisins, nuts, licorice and tiny candies. Bake at 375° for 10 minutes.
Cook vegetables in small amount of salted water.
Drain excess liquid and combine with remaining ingredients.
Serves 6 to 8.
Allow sherbet to soften to a mushy consistency.
Dissolve marshmallows in hot Jell-O mixture.
Add pineapple and oranges, then sherbet.
Add Cool Whip last.
Prepare hot roll mix.
Form into a ball.
Roll out bread dough flat.
Spread sauce over dough.
Add the pepperoni, pepper, mushrooms and cheese.
Roll tight like a jelly roll.
Mix all ingredients together.
Works best for London Broil steak.
Let marinate in refrigerator for 24 hours.
Barbecue grill to your liking.
Make jello as directed on box. Grind cranberries and orange. Add sugar to cranberries and orange and set aside until jello thickens.
Add nuts.
Saute onion in small amount of oil.
Add tomato paste.
Stir for a minute.
Add all other ingredients.
Then add 4 cans of water, using tomato paste can for measure.
Bring to boil.

i: 1 pkg. Winter mix broccoli and cauliflower (16 oz) or use fresh vegetables
d: Cook just to heat the broccoli and cauliflower then drain and put into casserole dish.
Spread over the vegetable the mushroom soup.

i: 1/4 c. or so water (if dough gets too thick for mixer)
d: Combine oil, sugar and eggs; beat at medium speed for 3 minutes.
Combine dry ingredients and add to mixture.
Fold in pecans and apples.
Bake in greased and floured tube pan at 325° for 1 hour and 20 minutes, or until cake is done.

t: Chicken Cooked With Vermouth And Tomatoes(Poulet Chasseur)
i: 75 ml dry vermouth or dry white wine
d: Peel and chop the shallots; peel the onions but leave them whole.
Melt the Crisco; add the oil and beat together for a moment.
Place the chicken in one layer in the pan; brown the pieces on both sides.
After adding the onions to flavor them a little and the shallots after 2 to 3 minutes so that the shallots melt but do not color.
Remove the pan from heat; add vermouth and when warm, ignite with a match.
Tip the pan until the flames die down.

i: 1 medium sweet red pepper, cored, seeded and cut into 1-inch pieces
1 medium sweet yellow pepper, cored, seeded and cut into 1-inch pieces.
1 medium size zucchini, cut lengthwise in half, then crosswise to 1/4-inch thick slices
d: Heat 1 1/2 teaspoons olive oil in large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add half the steaks.
Cook until brown on both sides, about 4 minutes for medium-rare or until desired doneness. Remove steak to platter; keep warm.
Repeat with remaining steaks.
Let raisins and water stand for 5 minutes.
Mix together other ingredients, then add raisins.
Fill muffin pans 3/4 of the way full.
Bake at 400° for 25 minutes.
Makes 12 to 16 muffins.
Using a quart jar or plastic container, layer onions and cucumber slices.
Pour vinegar mixture over slices.
Cover.
Keep in refrigerator.
Pickles will keep for several months.
These are similar to bread and butter pickles.

t: Cabbage Casserole In A Rice Cooker
d: Brown ground meat and sausage together.
Drain oil when cooked.
Mix all ingredients in a large bowl; then transfer into rice cooker.
When cooked, leave in cooker for 10 minutes before serving.

i: 2 lb. mild or sweet Italian sausage
1 small carton small curd cottage cheese
1 large and 1 small can tomato paste
d: Brown onions.
Add sausage and brown.
Add ground beef and brown.
Add seasonings and tomato paste.
(Adjust seasonings to individual taste).
Mix well and simmer while preparing noodles. Cook noodles in salt water with a little olive oil in it.
Position knife blade in food processor bowl.
Add all ingredients except onion.
Process 1 minute or until lumpy.
Stir in onions.
Yields 2 cups.
Mix together well.
Bake in buttered casserole dish at 350° for 45 to 55 minutes.

i: 2 cans (2.25 oz. each) sliced or chopped olives
d: Cut English muffins in half.
Set aside.
In large bowl mix together olives, cheese, onions, mayonnaise and salt.
Spread mixture evenly over 12 muffin halves.
Place on baking sheet. Broil 3 to 4 minutes or until cheese is bubbly and melted.
Cut in half before serving.
Make crust by melting 1 stick of oleo with 2 cups graham crumbs.
Pat into a 9 x 13 x 2-inch pan.
Mix sugar, eggs, and 2 sticks oleo until creamy, about 15 minutes.
Pour on crust.
Slice bananas and put on top of filling.
Top this with pineapple. Cover with Cool Whip and sprinkle with nuts and cherries on top. Chill overnight.
Dissolve Jell-O in 1 cup boiling water.
Add 1 cup cold water. Mix with other ingredients and chill until set.
Place spinach in large salad bowl; set aside.
Cook bacon in saucepan until crisp.
Add onion and mushrooms; saute until tender.
Stir in next 5 ingredients.
Cook thoroughly until well heated, stirring constantly.
Pour hot mixture over spinach and toss lightly.
Preheat oven to 375°.
In heavy 2-quart saucepan, melt butter. Combine bread crumbs and next 4 ingredients.
Dip chicken breast halves in melted butter; roll in bread crumb mixture.
Place in ungreased 9-inch square baking dish.
Bake in center of oven 50 minutes or until golden brown.
Meanwhile, add diced onion to remaining melted butter (1/2 cup).
Heat a small wok or skillet over medium-high heat and add cooking oil.
Add shrimp; toss and fry for 30 seconds.
Add scallops; stir and cook another 10 seconds.
With a slotted spoon, remove seafood to a plate.
Add to wok the broccoli and carrots and fry for 30 seconds; add bell pepper, snow peas and tomato and cook while tossing for an additional 30 seconds. Return seafood to wok.
Add sauce mixture and quickly toss until thickened.

In a saucepan, combine the first six ingredients. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat. Simmer for 5 minutes. Remove from heat. Add beets. Let stand at room temperature for 1 hour. Cover and chill 6 hours or overnight.

i: 1 tsp. each: salt, cinnamon and nutmeg
d: In mixing bowl, beat together the sugar, pumpkin, oil and vanilla.
Sift together the dry ingredients.
Add raisins and nuts.
Blend both mixtures together.
Drop by spoonfuls onto oiled baking sheet.
Bake at 350° for 12 to 15 minutes.

i: 3 c. wide noodles, cooked and drained
4 slices American cheese, cut in strips
d: Brown meat.
Drain off fat.
Arrange in layers cooked noodles, 1/2 of the cheese, onions, ground beef, green peppers and diced tomatoes.
Mix together tomato juice, Worcestershire sauce, salt, garlic salt, herb seasoning and pepper.
Pour over casserole. Bake at 350° for 20 minutes.
During last 5 minutes, arrange remaining cheese over the casserole.

i: 2 c. chopped red and green cherries
d: Mix well shortening, brown sugar and eggs.
Add buttermilk and stir in flour.
It will be easier to work with if you chill dough overnight.
Drop by teaspoon on baking sheet and bake at 350° for 8 to 10 minutes, or until done.
Yields 7 dozen.
Mix well.
Pour in loaf pan.
Bake for 1 hour at 350°.
Preheat oven to 350°.
In 12-inch skillet, over medium heat, fry bacon crisp; drain on paper towels.
Crumble and set aside.

i: 2 Tbsp. plain yogurt with live culture
d: Dissolve the milk powder in the milk and simmer 5 minutes. Pour into a heatproof bowl and let cool until you can just dip your finger into it.
Add the yogurt and stir.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap; wrap it in a thick towel and leave it in a warm place for 5 to 6 hours.
Alternatively, you can make the yogurt in a wide-necked vacuum bottle.
Preheat oven to 450°.
Put sea scallops in a 9-inch baking dish and drizzle with olive oil.
Cut orange sections from the white membrane over the baking dish so the juice is saved.
Arrange orange sections among scallops and bake for 8 minutes.
Add bay scallops and sprinkle with dill.
Broil for 4 minutes.
Soak corned beef in water to cover for 1/2 hour or longer if deeply corned.
Place a large sheet of heavy-duty foil on a shallow pan.
Remove corned beef from water and pat dry to remove any salt on surface.
Place in center of foil and pour over it 1/4 cup fresh water.
Sprinkle with the spice and arrange orange slices and vegetables over and around the meat.
Bring long ends of foil up over meat and seal with a tight double fold.
Thaw pastry.
Mix ground beef, salt, pepper and onion; bind with egg yolks.
Wash and quarter mushrooms.
Cover the bottom of an 8-inch loose bottomed cake pan with the bacon.
Arrange the cheese, herbs and mushroom quarters on top.
Cover the meat mixture, pressing it down firmly and smoothly on the top.
Beat eggs well; add sugar, milk and vanilla and mix well. Brown sugar in pan you are going to bake it in.
Pour everything in it and set in a pan of water.
Bake for 1 hour at 375°, then cool it and turn over to serve.
Wash zucchini; remove ends and slice lengthwise in 1/4-inch slices.
You should have about 5 slices.
Place on cookie sheet. Dot with small cubed butter (about 1/4-inch each).
Put thin slices of garlic in between butter cubes.
Heat under broiler until the zucchini begins to brown on edges.

i: 3 tsp. chili powder per lb. ground chuck
1/2 tsp. cayenne (add more for hotter chili)
d: Fry meat in skillet until done; drain well.
Put meat, chili powder, cayenne, salt and pepper in large pot.
Fill with water. Boil 3 to 4 hours, adding water when needed.
Add onions.
Cook until tender.
Add tomato sauce; stir well.

i: 3 Tbsp. fresh rosemary or 3 tsp. dried
d: Combine all ingredients in a food processor with metal blade or blender.
Blend for 30 seconds, until smooth.
Marinate for at least 1 hour at room temperature.
Spread over fresh tuna and bake.

In 10-inch skillet over medium-high heat, cook ground beef, onion, garlic and curry powder until meat is browned and onion is tender, about 10 minutes, stirring frequently; discard garlic. Add zucchini, water, salt, sugar and pepper; heat to boiling. Reduce heat to low; cover and simmer 15 minutes or until zucchini is tender, stirring occasionally.
Stir in tomato; heat through.
Combine dry ingredients.
Add milk, egg and onion; mix well. Drop by rounded teaspoonfuls into deep, hot oil.
Fry only a few minutes, or until golden brown on both sides.
May be served with tomato gravy and applesauce.
Boil lemon juice, water and sugar; cool.
Cut one large banana and put in mixture.
Pour over fruit; put kiwi on top.
You can put cherries in the middle.
Combine all and heat in double boiler. Serves 6.

i: 1 (15 oz.) can unsweetened pineapple chunks, undrained
d: Place chicken in a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish.
Combine Worcestershire sauce and next 3 ingredients; stir well.
Pour over chicken.
Cover and marinate in the refrigerator for 1 hour.
Allow cheese to set at room temperature.
Cut into chunks and blend until fluffy.
Add Worcestershire sauce and onion.
Cool in refrigerator and shape into two or three balls.
Roll in chopped nuts.
Bring lentils to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for about 1 hour.
Add chopped onions, celery and carrots; stir while simmering 10 minutes.
Add chopped tomatoes and minced garlic; simmer 3 minutes.
Add parsley, tomato paste, thyme and salt, if desired.
Simmer 3 minutes.

t: Lemon Meringue Pie(I Make This Often, It'S Good!)
d: Combine sugar, cornstarch and salt.
Add water very gradually and cook in double boiler in boiling water until it cooks and thickens, stirring constantly.
Beat egg yolks and pour hot mixture over them gradually, stirring constantly.
Just before removing from heat, add lemon juice, rind and butter.
Mix well and pour into crust.
Put meringue on top.
Cook ground beef and onions; add seasonings and tomato soup and sauce.
Cook until onions are soft and golden.
Combine.
Fry in deep fryer at 375°.

i: 1 (6 oz.) can frozen lemonade concentrate, thawed
d: In a blender container, blend raspberries until smooth. Strain and discard seeds.
In a pitcher, combine raspberries, lemonade and 1 cup water.
Chill.
To serve, combine raspberry mixture with the soda.
Serve over ice.
Makes 8 (8 ounce) servings.
Combine biscuit mix, milk and cheese in a large bowl.
Mix until a soft dough forms.
Drop by spoonfuls onto a baking sheet. Bake at 450° for 8 to 10 minutes or until lightly browned.
Mix the butter and garlic powder in a small bowl.
Brush the butter mixture over the warm biscuits.
In large bowl combine flour, brown sugar, baking powder, ginger and baking soda.
In another large bowl stir together eggs, carrot, zucchini, raisins, walnuts, oil, honey and vanilla.
Add carrot mixture to flour mixture, stirring only until combined. Spread batter in an ungreased 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan.
Bake in a 350° oven approximately 25 minutes or until a toothpick inserted comes out clean.
Makes 36 bars.
In a large sauce pan cook cranberries in 2 1/4 quarts of water until berries pop. Add remaining ingredients and simmer 30 minutes. Strain out solids. Serve hot.
Place all ingredients in food processor fitted with a metal blade.
Process just until powdery (about 30 seconds).
Store in airtight container for up to 6 months.
Sift flour, baking powder, salt, soda and sugar.
Cut in shortening.
Blend in egg, milk and vanilla.
Roll on floured board.
Cut with cookie cutter.
Place on ungreased cookie sheet. Bake at 375° for 8 minutes.
Preheat oven to 400°.
Fill 12 cupcakes tins with paper liners. Mash bananas with a fork.
In large bowl, mix egg, sugar and vegetable oil.
Add and mix milk and mashed bananas.
In a small bowl, stir flour, poppy seed, salt, baking soda and baking powder. Add flour mixture all at one time to the liquid mixture.
Using a wooden spoon, stir just until dry ingredients are moistened.

i: 1/3 c. fat-free mayonnaise or salad dressing
d: Prepare fettuccine as directed on package.
Drain and set aside. Gradually add skim milk to fat-free mayonnaise and garlic in small saucepan.
Heat thoroughly, stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat; blend in Egg Beaters.
Toss with fettuccine and mushrooms until well coated.
Add Parmesan cheese and mix well.
To make crust, mix all ingredients to form dough.
Spread in pizza pan.
Bake at 400° for 15 minutes.
Add yeast to 1 cup warm water; combine and refrigerate 3 to 5 days (covered jar).
Place spareribs in shallow baking pan.
Roast, uncovered, in a moderate oven at 350° for 1 1/2 hours.
Pour off fat.
Melt margarine in a pan.
Add onion and garlic; cook until tender.
Add remaining ingredients and bring to a boil.

i: 6 c. cooked, fresh corn kernels (about 12 ears)
d: In a 6 to 8-quart saucepan, combine all ingredients.
Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, stirring occasionally.
Reduce heat to medium and cook for 15 minutes.
Remove bay leaf.
Fill 1/2 pint jars or pint jars.
Process in water for 20 minutes.
Toss together chicken, rice and celery in large bowl.
Blend remaining ingredients.
Stir into chicken mixture.
Serve on shredded lettuce and garnish with black olives.
Yields 6 servings.

i: 30 oz. jar of your favorite spaghetti sauce
d: Brown and cook the ground beef.
Add the salad dressing, spaghetti sauce, and cooked macaroni.
Heat on medium heat.
Top with grated Cheddar cheese.
Serves 4 to 6.
Brown meat. Add all ingredients, except noodles and cheese. Season to taste.
Simmer for at least 3 hours.
Do not skim off too much fat, or lasagna will be dry.
Cook noodles as directed on package, but add 1 tablespoon oil to prevent sticking.
Place in pan as follows:
layer of noodles and layer of cheese.
Dredge veal in flour.
Saute in olive oil.
Brown on both sides.
Add mushrooms and shallots until wilted.
Add Madeira sauce, 1 1/2 cups water, tomato paste and sauce mix.
Mix well. Simmer 10 minutes.

i: 1 lb. large red potatoes, scrubbed and quartered
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
Place potatoes in a small roasting pan or iron skillet.
Drizzle oil over potatoes.
Sprinkle with garlic and season with salt and pepper.
Bake potatoes for 45 to 50 minutes until tender and browned.
*Cheddar/Swiss/Monterey Jack (use alone or in combination).

i: 3 or 4 chicken breasts, cooked until tender
2 heads lettuce, broken into bite size pieces
d: When chicken is cool, break into small pieces.
Store each of the first 3 ingredients in a Ziploc bag until ready to use.
In a skillet, brown the sesame seed, almonds and ginger.
Store in Ziploc bag until ready to use.

Buy whole turkey breast; remove all skin and bones. Cut into pieces about the size of your hand. Pour marinade over turkey and refrigerate for at least 8 hours (up to 48 hours). The longer it marinates, the less cooking time it takes.

i: 1 (16 oz.) can frozen pink lemonade concentrate, thawed
d: Mix
cranberry
juice and lemonade in large punch bowl. Just before serving, pour in ginger ale.
Serves 25.
Saute celery, onion and green pepper in 1/4 cup margarine until tender.
Add tomatoes, water and seasonings.
Cover and simmer 30 minutes.
Add shrimp and cook 4 to 5 minutes.
Mix cornstarch with water in a cup until all lumps dissolve.
Stir into sauce.
Cook potatoes, onions and ground beef in skillet on medium heat until redness is gone from meat, stirring constantly, so potatoes and onions won't scorch.
Add small amount of water, 1/2 cup.
Season to taste.

i: 1 large green pepper, cored, seeded and chopped
d: Heat oil in a heavy bottom pot; saute onions, pepper and garlic with oregano, cumin, thyme, coriander and chili powder.
Stir in tomatoes, tomato paste, celery, carrots and corn.
Simmer, covered, for 40 minutes; then add beans and cook for 15 minutes.
Add salt, hot pepper, liquid and vinegar to taste.
Before serving, you may combine some cabbage and cilantro to serve as a garnish.

i: 1 (16 oz.) can whole kernel corn, drained
d: In skillet at medium heat, brown ground beef; drain.
Add catsup, mustard, barbecue sauce, potatoes, pork and beans, corn and onion flakes.
Reduce to low heat; simmer for 15 to 20 minutes or until heated through.

i: 1 yellow bell pepper, seeded and diced
d: Mix diced vegetables together in a small bowl. Drizzle olive oil over vegetables, then the red wine vinegar. Stir lightly and salt to taste. You can adjust the tartness by adding more vinegar. Serve immediately. Makes 4 large servings.
Bring vinegar, sugar, salt and spices to a boil; pour over zucchini (sliced and unpeeled) and onions.
Let stand 1 hour. Cook 3 minutes.
Pack in hot jars.
Put lids on, and process in boiling water bath for 5 minutes.
Makes 6 or 7 pints.
Saute onion in 2 tablespoons margarine.
Add next six ingredients and mix.
Spread in a casserole dish.
On day before, cut spareribs into 2-rib portions.
In large pot over high heat, heat to boiling ribs and enough water to cover.
Reduce heat; cover and simmer 1 hour.
Drain and refrigerate.
Grill for 50 minutes until desired doneness.
Blend mayonnaise and water.
Add remaining ingredients except noodles and eggs.
Pour into casserole dish and bake at 350° for 20 minutes.
Garnish with sliced eggs and Chinese noodles.

i: 2 c. Tang instant orange drink mix
d: Beat one egg; lay a slice of American cheese over it and put into microwave for two minutes.
Put on toast.
This makes an easy breakfast sandwich that your kids will eat.
Heat oven to 450°.
Mix baking mix, milk and cheese until soft dough forms.
Beat vigorously for 30 seconds.
Drop dough by spoonfuls onto ungreased cookie sheet.
Bake for 8 to 10 minutes, or until golden brown.
Mix margarine and garlic powder; brush over warm biscuits before removing from cookie sheet.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Grease and dust with flour a 17 1/4 x 11 1/2 x 1-inch baking pan.
In a medium mixing bowl, beat eggs for 5 minutes on medium speed.
Add all other ingredients one at a time, in any order, mixing well after each addition, until entire mixture is creamy smooth.
Brown beef in soup pot with bacon strips.
Add other ingredients, except frozen vegetables and crab meat.
Cook 3 hours or until meat is tender.
Add rest of ingredients and simmer covered until vegetables are done.
If you want it hotter, add a few dashes of Tabasco sauce, or have it available on serving.
Combine meat and other ingredients (except last) in a bowl and make into small balls.
Then after put into pot with tomato sauce poured over them.
Cook slow about 1 hour.
Combine sauerkraut, caraway seed, 1 teaspoon brown sugar, onion slices and margarine.
Place in shallow square baking dish.
Cut unpeeled apples into halves and remove cores.
Brush cut surfaces with margarine and sprinkle with brown sugar.
Arrange apple halves on the sauerkraut in a diagonal line across the center of the casserole.
Place 2 frankfurters on each side of the apple halves. Bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes.
Clean codfish well.
Boil and drain.
Clean again.
(Get rid of all salt.)
Boil again until tender.
Boil potatoes (firm).

i: 1 can French cut green beans, drained
d: Rinse beans
in
cold water twice and drain.
Fry bacon until crisp;
drain
and crumble.

i: 2 cans Bush's Baked Beans with Onions
d: Drain juice off of beans and discard. Drain juice off of pineapple and keep. Add 1/2 of the juice to mixture. Mix all ingredients. Add 1/2 retained pineapple juice
(all that you kept). Cook in crockpot on low for 4 hours without lid. Or bake in 350 degree oven for 45-60 minutes.

i: salt, Tabasco and red pepper to taste
d: Brown onions and meat; drain.
Add other ingredients; simmer. It's ready when you're ready, but even better the second day.

Sprinkle cracker crumbs in a 9 x 9-inch pan. Layer chocolate chips, coconut and pecans. Pour condensed milk over top of layered ingredients. Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes. Cool and cut into squares.

i: 1 (15 oz.) can chickpeas, rinsed and drained (garbanzo beans)
d: Place sundried tomatoes in small bowl.
Add hot water to cover; let stand 10 to 15 minutes or until tomatoes are softened. Drain well; cut tomatoes into quarters.
In food processor or blender, process chickpeas, yogurt, tahini, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic and ground pepper until smooth.
Stir in sundried tomatoes.
Refrigerate humus 3 hours or longer to blend flavors. To serve, spoon humus into small bowl; serve with pita wedges and celery, carrot and zucchini sticks for dipping.
Yield:
Brown hamburger and onions together.
Drain excess fat.
Mix all ingredients together with meat.
Bake for 45 minutes at 350°. Can be cooked in crock-pot on high.
Cream together egg, sugar, shortening and molasses.
Add rest of ingredients.
Roll in small balls, then roll in sugar.
Bake at 375° for about 10 minutes.

i: 1 (10 oz.) can refrigerated buttermilk biscuit dough
d: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Place biscuits 2-inches apart on an ungreased cookie sheet. Bake in preheated oven for 8 to 11 minutes or until golden brown. Place bacon in a large, deep skillet. Cook over medium-high heat until evenly brown. Drain and set aside.
Pinch lettuce in small pieces.
Combine all other ingredients except Fritos.
Add them just before serving.
Brown ground beef and onion in a large skillet. Drain and stir in barbecue sauce and brown sugar.
Heat until very hot. Set aside.
Separate biscuits and place each in an ungreased muffin cup.
Press dough to cover bottom and sides of cup. Spoon mixture into cups. Sprinkle with grated cheese. Bake at 400° for 10 to 15 minutes or until golden brown.
Cut
squid
into
rings or 3-inch pieces.
If tentacles are large,
cut

i: 2 small cans green chiles, seeded and chopped
d: Mix tomatoes, chiles, olives and onion. Add olive oil and vinegar.
Serve with corn chips.
(Or use canned tomatoes and blend all ingredients in a blender, leaving out oil and vinegar.)
Mix sugar, margarine, eggs and vinegar.
Mix well.
Add coconut, pecans and raisins.
Pour in unbaked pie shell.
Bake at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes.
Double amount of cukes, just make sure you have enough syrup to cover cukes after you have packed jars.
You may have to make some more, some cukes will take more some will take less, depends on how you pack jars.

i: approximately 1 Tbsp. dried parsley, soaked in water, then patted dry on paper towel
d: Mix all ingredients.
Form into a ball and roll in chopped pecans.
Serve with assorted crackers.
Separate artichokes and drain most of juice.
Add mayonnaise and Parmesan cheese.
Mix with beater until creamy as possible. Bake at 350° for about 15 minutes or until bubbly.
Beat margarine and sugar until creamy.
Add eggs, milk and vanilla and beat well.
Add flour, baking soda and salt.
Mix well. On ungreased cookie sheet, drop by tablespoonful.
Bake at 375° for 10 to 12 minutes.
Cool one minute before removing from cookie sheet.

i: 1 lb. chicken breast, chopped in squares
d: Heat oil in large skillet on medium high heat.
Add chicken, onion, and pepper.
Cook and stir until chicken is cooked.
Add corn, broth and salsa. Bring to a boil.
Stir in the rice and cover.
Remove from heat.
Rub outside of each
duck
well with oil.
Place celery pieces inside of each duck
and salt and pepper to taste, both inside and out.
Roast in hot

i: 1 (16 oz.) can fruit cocktail, well drained
d: Combine all ingredients, mixing well.
Chill about 1 hour. Makes 12 servings.

i: 2 pkg. (10 oz.) frozen strawberries, thawed and chopped
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
Combine dry ingredients in large bowl. Form a shallow well in center of mixture and add remaining ingredients.
Stir well.
Spoon mixture into two greased and floured loaf pans.
Bake 1 hour.
Cool 10 minutes in pans.

i: coconut, chopped nuts and chopped maraschino cherries for decoration
d: Bake cake mix as directed on package in 9 x 13-inch pan.
Boil for 5 minutes the pineapple with juice and the sugar.
Pour on top of hot cake and cool.
Add bananas on top.
Sprinkle with instant vanilla pudding.
Top with Cool Whip, then sprinkle with coconut, nuts and cherries.
Mix
all with flour.
May have to add a little more flour but make a soft dough.
Knead real good.
Put in a greased bowl.
Let rise until double in bulk, about

t: Poverele (Chilled Squash Salad With Fresh Mint)
d: Peel squash and cut into circles.
Cook squash until tender (do not overcook).
Combine squash, mint leaves (fresh only) and white vinegar.
Chill for 3 hours.
Serve with crusty Italian bread for dipping.

i: 1/2 lb. fresh green string beans, cut in 1 1/2-inch pieces
2 firm heads bibb or Boston lettuce
d: Cook string beans in boiling water to cover by 1 inch.
Boil briskly, uncovered, for 4 to 5 minutes.
Drain string beans and run under cold water.
Spread string beans on towel to drain. Refrigerate.
In bowl, combine vinegar, onions, thyme, savory, dry mustard, salt and pepper.
Mix ingredients well with whisk.
Brown meat.
Add the next 2 ingredients and refried beans. Line a 13 x 9-inch pan with tortilla chips.
Spread meat mixture. Layer chilies and cheese.
Bake at 325° for 25 minutes.
Let stand 10 minutes.
Serve with taco fixings, such as chopped lettuce, tomato, black olives, sour cream, etc.

i: 1 medium size bitter-melon (fresh or canned), sliced 1-inch thick
d: Combine all ingredients except eggplant, bitter-melon and okra.
Bring to a boil.
Lower heat and simmer, covered, until beef is tender.
Add eggplant, bitter-melon and okra; cook for 15 minutes.
If using canned bitter-melon, add last 3 minutes of cooking.
Shred cabbage, carrots and peppers.
(Can use large package of shredded cole slaw, but add peppers to it.) Salt to taste.
Combine all ingredients.
Cover and simmer for 2 hours. Serves 10.
Brown pork in oil and set aside.
Saute celery and onion in pork juices until limp.
Make jello and mix but substitute 1 pint of sour cream for 1 cup of water.
(Beat some cream with some jello to avoid curdling) and then add 1 can crushed pineapple, drained.
Let set and unmold.
May garnish with avocado slices.
Thoroughly cream together Crisco, oleo and sugar.
Mix in eggs and vanilla.
Alternately add flour with soda and buttermilk until blended.
Bake at 350° for about 30 minutes.
Makes 3 layers. Between cooled layers, spread cooled filling.
Slice one cup of strawberries and add sugar to taste (1/4 cup).
Stir gently until sugar dissolves.
For each serving, place a slice of Angel Food cake on dessert plate with a scoop of vanilla ice cream on top.
Spoon strawberries on ice cream and serve.

t: Pepper Steak A La Cheddar Noodles
i: 1 1/2 lb. steak, cut into 2 1/2 x 1/2 x 1/8 strips
1 1/2 c. green peppers, cut into narrow strips
d: Melt butter.
Add beef and sprinkle with garlic powder.
Saute until browned.
Remove meat from skillet.
Add onion and green pepper; saute 2 minutes.
Return to skillet with tomatoes and bouillon cube.
Place cabbage leaves in microwavable dish with 2 to 4 tablespoons water and microwave for about 3 minutes or until cabbage is limp.
Combine egg, milk, onion, salt, pepper, meat and cooked rice.
Place about 1/4 cup meat mixture in center of each leaf; fold in sides and roll ends over meat.
Place in crock-pot. Combine tomato sauce with brown sugar, lemon juice and Worcestershire sauce.
Pour over cabbage rolls.
Cover and cook on low 7 to 9 hours.
Mix low-fat yogurt and lemon juice or relish together.
Add Equal to taste; mix well.

i: 3/4 c. cold low-fat or skim milk
d: Heat oven at 450°.
Cut butter into flour until particles are fine like crumbs.
Add milk and stir with fork only until dough leaves side of bowl.
Turn dough out onto lightly floured board; knead just until smooth.
Roll dough out about 1/2 inch thick and cut with floured cutter.
Place on lightly greased baking sheet. Bake 10 to 12 minutes.
Soak the beans overnight in a large kettle of water.
Simmer them for about 1 hour in the water in which they were soaked.
Mix the first 4 ingredients together.
Mix the remaining ingredients in a saucepan.
Boil for 10 minutes; pour over first mixture.
Let set for 45 minutes before serving.
Good with fish.
Mix first four ingredients in a bowl.
Mix last three ingredients in a separate bowl. Combine and chill 2 to 4 hours. Drain and serve with chips.
Brown sausage.
Remove sausage and cook the pepper, onions and celery in as little grease as possible.
Boil 7 cups water.
In boiling water, put soup, rice and almonds.
Don't cook. Immediately stir all ingredients together.
Cover and bake at 350° for 40 to 45 minutes.
Mix together softened Cheddar cheese and mayonnaise.
Stir in remaining ingredients; stir alot.
Can keep for two weeks or more in refrigerator.
Serve with Wheat Thins crackers, Fritos, etc. Hot, hot, hot!
But loved by all.
Cook and remove chicken from bone and put in bottom of pan. Sprinkle salt and pepper over chicken.
Heat 2 cups chicken broth and can of soup and pour over chicken.
Mix flour, margarine (melted) and buttermilk, then spoon evenly over top of chicken. Bake at 425 degrees for 25 to 30 minutes or until golden brown.
Mix together the browned beef, onion, pepper, seasoned salt and tomato sauce.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Put 3 cups (crushed lightly) tortilla chips in a 2-quart casserole.
Sprinkle with 1 cup shredded Cheddar cheese.
Pour the meat sauce over the cheese.
Top with remaining cheese.
Drain fruit; mix all ingredients.
Can be made the night before.
Put cream cheese in mixer bowl 30 minutes to soften.
Add salt and vanilla.
Add 2 cups sugar; beat.
Add 2 more cups of sugar; beat.
Add remaining sugar and beat well.
Cover and chill in refrigerator until firm.

Cream together the butter and sugar. Add egg yolk and vanilla. Combine flour, baking powder and cinnamon. Combine with other ingredients. Grease sheet cake pan and pat flour mixture down into pan. Beat the egg white until frothy and spread over top of mixture. Chop the nuts and pat into mixture. Bake 1 hour at 300°. Cut while hot.

Brown ground beef and drain. Stir in Sloppy Joe mix, tomato paste and water; bring to a boil. Simmer 10 minutes; stir occasionally. Cook macaroni until done; drain. Mix all together and add corn and 1 cup cheese. Sprinkle the other cup of cheese on top. Bake in 9 x 13-inch baking dish at 325°, uncovered, for 30 to 45 minutes.
Combine dry ingredients in large bowl.
Mix honey and oil together.
Add honey and oil to dry mixture and mix thoroughly until moist and in clumps.
Preheat oven to 300°.
Spread mixture in 2 or 3 pans with sides.
Bake 15 minutes.
Heat oven to 375° to 400°.
Put shortening in a large cake pan and melt it.
(Put in oven to melt.)
In a mixing bowl, mix all the remaining ingredients by hand.
Beat about a minute, then pour into pan and bake in hot oven until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, about 25 to 30 minutes.
Place flour and salt in large bowl.
Mix until blended well. Add Crisco with pastry blender.
Cut Crisco into flour until size of small peas.
Pour in 7-Up (1/2 cup); stir with fork. Dough should be kind of sticky.
Turn onto floured board. Smooth out to small circle with hands, making sure flour stays under at all times.
Roll, then roll to size of pie plate needed. Flute edges under.
Cook spaghetti; drain (should have about 3 cups).
Stir butter into spaghetti.
Stir in Parmesan cheese and eggs.
Form spaghetti mixture into a "crust" in a buttered 10-inch pie plate.
Spread cottage cheese over bottom of spaghetti crust.
Combine mayonnaise and seasonings.
Mix with chicken, fruit and celery.
Place a bed of lettuce on 2 dinner plates and spoon 1/2 of chicken mixture on top of each.

i: 4 stalks celery, cut into 1/4-inch diagonal slices
2 green peppers, seeded and cut into 1/4-inch slices
1 (16 oz.) can artichoke hearts, drained and halved
d: Combine ingredients in large bowl.
In a large pot saut onions and bacon until tender.
Add cabbage, water, salt and pepper.
Cover and simmer until done.
Mix cornstarch with water and add to cabbage.
Cook until liquid is slightly thick.

i: 1 (3 oz.) pkg. whipped cream cheese with chives
d: Cook pasta shells, drain and toss with margarine, set aside. Brown ground beef and drain.
Stir cream cheese into ground beef, add taco seasoning (enough to taste) and salt, simmer for 5 minutes.

i: 1 (7 oz.) pkg. tortellini with Parmesan cheese
d: Saute onion and garlic in butter.
Add crawfish (slightly rinsed) and continue cooking approximately 5 minutes.
Add Parmesan cheese, parsley and whipping cream.
Prepare tortellini as directed.
Fold in with sauce and season with salt and red pepper.
If sauce needs thickening, mix cornstarch with water and add to sauce.
Mix cucumbers, onions, peppers and salt.
Cover with chopped ice for 3 hours; drain.
Combine remaining ingredients.
Pour over cucumbers and bring to a boil.
Pour into jars.
This is good on hot dogs or hamburger sandwiches!
Soften yeast in water; let stand while combining sour cream, sugar and baking soda in large bowl.
Add 2 cups biscuit mix, then yeast mixture.
Mix well.
Stir in 3 more cups biscuit mix.
Turn dough onto board dusted with 1/2 cup biscuit mix; knead to form smooth ball.
Shape dough into small rounds the size of a walnut. Place close together in buttered 9 x 13 x 2-inch pan.
Slice sirloin steak into thin strips.
Heat 1/4 cup vegetable oil and add to meat.
When brown, add 1 cup water, 1 medium sliced onion, garlic and ginger.
Heat to boiling; reduce heat, cover and simmer 15 minutes.
Add green pepper strips during the last 5 minutes of simmering.
Blend cornstarch and soy sauce; stir into beef mixture.
Brown ground beef and sausage.
Stir in spices and cheese. Heat until cheese is melted.
Put 1 Tbsp. meat mixture on thin slice of rye bread.
Cut slices into 4 pieces each.
Brown in oven for 15 minutes.
Serve warm.
Combine first 7 ingredients in crock-pot; mix well.
Add salt and pepper.
Cook on low for 3 to 4 hours or until done to taste. Heat cheese and tomatoes in saucepan until cheese melts, stirring constantly.
Layer lettuce, ground beef sauce, tomatoes, cheese sauce and crushed corn chips on individual plates.
Yield:
12 servings.
Preheat oven to 400°.
Lightly grease a 10-inch pie plate. Sprinkle bacon, cheese and onion in pie plate.
Beat milk, Bisquick, eggs, salt and pepper in a medium size bowl with electric mixer until smooth, about 1 minute.

Pour into pie plate. Bake in hot oven (400°) for 35 minutes or until golden brown and knife inserted halfway between center and edges comes out clean. Garnish with tomato slices and bacon strips if you wish. Refrigerate remaining quiche.

i: 1 large bottle Ranch dressing with bacon
d: Dice all of the ingredients into small bite size pieces in a large mixing bowl.
Combine all ingredients and salt lightly.
Do not use broccoli florets, just the stems; peel and dice.
Pour the entire bottle of Ranch dressing into mixture; stir and chill.
Will keep in refrigerator for days.
Mix all ingredients together and serve with raw vegetables. If thinned with a bit of milk, this also makes a good salad dressing.

t: Curried Butternut Squash Bisque(A Spicy First Course Soup)
i: 3 lb. butternut squash, peeled, seeded and cubed
salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
d: In a heavy soup pot, heat oil over medium heat.
Add onions and garlic; saute for 2 to 3 minutes or until slightly softened.
Stir in curry and cumin and cook for 1 minute.
Add chicken stock, cider, rice and squash; bring to a boil.
Reduce to low heat and cover, then simmer for 30 to 40 minutes, or until squash is tender. Pour mixture through a strainer set over a large bowl.
Puree the solids in a food processor or blender until very smooth.
Saute onion in oil until clear.
Add remaining ingredients and simmer over low heat 20 to 30 minutes, until desired thickness. Keeps up to 1 month in refrigerator.

i: 1 pkg. or can English peas, uncooked
d: Layer first 5 ingredients in large dish.
Cover with mayonnaise; sprinkle with sugar and grated cheese.
Cook bacon and crumble over mixture.
Cover dish tightly with foil and refrigerate overnight.

i: chopped garlic clove or garlic powder
d: Brown hamburger meat.
Simmer first 9 ingredients for about 30 minutes.
Add garlic clove or garlic powder.
Oregano may be used, if desired.
Pour over spaghetti.
Add mushroom soup and 1 can tomato soup.
Dissolve Jell-O in boiling water.
Drain pineapple and reserve juice; add water to juice to make 2 cups.
Stir into Jell-O mixture.
Chill until the consistency of unbeaten egg whites is reached.
Stir in pineapple, carrots, cheese and pecans.
Pour into a lightly oiled 6 cup mold.
Pour 2 1/2 cups batter into a 9 x 13-inch cake pan. Bake at 350° for 15 minutes.
Melt the caramels, milk and margarine together over baked cake.
Pour rest of batter over caramel mixture. Sprinkle pecans and chocolate chips on top. Bake at 350° for 20 to 25 minutes.
Boil noodles until done.
Brown hamburger meat.
In a large bowl, combine hamburger meat, Ragu sauce, sour cream, cottage cheese, salt and garlic powder.

i: 1 lb. cooked shrimp or crab flakes
d: Mix all ingredients, except parsley, shrimp (or crab) and cocktail sauce.
Spread on glass plate.
Top with cocktail sauce and shrimp (or crab); sprinkle with parsley.
Serve with chips or crackers.

i: 2 cans white chicken chunks in water
d: Combine, adding just enough mayonnaise to moisten ingredients. Serve on lettuce leaf with cheese and crackers.
For sandwiches, leave out raisins and apples.
Mix sugar, cocoa, milk and oleo in a saucepan.
Bring to a boil and boil for 1 minute.
Remove from stove and add remaining ingredients.
Stir well.
Drop by teaspoon on wax paper.
Makes 4 to 5 dozen.
Separate eggs; beat whites for 2 minutes in a small bowl of electric mixer.
Into large bowl of mixer, sift together flour, baking powder, salt and sugar.
Add milk and shortening or oil. Mix at speed 1 for 1 minute.
Add beaten whites and mix at speed 1 for 1 minute.
Bake on hot waffle iron until golden brown.
Brown and drain ground beef, onion, salt and pepper.
Add Prego, tomato juice, oregano and sweet basil; simmer 20 minutes. Cook macaroni until tender and drain.
Grate cheese.
Layer in 2-quart casserole dish the meat sauce, macaroni, cheese, macaroni and meat sauce.
Bake at 325° for 30 to 35 minutes.
