Add remaining cheese on top and bake until melted.

i: 1/2 lb. each: fresh green beans and fresh sliced mushrooms
fresh basil, pinch of oregano and fresh garlic (lots)
d: Saute ground lamb in olive; pour off fat.
Put in Dutch oven. Add chickpeas.
Saute mushrooms, celery and onions in olive oil. Add to lamb stew; add tomato sauce.
Add garlic, basil, oregano, salt and pepper.
Add green beans, yellow pepper and tomato.
Cook 45 minutes on medium-low.

i: 1 to 2 boxes thawed frozen green peas
d: Layer in round, clear glass bowl in order listed:
lettuce (in bite size pieces), chopped celery, chopped onions, thawed peas and layer of mayonnaise.
Sprinkle with sugar and Parmesan cheese. Refrigerate overnight.
Pour boiling water over meal.
Let stand until cool.
Add sugar, salt and beaten egg.
Dissolve soda into buttermilk and add to batter; mix well.
Add melted shortening; pour in greased deep dish or skillet.
Bake at 400° for 25 to 30 minutes.
Mix ingredients together.
Use for deep frying vegetables when camping.
Use for onions, green pepper rings, zucchini slices, cauliflower, mushrooms and cheese as well as hot dogs.
Brown hamburger and onions.
Add all ingredients except tomato sauce and lemon juice.
Add these just before serving.
Pour hot water over Jell-O.
When dissolved, add sugar.
Cut orange in pieces with peelings and put in blender with cranberries and orange juice.
Blend.
Combine all ingredients and chill in refrigerator overnight.

i: 2 (16 oz.) cans pork and beans, drained
d: Mix all ingredients, laying strips of bacon on top.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Serves 8.
Microwave chocolate and milk in 1 1/2-quart microwavable bowl on High 2 minutes, stirring after 1 minute.
Stir until chocolate is completely melted and smooth.
Stir in vanilla, salt and nuts. Spread in greased 8 x 4-inch or 9 x 5-inch loaf pan.
Refrigerate until firm, about 2 hours.
Makes about 2 dozen pieces.
Preheat oven to 400°.
Sift dry ingredients together, then, using a pastry blender or two knives, cut in butter.
Combine milk and egg and slowly add to flour until mixture is wet enough to hold together.
Turn onto a floured board.
Knead, then roll to 1/2-inch thickness and cut with a biscuit cutter.
Bake for 10 to 12 minutes or until lightly golden.

i: 4 1/2 lb. apples, peeled, cored and sliced
d: Mix all ingredients in a large bowl.
Freeze or pour into prepared pie shells and bake in a 375° oven for 40 to 50 minutes. Makes filling for two 9-inch pies.
Keeps for 6 months frozen.
Blend milk and flour.
Cook to a thick paste in a small saucepan over medium heat, stirring constantly, until all mixture leaves sides of pan and forms a ball on the spoon.
Cool.
Blend the oil and spices in a small bowl.
Rub mixture over the entire roast.
Place in shallow pan and bake at 350° for 1 to 1 1/2 hours or until internal temperature is 155°.
Let stand for 10 minutes before slicing.
Early in day (6 to 8 hours) ahead, cut up cabbage and place in bowl.
Slice onion and place on top of cabbage.
Mix together and heat:
sugar, vinegar, celery seed, oil and salt.
Heat until sugar dissolves.
Pour over cabbage; do not stir.
Line an 8 x 8 x 2-inch baking pan with aluminum foil.
Sift together sugar and cocoa into 2-quart baking dish.
Add evaporated milk and butter.
Do not stir.
Place in microwave for 2 1/2 minutes or unti margarine is just melted.
Stir in vanilla and nuts to blend all together.

i: onion, garlic, celery salt and paprika
d: Saute rice, spices and butter. Add other ingredients and cook, covered, at 350° for 1 hour.
Stir coconut in frying pan until crisp; set aside.
Cook onion, ginger and garlic in oil.
Saut pork in same pan.
Spoon off fat.
Add lime juice, cilantro, peanuts, coconut, chilies and soy sauce.
Put in a bowl and serve with lettuce leaves to wrap pork.
To make dressing mix together sugar, salt, pepper, celery seed, mustard and vinegar.
Add mayonnaise, slowly, stirring well. Refrigerate dressing until ready to make slaw.
Cut garlic in half, rub salad bowl with cut side of garlic clove.
Add cut cabbage.
Pour desired amount of dressing over cabbage and mix well.
Chill.
Mix all ingredients together in buttered casserole dish. Sprinkle grated cheese over top of casserole.
Bake at 350° until bubbly, at least 1 hour.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.
After
topping	has been added, bake in a 350° oven for 30 minutes.
Potatoes	may be prepared several days ahead and refrigerated.
Topping should not be added until you are ready to bake them.
Place one whole chicken (cut) on sheet pan sprayed with Pam or greased with butter.
Place in oven set at 450° for 3 hours.
Stir yeast, salt and sugar into flour.
Heat milk until hot to touch.
Add milk and oil to dry ingredients, beating until well blended, for about 5 minutes.
Then with a spoon, add as much flour as needed.
Turn out onto a floured surface and knead until smooth. Place into a greased or oiled bowl and cover with a towel.
Let rise until double.
Saute chopped onions in 1/2 cup oil.
Stir until golden. Drain the onions and set them in a bowl.
Add 1 cup raw rice, chopped parsley, salt, pepper and a dash of garlic.
(Meat is optional.)
Mix them all.

t: Vinegar Sweet Pickles(This Recipe Was Made Up By Olive Bowman.)
d: Place fresh gathered cucumbers into jars cold.
It is better to quarter them and use enough small cucumbers to make a wedge in center of jars so they won't float.
Put warm solution over cucumbers and seal tightly with jars that have been placed in cold pack canner.
Let water boil over tops of jars for 10 minutes in order for them to seal.
Remove from canner and screw tightly. If you overprocess them, they will shrink and be no good.
Make several solutions for a great deal of cucumbers.
Bring to a boil and set for 1 hour.
Drain canned vegetables into a bowl.
Add green peppers, celery and pimento.
Mix together oil, vinegar, sugar, water, paprika and mustard until sugar melts.
Pour over vegetables.
Marinate for 24 hours.
Usually yields 2 empty jars of Tang.
Mix all ingredients. Use 2 teaspoons per cup of hot water.

i: 1 1/2 lb. raw shrimp, peeled and cleaned
d: Brown shrimp in oil.
Add vermouth, garlic, salt and pepper. Saute until liquid is nearly gone.
Sprinkle with parsley and lemon juice.
Serves 4.

i: 1 pkg. frozen spinach, cooked and drained
1 can water chestnuts, drained and sliced
1/2 small onion, chopped or dehydrated onions (use your own judgement)
d: Mix all ingredients together and refrigerate overnight.
Cut the top off 1 loaf of Vienna Italian bread on an angle.
Take top off bread and cut into cubes.
(Place in plastic bag until ready to use.)
Keep bread fresh and when ready to serve, put dip in center of loaf and dip with cut up bread.
Slice apples into a greased 9 x 9-inch pan.
Mix flour, sugar and baking powder.
Stir egg into dry
mixture, until it forms beads.
Pour over apples.
Melt margarine with cinnamon and pour over top.

t: Fresh Tomato Sauce For Pasta Or Pizza Crust, Etc.
d: Heat oil in a large skillet.
Add onion and garlic.
Saute 3 minutes.
Add tomatoes and remaining ingredients.
Stir well and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to medium-low and cook 10 minutes or until reduced to 4 cups.
Preheat oven to 400°.
In a large bowl, pour boiling water over sugar, Crisco and salt; let cool.
Meanwhile, dissolve yeast in lukewarm water and let set 5 minutes.
Stir in 1 tablespoon sugar; add to cooled water mixture.
Beat eggs thoroughly and put into water mixture.
Mix 4 scant cups of flour into water and mix with mixer.
Marinate chicken in soy sauce for 1 hour and drain.
Dot with butter; brown on both sides under broiler.
Place in casserole dish; add pineapple juice and cornstarch.
Simmer together on low heat (300°) for 1 1/2 hours.
Add tidbits and mushrooms.
Heat through.
Mix Ricotta, eggs, 1/2 cup Romano, salt, pepper and Accent until well combined.
Combine butter and graham cracker crumbs. Add condensed milk and stir. Add the remaining ingredients and mix thoroughly.
Scald and peel tomatoes.
Cut up or mash with hands.
Put on to cook.
While tomatoes cook, peel and grind the onions and hot peppers.
Add to tomatoes.
Cook until very thick.
Heat
about 10 minutes in microwave.
Stir during first four minutes and again after the next four minutes.
Beat Scramblers in large bowl.
Mix in evaporated milk, sugar and vanilla until sugar dissolves.
Pour into freezer and add enough 2% milk to fill 1 gallon freezer.
Freeze and enjoy.
Can add 3 bananas, 1 pint strawberries or 6 peaches.

t: Curry Chicken With Pineapple In Coconut Milk
i: 1 1/2 to 2 lbs. boneless chicken cut into bite size pieces or shrimp, peeled
1 6 oz. can bamboo shoots, cut into slivers
1-2 oz. curry paste, about 1 teaspoon, adjust to taste (do not substitute powder)
2 20 oz. cans pineapple tidbits with juice
2 tsp. dried basil or 2 tbsp. fresh basil, cut up
1 14 oz. can baby corn (on cob) drained, cut into bite size pieces (optional)
d: Saute cut chicken in oil. If using shrimp, DO NOT cook through, it will be tough. Add curry paste and mix to coat.
In a large pot, combine remaining ingredients. Add cooked chicken or shrimp (do not drain). Boil for 3 minutes, longer is you want carrots crisp-tender.
Serve over rice, saffron rice is best. It's better the next day!

i: saltine crackers (enough to make about 2 c.)
d: Grease medium size baking dish. Add a thin layer of crackers, crushed. Arrange a layer of oysters. Use half the margarine and dot over the oysters. Sprinkle with pepper. Repeat layers until all oysters are used. Be sure to top with a layer of crackers. Take oyster liquid, milk, rest of margarine plus Worcestershire sauce and heat in pan; do not let it boil. Pour over casserole until you can see the milk. Bake at 350° for about 20 minutes.
Cook rice.
Brown meat alone.
Brown carrots, green peppers and onion (do carrots first).
Add vegetables to meat.
Add soy sauce, pepper and celery.
Add rice, then bean sprouts.
Combine cornmeal, salt and pepper in plastic bag.
Add okra and shake well to blend and coat 1 cup of okra at at time to cornmeal mix.
Put 1/4 cup oil into a cast iron frying pan and heat until hot.
Add coated okra a little at a time until brown.
Yield about 10 servings.
(Add additional oil as needed.)
Boil raisins in 1 3/4 cups water for 5 minutes.
Combine brown sugar, cornstarch, cinnamon and salt.
Moisten with remaining 1/4 cup water.
Add to raisins, stirring until mixture boils.
Remove from heat and add butter and vinegar.
Pour into pastry lined 9-inch pie pan.
Saute onion and celery in butter until soft.
Add flour and blend.
Add milk and blend.
Remove from heat.
Add egg yolks, crabmeat, salt, pepper and cheese.
Put in a casserole and top with cheese.
Bring sugar, margarine, syrup and milk to a boil.
Boil for 2 minutes, stirring.
Add cocoa.
Boil 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add vanilla.
Stir until it thickens.
Saute onions and celery in 1/2 cup water until tender.
Blend sunflower seeds and sesame seed.
In bowl, mix together the oatmeal, blended seed, pecan meal, yeast, onion powder, garlic powder, Soy Supreme, bread crumbs, then add soy sauce, onions and celery.
Add water; mix well.
Bake in sprayed baking dish at 325° for 60 minutes until brown.
Serve with Creamy Cashew Gravy.
Mix milk and Cool Whip; refrigerate for one hour.
Add cherry pie filling and crushed pineapple.
Mix well; add pecans and refrigerate until serving time.

i: 1 can lima beans or frozen lima beans, cooked
d: Drain all vegetables.
Mix everything else into the vegetables and refrigerate.

i: pepper and garlic powder to taste
d: Combine flour, salt, pepper and garlic powder in a bag.
Shake the chicken pieces individually until coated.
Brown in the butter in a skillet over medium-high heat, pour off fat.
In a bowl, combine remaining ingredients.
Add to skillet and simmer, covered, 25 minutes or until chicken is tender.
Serves 4.
Mix ingredients.
Pour into unbaked pie shells.
Sprinkle more nuts on top.
Bake at 350°
for 35 minutes.
Makes 2 pies.
Peel, cook and slice sweet potatoes into 1-inch slices.
Bring sugar, nectar, butter, lemon peel, cinnamon and nutmeg to a boil. Boil gently for 2 minutes.
Arrange potatoes in a 12 x 8 x 2-inch pan.
Pour sauce over potatoes.
Sprinkle with nuts.
Bake at 350° for about 30 minutes or until potatoes are heated through and sauce is bubbly.
Cut shortening into flour with a fork until the mixture is the consistency of small peas and corn meal.
Combine remaining ingredients and mix with flour.
Roll dough to 1/8-inch thickness. Cut into various shapes with cookie cutters.
Bake at 350° for 8 to 10 minutes.
Combine all but last two ingredients together and pour into a well-greased and floured sheet cake pan.
Top with brown sugar and chopped pecans.
Mix all ingredients together and cook for 5 minutes.

i: 1 c. cherry or raspberry jam (warm jam to use)
d: Mix well with fingers the oats, flour, sugar, salt and shortening.
Pat 1/2 mixture into greased 9 x 9 x 2-inch pan. Spread jelly evenly over the dough.
Sprinkle remaining batter over the jelly layer.
Lightly pat down the topping with your fingers.
Bake in a preheated 350° oven for 25 to 30 minutes, or until golden brown.
Cool in pan on a rack.
Heat the oil in a large pan or skillet.
Lightly saute the onions, garlic and mushrooms.
Add the wine and reduce to one quarter the original volume.
Add artichoke hearts and heavy cream and cook until the sauce is the correct consistency.
Add the red pimento.
Then poach the scallops in this sauce for approximately 2 minutes.
Mix together.
Dipping hands in water, make small balls. Grease pans or use Teflon cookie sheets.
Bake at 425° until brown.

i: 1 diced onion (red gives salad nice color)
d: Mix first 4 ingredients together well and add remaining ingredients. Mix vegetables with dressing and refrigerate several hours or overnight.
Wash tomatoes and remove stem ends.
Cut tomatoes in half crosswise.
Melt butter in saucepan.
Brush over cut surface of tomatoes with butter.
Combine bread crumbs, rosemary, basil, salt and pepper.
Sprinkle crumbs over tops of tomatoes.

i: 1 envelope Ranch buttermilk salad dressing mix
d: Mix Ranch salad dressing mix and oil until blended.
Add spices.
Pour over crackers.
Put in warm oven (about 200°) and stir several times.
Heat about 40 minutes or until dressing is gone.

Mix together cabbage, onions, pepper and pimentos. Make a syrup from vinegar, mustard seed, turmeric, sugar, celery seed and salt. Stir and simmer syrup for 6 minutes. Cool and then pour over slaw mixture. Let set at least 24 hours before using. The longer it sets, the better it is. Will keep as long as 5 or 6 months. Store in glass jar or similar container.
Heat chili.
Cook eggs sunny side up.
Place eggs over chili and serve with warmed tortillas.
Mix and cook 30 minutes.
Put in hot jars and seal.

i: 1 large can peach or apricot halves or both
d: Melt butter; add sugar and curry. Drain and dry fruits and place in large 1 1/2-quart casserole. Add butter mixture. Bake 1 hour, uncovered.
Dissolve sugar in beer and add to biscuit mix.
Mix and bake in accordance with directions on biscuit mix container.

i: 1/2 lb. breakfast sausage, browned and drained
d: Mix all ingredients together.
Place in a lightly greased, large, heavy cast-iron skillet.
Bake at 400° for 30 minutes or until browned.

i: 1 small jar stuffed green olives, chopped
d: Combine all ingredients except crumbs. Form into a ball and roll in bread crumbs. Serve with crackers.

i: 8 oz. crushed pineapple, drained and reserving juice
d: Mix together flour, sugar and oil well.
To this mixture, add egg yolks, cinnamon, water, pineapple and nuts.
Blend well.
Add egg whites and blend.
Grease and flour tube pan and bake at 325° for 1
hour and 10 minutes.

i: 2 cans Show Boat pork and beans
3/4 c. Kraft hickory flavor barbecue sauce
d: Mix all ingredients together.
Place in a 350° oven for 1 hour.
Mix Jell-O and boiling water in large container.
Add cold water and stir.
Add sugar and stir.
Add lemonade and stir.
Add pineapple juice and stir thoroughly.
Freeze in appropriate containers (large mouth plastic gallon jugs are good).
Sift and measure the flour.
Cut the butter into the flour. Break the egg into a measuring cup, then add enough water to come up to the 3/4 cup line.
Add to the flour; mix lightly with a fork. Drop by tablespoonfuls over boiling meat and broth or into well-greased steamer placed over boiling water.
Cook for 15 to 20 minutes, or until dumplings are tender.

i: 8 oz. rigatoni or other favorite pasta
d: Cook and drain pasta.
While it's cooking, heat oil in large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add shrimp, peppers, mushrooms, garlic and basil.
Cook, stirring frequently, to prevent sticking, until shrimp are cooked through and peppers are almost tender (3 to 4 minutes).
Stir in tomatoes and picante sauce; simmer 2 to 3 minutes (stirring frequently).
Add pasta; mix well.

i: 2 1/2 lb. pickling cucumbers, thinly sliced (8 c.)
1 green pepper, cut into thin strips
d: In a large mixing bowl, combine cucumbers and onion.
Sprinkle with salt.
Cover with ice water and let stand for 3 hours.
Drain well.
Rinse and drain again.
Combine all ingredients.

i: 3 envelopes instant chicken broth and seasoning mix (Weight Watchers brand)
d: Combine turkey, bread crumbs and garlic powder.
Form into balls.
Spray large skillet with cooking spray.
Brown turkey balls on all sides; drain.
In same skillet, heat Butter Buds and saute onions.
Add flour.

i: 1 (10 oz.) can diced tomatoes with green chilies
d: In large bowl combine all ingredients; mix until well blended. Pour batter into preheated 9-inch cake pan coated with vegetable spray.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes or until bread tests done with toothpick.
Remove from pan; cool on wire rack.
Serve warm. Yields 10 servings.

i: 1 c. each: candied pineapple, lemon peel, orange peel, citron, cut up
1 c. whole red and green cherries
4 1/2 c. blanched almonds and pecans, cut up
Betty Crocker fluffy white frosting mix
d: Bake cake mix, following package directions.
Cool.
Crumble cake into very large bowl or pan.
Add fruit to crumbled cake. Make Betty Crocker frosting mix, following package directions. Mix with cake and fruit.
Pack tightly into foil-lined 10-inch tube pan or two 9 x 3-inch loaf pans.
Cover cake with foil and chill in refrigerator at least 24 hours.

i: 15 oz. tomato sauce or homemade tomatoes
d: Brown meat, onion and garlic.
Stir in tomatoes and spices. Simmer, uncovered, 10 minutes.
Combine cottage cheese, egg and Parmesan cheese; set aside.
Slice zucchini.
Put in casserole dish (13 x 9 x 2-inch), half of zucchini, half of cottage cheese mixture, half meat mixture.
Then repeat and bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Drain asparagus.
Alternate layers of asparagus, grated onion and grated cheese.
Pour on a layer of soup.
Sprinkle with onion. Place in oven at 350° until bubbly.
Remove and place buttered bread crumbs on top and brown while dressing.
Cut broccoli into bite-size pieces.
With knife held in slanting position, almost parallel to the cutting surface, cut tenderloin crosswise into 1/8-inch thick slices.
In medium bowl, mix pork with next 5 ingredients.
Combine ingredients with 1/2 cup water and simmer 30 minutes. Pack in hot sterile jars to within 1/2 of top.
Seal at once and water bath 10 minutes.
Makes 7 half pints.
Chill before serving.
Mix jello into boiling water; add cold water and cornstarch. Bring to a slow boil.
Boil 1 minute and cool to warm.
Add fresh fruit, bananas and vanilla.
Top with fat free yogurt.
Serve over your favorite pancake or waffle.
See oatmeal pancake recipe.

i: 1 can Trappey's navy beans with jalapenos
d: Drain Trappey's beans; coarsely chop onion and jalapeno peppers.
Combine all ingredients and refrigerate.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Alter olive oil and vinegar to taste.
Combine all ingredients with electric mixer.
Pour into baking dish.
Bake 35 to 40 minutes at 300° until about firm.
Add topping during last 15 minutes of baking time.
Mix flour and melted margarine.
Pat mixture in 9 x 13-inch baking pan.
Bake at 375° for 10 minutes.
Let cool.
Melt Milky Way bars and 1 stick margarine in double boiler. Set aside.
Heat oven to 350°.
Grind rosemary in a spice or coffee grinder.
Combine with remaining ingredients and spread on a baking sheet.
Bake for 10 to 12 minutes, or until crisp and brown.

i: 3 (1 qt.) bottles cranberry juice cocktail, chilled
1 (12 oz.) can frozen lemonade concentrate, thawed
d: Combine cranberry juice cocktail and concentrate.
Stir well; chill.
Just before serving, pour into a 4 quart punch bowl.
Add scoops of sherbet.
Slowly add ginger ale.
Makes about 50 (1/2 cup or 4 ounce) servings.
Combine ingredients in an electric blender or with the potato masher.
Delicious with pita bread, as a dip, or as a sandwich on whole wheat bread.

i: 1 (8 oz.) small can crushed pineapple, drained
1 pkg. Dream Whip or small Cool Whip
d: Cream together cream cheese and mayonnaise.
Add and mix marshmallows, pineapple, fruit cocktail and Cool Whip.
Freeze.
Mix yeast, warm water and 2 tsp. sugar until dissolved.
Mix all ingredients.
Let rise in bowl 10 minutes.
Roll it out and let biscuits rise on the pan for 10 minutes.
Combine ingredients and boil uncovered 15 minutes.
Thicken with a small amount of flour if the mixture is too thin.
Ladle into warm canning jars, leaving 1/8-inch headspace.
Seal immediately.
Process in boiling water bath 15 minutes.
Very good with roast, men love it!

i: 1 pkg. frozen Italian cut green beans
d: Cube beef and brown in salad oil.
Add chopped onion, celery and bell pepper; brown.
Mix together the water, cornstarch and soy sauce; add to mixture.
Add the mushrooms and green beans and cook for 10 or 12 minutes or until beans are done, stirring occasionally.
Serve over cooked Minute rice.

i: 14 1/2 oz. Pet milk (new cans are only 12 oz. You must add to make 14 1/2 oz.)
d: Stir together first 3 ingredients.
Boil 7 1/2 minutes or until it makes a soft ball when dropped in cold water.
Remove from heat and add chocolate chips and marshmallows.
Add vanilla.
Do not use a mixer!
Stir until marshmallows are melted.
Cream butter.
Add sugar, creaming until light and fluffy. Add slightly beaten eggs and other ingredients.
When all is blended, pour into unbaked pie shell.
Bake on lower shelf of oven at 350° for 45 minutes.
Makes two 9-inch pies.
Beat egg.
Stir in milk.
Add blueberries and oil.
Pour in flour mixture that has been sifted together (flour, salt and baking powder) all at once and stir until flour is moistened. Fill paper lined muffin tins about 3/4 full.
Bake at 400° for 20 minutes.
Makes 12 muffins.
Beat eggs in separate container.
Mix other ingredients.
In large skillet, lightly buttered, put sausage mix.
Add eggs; stir until eggs are softly cooked.
Remove from heat.
Place eggs in spaghetti strainer (something to let the juice drain through). Soften flour tortillas.
Cook and drain broccoli.
Add rice and cheese. Saute onion in oleo and add to mixture.
Pour into greased casserole dish and bake at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes.
Lightly grease a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Mix soup, sour cream and chives together.
Layer in pan the potatoes, soup mixture and cheese.
Repeat, ending with grated cheese on top.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Soften cream cheese with fork.
Add horseradish and Old Bay. Combine and add shrimp with a little of juice from each can.
Form into a ball in plastic wrap.
Put in refrigerator for a couple of hours.
Before serving, pour some cocktail sauce over ball and add as necessary.
Serves 10 to 15 people.
Brown hamburg and onion in oil. Work hamburg with fork until fine. Add remaining ingredients. Salt and pepper.
Simmer no less than 2 hours.
Core cabbage; slice head into 1 1/2-inch wedges.
In large skillet, melt margarine.
Add cabbage, parsley, sugar, pepper and broth.
Cover skillet and cook cabbage over medium heat for 12 minutes, basting several times with pan juices.
When cabbage is nearly done, sprinkle it with caraway seed.
Serve cabbage with a little of the pan juices.
Brown sausage; add beaten eggs (usually 6) to sausage and scramble together.
Heat tortillas in wet paper towels (30 to 60 seconds).
Place cheese slice on hot tortilla and add sausage and eggs.
Use hot sauce as desired.
Roll 'em up and eat.
Shred cabbage fine.
Grate peeled carrots into cabbage.
Slice scallions (mostly green and some of white); mix with cabbage and carrots.
In separate bowl, mix remaining ingredients.
Pour over cabbage and mix well.
Refrigerate for 2 hours before serving.
Brown lamb and onion.
Add beans, ketchup, brown sugar, vinegar and mustard in a large casserole.
Top with bacon.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.
Mix Kool-Aid, sugar and water.
Add pineapple juice and chill. (One package cherry Kool-Aid and one package raspberry Kool-Aid make a good flavor combination.
For green punch, use lemon-lime Kool-Aid.)
Pour over ice in punch bowl.
Add chilled ginger ale. Serve at once.
Makes 50 half cup servings.
Cream shortening and sugar, add egg and molasses.
Sift remaining ingredients into egg mixture.
Make balls, walnut-size. Dip tops in sugar.
Place apart on greased cookie sheet.
Do not press balls down.
Bake at 350°.
Preheat oven to 350°. Grease and flour a 10-inch fluted tube pan. Set pan aside.

i: 1/4 c. ketchup or 2 Tbsp. tomato paste
d: Singe, wash, dry and cut up chicken.
Brown it in heated butter or oleo.
Mix in onion, Accent, salt, ketchup or tomato paste and boiling water.
Cover and simmer 25 minutes.
Lift out chicken. Combine cornstarch, curry powder and cold water; stir into gravy. Cook, stirring constantly, until smoothly thickened.
Return chicken pieces to gravy.
Bake and peel potatoes (or boil with skins on) at 400° for 1 hour.
Cool and peel potatoes. Mash pulp and stir in raisins, sugar, cinnamon and pineapple.
Pour into baking pan and sprinkle with chopped pecans.
Bake, covered, at 400° for 15 minutes.
Mix all ingredients except pimentos.
Boil slowly for 1 hour, stirring occasionally.
Add pimentos; boil for 5 minutes more. Pack into jars and seal.
Melt chocolate chips until smooth.
Remove from stove.
Add evaporated milk and vanilla.
Pour mix into 9 x 9-inch metal or glass pan.
Refrigerate until solid.

i: 1/16 tsp. baking powder (I just take a pinch in my fingers)
d: Beat eggs, salt and baking powder together good.
Then add flour to make a stiff dough.
Flour linen table cloth and sprinkle with flour.
Put dough on floured table cloth and knead until smooth.
Roll out on cloth and let dry.
When dry, fold in half, then in half again until a narrow roll.

i: 1 can condensed cream of mushroom soup (undiluted)
1 Tbsp. fresh dill, chopped or 1 tsp. dill weed
d: Dip pieces of chicken in melted margarine, then roll pieces in crushed Rice Krispies.
Season to taste.
Place in pan lined with aluminum foil.
Bake for 1 hour at 350°.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Place liners in 12 (1/2-cup) muffin cups or lightly grease.

i: 1 (6 oz.) pkg. long grain and wild rice
d: Place rice, seasoning packet, butter, water chestnuts, mushrooms, onion and soy sauce in a 3-quart casserole.
Microwave on High (100%) for 3 to 4 minutes or until onion is tender.
Stir halfway through cooking time.
Pour in chicken broth.
Cover with a tight fitting lid or plastic wrap.
Microwave on High for 5 to 7 minutes or until boiling.

i: 1/2 c. Vidalia or sweet red onions, chopped
d: Mix all the ingredients and stir in dressing.

i: 6 Tbsp salt (I use half the amount)
d: Mix together and use to season your favorite meat.
Brush shrimp with oil and broil for 3 minutes.
Mix olive oil and lemon juice and pour over broiled shrimp.
Serve.
Peel onions; cut into even slices.
Heat butter in large skillet.
Add onions and toss for a few minutes until coated with butter.
Do not brown.
Add vinegar, brown sugar and curry powder. Stir to blend.
Add parsley.

i: 1/3 c. lime juice (fresh is best, about 3 limes)
2 lb. top round, sirloin or flank steak
d: In a small bowl whisk together all ingredients except steak and lime wedges.
Score steak across top or pierce top of steak with a fork.
Place in a shallow glass baking dish and pour lime juice mixture over the steak; turn to coat both sides. Refrigerate 6 to 8 hours to "marry" the flavors, turning occasionally.
Remove steak from marinade and grill 5 or 6 minutes on each side or to desired doneness.
Slice thinly on the diagonal; serve with lime wedges.
Makes 6 servings.
Chop cabbage, onion, bell pepper and carrot. Add sugar and set aside. Boil 1/2 cup olive oil, vinegar, salt, pepper, celery seed and mustard until salt dissolves.
Cool and pour over cabbage mixture.

i: 8 c. cooked chicken, cut into large chunks
1 c. thinly sliced bell pepper strips
d: Cook rice; refrigerate until cool.
Toss rice, cauliflower and French dressing; cover and refrigerate for 2 hours.
In a large bowl, combine the mayonnaise, curry powder, salt and pepper.
Stir in milk.
Add chicken and toss.
Refrigerate for 2 hours.
Combine gelatin, pecans and coconut.
Stir in milk and vanilla; mix well.
Chill one hour.
Shape into strawberries. Roll in sugar.
Tint almonds as leaves.
Put one almond on top of strawberry.
Dissolve Jell-O in boiling water.
Fold in frozen berries; add pineapple and nuts.
Pour in bowl and refrigerate.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Combine in large bowl Ricotta, Parmesan, Romano and 1/2 Mozzarella cheese.
Add eggs and parsley. Soak noodles in cold water for 10 minutes.
In pan, spread 1/2 cup sauce, then place 3 noodles on top.
Spread 1/3 Ricotta filling over noodles.
Pour 1 cup sauce over cheese filling. Repeat layers, ending with sauce.
Mix all of ingredients together and roll into 80 to 100 balls.

i: 3/4 c. cornmeal or corn tortilla flour
1 1/3 c. shredded Jack cheese with chilies
1/4 c. (1/8 lb.) melted margarine or butter
d: Mix flour, cornmeal, baking powder, salt and 3/4 cup cheese. Make a well in the center.
Mix beaten egg, margarine, honey and milk.
Pour into well in center of mix.
Stir just to moisten. Grease twelve 2 1/2-inch muffin tins.
Place 2 or 3 strips of corn husks in each tin.
Fill with batter and sprinkle with remaining cheese.

Mix and roll out like a jelly roll. Cover with 2 cups apples, cinnamon and nutmeg. Dot with butter. Roll up as a cinnamon roll. Cut and lay in baking pan. Add boiling water and brown sugar. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.
Cut pork into long strips about 2 inches wide and 2 inches thick.
Soak the first six ingredients for half a day or overnight.
Rub the meat (at room temperature) with garlic, sugar and honey.
Heat oven at 400°.
Roast the pork for 35 minutes, turning strips over once during roasting process.
Remove pork from oven and slice.
Combine ingredients; form into balls.
Brown.
Add sauce. Simmer for 45 minutes.

i: 1 (10 oz.) can tomatoes and green chili peppers, cut up
d: In a medium saucepan, bring water to boiling.
Stir in tomatoes and green chilies, corn, rice, chicken bouillon granules and onion.
Turn into a 10 x 6 x 2-inch baking dish.
Cover and bake in a 350° oven for 35 to 40 minutes or until rice is tender. Spoon sour cream down center of rice.
Serves 8.
Layer potatoes and onion in bottom of baking dish.
Dot with butter or oleo.
Add salt and pepper.
Cover with corned beef hash.
Follow with creamed corn.
Top with bread crumbs.

i: 1/2 c. sour milk (add 1 Tbsp. vinegar or lemon juice to sour 1 c. milk)
d: Mix sugar and shortening.
Add flour and salt until it crumbles.
Put aside 1/2 cup of this mixture for topping.
Add egg to remainder.
Add soda and baking powder to sour milk, then mix everything together.
Sprinkle crumbs on top.

i: 3/4 tsp. ginger or apple pie spice
d: Cream Spry; add sugar.
Beat in eggs, then molasses.
Mix well.
Add sifted dry ingredients alternately with buttermilk. Using a spoon, place in strips on greased cookie sheet.
Sprinkle top with granulated sugar.
Bake at 375° for about 12 to 15 minutes.
Mix egg and Parmesan cheese together.
Cook spaghetti and drain.
Mix in 1 tablespoon butter, then mix into spaghetti with the egg and Parmesan cheese.
Grease bottom of 9 x 13-inch pan with a little oil.
Wash tomatoes and pat dry.
Cut tomatoes in 1/4-inch slices. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Dip each slice in corn meal and lay aside on waxed paper.
Heat oil.
Fry tomato slices until golden brown.
Drain on paper towels.
Brown
meat and drain.
Add seasoning.
Mix with cheese until melted.
Spread over rye bread.
Broil slices on cookie sheet until golden brown.
Boil spaghetti according to directions on package.
Drain off hot water and rinse spaghetti in cold water 3 times.
Add Zesty Italian dressing.
Mix well.
Add at least a half bottle of seasoning mix.
Stir well.

i: 2-inch piece fresh ginger, peeled and coarsely chopped
d: In blender, puree onion, garlic, ginger and lemon juice to make a paste.
Add yogurt and all spices; blend to combine.
Add oil and salt and pepper to taste; blend to combine.
Remove skin from chicken.
On breast halves, use a sharp knife to make 2 or 3 (3-inch) slits halfway to the bone.
With the tip of the knife, poke 2 or 3 holes to the bone in the thigh and drumstick of each chicken leg.
In large cake dish, break enough lettuce to cover bottom of dish.
Place green onions and celery over lettuce.
Drain and place water chestnuts over green onions and celery.
Cut broccoli and cauliflower and place over water chestnuts.
Layer frozen peas over broccoli and cauliflower.
Sprinkle lightly with salt.
Rinse wheat in water until water is no longer cloudy.
Soak for 5 minutes.
Squeeze dry by cupping hands and pressing between palms of hands.
Use food processor to pulsate parsley to save time or chop fine on cutting board.
Chop onions, mint leaves and tomatoes very fine.
Place vegetables in a large bowl; add wheat, lemon juice, oil, salt and pepper and mix well.

i: 5 c. tomato sauce (1/2 of No. 10 can)
d: Brown meat and drain.
Saute garlic and onion in olive oil. Add to meat.
Add remaining ingredients and simmer 2 to 3 hours, stirring often.
Add water if necessary to keep right consistency. Add olives, oregano or basil to taste.
Serves 50.
Brown beef in 2 tablespoons butter.
Drain.
Garlic to taste. Add sugar, salt and pepper.
Then add tomato sauce and let simmer on low for 15 minutes.
In a mixing bowl, combine cabbage, celery and peppers.
In a small bowl, combine sugar, salt, water and vinegar.
Taste mixture to be sure you have enough vinegar.
Pour over vegetables and toss.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.
Easy to do; even better the next day.

i: 1/2 tsp. chopped fresh or 1/4 tsp. dried thyme
5 to 6 small potatoes, cut into fourths
d: Heat oven to 450°.
Spray an 8 x 8 x 2-inch pan with nonstick spray.
Mix the oil, lemon and thyme and toss with the potatoes. Place potatoes in pan.
Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Bake 20 minutes; turn and bake 15 minutes longer.
Makes 2 servings, 2 grams fat per serving.
Combine milk, shortening, sugar and spices.
Cool to lukewarm. Add yeast, softened, in warm water.
Add eggs and mix well.
Add flour; beat well.
Add fruits and remaining flour.
Let rise until double in bulk. Punch down and knead lightly.

i: 1 Tbsp. cider vinegar or red wine vinegar
d: In saucepan, heat olive oil.
Add onions and cook until tender.
Add meat and cook until done.
Add chili powder, salt and pepper.
Stir in tomatoes and beef broth.
Bring to a boil, then lower heat.
Bring vinegar, sugar and spices to a boil.
Add cukes and onions. Boil 2 minutes.
Pack in jars and seal.

i: 1 small pkg. Jiffy corn muffin mix
d: Saute the onion and green pepper.
Combine the corns, corn muffin mix and beaten eggs.
Blend all ingredients, except sour cream and cheese, together in ovenproof baking dish.
Top with the sour cream and cheese.
Bake at 350°, uncovered, for about 45 minutes, or until done.
Bring 3 cups water and salt to a boil in a medium saucepan; add rice.
Cover; reduce heat to medium and let simmer for 25 minutes or until all water is absorbed.
Place in separate bowl and set aside.
In same saucepan, combine carrots, peas, corn and 1 cup water.
Steam for 8 to 10 minutes or until vegetables are crisp but tender.
Drain.

i: 1/2 lb. smoked, grilled or broiled mullet
d: Break fish into bite sized portions and set aside.
Mash peas lightly, just until they are broken.
Combine peas, onion, pepper, garlic, pepper sauce, salt and vinegar in a saucepan and cook over medium heat for 10 minutes.
Reduce heat to low, add mullet and cook until heated through.
Serve immediately.
Serves 6.
Cut a few slits
in
fat
side
of roast, then rub roast with salt and
pepper.

i: 1 lb. chicken or pork, cut in small pieces
d: Place cooking oil in skillet and heat.

Add cut up onion and meat. Cook completely. Beat eggs and mix with meat. Add cooked rice, salt and pepper. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring often. Catsup or soy sauce may be added while cooking, if desired. Serves 5.

i: 1 c (2 sticks) butter or margarine softened
d: Combine flour, baking soda, and salt in small bowl.
Chop zucchini, red and green peppers and onions; drain off excess liquid.
Add rest of ingredients and cook until tender, about 45 minutes.
Pack in jars and seal.
Makes about 12 pints.

i: 1/2 pkg. chocolate fudge cake mix without pudding or 1 Jiffy mix
1/2 pkg. white cake mix without pudding or 1 Jiffy mix
d: Mix according to directions and add oil.
Bake in 9 x 13-inch pan.
Blend dry ingredients.
Cut in shortening until coarse like peas.
Make a well in the center.
Pour in 1/2 cup of milk; toss or mix.
Add egg with rest of milk.
Mix lightly with a fork. Roll or pat to 1/2 or 3/4-inch.

i: 1/2 (15 oz.) pkg. folded refrigerated unbaked pie crust (1 crust)
1 c. loosely packed fresh basil leaves
d: Unfold pie crust according to package directions.
Place in a 9-inch quiche dish or glass pie plate.
Flute edge; press with the tines of a fork, if desired.
Prebake according to package directions.
Remove from oven.
Sprinkle with 1/2 cup of the Mozzarella cheese.
Blend all ingredients until smooth.
Serve on crackers.
This is great for children's birthday parties.
Put some on a cracker; add a thin slice of ripe olive.
Mix food coloring in water and add to cornstarch.
Add less water for thicker solution.
Add more water for a thinner one.
Cook potatoes in water until soft; drain. Add cream cheese, sour cream, butter, salt, pepper and onions; beat. Put into a 9 x 12 casserole dish.
Mix chocolate, milk, sugar and syrup.
Cook until a drop in cold water forms a soft ball.
Remove from heat; add vanilla and butter.
Beat mixture until it becomes thick.
Add pecans and pour into greased pans.
Cut into squares when cool.
Mix all ingredients in large bowl.
Set over night or for several hours in the refrigerator.
Eat cold.
Bake uncovered 45 minutes at 350°.

i: 2 lb. chicken, skinned and cut into small pieces
1 tsp. chili powder or to taste
d: Wash the chicken and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a Dutch oven.
Add chicken and brown over medium heat.
Remove and reserve chicken.
Add onions, cinnamon stick, cardamom and cloves to pan.
Saute until onions are lightly browned.
Cook
broccoli 6 to 8 minutes on High
or
until
done. Set aside.
In a large mixing bowl, combine soup, rice, cheese and margarine.	Cook for 5 to 7 minutes
Mix all ingredients, adding lemon juice.
This acts as a thickening agent.
Pour into 2 graham cracker crusts and chill.
Dissolve sugar in water in large container.
Add lemon juice, orange juice and pineapple juice.
Add ginger ale just before serving.
Serve over crushed ice.
Yield:
34 servings.

i: 1 (14 oz.) can sweetened condensed milk (not evaporated milk)
d: In large mixing bowl, combine all ingredients except pastry shell; mix well.
Pour into pastry shell.
Bake at 425° for 15 minutes.
Reduce oven temperature to 350°; bake for an additional 35 to 40 minutes until a knife inserted 1-inch from the edge comes out clean.
Cool.
Combine all ingredients in a jar with a top and shake until blended.
Store in a cool place and shake before pouring over salad.
As served on the ship "The France."
Delicious!

i: 2 (8 oz.) cans frozen orange juice, undiluted
2 No. 6 oz. cans frozen lemonade undiluted
d: Mix well and serve cold or with ice cubes.

i: 1 (10 3/4 oz.) can cream of chicken soup
d: In large saucepan, combine water, broth, soup, chicken, onion, garlic, basil and oregano.
Bring to a boil; simmer uncovered 30 minutes.
Add tortellini and vegetables.
Simmer 10 minutes. Serve with cheese.

i: 4 c. or 1 (28 oz.) can turkey, cut into small chunks (including juice)
d: Mix together and bake for 40 minutes at 350°.

i: 1 jalapeno pepper (more if you like it hotter)
d: Seed peppers and chop fine.
Mix peppers, sugar and vinegar. Boil 1 minute.
Cool 5 minutes.
Add Certo and stir.
Pour into hot jars and seal.

i: 1 small potato, cut in thick slices
1 small onion, cut in thick slices
d: Place chicken, vegetables and rice on a large piece of heavy-duty foil.
Add remaining ingredients and wrap.
Cook over hot coals, turning every 20 to 30 minutes, for 1 hour and 15 minutes.
Serves 1.
Heat together the sugar and vinegar until sugar dissolves. Pour over vegetables.
Make at least 24 hours before serving. Makes 1 1/2 quarts.
Keeps well.
Combine first five ingredients.
Stir in pecans.
Combine remaining ingredients and add to flour mixture, stirring until dry ingredients are moistened.
Spoon batter into 2 greased and floured 8 1/2 x 4 1/2 x 3-inch loaf pans.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour and 10 minutes.
Cool in pan 10 minutes and remove and leave on wire racks.
Cook chicken and cut up.
Cook broccoli and drain.
Mix all ingredients and pour into greased casserole.
Bake at 350° for 30 to 45 minutes.
Freezes well.
Blend cheese and margarine; stir in flour, paprika and salt, mixing well.
Blot olives on paper towels until dry.
Divide dough into 24 pieces.
Mold a piece of dough around each olive.
Arrange on cookie sheet and bake at 425° for 8 to 10 minutes or may be frozen and packed in plastic bags up to 2 months before serving. Bake at 400° for 15 to 20 minutes, if frozen.
Recipe may be doubled or tripled.

i: 1 small sweet red pepper, thinly sliced
d: Combine first six ingredients in a medium saucepan. Bring mixture to a boil, stirring until sugar dissolves. Set aside to cool.
Combine together all ingredients.
Bake in a loaf pan at 350° for about 45 minutes.
Mix both cans of corn, egg, salt and pepper together.
In a casserole dish spread a layer of mix and then cover it with crushed crackers until covered.
Repeat again and top it off with 2 tablespoons of butter and then pour milk in.
It will just cover the bottom.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

i: 1/2 c. regular shortening or unsalted butter, melted
d: In large bowl, mix cereal, flour, sugar, soda and salt.
Beat in the buttermilk, shortening and eggs until just mixed.
Pour in storage container.
Cover and refrigerate several hours or up to 6 weeks.
Combine all ingredients except pie shell in a small bowl.
Mix well.
Pour into pie shell. Bake at 375° for 50 to 55 minutes or until firm.
Heat oven to 450°.
Generously grease 8 medium muffin cups. Beat eggs slightly in medium bowl.
Stir in remaining ingredients with fork, just until smooth (do not overbeat).
Fill muffin cup 3/4 full.
Bake 25 minutes.
Decrease oven temperature to 350°. Bake 10 to 15 minutes longer or until deep golden brown.
Mix all ingredients together for marinade.
Place chicken pieces in single layer in shallow baking pan (12 to 15 pieces). Pour marinade over chicken.
Marinate 4 to 6 hours or overnight in refrigerator.
Bake at 325° for 1 1/2 to 2 hours.
Turn once and baste with sauce.
Serve sauce over rice.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Bake chicken breasts for 20 minutes. Remove from oven and spoon salsa over chicken and then put 1 slice cheese on each piece of chicken.
Return to oven and cook for 20 minutes.
Blend together sugar, salt, flour and cornstarch.
Stir in water.
Cook over low heat, stirring constantly until mixture thickens.
Stir a little of this hot mixture into beaten egg yolks.
Combine mixes and cook a minute longer, stirring constantly.
Add lemon juice, rind and butter.
Mix well and chill.
Serve with raw vegetables.
Mix lemonade with milk.
Fold in other remaining ingredients. Pour into pie shells.
Chill and serve.
Mix and freeze all ingredients.
Take out about 3 hours before serving and pour in ginger ale.

i: 2 (1 lb) pkgs frozen Spinach, thawed and drained well
d: Squeeze remaining liquid from spinach and place in large bowl, tear into small pieces.
Beat eggs, crumble feta cheese and add to spinach.
Saute onion and garlic in olive oil until brown, add to spinach, season with pepper and oregano.
Butter a large oblong baking dish.
Cream butter
and
sugar.
Add eggs, one at a time, and mix well.
Add buttermilk.
Add flour, 1 cup at a time, mixing well. Add vanilla.
Mix zucchini, onion and pure salt together well.
Let stand overnight in cold water.
Drain.
Mix remaining ingredients.
Pour into first three.
Bring to boil.
Mix well.
Let stand in refrigerator 45 minutes.
Dip and fry fish.
Slice onions 1/8 inch thick.
Sprinkle with sugar and add ice water to cover.
Soak for 1 hour.
Make batter of flour, salt, water, vegetable oil and beaten egg whites.
Drain onion rings and dip into batter.
Fry at 375° until brown.
Turn oven to 300°.
Beat butter and
sugar
until it is fluffy.
Add eggs, beating well after
each

t: Chicken Breast And White Bean Salad
i: salt and ground pepper to taste
1 zucchini, scrubbed and cut into 1/4 x 1/2-inch strips
2 c. (16 oz. each) white beans, rinsed and drained
d: Season chicken with thyme, salt and pepper.
In large nonstick skillet, over medium-high heat, add 1 tablespoon oil.
Add chicken and brown for 2 minutes, reduce heat to medium-low and cook for 2 minutes per side.
Remove and reserve.
Add zucchini, onion and dried tomatoes.
Saute briefly; add beans and toss until heated thoroughly.

i: 5 c. pinto or white beans or 5 lb. well scrubbed potatoes, chopped
d: Soak pinto or white beans overnight.
Drain, then cook in 6 quarts water until tender.
Heat olive oil in large soup pot over medium heat.
In the order given, add the following, stirring after each addition:
onions, celery, carrots, garlic and cabbage.
Add the cooked beans or potatoes, 4 gallons hot water, the canned tomatoes, salt and pepper.
Put all in saucepan and cook until thick.
Heat oven to 450°.
Grease pie plate, 10 x 1 1/2-inches. Sprinkle chillies and cheese in plate.
Beat remaining ingredients until smooth.
Pour into plate.
Bake until knife inserted between center and edge comes out clean, 25 to 30 minutes.
Let stand 10 minutes before cutting.

i: 4 slices thick bacon, cut into 1/4-inch pieces
generous dashes of red and black pepper
d: Soak peas. Rinse and drain. Cook bacon until browned. Add onion, green pepper and garlic. Saute onion until tender. Add beans, 2 cups water and seasonings. Cover and simmer 40 to 50 minutes or until peas are tender. Remove bay leaf and stir in rice. Continue simmering 10 minutes or until all liquid is absorbed.
Put all together in skillet with 1 tablespoon Crisco butter or butter and fry until done.
Mix all ingredients together; pour into a 9 x 13-inch baking pan sprayed with nonstick cooking spray.
Freeze for 2 to 3 hours. Cut into squares and store in freezer.
Combine lettuce, onion, and tomatoes. Brown beef in pan, drain, and let cool slightly. Add beef, cheese, and nacho chips to veggies. Pour on dressing. Toss and serve.
Add cabbage and salt and let stand for 2 hours.
Boil sugar, vinegar and water for 5 minutes and let cool.
Squeeze cabbage dry and add remaining ingredients.
Mix and refrigerate.
Will keep for 6 weeks.
Puree water, Ricotta, 1 tablespoon flour, chutney, curry and salt in food processor until smooth.
Dredge chops in remaining flour.
Heat oil in nonstick over medium heat.
Add chops.
Cook 4 to 6 minutes or until no longer pink.
Transfer to serving dish. Pour off fat from skillet.

i: 1/4 c. chicken bouillon or 16 cubes, crushed
d: Combine all of the above ingredients.
Store in covered container.
To prepare, combine 1/4 cup of the above mixture and 1 3/4 cups water.
Add 1/4 cup butter or oleo, melted.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat; cover and simmer 6 minutes.
Add 2 1/2 cups crumbled bread.
Mix
all together and pour over ice ring in punch bowl. Great for weddings.
Beat eggs slightly.
Add mushroom soup; beat and blend with milk and soy sauce.
Mash tofu coarsely, and combine all ingredients; mix well.
Bake in oiled loaf pan, set in a pan of hot water, at 350° about 45 minutes, or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean.
Let stand 10 minutes before unmolding.
Place the lamb in a shallow pan.
Combine the oil, lemon juice, rosemary, pepper and bay leaf.
Blend well and rub the mixture all over the lamb.
Cover and let stand for 2 hours or longer, unrefrigerated but in a cool place.
Turn the meat occasionally.
Separate buns and place in oblong baking dish with brown side up.
Melt butter and pour over buns.
Beat eggs, sugar, milk and vanilla together and pour over buns.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Chop bacon, tomato, onion and grated cheese.
Mix all ingredients except biscuits.
Separate each biscuit into 3 thinner biscuits.
Lay each biscuit over a mini muffin pan cup.
Press biscuit into cup. Fill cups with bacon mixture and bake 10 - 12 minutes until golden brown.
Beat egg; add milk and oil.
Stir in flour and sugar.
Mix just enough to blend.
Fill greased muffin tin 2/3 full.
Bake 20 minutes at 425°.
Mix flour, sugar, salt and pepper.
Add to corn.
Add melted butter. Add eggs.
Add milk.
Pour into greased casserole.
Place in a pan of water. Bake at 325° for 1 hour.

i: 1 lb. fully cooked smoked sausage, sliced
d: Rinse the black-eyed peas and place them in a 4 1/2-quart Dutch oven.
Add 4 cups cold water.
Bring them to a boil; reduce heat and simmer for 2 minutes.
Remove from heat.
Cover and let stand for 1 hour.
Drain and rinse the peas.

t: Chicken With Sun-Dried Tomatoes And Artichokes
i: 1 can chicken broth (reserve 2 Tbsp. broth and mix with cornstarch)
d: Season chicken with salt.
Coat with flour; shake off excess. Heat oil in skillet.
Add chicken; cook per side until no longer pink throughout.
Add wine to skillet; cook 30 seconds.
Remove chicken and cover.
Add broth, artichokes and dried tomatoes. Bring to boil.
Cook beef in 2 tablespoons oil and drain well.
Add onions, spaghetti sauce, tomatoes and undrained mushrooms.
Mix and simmer 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook shells; drain and rinse.

i: 1 large bag spinach, washed and stemmed
5 strips bacon, fried crisp and crumbled
1 can bean sprouts, rinsed in cold water, drained and dried with paper towel
d: Combine and add dressing just before serving.
Peel and core onion.
Stuff center with butter.
Pour brown sugar over the top.
Place in microwave-safe dish sized to hold the onions.
Cover with wax paper.
Microwave on High for 2 minutes per onion.
Grind peppers and onions.
Pour boiling water over them and let stand 2 hours.
Drain and add vinegar, salt, sugar and mustard seed.
Cook until tender.
Jar and seal.

i: 4 c. cooked macaroni, rinsed and cooled
d: Combine all ingredients.
Chill and serve.
Serves 8 to 10.
Slice squash in round portions.
Add onions, green peppers, red peppers and garlic. Sprinkle with salt. Cover with 2 trays of ice and let set 3 hours.
Drain real good.
On stove, heat the vinegar, sugar, celery seed, mustard seed and turmeric.
Add drained squash. Boil about 10 minutes. Seal in hot jars.

t: Lamb Sauce For Lamb On The Rod
d: Put in blender and blend until foamy.
Can add pepper rings.
In Dutch oven or large pot, brown meat and onions; drain.
Add tomatoes and seasonings; bring to a boil.
Add corn and celery. Reduce to simmer, cover, stir occasionally.
Cook at least 1/2 hour but best if simmered longer.
Cut outside skin off jicama.
Slice into 1/4-inch pieces. Slice smaller, if desired.
Peel skin off of cucumbers; slice small in 1/8-inch pieces.
Peel oranges into sections.
Squeeze 1/4 sections of lemon over vegetables and fruit.
Add more, if desired.
Combine 1 package of raspberries with sugar in a 2-cup measure.
Microwave on Medium for 4 minutes.
Crush berries. Combine remaining ingredients in a large bowl.
Add crushed berries.
Add ginger ale (before serving) and 1 package uncrushed, thawed raspberries.
Serves 6.
Cook carrots in boiling water for 15 minutes until tender-crisp.
Drain.
Combine pepper and remaining ingredients in saucepan.
Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring frequently. Pour hot mixture over carrots.
Cover and marinate in refrigerator at least 8 hours.
Yields 12 servings.
Cream together first 4 ingredients.
Mix dry ingredients and add to creamed mixture; add vanilla.
If desired, add 1/2 cup nuts and 1/2 to 3/4 cup chocolate chips.
Bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes.

i: 2 1/2 qt. shredded de-seeded and unpared zucchini
d: In a large bowl, combine zucchini and onions.
Sprinkle with salt; let stand overnight.
The next day, put 6 (1-pint) or 12 (1/2-pint) canning jars with rings and lids into a large pot of water.
Bring to a simmer.
Drain zucchini and onions.
Rinse in cold water; drain.
Mix all ingredients, but ginger ale, the day before.
Fill ice trays with mixture and put 1 cherry per cube (optional).
Freeze until ready to serve.
Mix ginger ale and ice cubes in large punch bowl.

i: 1 lb. stew meat (London broil), cut into 1-inch squares
d: In a large heavy skillet or Dutch oven, brown stew meat in olive oil over medium heat. Reduce heat to low and add garlic and onion; cook until onion is clear.
Add rest of ingredients.
Cover and simmer 1 hour.
Serve with boiled potatoes and heavy bread and sliced Edam cheese.
Makes 6 servings.
Sift together flour, soda, salt, ginger and cinnamon.
Cream together sugar and shortening.
Stir in molasses, water and coconut.
Blend in the dry ingredients gradually.
Chill dough for at least 2 hours.
Roll out 1/3 at a time on a floured surface to 1/8-inch thickness.
Put dry yeast in water and add sugar.
Stir until yeast is dissolved.
Add the flour and salt; mix well.
Divide dough into 2 parts.
Make into 18 or 20 equal sized balls and roll to 5-inches in diameter.
Place on cookie sheet and let rise until puffy. Bake at 500° for about 5 minutes, until puffed, but not too brown. Good with dips or stuffing for sandwiches.

i: 1/2 c. low cholesterol egg substitute or 1 egg and 2 whites
d: Mix flour, sweetener, baking powder and cornmeal together. Combine eggs, milk and oil; add to flour mixture and stir until just moistened.
Pour into a 9 x 9 x 2-inch pan sprayed with nonstick cooking spray.
Bake at 425° for 20 to 25 minutes.

i: 1/2 c. onion, broccoli, cauliflower, tomatoes and mushrooms
d: Mix together cottage cheese, cream cheese, Ranch dressing mix and mayo.
Spread crescent rolls on 8 x 10-inch sheet.
Put in oven as directed for 10 minutes.
Let cool.
Spread on first mixture.
Spread mixture of veggies over cold dough.
Preheat oven to 400°.
Grease muffin cups.
In large bowl, mix the first 7 ingredients.
Beat eggs; stir in oil and zucchini. Stir all together until moistened.
Bake for 25 minutes.
Drain syrup from fruit into saucepan.
Bring to a boil; boil rapidly until reduced to 3/4 cup.
Add molasses, vinegar, stick of cinnamon and curry powder.
Add drained fruit; simmer 5 minutes.
Garnish with cherries.
Serve hot or cold.

i: 12 c. fresh spinach, washed and torn into bite-size pieces about 2 bunches
d: Heat chicken broth to boiling.
Add mushrooms, then spinach; cook 2 minutes.
Add bean sprouts and hot sauce.
Serve at once Makes 4 qt.
One c. serving:
33 calories, 1 g. fat, 2 mg. cholesterol.
In mixing bowl, whisk together minced shallot, apple cider vinegar, Dijon mustard and chopped tarragon.
Gradually whisk in extra virgin olive oil until thickened.
In large bowl, toss apple cider vinegar-tarragon mixture with mixed salad greens.
Transfer salad to serving bowl and sprinkle cranberries and walnuts on top. Makes 2 servings.
Simmer beef and sausage until brown.
Drain all fat.
Simmer onion, pepper and celery until tender.
Add to beef mixture.
Add oregano, salt, pepper and garlic, tomatoes, tomato paste and Ragu sauce.
Simmer while cooking noodles.

t: Butter Herb Baked Fish(Shared By Larry Sherman)
d: Combine crackers, cheese and spices.
Dip fish in butter, then cracker mixture.
Bake in Pyrex dish for 30 minutes at 350°.
In medium saucepan, combine sugar, milk, egg yolks and margarine.
Cook and stir over medium heat until mixture comes to a boil.
Remove from heat.
Stir in coconut, pecans and vanilla. Cool 15 minutes and spread on top layers of German chocolate cake.
Put into large mixing bowl and mix well.
Keep in refrigerator.

i: butter, salt, pepper and diced onion to suit taste
d: Mash 4 to 5 cooked potatoes with liquid from cooking.
Add butter or margarine, salt, pepper and diced onion to suit taste. Slit 10 hot dogs from end to end leaving each end uncut.
Stuff dogs with mashed potatoes and sprinkle grated cheese on top.
Put under broiler for about 5 minutes.
Watch closely.
Combine cornstarch and sugar.
Stir in crushed pineapple and water.
Bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
Boil until clear; chill, then add lemon juice.
Serve on ice cream or pudding.
Debone and remove skin from chicken.
Dredge the chicken pieces in flour seasoned with salt and pepper.
Set aside.
Saute onion and garlic in some olive oil, just until onions are clear (not browned).
Remove from pan and deglaze the pan with white wine. Pour the wine over the onion and garlic.

i: 4 chicken breasts, cooked, deboned and chopped coarsely
1 c. seedless green grapes, cut in half
d: Toss chicken pieces, crabmeat, grapes, walnuts and celery together.
Combine mayonnaise, lemon juice and curry powder and pour over chicken mixture.
Toss again.
Chill.
Serves 6 to 8.
Finely chop the onion and peppers and mince the garlic.
Saute in 1 tablespoon oil in a Dutch oven.
Drain the pinto beans, reserving the fluid.
Add beans to onion, pepper and garlic and simmer for about 1 hour.
Mash beans.
If beans are dry, add reserved fluid.

Place squash and onion in a colander and sprinkle with salt. Let set over sink until all moisture is gone. Add flour, corn meal, pepper, cheese and egg and mix together. Form into patties and fry in butter.

i: 1 lb. Kielbasa sausage, cut in small chunks
d: Combine in large pot 5 cups water, salt, yellow coloring, garlic, pimentos, red pepper, ground pepper, Kielbasa, onions, butter and oil.
Bring to boil, add rice.
Cover, turn on low and simmer 20 to 25 minutes until almost all water is gone.
Remove from heat, uncover and let sit 5 minutes before serving.
Mix honey, mustard and vinegar.
Add pepper, cayenne pepper and chili pepper.
Stir in ketchup and sugar.
Bring to a boil in a medium sauce pan.
Cook ground or chopped peppers and onions in oil for 1/2 hour. Cook tomatoes and garlic as for juice.
Put through the mill.
(I use a food processor and do my tomatoes uncooked.
I then add the garlic right to the juice.)
Add peppers and onions to juice and remainder of ingredients.
Cook approximately 1 hour.
Peel, core and fine dice the apples or "coarse grate" in processor.
Place sugar and water in deep pan over low fire. While syrup is cooking, make up one batch of Best Biscuit mix. Roll dough 1/4-inch thick in a rectangle.
Spread apples evenly over dough.
Then roll up and cut as for cinnamon rolls.
Place in a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Dot with oleo and sprinkle cinnamon on top.
Thaw sausage meat the night before.
Brown in frying pan and drain any excess fat.
Set meat aside.
Peel zucchini and slice into 1/4-inch thick rounds.
Peel and slice onions and garlic.

i: 2 (8 oz.) cans tomato sauce or 1/2 (32 oz.) Aunt Millie's sauce (if you prefer) *
d: Set meat aside while you cook on top of the stove (for a very short time) the soup mix and water.

i: 1/2 c. plus 1 Tbsp. all-purpose flour, unsifted
d: Combine first 5 ingredients; mix well.
Stir in flour; drop by tablespoons into 1/2-inch hot oil.
Cook until browned, turning once.
Drain on paper towel.
Yields 6 servings.
Delicious!!!

i: 1 (28 oz.) can whole tomatoes (undrained), cut up
d: In medium skillet, brown meat with onion and drain.
Add all remaining ingredients, except kidney beans.
Bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover and simmer for 1 hour.
Add kidney beans; simmer until thoroughly heated.
Makes 7 (1 1/2-cup) servings.
To cook in a slow cooker, cook on high setting for 3 to 4 hours.

i: 2 large apples, pared, cored and diced
d: Preheat oven
to
Combine
lemon
juice,
Boil noodles in salt water until tender.
Drain very dry.
Put in large bowl.
Melt butter in hot noodles.
Add 9 beaten eggs. Add both cans of fruit with juice.
Add vanilla, sugar, raisins and orange juice.
Mash and bake 15 to 20 minutes in a 400° oven in a greased baking dish.
Combine all ingredients.
Chill for several hours.
Serve with pita, as a salad, on lettuce or as a dip with crackers.
Cut fish in length 4 x 6 x 1-inch.
Soak in brine 5 hours. Lay on paper towel to dry.
Put honey on before smoking.
Heat tamales in microwave 2 minutes. Stir. Add beans.
Heat 2 more minutes. Stir.
Add cheese and heat 2 more minutes. Serve warm with tortilla chips.

i: 1 whole breast, cut into 4 pieces and skin removed
d: In a large bowl, cover chicken with buttermilk; marinate for about an hour.

i: 2 lb. beef (round or sirloin), cut in 1/8 x 2-inch strips
as many onions and peppers as you like
d: Melt butter and brown steak.
Add onions and peppers to meat and add all other ingredients except cornstarch.
Cook on low heat 10 to 15 minutes and then thicken with cornstarch.
Serve over rice.
Heat vinegar mixture to boiling point.
Remove from heat and let cool.
Prepare any combination of vegetables suggested above. Slice thin, peppers, carrots, garlic, half onions and cut in 1/4-inch strips.
Separate cauliflower into small flowerets. Rinse beets.
Drain mushrooms (4 quarts in a gallon jar).
Pour vinegar mixture over vegetables and keep refrigerated.
In a large kettle, fry meat in oil until dark brown.
Then add 1-quart of water. Cover and simmer two hours.
Add remaining ingredients, except flour and cornmeal.
Cook at gentle simmer for 30 minutes, removing bay leaves after 15 minutes.
Make a paste of flour, cornmeal and water.
Stir into mixture, cooking another five minutes at simmer while stirring often.

i: 1 pkg. Betty Crocker butter pecan cake mix
d: Mix boiling
water and poppy seed together.
Set aside. Mix remaining
ingredients.
Add seed and water mixture.
Pour into 2 greased
Brown beef and chili powder in skillet.
Drain off grease. Add soup mix and water.
Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and cook 10 minutes, stirring often.
On top of chips, add other ingredients. Top with cheese and tomatoes.

i: 1/3 c. plain nonfat yogurt for garnish (optional)
d: These are wonderful.
If possible, grill over hot coals, but are delicious pan-fried also.
Cut bacon into pieces and fry with onion.
Drain off fat.
Add vinegar, sugar, beans and salt and pepper to taste.
Simmer 10 minutes.
Serve hot.
Beat together margarine, sugars and eggs until creamy.
Then add salt and flour, coconut, chocolate chips and pecans.
Pour in two 9-inch cake pans, greased and floured.
Bake at 350° for 20 to 30 minutes.
Let cool and sprinkle with powdered sugar or leave plain.
Mix the flour, baking powder, water, butter and salt to make the dough.
Knead the dough into a soft mass.
Form the mass like a Mexican tortilla.
For each made-up tortilla, add some tuna; fold them in half and, with a fork, pinch the edges to tighten.
Deep fry until golden brown.

i: 1 1/2 sticks margarine or 1 stick margarine and 1/2 c. Crisco
d: Cream sugar and butter. Add eggs, bananas and nuts. Add flour. Beat well. Pour into 9 x 5 pan. Bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes.

i: 1/2 c. soy sauce (low sodium is fine)
d: Marinate meat all day or overnight, especially with a thicker cut of meat.
Cut pork in 1-inch cubes, discarding bone.
Brown pork in oil in large skillet, stirring frequently, for 15 minutes.
Combine gravy mix, water, honey, soy sauce, vinegar, ginger and garlic salt.
Add to skillet.
Cover; cook for 20 minutes.
Add carrots, onion and green pepper; cook at least 10 minutes.
Beat eggs and sugar in a thick boiler.
When mixture is hot, add the flour, nuts and vanilla and mix thoroughly.
Bake in flat pan at 300 degrees for 20 minutes.
This cake will be chewy and will stay moist and fresh if wrapped tightly.

i: 1/2 bottle or more your favorite barbecue sauce
d: Prepare ribs.
Remove excess fat, wash and pat dry with paper towel.
Place ribs in baking pan; sprinkle with salt and pepper. Cover with chopped onions and chopped celery.
Sprinkle with Worcestershire sauce.
Bake slowly in 325° oven until tender (approximately 1 1/2 hours).
Drain fat and pour 1/2 bottle or more barbecue sauce over ribs and return to oven for 1/2 hour, baste occasionally and remove from oven.

i: 1/2 c. long grain rice, soaked 1/2 hour in water
d: Broil onions 20 minutes.
Cut off tops.
Slice down center side; cut off bottom.
Separate the onion circles (leaves).
Mix together beef and next 5 ingredients.
Add tomatoes (helps cook the rice).

i: 1 (8 oz.) can stewed tomatoes, chopped (reserving liquid)
d: Heat oil in Dutch oven over medium-high heat until hot.
Add chicken and stir until browned.
Add rice, onion and garlic. Cook, stirring, until onion is tender and rice is lightly browned. Add broth, tomatoes, tomato liquid, paprika, ground red pepper and saffron.
Bring to a boil over high heat; stir.
Reduce heat to low.
Cover and simmer 10 minutes.

i: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen whole kernel corn, thawed
d: Soak beans in water to cover by 2-inches in large saucepan overnight. Drain; cover with 6 cups fresh water. Bring to boil; reduce heat and simmer, covered, 40 to 60 minutes, until tender. Drain. (Can be made ahead. Cover and refrigerate up to 2 days.)
Dredge roast in flour.
Brown slowly on all sides in hot fat. Season generously with salt and pepper.
Add onions.
Mix remaining ingredients except mushrooms and add to roast.
Cover and cook slowly at 300° for 2 1/2 hours.
Then add mushrooms. Blend 2 tablespoons flour to thicken gravy.

i: heavy plastic spoons, color of your choice
chocolate (or colored) candy melts or chocolate chips
d: In a small dish, melt chocolate in the microwave.
Dip spoon into melted chocolate, filling bowl of spoon.
Place spoon on a cookie sheet lined with plastic wrap.
Repeat with remaining spoons.
Chill until set.
Re-dip spoons to coat underside.
In a large saucepan, heat oil and brown the beef.
Add onion soup mix and paprika, blended with tomatoes.
Simmer, covered, stirring occasionally, for 1 1/2 hours or until beef is tender. Stir in flour and salt, blended with water.
Bring to a boil, then simmer, stirring constantly, until sauce is thickened, about 5 minutes.
Cook noodles according to package directions.
Garnish with parsley.
In small bowl, sift flour and mix remaining dry ingredients (first 5 items).
In medium bowl, cream butter until soft. Gradually add sugar, creaming until fluffy.
Add well beaten egg and dark corn syrup.
Blend in dry ingredients in fourths, mixing well after each addition.
Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for several hours or overnight.
Prepare a surface for rolling dough.

i: 4 large red new potatoes (about 2 1/2 lb. total)
1 or 2 cloves garlic, minced or pressed
d: Place potatoes in 1-inch of boiling water. Cover and cook until tender when pierced (about 30 minutes).
Drain.
Let stand for about 10 minutes and cut into 3/8-inch slices.
Place potatoes in a large salad bowl. Add parsley and onion. Blend together mustard, salt, pepper, tarragon, garlic, vinegar and oil.
Pour over potato mixture and toss gently to coat. Cover and let stand at room temperature for at least 3 hours. Chill, if desired. Makes 8 servings.
One day ahead:
Put cranberries and both oranges (use peeling from one) through meat grinder or blender.
Add sugar and let stand overnight.

Preheat the oven to 375°. Slice about 1/2-inch off the top of the garlic. Wrap it in aluminum foil and bake for 35 minutes. Remove foil and press until all flesh squeezes out. You should have 1 heaping tablespoonful of puree. Mix it with the yogurt, parsley, black pepper and salt.
Mix ground beef, ham, eggs, milk, mustard and corn flakes. Grease muffin tins and sprinkle brown sugar over bottom of muffin cups.
Spread pineapple over brown sugar.
Shape meat mixture into balls and place in muffin cups.
This is great for entertaining. (This can be made in 10 x 15-inch pan.) Bake at 350° for 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
Makes 15 individual loaves.
Chunk squash.
Par-boil, drain and mash.
Leave skin on.
Put in baking dish.
Beat egg yolks lightly.
Add all ingredients except egg whites.
Combine egg, onion, cracker crumbs, ground beef, salt and pepper in a mixing bowl.
Combine tomato sauce, brown sugar, vinegar and mustard in small bowl.
Add half of tomato mixture to ground beef mixture and blend thoroughly.
Shape in loaf and make depression in top of meat loaf and pour sauce over top of meat loaf with ketchup.
Marinate chicken at least 2 hours or overnight.
Bake at 350° or on a grill.

t: Wild Rice Rose(Serve With Lamb Or Chicken)
d: Soak rice overnight; drain.
Wash and drain.
Mix with remaining ingredients.
Place in buttered 2-quart casserole dish. Cover and bake for 1 hour at 350°.
Serves 4 to 6.

t: Swiss Cheese And Veggies In Pitas
i: 1 tsp. snipped fresh dill weed or 1/2 tsp. dried dill weed
d: Mix all ingredients.
Separate 3 pita breads along cut sides to form pockets.
Arrange some salad greens and veggie mixture in pockets.

i: 1 small head or 1/2 large head lettuce
d: Make 24 hours before serving.
Use large casserole or large glass pan.
Shred or tear lettuce into dish, approximately 1/3 full.
Layer on top of the following (do not stir): green peas, green pepper, celery, red onion.
Frost entire top with the following mixture:
mayonnaise, sour cream and sugar.
Cook corn in large amount of boiling water for 5 minutes. Plunge in cold water.
Drain.
Cut corn from cobs.
Do not scrape cobs.
Measure 8 cups corn.

i: 2 large zucchini, cut in large cubes
2 green or red peppers, cut up
3 medium size tomatoes, peeled and sliced
d: Heat oil in large saucepot; add zucchini, peppers, onions and tomatoes.
Cover with lid.
Do not add water as water cooks out of zucchini.
Cook until zucchini is soft, about 1 hour.
Add green beans last.
Especially good served with cooked smoked sausage or ham.
Dissolve brown sugar in 1/4 cup water and cook over moderate heat until the sugar browns.
Line a suitable mold with the mixture evenly and set aside.
Scald milk in double boiler for 15 minutes.
Blend the egg yolks with the sugar, milk and flavoring. Pour into the mold lined with caramelized sugar.
Place this in a bigger pan half filled with water and bake until mixture becomes firm.
Cool before removing from mold.

t: Three-Grain Pilaf With Dried Apricots And Cherries
d: Combine first 2 ingredients and soak in apple juice.
Saute onion in butter over medium-high heat for about 5 minutes or until lightly browned.
Add millet and cook 3 to 4 minutes longer.
Stir in quinoa, bulgur and broth; bring to a boil.
Drain fruit and add to grains.
Shake in soy sauce.
Crush vanilla wafers in food processor.
Beat eggs and sugar. Add all ingredients.
Spoon into greased tube pan.
Bake at 325° for 1 1/2 hours.
Cake tastes better after 2 or 3 days.

i: 1 large tomato, chopped in small pieces
d: Mix and add Italian dressing to taste.
Dissolve the gelatin in hot water; add all other ingredients. Pour in mold; chill until firm.
Toast pine nuts in 350 F oven for 5-10 minutes.
In a large glass or stainless steel bowl, whisk together the vinegar, mustard, salt and pepper.
Add the oil slowly while whisking.
Add the parsley, scallions, tomatoes and mushrooms and toss.
Top with roasted pine nuts.

i: 3 to 4 lb. boneless short ribs (fat trimmed)
d: Sprinkle salt and pepper on all sides of short ribs and put into Dutch oven or a 3-quart covered casserole dish.
In a small saucepan, combine the tomato soup with the molasses, vinegar, liquid smoke and minced onion; bring to a boil and simmer for 5 minutes.
Pour over short ribs; cover and bake in a 275° oven for 3 to 4 hours, or until very tender.
Just before serving, spoon off any excess fat.
(If you prepare this dish ahead, refrigerate it and then lift off the solidified fat before you reheat it.)
Makes 8 to 10 servings.

i: 6 chocolate covered round mint cookies or patties
d: *Tip:
To tint coconut, place coconut and 1 to 2 drops of green food coloring in a jar or resealable plastic container. Seal container and shake until all colored.

i: 1 head lettuce, broken into bite size pieces
d: Using large glass or plastic bowl that has flared top, layer vegetables in order named; do not mix.
Spread mayonnaise over top to seal.
Sprinkle sugar, then cheese over this.
Seal with covering of plastic wrap or tight fitting lid.
Refrigerate 8 to 12 hours.
When ready to serve, sprinkle top with crumbled fresh fried bacon or artificial bacon bits.
Mix hot milk with sugar, salt and shortening and cool to lukewarm.
Soften yeast in lukewarm water and stir into first mixture.
Add beaten egg and stir in flour gradually.
Mix thoroughly, but do not knead.
Place in refrigerator and keep dough in tightly covered container.
When ready to use, let the dough come to room temperature before shaping into rolls.

i: 1 (13 1/2 oz.) can pineapple tidbits, drained (reserve syrup)
d: Mix thoroughly beef, crumbs, onion, egg, salt, ginger and milk. Shape mixture by tablespoon into balls.
Melt shortening in large skillet; brown and cook meatballs.
Remove meatballs.
Pour fat from skillet.
Mix cornstarch and sugar.
Stir in reserved pineapple syrup, vinegar and soy sauce until smooth.
Clean steaks and place in shallow pan.
Mix all other ingredients.
Pour over steaks and refrigerate for 2 hours.
Place steaks on greased (Pam) broiler pan.
Cook 4 to 5 inches from heat for 8 minutes.
Turn, baste and cook 5 minutes longer.

i: 10 oz. pkg. okra, frozen, cut up
d: Add tomatoes, celery, green pepper, onion, parsley and bouillon cubes to chicken broth.
Simmer 24 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
Add chicken and okra; cook for 6 minutes longer.
Add rice and corn.
Heat and serve.
Makes 6 servings, 1 cup each; 155 calories per serving.
Heat oven to 400°.
Grease pie plate.
Cook and stir beef and onion; drain.
Stir in seasoning.
Spread in plate; top with chillies.
Beat milk, Bisquick and eggs until smooth.

i: 1/2 c. spaghetti sauce or pizza sauce
d: Preheat oven to 400°.
Put spaghetti sauce, then other toppings on muffin halves.
Bake 8 to 10 minutes.
Serves 2.
Easy for kids!

i: 1/4 tsp. crushed hot red pepper flakes
d: Heat oil in large nonstick skillet.
Add zucchini and half of garlic.
Saute until zucchini is softened, about 10 minutes.
Add remaining garlic, salt and pepper flakes to zucchini mixture.
Let cool to room temperature.
To serve, sprinkle the zucchini with vinegar.
Heat sugar, salt, celery seed, mustard seed, vinegar and red pepper sauce to boiling; boil 2 minutes.
Remove from heat.
Stir in corn (with liquid) and remaining ingredients.
Cool.
Cover. Refrigerate several days to blend flavors.
Melt butter and olive oil in pan.
Saute garlic until soft and golden with 2 teaspoons of salt.
Add cloves and simmer 2 minutes. Toss with 1 pound of linguine and serve with Parmesan cheese.
Brown meat, drain well.
Add onions, taco seasoning, add water. Simmer 20 minutes.
Add refried beans and heat through.
Mix together in small bowl, sour cream and 1 bottle taco sauce; set aside.
In 2-quart casserole, layer 1/3 meat mixture, 1/2 sour cream mixture, 1/2 cup grated cheese.
Repeat 1 more time, ending with meat mixture.
Cook pasta and drain.
In a large bowl toss pasta, cheese, onions, radishes, olives and cucumbers.
In a small bowl, combine remaining ingredients.
Toss with the pasta and spread evenly. Chill 1 to 2 hours.
Serve.
With your hands, mix turkey in a bowl with chopped onion, chopped green pepper, tomato sauce, Worcestershire sauce, soy sauce, allspice, salt and pepper.
Form mixture into 8 or 9 oval balls.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Grease and flour 4 1-pound coffee cans. Roll dates and nuts in flour. Combine all ingredients and mix well.
Fill can 1/2 full. Bake 1 hour. Cool in can 10 to 15 minutes. Turn out on rack.
Excellent on all meats.
Put cream cheese out to soften.
Scrub potatoes and dry. Grease them with butter and salt the skins.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Remove from oven and cool to handle.
Cut them in half lengthwise.
Remove cooked potato to bowl, being careful to reserve the skins whole.
Combine sugar and cream of tartar.
Stir until sugar is dissolved.
Cover pan and boil 5 minutes, allowing steam to dissolve any grains of sugar on sides of pan.
Uncover and boil without stirring to soft ball stage.
Wipe all crystals from the sides of the pan with a damp cloth.
Pour into shallow pan, plate or onto a marble slab which has been rinsed with cold water.

i: 1 pkg. almonds (you can use pecans or peanuts)
d: Put almonds on cookie sheet and roast in oven.
Melt chocolate bark in double boiler.
Mix nuts in chocolate.
Beat until cool enough to form mounds when dropped by spoon.
You can make with pecans and peanuts the same way.
Slice tomatoes thinly and place in bowl with salt between layers.
Leave overnight.
Next day, rinse, drain and put in large pot with all remaining ingredients.
Cook slowly, uncovered, until tomatoes are tender and vinegar is nearly absorbed.
This will take close to 5 hours.
Makes anywhere from 2 1/2 to 4 pints.
Crumble the cornbread in a large dish. Add in layers, beans, peppers, onion, tomatoes and bacon.
Add a few sliced dill pickles on top of bacon.
Drain artichokes and chop.
Mix with mayonnaise and Parmesan cheese.
After well mixed, bake, uncovered, for 20 to 30 minutes at 350°.
Serve with crackers.

i: 1 box Duncan Hines butter cake mix
1 (11 oz.) can mandarin oranges, cut up (use juice, too)
d: Mix together.
Bake in 3 layers or an oblong pan for about 20 minutes at 350°.

i: 1/2 cake yeast or 1 pkg. dry yeast
d: Combine flour, salt, soda and sugar.
Cut in Crisco.
Dissolve yeast in buttermilk.
Combine dry ingredients in liquid and roll out on floured board.
Cut with floured cutter.
Let rise 1 1/2 hours.
Mix well the first 6 ingredients; shape into balls.
Brown in the shortening.
Mix the soup with water; pour over meatballs. Simmer 20 to 30 minutes.

t: Beef Tenderloin With Marsala Wine Sauce
d: Crush fennel seed (I use wax paper and roll with rolling pin).
Bake or grill tenderloin until medium rare.
Meanwhile, prepare sauce.
Melt 1 tablespoon butter and olive oil in skillet; stir in garlic, red pepper flakes and crushed fennel.
Cook for 1 minute.
Add tomato paste; stir and cook for 2 minutes.

i: 1 (16 oz.) pkg. frozen collard greens, chopped
1 (16 oz.) pkg. frozen turnip greens, chopped
1 (16 oz.) pkg. frozen mustard greens, chopped
2 slices bacon, cut in small pieces
2 chicken bouillon cubes, dissolved in 1 1/2 c. hot water
d: Saute bacon and onion in pot.
Add dissolved bouillon cube, liquid, pepper flakes, salt and pepper.
Let simmer for 10 minutes.
Add greens.
Cook until greens are tender on low heat, from 1/2 hour to 3/4 hour (30 minutes to 45 minutes).
Brown hamburger and onions.
Drain off grease.
Add rest of ingredients and simmer.
Serve on hamburger buns.
Coat chicken and brown in oil; remove.
In drippings over medium heat, cook garlic, onions, green pepper, celery, curry and salt for 5 minutes.
Stir in tomatoes and raisins.
Add chicken. Cover and simmer until chicken is done, about 45 minutes.
Brown almonds in butter, shaking skillet occasionally.
Sprinkle almonds and parsley over chicken.
Dissolve yeast in water with 1 teaspoon sugar in cup.
Combine flour, 4 tablespoons sugar, baking powder, baking soda and salt. Cut in butter; add buttermilk, then yeast mixture.
Mix well. Knead a few minutes on floured board.
Put in covered buttered bowl.
Let stand 1 hour or doubled in bulk.
Roll out; cut into biscuits.
Saute butter and onion.
Pour in ketchup, liquid smoke and the rest of the ingredients.
Cook on low for at least 30 minutes.
To less thicken, add a little water.
Use just a "dab" of liquid smoke, or you will ruin it.
