You'll never buy bottled again!

i: enough milk to wet the mixture
d: Mix all dry ingredients together with the mayonnaise, then add enough milk to wet the mixture like muffin dough.
Pour into buttered muffin tins, approximately half full.
Bake at 350° for 25 minutes.
A wonderful dinner roll when you're in a hurry. Makes 6 rolls.
Pour dressing over broccoli slaw.
Add sunflower seeds and raisins.
Stir before serving.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.

i: 2 (10 1/2) cans condensed cream of celery soup
d: Cook broccoli with bouillon cubes and water until just tender. Heat celery soup.
Pour hot soup over broccoli.
Garnish with cheese and pimento.
Serve at once.
Makes 6 servings.
Stir or whisk liquids; add dry ingredients.
Pour into 2 unbaked pie shells.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.
(Middle of pie should shake and not be too firm).
Do not overbake!
Wash pork chops.
Salt and pepper each side. Smear each side of chop with prepared mustard (about 1 teaspoon).
Dredge in flour.
Brown in oil on each side in skillet.
Empty contents of one can of soup over chops.
Cook for 1 hour at 350°.
Prepare and bake pastry shell.
Cool.
Wash berries.
Remove caps and damaged spots (leave berries whole).

i: 1 1/2 to 2 lb. pork tenderloin, cut into 1 1/2 to 2-inch slices
d: Flatten slices with the heel of your hand.
Spread medallions on one side with mustard.
Saute in butter (mustard side first) for 5 minutes on each side.
Remove to warm platter.
Add beef broth and tarragon to pan and scrape up the drippings.
Simmer until half the liquid is gone.
Mix all ingredients together.
Line a 9 x 13-inch pan with your favorite pie crust (Pillsbury All-Ready crust works well). Pour in filling. Top with strips of pie crust.
Sprinkle top with sugar and dot with margarine or butter (about 1/2 cup).
Bake at 375° for 1 hour.

t: Jagerschnitzel(Veal In Brown Gravy And Mushrooms)
i: 1 lb. boneless pork or veal cutlets
d: Heat oil in large skillet over medium heat.
Pound cutlets with a meat tenderizer to flatten them.
Season cutlets with salt and pepper, dredge them in beaten eggs and then coat evenly with bread crumbs.
Place cutlets into skillet and fry until golden brown (1-2 minutes in each side).
Remove the meat from the skillet and drain on paper towels.
Keep the meat warm in the oven while you make the gravy.
Cream butter, shortening and sugar until very light and fluffy.
Add flavorings.
Add eggs one at a time and beat well. Add alternately the measured flour and baking powder with milk, beginning with flour mixture and ending with flour mixture.
Bake in a greased tube pan in a preheated 325° oven for 1 1/2 hours or until cake tests done.
For a sheet cake, bake in a greased 11 x 15-inch pan at 350° for 25 minutes or until cake tests done.
Mix and freeze to a slush.

i: 1 small pkg. frozen strawberries or 1 pt. fresh strawberries
d: Add water to Jell-O.
Stir to dissolve.
Add strawberries, bananas and pineapple.
Pour half into pan.
Let harden.

i: 2 qt. sliced, peeled, hard, ripe peaches (about 10 large)
d: Combine fruit and sugar; let stand 12 to 18 hours in cool place.
Sterilize canning jars.
Bring slowly to boiling, stirring frequently.
Boil gently until fruit becomes clear and syrup thick, about 40 minutes.
As mixture thickens, stir frequently to prevent sticking.
Skim, if necessary.
Melt butter.
Mix all ingredients and roll into balls.
Roll in chopped pecans or coconut, if desired.
Arrange romaine, Feta cheese, onions and olives on 4 salad plates. Combine remaining ingredients in a jar with tight-fitting lid and shake well. Just before serving, pour vinaigrette over salad.
Dip marshmallows in melted margarine; roll in candy. Wrap a crescent triangle around each marshmallow, completely covering the marshmallow and square edges of dough tightly to seal.
Dip in margarine and place in a greased muffin tin.
Bake at 375° for 10 to 15 minutes; remove from pan.
Put cucumbers in a gallon glass
jar.
Mix the vinegar and other ingredients.
Pour the vinegar mixture over cucumbers. Refrigerate.
Ready to eat after 1 week.
Mix all ingredients together.
Drop out on cookie sheet by tablespoon or pour into muffin cups.
Makes 12 muffins.
Bake at 400° for 15 minutes.

t: Spiced Bran Muffins(From "In The Kitchen With Rosie")
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
Coat a 12-well muffin tin with cooking spray.
Warm molasses and honey in a small saucepan over low heat just until it begins to steam.
Remove pan from heat and set aside to cool.
Whisk egg whites, yogurt and milk together in a large mixing bowl until blended.
Whisk in molasses-honey mixture. Using wooden spoon, stir in bran, flour, baking powder and spices. Fold in walnuts and raisins.
Mix the beef marinade with about 1/2 quart of water (you may want more) and pour over brisket.
Cook for 3 to 4 hours, or until brisket is tender.
When done, drain juice and let cool.
Slice meat and layer in electric skillet (or something you can put back in oven, if you prefer).
Pour barbecue sauce over; let simmer about 30 minutes.
Makes delicious sandwiches.
Combine peppers, onions, tomatoes and cilantro.
Squeeze the juice of all 4 lemons on mixture.
Add water and mix well.
If you want it hotter, add 5 to 6 Serrano peppers, chopped real fine. Then drink plenty of water or eat ice cream to cool off.
Enjoy!

i: 1 1/2 c. fresh corn, cut from cob
d: Cook corn and lima beans in a small amount of boiling water for 12 minutes or until tender; drain and set aside.
Cook bacon in a large skillet until crisp; remove bacon, reserving drippings in skillet.
Crumble bacon and set aside.
Saute chopped onion in bacon drippings until onion is tender; remove onion and set aside. Add flour to bacon drippings, stirring until smooth.
Cook 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add tomatoes and rest of ingredients.

i: 1 (12 oz.) pkg. Mueller's wide yolk-free noodle style pasta, cooked according to pkg. directions
d: Melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Saut meat and broccoli until tender. Toss in prepared pasta, then add tomatoes. Stir until hot. Season to taste and add cheese. Serve immediately.
Slice a layer of raw potatoes in bottom of greased casserole dish, then put a layer of raw onion with layer of hamburger.
Pour in cooked tomatoes, then season each layer to your liking with your favorite seasonings; season each layer.
Bake 1 hour in oven at 350° or until tender.
Dissolve gelatin in boiling water.
Add sugar and then cold water and all juices.
Yields 60 (4-ounce) servings.
Saute onions and mushrooms in olive oil.
When softened, mix in kasha moistened with egg.
Stir over medium-high heat about 2 to 3 minutes or until kernels are separate.
Reduce heat and add boiling water.
Cover pan tightly.
Simmer 8 to 11 minutes or until kasha kernels are tender and liquid absorbed.
Boil and stir margarine, sugar and dates for 3 minutes.
Add Rice Krispies and pecans.
Form into small balls and roll in powdered sugar.

i: 1 can Ro-Tel tomatoes with green chilies
1 pkg. Old El Paso taco seasoning mix
d: Heat in crock-pot until cheese is melted.
Serve warm with tortilla chips.
Add 2 Tbsp. sugar to 1/2 cup warm water.
Let stand 10 minutes. Mix in 1 1/2 cups sugar, 4 cups warm water, oil, yeast, salt and gradually add flour.
Mix up at 6 p.m., work down at 9 p.m.
Pan out at 10:30.
Let stand overnight at room temperature.
Bake in the morning at 350° for 15-20 minutes.

t: Dick'S Grilled Boneless Leg Of Lamb
i: 1 butterflied leg of lamb (have butcher prepare it)
1 piece fresh ginger (about equal in size to total of garlic)
d: Puree onion, garlic, ginger and pepper in food processor.
Mix together with shoyu, honey and oil.
Spread marinade on both sides of butterflied leg of lamb.
Put lamb and marinade in a plastic bag in refrigerator.
Lamb can marinate overnight.
Turn several times.
Combine first 6 ingredients; stir well.
Stir in pecans.
Pour into shell.
Bake at 350° for 25 minutes or until done.

i: 3 to 4 bananas, sliced in lemon juice
d: Drain all canned fruit, saving fruit cocktail juice (about 2 cups needed).
Whisk in Tang and instant pudding.
Pour over all the fruit.
This makes a big portion.
Mix flour, brown sugar and butter to crumbs.
Keep 1 cup crumbs aside.
Add boiling water, molasses, eggs, hot water and baking soda.
Mix all together with mixer.
Sprinkle with crumbs. Bake 35 to 40 minutes at 350°.
Makes two 9-inch pies.
Cook spaghetti in boiling salted water.
Do not overcook; it is preferable that spaghetti be firm.
After draining, place spaghetti in a large shallow saucepan.
Add tomato sauce and cheese.
Stir mixture over heat until the cheese melts and forms strands.
Arrange spaghetti in a serving platter.
Beat in a bowl until dough leaves sides.
Turn onto a lightly floured surface; gather into a ball.
Knead 10 times (I had fun doing this).
Cover with a bowl; let stand 15 minutes.
Sprinkle artichoke hearts with salt and pepper.
Mix parsley with bread crumbs.
Beat eggs slightly.
Dip hearts in egg; roll in bread crumbs.
Fry in hot oil about 3 minutes on each side or until golden brown.
Serve very hot.
For syrup, mix water, sugar and cinnamon.
Cook for 5 minutes (simmer), then add butter.
While syrup is cooking, in a bowl put flour and shortening.
Cut shortening into flour until it resembles coarse bread crumbs.
Add milk all at once.
Stir until moistened.
Soak the bulgur in lemon juice for 1/2 an hour.
Chop all the first 4 ingredients very fine.
Mix all together.
Add olive oil, salt and pepper to taste.
Serve with romaine lettuce!

i: 1/2 c. fresh or canned drained pineapple (optional)
d: Wash cranberries; drain in colander and coarsely chop 1/2 cup at a time in blender or food processor.
Wash and remove seeds from apple and orange (do not peel).
Chop other fruits.
Add to cranberries.
Add spices; mix well.
Flavor improves on standing in refrigerator.

i: 1 (7 oz.) or 1/2 (1 lb.) pkg. vermicelli (thin spaghetti), cooked as pkg. directs and drained
1 (4 oz.) can mushroom stems and pieces, drained
d: Preheat oven to 375°.
In large skillet, brown meat; pour off fat.
Add onion, cook and stir until tender.
Stir in remaining ingredients, except spaghetti and cheese.
Simmer 10 minutes. Stir in cooked spaghetti.
Turn into greased 2-quart shallow baking dish, 12 x 7-inches.
In a small mixing bowl, beat together the cream cheese, sour cream, oregano, garlic powder and red pepper.
Spread evenly in 9 or 10-inch quiche pan or pie plate.
Spread pizza sauce over the top.
Sprinkle with pepperoni, green onions and green pepper. Bake in preheated 350° oven for 10 minutes.
Top with cheese. Bake 5 minutes more, until cheese is melted and mixture is heated through.
Mix all dry ingredients and spread over crackers, then spread oil over.

i: 3 medium zucchini (1 lb.), sliced 1/4-inch thick (4 c.)
2 medium tomatoes, cut into wedges and seeded
d: In 2-quart microwave-safe casserole, microcook artichokes and water, covered, on 100% power (High) for 3 to 4 minutes or until thawed.
Stir.
Add next 3 ingredients.
Cover.
Cook on High for 9 to 11 minutes (low-wattage oven for 12 to 14 minutes) or just until tender; stir once.
Drain well.
Drain all fruit and place in a 2-quart buttered baking dish. In a small mixing bowl, combine cornstarch, curry powder, cinnamon and sugar.
Sprinkle sugar mixture over fruit.

i: 1 pkg. Hidden Valley Ranch dressing mix
d: In Dutch oven, cook ground beef, onion and bell pepper until beef is browned. Drain good. Add Ranch dressing mix and taco seasoning mix to ground beef and mix together. Add canned vegetables. (You may have to add a little water if mixture looks too thick.) Cook on medium heat until hot, then turn heat down to low and let simmer. Stir occasionally to make sure it doesn't stick to bottom of pan. Serve with grated cheese and Fritos.
Mix all ingredients in a deep dish or jar so liquid will cover mushrooms.
Chill overnight.
Drain and serve.
Mix dry ingredients with shortening until it looks a coarse meal.
Add warm water and knead dough well.
Divide into 12 small balls.
Roll into very thin circles.
Cook on hot griddle until done on both sides.

i: 1 (28 oz.) can crushed tomatoes, packed in puree
salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
1 lb. peeled and deveined large shrimp
d: In a large nonstick saucepan, heat the oil and saute the onion over medium heat for 7 to 8 minutes or until soft and lightly golden, stirring often.
Add the tomatoes, parsley, dill, garlic, sugar, salt and pepper.
Bring to a boil.
Cover, reduce heat and simmer slowly for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Preheat the oven to 375°.
Generously spray a shallow ovenproof dish with vegetable cooking spray.

i: 2 Tbsp. each chopped parsley, Parmesan cheese, wheat germ and diced onion
1/2 tsp. each celery seed and basil
d: Cook, drain and rinse shells.
Combine remaining ingredients except spaghetti sauce and the extra Parmesan cheese.
Spread 1/2 cup spaghetti sauce in bottom of shallow baking dish.
Fill shells with tofu mix and place in single layer in baking dish.
Top with remaining sauce and sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
Bake at 375° for 25 minutes or until hot and bubbly.
Combine and heat until smooth, peanut butter, margarine and vanilla.
Gradually
beat
in
Tear corn tortillas into small triangles.
Spread them on cookie sheets and bake for 30 minutes at 325°.
If you rub each tortilla first with a tiny bit of butter, 1/4 teaspoon to a side, the flavor steps up; cut the baking time down to 20 minutes.
Combine buttermilk, sugar, soda, salt and shortening.
Stir in yeast.
Mix in flour.
Stir well.
Turn onto floured board.
Cover and let stand 10 minutes.
Brown meat in cast-iron pot; drain well and return to stove. Add tomato sauce and 1 1/2 cups water; simmer.
Add chili powder and all herbs.
Mix well and allow to cook for about 10 minutes. Add salt and cayenne pepper to taste; allow to simmer until thick. Add sugar and cook for few minutes longer.
Serves 10 to 12 people.

i: 2 (10 oz.) pkg. chopped broccoli, cooked and drained
d: Put broccoli in 11 x 7-inch baking dish.
Melt 1 stick of margarine in saucepan.
Add flour and milk.
Cook until thick.
Add bouillon cubes.
Stir until dissolved.

i: 1 (16 oz.) pkg. Hillshire Farm smoked sausage
1 (10 3/4 oz.) can Campbell's cream of celery soup
4 c. cooked penne pasta (2 1/2 c. uncooked)
1 1/2 c. French's Cheddar French fried onions
d: Heat oven to 375°. Cut sausage into 1/4-inch slices and brown in skillet; drain. Combine soup and milk in 3-quart casserole. Stir in pasta, sausage, 1/2 cup French fried onions and peas. Bake 30 minutes until hot; stir. Top with remaining cheese and onions. Bake 3 minutes, until golden.
Combine sugar, oleo, pecans and vanilla
in a saucepan. Boil for 3 minutes. Split graham crackers about 40 small pieces. Put on cookie sheet and pour glaze over graham crackers. Bake at 300 degrees for 12 minutes. Put on wax paper.

i: 1 chicken, cut up or 8 meaty pieces
d: In medium saucepan, saute onions, green pepper and garlic in butter until tender, but not brown.
Add tomatoes and simmer, uncovered, 10 minutes.
Stir in 2 teaspoons salt, pepper, curry powder and thyme.
Simmer 5 minutes; set aside.
Beat eggs.
Add oil, sugar, vanilla, grated zucchini and nuts. Set aside.
Sift together flour, soda, salt and cinnamon.
Add to the liquid mixture.
Stir in until well blended.
Pour into 2 greased and floured loaf pans.

i: 1 (31 oz.) can beans and pork in tomato sauce
d: In large saucepan, cook beef, onion and garlic until beef is browned and onion is tender.
Stir in minestone soup, beans and pork, 3 1/2 cups of water, the celery, Worcestershire sauce and oregano.
Simmer, covered, for 15 to 20 minutes.
Makes 12 to 14 servings.
Mix together warm water with yeast.
Add salt, sugar, oil and flour.
Stir together and then let rise 1/2 hour in greased bowl. Put on cookie sheets which are greased and sprinkled with cornmeal.
Makes 3 cookie sheets. No kneading of dough is necessary.
Dissolve yeast in lukewarm water.
Heat buttermilk to lukewarm.
Add the salt, shortening and sugar to the buttermilk. Add the dissolved yeast, a cup of the flour with the soda and the egg.
Beat vigorously.
Beat and fold in the remaining flour until a soft dough.
Fold lightly (about 5 minutes).

i: 1/4 c. milk (more if needed to make paste)
d: Melt butter; pour in bottom of dish.
Add peaches.
Mix flour and sugar together.
Add milk to make paste of flour and sugar. Pour over peaches and butter.
Stir in flavoring.
Bake at 375° for 30 to 40 minutes or until brown.
Mix meat, egg, milk, rice, chile powder and 1 teaspoon salt. Form 1 1/2-inch balls.
Brown.
Combine tomatoes, water, onion and salt.
Bring to boil; add meat balls.
Cover and cook slowly about 1 1/2 hours.
Tear lettuce into bite-sized pieces; place in bottom of large salad bowl.
Layer remaining vegetables in order given.
Combine mayonnaise and sugar; spread over top, completely covering vegetables.
Sprinkle top with egg, bacon and cheese.

i: 1 Shady Brook Farms split turkey breast
8 oz. nonfat or low-fat plain yogurt
1/2 tsp. hot red pepper sauce as desired
d: Combine all ingredients, except turkey, in large shallow pan or bowl.
Add turkey; marinate for 1 hour in refrigerator, turning once.
Discard unused marinade.
Cover the breasts with foil; roast for 30 minutes in a 350° oven.
Baste occasionally. Uncover; roast for an additional 30 to 40 minutes, until brown.
In a 1 1/2-quart casserole place pecan halves.
Sprinkle with seasoned salt.
Cut butter into 4 pieces and arrange evenly over top.
Microwave at High 5 to 6 minutes. Mix to evenly distribute butter.
Serve warm or cold.
Place chicken in covered casserole.
Mix liquids and spices and pour over chicken. Bake, covered, for 1 1/2 hours at 350°. Serves 4.
Shred together the first 4 ingredients. Sprinkle with the salt.
Let stand overnight.

i: 2 (6 oz. each) cans frozen limeade, thawed
2 (12 oz. each) bottles lemon-lime carbonated beverage, chilled
d: Mix limeade concentrate, cold water and carbonated beverage in a large punch bowl.
Spoon scoops of sherbet into bowl.
Serve immediately.
Yields 15 servings (about 1/2 cup each).
Debone chicken breasts and cut in small pieces.
Dip in beaten eggs, then roll in Italian bread crumbs.
Let stand for 15 minutes.
Brown in oil.
Put in a large casserole dish.
Cover with mushrooms, Mozzarella and Cheddar cheese.
Spread cream cheese on ham.
Place breadstick on cheese.
Roll up.
Layer the first 5 ingredients.
Mix mayonnaise with cheese. Top with the hard-boiled eggs (crumbled) and the bacon (crumbled). Can be covered with wrap and served from refrigerator the next day.

Mix egg, milk, cooking oil, water and sugar. Mix in the yeast. After the yeast is active, gradually add in flour in a mixing bowl. After it is mixed well, it will form a large ball. Let it stand at room temperature, covered, with a towel or plastic wrap, until it has doubled in size. Make into rolls, leaving 1/2-inch between each on a greased baking sheet. Cover with plastic wrap and place into the refrigerator. The rolls will have to rise again at room temperature before baking. Top with margarine or butter for crispy brown tops and bake at 400° for 15 to 20 minutes.
Beat egg whites until very stiff; add sugar gradually and baking powder.
Beat until stiff.
Fold in crackers, nuts and vanilla.
Put in buttered pie pan; bake at 350° for 23 minutes. May be served with whipped cream.

i: skinned chicken parts (I use family pack of thighs)
d: Pour lemon juice over chicken.
Sprinkle with cinnamon.
Broil for 8 minutes in a deep baking pan.
Slice onion, pepper, mushroom and garlic.
Dump on chicken.
Pour tomato juice over all until almost covered.
Fix 1 box jello by directions; add 3/4 can of peaches (no juice).
Let set up.
Mix other box jello with 1/2 cup boiling water; stir until dissolved.
Add 1/2 Cool Whip.
Beat until mixed well.
Chill.

i: 1/4 c. chopped onion or 1 Tbsp. minced onion
1 (10 oz.) can Hungry Jack refrigerated flaky biscuits
d: Heat oven to 375°.
Grease 10 muffin cups.
Brown ground beef and drain.
Stir in barbecue sauce, onion and brown sugar.
Cook 3 minutes to blend.
Stir constantly.
Saute shrimp and shallots in butter and oil.
Add garlic when you turn shrimp (turn shrimp when they are pink halfway through). Serve as is or on top of pasta.
Saute rice in butter in a large frying pan until dark brown. Add onion when rice is about 1/2 browned.
Add mushroom soup and bouillon.
Stir.
Simmer on low, stirring occasionally, until liquid is absorbed (about 20 to 30 minutes).

i: 3/4 c. or 1 1/2 sticks butter or margarine, melted
1 (12 oz.) jar or 1 c. raspberry, apricot, blackberry or strawberry preserves or jam
d: In large bowl, combine dry cake mix and oats; stir in melted butter until mixture is crumbly.
Place 1/2 of crumb mixture in pan and spread evenly.
Combine preserves and water.
Spoon over crumbs.
Cover with remaining crumb mixture; pat firmly to make top even.
Bake in preheated 375° oven for 18 to 23 minutes; top should be very light brown.

i: 3 large ripe tomatoes, peeled and halved
d: In a 5-quart pan, add 3 quarts of water and all ingredients, except garlic, olive oil and paprika.
(Mash garlic; mix with paprika and olive oil and add to the beans after beans are tender.) Add water as needed.
Serves 6.
Sift flour, sugar, baking powder and salt into mixing bowl. Add the beaten egg.
Mix in milk and vegetable oil just until ingredients are mixed.
Stir in chips and orange peel.
Line muffin tins with paper liners or grease tins.
Fill 2/3 full with batter.
Sprinkle nuts on top.

i: 3 c. grated carrots (grate carrots and set aside)
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
Sift sugar, flour and cinnamon together.
Add oil and eggs.
Mix well.
Add carrots.
Mix well. Fold in pecans.
Brown sausage in skillet. Drain off fat.
Slice into bite size pieces and reheat with vegetables (to soften).
Boil noodles according to package directions. Drain.
Put in casserole dish and mix with sausage, vegetables and Prego sauce.
Bon appetit!

i: 2/3 c. grape jelly (can use apple jelly)
d: Mix mustard and jelly together.
Place in crock-pot.
Put in little Smokies.
Cook on high until good and hot or you can cook on low all day.
Place 1 1/2 cups water in saucepan and bring to boil; add pinch of salt and rice.
Bring to boil again, cover.
Turn heat down to cook rice approximately 50 minutes.
When rice is done, combine tahini, tamari sauce and water in small saucepan.
Bring to boil and cook five minutes.
Add water to create creamy consistency. Combine rice and sesame sauce and serve while hot.
Peel and dice tomatoes in large pan. Dice onion and peppers, add to other ingredients and cook on medium low heat for 30 minutes.
This can be put in jars and sealed.
Good kept in refrigerator for a week.
*You can use ground beef also.
Cook the rice.
Wash the tomato, mushrooms and green pepper. Cut the mushrooms in half and mince the green pepper. Peel and slice the onion.
Melt the butter in the skillet and saute the onion until soft.
Add the other vegetables, tomato paste, salt, cayenne and curry powder.
Cook for 15 minutes, stirring.
Stir in the cooked rice and serve.

t: Eva'S Mexican Squash With Ground Meat
i: 3 Mexican squash, diced and seeded (if tender, don't peel)
d: Brown meat
in	oil and pour off the grease.
Add onion and bell pepper.
Continue cooking the vegetables lightly.
Mix all ingredients in small bowl, cover and refrigerate overnight.
Serve with fresh vegetables as an appetizer.
Mix all ingredients together.
Pour into a 9 x 13-inch pan. Freeze until firm.
Allow to set out approximately 15 minutes before serving.
Mix ingredients together; form into small balls.
Bake in oven at 400° for about 15 to 20 minutes.
Cream butter and sugar.
Add eggs and beat well.
Add pineapple; stir in bread and cinnamon.
Bake in greased casserole, uncovered, for 1 hour or lightly browned at 350°.

i: 2 Tbsp. each: red wine vinegar and water
1 c. each: quartered mushrooms and julienne-cut red bell peppers (thin strips)
d: In medium salad bowl, combine spinach with mushrooms and red pepper strips.
In small bowl, combine vinegar with water, sugar and mustard.
Add oil, salt and pepper; mix well.
Pour dressing over salad and toss to coat.

i: Uncle Ben's fiesta rice (1 box) or you can substitute Lipton's Spanish rice
d: Preheat oven to 425°. Cook chicken in skillet until done (browned and juices evaporate), stirring often. Add soup and salsa and heat through. Spread about 1/3 cup mixture on half of each tortilla. Moisten edge of tortilla with water; fold and seal. Place on baking sheet. Bake for 5 minutes or until lightly browned and hot. Serve with fiesta rice and tortilla chips and salsa.
Melt shortening in skillet.
Mash beans with fork.
Add beans and salt to taste.
Heat and stir in skillet.
Use jelly roll pan.
Grease and flour pan.
Whip eggs until thick and foamy.
Add sugar gradually to eggs.
Fold in pumpkin and lemon juice. Sift together all dry ingredients and fold into egg mixture, beating just until batter is smooth (can use electric mixer, low speed).
Pour into pan.
Mix all ingredients together.
Pour into 2 greased and floured loaf pans.
Bake at 325° for 1 hour or until done.
Roll bread dough into a 14-inch circle. Layer all ingredients. Fold sides and ends in and pinch to seal. Bake 25 minutes at 350°. Let stand 10 minutes. Serve.

i: 2 cans green beans (save 1 can of juice)
d: Saute bacon and onions.
Add 2 cans of green beans and 1 can of juice.
Add sugar and vinegar.
Add salt and pepper.
Simmer for 15 to 30 minutes.
Combine dry ingredients and make a hole in the center.
Add oil, water, eggs and pumpkin; mix together.
Pour into 4 small greased loaf pans.
Bake at 350° for 55 minutes.
If desired, raisins can be added to mixture.
Blend flour, sugar and butter together until mixture is crumbly.
Add 1/4 cup pecans and reserve the rest.
Press mixture into an ungreased 9-inch square pan.
Bake 15 minutes or until edges are lightly browned.
Mix chicken, sour cream, enchilada sauce, green chiles and chicken broth in pan and heat.
Fry tortillas until softened. Layer in shallow pan the sauce, tortillas and onion and top with cheese.
Bake at 375° for 30 minutes.
Cut cheese into cubes in a saucepan; add salsa to keep cheese from sticking.
Brown hamburger; drain.
Salt and pepper to taste. Add Tabasco sauce.
Combine hamburger and cheese.
Excellent dip for nachos or taco salad.
Saute onions until tender with the garlic.
Add remaining ingredients and cook for 1 hour in crock-pot on high or simmer on stove until done.
Put oranges, apples and cranberries through food chopper. Drain pineapple and add. Dissolve Jell-O, sugar in water (heat to dissolve thoroughly).
Mix into mixture well and add chopped nuts. Let set in refrigerator about 24 hours before serving.
Mix flour, sugar, cocoa and baking soda.
Blend in remaining ingredients.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Bake 30 to 35 minutes by inserting toothpick into center.
When clean when pulled out tells you it's ready.
Let cool and remove from pans.
Combine and chill rice, onion, vinegar, salad oil and curry powder.
Add remaining ingredients and mix lightly.
Grind vegetables using coarse blade.
Sprinkle with salt; let stand overnight.
Rinse and drain.
Combine remaining ingredients. Pour over vegetables.
Heat to boiling.
Simmer for 3 minutes. Makes 8 pints.

i: 1 or 2 small jalapeno peppers, seeded and minced
d: Combine all ingredients, stirring gently; cover and chill.

i: 3 c. sweet potatoes, mashed and whipped
d: Mix together and pour into casserole dish.
In a bowl, combine the cranberry sauce, concentrates, water, cinnamon and allspice.
Cover and freeze for 8 hours or overnight. Remove from freezer 45 minutes before serving.
For each serving, combine 1/2 c. slush mixture with 1/2 c. lemon-lime soda.
Cook noodles according to package directions; rinse and drain. Brown hamburger in skillet.
Add next 6 ingredients; mix well. Cook for 5 minutes; remove from heat.
Combine noodles with cottage cheese, sour cream and green onions.
Alternate layers of noodle mixture and meat mixture in 2-quart baking dish, ending with meat mixture.
Top with Cheddar cheese.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes, or until heated through.
Put 2 cups sugar and milk in a saucepan.
Add syrup.
Put the 1/2 cup sugar in a small skillet and pour enough water over it to moisten good.
Turn on low heat under saucepan.
While it is heating, brown the sugar in the skillet.
When brown, pour it into the saucepan.
Season chicken with curry powder, salt and pepper.
Brown in oil until golden.
Combine orange juice, water, brown sugar, dry mustard and mix well.
Add to chicken; simmer covered for 20 minutes.
Lift chicken from broth and add rice.
Arrange chicken pieces over rice.

t: Green Bean And Radish Salad In Sour Cream Vinaigrette
i: 2 c. thin sliced radishes (red and white)
d: Bring plenty of lightly salted water to a boil.
Add the beans and cook until just tender, about 6 or 7 minutes.
Drain and run under cold water to stop further cooking.
Transfer the beans to a serving bowl.
Sprinkle with 1 tablespoon vinegar, sour cream and oil.
Whisk the dressing until smooth.

i: 1 pkg. Pepperidge Farm corn bread stuffing
d: Boil chicken until tender.
Cool and debone.
Mix milk, celery soup
and	chicken
soup.	Add
1 cup chicken broth to mixture. Melt
Blanch, peel, quarter and de-seed tomatoes.
Combine all ingredients.
Cook down FOREVER! Now for the weird part, it seems to work best if I put the hot sauce through a blender and bring back to canning temperature.
(But be so careful - the hot sauce is much more likely to end up on the floor, ceiling, cupboards, face, etc.) It's well worth the effort as it comes out much smoother.
Put through water bath for 40 minutes.
Yields approximately 24 quarts. (That's a lot, but since it takes so much time and energy to make, it'll last a couple years at least!)
Melt the chocolate bits and shortening in a saucepan over low heat.
Stir until smooth.
Dip animal crackers into chocolate and lift with a fork.
Place on waxed paper to harden.
With fingers, crumble butter and enough flour (mix flour and salt first) to make a coarse, meal-like texture.
Add enough milk to stick it all together.
Turn onto floured surface, roll and cut. Bake at 400° for 10 to 12 minutes.
Makes 8 to 10 biscuits.

i: 1 lb. boneless, skinless chicken breast, cut in 1-inch cubes
d: In a 5-quart pot, heat oil.
Add chicken and saute until meat is not pink.
Add water, mushrooms, cabbage, bamboo shoots and bouillon cubes.
Bring to boil.
Reduce heat and simmer 1/2 hour. In small bowl, dissolve cornstarch and stir with fork until smooth.
Stir into soup.
Combine egg, sweet potatoes, sugar, shortening and margarine in a bowl; mix well.
Stir in enough flour to make a soft dough. Knead lightly several times on floured surface.
Roll to 1/4-inch thickness; cut with 2-inch biscuit cutter.
Place on greased baking sheet.
Bake at 350° for 15 minutes.
Makes 17 biscuits.
Cream sugar, eggs and salad oil until well blended.
Sift together flour and spices.
Dissolve soda in buttermilk and add flour mixture and buttermilk to the egg mixture.
Add fig preserves and pecans.
Bake in a greased tube pan at 350° for about 1 hour or until done.
This makes a large cake that will keep in the refrigerator indefinitely.

i: 12 c. green or half ripe mangoes, peeled and sliced
4 Hawaiian red peppers, seeded and chopped
d: Combine mango slices and salt.
Let stand overnight.
Rinse with cold water and drain.
Combine sugar and vinegar; simmer 30 minutes.
Add mango slices and remaining ingredients and simmer for 1 hour or until mangoes are tender and chutney is of desired consistency.
Pour into hot sterilized jars and seal.
Melt shortening in skillet.
Lightly brown the ground beef with salt, black pepper, red pepper, onion and chili powder.
Sprinkle flour over mixture.
Stir in Worcestershire sauce, tomatoes and catsup.
Simmer 15 to 20 minutes.
Makes 18 to 20 servings.

i: 1 (16 oz.) bottle chilled lemon-lime carbonated beverage
d: Put pineapple juice and sugar in pitcher.
Add 1/2 cup lime sherbet; stir mixture until smooth.
Pour 1/2 cup juice into each of 4 glasses; add 1 scoop sherbet to each glass.
Fill with lemon-lime beverages.
Mix jello with hot water and then add cold.
Dissolve good. Then add crushed pineapple and juice.
Cool awhile in refrigerator while you grind or chop the cranberries and orange peel, then add all the rest of the ingredients to cool mixture.
Put in refrigerator until set.
You could add sliced apples.

i: 1 can Pillsbury pizza crust from the dairy case
1/2 c. Kraft Healthy Favorites shredded mild Cheddar cheese
1/2 c. Kraft Healthy Favorites shredded mild Mozzarella cheese
8 oz. Perdue ground turkey (not all white meat), cooked in microwave and drained
d: Grease well a jelly roll pan.
Spread pizza crust in pan. Spread a thin layer of pizza sauce over the crust.
Crumble the ground turkey over the sauce.
Add the shredded cheese.
Bake in 425° oven for 15 to 18 minutes.
If you like, you can add onions, green peppers and mushrooms before sprinkling the cheese on top.

i: 2 Tbsp. minced fresh or 2 tsp. crumbled dried basil leaves
1 (1 lb.) loaf frozen white or whole wheat bread dough, thawed
1 large egg yolk, beaten with 1 Tbsp. water
d: Combine mushrooms, onion, garlic and basil with half the broth in a 10 to 12-inch frying pan over medium-high heat.
Stir often until vegetables are browned and begin to stick in pan, about 8 minutes. Add remaining broth. Stir to scrape browned bits from pan; cook until liquid evaporates.
Stir in flour and ham.
Remove from heat.
Combine and refrigerate until ready to serve.
Makes lots of cookies!!!

i: 3/4 loaf Italian sliced bread, broken in pieces
d: Soak bread in hot milk.
Set aside.
Beat together eggs and sugar.
Add to soaked bread/milk mixture.
Add vanilla, raisins and grated apple.
Add brandy last.
Shred cabbage.
Add rest of the ingredients.
Bake at 375° for 1 1/2 hours.

i: 1/2 lb. cooked sirloin or roast beef (rare)
1 onion, thinly sliced and separated in rings
d: Slice meat in julienne strips.
Add sliced onion, salt and pepper to taste.
Sprinkle lemon juice over mixture and mix in sour cream.
Chill well.
Serve in crystal bowl with crackers. Great appetizer!

t: Hot Crab, Artichoke And Jalapeno Dip With Pita Triangles
i: 2 (14 oz.) cans artichoke hearts, drained and chopped fine
1/2 c. drained and chopped bottled pimiento or roasted red pepper
1 1/2 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice or to taste
4 tsp. Worcestershire sauce or to taste
3 bottled pickled jalapeno peppers or to taste, seeded and minced (wear rubber gloves)
d: In a small heavy skillet, cook the bell pepper in the oil over moderate heat, stirring, until it is softened and let it cool.
In a large bowl, combine the bell pepper, artichokes, mayonnaise, scallions, pimiento, Parmesan, lemon juice, Worcestershire sauce, jalapeno peppers and celery salt.
Blend the mixture until it is combined well and stir in the crab meat gently.
Drain fruit; save juices.
Heat juices and Cola to make 2 cups.
Bring to boil and dissolve Jell-O in heat of liquid.
Add fruit; refrigerate.
Brown ground turkey in skillet.
Drain off excess fat.
Add meat into spaghetti sauce and simmer over stove while preparing the rest of the recipe.
Prepare pasta according to directions on box.
Spray with nonstick oil; add pasta and pour meat sauce over. Arrange pepperoni on top.
Sprinkle shredded cheese over the top. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Combine ingredients, except bread, cucumber and zucchini, in food processor or blender on high until smooth.
Spoon into serving bowl.
Serve with bread and veggies.
Makes about 2 cups.
If eggs are small, use a little less flour; if eggs are large use a little more flour.
Pound chops and pepper to taste.
Heat oil in skillet and lightly brown chops on both sides.
Remove from skillet.
Add onion and garlic to skillet and saute about 4 to 5 minutes.
Add flour and stir into onion mixture until blended.
Add bouillon and mustard, stirring until mixture is smooth, then add meat.
Brown sausage and drain. Melt cheese. Mix cheese, sausage and sauce. Put in crock-pot and let it get hot. Good with tortilla chips.

i: 1 (7 oz.) can tuna, drained and broken in chunks
2 English muffins, split and lightly buttered
d: Combine tuna, celery, raisins and green onion in a small bowl. In a measuring cup, stir together mayonnaise, lemon juice, curry and salt.
Fold in tuna mixture.
Toast English muffins. Immediately spoon tuna mixture on muffins.
Top each sandwich with slice of cheese.
Broil for 2 to 3 minutes or until cheese melts. Serve hot, garnish with celery leaves.
Makes 4 sandwiches.

i: 1 (4 oz.) can chopped green chilies, drained
d: Combine first 6 ingredients in a large bowl.
Combine corn meal and remaining ingredients; stir into corn mixture.
Pour into a lightly greased 11 x 7 x 1 1/2-inch baking dish.
Bake at 350° for 50 minutes or until a knife inserted in center comes out clean.
Mix all together, except flour and milk.
Mix flour and milk until smooth; then mix all together and pour into a greased baking dish.
Bake at 375° approximately 1 1/2 hours or until firm and slightly browned.
Cut nuts, dates and orange candy into small pieces.
Add coconut.
Melt butter and pour over nut mixture.
In another bowl, mix eggs, sugar, flour, soda and milk.
Pour over the nut mixture and mix well in a large bowl.
Bake in tube pan 2 hours at 300°. Let cool in pan.

i: 1 pkg. Good Seasons Italian salad dressing
3 to 4 jars whole mushrooms (4 1/2 oz. size jar), drained
d: Mix ingredients together and add mushrooms.
Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Pour off sauce and serve mushrooms with toothpicks.
Remove skin and bones from salmon; mash fine.
Add salt, pepper, paprika, lemon juice, beaten egg yolks, cracker crumbs and hot milk.
Fold in egg whites, which have been beaten stiff.
Pour into well-greased pan.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Serves 4 to 5.

i: 10 oz. boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut in strips
1/2 c. each carrots and celery, diagonally sliced
1/2 c. green bell peppers, sliced in strips
d: In glass bowl, combine chicken, soy sauce and garlic powder; stir well and set aside.
Heat 2 teaspoons oil in skillet or wok. Stir in all vegetables and stir-fry 3 to 5 minutes until onions are translucent.
Remove to bowl.
Heat remaining 2 teaspoons oil. Add chicken and stir-fry until chicken is cooked, about 5 minutes.
Return vegetables to skillet or wok.
Stir in cornstarch mixture and continue cooking until sauce thickens.
Combine first 5 ingredients.
Stir in pecans.
Shred enough zucchini to measure 2 cups.
Combine zucchini and remaining ingredients.
Add to flour mixture, stirring just until dry ingredients are moistened.
Spoon batter into 2 greased and floured 9 x 5 x 3-inch loaf pans.
Lightly brown bacon and onion in skillet.
Add remaining ingredients.
Turn on low heat and simmer 20 minutes.
Cook beans.
Brown meat, then add other ingredients and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add meat mixture to beans and simmer for one hour.
Mix all ingredients.
Use 1 tablespoon (heaping) per cup of water.
Parboil potatoes 15 minutes, peel and cut in half lengthwise. Place in a baking dish.
Combine sugar, salt, oleo and water. Boil for 3 minutes.
Pour syrup over potatoes.
Bake at 350° for 1 to 1 1/4 hours.
Place in an 8-cup glass measure 2 cups water.
Microcook on High for 6 minutes until boils.
Add 1 cup cracked wheat bulgur. Stand, covered, 1 hour.
Drain excess liquid.
Chill before serving.

i: 2 c. fresh or thawed, frozen broccoli florets
d: Preheat oven to 450°.
Spray a 6-cup shallow baking dish with nonstick cooking spray.
Cut the Cheddar-flavor cheese and the Mozzarella into 1/4-inch cubes.
Combine all ingredients, except apples and pecans, mix well. Fold in fruit and nuts. Bake in well greased tube or bundt pan at 350 degrees for approximately 55 minutes.
Cool in pan. May serve with whipped cream.

i: 1/4 c. firmly packed light brown sugar
1/2 c. dry cranberries or cherries, coarsely chopped
1/2 c. chilled butter, cut into pieces (1 stick)
d: Preheat oven to 400°.
Butter 2 large cookie sheets.
Sift flour into bowl.
Mix in both sugars.
Add butter and rub in fingertips until mixture resembles coarse meal.
Add 1/2 cup dried fruit.
Make oatmeal.
Cool about 20 minutes.
Add remaining ingredients; beat until well blended.
Bake in a 9 x 13-inch pan at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes.
Mix and sift first 6 ingredients.
Stir in unbeaten eggs and vanilla. Fold in apples and walnuts.
Turn into well greased 9-inch pie plate.
Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes until brown and crusty.
Serve warm or cold with topping or ice cream.

i: 2 c. soaked chickpeas or canned chickpeas
d: Cook chickpeas in enough water to cover until tender, 1/2 to 1 hour.
Saute onion in olive oil.
Add to chickpeas.
Add carrots and zucchini.
Cook on low heat until vegetables are tender.
Add a little water if necessary to keep moist.
Melt butter in pan.
Add flour and salt.
Stir until smooth. Add milk gradually and stir constantly until thickened.
(Makes one cup white sauce.)
Bring chicken broth to a boil, add chopped giblets and eggs.
Add white sauce and cook until thick.

i: 1 1/2 lb. ground beef, cooked and drained
1 small frozen bag of each: carrots, peas, green beans and corn
d: Put all ingredients into large pot; cook until the vegetables are tender (about 1 hour).
Great for freezing!

i: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen broccoli, cooked and drained
d: Heat process cheese spread, milk and mayo over low heat; stir until sauce is smooth.
Add remaining ingredients.
Mix well. Bake at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.
Saute onion, turkey and celery in margarine.
Add 1 2/3 cups water and seasoning.
Mix. Cook 5 minutes.
Then add stuffing mix and let set.

i: 1/4 lb. sharp Cheddar cheese, cubed to 30 pieces
d: Separate biscuits; cut into thirds.
Wrap dough around cheese cubes; seal well. Fry in hot oil for 2 to 3 minutes until golden brown. Yield: 30 puffs.

i: 2 cans (1 lb. size) Stokely's shredded kraut
d: This salad should
be
made
to 48 hours before you plan to serve it.
(I like to use the sweet/sour kraut.)
Use your favorite biscuit dough.
Roll out like for jelly roll.
Sprinkle with sugar.
Cut butter up; put on top.
Roll up in rolls; prick with fork.
Put rolls in an 8 x 8-inch Pyrex bowl. Pour milk over rolls; milk should almost cover rolls.
Cream margarine, sugar and eggs; stir in buttermilk.
Sift flour and soda together; add to creamed mixture.
Fold in pecans, cherries and dates.
Drop small amounts onto lightly greased cookie sheet.
Bake at 400° for 8 to 10 minutes.
Yields 6 dozen cookies.
Heat sugar, salt, celery seed, mustard seed, vinegar and red pepper in medium saucepan.
Boil for 2 minutes.
Remove from heat; stir in corn with liquid, green pepper, minced onion and pimento. Refrigerate overnight or several days to blend flavors.
Boil chicken until tender.
Take skin and bones out.
Combine all in the pan and bring chicken broth to a boil.
Add dumplings one at a time.
Let boil until tender.
Add water if needed.
Combine all ingredients in a Dutch oven and simmer 1 to 2 hours.
Serve over cooked rice with cornbread.
Cook garlic in oil 1 minute.
Stir in next 6 ingredients. Heat to boiling constantly stirring.
Reduce heat & simmer until thickened about 3 minutes.
Cook & drain sausage, stir in 1 c. salsa mixture.
In ungreased casserole dish layer 1/2 of cheese (sliced 1/4 inches) then sausage mixture.
Top with 1/2 cheese. Bake uncovered at 400 degrees 8-10 minutes.
Grind the first 4 ingredients.
Sprinkle 5 teaspoons of salt over and let set overnight in refrigerator.
Next a.m. rinse mixture well and drain in colander.
Set aside.

i: 1 pt. cherry tomatoes with stems removed, washed
1/2 tsp. coarse salt or to taste
d: With a small sharp knife, cut a small cross in the stem end of each tomato.
Put the tomatoes in a single layer in a medium size saucepan.
Add the oil and garlic and sprinkle with the salt. Saute over low heat until tomatoes are just heated through, about 5 minutes, stirring gently several times.
Sprinkle with the parsley and serve hot.
Serves 6.

i: 2 (28 oz.) bottles lemon-lime carbonated beverage, chilled
d: Spoon orange sherbet by tablespoons into a large punch bowl. Stir in orange juice and lemon juice.
Slowly pour in chilled lemon-lime carbonated beverage; stir gently to mix.
Makes 36 ounces.
Boil or bake fish; remove all bones, then mash.
Beat egg whites until stiff; set aside.
Beat egg yolks until creamy; add flour, salt, pepper, a little garlic and onion salt and parsley. Mix together, then add fish and mix well.
Add egg whites; fold in.
Drop into hot grease until golden brown.
Mix together and put into covered container.
Shake well and it's ready.
Can be frozen.
Brown ground beef; drain excess fat.
Add Miracle Whip, spaghetti sauce and macaroni.
Heat on medium, stirring occasionally.
Top with Kraft cheese.
Serves 4 to 6.
Combine all ingredients and cook on medium until it boils, then cook on low for 3 to 4 hours.
Add water if necessary to desired thickness.

i: 2 c. meat (hamburger, shredded pork, chicken, shrimp)
d: Shred cabbage.
Dice carrots and onion.
Cook meat and drain grease.
Add vegetables to skillet and stir-fry until crisp-tender.
Mix in soy sauce.
Place a heaping tablespoonful of filling in center of each egg roll skin.
Mix together until smooth.
Mix together and pour over 1 cut up fryer.
Marinate at least 1 hour or longer if you wish, then cook on hot grill until done.
Place chicken in a shallow baking dish.
Add oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano and pepper.
Toss chicken with seasonings; cover and marinate, refrigerated, for at least 1/2 hour.
Grill or broil chicken 6 to 8 inches from heat source for 15 minutes, turning 2 to 3 times.
Place Feta slices on top of chicken; continue to cook for 3 to 5 minutes, until cheese softens and chicken is cooked through.
Fry bacon; drain and chop.
Chop other ingredients.
Mix mayonnaise, sugar and vinegar.
Combine all.
Use day prepared.

i: 1/2 lb. red and 1/2 green, cut in small pieces
1 lb. candied pineapple, cut in small pieces
d: Mix nuts, fruit and flour until flour covers nuts and fruit good.
Add other ingredients and let batter stand 30 minutes, then drop from small spoon onto a cookie sheet.
Bake approximately 12 to 15 minutes in 350° oven.
Mix together by hand the first 5 ingredients (Raisin Bran, Crisco, white sugar, eggs and buttermilk).
Blend flour, baking soda and salt together and add.
Mix well.
Bake at 400° for 15 minutes or until golden brown.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Beat egg whites until foamy; add salt and cream of tartar.
Beat until stiff.
Add 2 tablespoons of sugar at a time, beating well after each addition.
Stir in vanilla, chocolate chips, nuts and coconut.
Drop by teaspoon onto foil lined cookie sheets.
Line bottom of 9 x 13-inch pan with foil.
Sprinkle the rice on the foil and place chicken pieces on rice.
Mix soup and water, then spread over chicken.
Sprinkle onion soup mix over all. Cover and seal.
Bake at 350° for 2 1/2 hours, without peeking.
Melt butter in Pyrex dish.
Sift dry ingredients together. Pour batter into melted butter.
Pour fruit into batter.
Don't stir.
Bake at 350° for 40 or 45 minutes.

i: 2 c. sifted Martha White self-rising flour
d: Hat oven to 350°.
Grease an 8 1/2 x 4 1/2 x 2 1/2-inch loaf pan.
Mix together thoroughly sugar, shortening and eggs.
Stir in buttermilk, mashed bananas and flour, then soda and fold in chopped nuts.
Turn batter into prepared pan.
Bake 50 to 60 minutes.
Mix ingredients together and pour in deep casserole dish. Bake for 1 hour at 400°.
Combine egg, vinegar, mustard, garlic, salt and herbs in blender or food processor and blend well.
Add oil and buttermilk and continue to blend.
Transfer to bowl and whisk in mayonnaise.
Makes enough canape spread for 24 Melba toast rounds or dry, crisp crackers.

i: 1 1/2 c. vanilla or peach ice cream
2 fresh peaches, peeled and pitted or 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen peach slices, thawed
d: Combine all ingredients in a blender container.
Cover.
Blend at medium speed until smooth, about 15 seconds.
Serve immediately.
Makes 2 servings.

i: 1 (15 oz.) can black beans, rinsed and drained
d: Mix all ingredients and refrigerate. Serve with tortilla chips. Can be refrigerated for 3 days.
Rinse rice until clear and drain.
Soak saffron in 2 tablespoons of hot water.
In a heavy 3 to 4-quart pan, melt butter.
Add cinnamon and cloves and coat with butter.
Add onions and cook 7 to 8 minutes until soft and golden brown.
Add rice and cook 5 minutes, stirring frequently.

i: 1 green pepper, cut into small pieces
d: In a large skillet, put in a couple of tablespoons olive oil and saute garlic, onions, tomatoes and green pepper.
Saute until pepper is tender and tomatoes are mushy.
Set aside.
Saute zucchini in some more olive oil.
Cook until zucchini is 1/2 done, just tender.
Heat oven to 350°.

i: 3/4 c. oats, quick cooking or regular
d: Heat milk over low heat until just hot, stirring occasionally. Stir in oats.
Remove from heat and let stand for 5 minutes. Combine flour, baking powder, cinnamon, ginger, soda and salt.
Bone chicken and discard skin.
Mince the chicken and combine with 1 teaspoon cornstarch and the egg whites, beaten until frothy.
Bring chicken broth to a boil.
Dissolve remaining cornstarch in water and add gradually to broth.
Add chicken mixture, corn, sherry and soy sauce and simmer a few minutes. Serve garnished with ham (boiled, baked or most flavorful and closest to the Chinese, Smithfield ham).
Serves 4.
Mix all together and bring to boil.

i: 1 can fruit topping mix (cherry, blueberry, apple or boysenberry)
d: Preheat oven to 375°.
Mix 3/4 cup sugar and butter.
Heat. Add crumbs.
Press into 13 x 9-inch pan and set aside.
Beat cream cheese.
Add eggs, 3/4 cup sugar and vanilla.
Soak the rice for 1 hour in warm water.
Cook the meat in water.
Remove the meat and add a little butter or margarine in the water.
Put this water left over with the rice.
Fry the eggplant and cauliflower with cooking oil.
In a large pot, put the meat in the bottom, then the eggplant and cauliflower, then the rice and water from meat on top.
Mix all ingredients and put into unbaked pie shell.
Bake at 450° for 10 minutes.
Reduce heat to 400° and bake 30 to 40 minutes.
Cook the first 7 ingredients 15 minutes.
Mix the kidney beans and pork and beans.
Mix all together.
Bake in uncovered dish 45 minutes at 325°.

i: 1 c. Quaker oats (Quick or old-fashioned), uncooked
d: Beat 1 cup sugar and margarine until fluffy.
Mix in egg whites.
Stir in combined flour, oats and baking soda.
On wax paper, press into 16 x 12-inch rectangle.
Spread combined pumpkin, 1/2 cup sugar and spice over dough to 1/2 inch from edge. From narrow end, roll up dough.
Sprinkle sesame seed around roll, pressing gently.

i: 1/2 hot chili pepper, seeded and diced
1 1/2 lb. kale, mustard or turnip greens, cut steams, wash and drain
d: In a large saucepan or Dutch oven, combine oil, red peppers, garlic and chili pepper.
Saute over medium heat for 2 or 3 minutes, stirring until peppers are tender.
Stir in chicken broth and kale, mustard or turnip greens.
Bring to a boil, then toss greens in pan and cover with liquid.
Reduce heat to medium and cook 25 minutes or until greens are tender.
Serves 4. Contains 0 cholesterol and 2 grams fat.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Melt butter and chocolate in saucepan over low heat.
Remove from heat.
Add sugar.
Add 1 unbeaten egg; mix with a fork.
Add other egg; mix again.
Run crackers through food chopper or food processor.
Chop pecans and cherries and add to crumbs.
Add raisins.
Melt oleo and chopped marshmallows in top of double boiler and add to crumb mixture.
Mix well.
Pack into loaf pan and set in refrigerator for at least 6 hours before slicing.
Cook sugars, syrup, cream and salt over high heat until mixture forms a soft ball (235°) when dropped in water.
Remove from heat. Add flavoring and pecans.
Beat until creamy.
Drop from spoon onto waxed paper.
Crumble beef into a 1 1/2-quart casserole dish.
Cover loosely and microwave for 3 minutes on High.
Break up and stir.
Cover and microwave until very little pink remains, 2 to 3 minutes longer; drain.
Stir in spaghetti sauce, tomato sauce, 1 tablespoon parsley and oregano.
Cover tightly and microwave on High to boiling, 3 to 4 minutes.
Heat oven to 375°.
Mix margarine, sugar, eggs and vanilla thoroughly.
Blend together flour, soda and salt; stir in.
Mix in peanuts.
Drop rounded teaspoonfuls of dough about 2 inches apart on lightly greased baking sheet.
Flatten with bottom of greased glass dipped in sugar.
Cook rotini.
Set aside to cool.
Mix together mayo, water, vinegar and sugar until smooth.
Add rest of spices, onion and celery to dressing.
Pour over rotini and mix thoroughly. Refrigerate overnight.
Stir salad two or three times during refrigeration.

i: 1 packages hot dogs-chopped into bite sized pieces
d: Heat mustard and jelly on stove slowly until melted. Add hot dogs. Or, place all ingredients in a crock-pot. Cook until all the juice is absorbed. May increase or decrease recipe. Just use the same ratio of jelly/mustard. If mustard is tart, you may need to add a little more jelly.

In a wide shallow bowl, beat 1st four ingredients. For each slice, melt 1 tablespoon of butter in skillet. Dip bread in egg mixture; turn and coat the other side. Cook slices in butter, browning on both sides. Serve with Sauce as follows.

i: 1 to 2 lb. browned pork sausage, drained
1 1/2 to 2 c. boiled white instant rice
d: Cook all ingredients at the same time individually.
In a casserole dish, layer sausage, rice and broccoli.
Top off with Velveeta cheese.
Microwave until cheese melts.
Dissolve jello in water and mix remaining ingredients.
Chill until ready to serve.
Mix beans and taco seasoning mix.
Spread in bottom of casserole dish.
Top with olives, salsa, cheese and sour cream. Serve with tortilla chips.

i: 1 lb. boned and skinned chicken breasts, cut into 1/2-inch strips
d: Microwave the potatoes 8 to 10 minutes until tender. While potatoes cook, in a large skillet, toss and brown chicken and garlic in oil over high heat 5 minutes. Add potatoes and rosemary. Saute and toss until potatoes are lightly brown.
Season with salt and pepper.

i: buy or make an angel food cake
d: Slice top off cake to make 2 layers.
Make jello using 3 cups water for both.
After it sets, add sliced strawberries.
Mix in 1 cup Cool Whip.
Top bottom cake with mixture, fill in hole.
Put top layer cake over bottom layer.
In a medium skillet, cook onion, celery and garlic in margarine until tender, but not brown.
Stir in ketchup, water, brown sugar, vinegar, chili powder, salt and pepper.
Simmer, uncovered, for 10 to 15 minutes.
Add pork; heat through.
Serve meat on buns with sauce.
Combine sugar and water in large saucepan; boil until sugar melts.
Remove from heat.
Add remaining ingredients, except ginger ale.
Pour into empty milk cartons and freeze.
When ready to use, thaw to mushy stage (a few hours in refrigerator), then place one or two cartons at a time in punch bowl and pour cold ginger ale over it.
(The mixtures in cartons will keep in freezer for several months.)
Brown sausage; drain thoroughly, blotting extra grease with paper towels.
Mix sausage with cheeses, eggs and oregano; save a couple tablespoons of egg to brush on top of roll.

i: 1 1/2 c. shredded American or Cheddar cheese
d: Split muffins and spread with mixture.
Quarter muffins and bake at 350° for 10 minutes.
Makes 32 quarters.

i: 1 (4 oz.) pkg. sliced corned beef, cut in bite size pieces
d: Spread sauerkraut in baking dish.
Top with sliced tomato, dressing and corned beef.
Sprinkle with cheese and caraway seed. Toss rye bread crumbs with butter and sprinkle over casserole. Bake, uncovered, at 375° for 20 to 25 minutes.
Saute onion and garlic in oil.
Add broths, tomato sauce, cumin, chili powder, jalapenos and Worcestershire. Bring to a boil, cover and simmer for 1 hour.
Add chicken and tomatoes. Cook 15 minutes. Cut corn tortillas in narrow strips.
Fry crisp. Place a few tortilla strips in the bottom of each bowl.
Fill with hot soup.
Top with more chips and shredded cheese.
Cut stems from mushrooms.
Finely chop enough stems to measure 1/3 cup.
Cook and stir chopped mushroom stems, onion and green pepper in 3 tablespoons margarine until tender, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat.
Stir in bread crumbs, salt, thyme, turmeric and pepper.
In large skillet, brown beef and onion; drain.
Stir in sugar, oregano, chili powder, beans, tomato sauce and corn.
Simmer 10 to 20 minutes.
Lay tortilla chips on top with cheese sprinkled on them.
Cover.
Simmer 2 to 3 minutes or until cheese is melted. Serve immediately.
Remove skin from chicken; place chicken in a 9 x 13-inch baking pan.
Pour sauce over chicken.
Place 1 slice of cheese on each thigh.
Bake in a 350° oven for 1 hour.
Serve on a bed of pasta.
Leave salmon whole, just clean and gill.
Wash thoroughly. Squeeze lemon over entire fish.
Season entire fish.
Wrap and seal in foil.
Cook on smoker (indirect heat) at 350° for 50 to 80 minutes (10 minutes per pound) or until flesh flakes with fork, or in oven.
When opening foil, beware of steam.

i: 2 tsp. minced fresh thyme or 3/4 tsp. dried thyme
d: Place asparagus on baking pan (with sides) lined with heavy-duty foil.
Drizzle with oil and toss to coat.
Sprinkle with thyme, salt and pepper.
Bake, uncovered at 425 degrees for 10 to 15 minutes or until crisp-tender.
Serves:

i: 6 chicken breasts or 8 to 9 skinned chicken thighs
d: Combine first 5 ingredients and set aside.
Dip chicken in salad dressing, then dredge in bread crumb mixture.
Place chicken, bone side down, in a 9 x 13 x 2-inch baking dish, coated with cooking spray.
Bake, uncovered, for 45 to 50 minutes (breasts), less for thighs, or until tender, at 350°.
Low calorie and tasty!
Easy to prepare.
Boil first 5 ingredients and pour while hot over last 3 ingredients.
Cool and store in refrigerator 2 or 3 days before serving.
Brown hamburger; drain fat off.
Add remaining ingredients. Use empty 16-ounce can to add 5 cans water to mixture.
Bring to boil.
Reduce heat and simmer 30 to 45 minutes.
Very filling soup.

i: 1 lb. small, unpeeled red potatoes, halved lengthwise and sliced (about 3 c.)
1 Tbsp. chopped unsalted dry roasted peanuts
d: Place potato slices in a medium saucepan.
Cover with water; bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
Cover; cook for 5 minutes. Add green beans; cook, uncovered, for 8 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Drain; set aside.
Combine curry powder and the next 4 ingredients in a large nonstick skillet; cook over low heat for 5 minutes.
Stir in yogurt and garlic; cook until mixture is warm.
Heat until jelly is melted.
Combine ingredients.
Serve with fresh vegetables (pepper, cucumber, celery, cauliflower, etc.)
Cut bacon into pieces and brown.
Add ground beef and cook until all red is gone.
Add noodles, onion, salt, pepper, chili sauce, peppers and tomatoes.
Be sure noodles are moistened.
Cook on high until steam appears.
Turn burner down and continue cooking 45 minutes.

i: 3 lb. buffalo or catfish or fish of your choice
d: Clean and dress
the
fish.
Cut
into

t: Pie Crust(I Can'T Believe It Works)
d: Mix flour, salt and shortening.
Add egg, vinegar and water, mixing with a fork until flour is moistened.
This is a crust that you can play with.
Saute carrots, celery and onion in oil until soft.
Add 6 cups chicken broth, tomatoes, string beans, bay leaf, oregano and basil; simmer for about 1 hour.
Add other beans and macaroni; let simmer until macaroni is soft.
If more broth is needed, add, so it won't be too thick.
Season to taste.
Top with grated Parmesan cheese when serving.

i: 1 can black pitted olives, cut twice across
d: Mix well.
Put in a 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan.
Grate or slice 1 small package of Velveeta cheese on top.
Cover with foil and bake in 350° oven for 1 hour.
Serves 10.
Can be frozen.
Mix well all ingredients.
Great with raw vegetables.
Cover cucumbers, onions and salt with ice; let soak 3 hours. Pour off water. Mix remaining ingredients. Add cucumbers and onions to vinegar mixture. Bring to a boil.
Boil 5 or 10 minutes. Fill hot jars and add 1 clove garlic, 1 sprig dill or 1 teaspoon dill seed. Seal jars and let stand for a few days.

t: New England Christmas Eve Seafood Casserole
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
Cook seafoods and peas.
Cut scallops in half, also shrimp if they are large.
Cook rice 30 minutes. Saute green pepper, onions and mushrooms.
Mix together mayonnaise, curry powder and worcestershire.
Combine with peas, seafood, salt and pepper.
Soak bread pieces in milk.
Combine all ingredients to mixed meat.
Form into loaf; place in greased loaf pan.
Bake at 350° for 1 3/4 hours.
Place dry beef in
bottom
of
dish.
Wrap bacon around chicken.
Spread soup over top of all.

Preheat oven to 325°. Spread 1 tablespoon each of olive oil on 2 cookie sheets, then put them into oven to preheat for 10 minutes. Slice the potatoes lengthwise, thin. Place slices on the hot cookie sheets. Don't overlap potatoes. Sprinkle with salt and pepper. Bake chips for 10 minutes; rotate baking sheets in oven and bake another 10 minutes. Flip each chip, then bake another 5 minutes or until golden brown. Remove chips to a paper towel to cool. These chips are great with any dip.

i: 1/3 c. buttermilk with little bit very hot water to make very stiff dough
d: Drop by tablespoonful into deep fat and turn when golden brown.
Saute beef, onion and garlic in butter.
Add curry powder, water and salt.
Simmer for about 30 minutes. Roll out and cut pastry to desired size (2 to 3-inches in diameter).

i: 1 lb. chopped meat, browned and drained
1 medium-large pkg. Doritos tortilla chips, crushed
d: Simmer browned chopped meat with kidney beans for 10 minutes and drain.
Allow to cool.
Mix remaining ingredients together. Add meat and beans.
Add dressing.
Mix first 3 ingredients and bring them to a boil.
Brown the 1/3 cup sugar; add to first mixture while cooking to soft ball stage.
Drop on oil paper.

i: 1 (8 oz.) jar taco sauce or salsa
d: Mix ingredients and chill.
Top with shredded Cheddar cheese, chopped tomatoes, green pepper, black olives and onions.
Serve with taco chips.
Cut corn from cob and scrape cob or grate corn.
Mix flour, salt and baking powder.
Add grated corn and bacon drippings.
Add enough milk to make a good batter.
Mix well and pour into a greased iron skillet (hot).
Bake at 425° until golden brown. Good with cow butter.

i: 2 medium tomatoes, seeded and coarsely chopped
d: Coat a nonstick skillet with cooking spray; add oil. Place over medium high heat until hot. Add watercress and arugula. Saute until wilted. Add tomatoes; cook until thoroughly heated. Sprinkle with lemon juice. Yields 4 servings (46 calories and 37% fat per serving).
Combine Cool Whip and milk.
Refrigerate 1 hour.
Add remaining ingredients and let set overnight.

i: 1 (6 oz.) can tomato paste (I use 2 cans)
1 (10 count) can biscuits (I use Hungry Jack)
d: Brown
and
drain beef; stir in tomato paste, onion and seasoning.
Mixture
will
be thick.

i: 1 jar Ragu low-fat tomato and herb spaghetti sauce
d: Arrange washed, dried chicken pieces in a large casserole dish.
Pour Ragu sauce over top, covering pieces well.
Sprinkle Parmesan on top.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour or until chicken is very well done.
Serve over hot, buttered spaghetti or fettucine.
Combine potatoes, milk and butter.
Beat with electric mixer at medium speed, rinsing beaters several times to remove strings from potatoes.
Mix all dry ingredients, add alternately to potato mixture.
Then add egg and mix thoroughly, add flavoring and beat. Prick two pie crusts with a fork.
Pour potato mixture into pie crusts.
Bake 45 minutes in a 350° oven.
Mix sugar, flour and cocoa.
Melt shortening and oleo.
Add water and boil.
Pour over dry ingredients.
Add buttermilk, soda, eggs, vanilla and salt.
Pour into greased 9 x 13-inch pan.
Combine chicken and soy sauce; let stand 15 minutes.
Heat oil over medium heat; add rice and cook until golden brown, stirring frequently.
Add chicken and broth; reduce heat and simmer, covered, for 20 to 25 minutes, or until rice is tender.
Remove cover; stir in onion, green pepper, celery and broccoli.
Cook until liquid is absorbed.
Serve immediately with extra soy sauce.
In covered Dutch oven or saucepan (4-quart) over medium heat, cook butter, carrots, onions, cabbage, parsley and salt for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add chicken broth, green beans and caraway seed.
Reduce to low heat, cover and simmer 15 minutes or until vegetables are fork-tender.

i: 1 lb. Mexican Velveeta, melt in microwave
d: Fry beef and drain.

Mix whole wheat flour with water into a firm texture.

Keep rinsing until all graininess is removed and water is clear from rinsing.

i: 1 c. or more to taste frozen loose-pack hash brown potatoes
d: Separate crescent dough into 8 triangles and place in an ungreased 12-inch pizza pan OR roll out in an oblong jelly roll pan or Pyrex dish. Press over bottom and up sides to form a crust and seal perforations. Brown sausage; drain fat and spoon over crust. Sprinkle with potatoes. In a bowl, mix together eggs, milk, salt and pepper and pour over potatoes. Top with Cheddar cheese. Bake in 300° to 325° oven for 25 to 30 minutes. Watch the crust to make sure it doesn't brown too quickly! Cut in squares.

i: 1 c. cracker crumbs or bread crumbs
commercial dark honey mustard or Ranch-style salad dressing
d: Peel onion, leaving root end intact.
Cut onion vertically into quarters, cutting to within 1/2 inch of root end.
Cut each quarter into thirds.
Melt the butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat.
Add the cream, garlic powder, and pepper and simmer for 10 - 12 minutes or until thick.
At the same time, bring 4 - 6 quarts of water to a boil and add the pasta.

i: 1/2 tsp. each: baking powder and ground cardamom
d: In a large mixing bowl, beat together shortening and sugar until well blended.
Beat in eggs.
Add pumpkin and water and stir until blended.

In another bowl, combine all-purpose flour, whole wheat flour, soda, salt, baking powder, cardamom, cloves and cinnamon; add to pumpkin mixture and stir just until well blended. Stir in raisins and chopped pecans.
Pour batter into 2 greased and flour-dusted pans, each about 5 x 9-inches (or in 3 pans each about 3 3/8 x 7 3/8-inches).
Bake in a 350° oven for about 1 hour and 15 minutes (or about 1 hour for the smaller pans), until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Soak cucumbers overnight in clear water; drain. Pack in jar with dill, pepper and garlic. Bring to a boil salt, turmeric powder, alum, vinegar and water. Pour over cucumbers and seal.
Mix cucumbers, peppers, onions and salt and let stand 30 minutes; drain.
Combine remaining ingredients and pour over cucumber mixture.
Bring to a boil over high heat.
Boil for 5 minutes.
Fill jars and seal.
Mix flour, 2 tablespoons salt, 1 tablespoon pepper and 2 tablespoons seasoned salt until well blended. Coat steaks with flour mixture. Brown lightly in hot oil.
Transfer to steam table pans.

t: Potatoes And Tomatoes With Fresh Coconut
i: 6 cloves garlic, peeled and finely chopped
1 1/4 lb. fresh tomatoes, peeled and diced into 1/2-inch pieces or 16 to 20 oz. tin Italian tomatoes
d: Peel potatoes, then dice into 3/4-inch pieces and put into bowl of cold water.
Heat oil in heavy 3-quart pot over medium-high flame.
When hot, add finely chopped garlic.
Stir about 5 seconds before adding red pepper and cumin seed.
Stir for another 3 seconds.
Garlic should brown lightly, red pepper should darken and cumin seed should sizzle.
Mix corn, tomatoes, corn meal, cheese, onion and green pepper. Let stand 30 minutes.
Add eggs, milk and seasoning.
Pour into buttered baking dish at 325° for 1 1/4 hours or until firm in the center.
This may be served from the dish or may be baked in a loaf and unmolded onto a platter.
Buttered peas may be used as a garnish with sections of hard-cooked eggs on the platter.
Mix boiling water and Jell-O and let cool until room temperature.
Then add milk and pudding.
Put in 9 x 13-inch pan. Refrigerate.
Cut into squares.
Cook rice; cut chicken into 1/2-inch strips and sprinkle with garlic salt and pepper.
Saute chicken and celery in margarine 4 to 5 minutes or until chicken is done.
Stir in spaghetti sauce and Italian seasoning.
Bring to a boil and then simmer 10 minutes.
Serve over rice.

i: 1 celery heart, ribs separated and trimmed
d: Soften the cream cheese to room temperature.
Blend together the white vinegar, light cream and curry powder.
Add the cream cheese and work the mixture into a smooth paste.
Stir in the chutney and refrigerate for several hours or overnight if possible.
About 1 hour before serving, stuff the mixture into the celery ribs and cut them into 1 inch pieces.
Wipe off bologna and
leave
skins
on.
Cut
into 4 or 5-inch lengths.

i: 3 c. or 1/2 lb. fresh bean sprouts
1/2 c. sliced canned mushrooms or 1 c. fresh, sliced
d: Marinate strips of pork and onion in soya sauce for 20 minutes. Dissolve cornstarch in water.
Heat oil until it begins to smoke in a wok or skillet.
Add pork and onion and stir-fry until pork is browned. Cover and cook 3 more minutes until thoroughly cooked.
Uncover and add bean sprouts and celery. Stir and cook 2 minutes. Add rest of ingredients and stir-fry thoroughly.
Cover and cook 3 more minutes.
Uncover and add cornstarch solution, stirring thoroughly, before adding to the wok.
Saute onion and garlic in pan sprayed with nonstick cooking spray plus 2 tablespoons stock.
Put remaining ingredients, except cornstarch mixture and broccoli flowerets, in a food processor fitted with the metal blade and puree.
Return to pan.
Add cornstarch mixture and cook over low heat until thickened, stirring constantly.
Add broccoli flowerets; heat and serve. Makes 5 servings.
Place roast in deep pot or casserole.
Mix sugar, ginger, soy sauce and soup.
Pour over meat.
Cut onions in thick slices. Place slices over meat.
Cover tightly and bake at 300° for 4 hours and 30 minutes.
Yields 6 servings.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Bake chicken in foil.
Salt and pepper to taste, dot with margarine and bake for 25 to 30 minutes.
Cut chicken in bite size pieces.
Pour water into skillet.
Heat on medium high.

i: 1 (16 oz.) can pear halves, drained and chopped
d: Combine all ingredients in a saucepan; bring to a boil over medium heat.
Reduce heat and simmer until thick.
Let cool and store in refrigerator.
Butter each ear of corn and sprinkle with curry powder.
Wrap each ear of corn in a sheet of aluminum foil.
Place on grill for approximately 10 minutes, or until cooked.

i: 1/2 tsp. chili powder or to taste
d: Spread out the nachos in a single layer on a baking sheet. Cook the onion with the meat until no pink remains, but do not brown.
Add tomato sauce and seasoning; combine well.
Place a spoonful of meat mixture on each chip and sprinkle grated cheese over all.
Place under preheated broiler for 2 to 3 minutes or until cheese has melted.
Sprinkle tomato and lettuce over all. Serve at once.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Grease and flour sheet cake pan (9 x 13-inch).
Sprinkle nuts and coconut in pan.
Spread to cover. Mix cake according to package directions.
Pour evenly over nuts and coconut, but do not stir.
Melt margarine and cream cheese in pan over low heat.

i: 1 small onion, chopped into tiny pieces
d: Mix well and chill.

t: Batter For Fried Shrimp Or Onion Rings
d: Sift dry ingredients.
Add water and oil; beat until smooth. Fold in egg white just before frying.
Coat fan-tail shrimp or onion rings and fry in deep fat until brown.
Wash the broccoli and cauliflower in cold water. Cut into bite sized pieces. Toss in olive oil. Sprinkle with garlic salt and lemon juice. Add in the olives.
Place beans in a large kettle; add water, cover and soak overnight.
Next day, add onion, green pepper, celery, ham, salt, cayenne and black pepper to beans in the soaking water.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to simmer.
Cover and cook 2 hours or until beans are tender.
Stir in onion tops.
Uncover and cook about 30 minutes longer.
Beat egg whites until forms a soft peak, add 1/2 sugar, beat. Then add other half until sugar is completely dissolved.
Grease pans, dip pecans in egg whites.
Place flat on baking sheet.
Bake in 350° oven until light brown.
Bring the milk to a boil; cool to lukewarm.
Dissolve the yeast with the warm water.
Mix and add 6 cups flour.
Let set for 30 minutes, then add the beaten eggs and egg yolks.
Add the remaining ingredients.
Let rise until double, then roll and cut. Let rise, then deep fat fry in vegetable oil.

i: 1 lb. venison steaks or tenderloin (1/2-inch thick)*
d: Mix the eggs and milk together in a shallow dish.
When blended, add the steaks to the mixture.
Cover; place in the fridge for 2 to 3 hours, turning over steaks occasionally.
Add salt and pepper to the flour to taste.
Remove steaks from the milk and egg mixture.
Roll in the flour; cook in oil over medium-high heat.
Let onions come to a boil.
Put into sterilized jars and seal.
Heat oven to 350°. Mix all ingredients. Roll into small balls. Place on baking sheet.
Bake for about 2 minutes.
Makes about 75 to 100 meat balls.

i: 1 box Uncle Ben's long grain wild rice
1 can artichoke hearts, drained and quartered
1/3 c. Paul Newman's oil and vinegar dressing
d: Cook rice.
Add rest of ingredients with rice.
Best when made 1 to 2 days in advance.
Optional:
Add chicken or shrimp.
Mix all ingredients except cereal, then stir it in.
Bake at 400° for 20 minutes.
This makes a big batch.
You can bake half and store the remainder in the refrigerator for a month and use as desired.
Use a one-gallon wide mouth jar.
Slice cucumbers and onions thinly and layer tightly in jar.
Mix remaining ingredients and pour over cucumbers.
Shake daily.
Store in refrigerator.
Ready to eat in 5 days.

i: 1 1/2 lb. top round or sirloin steak (boneless)
1 c. beef broth (bouillon, canned and undiluted)
1 c. green bell pepper, cored, seeded and cut into thin strips
d: Trim all fat from the meat and cut into pencil-thin strips. In a deep skillet or Dutch oven, heat oil, garlic and salt.
Add the meat and brown over high heat, about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally with a fork.
Add the beef broth.
Cover and simmer for 15 to 20 minutes or until the meat is fork-tender.
Stir in the green pepper strips, celery, onion and 1/2 cup of Coca-Cola. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes.
Do not overcook; the vegetables should be tender-crisp.
Combine first 8 ingredients.
Beat together eggs, oil and pumpkin.
Add to dry mixture.
Bake in a greased and floured 13 x 9 x 2-inch or 3 loaf pans at 350° for 45 minutes.
Fry bacon crisp in large skillet.
Pour off drippings.
Cover bottom of skillet with oil.
Saute pepper and onion just until tender.
Meanwhile, cook rice according to directions.
Pour rice over onion and pepper.
Fix Rice-A-Roni as directed on box.
Brown hamburger and onion with salt and pepper.
Add to rice mix.
Prepare 4 large bell peppers and arrange on casserole dish.
Stuff and bake at 375° for 45 minutes.
A meal in one with your favorite veggies.
In a large skillet or wok, heat the oil until quite hot.

Add the walnuts and saute for 1 minute. Add the broccoli and cook over medium heat, tossing frequently, until tender and crisp, about 4 minutes. Add the soy sauce and cook 1 minute more. Serve immediately, garnished with red bell pepper.
Mix all ingredients and bake at 350° for 12 to 15 minutes.

Cook beans. Cook chicken and cut up into small pieces. Save broth. Combine beans, chicken, chilies, garlic, onions, oil, chicken stock, cumin, oregano, salt, onion salt and pepper. Simmer until desired thickness.
Combine all ingredients.
Pour into greased skillets.
Bake at 425 degrees for 30 to 40 minutes.
Makes 2 (10 inch) skillets or large rectangular baking dish.
Mix all ingredients together.
Drop onto lightly greased cookie sheet by rounded teaspoonfuls.
Bake at 350° for 8 to 10 minutes.
Best when chewy or soft.

i: 3 to 4 lb. hen or 6 chicken breasts
2 Tbsp. flour, dissolved in cold water with 1 c. milk added (add this to broth)
d: Cook chicken until tender; remove all bones and skin.
In large pan, arrange layer or chicken and 1/2 cup margarine.
Roll dough and cut in strips.
Place over chicken.
Alternate this step until all chicken has been used, then pour chicken broth over this and roll top crust.
Place on pie and slit in center.
In a large skillet over medium-high heat, brown beef with onion and garlic; drain well.
Stir in remaining ingredients (including chili powder, hot sauce, etc., if desired) except sandwich buns. Bring to boil.
Reduce heat; cover and simmer 40 to 45 minutes or until lentils are tender, stirring occasionally and adding water if mixture becomes too dry.
Spoon 1/2 cup mixture onto each bun, toasted, if desired.
Yields 6 servings.
Cube steak and brown in skillet with garlic and pepper and a little oil.
Add soy sauce and water.
Cover and cook 20 minutes. Add sliced bell peppers and onions.
Cook 10 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon cornstarch and cold water to thicken.
Serve over rice.

i: 2 (10 oz.) cans tomatoes with green chiles, undrained
d: Drain tomatoes, reserving 1 cup juice.
Combine juice, water and next 5 ingredients in a large saucepan.
Bring to a boil; add vegetables and rice.
Cover, reduce heat and simmer 20 minutes or until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed.
Yield: 4 to 6 servings.
Dissolve Jell-O with boiling water.
Add rest of ingredients. Place in refrigerator to set.
Serve with Cool Whip.
Cream the cream cheese and mayonnaise and add rest of ingredients except pecans.
Chill 2 hours, then work into a ball and roll in chopped nuts.
Serve with Wheatsworth crackers or Sociables.

i: Boston butt or a beef roast
d: Oven Method:
Rinse roast and place on several layers of aluminum foil.
Pat on the mixed dry ingredients.
Wrap meat securely in foil.
Bake for at least 6 hours at 250° until meat falls apart.
Take out bone and fat, then let meat stand in juices overnight in foil.

i: any combination of the following: cauliflower, broccoli, carrots, green onions, green peppers, celery, zucchini and cherry tomatoes
d: Combine vegetables with mayonnaise and mustard.
Split pita bread; fill with vegetable mixture.
Add lettuce, if desired. Serves 4.
Dissolve jello in 2 cups hot cider.
Stir in raisins and cool. Add remaining cider and chill until slightly thickened.
Add rest of ingredients and chill until firm.
On each flour tortilla, place sour cream down center, chunks of chicken, salsa, onions, cheeses, peppers and more salsa (as desired).
Roll side of tortilla up and secure with a toothpick. Place burritos on a baking sheet and broil in oven for a few minutes, until burritos begin to brown and crisp.
Serve.
Good with Spanish rice.
Put meat, onions and potatoes in skillet with oil. Fry. Add salt, tomato puree, tomatoes, curry powder and garlic. Simmer 1 hour. Serve over rice.
Combine all ingredients except beans and bacon, mixing well until sugar is dissolved.
Gently stir in beans (with juice) and place in oblong baking dish.
Layer bacon across top.
Bake at 300° for 45 minutes.
Remove from oven; turn bacon slices and spoon off grease.
Return to oven and bake 45 minutes longer.

i: 1 pkg. (dry) Hidden Valley Ranch dressing mix
d: Mix and put in crock-pot.
Cook all day.
If cooked on range top, simmer 2 to 3 hours.

t: Cecil B. De Mille'S Baked Beans
d: Fry bacon until crisp. Drain off most of the fat.
Add onion to bacon and saute until onion is soft.
Drain butter beans and kidney beans. Combine all in large 3 quart casserole.
Bake at 350 degrees for 1 hour uncovered.
Serves about 10.

i: 1 large chopped onion, sauteed in 3 Tbsp. butter
d: Simmer broccoli, onions, carrots, celery and cauliflower in chicken broth until tender.
Mix flour and 1/2 cup milk.
Add 3 1/2 cups milk and 1/4 cup butter.
Stir until thick.
Add cheese, stir until melted.
Freezes well.

i: 1 bunch green onions, including tops, chopped
d: Cook bacon until crisp.
Drain and crumble.
Combine bacon with tomatoes, cucumber and onions.
Sprinkle with garlic salt, sweet basil and pepper.
