Mix well and chill.
Combine vinegar and salad oil; shake well.
Preheat oven to 375°.
Brown ground beef.
Mix with refried beans, oregano and cumin.
Fill uncooked manicotti shells. Arrange in a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Combine taco or picante sauce and water and pour over shells.
Bake 20 minutes.

i: 4 New York strip steaks (8 to 10 oz., 1 to 1 1/2 inches thick)
d: Place meat in large plastic bag.
Add all of the ingredients; seal bag.
Chill from 1 to 24 hours; turn bag occasionally.
Drain steaks.
Cook on low to medium flame on grill to desired perfection.
Brown chicken.
Mix other ingredients and pour over chicken. Simmer 1 hour at 350° in oven.
Last 15 minutes, add 1 green pepper and water chestnuts.

i: 6 oz. hot Italian sausage or spicy bulk
d: In large bowl, combine all ingredients; mix well.
Place in 4 1/2 x 8-inch loaf pan.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour; drain.
Soften gelatin in cold water and dissolve in boiling water. Add lemon juice, onion and seasonings.
While gelatin mixture is still liquid, stir in avocado.
Fold in mayonnaise.
Pour into 10-inch ring mold and chill until firm.
Unmold and fill center with chilled seafood or chicken salad.
Surround with cherry tomatoes.

i: 3 c. boned, cut up, cooked squirrel
1 (10 3/4 oz.) can condensed cream of chicken soup
d: Preheat oven to 375°.
Heat squirrel, milk, sour cream and soup to boiling.
Spoon into a rectangular 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish.
Beat remaining ingredients, except cheese, with a wire whisk or hand beater until almost smooth.
Pour evenly over hot squirrel mixture.
Sprinkle with cheese.
Melt chocolate chips and paraffin wax in double boiler.
Melt margarine in separate pan.
Mix graham cracker crumbs, powdered sugar, peanut butter and melted margarine.
Mix well.
Form into balls or logs with hands, then dip logs into chocolate chip mixture.
Lay logs on wax paper until dry.
Coarsely grind squash, onions and bell peppers; add salt.
Let set overnight.
Next morning, drain.
Wash in cold water, using a fine wire colander.
Squeeze out excess liquid.
Mix together the rest of the ingredients; bring to boil.

i: 1 (16 oz.) bag frozen broccoli, carrots, water chestnuts and red pepper combination
1 (2.8 oz.) can Durkee French fried onions
d: Place chicken breasts in an 8 x 12-inch baking dish.
Pour salad dressing over chicken.
Bake, uncovered, at 400° for 20 minutes.
Place rice, vegetables and 1/2 can Durkee French fried onions around and under chicken.
Combine chicken broth and Italian seasoning and pour the mixture over chicken and vegetables.
Bake, uncovered, 25 minutes.
Steam potatoes until tender.
Cool slightly and cut into large pieces.
Transfer to a large serving bowl.
Cook bacon very crisp and mince.
Pour off all but 2 tablespoons bacon grease and return bacon to pan with onion.
Cook about 1 minute, then stir in flour and sugar.
Drain and wash the chick peas.
Boil in little water till the chick pease are very soft.
Render suet until cream colored.
Remove suet, leaving the liquid.
Add meat; cook until lightly brown.
Add onion and salt; cook 20 minutes on low heat.
Add other ingredients and cook 1 hour or until meat is tender.
Add water if needed or tomato juice.
Mix sugar; add water and cook until thick.
Cool.
Beat egg whites. Add vanilla, salt and the 6 tablespoons sugar.
Add the thickened cornstarch and sugar mixture.
Beat until smooth.
Pour over any one-crust pie.
In a plastic/paper bag, combine flour, salt and pepper.

Shake to mix. Add chicken and shake to coat lightly. Remove chicken and reserve excess seasoned flour. In large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon olive oil over medium heat. Add chicken and brown on one side, about 5 minutes. Add other tablespoon oil; turn chicken and brown on second side.

Transfer chicken to a plate; set aside. Add margarine to skillet; melt and cook onions, stirring until softened, 2 to 3 minutes. Stir in remaining seasoned flour; cook, stirring, about 1 minute. Add chicken broth, 2 tablespoons lemon juice and thyme. Bring mixture to a boil, stirring constantly. Return chicken to skillet; reduce heat and cover. Cook chicken about 5 minutes. Remove chicken. Stir in remaining 1 tablespoon lemon juice into sauce. Stir and pour over chicken. Serve chicken with lemon wedges and a sprinkling of parsley.

i: dash of liquid red pepper seasoning
4 boneless, skinned chicken breast halves (about 1 lb.)
4 rectangular club rolls or small submarine rolls, split
d: Combine lime juice, garlic, cumin and red pepper seasoning in medium-size bowl.
Add chicken.
Refrigerate covered for 30 to 60 minutes.

i: red pepper, black or white pepper to taste
d: Saute onions in oil.
Add rest of ingredients and cook; simmer for about 1 hour.

i: 1/2 c. capers with a little juice
d: Mix above ingredients in bowl. Place 6 to 8 (depending upon size) boneless, skinless chicken breast halves in single layer in baking pan. Pour marinade over chicken. (Optional: Sprinkle 1 cup light brown sugar over chicken and then pour
1 cup white cooking wine over top.) Bake 40 to 50 minutes or until chicken is done in 350° oven.
Heat milk
slowly over low heat.
Add eggs, sugar, soda and salt.
Cook, stirring quite often.	Then add can Pet milk. Cool. Place
in
refrigerator.
Combine eggs, sugar, corn syrup, pumpkin and vanilla.
Mix well.
Stir in pecans.
Pour into pie shell.
Bake in preheated 375° oven for 55 to 60 minutes.
Cool and serve.
In mixing bowl, combine the flour, cornmeal, sugar, baking powder, chili powder and salt.
Chop scallion.
Beat milk, applesauce and egg until just combined and stir in scallion and corn.
Stir flour mixture into milk mixture until just combined but still lumpy.
Spoon into prepared muffin pans.
Bake until edges start to brown.
Heat oven to 350°.
Mix rice, 1/2 cup water, onion, salts, garlic powder and pepper.
Shape into balls.
Place in ungreased baking dish.
Mix remaining ingredients and pour over meatballs. Cover with foil and bake 45 minutes.
Uncover and bake 15 minutes more.
Blend all together with hands until well blended.
Roll into small balls.
Bake on ungreased cookie sheet at 350° for about 15 to 20 minutes or until done.
Place candy coating in microwave.
Melt according to directions. Stir melted coating in remaining ingredients.
Spread on wax paper to cool. Break into pieces and store in an airtight container.
Blend cream cheese and sour cream until most of the lumps are gone. Add spices and mix in well.
Now top mixture with grated Cheddar cheese, chopped tomatoes and sliced black olives. Chill for about 1 hour. Serve with tortilla chips.

i: 3 c. fresh Ricotta or cream-style cottage cheese
1/2 c. grated Parmesan or Romano cheese
d: Brown meat slowly. Spoon off excess fat.
Add next 5 ingredients and 1 cup water. Simmer, covered, 15 minutes.
Stir often. Cook noodles in boiling salted water until tender. Drain and rinse. Beat eggs. Add remaining ingredients, except Mozzarella.

Layer half the noodles in a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish. Spread with half the Ricotta filling. Add half the Mozzarella cheese and half the meat sauce. Repeat. Bake at 375° about 30 minutes. (Or assemble early and refrigerate; bake 45 minutes.) Let stand 10 minutes before serving.
Serves 8 to 10.

i: 3/4 c. firmly packed light brown sugar
d: Prepare brownie mix according to directions, using 1 egg method.
Put in pan.
Mix other ingredients and sprinkle over top of brownie mix.
Bake 25 to 30 minutes.
Brown ribs in hot fat.
Remove from pan.
Brown onion in fat. Add remaining ingredients.
Simmer about 30 minutes.
Pour on ribs.
Bake, uncovered, in moderate oven at 325° to 350° for 1 hour.
Cook all ingredients on low heat.
Stir constantly to prevent sticking until thickened.
Remove from stove.
Add lump of butter. Cool; ready for use.
Grease 1 1/2-quart baking dish.
Place toast, cut into quarters, in dish.
Sprinkle raisins over top.
Combine eggs, sugar, salt, milk, water and vanilla.
Pour over toast.
Let stand 10 minutes.
In skillet, fry sausage until done and crumbly.
In medium-size bowl, microwave Velveeta cheese until creamy.
Remove from microwave.
Add salsa, onions and sausage.
Stir well.
This will keep in refrigerator for 2 weeks.
Mix all together and boil.
Pour into hot canning jars, already cleaned and prepared.
Seal jars.
Mix well and keep in
a tight container.
Put 2 heaping teaspoons to a cup.
Finish filling with hot water (boiling).

i: 1 (4-inch) piece fresh peeled ginger, finely chopped
1/4 c. finely chopped fresh cilantro leaves
d: Place ginger, a little at a time, in a garlic press to extract juice (you should have about 1 1/2 teaspoons). In a blender container or food processor bowl, combine ginger juice, pineapple juice, orange juice, yogurt, lime juice and cilantro. Cover and blend or process until nearly smooth. Serve immediately. Makes about 6 servings.
Bring sugar, butter and milk to a boil and boil 1 minute. Remove from stove and add chocolate chips and vanilla and beat. (Might need about 1/2 cup powdered sugar.)
Put punch in freezer.
Put frozen slushy punch in punch bowl and add ginger ale or Sprite when ready to serve.

i: 1 (2 1/2 to 3 lb.) broiler-fryer chicken, cut up
4 medium carrots, peeled and cut on bias 1/2-inch thick
d: In a large skillet, brown chicken pieces on all sides, about 15 minutes.
Remove and drain all but 2 tablespoons fat from skillet.
Add rice, onion, salt and poultry seasoning.
Cook and stir until rice is lightly browned; add mushrooms, carrots and water.
Put chicken on top.
Sprinkle with paprika.
*Suggested Toppings: Pepperoni, broccoli, mushrooms and meat.

i: 1 to 1 1/2 lb. ground beef, browned and well drained
d: Mix all together and cook slowly until vegetables are tender.

i: 2 medium tart cooking apples, pared and sliced (about 2 c.)
d: Heat the oven to 350°.
Divide the apples between 2 (10 ounce) custard cups.
Sprinkle water over apple slices.
Mix flour, sugar, margarine and cinnamon with a fork until crumbly; sprinkle over apples.
Bake, uncovered, until the topping is light golden brown and apples are tender, 25 to 30 minutes.
Makes 2 servings.

Select a large casserole dish. Put a layer of the okra at the bottom, then a layer of tomatoes, season with salt and pepper and sprinkle some of the chopped onions and bell pepper over top. Make another layer, beginning with the okra, then tomatoes. Season again with salt and pepper and use up the remaining onion and bell pepper.
Cover top entirely with bacon, overlapping strips.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour or until brown and tender.
In a large bowl, thoroughly mix flour, yeast, sugar and salt. Add water and margarine; stir until well blended, about 1 minute. Add 1 1/2 cups flour; stir vigorously for 2 minutes. Add additional

1 1/2 cups to 2 cups flour to make a soft dough. Turn dough out onto lightly floured surface; knead until smooth and elastic, about 5 minutes. Spray dough with Pam; cover with bowl. Let rest 10 minutes. Punch down; divide into 2 pieces. Roll each piece to 11 x 7 rectangle. Roll up tightly from short end. Pinch seam to seal; place seam side down in loaf pan, sprayed with Pam. Cover; let rise in warm, draft-free place until doubled in size, 1 hour and 15 minutes. Bake
in 375° oven for 35 to 40 minutes. Cool on wire rack.
Combine all ingredients except coconut and mix well.
Chill 3 to 4 hours.
Shape into 1-inch balls and roll in grated coconut. Makes 2 dozen.

Dice chicken into small cubes. Chop onion and celery very fine. Saute all in olive oil until chicken is cooked. Transfer into a large pot - 5 qt. or larger. Add cooked rice and the remaining ingredients.
Simmer till rice has expanded.
Add salt and pepper to taste and more water as needed.
Left over soup will thicken.
Blend all ingredients.
Pour over steamed cauliflower and serve.
Good hot or cold.
Cream butter and sugar; add eggs, one at a time, beating thoroughly.
Add vanilla.
Add graham cracker crumbs and baking powder together and add alternately with milk to the first mixture. Mix well, then add crushed pineapple (undrained) coconut and pecans.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour in tube pan.
Dust with confectioners sugar.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Mix sugar and flour.
Add eggs and cooled margarine.
Mix well.
Stir in pecans, chocolate chips and vanilla.
Pour into unbaked pie shell.
Brown beef in skillet and drain off fat.
Put all ingredients in crock-pot (3 1/2 or 4-quart model).
Stir thoroughly.
Cover and cook on low 7 to 9 hours (high 3 hours).
Mix all ingredients and chill in refrigerator.
Put vegetables through food grinder.
Mix with the salt.
Let stand overnight.
Rinse with cup of water and drain well in colander.
Cream
shortening and peanut butter.
Gradually add 1/2 cup sugar
and	brown
sugar, beating until light and fluffy. Add egg,
milk	and
Cook, covered, for about 1 hour at 250° in a 2-quart casserole.
May add ripened bananas.
Serves 7 to 8.
Saute hamburg until blood disappears.
Add rest of ingredients and simmer 1 hour.
Brown hamburger, pepper and onion.
Add all other ingredients and simmer for 30 minutes.
Serve with cornbread or crackers. Parmesan cheese added on top of individual bowls is good also.

i: 1/4 c. rice vinegar or white wine vinegar
d: Combine first 3 ingredients in bottom of a large platter. Add fish, skin side up, to ginger mixture. Cover and marinate in fridge for 20 minutes. Remove fish from marinade; pat dry with paper towel to remove excess marinade.

i: 1 large jar taco salsa (hot or mild)
d: Cook sausage and drain.
Cook hamburger meat and drain.
In a crock-pot, put sausage on high.
Cut Velveeta and put on sausage. Mix well.
Then put hamburger meat and then put Mexican Velveeta, hot or mild; mix well.
Then add large jar taco salsa, hot or mild.

i: 1 (2 1/2 to 3 lb.) broiler-fryer chicken, cut up
d: Simmer chicken, garlic and 1/2 teaspoon of salt in water in large saucepan for 30 minutes.
Remove chicken; drain, reserving 2 cups stock.
Cool chicken to touch.
Remove skin and bones.
Cut chicken into bite size pieces. Cook rice in oil in 10-inch skillet over low heat, stirring occasionally, until golden brown.
Stir in reserved stock, chicken, picante sauce, cumin, pepper and remaining 1 teaspoon salt.
Combine cracker crumbs and 6 tablespoons melted butter, mixing well.
Press crumb mixture into bottoms of a 13 x 9-inch pan. Combine 1/2 cup butter, sugar, vanilla and eggs.
Beat until fluffy.
Spread sugar mixture over crumb layer.
Slice bananas lengthwise.
Arrange over sugar mixture.
Cut hard-boiled eggs in half and remove yolks.
Mix with mayonnaise, salt, pepper and crumbled bacon.
Put mixture back into eggs and serve.
Kids love them.
Serve for breakfast with toast.

i: 1 lb. lean, boneless pork tenderloins, thinly sliced
d: Combine olive oil and garlic in blender or food processor. Process, then add rosemary and whirl 1 minute more.

i: dash of ajinomoto and black pepper
d: Lightly coat spareribs with flour.
Fry with ginger and garlic.
In separate pot, combine the rest of the ingredients except for the pineapple.
Bring to a boil.
Add the ribs and simmer until tender.
Last 10 minutes of cooking, add the pineapple.

i: 1 envelope Lipton Recipe Secrets Fiesta Herb with Red Pepper soup mix
1 c. finely crushed plain tortilla chips (about 3 oz.)
1 1/2 lb. boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut into strips
d: Preheat oven to 400°.
In a medium bowl, combine Fiesta Herb with Red Pepper soup mix and tortilla chips.
In a large plastic bag or bowl, combine chicken and egg, beaten with water, coating well.
Dip chicken in tortilla mixture, coating well.
In a 15 1/2 x 10 1/2 x 1-inch jelly roll pan sprayed with no-stick cooking spray, arrange chicken and drizzle with margarine.
Bake, uncovered, for 12 minutes or until chicken is done.

i: 1 1/2 lb. lean pork loin, trimmed of fat and cut in thin strips
1 (20 oz.) can pineapple chunks or tidbits, with juice
d: Heat oil in skillet; brown meat; set aside.
Drain pineapple and combine the juice with the water, vinegar, brown sugar, cornstarch and soy sauce.
Mix well.
Cook sauce in the skillet until clear and slightly thickened.
Add meat and cook over low heat for about 55 minutes.
Add green pepper, onion slices and pineapple chunks and simmer about 5 more minutes.
Slice all squash, peel potatoes and onion.
Mix thoroughly in a bowl and cover with water.
In a Ziploc bag combine cornmeal and flour.
Add one handful of vegetable mixture to Ziploc bag at a time.
Shake to coat.
Pan fry until golden brown and potatoes are cooked.
Mix and roll into log, then roll in powdered sugar.
Keep cool.
Mix the first 4 ingredients together.
In a bowl, mix together the remaining ingredients and pour over salad.
Chill overnight.
Brush bottom of rectangular pan, 13 x 9 x 2-inch, with oil. Mix Parmesan cheese, Bisquick, seasoning, paprika and pepper; coat chicken.
Place, skin side down, in pan.
Bake 45 minutes; turn chicken.

i: 1 large jar spaghetti sauce with mushrooms
d: Cook noodles; drain.
Brown hamburger and Italian sausage together; drain.
Put together cottage cheese and Ricotta cheese in bowl.
Put spaghetti sauce in bowl and add meat mixture after browning.
Stir both cheese and meat mixtures good.

i: 1 green, red or yellow bell pepper, chopped
d: Brown chicken pieces in hot oil for 5 minutes; drain off fat. Add desired La Victoria salsa and water.
Reduce heat; cover and simmer for 30 minutes.
Uncover; add corn and bell pepper (add more water if necessary).
Cover and cook 10 minutes more.
Makes 4 servings.
Mix all ingredients together; store in an airtight spice container.
Two tablespoons equals 1 commercial package.
Mix with ground beef for tacos or burritos.

n: shredded reduced fat monterey jack cheese
i: 1/2 c. shredded reduced fat Monterey Jack cheese (2 oz.)
d: To make 1 quesadilla, spread 2 teaspoons Ricotta over tortilla.
Sprinkle with heaping tablespoonful Monterey Jack cheese and 2 teaspoons diced chilies.
Top with 1 tortilla. Repeat to make 2 more quesadillas.
Blend together first 8 ingredients in electric blender until liquid consistency.
(It is very important to use either a blender or a food processor in order to achieve a smooth, creamy dressing.)
Then add eggs, one at a time.
Beat well after each addition.
Add Mazola oil, a small amount at a time, blending after each addition.
Makes 1 quart.
Cook meats, onion and garlic until meat is browned and onion tender.
Stir in flour, tomato sauce, undrained tomatoes, chilies and chili powder.
Cook and stir until thickened.

t: Rice With Lamb Sauce(Riz Bi Yahkna)
d: In saucepan, brown meat and onion in oil.
Add tomato sauce and seasoning.
Let cook over med. heat till meat is cooked through and sauce thickens a bit. Meanwhile in another saucepan, melt butter and add rosemarino.
Stir constantly till golden brown.
Add rice and water to cover all.
Cover and slowly boil till water is absorbed and rice is cooked.
Do not peel tomatoes.
Chop/dice all ingredients.
Start with one serrano pepper and taste.
If your desire for the fire is greater, then add more peppers.
Enjoy with chips, vegetables, etc.
Cream the cream cheese; add the milk and beat smooth.
Add lemon juice, pineapple and nuts.
Beat well.
Pour into graham cracker crust and chill.
Slice in small servings (very rich) and top with dollop of Cool Whip.

t: Les Grives A La Broche(Spit Roasted Thrushes)
d: Six hours before cooking is to begin, pluck, singe, lard and truss the thrushes.
They should not be drawn. Put the birds into a deep dish just big enough to hold them in one layer.
Cover with the white wine, juniper berries, sliced onion, garlic cut in half, thyme and cayenne pepper.

Leave to marinate for 6 hours at room temperature, turning the birds every hour. Toast 6 slices of bread lightly and arrange in a dripping pan so they will catch the cooking juices of the thrushes. Dab the thrushes dry with the kitchen paper.
Smear with olive oil.
Put them on a skewer and grill for 10 to 15 minutes, ideally in front of a blaze of vine branches.
Mix all together and let sit for several hours.
Use as dip for vegetables.

i: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen chopped spinach, drained
d: In a large bowl, combine flour, salt and baking powder.
Stir in beaten eggs, milk and oleo.
Add remaining ingredients and stir until moistened.
Pour into a lightly greased 9 x 13-inch pan. Bake at 350° for 35 minutes.
Cut into small squares.
Can be made ahead, frozen and reheated.

i: 1/4 lb. chicken, ground beef, pork chop, beef or bacon strips
d: Put oil in a heavy pan or wok; add meat and vegetables to oil and cook until tender.
Add spices and eggs, then add rice and stir slowly on low heat for 5 to 10 minutes.
Serve hot.
Open milk and put in large bowl into freezer.
Dissolve jello in hot water.
Add marshmallows and dissolve.
Take off heat and add chopped cabbage, walnuts and pineapple.
Whip evaporated milk until thick, then fold into jello mixture.
Refrigerate.

i: 3/4 c. Hellmann's light or reduced-fat mayonnaise dressing
1 lb. boneless, skinless chicken breast, grilled and sliced
d: To make dressing, in a small bowl, combine first 6 ingredients. In a large bowl, toss vegetables with half of dressing.
Arrange on lettuce-lined platter.
Top with chicken.
Serve with remaining dressing.
Makes 4 servings.
Mix in order given.
Place in 9-inch unbaked pie shell.
Bake at 425° for 15 minutes, then at 350° for 45 minutes.
Done when knife inserted comes out clean.
Beat together egg, oil and applesauce.
Add flour, baking soda, baking powder and spices; beat well.
Stir in raisins.
Put batter in oiled, floured muffin pans.
Bake 20 to 25 minutes in 375° oven.
Good topped with cream cheese.

i: oregano, basil and garlic to taste
d: Cook sauce over low heat for 1 hour.
Drain pineapple.
Cube banana.
Blend in blender 45 to 60 seconds or until smooth.

i: 1 small can water chestnuts, sliced and drained
d: Mix all ingredients together except dressing mix and margarine.
Pour into casserole dish.
Sprinkle desired dressing mix over top of casserole.
Melt margarine and dribble over top of casserole.
Bake at 350° uncovered for about 40 to 45 minutes.
Mix all ingredients together and refrigerate.
Sift flour, baking powder, salt and sugar.
Add the next 3 ingredients and mix only until dry ingredients are dampened.
Fold in berries.
Spoon into greased or lined muffin pans, filling 2/3 full.
Sprinkle with sugar.
Bake at 400° for 20 to 25 minutes. These are Stephanie's favorite.

i: 1 frying chicken (2 1/2 to 3 lb.), cut up
d: Blend milk and egg.
Combine flour and seasoning in plastic bag.
Shake chicken in seasoned flour.
Dip chicken pieces in milk-egg mixture.
Shake chicken a second time in seasoning mixture to coat thoroughly and evenly.
Brown meat in a large saucepan and drain.
Add next 4 ingredients.
Bring to boil and simmer for 10 minutes.
In a small bowl, mix Ricotta cheese or cottage cheese, egg and Parmesan cheese.
Put a little of the meat sauce in bottom of 13 x 9 baking dish and alternate layers as follows:
noodles, 1/3 meat sauce, half of cheese mixture.
Blend salad oil with vinegar in saucepan.
Add rest of the ingredients.
Bring to a boil and simmer 5 minutes.
Cut chicken into two 2 1/4 inch x 1 inch x 3/4 inch strips. Flatten chicken breast to 1/4 inch thickness.
Place a strip of cheese down center of each breast.
Fold chicken over cheese and secure with toothpicks.
Rub mexican seasoning over chicken.
In a large skillet, brown chicken in oil on both sides.
Transfer to a greased 8 inch square baking dish.

i: 1 lb. fresh or frozen peeled shrimp
d: Thaw shrimp, if frozen.

i: 1 c. chopped nuts or 1 c. raisins (optional)
d: Sift dry ingredients.
Mix eggs, oil, water, etc.
Bake in loaf pan for about 1 hour at 350°.
Can use cupcake tins.
Combine all the ingredients in a blender and blend until smooth. Chill or serve immediately.

i: 1/2 to 3/4 c. stale whole wheat bread crumbs (about 1 slice, whirred in a blender or food processor until fine)*
1/2 large or 1 small stalk celery, diced fine
8 to 10 large button mushrooms, centers and stems removed with a spoon
black pepper and hot red pepper flakes to taste
3 Tbsp. canned white kidney beans or chickpeas
d: *Use light variety bread.

Cube cheese and put in a saucepan. Over medium - low heat, add just enough milk to melt the cheese to the desired consistency, stirring constantly so cheese doesn't burn. Blend in finely chopped jalapenos to taste. Leftovers may be refrigerated and reheated on the stovetop by adding a little milk and stirring over medium - low heat.
Place sugar in a bowl.
Add oil and eggs; beat well.
Sift flour, baking powder, cinnamon, soda and salt.
Add flour mixture to egg mixture and blend.
Add pecans and apples.
Pour batter into greased and floured 9 x 13 x 2-inch pan.

i: 2 qt. chopped cucumbers (about 4 medium to large)
2 c. chopped sweet red peppers (about 4 medium)
2 c. chopped sweet green peppers (about 4 medium)
d: Combine cucumbers, peppers and onion.
Sprinkle with turmeric. Dissolve salt in 2 quarts cold water and pour over vegetables.
Let stand 3 to 4 hours.
Drain.
Cover vegetables with cold water and let stand 1 hour.
Drain thoroughly.

i: 2 c. Ricotta or cream-style cottage cheese
d: In a large skillet, cook sausage, onion, celery and carrot until meat is lightly browned.
Drain off excess fat.
Stir in tomatoes, paste, sugar, oregano, 1 teaspoon salt and 1/4 teaspoon pepper; simmer, uncovered, for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.

Make syrup and bring to a hard boil. Add 8 finely chopped apples. Roll dough as for pie crust. Cut into squares. Add a small amount of syrup in bottom of a pan. Peel and core apples. Wrap each apple in a square of dough.
Place in pan.
Pour half of the syrup over dumplings.
Make sure that the top of the dough is secured around apple.
Bake at 350° for 60 minutes or until done. Ten minutes before removing from oven, pour remaining syrup on top of dumplings.
Cut beef into small chunks.
Heat a deep pan with olive oil and add onion, stirring frequently, until brown.
Add paprika, 1/4 cup water and beef.
Stir constantly until water evaporates.
Add remaining ingredients, except beef broth.
Mix well.

i: 1 lb. boneless pork loin cutlets, pounded to 1/4-inch thickness
d: Dredge cutlets in flour, shaking off excess.
Heat 1 tablespoon butter in a heavy skillet over medium heat.
Add cutlets and brown on both sides about 5 or 6 minutes.
Stir together remaining 2 tablespoons of butter, honey and pecans.
Add to skillet and stir gently.
Cover and simmer gently for 7 to 8 minutes.
Cut cabbage in 4 pieces.
Wrap 1 bacon strip around each quarter or a pat of butter.
Season and wrap in foil.
Place on grill about 10 minutes.
Turn over and cook about 10 minutes more.
Melt together margarine and cocoa.
Cool.
Gradually add beaten eggs.
Sift flour, sugar and salt and stir into batter.
Add vanilla and pecans.
Bake at 375° for 25 minutes in 14-inch jelly roll pan.
Mix together in saucepan everything except egg yolks.
Beat egg yolks and add to mixture.
Cook over medium heat until thick. Pour in baked pie shells.
Two-pie recipe.

i: 12 pods of okra (more or less; do not use too small or too large pods)
d: Cut okra into 1/4-inch slices.
Beat egg with the milk.
Bring cooking oil to hot temperature as for French fries.
Sift flour in pan.
Dip 5 or 6 slices in milk mixture. Drop in flour, roll, then put in hot oil. Work fast and drop in oil at once. Fry until light brown. Drain on paper towel.
Salt and serve warm.

i: 1 lb. 15 oz. can pork and beans
d: Brown bacon and onion but don't cook until crisp.
Add other ingredients, mixing well.
Bake at 375° for 45 minutes.
To prepare barbecue sauce, mix all ingredients except chicken together in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Boil for 5 minutes. Put chicken in a foil-lined pan and pour barbecue sauce over chicken evenly.
Cover with foil and bake at 350° for 1 1/2 hours.

i: 1 lb. or more ground beef, browned
d: Mix and bake in covered dish 1 1/2 hours at 375°.
Add water to keep moist while baking.
Mix dry yeast with water.
Mix all ingredients together until soft.
Store in icebox until ready to use.
Dissolve yeast in warm water.
Combine milk, sugar, salt, shortening and cool to lukewarm.
Add 1 cup of each flour and beat well.
Add yeast mixture and eggs, stir in remaining flour 1 cup at a time until you can make a soft dough.
Place in greased bowl, turning once to grease surface, cover, place in refrigerator.

i: 2 1/2 lb. lean beef, cut into bite size pieces (if bone is removed, cook with meat and throw away later)
1 (6 oz.) can tomato paste or 6 medium size tomatoes
1 large bunch celery (leaves and tops only), chopped
1/2 green and 1/2 red bell pepper, cube chopped medium fine
d: In a large Dutch oven, lightly brown the meat in oil and garlic at medium-low heat.

Slowly mix in water and tomato paste. Add onions, celery and bell peppers. Simmer, covered, 3 to 5 hours until meat is very tender. Check often and add water as needed, making certain there is about a quart of thin sauce when ready to add the pasta.
Add salt, pepper and homemade pasta.
Simmer, uncovered, 20 minutes to 1/2 hour, stirring often, until broth is absorbed.
Stir in parsley. Serves 6.

i: 1 1/2 c. (6 oz.) shredded sharp natural Cheddar cheese
1 (12 oz.) can whole kernel corn, drained
2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen broccoli spears, cooked and drained
d: Melt 2 tablespoons margarine; stir in flour and salt.
Add milk gradually, mixing until smooth.
Cook until mixture boils, stirring constantly.
Add cheese, stirring until melted.
Mix in 1/4 cup corn flakes and corn.
Arrange broccoli in a 7 1/2 x 11 3/4-inch baking dish.

i: 1 large potato, peeled and thinly sliced
d: Combine veggies and spoon into 4-quart casserole.
In small saucepan, combine broth and herbs.
Pour over vegetables.
Cover and bake 40 minutes at 350°.
Combine all chopped ingredients with olive oil and tomato sauce.
Let stand overnight in refrigerator.
Put 2 teaspoons of mixture on a split mini bagel, then broil until bubbly.

i: 1/2 c. finely crushed saltine cracker crumbs (14 crackers)
2 oz. process Swiss cheese, shredded (1/2 c.)
d: Combine the first 6 ingredients.
Add beef; mix well.
Pat mixture into a 10 x 8-inch rectangle on waxed paper.
Combine cooked rice, cheese and green pepper.
Pat onto meat, leaving 1 inch margin around edge.
Roll jelly roll fashion, beginning with narrow side.

i: 1 Tbsp. yeast, dissolved in 1/4 c. warm water
d: Mix milk, shortening, sugar and salt; add yeast dissolved in water.
Add flour.
Let rise in dish 15 minutes.
Roll out on floured board about 1/2 inch thick.
Cut with cookie cutter.
Dip in melted butter and fold in half.

i: 1 (16 oz.) can crushed pineapple, drained (save juice)
2 small cans mandarin oranges, drained (save juice)
d: In a pot, cook sugar, eggs, flour and salt; mix well.
Add fruit juices.
Bring to a boil.
Cool well.
Cook and strain acini de pepe.
Stir macaroni into cooked custard juices.

In casserole dish, layer 2 cups peas, tomatoes, onion, bell peppers and salt and pepper to taste. REPEAT LAYERS. Sprinkle Parmesan cheese on top. Place 6 slices of bacon on top of cheese and bake until bacon is done and onions are tender. You may need to place under broiler to get bacon crispy.
Peel and cut potatoes lengthwise in quarters.
Place in roaster with onion, celery, parsley and bouillon, mixing well. Add paprika, salt and pepper, again mixing well to taste.
Add oil.
Roast at 350°, turning several times until golden brown.
Mix cream cheese and sugar. Add Cool Whip and pour into crusts. Topping of your choice: cherry pie filling, blueberry filling, peach, etc.

i: 1 c. lemon juice from fresh squeezed lemons
d: To make 2 quarts, dissolve sugar in hot water.
Add lemon juice and remaining water.
Add lemon peel if desired.
Serve over ice.
Mix together.
Pat out.
Place 2 to 3 slices of cheese.
Roll up.
Place in pan.
Baste with ketchup and brown sugar.

i: 1 pkg. creamy Italian style salad mix
d: Mix the oil and the creamy Italian style salad mix.
Pour over the crackers and stir several times.
If you put the crackers and mixture in a Tupperware bowl with lid, you can just turn the bowl upside down every so often to mix.

Mix all well in medium bowl and cut one 16 ounce loaf of Italian bread into 1-inch slices. Dip bread into mixture and put into large glass baking dish. Pour any remaining mixture over bread. Refrigerate overnight.

i: 1 (10 3/4 oz.) can each: 97% fat free cream of mushroom and cream of chicken soup
1 (8 to 10 oz.) can green chile salsa
1 lb. fat free Cheddar cheese, grated
d: Wrap chicken breasts in foil and bake 1 hour at 400°.
Cool and bone chicken.
Break into large pieces.
Cut tortillas into 1-inch strips or squares.
Mix soups, milk, onion and salsa.

i: 1 c. sliced onion, separated into rings *
1/4 tsp. dried oregano (or more according to your taste)
d: Combine first 6 ingredients in a large skillet; cover and cook over medium heat for 7 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add tomato and oregano.
Cook, uncovered, 2 minutes or until tomato is heated thoroughly.
Mix all together except Parmesan cheese.
Put in small, shallow pan.
Sprinkle
cheese
on	top.
Bake
Rinse brisket and place on a large pan with the fatty side up. Cover top generously with minced onions.
Sprinkle 2 ounces of liquid smoke.
Cover pan with aluminum foil and seal tightly. Cook at 250° for 8 to 10 hours.

i: 1 can pinto beans, rinsed and drained
d: Saut celery, onion and garlic in vegetable oil until tender; add the taco seasoning and 1 cup water. Stir until mixed well. Add tomatoes and all beans with juice except pinto beans (rinse and add). Bring to a boil and simmer on low for 20 minutes. Remove from heat and allow to cool.

i: 1 (1 lb. 12 oz.) can Italian style peeled tomatoes (do not drain)
1/4 c. chopped, pitted, salt-cured, black olives
1 tsp. crushed dried red pepper or to taste
pinch of coarse ground black pepper
1 (2 oz.) can anchovies, drained, blotted dry and cut into small pieces
d: Heat oil in a large skillet.
Add garlic and saute, over low heat, for 1 minute (do not brown).
Stir in tomatoes with juice, olives, capers, red pepper, oregano and black pepper.
Cook, over medium heat, stirring to break up tomatoes, until sauce thickens, about 15 minutes.
Stir in anchovies and parsley; simmer 2 minutes.
Add salt to taste.
Dissolve Jell-O in hot water.
Chill.
Add remaining ingredients and let set.
Combine onion, garlic, celery, carrots, potatoes and chicken broth in a large Dutch oven; bring to a boil.
Cover, reduce heat and simmer 15 to 20 minutes or until potatoes are tender.
Stir in corn; remove from heat.
Melt butter in a heavy saucepan over low heat; add flour, stirring until smooth.
Cook 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Gradually add milk; cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until thickened and bubbly.
Cook noodles per package directions and drain.
In a saucepan, melt butter.
Blend in flour, onion, garlic powder and 1/8 teaspoon pepper.
Add chicken broth and milk.
Cook and stir until bubbly. Stir in 1/2 cup grated cheese and mushrooms.
In a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish layer half the noodles, all the asparagus, chicken, Mozzarella and 1/3 of the milk mixture.
Spread bean dip in a 9x13 inch dish.
Mash avocados with lemon juice in bowl.
Stir taco seasoning into sour cream in a small bowl.
Layer avocado mixture and sour cream mixture over bean dip.
Top with cheese and tomatoes.
Sprinkle with green onions and olives.
Stir until smooth paste.
Slowly add sugar and salt.
Boil mixture for 3 minutes.
Remove from heat.
Add vanilla.
Pour mixture in jars and seal.
Heat olive oil in pan.
Add shrimp and spices.
Saute for 20 to 30 minutes.
Add wine and simmer for 10 to 15 minutes.
Serve hot with French bread.

i: 2 1/2 c. chopped apples (leave some skin for color)
1 1/2 c. seedless grapes, cut in half
1/2 c. or more chopped walnut or pecans
d: Place the eggs, pineapple juice, sugar and salt in a saucepan and blend and cook until it boils.
Cool.
Peel, pit and mash avocados in medium size bowl, with lemon juice, salt and pepper.
Combine sour cream, mayonnaise and taco seasoning mix in another bowl.

i: green food coloring (I add a little)
d: Mix Jell-O, hot water and Pet milk until Jell-O is dissolved. Add other ingredients and put in bowl or pan. Let set. Mix after it has partially set.
Combine all ingredients, except catsup and corn syrup.
Shape mixture into a loaf in baking dish.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

i: 3 Tbsp. red bell pepper, finely chopped
3 Tbsp. green bell pepper, finely chopped
6 Tbsp. green onions, finely chopped (including 2 inches of green tops)
1 1/2 c. jalapeno Monterey Jack cheese, grated
d: On the day before:
Drain yogurt through cheesecloth and refrigerate.
Chop peppers and onions and refrigerate.
Grate cheese and refrigerate.
Use 9 x 13-inch casserole.
Spray with Pam.
Put all ingredients in a jar.
No cooking.
Keep in refrigerator.
Pour all ingredients in blender (or mixer and bowl) and blend. Done.
Pour into container and serve.
Sift flour with baking powder, soda and salt.
Add buttermilk and mix lightly with a fork.
Turn out on a floured board or pastry cloth.
Knead lightly 4 or 5 times.
Roll or pat out to 1/4-inch thick; cut into rectangles 1 x 3-inch with a floured knife.
Drop into boiling stew.

i: 1 c. tomatoes or 5 medium fresh tomatoes
d: Wash pork chops in vinegar.
Season and let sit for awhile. Chop celery, tomatoes, onions and green pepper.
Saute in a small gram of oil; add tomatoes.
Cook about 20 minutes.
Flour and fry chops in a small amount of oil, let drain.
Place in a casserole dish and pour sauce over pork chops.
Sprinkle salt on vegetables.
Cover with water and let stand for 2 hours; drain.
Heat sugar, vinegar, mustard seed, prepared mustard and turmeric to a boil.
Pour vegetables in and bring to boil second time.
Pack in jars and seal.
Makes approximately 5 pints.
Sprinkle ribs with garlic salt (optional).
Mix ingredients, coat
ribs on both sides and put in glass pan to marinate overnight.
Fill
the bottom of a broiler pan with water, cover pan rack
with
Chop tomatoes and cucumbers medium fine.
Prepare tahini by mixing it with 2 tablespoons warm water.
Add lemon juice and mix tomatoes with other ingredients.
Garnish with olive oil.
Chopped onions and dried mint are optional.
Mix the ingredients and add to potato salad or macaroni salad.
Cut lemon in half and rub lemon pulp on pork chops.
Rinse lemon pulp off and dry pork chops with paper towel.
Heat olive oil in saucepan.
Season chops with salt, pepper, sage and garlic. Place chops in pan and sear for 3 minutes on each side.
Add chopped onions and peppers and let chops cook for 2 additional minutes on each side.
Add remaining ingredients and let simmer for 15 minutes.
Brown ground beef.
Add remaining ingredients, except cabbage and continue cooking over medium heat for 10 to 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add cabbage.
Cover; reduce heat and simmer until cabbage is done.

i: 1 pkg. Hidden Valley Ranch buttermilk original mix
d: Combine Hidden Valley mix and oil; add dill, garlic and lemon pepper.
Pour over crackers and stir to coat.
Bake on cookie sheets for 15 to 20 minutes at 250°.
Store covered.

i: 3/4 c. Italian seasoned dry bread crumbs
d: Preheat oven to
Dip chicken into eggs then into bread crumbs and arrange in a 9
by 13 inch baking dish.
Bake uncovered for 20 minutes. Take out and pour pasta sauce over chicken and top with cheese.
Bake 10 minutes or until chicken is no longer pink.
Remove core from the apples and take a little slice off the bottom to keep the apples from falling over.
Mix up some raisins and brown sugar.
Add some red hots or raisins if you want.
Stuff inside
the
apples.
Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
In shaker jar, combine lemonade concentrate, soy sauce and seasonings.
Shake ingredients vigorously to blend.
Pour half of marinade into shallow pan and add chops in single layer.
Pour remaining marinade over steaks; cover pan and place in refrigerator approximately 24 hours.
Meat should be turned once or twice during refrigeration.
Remove from refrigerator three or four hours before grilling.
To make a grilled cheese sandwich properly you must first decide what kind of bread to use, white, wheat, rye or raisin..raisin?
Then consider the kind of cheese you have available, American, Swiss, Cheddar, Limburger..Limburger?
You will also need butter, or oleo, or lard.. lard?
A kitchen countertop will be useful, as well as a knife, spatula, an iron skillet with a lid or cover, a stove and a helper to clean up the mess when you are through.

i: 2 pkg. chopped broccoli, thawed but do not cook
d: Cook noodles and drain.
Add all ingredients together.
Bake at 375° for 40 to 50 minutes, uncovered, in a large 9 x 13-inch pan.
It can serve 10 to 12 people, depending on appetites.

i: 1/2 bunch mint, chopped fine or 2 Tbsp. dried mint
1 large cucumber, peeled and chopped fine
d: Wash burghul.
Drain and place in a large bowl.
Squeeze out moisture by cupping your hands and squeezing.
Add all chopped vegetables and mix well.
Add oil, lemon juice, salt and pepper. (It's a long process, but chop the vegetables as fine as possible for the more true Tabbouleh.)
Allow burghul to soak and absorb the juice (about 1 hour).
Mix together and pour into a greased 9 x 13-inch pan.
Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.

i: 1 (16 oz.) can whole berry cranberry sauce
d: Mix cream cheese, margarine and sugar.
Add fruit and walnuts. Whip cream and fold into fruit and cream cheese mixture.
Pour into round mold.
Freeze 6 hours.
Remove from mold shortly before serving.
Makes 12 servings.

i: 3 (3 oz.) boxes Jell-O (cherry, strawberry or raspberry)
d: Mix sugar and rhubarb.
Let stand until liquid forms, then cook 10 minutes after starting to boil.
While still on heat, add Jell-O and stir for 5 minutes until dissolved.
Pour into hot sterilized jars and seal.
Makes 2 pints and 6 quilted jelly jars. Can be used on ice cream and many others.
Combine apple juice, pineapple juice and lemonade.
Sweeten to taste.
Add chilled ginger ale just before serving.
Preheat oven to 425°.
Grease and sprinkle cornmeal in an 8-inch baking pan or skillet.
Combine all ingredients in bowl, mixing well. Pour into pan or skillet and bake 25 to 30 minutes until golden brown.
Combine oats and buttermilk and set aside.
Cream shortening and sugar until light and fluffy.
Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
Stir in bananas and oat mixture.
Combine flour, baking powder, baking soda and salt. Stir into batter.
Spoon batter into a greased 9 x 5 x 3-inch loaf pan.
Brush 6 tortillas with water and place on an ungreased baking sheet.
Spread 2/3 cup of cheese over each tortilla.
Sprinkle with some diced olives and chilies.
Top each with another tortilla.
Brush tortilla lightly with water.
Cover baking sheets with foil.

i: 4 lb. chicken wings, tips cut off and split at joint
d: Prepare chicken wings.
Combine remaining ingredients.
Pour over chicken wings; marinate at least 4 hours.
Line baking pan with foil.
Add chicken and marinade.
Bake at 250° for 3 1/2 hours.

t: Coconut Shrimp With Red Curry Sauce(Yield: About 20 Shrimp)
i: kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
1 1/2 lb. jumbo shrimp, peeled (with tails on)
Red Curry Sauce (recipe follows; great even without Red Curry Sauce)
1 green onion (white and green part), chopped
d: Heat 3-inches of oil in a large deep skillet or heavy pot to 325°.
Place shredded cabbage in covered dish in refrigerator for several hours. Mix ingredients in order given 30 minutes before serving. Chill and serve.

i: 1 lb. fusilli pasta, broken into three's
d: Put small amount of oil in a large frypan.
Cook green pepper and onion until tender.
Remove from pan and put on the side.
Cook chopped meat, then drain all fat.
Return chopped meat, pepper and onion to frypan.
Mix the 2 cans of tomato soup together.
Bake cabbage for 1 hour.
Remove the charred outer leaves. Cut the cabbage into 1-inch wedges with the core attached. Simmer the water, vinegar and spices for 5 minutes.
Pour over the cabbage in a jar and seal.
Keep refrigerated.

i: 2 pkg. frozen broccoli, prepared according to pkg. directions, then drained well
1 c. cooked bacon, ham or turkey (optional)
d: Beat eggs well, then add broccoli.
Add milk, then fold in mayonnaise.
Add minced onion.
Fold in the cheeses; mix all ingredients well.
(If any of the optional ingredients are used, this recipe will make at least 2 quiches.)
Pour ingredients into unbaked pie shell.
Sift dry ingredients together.
Dissolve soda in buttermilk. Cream butter, sugar, jam and eggs with mixer.
Add dry ingredients alternately with liquid.
Bake in 3 greased and floured 9-inch pans at 350°.
Put frosting between layers and on outside of cake.
Put chicken in pot and generously season with curry powder. Let sit several hours or overnight.
When ready to cook, add some water to the pot (enough to cover the chicken).
Toss in some chopped onion and celery to suit your taste and add seasoned salt and pepper.
Boil until chicken falls off the bones.
When chicken is done, scoop out all meat, bones and pieces and pick out the good meat from the bones and "oocky" parts.
While you are picking through the chicken, boil some chopped potatoes in the pot and when the potatoes are tender, add the chicken meat back into the pot.
Mix the first 8 ingredients.
Sprinkle cinnamon on top.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Serves 8.
Great with ham.

i: 2 large cucumbers, peeled, seeded and chopped
d: Chop everything very fine and mix with the mayonnaise.
Cook meat; cool and drain off fat.
Combine all other ingredients; add meat back to mixture and simmer for 45 minutes.

i: 12 oz. jar salsa (mild to hot as you prefer)
d: Cut the Velveeta into 5 blocks.
Place in crock-pot on high. Add jar of salsa.
Stir frequently until all cheese is melted. Reduce heat to low and continue to stir occasionally.
Serve with nacho chips.

i: 1 small jar chipped beef, cut up
d: Place chipped beef on bottom of baking dish.
Place chicken on top of beef.
Mix soup and cream together; pour over chicken. Bake, uncovered, at 275° for 3 hours.
Cut cucumbers in chunks or slice.
Soak in salt water 5 hours or overnight 7
tablespoons
salt to 2 1/2 quarts water. Covers about same
amount
as
Mix well.
Brush on uncooked meat and let stand for 1 hour. Barbecue and keep moist.

i: 1 bottle Orville Redenbacher's buttery flavored popcorn oil
d: Mix together popcorn oil and dressing mix; set aside.
Put cereal, nuts, pretzels and crackers in big bowl and mix together. Put oil mixture on to coat and let set for 6 hours tossing ingredients together every couple of hours.

i: 2 large (8 oz. each) baking potatoes, scrubbed and sliced thin (4 c.)
2 medium size onions, halved lengthwise and cut in thin wedges (3 c.)
1 medium size red bell pepper, cored, seeded and cut in thin strips
d: Adjust shelves to divide oven in thirds.
Heat oven to 450°. Grease 2 jelly roll pans and one 8-inch square baking dish.
Toss potato slices with 1 tablespoon of oil; spread in a single layer in 1 jelly roll pan.
Toss onions and bell pepper with remaining tablespoon of oil.
Spread in a single layer in other jelly roll pan.
Bake both pans 12 to 15 minutes, stirring once, until vegetables are just tender.
Cream together shortening and sugar.
Sift together flour, allspice or nutmeg, cinnamon and cocoa.
Add jam, raisins and nuts to cream mixture.
Beat eggs and add to cream mixture.
Dissolve soda in buttermilk.
Add flour and milk alternately to sugar mixture. Bake in 350° oven for 60 to 75 minutes.

i: 2 large green peppers, cut in strips
1 lb. top round or sirloin steak, cut in thin slices
d: Pour oil in 3-quart microproof casserole or baking dish.
Add beef strips and stir to coat with oil.
Add onion, garlic, salt, pepper and tomatoes.
Cover and cook on High (maximum power) 4 minutes, stirring once during cooking.
Add green peppers and soy sauce, stir.
Cook, covered, on High (max power) 4 to 5 minutes or until green pepper is just tender.

i: 1 small Hidden Valley Ranch buttermilk recipe original Ranch salad dressing mix
d: Combine salad dressing mix, oil, dill weed and garlic.
Pour over oyster crackers; stir to coat.
Place in warm oven 15 to 20 minutes.
Store in tight container.

i: 1 lb. bulk pork sausage, cooked, drained, and crumbled
1 cup frozen loose packed hash browns, thawed
d: Separate rolls into 8 triangles and place on an ungreased pizza pan, points toward center.
Press dough over bottom and sides to form a crust.
Layer sausage and potatoes on crust.
Top with cheddar cheese.
Beat eggs; add milk, salt and pepper.
Pour over cheese.
Beat eggs and egg white until creamy.
Add milk and cream. Sprinkle in salt and pepper.
Stir in meats and cheese.
Pour into an unbaked pie shell.
Add mushrooms; layer tomatoes.
Sprinkle with additional cheese, if desired.

i: 3 c. string beans, cut in fine slices
2 breasts of chicken, cut into small pieces
d: Saute the garlic in olive oil.
Add onion.
Cook until soft. Add chicken breasts.
Brown.
Add string beans, cabbage, carrots and garbanzo beans.
Stir and cook until half done.
Saute until brown the hamburg and rice in olive oil.
Add salt, pepper and garlic.
Add 1 1/2 cups of water; cover and cook 10 minutes.
Add the zucchini, tomatoes, green pepper, onion and parsley.
Cook 10 minutes.
Pour the boiling water over the Jell-O and sugar. When cool, add the pineapple juice.
Let stand at least 24 hours in refrigerator.
Add the ginger ale before serving.
Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
Saute onion until tender.
Brown ground beef.
Add other ingredients.
Cook several hours.
Serve over hamburg rolls.
Peel and cut up cukes, removing all seeds.
Cook until slightly tender and place in jars and seal.
Very good!

Dissolve jello as directed on package, add sugar while jello is hot. Add pineapple juice and frozen lemonade diluted to make 1 quart. Add limeade diluted as directed. Add ginger ale immediately before serving.
Boil cranberries, water and sugar until berries pop (5 minutes).
Add cherry gelatin and stir until dissolved.
Allow to cool.
Add remaining ingredients and chill overnight.

i: 2 Tbsp. fresh parsley or 1/2 Tbsp. dried
4 Tbsp. olive oil (gives the correct flavor)
1 lb. fresh, cleaned and uncooked shrimp (scallops or squid may be substituted)
d: Place garlic and parsley in oil and simmer in a skillet over medium heat until garlic becomes yellow.
Add shrimp.
Simmer 10 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add cooking wine and simmer over a medium heat 10 more minutes, stirring frequently.
At this point, if the sauce is not thick enough, 1 to 3 tablespoons of plain bread crumbs may be added as a thickener.
Serves 4.
Scrub ham with a brush to remove any mold on the skin.
Soak ham in cold water for 8 hours.
Place ham in roaster, fat side down, add 5 cups water.
Cover top of pan with foil (making airtight as possible) before putting on the roaster lid.
Place roaster in a cold oven and set temperature at 500°.
Bake at 500° for 10 minutes.
Chop peanuts into bowl.
Put foil on a plate.
Melt chocolate pieces in skillet on low heat, stirring all the time until melted. Peel banana.
Cut in half and place on foiled plate.
Spread chocolate on all sides of banana.
Then roll in peanuts to coat. Put on waxed paper and put in freezer until hard.
Press rolls in 9 x 15-inch pan.
Bake at 350° for 10 minutes. Spread mixture of cream cheese, mayonnaise and Ranch dressing onto cool crust.
Press chopped vegetables and cheese onto mixture. Cut into squares.
This dish freezes well.
Chop all the ingredients fine and boil for 20 minutes.
In medium saucepan, heat oil.
Saute bell pepper and onion for 2 minutes.
Lower heat and add paprika and taco seasoning.
Stir well.
Add can of beans and mash with potato masher.
Add 1/3 cup water.

t: Herbed Pita Toasts With Feta Cheese
d: Heat oven to 350°.
Place pita rounds on a baking sheet and brush with olive oil.
Sprinkle with oregano, salt and pepper. Bake until crispy and lightly golden, about 10 minutes.
Remove from oven and break pitas in half.
Slice Feta cheese, drizzle with olive oil and serve with pitas.
Mix sugar, oil, vinegar, Accent, pepper and Ramen noodle flavoring together and refrigerate overnight.

i: 1 lb. lean ground beef or turkey
1/4 tsp. Tabasco sauce (may be adjusted to taste)
d: Brown ground beef and drain well.
Add to other ingredients in crock-pot.
Stir well, cover and cook on low for 6 to 8 hours (high for 2 to 3 hours).
Serve with tossed salad and crusty French bread for a quick and easy meal.
Combine all ingredients.
Chill for 2 hours before serving. Garnish with lettuce and slivered almonds.
Serves 8.
One serving is a legal W.W. lunch.
In a skillet, brown meat and drain.
Add taco seasoning and salt.
Add onion, cheese and crunched-up Doritos.
Stir and chill. Serve with chips.
Set oven for 425°.
Combine beef and onion in baking dish. Brown in oven and drain.
Cook macaroni, then mix with spices, beef and onions.
Add 1 1/2 cans tomato sauce, tomato paste and water.
Mix in some of pepperoni.
Top with cheese, pepperoni and remaining tomato sauce.
Have butcher butterfly a leg of lamb, or if your able, remove the bone yourself.
Flatten the meat and roast on grill until done to your taste.
It should take about 30 minutes, turning at 5 minute intervals.
Spread with the Herb and Mustard Sauce and slice.
Mix dry pudding with pineapple.
Add Cool Whip, then marshmallows and nuts.
Put in mold and refrigerate.
Brown beef and onion in skillet for 5 minutes.
Add salsa, tomato sauce and bouillon cube; bring to a full boil.
Stir in rice; cover and remove from heat.
Let stand 5 minutes and fluff with a fork.
Combine all ingredients and pour over chicken.
Soak for 5 to 6 hours or overnight.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.
Drain marinade and cook at 450° for 15 minutes.
Pour boiling water over bran.
Stir well and let cool.
Mix all ingredients.
This will keep in refrigerator 6-8 weeks.
Bake 20 min. at 400 degrees in greased muffin pan.
Brown meat with garlic; drain fat.
Add tomato sauce, tomatoes, salt, pepper and oregano.
Cover and simmer for 20 minutes.
Cook noodles until tender, rinse in cold water and drain.

Arrange and alternate layers in baking dish with noodles on bottom, followed by Mozzarella cheese, cottage cheese, meat sauce and Parmesan cheese. Continue until dish is filled, ending with Parmesan cheese.
Bake at 375° for 45 minutes.
In pan, add butter.
When butter is hot, add cloves, cumin and bay leaf.
When bay leaf turns brown, add peas and coriander powder.
Add washed and drained rice.
Stir well.
Add salt and water.
Boil syrup and sugar together for two minutes.
Pour slowly over eggs and stir well.
Add margarine, vanilla and nuts. Pour into unbaked pie shell and bake at 375 degrees for 50 minutes.

i: 1/4 c. Tahini (optional) (a sesame paste found in health stores)
d: Blend all ingredients together in food processor.
Serve at room temperature with warm wedges of pita bread.
Roll out biscuits and cut each in half.
Wrap biscuit halves around each apple quarter.
Pour remaining ingredients over apple wraps.
Bake at 375° for one hour.
Slice zucchini and cook in microwave for 3 minutes.
Layer in a 9 x 13-inch pan in order given.
Bake for 1 hour at 350°.

t: Chocolate Caramel Pecan Bars(Barbara Morkus, Carbondale, Colorado)
i: about 1 c. (1/2 lb.) butter or margarine, cut into chunks
2 c. (12 oz.) semi-sweet chocolate baking chips or chunks
d: In a bowl, beat to blend 1 cup butter, brown sugar, egg yolk and vanilla. Add flour and mix well.
Press mixture evenly over bottom and sides of a buttered 10 x 15-inch pan. Sprinkle nuts over dough. Bake in a 350° oven until crust is golden brown, about 25 minutes.
Pour caramel into crust and spread evenly. Sprinkle with chocolate. When chocolate softens, about 5 minutes, spread smooth.
Let cool.
Cut into 30 squares, then cut each in half diagonally.
If making ahead, cover and chill up to 1 day.

i: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen, sliced strawberries, partially thawed
d: Dissolve jello in boiling water and let cool.
Add strawberries, pineapple, cranberry sauce, celery and pecans.
Put into a 12 x 8 x 2-inch pan or your favorite mold, sprayed lightly with cooking spray.
Cover and chill until firm.
Unmold on lettuce-lined plate.
Serves 12.
Work flour in.
If it separates when rolled, you need to work in a little more flour.
Roll on aluminum foil.
Wipe countertop with a damp cloth so foil will stick to counter.
Roll thin.
Use tracing wheel to score in little squares.

i: 8 oz. cream cheese, beaten until creamy
d: Beat the cream cheese until creamy.
Beat in sour cream.
Add chilies, onion and cheese.
Mix.
Heat the flour tortillas. Spread mixture on the tortillas.
Roll up and refrigerate.
Brown beef.
Add onion, green pepper, salt, brown sugar, chili powder, bay leaf and rest of the ingredients.
Cook, covered, until rice and vegetables are done.
Stir often.
Serves 6 to 8.
Fry onions in butter.
Add flour, salt, curry powder and chicken stock.
Stir constantly until mixture is thick.
Add milk, lemon juice and chicken.
Heat well.
(May be made in advance and reheated.)

i: 15 oz. can black beans, drained and rinsed
d: Combine cornstarch and water in small bowl.
Stir together all remaining ingredients, except cilantro, in saucepan.
Add the cornstarch water mixture; bring to a boil, stirring occasionally. Add cilantro.
Garnish with tortilla chips and shredded Cheddar cheese.

i: 1 tsp. each: salt, sugar and soda
d: Place
flour
in
bowl.
Make a well in the center; add sourdough
starter.
Cook butter and garlic together until garlic is done, then add Dales Sauce.
Cook until boiling.
You can add flavor or cornstarch to thicken if necessary.
Serve over steak, hamburgers or chicken.
You can also use this as a bread dip.
In a bowl, mix oleomargarine, sugar and cocoa.
Add 3 eggs while mixing.
Slowly add flour, baking powder, salt, soda and cinnamon.
Beat in 1/2 cup milk.
Stir in zucchini, nuts and vanilla; mix well.
Pour into a greased and floured 9 x 12-inch cake pan.
Prepare fish, marinate in remaining ingredients for 30 minutes.
Place on a greased sizzle platter.
Brush with melted butter.
Broil until done (do not turn).
Remove to plate, keep warm.
Place platter on burner.

i: 1 (1 oz.) pkg. dry Hidden Valley Ranch dressing
d: Mix together cream cheese, Ranch dressing and green onions. Spread on the flour tortillas.
Drain vegetables, pat extra liquid out on paper towels.
Sprinkle equal amounts on the 4 tortillas. Roll tortillas up tightly to form logs and refrigerate at least 2 hours before serving.
Slice in 1/2-inch pinwheels and garnish with salsa or guacamole.
Boil chicken quickly.
Separate into serving pieces or strips. Cover with spaghetti sauce mix (I use Giant's brand).
Add vegetables.
Simmer on stove or in oven.
Just before time to serve, add prepared shrimp (the amount and size you can afford that week)!
Cook 3 or 4 minutes.

i: Chicken, cooked and deboned, save broth
1 bunch parsley, chopped fine, but not the stems
d: After chicken is cooked, place the rest of the ingredients in the left over broth, except the noodles.
Bring to a rolling boil. Simmer until onions are done, then add noodles.
Cook until noodles are done.
This makes a large pot of soup, for about 10.
Beat together shortening, sugar, egg, water and vanilla until creamy.
Combine remaining ingredients.
Add to creamy mixture and mix well.
Drop by teaspoon onto greased cookie sheet.
Bake in 350° oven for 8 to 10 minutes.
Makes 5 dozen cookies.
Mix dry ingredients well; stir in raisins.
Add oil, milk and egg.
Mix lightly, not too much.
Drop by spoon into greased and floured muffin cups.
Bake at 425° for 20 minutes.
Makes 12 medium muffins.
Fry sausage in skillet, stirring until crumbly and browned. Combine sausage, cheese, pepper and biscuit mix.
Add water and mix well.
Form into small balls.
Place on cookie sheet and bake at 375° about 20 minutes or until lightly browned.
Serve hot or cold.
May be frozen before baking.

t: Impossible Chicken And Broccoli Pie Or Broccoli And Cheddar Pie
d: Heat oven to
Grease
10 x 1 1/2-inch pie plate. Rinse broccoli
under
running cold water to thaw; drain thoroughly. Mix broccoli,
Put can of milk in pot with lid on and keep covered with water.
Boil (barely bubbling) for 2 1/2 to 3 hours.
Remove; add hot coffee and beat until smooth.
Add pecans; put in pie shell and top with whipped cream.
Place nuts in a single layer in a large, shallow baking pan. Heat in a 350° oven for 10 minutes, stirring once.
Place toasted nuts in a very large buttered bowl; set aside.
Combine sugar, corn syrup, water and unsweetened chocolate in a large saucepan.
Bring to boil, over medium-high heat, stirring constantly.
Clip a candy thermometer to a side of the pan.
Cook over medium heat to 238°, soft ball stage without stirring, about 12 to 15 minutes.
Mix flour, meal, salt and pepper in a dish.
Melt shortening in an electric skillet.
Roll chicken in batter and fry in electric skillet for 45 minutes at 350°.
Cook the pasta in boiling water (do not overcook); drain in colander.
Pour warm pasta in bowl and pour Wish-Bone dressing over.
Stir.
Cool at room temperature.
Meanwhile, dice green peppers and thinly slice onions.
Cube the cheese into 1/4-inch cubes.

i: milk, added to salmon liquid to make 1 c.
d: Drain the salmon, reserving the liquid.
Add milk to the liquid to make 1 cup.
Beat eggs and add to the liquid.
Flake the salmon.
Add the other ingredients; mix thoroughly.
Line a greased loaf pan with aluminum foil.
In a covered saucepan, simmer cranberries and water for 20 to 25 minutes or until tender.
Press through a sieve or food mill. Return to saucepan.
Add apples, cover and simmer for 35 to 40 minutes or until apples are tender but retain their shape.
Add sugar.
Simmer for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Yields 8 to 10 servings.

i: 1 box frozen chopped chicken or 1 lb. chicken, chopped and cooked
d: Heat 1 tablespoon cooking oil (any variety) in large frying pan.
Add onion and cook until clear.
Add chicken and cook until thawed.
Add broccoli and mushrooms and cook about 5 minutes until heated through.
Add bean sprouts and water chestnuts.
Mix stir-fry seasoning according to package directions and pour over stir-fry.

i: 3 or 4 servings bass or Walleye fillet
d: Clean and dry fish.
Mix eggs and A.1. sauce.
Marinate fish in sauce 10 minutes.
Crush crackers fine and coat fish.
Place fish fillets on oiled heavy foil.
Bake 8 minutes at 400°.
Toast the bread and butter it.
Dice bread into 1/2-inch pieces and combine with tomatoes.
Add sugar and spices.
Pour into greased casserole dish and top with rest of butter.
Bake at 350° until lightly browned, 30 to 40 minutes.
Good with white beans and rice.
Lightly brown onion and garlic in salad oil.
Stir in rice, consomme, water, mushrooms, and salt and pepper.
Bring to a boil, then lower heat and cook 4 minutes or until rice is tender and liquid absorbed, stirring occasionally.
Yield: 4 to 6 servings.

i: 3/4 c. orange flavor instant breakfast drink
d: Combine all ingredients, chill and serve.
Combine first 5 ingredients in a large mixing bowl.
Add eggs and oil, stirring until dry ingredients are moistened.
Do not beat.

Stir in vanilla, pineapple, 1 cup pecans and bananas. Spoon batter into 3 greased and floured 9-inch round cake pans. Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in center comes out clean.
Cool in pans 10 minutes.
Remove from pans and cool completely.

i: 4 boneless chicken breast halves, skinned and cut into strips
d: In large skillet, heat oil.
Add broccoli and carrots.
Stir over medium heat until tender-crisp.
Add next 5 ingredients; cook until chicken is done.
Stir in pasta, onions and cheese; heat thoroughly.

t: Chicken And Asparagus Casserole Dish(A Good Company Dish)
i: 6 whole chicken breasts (I use 4 whole chicken breasts)
d: Boil chicken breasts in seasoned water until tender.
Cool, debone and tear bite size pieces.
Set aside.
Saute onion in butter and add remaining ingredients, except asparagus and almonds.
Simmer sauce until cheese melts.
To assemble: place a layer of chicken in a large casserole dish, a layer of asparagus and a layer of sauce.
Cook chicken in broth with bay leaf and garlic clove.
Cool; cut in small pieces.
Cut eggs in small chunks.
Set aside.
Mix together next 6 items.
Add chicken and eggs.

i: 1-1/4 lb. red potatoes, cut into 2-inch chunks
3-1/2 Tbsp. oil-cured black olives, pitted and coarsely chopped
1 small lemon, peel grated, 2 tsp. juice reserved
d: Prepare grill.
Place potatoes in a steamer basket over boiling water.
Cover pan and steam 10 minutes or until tender.
Drain, transfer to a bowl and toss with oil.
When cool enough to handle, thread potatoes onto skewers.
Mix cream cheese and Cheddar cheese together and form into a flat round.
Top with remaining ingredients.
Surround with crackers and serve.
Mix hamburg, rice and egg together, then roll into meat balls. Put tomato soup, gravy and meatballs in kettle.
Cover and cook slowly about 45 minutes or until meatballs are done.
Preheat oven to 450°.
Sift together dry ingredients.
Cut in shortening until coarse.
Make a well in center and pour all the milk.
Stir until barely damp.
Drop by spoonfuls onto ungreased cookie sheet.

i: 1 tsp. cinnamon or Tbsp if you want more
d: In a 9 X 13 pan melt the butter. Roll the apples in crescent rolls. Line in the pan and pour Mountain Dew over the top. Mix sugar and cinnamon and sprinkle over dumplings. Cook at 350 degrees for 30 minutes. Serves 8.
Skin the chicken pieces and score them deeply.
Mix thoroughly chili and curry powder, tomato paste, salt and yogurt.
Marinate chicken pieces in the mixture for four to five hours.
(If desired, this can be marinated in the refrigerator overnight.)
Arrange the chicken pieces in the baking pan and pour the remaining excess marinade over chicken.
Bake at 375° for 45 minutes, occasionally basting.

i: 1 lb. smoked sausage, cut into 1/2-inch slices
d: Cut sausage and place in 2-quart dish.
Add hot water.
Cover and cook in microwave on High for 5 minutes.
Drain.
Stir in chutney and sour cream.
Reheat (before serving) on Medium for 2 minutes.

i: 2 lbs. Boneless sirloin, cut into 2-inch pieces
2 sweet green peppers cut into 1-inch pieces
d: In small bowl, whisk oil, garlic, rind and juice, vinegar, oregano, salt and pepper. Pour half into large storage bag; add beef. Seal; refrigerate 1 to 2 hours. Divide steak, peppers, onion and mushrooms into 6 equal portions. Thread each portion onto large metal skewers. Heat broiler. Place skewers on large broiler-pan rack. Brush with some of remaining marinade. Broil 5 minutes. Turn over; brush with remaining marinade. Broil 5 minutes. Serve with rice.

i: 1 (1-1/2-inch thick) Pacific Salmon with skin on
d: Marinate salmon 6 hours in Wishbone dressing.
Gas grill: Spray PAM on extra hot grill.
Place salmon, skin side down, and cook for 7 minutes.
Do not flip.
Charcoal grill:
Cook for 11 minutes.
Butter shallow baking dish.
Arrange fish in dish.
Melt butter in saucepan.
Add remaining ingredients and pour over fish. Bake at 350° for 20 to 25 minutes.
Brown ground beef in skillet.
Add 1 teaspoon salt, chili sauce and pepper.
Simmer 5 to 10 minutes.
Combine flour, cornmeal and 1 teaspoon salt in mixing bowl.
Cut in shortening until particles are fine.
Combine egg and water; sprinkle over flour mixture while stirring with fork until dough holds together.

i: 4 to 6 c. diced cooked chicken or turkey
d: Line bottom of a 9 x 13 x 2-inch casserole dish with diced chicken. Add salt and pepper, if needed. Combine soup, sour cream and broth and pour over meat. Mix crushed crackers, margarine and poppy seed. Sprinkle over meat and mixture. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Cook spaghetti and drain.
Mix with egg and milk.
Spread over bottom of 9 x 13-inch pan.
Top with spaghetti sauce, grated cheese and pepperoni or other toppings.
Bake at 350° for 1/2 hour.
Blend dry ingredients.
Add remaining ingredients and mix well. Pour into large rectangular pan or two 8-inch round layer pans. Bake at 350° for 45 to 50 minutes.
May add chopped walnuts and/or raisins.
Sprinkle top with powdered sugar after baking or frost with light chocolate frosting.
Stores well.
Cut potatoes into thick wedges, leaving skin on.
Combine remaining ingredients.
Brush mixture on potatoes.
Brown 15 minutes on each side.

i: a few apple slices and sprig of mint
d: Pour water and apple juice into a glass.
Stir in lemon juice.
Garnish with apple slices and mint.

t: Jello Salad(This Is Almost Like A Dessert, Yummy.)
d: Cook until dissolved the water, pineapple juice and marshmallows.
Add lemon and orange jello, stirring until dissolved.
Cool until almost set at room temperature.
Add carrots, celery, cottage cheese, mayonnaise and chopped nuts. Fold into jello mix.
Fold in Dream Whip or other whipped cream. Pour into mold and chill.
Cook chicken until tender.
Add onion, salt, pepper, tomatoes and paprika and cook about 20 minutes.
Then add noodles and cook another 20 minutes.

i: 1 (3 oz.) pkg. Jell-O (your choice of color)
d: In one cup of water, dissolve Jell-O and sugar.
Add to pineapple juice.
Just before serving, add ginger ale or 7-Up and ice.
Takes just a few minutes to make.
Brown onions and green peppers.
Dice the top round steak. Put into pan with peppers and brown.
Add hot water and half bottle of Worcestershire sauce.
Cook for approximately 45 minutes.
Mix flour and water to make gravy.
Fry onion in butter until golden.
Cover pan with foil.
Lay fish out.
Put butter, garlic and 1 teaspoon of orange juice on top of fish.
Broil for 20 to 25 minutes.
Rub the chicken inside and out with the cut surface of the lemon.
Rub outside with two tablespoons softened butter.
Place on rack in a roasting dish and bake in a 375° oven for 1/2 hour per pound.
Remove chicken and keep warm.

i: 1 (21.5 oz.) pkg. Betty Crocker fudge brownies
1 c. M&M's semi-sweet chocolate candies for baking
d: Heat oven to 370°.
Lightly grease cookie sheet.
Stir together brownie mix (dry), flour, oil, water and eggs in a large bowl.
Stir in candies.
Drop by scant 1/4 cupfuls 3 inches apart onto cookie sheet.
Bake 12 to 15 minutes or just until set and tops are dry.

i: 1 pkg. shredded Parmesan or mild Cheddar cheese
d: Layer the first 4 ingredients in a bowl; do not stir. Refrigerate overnight.
Before serving, top with the tomato, eggs and the mayonnaise, mixed with Parmesan or mild Cheddar cheese (spread over top).
When serving, reach spoon to bottom of bowl to get all layers.
Serves 8 to 10.
Melt butter.
Mix in remaining ingredients.
Roll chicken in mixture.
Bake at 375° for 1 hour.
Heat the oil.
Add the onion and saute until soft.
Add the carrots and saute.
Add the water, lower the heat and cover; cook for 7 to 10 minutes until soft.
Add the salt and the curry powder; mix.
Cook the rice separately.
Warm milk, add yeast and sugar; let melt.
Add the eggs, salt, margarine (melted) and oil.
Add flour and knead dough approximately 20 minutes.
Cover and refrigerate overnight.

i: 1 small can frozen lime, lemonade, orange juice or pink lemonade
drop or two of food coloring
d: To the citrus juice, add appropriate food coloring.
Add condensed milk and stir.
Add Cool Whip and blend.
Evenly divide mixture into two pie shells.
Chill.
Ready in 5 minutes.
Mix sugar and cornstarch; add vinegar, oil and water.
Bring to boil.
Add beets and cook over low heat 30 minutes or until thick.
Dissolve yeast in warm water.
Add buttermilk.
Add dry ingredients and knead 5 minutes.
Roll 1/4-inch.
Cut and place on greased pan.
Let rise 1 hour.
Put margarine in bottom of pan. Wash and slice squash.
Add onion and pepper and salt to choice.
Start to boil margarine with other ingredients in pan also, then cover and turn down to low temperature.
Cook until tender.
Serve.

Dissolve yeast, 1/3 of sugar, in small bowl with warm water. In a large bowl, mix rest of sugar with cardamom, salt, egg whites, milk, and oil. Add yeast and rest of ingredients. Mix well to blend. Knead on floured board 5 minutes until smooth, elastic. Shape into ball, place in sprayed 8 inch round cake pan. Cover with damp cloth, let dough rise to double. Bake 350° 35-40 minutes until golden brown.

i: 1 lb. fresh spinach, torn into bite-size pieces
1 medium green pepper, cut into rings
1 medium yellow pepper, cut into rings
1/4 tsp. dried oregano leaves or dill
d: Blanch green beans in boiling water for 5 minutes, until tender-crisp.
Rinse under cold water and drain.
Rinse spinach thoroughly and drain.
Tear into bite-size pieces.
In a large bowl, toss all ingredients together until well blended.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.

t: "Simply Delicious" Chicken Broccoli Lasagna(6 To 8 Servings)
i: 1 tsp. each salt, oregano and basil or to taste
12 oz. lasagna noodles, cooked and drained
d: Will need a 13 x 9-inch pan sprayed with nonstick spray. Preheat oven to 350°.
Grind ingredients together and let stand overnight in salt, then rinse good with cold water and drain.
Turn into large pan and add sugar, vinegar, turmeric and water.
Bring to a boil. Simmer for 3 minutes, then put in canning jars and seal.
Makes approximately 6 quarts.

i: 1 (14 oz.) can Eagle Brand condensed milk
1 (4 oz.) pkg. instant vanilla flavor pudding mix
d: In
large bowl combine pudding mix, water and condensed milk. Beat
well.
Chill	5
minutes, then fold in Cool Whip. In	2 1/2-quart
glass

i: 1 1/2 c. fresh or frozen blueberries (do not thaw frozen berries)
d: Preheat oven to 400°.
Spray muffin tin with no-stick spray.

i: 2 pkg. green onions, sliced or chop all
d: Cut veal into strips (two fingers wide).
Rinse strips to moisten.
Dip in bread crumbs and brown (medium).
Pour off excess fat.
Saute green onions and mushrooms until clear.
Add browned meat, wine and Worcestershire; simmer until vegetables reach desired stage.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Wash potatoes.
Place potatoes in small roasting pan.
Drizzle olive oil over potatoes.
Sprinkle garlic, salt and pepper over potatoes.
Bake for 45 to 50 minutes, until tender and browned.

t: Baked Chicken With Tarragon And Fennel
i: salt and fresh ground black pepper to taste
d: Place chicken breast on foil-lined baking pan.
Sprinkle with seasonings.
Cover loosely with foil to help retain moisture.
Bake at 350° for 45 to 50 minutes, until cooked through.
Grease and flour an 8-inch square pan.
Stir together egg, brown sugar and vanilla. Quickly stir in flour, soda and salt. Add nuts.
Spread in pan and bake at 350° for 18 to 20 minutes. Should be soft in center when removed from oven.
Do not overbake. Leave in pan and cut into 2-inch squares.
Makes 16 squares.

i: 2 or 3 lb. beef chuck or round steak, fat trimmed
1 c. red and/or green peppers, cut into 1/2-inch squares
d: With a very sharp knife, cut beef across grain into thin strips, 1/8-inch thick.
Combine soy sauce, garlic and ginger. Add beef.
Toss and set aside while preparing vegetables.
Heat oil in large frying pan or wok.
Add beef and toss over high heat until browned.
Cover and simmer for 40 to 60 minutes over low heat.
Line a bowl with lettuce.
Toss the first 6 ingredients together and place in the lettuce-lined bowl.
Mix the last 6 ingredients well.
Pour over the salad and toss lightly.

i: black and red pepper and oregano to taste
d: Heat oil and 2 tablespoons butter in saucepan over low heat. Add garlic, parsley, pepper and oregano.
Simmer 5 minutes.
In another pan, melt remaining butter over low heat.
Add flour and, stirring constantly, cook about 2 minutes.
Slowly add clam juice while stirring.
Combine the contents of both saucepans and simmer for 5 minutes.
Mix chicken and the first 7 ingredients together.
Let stand 1 hour.
Spread in a 9 x 10-inch pan.
Sprinkle crushed corn flakes and Parmesan cheese on top.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes to 1 hour.
Break and separate Ramen noodles.
Combine all of the ingredients.
Thaw strawberries.
Sift dry ingredients together in a large bowl.
Stir in pecans.
In a separate bowl, whisk together eggs, oil and strawberries.
Stir into dry ingredients.
Mix lightly. Spoon into greased muffin tin.

i: 1 red or green bell pepper, chopped
d: Put peppers, onion and garlic in blender with juice from tomatoes.
Blend a few seconds to chop; add remaining ingredients. Blend a second or two to chop up tomatoes; stir.
Serve with tortilla chips.
Layer a 9 x 13-inch pan with one can of the crescent rolls. Brown the ground beef in a skillet.
Drain the beef and place it on top of the crescent rolls.
Spoon the taco sauce onto the beef. Sprinkle the Cheddar cheese over the beef and taco sauce.
Cover all of this with the remaining can of crescent rolls.
Bake at 350° for approximately 15 minutes.
Mix all ingredients and pour in a greased pie plate (use a bigger pan and make thinner for an appetizer).
Bake at 400° for 35 minutes.
Soak garlic in olive oil for at least 1 hour.
Scrub potatoes; cut lengthwise into 8 wedges each.
Place wedges in a bowl and cover with very cold water.
Allow to stand for 30 minutes.
Drain and pat dry with paper towels.
Remove garlic from oil.
Melt chocolate chips. Take off heat.
Add milk and peanuts. Drop by teaspoonfuls on wax paper.
Let harden in cool place.
Simmer onion and celery in butter.
Add flour; mix thoroughly. Add chicken stock and cook until smooth.
Add tomato juice, seasonings and chicken.
Arrange rice in a ring and pour hot curried chicken in the center.

i: 1 (10 3/4 oz.) can Campbell's golden corn soup
1/2 c. finely chopped celery and onions
1 1/2 c. Pepperidge Farm herb seasoned mix
d: Combine soup, celery and onions in a 9-inch greased pie plate. Spoon stuffing mixture in.
Arrange chicken over stuffing mixture.
Press lightly into stuffing.
Combine sugar, margarine and mustard.
Spread evenly over chicken.
Bake at 400° for 25 minutes or until chicken is no longer pink.
Mix all in large crock.
Bring to a boil slowly.
Then simmer until as thick as you'd like.
Set into clean jars (hot).
Seal in hot bath for 15 minutes, or until pressure cooked 10 minutes at 5 pounds.
Cook chicken until tender.
Remove from bone.
Bring 2 cups broth to a boil with onion.
Remove from heat; add rice and let set, covered, for 5 minutes.
Add all remaining ingredients.
Mix well.
Slice onions and arrange in a 1-quart baking dish.
Mix the rest of the ingredients and pour over the onions.
Bake at 300° for 1 hour.

Beat the yolk in a mixing bowl, adding some olive oil drop by drop. When mixture begins to thicken, alternately add a few drops of lemon juice and a few drops more of olive oil until the desired consistency is obtained. Lemon juice thins the dressing and olive oil thickens it. If more lemon juice is added, oil must also be added, as the dressing must be stiff when ready to use. Refrigerate. Use within 2 to 3 days.
Combine oil and sugar blend.
Add eggs one at a time.
Add vanilla.
Combine flour, soda, salt, cinnamon, nutmeg and sift. Add to previous mixture.
Fold in apples, nuts and dates.
Bake in 325° oven for 1 1/2 hours.
Set salt for 3 hours over cucumbers and onions.
Drain and put into large container and add the rest of the ingredients to onion and cucumbers.
Store covered in refrigerator.
Steam green beans.
Mix Dijon mustard, salt and pepper and lemon.
Whisk in olive oil and toss with the green beans.
Serve hot.
Cut pork into thin strips.
Dredge in the flour.
Cook quickly but thoroughly in the hot oil.
Remove meat from skillet.
Add remaining ingredients to drippings and cook 2 minutes until crisp but tender.
Stir pork strips in and serve.
Crush taco
chips
into 9 x 13-inch dish.
Brown ground beef; season
with envelope taco seasoning.
Add refried beans to beef; mix

i: 1 whole chicken, boned and cut up
d: Heat together soup, chilies, sour cream, chicken and onions. Fill tortillas with 1/2 of soup mixture and roll up.
Pour remaining soup on top.
Top with cheese.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

i: 1 (15 oz.) can garbanzo beans, drained (reserve liquid)
d: In small frying pan, toast sesame seed over low heat, shaking pan often, until golden brown (about 3 minutes).
