Transfer seed to blender or food processor and add garbanzos, 2 tablespoons olive oil, lemon juice, garlic and 6 tablespoons reserved liquid. Whirl, adding more liquid if necessary, until hummus is smooth, but still thick.
Season with salt and pepper.
Place in shallow bowl.
Drizzle with remaining 2 tablespoons oil.
Makes 2 cups.

i: 1 (4 serving size) pkg. chocolate instant pudding
1 (16 oz.) pkg. chocolate sandwich cookies, crushed
d: Will also need 8 to 10 (7 oz.) clear plastic cups
Cut the marshmallows in quarters and put into a very large bowl in double boiler.
Melt chocolate.
When melted, add milk and keep mixing until smooth. Add the vanilla and stir.
Let cool slightly. Add nuts.
Pour over marshmallows and mix.
Put in shallow loaf pan lined with waxed paper and let cool in refrigerator overnight.
Mix well.
Drop by small spoonfuls into hot soup.
Cook 12 minutes.

i: 2 lb. catfish nuggets or 3 whole catfish
d: Wash fish.
In baking dish, add lemon juice, bay leaf, parsley, fish, butter, salt, pepper and water.
Bake 30 minutes in 350° oven.
Serve with rice and buttered spinach.
Spread beans on serving platter. Mix sour cream with taco seasoning and spread over beans. Cover with shredded cheese and sprinkle with remaining ingredients.

i: 1 (6 oz.) pkg. frozen pea pods, thawed
d: Thaw shrimp, if frozen and drain well.
Peel and devein shrimp.
For sauce, stir together water, cornstarch, soy sauce, sugar, bouillon granules, lemon peel and juice and pepper.
Set aside.
Preheat a wok or large skillet over high heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of the oil.
Boil together for 5 minutes applesauce, sugar and shortening; cool.
Mix rest of dry ingredients and add to cooled applesauce mixture.
Bake 2 hours at 250°.
Slice peeled potatoes in round, pretty thin slices.
Layer in a deep casserole dish with butter and salt.
Pour can of milk over the potatoes.
Make sure they are completely covered with milk. Bake, uncovered, for about 1 hour at 475°.
Mix all ingredients.
Drop by teaspoon into hot cooking oil. Cook until golden brown, about 3 minutes.
Makes 24.
Melt butter; add to flour.
Mix well.
Add powdered sugar, vanilla, pecan chips and chocolate chips; mix well.
Chill for 30 minutes.
Roll mixture into small balls.
Bake at 400° for 10 minutes.
Brown hamburger; drain off fat.
Add pepperoni and pizza sauce.
Mix well and simmer 5 minutes.
Add cheese just before serving.
Spoon onto bun and enjoy.

i: 8 oz. cooked ham, cut in julienne strips
d: Sprinkle 3 cups cheese in bottom of 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish.
In saucepan, saute mushrooms, onions and red pepper in oleo until vegetables are tender but not brown; drain well.
Place vegetables atop cheese.
Arrange ham strips atop vegetables. Sprinkle remaining 3 cups cheese atop ham.
Cover and chill overnight in refrigerator.

i: 1/2 tsp. each: basil, salt and sugar
d: Saute onion in olive oil until transparent.
Add garlic and saute for 1 minute.
Stir in remaining ingredients.
Simmer at a very low heat, uncovered, for about 1 hour, stirring occasionally. Remove bay leaf.
Makes about 1 cup.

i: 1 loaf frozen bread (Rhodes brand works well or frozen sweet bread)
d: Thaw loaf of bread.
In 9 x 13 pan, melt 2 tablespoons oleo, 1/4 cup brown sugar and syrup.
Roll out dough to 12 x 16-inches. Spread with 2 tablespoons oleo.
Sprinkle the other 1/4 cup brown sugar, cinnamon and nuts.
Roll up from long side like a jelly roll; slice into 12 pieces and put in prepared pan.
Bake at 360° for 15 to 20 minutes or according to recipe on bread package.
Sprinkle sliced bananas with lemon juice to prevent them from turning black.
Combine oranges and mix walnuts, coconut and cherries; add sugar and mix gently.
Serves 8 to 10.
Cut breasts into fillets.
Dip fillets into flour and fry in 1 to 2 tablespoons oil.
Combine oil, vinegar, mustard and spices together.
Warm and serve over the top of the chicken fillets. Garnish with lemon slices.
Boil kielbasa in beer for 10 minutes.
Combine ketchup, mustard, vinegar and sugar in crock-pot.
Cut kielbasa in small bite size pieces and place in crock-pot.
Cook on high for 2 to 3 hours or low for 3 to 5 hours.
Enjoy.

i: 1 lb. carrots, cut in 1/4 to 1/2-inch slices
1 Tbsp. + 2 tsp. extra virgin olive oil
d: Cook carrots with water until tender (5 minutes in microwave or 10 to 15 minutes in covered pan over medium heat).
Combine olive oil, nuts, lemon peel and seasoning salt in a bowl.
Add carrots.
Toss.
Serve warm.
Serves 4.

i: 2 ripe avocados, peeled and mashed (about 1 1/2 c.)
d: Combine all ingredients.
Cover and chill.
Makes about 1 3/4 cups.

i: 3 lb. boneless beef chuck, thinly cut in 2-inch strips
d: Combine all ingredients in a slow cooker at simmer setting. Simmer for about 5 hours.
Serves 6 to 8.
Boil together sugar, water and vinegar to 238°, or until syrup spins a long thread when dropped from spoon.
Beat egg whites stiff.
Gradually add syrup beating constantly until frosting holds shape.
Add vanilla.
Makes enough for 2 layers.

In very large saucepan, combine sugar, evaporated milk, boiling water and Karo syrup; bring to a boil. Add baking soda; bring to a boil again. Add pecan halves, continue to boil, stirring constantly until firm ball stage. Remove from heat, and beat until thickens and loses shiny gloss. Drop by spoonfuls onto wax paper. Add a teaspoon of hot water if it becomes too hard. May be frozen or kept in an airtight container. A teaspoon of vanilla may be added at the end of cooking, if desired.

i: 10 c. zucchini, ground, do not peel
d: Grind first four ingredients.
Sprinkle with canning salt and let stand 8 hours.
Drain, rinse and drain again.
Then add remaining ingredients.
Mix all together.
Mix well and simmer 30 minutes.
Cream shortening, sugars, eggs and vanilla. Sift together flour, baking soda, baking powder and salt and add to creamed mixture.
Mix in oats, chocolate chips and nuts.

i: 1 pkg. dry Good Seasons Italian mix (red label)
d: Cook pasta, drain, add other ingredients, mix together and chill.
Beat egg whites at high speed with an electric mixer until stiff peak forms.
Set aside.
Beat butter and shortening until creamy; gradually add sugar beating well.
Add egg yolks, 1 at a time, beating until blended after each addition.
Combine flour, cocoa and baking soda; add to butter mixture alternately with buttermilk, beginning and ending with flour mixture.
Beat at low speed until blended after each addition.
Bake in baking dish covered with foil at 350° for 25 minutes.

i: 4 large boneless chicken breast halves or 8 small
3 Tbsp. chopped, fresh chives or green onions
d: Place chicken breast halves between sheets of waxed paper or plastic wrap.
Pound slightly with mallet.
Sprinkle with salt and black pepper.
Heat 1 tablespoon each of oil and butter in large skillet.
Cook chicken over high heat for 4 minutes on each side. Do not cook longer or they will be overcooked and dry.
Transfer to warm serving platter.
Mix all ingredients and put into loaf pan. Bake at 350 degrees for 45 to 60 minutes.
Mix sugar, flour and pineapple, then add other ingredients. Put in baking dish.
Mix all ingredients and form into patties.
Broil until done and serve topped with sliced onion, lettuce, tomatoes with or without bun.

i: 2 (15 or 16 oz. cans) black beans, drained
2 Tbsp. olive oil or white wine
1 green pepper, chopped in large pieces
d: Saute onion in oil or wine.
Add green pepper, garlic and cumin.
Stir in chili powder and saute.
Add beans and tomatoes. Stir in brown sugar.
Cover; simmer on low heat for 10 minutes. Serve over couscous or brown rice.
Top with cheese over a baked potato.
Boil together for about 1 hour until tender.
Bring pineapple to boil and add jello.
Stir well to dissolve jello.
Cool and add buttermilk.
Stir Cool Whip in and add pecans.
Refrigerate to set well.
Boil pasta, drain and run under cold water.
Add all vegetables; toss with dressing and salad seasoning.
Better if made a day ahead.

In a large skillet, brown beef and onion; drain. Add tomatoes and water; bring to a boil. Stir in macaroni; bring to a boil. Reduce heat; cover and simmer for 10 minutes or until macaroni is done, stirring once. Stir in cheese. Yield: 4 servings.

i: 1 piece fresh ginger (1 1/2 x 1-inch), peeled and coarsely chopped
d: Preheat broiler.
Halve eggplant lengthwise and then cut crosswise into 1/2-inch slices.
Brush slices on both sides with about 3 tablespoons of the oil and arrange them in a single layer in large broiling tray.
Broil 3 inches from heat source, about 7 minutes; turn with spatula and broil another 7 minutes, or until eggplant is nicely browned.

i: 1 small head cauliflower, chopped into small pieces
1 or 2 bunches broccoli, chopped (use juste the "beads", not the stalk)
1 (8 oz.) pkg. mushrooms, rinsed and sliced thin
1/2 c. Hormel real bacon bits or pieces
d: Place all vegetables in a large bowl.
Combine rest of ingredients and stir until well blended.
Pour over vegetables, toss and refrigerate until ready to serve.

i: 1 whole young fryer less than 4 lb.
d: Preheat grill (if no grill, preheat oven to 375°). Rinse chicken, pat dry.
Drizzle with sesame oil and message in gently, sprinkle with garlic salt (including cavity).
Massage into skin. Place chicken in pie pan, add about 1 inch water to pan - very important.	This keeps the chicken moist inside by steaming as it cooks.
Place pan on grill.
Grill for about 1 hour on low heat or bake in oven for about 1 1/2 hours or until skin is crispy and juices run clear.

i: 1 head iceberg lettuce, torn into bite-size pieces
3 to 4 stalks celery, sliced or chopped
1 to 2 c. mayonnaise (spread to cover and seal the first four layers)
d: In a large bowl, layer the following:
lettuce, peas, celery, onion and mayonnaise.
Cover mayonnaise with Parmesan cheese.
Top with bacon.
Cover and refrigerate approximately 8 hours prior to serving.
Serves 8 to 10.
In 2-quart pan, combine the rice and oil.
Stir over medium heat until rice is golden brown (5 minutes).
Add stock, apple juice, onion, garlic, lemon juice and thyme.
Reduce heat to low; cover pan and simmer until all liquid has been absorbed and rice is tender, about 20 minutes.
Fluff with fork before serving. Serves 6.

i: 3 chicken breasts, skinned, cooked and chopped
2 (4 oz.) cans diced jalapeno peppers, drained
d: Combine chicken, corn, broth, celery and onion.
Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat; simmer for 15 minutes, uncovered.
Add salsa. Stir 2 tablespoons water into cornstarch.
Add to chicken mixture. Cook and stir until bubbles.
Cook an additional 2 minutes. Prepare cornbread according to package.
Boil Swiss chard.
Drain and squeeze out water; chop up. Brown the ground sirloin with onion and garlic in oil.
Season with salt and pepper.
Stir in Swiss chard.
Serve on hard rolls.
Drain beans and peas.
Add onion, pepper and pimento.
In a saucepan, combine sugar, vinegar, salt and chili powder.
Mix and bring to a boil.
Simmer.
Mix vegetables and sauce together and refrigerate.

i: 1 c. bread crumbs, softened with milk (1/4 c.)
d: Combine ingredients.
Shape into loaf and bake in a greased baking dish at 350° for 30 minutes.
Mix together lightly ground beef, bread crumbs, egg, milk, onion, salt, pepper and nutmeg.
Gently form into small balls (1 1/2-inches).
Brown in hot fat.
Add about 1/4 cup hot water. Cover.
Simmer 20 minutes.
Serve hot with slightly thickened pan gravy.

i: any seasoning to match your taste
d: Cook patties until done; drain grease.
Simmer sauce, then mix cheeses, sauce, patties, garlic and herbs.
Cook for 20 to 30 minutes.

i: chicken wings (as many as you need for dinner)
d: Clean wings.
Flour and fry until done.
Place fried chicken wings in microwave bowl.
Stir in barbecue sauce.
Microwave on High (stir once) for 4 minutes.

Boil chicken breasts until done. Allow them to cool and debone them. Cut into small pieces. Cook spaghetti in chicken broth according to the time on the package. When done, drain and add cheese. Stir it until it has melted completely. Saut chopped onions and bell peppers for about two minutes in 1/2 stick of margarine. Add this to spaghetti. Add chicken, pimentos, Ro-Tel tomatoes and English peas to spaghetti. Heat until hot, but do not cook.
Mix sauerkraut, green bell pepper, celery, pimento and onion together.

i: 1 large can yams, drained and mashed
1 small can crushed or chunk pineapple (reserve 1/2 c. juice)
d: Mix all ingredients well, including pineapple juice; mash together and put into greased 2 1/2-quart casserole.
Heat through well in 350° oven for about 20 minutes.
Cover top with miniature marshmallows and return to oven long enough to melt marshmallows. Serve immediately.
Brown ground beef with onion, garlic salt, salt and pepper. Place 3 lasagna noodles into boiling water.
Don't overcook noodles or they will split.
In bottom of 9 x 12-inch pan, put thin coating of spaghetti sauce.
Put the noodles in bottom of pan and put 3 more in water.

Put on top of noodles in layers: drained ground beef (cooked), Ricotta cheese, Mozzarella cheese, sprinkle of grated cheese, drizzle with spaghetti sauce, sprinkle of parsley flakes and sprinkle a few anise seed (if you like).
Do 2 more layers; use plenty of Mozzarella cheese and plenty of sauce.
Boil chicken and sausage.
Add spices together until tender. Debone chicken.
Cut sausage and chicken into small pieces.
Mix rice with broth.
Put ingredients together and bring to a boil. Cut on low heat, cover tightly, 20 minutes.
Then cut burner off and wait until rice has softened, about 15 minutes.
Mix carrots, celery and tuna together.
Add mayonnaise.
Just before serving, add noodles.
Serves about 6.
Boil carrots in water or cook in small amount of water in microwave just until tender; drain and place in 2-quart covered casserole.
Add onion, green pepper, corn and celery.
Combine the remaining ingredients; heat 3 minutes in the microwave on High. Stir and pour over vegetables.
Cool, cover and marinate overnight in the refrigerator.
Makes 12 servings.
This salad will keep in the refrigerator at least one week.
Spread softened cream cheese on tray.
Mix together catsup, horseradish and shrimp.
Spread over cream cheese.
Mix together green onions, green peppers, chopped tomatoes and chopped black olives.
Spread over shrimp sauce.
Sprinkle Mozzarella cheese over vegetables.
Dissolve orange juice, lemonade, limeade and water together; add ginger ale and serve.
Mix all ingredients together and roll out on floured board. Cut out and bake at 350° until light brown on greased cookie sheet, about 12 to 15 minutes.
Makes 14 biscuits.
Mix flour, brown sugar, margarine and nuts until crumbly. Spread on cookie sheet lined with heavy-duty Reynolds Wrap.
Bake in 350° oven for 13 to 15 minutes.
Cool; break apart into crumbs. Line an 8 1/2 x 4 1/2 x 2-inch loaf pan with heavy-duty aluminum foil, leaving a 1 1/2-inch collar around top.
Spread 2/3 of crumb mixture in bottom.
Beat egg yolks until thick and lemon color; blend in condensed milk.
Add lemon juice and rind; stir until thickened.
Preheat broiler.
Line cookie sheet with aluminum foil.
Melt butter in microwave.
Slice bread into 3/4-inch slices.
Dip slices in butter and sprinkle both sides with garlic.
Arrange slices on cookie sheet and spread cheese on top of each slice. Sprinkle oregano lightly on cheese.
Combine sugar, syrup, milk and salt.
Cook until a soft ball forms when dropped in cold water.
Remove from heat.
Add vanilla and peanut butter.
Cool for 5 minutes; beat until mixture is creamy and begins to thicken.
Pour contents into a buttered pan; cut into squares.

Grease and flour 9 x 13 inch pan. Put coconut and nuts in bottom of pan. Mix cake mix and pour over coconut and nuts. Put melted margarine in mixer with cream cheese and powdered sugar. Mix well. Spoon over cake mix and bake 350° 35 to 40 minutes. Do not cut until cold.
Peel eggplant and cover with water; boil until tender.
Drain and cool.
Add egg and pepper and mix well.
Add Bisquick mix. Drop mixture into deep hot fat (oil) until brown.
Cut up and brown chicken in fat and put aside.
Using a clean pan, brown onions, pepper and garlic in bread.
Add tomatoes and seasonings; cook for 10 minutes.
Place chicken in casserole and pour in sauce, adding fat in which chicken was browned.
Cover with brown paper and lid; bake in a 350° oven for 45 minutes. When ready to serve, add blanched and browned almonds and well-washed currants.
Serve with rice.
Thaw peas and drain well.
Add rest of ingredients and season to taste with salt and pepper.
Toss lightly and refrigerate.
Using meat grinder on coarse, grind meats, peppers, garlic and onion.
Add olive oil to the bottom of 5-quart Dutch oven.
Brown meats; pour off excess fat.
Add rest of ingredients.
Bring to a boil.
Stir occasionally, then cover.

i: 3 (12 oz.) cans frozen orange juice, diluted
d: Combine orange juice and ginger ale and pour over orange sherbet.
Float slices of orange and sprigs of mint in punch bowl for garnishes.
Yield:
50 (8-ounce) servings.

i: 1 1/2 c. canned tart cherries (reserve the juice)
d: Cream together butter and 1 cup sugar.
Sift together flour, baking powder and salt.
Add alternately with milk to creamed mixture.
Pour into greased casserole dish.
Combine cherries with remaining 1/4 cup sugar and 1/4 cup reserved cherry juice.
Heat cherry mixture and pour over batter.
Trim any excess fat from ribs.
Place in large, shallow baking pan so that ribs are in a single layer.
Measure seasonings and spices and mix together.
Sprinkle generously over ribs on both sides.
Slice onion over top and pour tomato sauce over all.
Add water and sprinkle sugar over sauce.
Heat on medium, stirring occasionally.
Top with cheese. Serves 4 to 6.

i: 1 (16 oz.) jar thick 'n chunky salsa (mild, medium or hot)
d: Brown ground beef; pour off excess fat.
Add taco seasoning mix and water.
Put 3 to 4 tablespoons taco meat in each flour tortilla.
Roll and place seam down in a glass casserole.
Top with thick 'n chunky salsa, Cheez Whiz and Colby cheese. Microwave 6 to 8 minutes, until cheese has completely melted. Serve with cucumbers and sliced tomatoes.
Mix first 5 ingredients and add next 4 ingredients.
Using a 9 x 9-inch pan, bake in 350° oven for about 45 minutes.
Top with Cool Whip.
Serves 9.
Brown hamburger and onion; drain off grease.
Add remaining ingredients.
Simmer 15 minutes.

i: chopped vegetables of your choice (cauliflower, tomatoes, peppers and onions)
d: Pat dough in a round pizza pan.
Bake at 350° for 10 minutes. Cool.
Add Ranch dressing to sour cream and season to taste with Italian seasoning.	Spread on baked crescent rolls.
Layer chopped veggies on top of dressing mixture.
Chill.
Clean roasting chicken, removing insides.
Salt and pepper (I use lemon pepper).
Stuff the bird with the lemon, orange, onion and garlic.
Bake at 450° for 1 1/2 hours.
If bird starts to get too brown, you can cover it with foil.

i: 6 (4 oz.) skinned, boned chicken breast halves
d: Combine breadcrumbs and rind, stirring well.
Combine lemon juice, mustard and lime juice; brush mixture over both sides of chicken.
Dredge chicken in breadcrumb mixture.
Place chicken in a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish coated with cooking spray; sprinkle with any remaining breadcrumb mixture.
Bake, uncovered, at 375° for 15 minutes.
Turn chicken and bake an additional 15 minutes or until done.
Mix all the ingredients and pour into a greased pan. Bake at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes.
Preheat oven to 375°.
Peel and slice sweet potatoes.
In a big ovenproof dish, place 1/4 margarine on bottom.
Layer 3 pork chops, layer sweet potatoes and layer 1/4 margarine.
Sprinkle half the sugar over top.
Repeat the pork chops, sweet potatoes, margarine and sugar.

i: 3 to 4 large potatoes, peeled and sliced thin
1/2 pkg. crisp-fried, smoked bacon (reserve bacon grease)
1 medium onion, chopped fine or 6 to 8 spring onions
d: Into heated frying pan, add 2 tablespoons of bacon grease and place potato slices.

Cover and cook until potatoes are done, stirring often, then remove and place potatoes in mixing bowl and add bacon bits, onion, eggs, vinegar and salt and pepper to taste. Add a small amount of bacon grease.

i: 1/2 lb. fresh mushrooms, cleaned and sliced
d: Split breasts in half (can leave whole), remove bone and dust with flour.
Heat oil and butter; add garlic, brown and discard. Add chicken.
Brown thoroughly.
Season with salt and pepper.
Add Prosciutto, rosemary, bay leaf and mushrooms.
Cook 3 to 4 minutes to saute mushrooms.
Beat egg and water together.
Gradually beat in enough flour so mixture is smooth and holds together.
Drop by teaspoon into boiling soup or stew and boil about 5 minutes.
Blend shortening, brown and white sugar in bowl.
Beat in vanilla and eggs.
Sift remaining dry ingredients together, add to sugar, egg mixture blending well.
Stir in M&M's.
Bake at 325° for 10 minutes.
Brown beef, onions and celery (I usually begin beef in a small amount of water and after awhile, pour away drippings).
To the beef, add onions and celery; continue browning.
Mix in casserole dish the soups, water, rice and soy sauce.
Add the browned beef mixture and stir.
Bake for 1 hour at 350°.
Place topping of dry chow mein noodles on each serving.

i: 1/2 to 1 envelope original Ranch dressing mix
d: In a bowl, combine cabbage, carrots and green pepper.
Chill. Mix Miracle Whip, sugar, vinegar, Ranch dressing mix, celery seed and salt.
Toss with vegetables.
Boil sugar and water until forms hard ball in cold water. Place Marshmallow Creme in bowl.
Pour sugar and water mixture over creme.
Beat until stiff; feed in nuts and vanilla.
Drop from teaspoon onto wax paper.

i: 1 small red pepper or 1 carrot, shredded
d: Mix all ingredients together.
Let stand for 2 hours.

i: 1 (16 oz.) box Ronzoni curly edge lasagna
2 c. (8 oz.) Mozzarella cheese, diced or shredded
d: Brown beef or sausage, cut in small pieces, in oil in 3-quart saucepan; drain.
Add spaghetti sauce and simmer 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Cook lasagna; drain.
(Separate lasagna and lay out flat on wax paper or aluminum foil to keep pieces from sticking together as they cool.) Spread about half cup meat sauce on bottom of 13 x 9-inch baking dish.
Cover with layer of lasagna.
Add some Mozzarella and Ricotta cheeses and cover with sauce.
Mix all ingredients and place them on the steak for 45 minutes to 2 hours. Grill and enjoy.
Heat a large pot.
Cook carrots, onion, celery and garlic in 1 cup stock until tender.
Add remaining ingredients, except beans, macaroni, Tabasco and cheese.
Cook until vegetables are tender. In blender or processor, process 1/2 cup garbanzo beans and 1/2 cup kidney beans until pureed.
Set aside.
Add remaining ingredients; simmer 10 minutes.

i: 1 (1 lb.) can sliced peaches or halves
d: Cut peaches and pears into bite size pieces.
Combine fruits in 2-quart casserole.
Blend margarine, brown sugar and curry powder.
Spoon over fruit.
Bake at 325° for 30 minutes.
Serve warm.

t: Baked Bluefish Fillets With Creole Sauce
d: Mix the first 5 ingredients together in a 2-quart pan and saute together for 10 minutes over medium heat.
Add remaining ingredients, all but bluefish fillets and mix well over medium-low heat until vegetables are fully cooked, about 15 minutes.
While this is cooking, bake bluefish fillets in a 400° oven until fully cooked, approximately 30 minutes.
When fish is cooked, portion on plates and top with Creole sauce.
Spray 9 x 13 inch pan.
Place layers of tomato, onion, zucchini and cheese.
Sprinkle with salt and pepper and liberally with Italian seasoning.
Sprinkle about 1/2 cup crushed Ritz over all.
Make second layer same as first, beginning with tomato and ending with cheese, top with more seasoning and crushed Ritz. Cover with foil and bake 30 minutes at 350°.
Remove foil and bake another 30 minutes.

t: Spiced Apple Rings Or Lime Pickles(Takes 3 Days To Make)
d: Scoop center out of cucumbers. Cut into 1/2-inch rings.
Add lime and enough water to cover all the cukes.
Let stand 24 hours in plastic container. Drain and rinse well.
Cover with ice cubes. Let stand 3 hours.
Drain well.
Mix ingredients together.
Serve with raw celery, carrots, cauliflower and broccoli.
Cream shortening with 1 cup sugar until light and fluffy.
Add egg and molasses; mix well.
In separate bowl combine flour, soda, cinnamon, salt, ginger and cloves.
Add about 1/4 of dry mixture at a time to creamed mixture.
Mix until smooth after each addition.
Chill 1 hour.

i: 1 (16 oz.) can peas and carrots, drained
d: In medium saucepan, saute onion and margarine.
Stir in flour, salt and pepper.
Whisk in 2 1/2 cups milk.
Cook until slightly thickened.
Add next four ingredients.
Divide into 4 (8 ounce) baking dishes.
Mix ingredients.
Bake at 350° in oblong pan until cake falls (or about 45 minutes).
Cool and cut into squares.
Butter sides and bottom of 13 x 9-inch dish.
Boil and debone chicken.
Chop in bite size pieces.
Steam broccoli until crisp and lay broccoli in bottom of dish.
Spread chicken on top.
Place American cheese evenly on top of chicken.
Marinate chicken wings anywhere from 6 to 24 hours.
Remove from marinade and bake in a 350° oven for 60 minutes.
You can also use marinade for pork or beef.
Delicious!
Yield:
24 wings.
Mix all ingredients together well.
Roll into long log roll and chill in refrigerator for about 12 hours.
Slice and serve.
Boil together 3 minutes the first 4 ingredients. Remove from heat. Add the oatmeal, vanilla and nuts or coconut.
Mix well. Drop by teaspoon on wax paper.
Layer first 6 ingredients as listed in 9 x 13-inch pan.
Mix dressing, mayonnaise and sour cream and spread on top of salad. Sprinkle with shredded cheese.
Cover and refrigerate.
Can be made the night before.

i: 2 pkg. Oriental sesame salad dressing package from Hidden Valley
Minute rice (for as many people needed)
d: Cut chicken into bite-size pieces in a frying pan, cook chicken with the lemon juice and water.
When the chicken is done, add the 2 packages of dressing mix and simmer for at least 5 minutes.
Cook rice as directed.
Serve the chicken mixture over rice.
Wash and slice the cucumber finely.
Sprinkle with salt.
Place in a marinade previously prepared by boiling the vinegar, water, sugar and pepper together and allowing to cool.
Leave in a cold place for about two hours before serving.

i: 2 (40 oz.) jars your favorite spaghetti sauce
d: Brown meat and drain well.
Mix in 1 1/2 jars of spaghetti sauce and simmer about 2 minutes.
Save the other 1/2 jar for breadsticks.
Cook noodles as directed on package.
Drain and cover with cold water until ready to use.
Mix Cheddar and Mozzarella cheese together in large bowl.
In a medium saucepan, cook and stir almonds in hot oil until light brown; remove and drain on paper towels.
Add onion and garlic to saucepan.
Cook and stir until onion is tender, but not brown.
Stir in uncooked rice, cardamom, ginger, red pepper, cinnamon, cumin and nutmeg.
Cook and stir about 3 minutes to brown the rice.
Add chicken broth.

In a bowl, flake salmon. Mix in remaining ingredients except for olive oil. Take a handful of the mixture and form into a ball. In a frying pan, warm olive oil. Place ball into oil and let brown on one side. Roll ball over and flatten with spatula. Cook until dark brown on both sides.
Sift flour, baking powder and salt together.
Cream together butter and sugar.
Add egg yolks and vanilla.
Beat until light and fluffy.
Mix in sifted dry ingredients.
Shape into 1-inch balls.
Skin chicken breasts (optional for low-fat).
Place into a baking dish the breasts.
Add 1/3 cup water and place into a preheated oven at 325°.
After 30 minutes, add soy sauce.
Continue to cook for another 30 minutes, then add chopped pepper and sliced mushrooms.
Continue to cook an additional 20 to 30 minutes.
Melt margarine, cover bottom of 9 x 13-inch pan with graham crackers (single layer).
Drizzle margarine over graham crackers. Then, in layers, add coconut, chocolate chips, butterscotch chips, drizzle milk over and add pecans.
Bake at 350° for 20 minutes. Let cool and cut into squares.
Do not overcook!

i: salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
d: Saute in a deep skillet, preferably Teflon-coated, first 4 ingredients.
When the vegetables are al dente, make a white sauce with the oleo in the skillet:
add flour, stir and heat until bubbly.
Let cool a little, then remove from heat and add all at once, milk.
Stir well over low heat and add scallops, parsley, curry powder, salt, pepper and cayenne pepper.
Heat slowly and stir until sauce thickens and scallops are done.
Simmer all of the ingredients.
Strain into warmed mugs and top
with
a
dash of nutmeg.
This recipe can be doubled many times, according to the number of guests present.
Saute beef and onions until brown. Combine the rest of the ingredients with beef. Put in a 2-quart casserole dish. Bake at 400 degrees for 30 minutes. Serves 8 to 10 people.

i: 1 pkg. soft tofu, drained and rinsed
d: Mix ingredients; form into patties (flat).
Cook in frypan with small amount of oil.
Serve warm.

t: Italian Sauteed Chicken With Italian Mushroom Rice
i: 6 chicken breast halves, skinned and boned
d: Cut each chicken breast into 3 long strips.
Dredge strips with mixture of flour and white pepper.
In a heavy skillet, melt 1 stick butter and add olive oil, garlic, rosemary and basil.
Saute chicken in this mixture and lightly brown.
Add wine and cook on low heat until 1/2 of wine has been reduced.
This may be done 3 or 4 hours before serving.
Stir together the first 3 ingredients, then beat egg whites until stiff.
Fold into the first mixture.
This is a great batter for chicken!

i: 2 1/2 lb. shrimp, peeled and deveined (may substitute orange roughly or any firm fish, cut in 2-inch strips)
3 jars mild picante sauce (may use hot depending on your heat threshold!)
d: Mix all ingredients, except shrimp or fish and picante sauce. Bake in a 325° oven for 30 minutes. Stir in shrimp or fish. Continue to bake for another 30 minutes. Add picante sauce. Bake for 30 more minutes. Serve with rice and toasted French bread.

i: 1 pt. sour cream (low-fat is OK too)
d: Gently melt chocolate chips.
Stir in sour cream until fully blended.
Spread mixture over tortillas in layers. Chill. (Once it has set up, I prefer to serve it at room temperature, but refrigerate any leftover.)
Put beans in bottom of 9 x 13-inch pan.
Brown beef with onion and garlic.
Drain. Put over beans.
Sprinkle cheese over meat and salsa on top.
Bake at 350° until hot and cheese melts.
Cut lettuce and tomatoes and top with sour cream.

i: 1 can cream of chicken or chicken mushroom soup
bite size pieces of 4 to 6 chicken thighs or any other chicken parts you wish to use
d: Mix all together; pour into a 7 x 12-inch casserole.
Sprinkle with pepper and paprika.
Bake approximately 1 hour and 15 minutes at 350°.
Drain pineapple and save juice.
Put pineapple in a butter dish.
Mix sugar and flour and sprinkle over pineapple and add 1 cup pineapple juice.
Pour over.
Put a cup of grated cheese.
Melt 1 stick of butter and 2 tablespoons of brown sugar and stir up crushed crackers (Ritz).

i: 2 c. cut-up fully cooked smoked ham
1 c. shredded natural Swiss cheese (about 4 oz.)
1/3 c. chopped green onions or chopped onion
d: The pie that does the impossible by making its own crust. Makes 6 servings.
Beat egg in mixer bowl until foamy.
Add sugar; beat well. Add apples, pecans, flour, baking powder and vanilla; mix well. Pour into greased and floured 4 x 8-inch loaf pan.
Bake at 300° for 35 minutes.
Remove to wire rack to cool.
Serve warm with whipped cream.
Cake will rise to top of pan and fall, leaving a crusty top.

i: 1 (15 oz.) can black beans, rinsed and drained
1 (14 oz.) can stew ready cut salsa
1/4 c. Vintage lite red wine vinegar dressing
d: In a large bowl, combine first 5 ingredients; mix well.
Blend the salsa with red vinegar dressing;
pour over salad and toss well.
Cover and chill.
Serves 8.
Grease 9 x 13-inch baking dish.
Place noodles in a layer in the bottom of the dish.
Layer sausage on top, then layer spinach and cheese.
Combine eggs, milk and salt.
Pour over layers.
Bake 1 hour at 350°.

i: 1/2 c. sugar or as sweet as desired
1/2 c. vinegar or equal to amount of sugar used
d: Boil potatoes in skins. Peel and slice thin. Cook bacon until crisp, then cook onion in bacon fat. Mix in flour, sugar, salt and pepper. Stir water in gradually. Add vinegar gradually. Cook and stir until it boils for 1 minute. Pour over potatoes. Add the bacon. Cover and let stand until ready to serve.

i: 6 chicken thighs (or equivalent in other parts)
d: Place chicken in a baking dish, about 8 x 8-inch).
Place sliced Mozzarella cheese over chicken.
Pour sauce over all.
Top with Parmesan cheese.
Bake at 350° until chicken is cooked, about 45 minutes.
Serve with garlic bread and/or spaghetti noodles.
Combine the flour, baking powder, baking soda and salt; set aside.
In a mixing bowl, beat eggs until light.
Add buttermilk; mix well.
Add dry ingredients and beat until batter is smooth. Stir in butter.
Pour about 3/4 cup batter onto a lightly greased preheated waffle iron.
Sprinkle with a few pecans.
Grind, dice or shred tomatoes.
Place all vegetables in a large bowl; sprinkle with salt.
Let set for 1 hour.
Drain.
Brown ground chuck, onion and green pepper and drain fat.
Mix in taco seasoning, refried beans and sweet peas.
Add salsa to desired consistency.
Place meat mixture (approximately 2 tablespoons) in a flour tortilla.
Roll; place into cake pan. Fold side down.
Fill cake pan with enchiladas.
Mix flour, sugar, baking powder, soda and salt together.
Cut in margarine, dissolve yeast in warm water.
Stir into buttermilk and add to dry mixture.
Cover and chill.
Cover beans with cold water; let stand overnight.
In the morning, drain and cook with fresh water to which salt and soda have been added.
Let the beans simmer until tender.
Place onions and honey in the bottom of a casserole and cover them with the drained beans.
Add seasonings.
Rub the lid of the casserole thoroughly with the halved clove of garlic.
Slice okra into 1/4-inch slices.
Wash okra in cold water. Mix cornmeal, flour, salt and pepper together.
Roll okra in cornmeal mix.
Fry in hot skillet for 10 minutes until golden brown.
Drain on paper towel.
Serves 6.

i: 2 c. onions, cook and drain well
8 c. cucumbers, let stand in salt water for 1 hour.
d: Boil
for
20 minutes.
Cook cauliflower before you add to the rest of ingredients.
Mix together eggs, brown sugar, oil and vanilla.
Add flour, baking powder, salt and chocolate chips and mix well.
Put in greased 9 x 13-inch pan.
Bake at 350° for 25 minutes.
Place ingredients into a food processor and blend well.
Serve with Labanese bread.

t: Grandma Charron'S Rice And Artichoke Salad
i: 2 (6 oz.) jars marinated artichokes (use the oil too)
d: Cook rice and add onions, pepper, celery, artichokes, olives, curry and mayonnaise. Blend well. Add pecans last.
Serve warm temperature or cooled.
Combine margarine and crumbs.
Press firmly into bottom of a 9-inch square or 12 x 7-inch baking dish.
In a large mixing bowl, dissolve gelatin in boiling water.
Stir in Eagle Brand milk, pineapple and lime juice.
Fold in marshmallows and whipped cream. Pour into prepared dish.
Chill 2 hours or until set.
Wash and break lettuce into bite-size pieces.
Dice celery. Chop onion fine.
Peel cucumbers (if needed); dice.
Mix together just before serving.
Add dressing.
Toss to coat.
Dredge beef in flour and brown in Dutch oven.
Add remaining ingredients and cook slowly on low heat for about 3 hours. Thicken gravy if necessary.
Cook the sweet potatoes in boiling water 20 to 25 minutes or until tender.
Drain and cool.
Peel the potatoes and mash. Combine mashed sweet potatoes, frosting, vanilla and cinnamon; mix well. Spoon into a greased 12 x 8 x 2-inch baking dish. Bake, uncovered, at 350° for 18 minutes.
Sprinkle with marshmallows. Continue baking 5 minutes or until thoroughly heated.
Yields 6 to 8 servings.
Brown meat and drain grease.
Add bell pepper and seasonings. Then add tomatoes, paste and soup.
Cover and simmer 1 hour.
Keep adding water and stir.
Remove crust from bread and dip slices in wine vinegar. Combine all ingredients in a blender.
Add enough water to fill blender and liquefy.
(If there is not enough room in the blender, add 1 to 2 cups of water and blend in 2 batches.)
Soup should not be watery.
Chill well before serving.
Mix ingredients together and spread over French bread.
Bake at 375° for 10 to 12 minutes.
Serve warm.

i: 1 lb. bulk sausage, crumbled and cooked
1/4 lb. fresh mushrooms, sliced (or small can)
1 can (10 3/4 oz.) cream of mushroom soup
d: In a 9 x 12-inch casserole dish, layer the sausage, croutons, cheese and mushrooms.
Mix the milk, eggs, soup and mustard in a bowl and beat together until will blended.
Pour milk mixture over layers in casserole and refrigerate overnight or up to 24 hours. Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Let stand 15 minutes before serving. Serves 10 to 12.
Brown beef; stir to crumble.
Drain.
Put beef into 8-inch baking dish.
Top with sauce, Mozzarella cheese and sliced mushrooms.
Combine the biscuit mix, milk and eggs; beat until smooth, then evenly pour over casserole.
Bake at 400° for 30 to 35 minutes.
Mix all ingredients together.
Chill in refrigerator for 3 to 4 hours.
Serve with salsa chips.
Wash, peel and slice cucumbers and onions.
Cover them with salt and let stand overnight.
Bring vinegar and salt to a boil. Sift together mustard, turmeric and cornstarch.
Mix with a little cold vinegar and add to vinegar and sugar.
Drain cucumbers and onions from salt water.
Put in vinegar solution and bring to a boil.
Skin chicken.
Slash chicken flesh.
Combine ingredients in bowl.
Add chicken, turning to coat.
Cover and refrigerate several hours or overnight, turning occasionally.
Let chicken stand 1 hour at room temperature before cooking.

i: 1 to 2 Tbsp. red onion, thinly sliced
d: Whisk together vinegar, oil, lemon juice, salt and pepper. Add cucumbers, onions and tomatoes; mix well. Add basil; stir. Add Feta and stir. Feta will break up into smaller pieces when you stir the salad. Makes 4 to 6 servings.
Cut chicken into pieces for frying.
Melt butter in hot frying pan.
Add chicken and cook 10 minutes.
Add liver and gizzards and cook 10 minutes longer.
Add onions, curry, salt, wine and enough boiling water to cover.
Cook until chicken is tender.
Cook macaroni according to package directions.
Rinse and set aside.
Brown ground beef in skillet.
Drain.
Add Miracle Whip and spaghetti sauce.
Stir in noodles.

t: Avgolemono (Greek)(Egg And Lemon Soup With Rice)
i: 1/4 c. lemon juice or to taste
d: Boil broth, then add rice and cook until tender.
Meanwhile, mix egg yolks, lemon juice, salt and pepper and mix well.
Then add part of the chicken broth to the egg yolk mixture slowly, stirring constantly.
Then return the mixture into the remaining soup slowly, stirring constantly.
Crush bananas in large bowl.
Mix in all other ingredients. Pour into lightly greased loaf pan.
Bake 1 hour at 350° or until knife comes out clean.
Mix all of the above together and refrigerate.
Peel and quarter ripe cucumbers.
Remove seeds and put in large pan with 1/2 cup salt.
Let set overnight.
Drain and pour hot water over them and drain again.
Cook until tender in following mixture:
brown sugar, white sugar, vinegar, hot water, mustard seed and turmeric.
Soak the fingers in buttermilk for several hours; drain and salt and pepper to taste.
Heat about 1-inch of oil in frying pan. Shake the fingers in flour.
Place in frypan in hot oil on medium heat for several minutes, turning until all sides are brown.

i: 4 oz. piece smokes streaky bacon, cubed
d: Wash and dry chicken; cut up.
Melt butter in a pan with the oil.
Fry the bacon cubes until golden brown.
Remove bacon and drain.
Fry the chicken until brown, turning once.
Put the bacon and chicken into an ovenproof casserole.

i: 1 (1 1/2 oz.) can tomatoes and jalapeno peppers, drained
d: Combine all ingredients in 2-quart glass measure or microproof bowl.
Cook 5 to 7 minutes on High, or until cheese is melted, stirring once during cooking time.
Blend thoroughly with electric mixer.
Pour into serving bowl.
Serve with corn chips or dipping-style potato chips or raw, fresh vegetables.
Makes about 4 cups.

i: 1 small can chopped green chiles (hot or mild)
1/2 c. Cheddar or Monterey Jack cheese
d: Add or take away portions according to taste.
Combine
first
seven
ingredients then stir in boiling water. Add
flour
and mix.
Sprinkle buttered 10 x 15-inch pan with 1 cup nuts and half the chocolate chips.
In heavy saucepan, melt butter and sugar. Using a candy thermometer, heat mixture to 300°.
(This takes approximately 15 minutes.)
Pour this over mixture in pan. Sprinkle with remaining chocolate chips and let set until chocolate is soft. Spread evenly over candy and top with remaining pecans.
Serves 24.
Preheat oven to 250°.
Place crackers in large shallow baking pan.
In a small bowl, combine butter, taco seasoning mix, Worcestershire and salt.
Pour butter mixture over crackers; stir well.
Bake for 1 hour, stirring every 15 minutes.
Pour onto waxed paper to cool.
With a wire whisk mix cocoa, sugar, flour.
Add milk slowly, stirring constantly.
Cook over medium heat until thickened. Lower heat to low and add butter.
Allow to melt without stirring. After butter has melted stir.
Serve over hot biscuits while gravy is hot.
Mix first three ingredients together, add milk, cinnamon and cloves; mix well.
Pour in pie shell. Bake at 375° for 40 minutes.
*Can also use blueberries or cherries.
Toss pecans in bowl with oil until well coated.
Spread on cookie sheet; salt to taste.
Cook at 400° for about 10 minutes. Check; should be a little brown.
Remove from oven and cool.
Pack into containers.

i: tortillas - corn or flour, your choice
d: Beat: 6 eggs, add salt and pepper.
Fry as pancakes.
Mix all ingredients together.
Shake well.
Serve over spinach salad.
Sift together flour and sugar.
Combine butter, cocoa and water and bring to a rapid boil.
Pour over sugar and flour.
Mix well.
Add other ingredients and pour into greased 11 x 16-inch pan.
Bake at 400° for 20 minutes.
Make the same way as biscuits, but roll the dough into an oblong shape.
Spread top with melted margarine.
Sprinkle with generous amounts of cinnamon, sugar and brown sugar.
Roll jelly roll fashion and cut into 1/2-inch slices.
Put in greased pan; adding 1 tablespoon water to bottom.
Bake at 400° for 15 to 20 minutes; turn out onto plate.

i: 4 slices bacon, crisp cooked and crumbled
d: Mix dressing and sugar in a large bowl.
Add remaining ingredients.
Mix thoroughly but lightly.
Refrigerate.
Makes 10 servings.

i: 1/2 eggshell of half and half or Milnot
d: Add flour, 1/2 to 1 cup at a time, saving the last for kneading mixture.
Let set for 1 hour, then cut into pieces. Kneading with some of the flour, roll with noodle maker.
When dry enough, cut with noodle maker.
Brown the ground beef.
Drain and rinse meat. Salt and sprinkle on chili powder.
In Dutch oven, layer meat, sliced onions, potatoes and carrots.
Finish with onions.
Pour the tomatoes over layers. Bake, uncovered, until potatoes and carrots test tender. Great with cornbread!
Remove tops and seeds from peppers.
In a large kettle, bring water to a boil; cook peppers for 4 minutes.
Remove and drain. Cook rice according to package directions.
Peel and cut potatoes into quarters.
Boil in water 3 to 5 minutes; drain.
Mix remaining ingredients (not lemon) together. Place with potatoes in a buttered baking dish.
Drizzle more olive oil over top.
Mix all ingredients together in saucepan; cook until thick.
Mix first 4 ingredients.
Dip each piece of chicken in sauce. Place chicken, skin side down (you can also use skinless chicken), in 13 x 9 x 2-inch dish (sprayed with Pam).
Bake, uncovered, in 350° oven for about 1 hour.
Baste during cooking. Pour remaining sauce over chicken prior to baking.

i: 2 (5 oz.) cans chunk white chicken, drained
d: Spread 1/2 can enchilada sauce in 3-quart oblong baking dish. Set aside. Combine chicken, 1 cup cheese, chilies and onion. Spread about 1/3 cup mixture along one side of tortilla. Roll jelly roll fashion and place seam side down in dish. Pour remaining 1/2 can of enchilada sauce over enchiladas. Sprinkle remaining 1/2 cup of cheese on top. Cover with foil and bake at 350° for 25 minutes.
Cook noodles, then drain them.
Layer with all ingredients, starting with small amount of sauce.
Put cheese on top of each layer.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Mix all together and bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

i: 5 c. rhubarb, cut in small pieces
1 pkg. yellow cake mix, prepared according to directions
d: Place rhubarb in a well-greased 9 x 13-inch baking dish. Sprinkle Jell-O over rhubarb, then follow with the sugar and marshmallows.
Pour prepared cake mix over the top and bake at 350° for an hour or until tester comes out clean.
Wait about 5 minutes and then take a sharp knife and cut carefully around the outside of cake.
Turn cake out onto a heavy piece of aluminum foil which has been placed on a cookie sheet.
Be sure cookie sheet is large enough.
Foil must be long and wide enough.

i: 1 frozen loaf bread, let thaw overnight
d: Take thawed bread and roll out onto cookie sheet.
Set on door of oven preheated to 350° and let it rise for 15 to 20 minutes (until puffy).
Lay the slices of Provolone over entire loaf and then layer the pepperoni slices.
Roll bread in a jelly-like roll. Take egg white and baste bread.
Sprinkle sesame seed on top. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
In large bowl, combine the rice, green pepper, onion, celery, radishes and pickle relish.
Mix well.
In small bowl, combine mayonnaise and spices. Stir to blend flavors. Add the mayonnaise to the rice mixture and mix well.
Chill for several hours. Serves 8.

i: 1 (12 oz.) can frozen orange juice concentrate
d: In a large bowl, dissolve gelatin in boiling water.
Add 1 1/2 cups of sugar and stir until dissolved.
Add orange juice and lemonade, prepared according to directions on cans and pineapple juice.
Stir in remaining 1 1/2 cups of sugar and mix well.
Just before serving, add ginger ale.
Makes 50 cups.

Form ice cream into 4 balls. Place in a baking pan and freeze solid, about 2 hours. Mix cereal, sugar and cinnamon. Divide mix equally between 2 pie plates. Beat eggs with water. Roll each ice cream ball into crushed corn flakes, make sure corn flakes embed into ice cream balls. Dip ball in egg wash. Roll ice cream ball in second pan of crushed corn flakes, press corn flakes into ice cream again. Freeze ice cream for 6 hours. Shape tortilla into an hourglass form (with narrow waist) by cutting off curved slice from two opposite sides. One end will be the base for the ice cream, the other end is for decoration. Heat oil in wok or deep fryer. Place tortilla between two ladles or salad fork. Gently place tortilla in hot oil. Fry until crisp. Drain oil and sprinkle with cinnamon/sugar mix. Deep fry ice cream balls for 30-45 seconds. Place each fried tortilla in a large stemmed glass, with fan part of tortilla standing vertically above the glass. Set fried ice cream in base of tortilla. Top with whipped cream and a cherry. Makes 4 servings.
Cook macaroni and drain.
Add onion, pepper, celery, tomatoes, olives, salt and pepper.
Toss.
Cut meat and cheese in strips. Add dressing and toss.
Preheat oven to 400°.
Mix bisquick, milk, brown sugar, oleo and nutmeg.
Spread in an ungreased square pan (9 x 9 x 2-inch). Drain peaches, reserving 1 cup syrup.
Arrange peaches on mixture in pan.
Pour reserved syrup over peaches.
Bake until golden brown, about 40 minutes.
Drain pineapple; reserve juice.
Combine reserved juice and next 3 ingredients; set aside.
In a small dish, pour marinade over tenderloins.
Cover and refrigerate for 24 hours.
Cook on grill about 20 to 30 minutes on each side.
Grill pineapple slices for about 5 minutes and place on top of the tenderloins.
You will need 8 paper liners.

i: 2 cans water from whole tomato cans
d: Mix together.
Simmer for approximately 2 hours.
Add 3/4 cup flour to thicken.

i: 3 packets instant grits, cooked as directed
d: While grits are still hot, add cheeses and margarine.
Allow to cool; add eggs and milk.
Pour into baking dish.
Bake at 325° for 30 to 45 minutes.
This dish can be made ahead of time, frozen and reheated.
Serves 6 to 8.

i: 1 large can whole tomatoes, drain juice off and save
d: Mix hamburger, bread crumbs, Parmesan cheese, juice of tomatoes, egg, 1/2 teaspoon oregano and seasoning salt.
Scoop up a tablespoon of meat mixture into balls and brown in skillet.
Mix tomato sauce, cut up the whole tomatoes, diced up celery, 1/2 teaspoon seasoning salt, 1/8 tsp.
oregano, 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder and 1/8 teaspoon Italian seasoning and simmer.
When meatballs are browned, drain on paper towel, then mix into sauce and simmer for about 20 minutes.
Boil water and prepare noodles. Serve meatballs over noodles.
Clean and cut chicken in half or quarters.
Put shredded onion on top.
Mix juice from lime, oil, small amount of vinegar, bay leaves, salt and pepper.
Pour over chicken and marinate for several hours.
About an hour or so before serving, have a hot charcoal fire with grill ready.
Grill chicken, browning on all sides until almost blackened.
Brown ground beef.
Drain fat.
Add salsa and taco seasoning. Heat thoroughly over medium heat.
In casserole dish, spread even layer of smashed tortilla chips, then layer of meat.
Next, spread tomatoes evenly and sprinkle with Cheddar cheese.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

i: 1 pkg. Good Seasons cheese garlic dressing, mixed with oil and vinegar
d: Mix the first five ingredients together.
Brown hamburger. Drain and mix with taco seasoning mix and 1/2 cup water.
Simmer 5 minutes.
Let cool, then add to salad.
Preheat oven to 400°.
Shake chicken in bag of flour and seasonings. Line 9 x 13-inch glass baking dish with foil; add margarine and place dish in oven until margarine sizzles.
Place chicken in dish, skin down.
Bake for 30 minutes; turn and bake another 30 minutes.
Do not cover.
Yields 4 to 6 servings.

t: Carp Curry With Coriander And Tomato
i: 1 tsp. chili powder or to taste
2 lb. carp, cut into 8 steaks 1/2-inch thick
d: This carp curry, which freezes well, can also be made with catfish, bass, trout, perch, etc.
Preheat oven to 425°.
Prepare spinach according to package and drain well. Combine eggs, cheese, milk and onions. Add salt, celery salt, basil and garlic. Combine with spinach.
Pour into unbaked pie crust. Top by overlapping sliced tomatoes and crumbled bacon strips.
Cover edge of crust with tinfoil to prevent it from getting too brown.
Bake at 425° for 15 minutes. Reduce heat to 350° and bake for 25 to 30 minutes.
Let stand 10 minutes before cutting.

t: Peppered Dry Rub(For Steaks, Chicken, Roast, Pork)
d: Mix all seasoning in a zip lock bag. Wash and pat dry your meat of choice, place meat on a working tray or pan, you will need to be able to cover this. Poke holes in the meat on both sides, drizzle olive oil on meat and with a pastry brush spread it all over to coat. Next sprinkle the seasoning mix on the meat, making sure that the

entire meat is covered. Do this on both sides. Cover with wrap and place in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour. Cook meat on the grill for best results, but I have used this in the oven and it was really good.

t: Pasta With Spinach, Garlic And Oil
d: Stem, wash, drain and coarsely chop spinach leaves.
Set aside.
Cook pasta as package label directs until al dente; drain. In pan, combine oil and garlic.
Saute over medium heat about 2 minutes, or until garlic is golden.
Add spinach, pepper flakes and salt; saute until spinach wilts.
Remove from heat.
In a pitcher, dissolve cherry Jell-O in boiling water.
Stir in lemonade concentrate.
Add cold water and cranberry juice; chill thoroughly.
Just before serving, add ginger ale.
Mix gently.
Makes 16 (6-ounce) servings.
Soak raisins in boiling water for 5 minutes.
Drain; add all other ingredients.
Cover and chill.
Cut spareribs into servings.
Sprinkle with salt and pepper. Place in a roaster pan and cover with onions.
Combine remaining ingredients and pour over meat.
Cover and bake 1 1/2 hours at 350°.
Baste occasionally turning ribs once or twice.
Remove cover from roaster during the last 15 minutes of baking to brown spareribs.
Mix all ingredients together as listed.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Mix together egg yolks, sugar, vanilla, almond and baking powder.
Set aside.
Whip egg whites until very stiff and fold into yolk mixture.
Also fold in graham cracker crumbs and nuts. Pour into greased 9 x 13-inch cake pan.
Bake at 325° for 30 minutes.
Cool.
Melt the butter in a skillet and saut the bananas about 5 minutes turning them frequently.
Remove from the pan and set aside.
Beat the egg yolks with the salt, cayenne pepper and milk. Beat the egg whites until stiff and fold the yolks and whites together.
Transfer to a buttered 7 x 10 inch shallow casserole and arrange the banana slices on top.
Bake in a 350 degree oven for 20 minutes or until lightly browned.
Sprinkle with parsley and serve from the casserole.
Dip chicken breasts in egg, then roll in bread crumbs/Parmesan cheese mixture.
Place in microwave-safe baking dish with 1 tablespoon oil.
Cook on High for 2 minutes each side.
Beat all ingredients together except peanut butter until mixture is very smooth, then add peanut butter and beat until smooth and well blended.
Roll into balls and roll in sifted powdered sugar.
Refrigerate.
Makes about 100.
Mix hamburger, onion, soups, bean sprouts, soy sauce, rice and celery.
Combine ingredients except noodles.
Sprinkle noodles over top.
Bake 1 hour at 350°.
Debone chicken where only skin, drumstick and wings are left. Ground chicken meat.
Mix chicken meat and all ingredients, except boiled chicken and Vienna sausages.
Spoon the mixture into the chicken skin.
Spread it flat and alternately arrange quartered, boiled eggs and Vienna sausage.
When done, spoon in the other half of the mixture.
Close the skin with toothpick.

i: 2/3 c. chile salsa or taco sauce
2 c. grated Cheddar or Jack cheese or combination of both
d: Fry ground beef and onion until brown and crumbly.
Drain well.
In baking dish, mix browned meat and onion with beans, 1/2 the salsa, all seasonings, all chopped chilies and half the cheese.
Smooth out top of mixture and sprinkle with remaining grated cheese.
Drizzle remaining salsa over top of cheese.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

i: 2 (6 oz.) cans salmon, drained and skin and bones discarded
1 c. shredded Mozzarella cheese or Cheddar cheese
1 (6 oz.) pkg. Stove Top stuffing mix
d: Saute onions and green peppers. Combine all ingredients including onions and peppers and mix well. Cover and refrigerate for 10 minutes.

i: 6 pork rib or loin chops (cut about 3/4 to 1-inch thick)
d: Heat oil in a large heavy skillet.
Add garlic and cook until lightly browned.
Season chops with salt and pepper.
Put chops in skillet and brown on both sides.
Add mushrooms, green pepper, sieved tomatoes and wine.
Cover and cook over low heat about 1 hour or until tender.
Cook zucchini, onion, bell pepper, tomatoes, salt and pepper in water until tender.
Drain.
Add slices of cheese and oleo. Put into casserole dish.
Top with croutons and grated cheese. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

i: 1 piece (2-inch) fresh ginger, peeled and coarsely chopped
4 whole boneless, skinless chicken breasts (about 12 oz. each, 3 lb. total), halved
d: Place the ginger and garlic, 2 tablespoons lemon juice and 1/4 cup of the yoghurt in a food processor and puree until smooth. Scrape the puree into a bowl with the remaining 3/4 cup yoghurt, 3 tablespoons oil, the chili powder and 1 teaspoon salt.
Stir well. Cut each halved chicken breast crosswise into 3 pieces.
Add the chicken to the yoghurt mixture and toss to coat.
Marinate at room temperature for 1 hour.
Mix until sugar melts.
Spoon into containers.

i: 3 large potatoes, cooked, peeled and diced
d: Fry mustard seeds in hot oil (be careful they can burn quickly).
Add diced potatoes and fry 5 minutes.
Add tomatoes, green pepper, turmeric, grated ginger, cayenne, salt and coriander leaves.
Cook slowly until tomatoes are done.
Remove from fire and serve hot.

i: 2 tsp. crushed dried red pepper flakes
d: Cut each wing into 2 pieces at joints, leaving tip with blade. Heat oil in large wok.
Brown wings slowly on all sides.
Drain excess fat.
Combine 1/3 cup syrup, garlic, red pepper flakes and ginger.
Pour over turkey wings.
Cover and simmer 20 minutes.

i: 1/2 medium head iceberg lettuce, torn (3 c.)
10 oz. cooked lamb or beef, cut into julienne strips (2 c.)
2/3 c. olive oil or salad oil
d: In a mixing bowl, toss together endive and lettuce. Mound into 6 individual salad plates.
Arrange lamb or beef, tomatoes, Feta cheese, olives and onion atop greens.
Place sugar and egg white in top of double boiler over simmering water.
With electric mixer at high speed, beat constantly until mixture thickens and forms soft peaks, about 5 to 7 minutes.
Remove from heat.
Add yogurt, strawberry puree and vanilla to thickened mixture; stir until combined.
Pour mixture into container of ice cream freezer and process following manufacturer's directions.
Transfer to storage container, cover and freeze.

i: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen Italian style vegetables in seasoned sauce
d: Prepare vegetables according to package directions, omitting water.
Stir in Parmesan cheese.
Spread pizza sauce on each muffin half.
Top with vegetable mixture and Mozzarella cheese. Broil to melt cheese or microwave on High (100%) for 2 minutes. Makes 4 servings.
In a small bowl with fork or wire whisk, mix mayo, anchovy fillets, Parmesan cheese, lemon juice and black pepper.
Mix punch and ginger ale.
Be sure the mixture is cold and add sherbet when ready to serve.

i: 1 lb. ground meat, browned and drained
1 (1 lb.) can kidney beans, rinsed and drained
d: Add salt and pepper to meat.
Toss all ingredients together and serve.
Leftovers taste good too, only not crunchy.
Mix all ingredients with hands.
Make into 1-inch balls. Place on cookie sheet.
Freeze for about 5 minutes.
Remove from cookie sheet and place in plastic bag to freeze for future use. Bake when needed at 350° for about 25 minutes.

i: 1 pkg. butter flavored cake mix (with pudding)
d: Grease 3 (9-inch) layer cake pans; set aside. Prepare cake mix as directed on package, using oil, eggs and juice drained from oranges. Stir in oranges and nuts. Spread evenly in pan. Bake in preheated 325° oven for 25 minutes or until done. Cool. Remove from pan and cool.

i: 3/4 lb. Country Pride boneless skinless chicken breasts or chicken tenders
2 (14 1/2 oz.) cans lower-sodium or regular chicken broth
1 (15 oz.) can garbanzo or kidney beans, rinsed and drained
1 c. loose pack frozen mixed vegetables
1/2 c. cavatelli or other small pasta
1 tsp. dried basil or thyme, crushed
d: Rinse chicken; pat dry.
Cut chicken into bite size pieces. In a large saucepan, stir together broth, beans, mixed vegetables, onion, cavatelli, basil and pepper.
Bring to boiling; reduce heat.
Cover and simmer for 10 minutes.
Cut squash in half, remove seeds and bake cut-side down at 350° for 50 minutes.
In skillet, cook pork, celery and onion until meat is no longer pink and vegetables are tender.
Drain fat.
Stir in salt, curry powder and cinnamon.
Cook 1 minute more.
Stir in applesauce and bread cubes.
Beat eggs and add mayonnaise.
Add other ingredients and mix. Place in greased casserole and bake at 350° until bubbly.
Can be frozen and cooked in frozen state at 350° for 30 minutes.
Cook from 248° to 250° to 260°, depends on weather.
If humid, turn oven on to help dry out room.
Pour syrup into dry egg whites; mix until pulls from bottom of bowl and does not shine so much.
Add coloring and pecans.
Spoon out on wax paper.

i: 1 Tbsp. shortening (do not use butter, margarine or oil)
1 1/2 C. (3 oz.) chow mein noodles, coarsely broken
d: Line cookie sheet with wax paper. Place butterscotch morsels, peanut butter morsels and shortening in medium microwave safe bowl. Microwave on high about one minute. Stir. If necessary, microwave an additional 15 seconds at a time, stirring after each heating, just until morsels are melted and mixture is smooth when stirred. Immediately, add chow mein noodles; stir to coat. Drop mixture by heaping teaspoonfuls onto prepared cookie sheet. Let stand until firm. If necessary, cover and refrigerate until firm. Store in refrigerator in tightly covered container. Yield: 2 doz.

i: 1 can pork and beans (do not drain)
1/4 c. brown sugar (molasses can be used)
d: Brown bacon until crisp.
Remove from drippings.
Saute onions and garlic in drippings.
Remove from drippings.
Mix all ingredients together except onion rings and pour into casserole. Place onion rings on top.
Bake at 375° for 30 minutes.

i: 1 (6 to 10 oz.) envelope Italian salad dressing mix
d: Coat roast with the first 2 tablespoons flour.
Sprinkle with salt.
Brown in hot oil.
Add onions and saute 3 minutes.
Combine water, salad dressing mix and salt.
Pour over meat.

t: Linguini With A Tuscan White Clam Sauce
i: fresh herbs (either rosemary, basil, oregano or thyme may be used)
d: In a large saute pan, heat olive oil until it sizzles when a drop of water is added. To your saute pan, add onions, bay leaves and red peppers. Cook until onions become translucent.
Now add your cooked pasta and toss until well coated with sauce.
Serve in warmed bowls topped with freshly grated Parmesan cheese and minced fresh parsley.
Mix until salt and sugar are dissolved.
Pour over cucumbers; refrigerate.
Will keep a long time.
Tastes like bread and butter pickles.

i: 1 small jar cherries, drained and sliced
d: Mix cocktail, pineapple, cherries, oranges, peach pie filling and bananas. Melt margarine and add brown sugar and curry. Mix with the fruit. Sprinkle with cinnamon. Bake at 300° for 15 to 20 minutes.
Brown hamburger and drain.
Mix in crock-pot.
Bake on high for 4 to 6 hours.
Layer beans, bacon strips and brown sugar in pan.
Pour Coke over ingredients until just covered.
Bake in a slow oven at 250° uncovered for 4 hours.

i: 1/2 c. Hellmann's or Best Foods real mayonnaise
d: Combine mayonnaise, yogurt, green onion, curry powder, salt, mustard, pepper and Tabasco sauce.
Mix thoroughly.
Stir in cucumber.
Makes 2 cups sauce.

i: 2 lb. thin spaghetti, cooked and drained
d: Mix well and serve cold.
Wash spinach and pat dry.
Tear spinach and lettuce into bite size pieces; toss with onion.
Combine olive oil, lemon juice and honey in a tightly covered container.
Shake well.
Just before serving, toss dressing with salad.
Sprinkle sesame seeds atop. Serves 6.
Combine all ingredients, form into small balls.
Brown lightly in oil.
Drain off excess.
Add tomato sauce and water.
Cover tightly.
Simmer for 45 to 50 minutes, until rice is done.

In a large skillet, cook beef and onion until beef is lightly brown and onion is tender; drain off fat. Stir in salsa, taco seasoning and water; cook over low heat 5 to 6 minutes or until mixture thickens. Stir in corn and olives. Spoon into 8-inch square baking dish.
Place half the peanuts in an ungreased 11 x 7-inch pan.
In saucepan, melt butter and peanut butter chips over low heat.
Add milk and marshmallows.
Cook and stir until melted.
Pour over peanuts.
Sprinkle remaining peanuts on top.
Mix all ingredients together.
Mix all ingredients together.
Spread on bread.
Cut diagonally.
Put about 1 inch oil in frying pan and heat.
Place bread with filled side down and fry 15 seconds.
Turn and fry other side until golden brown.

Preheat oven to 350°. Grease and flour 12-cup tube or Bundt pan. Mix all ingredients (including the icing); mix at medium speed 2 minutes. Pour into prepared pan and bake 55 to 60 minutes. Remove from oven to wire rack and allow to cool 10 minutes. Invert onto serving plate and dust with powdered sugar.
Add sugar to cooled, melted oleo and eggs, one at a time.
Add dry ingredients all at once; mix thoroughly.
Add vanilla, chocolate chips and nuts.
Bake at 350° for 25 minutes in a 13 x 9-inch pan.
You must take out of oven before they are finished. It takes 15 minutes, but the middle is all soft.
If left in oven too long, they will be too hard.
Mix all ingredients together and refrigerate about 1 to 2 hours before serving.
This recipe is great as a dip with pita bread or sliced cucumbers and onions.

i: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. pasta bows (or other small pasta), cooked and drained
1 lb. fully cooked smoked ham, cut into julienne strips
1 small green bell pepper, chopped (about 1/2 c.)
d: Gently	toss ingredients together.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 6 hours.
Sprinkle roast with lemon salt, black pepper, chives and any other spices you like.
Cover with foil and cook at 150° for 8 hours.
Just before roast is done, uncover and let brown.
Peel and cut peaches in quarters. Add sugar and vinegar, let stand 3 hours or until juice comes up in sight. Cook till thick.
Peel chayotes.
Chop first seven vegetables and put in large kettle.
Put 1/2 cup salt on these.
Mix well and let stand for 3 hours.
Mix once or twice.
Heat oil in extra deep, 12 inch nonstick skillet on med-low. Peel and finely chop onion adding to skillet as you chop.
Seed and finely chop pepper, adding as you chop.
Raise heat to med-hi and cook 5 minutes or until onion is tender.
Brown meat.
Sprinkle onion soup mix and ginger over meat. Arrange layer of noodles over meat and add mushrooms.
Pour water over noodles.
Cook on low 20 minutes.
Blend flour into sour cream and add to stroganoff.
Cook 3 minutes.

i: 1/2 tsp. each: crumbled dried sage, ground cumin, garlic powder and granulated sugar
d: Put spices in a 1 gallon food storage bag.
Close bag and shake well.
Put 1 fillet in bag at a time and shake.
Coat large nonstick skillet with cooking spray.
Add oil and heat over medium heat.
Add catfish fillets, skinned side up, and cook 4 to 5 minutes until lightly blackened.
Use greased 9 x 13-inch pan or Bundt pan.
Melt butter; add brown sugar, pudding, milk and cinnamon.
Break up 1 loaf into round balls; place in pan.
Pour mixture over and break second loaf over this.
Rise 2 1/2 to 3 hours.
Bake 30 minutes at 350°; turn out immediately to cool.
Melt 1 stick butter in 9 x 12-inch pan.
Pour 1 1/2 cups graham cracker crumbs evenly on top of melted butter.
Pour can of Eagle Brand milk over that, then add chocolate and peanut butter morsels.
Then add pecans.
Bake for 20 to 30 minutes at 350° until brown.

i: 1 1/2 lb. fresh broccoli, cut into spears
d: Heat 1 inch water (salted if desired) to boiling.
Add broccoli.
Heat to boiling; reduce heat.
Cover and cook 12 to 15 minutes or just until stems are tender; drain.
Mix remaining ingredients; pour over broccoli.
Makes 6 servings.

i: 12 green peppers and 6 red sweet peppers, chopped (4 c.)
d: Chop all vegetables.
Sprinkle salt over vegetables and let stand overnight.
Rinse and drain.
Combine remaining ingredients; add chopped vegetables.
Boil for 5 minutes.
Put into sterilized jars and seal.

i: 1 can condensed cream of mushroom soup
d: Brown meat, vegetables and seasoning.
Make sauce of soup and sour cream.
Stir into meat mixture and heat through.
Serve over hot noodles.

i: 1 lb. bag walnut halves or pieces
d: Wash chicken wings and pat dry.
In a bowl combine the crumbled crackers or bread crumbs, herbs and Parmesan cheese. Add seasonings and mix well to blend.
Melt butter.
Dip chicken wings, first in melted butter, then into crumb coating.
Arrange chicken wings in lightly oiled ovenproof baking dish.
Bake chicken wings at 375° for about 30 minutes or until lightly brown and done.
Sprinkle Jell-O over pineapple.
Add cottage cheese, Cool Whip and nuts; mix well.
Refrigerate.
Serve on lettuce leaves or as a dessert.
Using a round pizza pan or a cookie sheet, lay crescent rolls out in pan and press edges together to form crust.
Brown sausage and cook bacon.
Crumble both on crust.
Beat eggs; add salt and pepper to eggs to taste.
Pour over meat and crust.
Top with cheese.
Brown ground beef, onion, green pepper and garlic; drain.
Add undrained tomatoes, sauce, paste, undrained mushrooms, water and all spices.
Bring to a boil; reduce heat.
Cover and simmer for 30 minutes.
Remove bay leaf and add cooked noodles.
Let the cucumbers or zucchini, onions and bell peppers soak with the salt for 30 minutes.
Combine the remaining ingredients. Bring to a boil, then add drained relish (the first 4 ingredients).
Let boil for 5 minutes.
Can and seal while hot.

i: 1 lb. mild Italian sausage, cut into 1 inch links
1 can sauerkraut, undrained (I use Del Monte)
d: In a skillet, cook sausage and onion until sausage is brown and onion is tender, drain.
Stir in apples, sauerkraut, water, brown sugar and caraway seeds.
Transfer to a 2 1/2 qt. baking dish.
Cover and bake at 350 degrees for one hour.
Garnish with parsley, if desired.
Make 6 to 8 servings.

t: Sweet Gherkin Pickles(Gherkins, Immature Cucumbers, Are Often Made Into Sweet Pickles.)
i: 5 qt. (about 7 lb.) cucumbers (1 1/2 to 3-inches in length)
d: First Day:
In the morning, wash cucumbers thoroughly.
Scrub with vegetable brush.
Drain cucumbers and place in large container.
Cover with boiling water.
That afternoon, 6 to 8 hours later, drain and cover with fresh boiling water.

i: 1 lb. pkg. spaghetti, cooked and drained
d: Mix well all ingredients together; let marinate overnight in the Italian dressing. Set out at room temperature all day before serving.
Preheat oven
to
Put ground beef in large bowl. Fold in	all ingredients.
Keep folding ground beef until the texture becomes
slightly
Preheat oven to 350°.
Wash chicken breasts and place in a medium size casserole dish.
Dust with paprika, seasoning and marjoram.
Cover chicken with spaghetti sauce.
Place onion slices on top of chicken.
Mix beaten eggs and sugar.
Add hot milk and pour into a large pie shell. Bake at 350° about 30 to 45 minutes (until crust is brown).
You may put dots of butter on top and sprinkle with nutmeg.
Slit hot dogs lengthwise almost to the end.
Place a strip of cheese in each one.
Separate crescent rolls.
Place hot dog at the shortest end and roll up.
Bake on greased pan at 375° for 12 minutes.

i: 16 oz. pkg. frozen vegetables (broccoli, cauliflower and carrots)
2 or 3 seasoned chicken breasts, cut in cubes
d: Put vegetables in 9 x 12-inch casserole dish in microwave with 1 tablespoon water.
Cook until tender, then set aside to drain. Debone chicken breasts; cube and season to taste.
Brown in oleo. Remove chicken when brown, leaving remaining oleo.
Saute onions. When ready, add cheese.
When melted slowly, add milk and stir. Then add vegetables.
Stir until well coated with cheese.
Grease a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Lay bread in 2 layers in pan. Cover each layer with grated cheese; sprinkle with bacon.
Mix eggs, milk and salt together; pour over top.
Let stand overnight in the refrigerator.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.

i: 1 c. dried chickpeas, lima beans or white beans
thin red onion slices, separated into rings (garnish)
d: Pick over peas or beans and discard any stones.
Place in colander and rinse thoroughly.
Soak in water to cover overnight. Drain; rinse again.
Place in saucepan and cover with fresh cold water.
Bring to a boil.
Lower heat and simmer, covered, until peas or beans are tender, but not mushy, about 1 hour.

i: 6 skinless breasts or you may use other parts of chicken
d: You will also need bags (zipper lot).

Stir together orange juice, soy sauce and ginger root. Add chicken. Let stand 30 minutes at room temperature. Drain chicken. Reserve sauce. Saute chicken in olive oil. Stir together reserved sauce and cornstarch. Stir into pan and heat until thick and bubbly. Stir in orange sections and cashews.

n: italian good seasons dressing mix powder
i: 2 pkg. Italian Good Seasons dressing mix powder
d: Put together in bag and mix.
Shake.
Chicken should be coated. Bake at 350° for 1 hour or until done.
Brown hamburger and add taco mix according to directions on package.
Mix picante mix and cream cheese together.
Make layers of hamburger, refried beans, picante mix and cheese.
Repeat for 1 more layer.
Mix together sugar, vinegar, oil and salt.
Rinse and drain beans.
Add to vinegar mixture.
Add onion and pepper.
Let stand overnight.
Mix and store in a glass jar.
Keep lid on tight.
This will keep for years.
Cook onions and pepper in 3 tablespoons oil.
Add hamburger and fry until hamburger falls apart.
Stir in rest of ingredients and put in electric frypan.
Cover and cook slow for 1 hour at 220°.

i: 1 (7 oz.) roll slice 'n bake refrigerated sugar cookies
d: While cookie dough softens, combine dates, sugar and water. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until mixture thickens.
Remove from heat and stir in lemon juice.
Set aside.

i: 3 c. pared, chopped and cooked carrots
d: Combine all ingredients in a large saucepan.
Cook slowly for 45 minutes or until it jells on spoon. Skim, pour into containers and seal.
Makes about 1 quart.
In a large saucepan, cover corn with water.
Bring to boiling. Simmer 5 minutes.
Drain.
Mix first 5 ingredients together, then stir in the remaining. Bake in a greased and floured cake pan at 350° for approximately 35 minutes or until done.

i: zest of an orange, julienned and boiled for 2 minutes, then drained
assorted greens: radicchio, arugula, bibb, watercress, etc.
d: Mix together the first 7 ingredients for vinaigrette.
Set aside.
Cook zucchini in boiling salt water for 5 minutes.
Cook beef and garlic, stir in spices and sauce.
Mix Ricotta, egg and parsley.
Place 1/2 zucchini in greased 8-inch square baking pan. Sprinkle with bread crumbs.
Spread 1/2 of Ricotta, 1/2 of beef mixture and 1/2 of Mozzarella.
Repeat.

i: 1/3 c. sour cream or heavy sweet cream
d: Add sugar to cream gradually, stirring constantly.
Add vanilla; beat thoroughly.
Serve over pound cake, ice cream or use as a fresh fruit dip.
Trim meat of all external fat.
Use a deep, heavy pot with lid.
Brown meat on all sides.
Add onion and garlic; cook until the onion is soft, about 3 to 4 minutes, stirring occasionally. Stir in coriander, cumin, tomato paste, 3/4 cup water, chilies and black pepper.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat, cover and simmer for 2 1/2 hours or until meat is tender.
Yield:
Combine all ingredients and marinate your meat (chicken, beef or pork) for 2 to 24 hours.
You can bake, broil or barbecue. Bake at 350° for about 1 hour or until done.
Cream together the sugars, oil and eggs.
Add baking powder, salt, flour and oats; mix well.
Then add coconut, corn flakes, chocolate chips and nuts (optional).
Mix well.
Drop on cookie sheet.
Bake 10 to 12 minutes at 325°.
In airtight container, make layers as follows:
lettuce, cauliflower, bacon, cheese and mayonnaise.
Chill 24 to 48 hours. Add salt and pepper to taste.
Toss before serving.
Cut biscuits into fourths.
Fill a small baggie with sugar and 2 tsp. cinnamon.
Shake biscuits in baggies.
Place into bundt pan.
Melt 1 1/2 sticks of oleo and add brown sugar, pecans and 1 tsp. cinnamon.
Pour batter over biscuits.

i: 1/2 c. pitted olives, cut in half
d: In 1 1/2 quart casserole, cook onion, garlic and oil on High, covered, 4 to 4 1/2 minutes in microwave.
Cut zucchini lengthwise in half, then crosswise in 1/4 inch slices.
Add onion mixture with next 3 ingredients.
Cook covered on High 10 minutes.
Stir once.
Add olives; cover and cook 5 to 7 minutes, until tender. Sprinkle fish steaks with oregano.

t: Greek Style Kabobs With Pita Bread
i: 1 lb. pork tenderloin (see note at bottom)
d: Cut pork into bite size pieces.
Season with lemon juice, salt, pepper, oregano and olive oil.
Skewer meat and cook on the grill.
Brush pita bread with a bit of oil and grill as well. When the meat is tender, put 1 skewer on each pita and slowly draw out the skewer leaving the pieces of meat in the pita.
Add the onion, tomato, parsley and sauce.
Roll the pita.
Wash and dry eggplant; remove stems.
Leave eggplant whole and unpeeled.
Make a slit the length of each eggplant only to the center, not completely through the other side.
In each slit, drizzle 1 tablespoon olive oil and season with 1/2 teaspoon salt and 1/4 teaspoon pepper.
Gently press eggplant together and rub completely with olive oil. Rub an 11 x 7-inch baking dish with olive oil.
Place eggplant in dish. Bake at 375° about 30 minutes or until tender.

i: 8 chicken legs (drumsticks with thighs attached, about 4 lb.)
1 tsp. dried tarragon, chervil or basil or 1 Tbsp. fresh
d: Heat oven to 375°.
Pour oil over chicken in a heavy 3-quart casserole.
Turn chicken in oil to coat evenly.
Add celery, onions, garlic, herbs, seasoning and cognac.
Mix well.
Cover casserole with foil and then with lid.
Mix in order.
Let set 1 hour.
Roll into 1-inch balls.
Roll balls in flour and put on greased cookie sheet.
Flatten with cup about 1/2; put thumbprint in middle and fill with strawberry jam. Sprinkle with white sugar.
Bake at 350° for 10 to 14 minutes.

i: 2 Tbsp. cream or half and half
d: Place chicken (without skin) in large kettle of water, along with celery and celery salt.
Cook for approximately 1 hour. Remove chicken and strip meat from bone.
Return meat to water and add noodles.
Cook for half an hour.

i: 1 tsp. cardamom or 1 tsp. vanilla extract
d: Beat eggs with sugar until thick.
Melt butter.
Cool.
Add slowly to egg and sugar mix, then add flour and cardamom.
Bake in krumkake iron on top of stove.
Remove with knife and roll in a cone shape on wooden cone.
In a glass or crockery bowl alternately layer the sliced cucumbers and onions.
In a medium saucepan combine sugar, vinegar, salt, mustard seed, celery seed, and ground turmeric. Bring to boiling, stirring just till sugar is dissolved.
Pour vinegar mixture atop cucumber-onion mixture; cool slightly. Cover tightly; refrigerate pickles at least 24 hours before serving. Store up to 1 month in refrigerator.
Makes 7 cups.
Add water to frozen concentrates as directed on cans and mix all together.
Add the pineapple and apple juice, mixing well. This is best to let stand several hours before serving, or freeze until about 3 hours before serving and set out to thaw until mushy.
Add ginger ale before serving.
Yields 25 servings.
Broil ribs on both sides to remove excess fat.
Saute onion in butter.
Place ribs and onions in crock-pot.
Mix all other ingredients together and glaze ribs.
Cook at 200° for 8 hours.

i: 1 lb. Ricotta or dry curd cottage cheese
3/4 c. grated Romano or Parmesan cheese
1 1/2 lb. Mozzarella cheese, sliced or grated
1/2 lb. lasagna noodles, cooked as directed on package
d: Baking Temperature: 350°.
Baking Time: 30 minutes.
Clean and chop parsley (use only leaves not stems); clean and chop fine 5 to 6 cloves from fresh garlic.
Lay out beef sandwich steaks flat.
Spread one clove chopped garlic per sandwich steak. Add one tablespoon flat parsley.
Sprinkle one rounded tablespoonful fresh Romano cheese; sprinkle 1/4 teaspoon crushed red pepper over meat.
Roll up Bragioles tightly and stick 5 toothpicks on meat ends to keep it together.
Warm up 3/4 cup olive oil in skillet (cover skillet).
Scald, peel and chop tomatoes; add all other ingredients and cook about 25 minutes (until thick enough) and place filled jars in boiling water bath canner and boil for 10 minutes.
Makes 12 (1/2 pints).
Add peppers to taste while cooking, for desired heat.
Mix ingredients until well blended.
Mixture will be extremely firm dough.
Turn onto large floured surface.
Sprinkle with flour and roll until extremely thin.
Move dough around as rolling to make sure underside of dough is floured well to keep from sticking. Cover thin dough with thin layer of flour.
Roll dough, beginning at one edge.

i: 1 lb. New York State strong cheese
1/8 tsp. or dash of red pepper (1/4 tsp. if doubled)
d: Grate cheese.
Let butter soften to room temperature.
Hand mix cheese and butter until smooth.
