Mix dry ingredients together, then pour into cheese and butter mixture.
Mix with hands until well blended.
Press onto cookie sheet (ungreased).
Cook corn; cut into 12 chunks.
Combine the cooked corn, rotini, tomatoes, zucchini and olives in a large bowl.
In a small bowl, combine remaining ingredients.
Pour over vegetable mixture. Cover and refrigerate 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Serves 8.
Cook fettuccine according to your taste of doneness; drain. Meanwhile, in small saucepan, melt butter over medium heat; gradually stir in cheese, then cream until well blended.
Continue heating sauce, stirring constantly, just to boiling point.
Remove from heat; stir in parsley.
Add sauce to hot fettuccine; toss lightly.
Serves 6.
Mix the first 3 ingredients in one bowl.
Mix the pudding, milk and Cool Whip in a large bowl, then blend and fold cream cheese mixture into Cool Whip mixture.
Layer cookie crumbs with the chocolate pudding combination in a clear glass container and decorate as you see fit.
Can be served with a little toy shovel. You can end with a crumb mix on top and put an artificial flower in center.
Mix all together in a large mouth gallon jar and fill with thinly sliced cucumbers and 2 sliced onions.
Slice chicken thin.
Put in frying pan with oil over low heat. Brown both sides with garlic.
Add juice of 1 lemon and cover. Lower heat and cook until done.
Garnish with other lemon slices and parsley.
Serve with rice.
Blend parsley and basil into butter.
Blend this with cream cheese.
Add Parmesan, olive oil, garlic and pepper.
When pasta is ready, drain.
Add 2/3 cup boiling water to cheese mixture. Stir and toss with pasta.
Spray a casserole dish with Pam.
Add your corn and all of the ingredients in a bowl; mix well.
Pour into casserole dish.
Bake for 20 to 30 minutes in 350° oven.
Serve hot.
Combine all ingredients in a wok.
(You may use a large pot but is better to use a wok.)
Stir ingredients to keep from sticking.
Cook until warm throughout.
Pierce potatoes; place on a paper towel.
Microwave until tender.
Let stand 5 minutes.
Combine other ingredients and microwave on High until tender (2 to 3 minutes).
Split potatoes and fill with vegetable mixture.
You may top with cheese or butter.
Brown meat; drain. Stir in beans and sauce.
Cover.
Simmer 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add Velveeta; stir until melted. For each serving, top chips with lettuce, tomato and meat mixture.

t: Easy Fruit Cobbler(Mrs. W. A. Broadway)
d: Melt margarine in bottom of casserole dish.
Mix flour, sugar and milk.
Pour this mixture over margarine in dish and top with peaches.
Bake at 350° until brown.
You may substitute any fruit you wish to use and also add cinnamon or other spices.
Heat to boiling 7-Up and drained pineapple juice; dissolve jello with this.
Pour into square or rectangle container.
Mix cottage cheese, Cool Whip, pineapple and almost all nuts (leave a few to sprinkle on top).
Mix with jello and chill.

i: 2 small (1 1/2) or 4 large potatoes, sliced thin (about 1/8-inch and about 3/4 inch)
5 garlic cloves more or less, sliced as thin as possible
8 sage leaves more or less, cut in 1/8 inch to 1/16 inch strips or use dry sage or your favorite herb
d: Steam potatoes over steamer until sticky and not quite done, still hard.
Include garlic, onion and herbs in steamer.
Turn frequently.
For serving 4, you may want to steam a batch of potatoes, set aside and steam the other half with herbs.

i: 1/2 lb. salt pork, ham hock or ham bone
d: Wash salt pork and place in a big pot with about 6 to 8 cups of water.
Boil gently, covered, for about 1/2 hour.
Pick and prepare collards for cooking.
Discard tough outer leaves and stems. Tear washed collard leaves and put in pot with pork. Cook collards for about 1 1/2 hours or until tender. Add salt and pepper near end of cooking time.
At serving time, drain the greens and chop coarsely.
Put the chunks of pork on the platter or in the dish with them. Be sure to serve with pepper sauce. Also serve with cornbread.
Boil chicken breasts in 1 quart water until tender.
Remove, cool and cut up.
Add cream of chicken soup to broth and bring to rolling boil.
Cover and cook 10 minutes on High.
Combine first four ingredients and mix well.
Stir in chocolate chips.
Mix together flour, 1 cup sugar, cocoa, soda and salt.
Add water, oil, vinegar and vanilla.
Beat well.
Fill cupcake papers 1/3 full of chocolate mixture.
Dredge chicken in flour.
Saute onion and garlic in olive oil until softened.
Add chicken.
Cook until browned on both sides. Add all other ingredients.
Reduce heat.
Cover pan and simmer 45 minutes.

i: hot dogs, serrated on top of mixture
d: If you figure out what to do with these ingredients, please let Mac know.
Thank you.
Separate broccoli florets and trim tough skins from stalks; cut up.
Bring to a boil the water and salt.
Add broccoli.
Cook 5 to 8 minutes.
Add spaghetti, cut in halves.
Cook until both are al dente.

i: 12 very thin, unpeeled sweet potato slices
salt, cinnamon-sugar mix or chili powder
d: On a rack, arrange the sweet potato slices in a circle. Sprinkle with either salt, cinnamon-sugar mix or chili powder. Cook in microwave on High 4 to 5 minutes, until dry.
Rotate rack halfway through cooking.
Let stand 1 minute.
Contains 40 calories; no fat.

i: 1 Tbsp. snipped fresh dill weed or 1 tsp. dried dill weed
1 tsp. coarse salt (kosher) or 3/4 tsp. regular salt
d: In a large skillet, brown roast in hot oil. Place roast in a 3 1/2 to 4 qt. electric crockery cooker, cutting if necessary to fit. Add the water to cooker. Sprinkle roast with 2 tsp. of the fresh dill weed or 3/4 tsp. dried dill weed, salt and pepper. Cover and cook on high heat setting for 5 to 6 hours or on low heat setting for 10 to 12 hours, until meat is tender. Remove roast from cooker, reserving liquid; cover roast and keep warm. Measure liquid from cooker; skim fat. Reserve 1 c. of the juices. Meanwhile, in a small saucepan, stir together yogurt and flour until well combined. Stir in the 1 c. reserved cooking liquid and remaining dill weed. Cook and stir until thickened and bubbly. Cook and stir 1 minutes more. Serve meat with sauce and noodles. Makes 6 to 8 servings.

i: 2 c. dry rice, uncooked, wash and drain
d: Melt butter and add chopped onion, oregano, salt and rice. Stir all the ingredients listed and simmer 10 minutes (can use iron skillet or Teflon).
Turn into large casserole and add beef consomme and water.
Add drained mushrooms; bake, covered at 400° for 1 hour.
Cream shortening and sugar.
Add eggs.
Mash bananas and lemon juice and mix with the shortening mixture.
Sift flour, baking powder and salt.
Add to above mixture.
Mix in nuts and stir well.
Combine all ingredients in a stainless steel or glass bowl. Cover and chill thoroughly, at least 4 hours.
Serve in chilled cups.
Top with croutons.
Makes 6 servings.
Cube steak and brown; drain.
In large pot, boil about 4 cups water, bouillon, onion, pepper and mushrooms for 20 minutes.
Add meat.
Boil rice; add to mixture.
Add soy sauce.
Cook for about 10 minutes.
Mix all of the ingredients and put into a medium sized baking skillet.
Bake at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes.
In mixing bowl, cream margarine and sugar together until light and fluffy.
Add 2 eggs and flavoring.
Stir in flour mixture and mix well.
Add nuts to batter.
Chill well (about 2 hours).
Shape into 3 loaves.
Mix gelatin, sugar and boiling water together until dissolved. Add pineapple juice, then enough water to make 1 gallon.
Freeze overnight.
Just before serving, put in punch bowl and add lemon-lime soda.
Stir until slushy.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Spray baking dish with nonstick cooking spray.
Lay the slices of bread in baking dish in two rows, overlapping them like shingles.
In a mixing bowl, beat together the egg, egg whites, milk, sugar, brown sugar and vanilla.
Pour the egg mixture over the bread.
In a small bowl, stir together the remaining ingredients.
Peel, cut apples into quarters.
Roll out biscuits.
Cut each in half.
Put 1 quarter apple in each biscuit half.
Fold dough over apple, place in a buttered sheet pan or large casserole dish. Mix next 5 ingredients; pour over dumplings, cut butter over top. Bake in a 350° oven until apples are done.
Take from oven, baste and continue baking until golden brown.
Chop broccoli and cauliflower into small pieces, discarding all stems.
Chop water chestnuts into small pieces.
Mix together mayonnaise, lemon juice and sugar; fold in all vegetables.
Mix together; chill for at least one hour.
Mix first 4 ingredients. Add canning salt and let stand overnight.
Next morning, squeeze out and rinse in cold water. Squeeze out cold water.
Put in cooking kettle and add remaining ingredients.
Boil all ingredients for 20 minutes.
Seal in jars. Makes 7 to 8 pints.

i: 6 chowder clam or 2 6 oz. can minced clams
d: Start 3 hours before dinner by putting clams in freezer. (they open real easy about 5 minutes before you start).
Open the clams into the bowl reserving all the clam liquid.
Start linguini water.
Crush and chop garlic, the finer the better. Chop parsley.
Rinse clams in clam juice by dunking them, this should remove any shell, dirt, or pearls.
Chop clams fine.
Combine first 6 ingredients in deep Dutch oven pan. Cook until soft. (Boil at 232°.) Add pecans and stir until coated. Spread and separate with fork; cool completely. Store in tin box.

i: 3 c. cut cooked chicken or turkey
1 c. condensed cream of chicken soup
d: Place chicken in ungreased 2-quart casserole or 9 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan.
Toss croutons, celery and onion with the 1/2 cup water and the margarine until croutons are moistened.
Reserve about 1 1/2 cups of this stuffing.
Spread remaining stuffing over chicken.
Mix soup with 1 1/2 cups water; pour over stuffing.
Top with reserved stuffing.
Pour mixture over ribs, brisket, chicken, pork chops, etc. Mighty fine eaten!
Mix first 6 ingredients together; beat well.
Stir constantly when cooking; cook over medium heat until mixture thickens. Remove quickly from heat; layer in bowl with banana slices and vanilla wafers (best warm).
Mix all above ingredients and marinate at room temperature for 1 hour.
Cook your favorite pasta (ziti) and toss with tomato sauce while still hot.
In large bowl, mix cottage cheese, Mozzarella cheese, eggs, parsley, onion powder, basil leaves and pepper until well blended. Set aside.
In medium bowl, mix together spaghetti sauce and cooled ground beef.
In 12 x 8 x 2-inch baking dish, spread 3/4 cup meat sauce; layer 3 noodles on top of sauce.
Spread with cottage cheese mixture and about 1 1/2 meat sauce.
Layer 4 more noodles on top of meat sauce.
Spread with cottage cheese mixture and meat sauce.
Mix brown sugar, oats, flour, butter and cinnamon.
Press 1/2 of mixture into a 9-inch square pan.
Cover with rhubarb.
Mix sugar and cornstarch together.
Add water and vanilla.
Cook until thick and clear.
Cream
butter;
gradually
add sugar, beating at medium speed until well blended.
Stir in chocolate and vanilla.
Add 1
Combine wieners and rest of ingredients in a bowl and marinate 4 hours. Place mixture in a saucepan and simmer for 10 minutes. Then transfer to a chafing dish to serve.
Mix all ingredients together except the marshmallows and pour into a 9 x 13-inch greased pan.
Bake at 350°.
Cake is done when toothpick comes out clean. Place marshmallows on hot cake and place into hot oven to melt.
Let cool, then cover with icing.
Saute onions in bacon drippings in a stockpot over very low heat for 1 hour, stirring frequently.
Add green bell peppers. Cook until mixture is dark brown in color, stirring frequently.
Mix all ingredients until moistened.
Roll into small balls and bake on lightly greased baking pan at 450° for 10 minutes. Serve hot.
These freeze well.
If baking while frozen, allow longer baking time.
Precook chicken with salt and pepper in small amount of water. Bone chicken.
Break it up and dice it.
Melt butter in skillet. Add onion, green pepper, celery, garlic and lemon juice.
Cook until tender.
Add chicken and stir.
Then add to curry sauce. Serve over hot rice.
Combine all ingredients in bowl.
Mix well.
Shape into 2 logs; wrap in foil.
Store in refrigerator.
Cut into slices. Yields 4 dozen.
Mix and put in ice cube tray.
Freeze until firm and serve.
Arrange chicken in pan; sprinkle with mint, rosemary, salt and pepper.
Now pour wine, Worcestershire, honey and oil mixture around chicken; add mushrooms to top of chicken and pecans final layer.
Cover with foil; bake at 350° for 1 1/2 hours.
Dissolve jello in 2 cups of hot water.
Add strawberries and stir until thawed.
Add bananas, pineapple and nuts.
Pour 1/2 of the mixture into dish.
Let set.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Sift together flour, soda, salt and cinnamon and nutmeg.
Add sugar and stir to mix thoroughly.
Make a well in center of dry ingredients and add all at once, the oil, eggs and water and pumpkin.
Mix well and add nuts.
Pour batter into 3 (8 x 4-inch) loaf pans.
Cream margarine and shortening with sugar.
Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
Add lemon.
Mix.
Add flour and 7-Up alternately, beating after each addition.
Bake or boil eggplants and cool them.
When cold, scoop out the centers and the chopped onion, the oil and vinegar.
Salt and pepper.
Chop tomato, jalapenos, onion and cilantro into small pieces; add olive oil and season with oregano and salt.
Dissolve gelatin in water.
Stir in lemonade.
Add ginger ale and ice.
Serve.
Makes 1 gallon.
Mix together flour and soda.
Add with fork until the consistency of corn meal the Crisco.
Mix in the buttermilk.
Roll or pat out dough.
Cut out biscuits and bake at 450° for 8 to 10 minutes.

i: 4 to 6 Tbsp. olive or canola oil
1 fresh sweet pepper (green, red, yellow or orange), sliced
d: Have all ingredients ready to stir-fry in wok or heavy skillet (high heat).
Saute garlic, ginger and fenugreek in 2 teaspoons sesame oil and 2 to 3 tablespoons olive or canola oil for 1 minute. Add carrots and onions.
Saute until onions are golden.
Remove and reserve.
Add oil, heat, then stir-fry chicken fast until very golden and crispy; remove and reserve.
Add oil; stir-fry fenugreek, poppy seed and broccoli or asparagus quickly as bhaji. Add reserved onions, carrots and chicken.
Thaw bread dough according to package directions on a lightly greased baking sheet.
Preheat oven to 375°.
Press thawed dough into a rectangle about 8 x 16-inch.
Prick top of bread with a fork.
Brush with oil.
Sprinkle to taste with salt and thyme. Bake 15 to 18 minutes, until golden.
Spray a nonstick skillet with cooking spray and heat.
Saute mushrooms, onions and peppers for about 5 minutes or until tender. Add rice, curry, salt and chicken broth.
Bring to boil.
Reduce heat to simmer; cover and cook 20 to 25 minutes until rice is tender.
Serves 4.
Grease pan with the butter.
Put marmalade in pan.
Sprinkle nuts around.
Dip biscuits in butter and then in sugar and cinnamon mixture.
Bake 30 to 40 minutes until golden brown.
Let cool for 5 minutes (no longer) and then invert on plate.
In a 3-quart casserole mix water and salt.
Bring to boil (6 to 8 minutes).
Add pasta and stir.
On Full power heat 15 minutes or until completely cooked.
Drain in colander.
Combine shrimp, green onion, cornstarch, 1 teaspoon salt and pepper in a large bowl. Toss to mix.
Stir-fry shrimp mixture in hot oil in wok for 3 to 5 minutes or until shrimp turn pink. Remove from wok. Stir rice, eggs and 1 1/2 teaspoons salt together in a bowl.
Pour into hot wok.
Stir-fry over medium-high heat for 2 minutes or until eggs are cooked. Add shrimp mixture. Stir-fry until mixed.
Brown hamburger and onions, drain fat off.
Add other ingredients and cook until done. Serve warm.

i: 3 Cup oatmeal- quick not old fashioned
d: Mix egg with the milk then add the remaining ingredients. Spread in well greased loaf pan.
Sprinkle with cinnamon.
Serve with milk.
Keep remaining loaf in refrigerator.
Heat slices if desired in microwave.
Peel and mash avocados.
Spread over platter.
Top with dip, taco seasoning, tomatoes, onions, olives, taco sauce, Cheddar cheese and Monterey Jack cheese.
Refrigerate.
Rub inside of bowl with garlic.
Combine soups and seasonings. Add chopped vegetables.
Mix well and chill several hours.
Even better the next day.
Serves 6.
Melt margarine, add sugar and eggs.
Beat well.
Add baking soda, dissolved in the milk and add sifted (not necessary with presifted flour) dry ingredients, nuts and raisins.
Spread in large greased cookie pan.
Sprinkle sugar over top.
Bake at 350° for 20 minutes.

i: 1/4 c. (1/2 stick) melted butter or margarine
1/4 c. firmly packed light brown sugar
d: Preheat oven to 375°.
Unroll crescents and divide into scored triangles.
Cut cream cheese block into 16 cubes.
Place one cube at base of each triangle and roll up to enclose cheese.
Bring ends of crescent together forming balls.
Spray 16 muffin cups with nonstick coating.

i: 1 can each pineapple slices, peach halves, pear halves and cherries
d: Arrange fruit in a glass lasagne dish. Sprinkle with the brown sugar, salt, lemon juice and curry. Add the fruit juice and spot with butter.
Bake 1 hour at 325°.
Cut 4 slices, almost but not quite all the way through Spam loaf.
Cut pineapple slices in half and place 1/2 slice in each slit in the Spam.
Place loaf in small pan or shallow casserole dish.
Arrange remaining 1/2 slices of pineapple around Spam loaf. Combine brown sugar and dry mustard with 2 tablespoons pineapple juice.
Spoon over Spam.
Bake in preheated oven at 350° for 25 or 30 minutes, basting occasionally.
Steam or boil cut green beans until firm.
Boil potatoes and peel.
Cut into bite size pieces.
Cut tomatoes to bite size pieces.
Slice or dice onion.
Salt or pepper to taste.
Sift flour with cinnamon, ginger, salt and soda.
Beat egg. Beat in sugar, shortening and molasses, then add flour and spice mixture.
Drop by small teaspoonful onto greased cookie sheet.
Do not press down.
Bake at 375° for about 8 minutes.

i: 4 to 6 chicken breasts, cut into 1-inch strips
1 pkg. Good Seasons Italian dressing mix
d: Mix egg, milk and poultry seasoning in bowl and set aside. Mix flour, dressing mix, salt, pepper and Accent in large bowl with fitted cover (or gallon Ziploc bag).
Heat 2 cups oil on stove in large frying pan.
Dip chicken strips in egg mixture and then in flour mixture and fry until golden brown, turning once.
Mix all until cheese melts. Put on party ryes on cookie sheet. Bake at 350 degrees for 10 minutes.
Cream greetings and smiles thoroughly.
Add handshake separately.
Slowly stir in love.
Sift sympathy and hospitality and fold in carefully.
Bake in warm heart.
Serve often.

i: 1 unbaked pie crust, cut into strips
d: Slowly cook oil, onions, celery, salt and beef, stirring constantly.
Add potatoes and tomatoes; stir well. Place in casserole dish. Place strips of pastry on top.
Bake at 425° for 30 to 40 minutes.
Thaw broccoli.
Cook chopped onion in butter until tender. Add rest.
No salt!
Excellent for potlucks.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Cut bacon in half
and wrap each chestnut.
Secure with a toothpick.
Bake 1 hour at 350° in a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Place chestnuts in an ovensafe dish
(but
Preheat oven to 325°.
Rub pork loin with olive oil, sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Bake on rack, 30 minutes per pound.
Remove from oven 30 minutes before done and punch holes in meat.
Pour glaze over loin and return to oven for another 30 to 40 minutes. Slice and serve with remaining glaze.
Mix ingredients together.
Roll out between waxed paper sheets to a thickness of 1/4-inch.
Remove top sheet.
Sprinkle with 1 1/2 cups grated Cheddar cheese.
Lift waxed paper and roll like a jelly roll.
Turn ends in to keep cheese inside.
Fry sausage and hamburger together; drain grease off.
Put in a bowl while warm; mix Cheez Whiz.
Put on party rye bread (rye bread) and broil until bread is lightly brown and crisp.
Watch close.

i: 1 bottle light soy sauce or teriyaki sauce
d: Trim tips and extra flap of fat on wings.
Mix ingredients in stainless, plastic or glass bowl.
Marinate wings 3 or 4 hours. (Overnight is better.)
Pat dry on paper towels, both sides.
In a single layer, saute in butter to brown both sides.
Brush with more hot sauce.

i: 1 c. peanut butter, smooth or crunchy
d: In a large bowl, mix together all ingredients until smooth. Chill for 1 hour.
For dipping, serve with assorted fresh or blanched vegetables and pickled baby corn.
Makes 1 1/4 cups.

i: 8 oz. uncooked whole wheat or regular fettuccini
1 1/2 c. julienne cut turkey ham or cooked ham
4 eggs, hard-cooked and cut into wedges
d: Cook fettuccini to desired doneness as directed on package. Drain; rinse with hot water.
Keep warm.
In large skillet over medium-high heat, heat oil.
Saute onions for 1 to 2 minutes or until crisp-tender.
Reduce heat to medium.
Stir in sour cream, milk, parsley, salt and pepper.

i: 1 c. frozen loose pack hash brown potatoes, thawed
d: In a skillet cook sausage until well done, drain.
Separate crescent rolls into 8-inch triangles.
Place dough in 12-inch pizza pan, with points toward center.
Press over bottom and sides to form crust.
Spoon sausage over crust. Sprinkle with potatoes.
Top with Cheddar cheese.

i: 3 1/2 to 4 c. eggplant, peeled and diced
1 clove garlic, pressed or garlic salt
d: Macerate eggplant with salt for about 30 minutes to remove liquid and soften.
Drain.
Saute in a film of olive oil, green pepper and onion; don't brown.
Set aside.
Dry eggplant on paper towels and saute in film of olive oil, 2 to 4 minutes, to brown. Add onion, pepper and remaining ingredients to eggplant.
Cook down to thicken.
Clean and wash one head of chicory.
Cut into manageable eating size.
In a proper size pot, place 2 cups water, pinch of sugar, pepper, tomato sauce (for taste and color) and the press of 2 large cloves garlic.
Add the cut greens to the pot containing the listed ingredients and cook, covered, at medium heat, until done (about 10 to 15 minutes).
Enjoy with toasted French or Italian bread.

t: Doug Cooper'S Polynesian Black Bean Soup
d: Boil beans in beef and chicken stocks until tender. Meanwhile, saute meat, onion and garlic.
Add diced tomatoes and juice after meat is cooked. Reduce this mixture to a paste. When beans are tender, remove 2/3 of beans and puree.
Combine pureed meat and bean puree. Put back into pot with beans.
Let simmer. Will thicken.
Season with salt and pepper. Garnish with parsley.
If sausage comes in casing, remove casing.
In 6-quart saucepot, brown sausage and break into small pieces.
Remove sausage from saucepot; drain on paper towel.
Refrigerate sausage.
*Do not have to precook noodles.
Mix all together and let sit overnight in refrigerator.
Bake in 375° oven for 20 minutes in greased muffin tins.
Makes 5 to 6 dozen muffins.
This mixture keeps in the refrigerator for 5 to 6 weeks.
Have fresh muffins for breakfast everyday.
Slow cook on low.
Drain.
Slow cook a little longer.
Salt to taste.

i: 1 can tomato sauce plus 1 can water
d: Heat oil in pan; add next 5 ingredients and saute until beginning to soften.
Add next 4 ingredients and simmer until soft.
Remove from pan and puree.
Return half of sauce to pan; add fish in single layer if possible and top with remaining sauce and olives.
Simmer until fish is cooked.

i: 2 pkg. (10 to 16 oz. each) frozen strawberries, processed in blender
1 can (6 oz.) frozen orange juice concentrate
d: Mix Jell-O in boiling water with sugar; add cold water and rest of ingredients, except ginger ale.
Chill.
When ready to serve, add ginger ale.
Freezes well.
Serves 100.
Swirl oil in a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Add rest of ingredients and mix well.
Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.

i: 1 bell pepper (red) or 1 small jar pimentos
d: Shred squash, peppers and onions together and let sit a few minutes.
Bring remaining ingredients to a hard boil.
Add shredded ingredients.
Bring all to a hard boil and put in sterilized jars.
Cook onion and cabbage in hot olive oil about 10 minutes.
Add fennel seed, tomato, salt and sugar.
Cover and cook for 15 minutes.
Pour in wine and lemon juice.
Cook 10 minutes.
Serve sprinkled with cheese.
Mix together dry ingredients, add lard or shortening and mix until crumbly.
Begin adding water until a nice soft dough.
Roll on floured board to desired size.
Poke hole in the middle. Either fry in frying pan with 1 inch of oil or electric fryer. Brown to desired color, turn over and fry on the other side and drain.

t: Masoods' Kheema Biryani(Saffron Rice With Ground Lamb)
i: 2 c. basmati rice or other long grain rice, washed and drained
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
In small bowl, drop saffron threads in; add boiling water and soak for 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, in heavy saucepan, bring 2 cups of water to boil.
Pour in rice and add salt.
Cook uncovered for 10 minutes.
Strain and put rice aside. Separately, heat oil.

i: 1 (9 oz.) pkg. milk chocolate chips (not semi-sweet)
d: Melt caramels with water and butter.
Add pecans and vanilla. Drop by teaspoons onto buttered or Pam sprayed wax paper.
Chill.

i: 1 1/2 inch piece grated ginger (or 2 tsp. powdered ginger)
1 to 2 Tbsp. cornstarch (depending on desired thickness)
d: Combine all ingredients, heat and serve.
Heat oil in skillet over high heat.
Cut chicken into strips and add to skillet with garlic.
Fry for 3 minutes.
Add vegetables and fry for 3 minutes longer.
Reduce heat, add Miracle Whip, soy sauce and ginger.
Simmer 1 minute.
Brown ground beef in saucepan, stirring until crumbly.
Drain. Add spaghetti sauce and water.
Simmer for 10 minutes.
Combine eggs, cheeses, parsley and seasonings in bowl; mix well.
Combine all ingredients and mix well.
Chill before serving. Yields about 2/3 cup.
Combine cornstarch and 1/4 cup apple juice; stir well and set aside.
Combine peaches, 1 cup apple juice, sugar and 1/2 teaspoon nutmeg in a Dutch oven.
Bring to boil; cover and reduce heat and simmer 8 to 10 minutes or until peaches are tender.
Stir in reserved cornstarch mixture.
Cook, stirring constantly, until thickened and bubbly.
Remove from heat, stir in almond extract. Combine 1/2 cup flour, salt and 1/8 teaspoon nutmeg in a medium bowl.
Place all ingredients in a food processor fitted with the metal blade.
Process 4 to 5 minutes until double in volume and very smooth and creamy.
Pour into a glass container.
Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Makes 2 1/2 cups.

i: 8 oz. ground beef, browned and drained
1 (16 oz.) can lima beans or 1 (15 1/2 oz.) can butter beans, drained
1 (15 oz.) can kidney beans, drained and rinsed
d: Preheat oven to 350°. Brown and drain ground beef. Fry bacon and crumble. Mix together with other ingredients. Bake for 35 to 55 minutes.
Cut zucchini and squash into 3/4-inch slices or chunks to make about 3 cups.
Cut red onion onto 8 wedges.
Mix vegetables together and toss with salad dressing.
Fold into foil packets. Grill 4-inches from hot coals for 10 to 12 minutes, turning once with tongs or bake foil packets in 400° oven for 30 minutes.
Spray muffin pan with Pam.
In a large bowl, mix flour, cheese, sugar, baking powder and salt.
Set aside.
In a small bowl, mix egg, milk and oil.
Add to flour mixture.
Stir until moist.
Very good for steaks, etc.
Put beef shoulder in pan; heat oven to 400°.
Peel onion and cut up; cut up carrots.
Peel and cut up potatoes. Wash and cut up celery stick.
Put in large pan and cover with Reynolds wrap and cook for 3 1/2 hours.

i: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. hot dogs, cut in 1/4-inch diagonal slices
d: Mix the first 6 ingredients together.
Add mixture to the hot dogs, sliced and browned in the vegetable oil until curled.
Stir constantly until mixture thickens and hot dogs are coated.
To serve, add the sliced green onion.
In a large pot, heat the vegetable oil until very hot.
Stir in Sofrito and stir for 20 seconds.
Add rice; stir until all ingredients are well mixed.
Add black beans, water, salt, garlic powder, pepper and envelopes of Sazon; stir and bring to a boil. Add olives and/or Vienna sausages (optional).
Reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for 20 to 25 minutes (until all water is absorbed).
Stir rice once with a large fork after 10 to 15 minutes.
Mix together
shortening,
brown sugar and white sugar. Sift and add
flour,
soda, eggs, hot water, vanilla and salt. Add oatmeal,
nuts,

i: 1 tsp. fresh rosemary or 3/4 tsp. dried rosemary
d: Combine oils, mustard, lemon juice, rosemary, garlic, salt and pepper.
Mix well.
Marinate fish in mixture 2 hours or longer. Drain marinade and reserve.
Drain pineapple, save juice.
Put pineapple in baking dish. Combine flour, juice, sugar and cheese.
Pour over pineapple. Combine cracker crumbs and margarine.
Sprinkle over top.
Bake at 350° for 20 to 30 minutes.
Wash green beans; trim ends and remove strings.
Cook green beans covered in a small amount of boiling water for 10 to 12 minutes or until crisp-tender.
Drain beans and set aside to cool. Combine beans and tomatoes in a shallow container.
Combine olive oil, Worcestershire sauce, salt and pepper in a jar; cover tightly and shake vigorously.
Pour over bean mixture; cover and chill 2 or 3 hours.
Saute onion and pepper in butter.
Broil bacon and crumble. Rinse sauerkraut and place in baking dish.
Add the beer, molasses and mustard to onions, peppers and bacon.
Cook and stir for 3 minutes.
Pour over sauerkraut and bake 30 minutes at 350°.
Mix all ingredients together.
Drop by spoonfuls in hot oil. Cook until golden brown.
When done, they will float.
Drain on absorbent paper.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Lightly butter a cookie sheet with sides.
(I use a jelly roll pan.)
Arrange graham crackers side by side with edges touching on pan.
Stir butter and brown sugar over medium heat until butter melts and sugar dissolves.
Mix in vanilla and boil 1 minute.

i: 1 lb. Jimmy Dean pork sausage (regular or hot)
1 (28 oz.) can red kidney beans, drained
d: In frying pan, cook sausage well and drain off excess fat; set aside. Brown ground beef in frying pan and drain off excess fat; set aside. Brown onions in olive oil in large heavy pot. Add cooked sausage, ground beef and remainder of ingredients except green pepper. Stir well and simmer covered for 45 minutes. Add chopped green pepper and simmer 15 minutes longer.
Beat butter; blend in sugar.
Mix in beaten eggs and mashed bananas.
Add sifted flour, salt, soda and wheat flour, alternated with hot water.
Stir in chopped nuts.
Bake in a greased 9 x 5-inch loaf pan at 325° for 1 hour and 10 minutes.
Makes one loaf.

i: 1 (8 oz.) carton (1 c.) vanilla or lemon yogurt
d: Combine and blend well.
Cover and chill.
Serve with assorted fruits for dipping.
Garnish with orange peel twists.
Makes about 1 1/4 cups.
Pour milk in bowl.
Pour thawed orange juice into milk and beat.
Gradually add sugar.
Freeze.
When you thaw punch, add 2 quarts ginger ale.
Make pastry for a one-crust pie.
Line pie pan.
Place all ingredients into a bowl and beat together with a rotary beater. Pour into pastry-lined pie pan.
Bake at 400° for 35 to 45 minutes just until a silver knife inserted 1-inch from the side of the filling comes out clean.
The center may still look soft, but will set later.
Preheat oven to 325°F.
*Smoke with cherry wood.
Preheat oven to 375°.
Grease large baking dish.
Flatten chicken breasts.
Combine next 7 ingredients.
Work until smooth. Spread cheese mixture evenly onto chicken breasts.
Roll and secure with toothpicks.

i: 1 to 2 whole chicken breasts, cooked, cooled, shredded or sliced thin
d: Cook saifon, a little at a time, in 1-inch of hot oil.
Cook until noodles "explode."
Turn and cook other side.
Remove and drain on paper towel.
Thinly slice the lettuce, cucumbers, celery, bell pepper, scallions and radishes.
(Please improvise on salad if you wish.)
In a large casserole dish, spread refried beans evenly across bottom.
In skillet, brown ground beef; season with salt, pepper and garlic to taste.
Drain fat and layer over beans evenly.

i: 1 large head cabbage, shredded or thinly sliced
d: Combine shredded cabbage, green pepper and onion in large bowl.
Sprinkle with sugar.
Combine remaining ingredients in a small saucepan.
Bring to a boil and pour over cabbage mixture. Cover tightly and refrigerate at least 4 hours.
Toss just before serving.
Can keep up to two weeks.

i: 2 c. (11 1/2 oz. pkg.) milk chocolate morsels
d: Combine butter, evaporated milk, sugar and salt in medium, heavy saucepan.
Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring constantly. Boil for 4 to 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
Remove from heat.
Mix flour,
sugar,
salt,
and	oleo
as for pie crust. Dissolve yeast
in warm milk; add to eggs.	Mix dry and liquid ingredients
Mix everything together.
Pour
into
well-greased pan. Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

i: 1 Tbsp. onion juice or grated onion
d: Combine all ingredients except the poppy seeds into a blender.
Blend for 2 to 3 minutes.
Stir in poppy seeds.
Let it chill.
Makes 1 3/4 cups.
Mix margarine, sugar, milk, vanilla, pecans and coconut.
Roll into marble-size balls.
Put in freezer while chocolate is melting.
Combine pineapple and gelatin.
Cook until dissolved.
Cool. Add nuts and buttermilk.
Fold in Cool Whip.
Chill 2 hours before serving.

i: 2 (6 oz.) cans frozen grapefruit juice concentrate, thawed
2 (6 oz.) cans frozen orange juice concentrate, thawed
2 (6 oz.) cans frozen limeade concentrate, thawed
ice cubes or fruited ice ring
2 liters (33.8 oz.) club soda or sparkling water, chilled
d: In a pitcher, combine first 3 ingredients.
Chill until ready to serve.
Pour citrus mixture into small punch bowl; add ice cubes (or ice ring), as desired.
Add club soda or sparkling water.
Just before serving, garnish with orange slices and strawberries.
Brown and drain hamburger and onion.
Mix all other ingredients.
Bake at 325° for 1 hour.
Brown hamburger; drain.
Add salt and pepper to taste and onion, if desired.
In saucepan, heat milk, soup and enchilada sauces until blended.
Grease 9 x 13-inch pan.
Place 3 tortillas in bottom, top with a layer of meat, cheese, then soup mixture. Make 3 layers of this making sure to reserve enough soup mixture to pour over top.
End with cheese on top.
Combine dry ingredients.
Add eggs, oil, pecans and strawberries.
Stir just until all ingredients are moist.
Grease 9 x 5-inch loaf pan.
Bake at 350° for 60 to 70 minutes.
Great served with cream cheese and strawberry jam.
Mix and let set at least 4 hours or overnight.
At last minute, add 1 large can chow mein noodles.

i: 2 pkg. uncooked Jimmy Dean Fresh sausage
1 1/2 c. all purpose baking biscuit mix
d: Preheat oven to 375°. Shred cheese and mix all ingredients. Form into one inch balls. Bake 15 minutes on ungreased baking sheet until golden brown. Makes 6 dozen. Sausage cheese balls can be frozen uncooked.
For a firmer texture add an extra 3 cups of baking biscuit mix and an extra 2 cups of shredded cheese to the ingredients listed above.
Mix shortening, sugar, egg and molasses very well.
Either stir or sift flour before measuring.
Blend all dry ingredients and stir into wet mixture and chill.
Heat oven to 375°.
Roll dough into 1 1/4-inch ball.
Dip tops in granulated sugar.
Spread ribs, meaty side up, in roasting pan. Sprinkle with salt and pepper. Arrange lemon slices on ribs and brown, uncovered in 450° oven for 45 minutes.
Put the beans in water for one night.
Next morning strain them.
Put them in a saucepan adding two (2) liters of water. Simmer them until they become soft (do not boil them completely). Add the chopped onion, carrots, celery, salt and pepper.
After ten (10) minutes boiling, add the tomato sauce and the cup of olive oil.
Leave the beans to boil until they become really soft.
You must probably add a little water during boiling.

i: 1/2 c. fine egg noodles (uncooked), broken in pieces
d: In saucepan, brown noodles in butter; stir often.
Add remaining ingredients.
Bring to a boil; stir.
Cover; cook over low heat 20 to 25 minutes or until liquid is absorbed.
Makes about
2 cups.

i: 1 can beef broth and 1/2 can water
garlic salt, salt and pepper to taste
d: Brown beef; add onion, carrots and seasonings, parsley, broth and water.
Cover and bake at 350° for at least 2 hours.
Add mushrooms, drained and peas, drained.
Thicken with cornstarch. Serve with hot rice.

i: 1 1/4 c. pecans, finely ground in blender
d: Cream sugar and eggs.
Add pancake mix and baking powder, then ground pecans.
Mix all together well.
Place in 8 or 9-inch greased cake tin.
Bake in 350° oven for 20 minutes.
Frost with whipped cream.
Combine coconut, baking mix, brown sugar and nuts.
Add eggs. Mix with spoon.
Do not overmix!
Bake in greased 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan for 40 minutes at 325°.
Cool.
Cut into squares.
In 1-quart microwave-safe bowl, combine peeled peaches, sugar, cornstarch, ginger, allspice and salt.
Microwave on high, uncovered, 3 minutes.
Fold all ingredients together.
Refrigerate 8 to 12 hours before serving.
Pour into two 8-inch pie shells or 1 large pie shell.
Bake at 350° for about 50 minutes.
Heat to boiling point the molasses.
Pour it over shortening. Sift together and stir this into the flour, baking soda, ginger and salt.
Shape 1/4 the mixture at a time, keeping the rest of the dough in the refrigerator until you are ready to cut it out.
Roll as thin as possible; cut with small, round cutter.
Bake at 350° until crisp and dry (8 to 10 minutes).
Makes 100 or more.
Cook bacon in skillet (large) until crisp; remove bacon, reserving drippings in skillet.
Crumble bacon; set aside.
Add remaining ingredients in skillet; cook over medium heat, uncovered, for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in bacon.

i: 10 oz. can Pillsbury refrigerator pizza crust
d: Grease 1 large cookie sheet and unroll pizza dough, cutting the rectangle in half crosswise, then cutting 8 (1-inch) strips. Place strips apart on cookie sheet and brush with margarine. Sprinkle evenly with seasoning and put into 350° oven for 11 to 17 minutes.
Bring out long enough to sprinkle cheese on top and return to melt.
Serve immediately with warm pizza sauce to dip. Makes 16 servings.
In a Dutch oven cook ground beef and onion until meat is browned and onion is tender.
Drain.
Add the next 8 ingredients. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat; cover and simmer for 1 hour, stirring occasionally.
Remove the bay leaf.
Serve over spaghetti.

i: 1 (29 oz.) can peaches, sliced (do not drain)
1 (3.4 oz.) pkg. instant lemon pudding mix
d: Place fruit in a large bowl. Sprinkle lemon pudding on top and mix well until thickened. Chill.
Mix cake mix according to directions.
Pour 1/2 of batter into a 13 x 9-inch pan and bake until done, 20 minutes.
Melt caramels in 1/2 cup Carnation milk, then pour over cake.
Scrub potatoes, cut each lengthwise into 6 wedges.
Place in a bowl and cover with cold water.
Let stand 30 minutes and drain. Pat wedges dry with paper towel.
Toss wedges with olive oil. Place, skin side down, on a baking sheet coated with vegetable cooking spray.
Combine salt, dried rosemary and pepper.
Sprinkle mixture evenly over wedges.
To sour milk, mix 1 tablespoon vinegar with milk.

i: 2 bell peppers (green or red), diced (about 2 c.)
1 (15 oz.) can pinto beans, rinsed and drained
d: Heat oven to 350°.
In medium saucepot, cook pork chop, onion and peppers in olive oil over medium-high heat 7 minutes.
Remove from heat and stir in remaining ingredients.
Transfer mixture to an 11 x 7-inch baking or casserole dish.
Bake 30 minutes or until beans are tender.
Makes 8 servings.
Combine zucchini, onions and salt in water.
Let set overnight; drain.
Add remaining ingredients and cook for 3 minutes.
Jar and seal.

i: 1 (14 oz.) can Eagle Brand sweetened condensed milk
3/4 c. plus 2 Tbsp. cold margarine or butter
d: In bowl, combine berries, sweetened condensed milk and rind. In large bowl, cut 3/4 cup margarine into 1 1/2 cups biscuit mix until crumbly.
Add berry mixture.
Spread in greased 9-inch square baking pan.
In bowl, combine 1/2 cup biscuit mix and sugar; cut in 2 tablespoons margarine until crumbly.
Add nuts. Sprinkle over cobbler.
Bake at 325° for 1 hour and 10 minutes or until golden.
Rinse and drain the corn, green beans and garden peas.
Bring to boil vinegar, sugar, oil, water, salt and pepper.
Cool.
Mix with corn, peas and beans and rest of the ingredients. Refrigerate.

i: 2 medium pears, peeled, cored and chopped
d: In a small saucepan, soften gelatin in 1/2 cup of the orange juice.
Stir in undrained, crushed pineapple and remaining orange juice.
Chill until partially set.
Fold in pears and Equal and turn mixture into a 6-cup mold.
Chill until firm.
Unmold onto a plate.
Cut each potato lengthwise into 8 wedges.
Combine olive oil and Creole seasoning in a zip-top plastic bag.
Add wedges.
Seal bag and shake to coat.
Arrange wedges, skin side down, in a single layer on a baking sheet coated with vegetable cooking spray.
Bake at 450° for 20 minutes or until golden brown.

i: 2 whole, boneless, skinless chicken breasts (1 1/2 lb.)
1 medium size green pepper, seeded and chopped into 1/4-inch pieces
2 Tbsp. Hungarian paprika (available in specialty stores or use regular paprika)
d: This is a lean and light dish that is ready to eat in 30 minutes.
Beat eggs until light and frothy. Add sugar and beat.
Mix baking powder and graham cracker crumbs. Then add to mixture.
Add vanilla and then the nuts.
Pour into 2 layer cake pans.
Bake at 325° for about 25 to 30 minutes.

i: Small bottle barbecue sauce with onions
d: Put in pot and let simmer for about 30 minutes.
Combine until crumbly.
Pat into 9 x 13-inch Pyrex dish.
Bake at 350° for 20 minutes.
Cool.
Sift flour, soda, baking powder and sugar.
Cut in shortening and add buttermilk.
Roll out to desired thickness.
Cut out biscuits; place on baking sheet.
Bake 10 to 12 minutes at 450°.

t: Chicken In Red Bell Peppers(Low-Fat, Big Taste!)
i: 1 boneless, skinless chicken breast fillet per person
fresh or dry tarragon (if fresh, chop and use 1 leaf per fillet)
fresh garlic gloves or garlic salt
1/2 to 1 tsp. olive oil per bell pepper
d: Preheat oven to 350°. Tear off a big old piece of Saran Wrap and lay the fillets on top of it, leaving enough

room to put the remaining wrap on top of the fillets to cover and allowing room for flattening in next step. Pound the fillets with a kitchen mallet or your fist until they are fairly flat. Remove the top layer of Saran Wrap and sprinkle the tarragon over the chicken as well as either the garlic salt or thin slivers of garlic. Salt and pepper to taste. Core the red bell peppers and wash them out so that there are no seeds or whitish pulp inside. Roll up the fillets (like cigars) and stuff each one into a red pepper. Pour just a little olive oil into each red bell pepper and place them in a glass baking dish, lying flat. Pour 1/2 cup white wine and 1/2 cup chicken stock into the baking dish, along with a clove of fresh garlic that has been sliced or pressed. Cover dish with aluminum foil and bake for 25 minutes. Remove foil and cook for another 25 minutes.
Boil until sugar dissolves. Let stand 4 minutes; stir in Certo and put in jars. (Turn jars upside down occasionally so that peppers will be evenly distributed throughout jelly when it jells.)
Liberally spray large nonstick skillet with cooking spray. Add oil and heat over medium heat.
Add garlic and onions.
Heat until soft (approximately 3-4 minutes).
Fill large saucepan with 2 inches of water.
Place chicken breast in steaming basket or stainless steal saucepan with lid.
Bring water to boil and steam chicken for 7 minutes.
Brown hamburger and onion.
Cook macaroni in 2 quarts salted water, then drain.
Add 2 cans tomato soup to the hamburger and macaroni.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Can be covered with cheese, then heated until cheese melts.
Can add chopped celery or peppers to hamburger as well.

Cook pasta according to directions. Rinse and drain. Add garlic and onion salt. Add broccoli and cauliflower. Pour dressing over mixture and stir. Serve. Tastes better after several hours in the refrigerator. Stir before serving.

i: 1 c. or more green onions, chopped
d: Mix together the avocados, salt, pepper and lemon juice.
Mix the sour cream, taco seasoning mix and mayonnaise or salad dressing.
Mix the green onion, black olives and tomatoes.
In a 13 x 9 x 2-inch dish, layer each each of the following:
bean dip, avocado mix, sour cream mix, green onion mix and top with the cheese.
Serve with Fritos or taco chips.
Mix all ingredients thoroughly.
Spread in ungreased, 9 x 5
x 3-inch pan or casserole dish.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour to 1 1/4 hours.

i: 12 slices bacon, crisply cooked and crumbled
d: Grease 10 x 1-inch pie plate.
Layer crumbled bacon and cheese in bottom.
Beat remaining ingredients (except lettuce and tomatoes) until smooth 1 minute on high with hand mixer.
Pour into plate over bacon and cheese.
Bake at 400° until golden brown, 30 to 35 minutes.
Serve with lettuce and tomatoes.
Sprinkle salt over onions and squash.
Put ice on top.
Let stand 3 hours; drain.
Mix the vinegar, sugar, seeds and turmeric. Heat well.
Add squash mix.
Bring to a full boil.
Melt chips, stirring constantly.
Add cashews and noodles; stir until completely covered with chocolate.
Scoop mixture out with a tablespoon and put on wax paper to cool and harden.
You may also use any cereal.
Cheerios and Kix work well.
Melt butter in
large
skillet.
Add remaining ingredients, then pecans.
Stir well.
Arrange in a single layer on a baking sheet.
Cook spaghetti; drain well.
Set aside.
Saute onion in 2 tablespoons of butter in a large skillet over medium heat, stirring until tender.
Add tomatoes and salt.
Cook, uncovered, over medium heat 10 minutes; stir occasionally.
Add mushrooms and olives.
Drain tuna.
Slice green pepper into strips.
Slice zucchini into thin rounds.
Cut pimiento into thin strips.
Toss lettuce, tomato, green pepper, zucchini, onion, pimiento and olives. Gently toss in tuna.
Combine mayonnaise, vinegar, oil, paprika and cayenne; blend well.
Melt butter in skillet.
Saute almonds over low heat, watching carefully so they do not get too brown.
Remove and cool.
Mix chicken, pineapple, celery and 3/4 of the almonds.
In a small bowl mix wheat germ, honey, lemon juice and mayonnaise.
Blend dressing with salad.
Put all ingredients into blender container.
Cover and process until well blended.
Use 1 level tablespoon with 6 ounces boiling water for each cup.
Rub doves with salt and pepper.
Brown in skillet with oil. Saute onion and celery with Worcestershire sauce for 3 minutes. Place doves in crock-pot.
Pour sauce over.
Add remaining ingredients.
Cover and cook 10 hours on low heat.

i: 3 pt. Brussel sprouts, cleaned and halved
8 strips thick sliced bacon, cut crosswise into thin strips
d: Fill 6 quart Dutch oven with water. Add 1 Tbsp. salt. Bring water to a boil; add Brussels sprouts. Reduce heat to medium. Cook 12 minutes, until crisp and tender. Drain and rinse under cold water to stop cooking. In large non-stick pan, over medium heat, cook bacon for 5 minutes until slightly crisp. Pour off fat. Add onion to bacon in skillet and cook for 5 minutes or until tender. Add sprouts, remaining salt and pepper. Heat thoroughly. Add vinegar and butter, stirring to melt butter.

i: chili powder to taste (or 2 to 3 tsp.)
1 (7 oz.) box bite size spaghetti, uncooked
d: Simmer all for 1/2 hour.
Stir occasionally to prevent sticking to pan.
Cream the margarine and sugar.
Add eggs, one at a time, beating after each one.
Add vanilla wafer crumbs, alternating with milk.
Add vanilla, coconut and pecans.
Bake in greased and floured tube pan at 350° for 1 hour.
Makes 18 servings.

i: 2 qt. tomato puree (made from about 3 qt. fresh tomatoes)
1 tsp. minced garlic or 3 to 4 cloves garlic
d: Cook green peppers and onion and run through sieve or blender. Mix all ingredients together.
Boil for 15 minutes and simmer until desired thickness.
Pour into pint jars.
Process in boiling water bath for 10 minutes, or store in refrigerator.
Beat eggs on high speed for 5 full minutes.
Gradually beat in sugar.
Stir in pumpkin and lemon juice.
Fold in sifted dry ingredients.
Spread in greased and floured 10 x 15 x 1-inch pan. Top with pecans and bake 15 minutes at 375°.
Turn out on a towel sprinkled with powdered sugar.
Heat oil and vinegar in skillet.
Add garlic until heated. Add vinegar, wine, salt and pepper to taste.
Then add shrimp. Cook shrimp 5 to 7 minutes (don't overcook).
Boil water.
Cook spaghetti until done.
Drain.

i: 1 (16 oz.) can crushed pineapple with juice
d: Skin and wash chicken breasts.
Pat dry.
Place in a shallow pan.
Mix pineapple juice, soy sauce, oil and ginger.
Cover chicken with marinade and cover.
Refrigerate for 8 hours; turn pieces over once.
Blend in blender just enough to mix.
Chill and serve with chips.
Cook shells and set aside.
In saucepan, saute garlic and mushrooms in margarine.
Mix with cottage cheese, spinach and Mozzarella cheese.
Spoon cottage cheese mixture into cooked shells and place in bottom of 9 x 13-inch pan sprayed with Pam. Pour spaghetti sauce over shells and sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
Cook at 350° for 30 minutes or until done.
Combine cheese, mushrooms, onions and mayonnaise.
Spread about 2 teaspoons of the mixture on each toast round.
Place pepperoni slice on top.
Place on baking sheet.
Broil 3 to 5 minutes until cheese melts.
Serve immediately, or keep warm in 250° oven.
Mix all together; roll into 1-inch balls.
Place on cookie sheet.
Bake for 20 minutes at 400°.
In large kettle, brown pork until crisp.
Add onions and garlic and saute until onions are tender.
Add zucchini, cabbage, bay leaf and water.
Bring to boil.
Cover and simmer 45 minutes. Stir in beans, tomatoes, spaghetti, salt and pepper.
Cook 15 minutes until spaghetti is tender.

i: 1 small green pepper, cut into strips
1/2 lb. pork, cut into thin strips or canned tuna or any meat desired, cooked
d: Heat oil in skillet.
Add onion, green pepper and garlic; cook until tender-crisp.
Add tomatoes, okra and cooked meat.
Bring to a boil.
Stir in rice.
Cover.

i: 1/2 c. cucumber, chopped, seeded and peeled
d: In covered blender or food processor, combine V-8 juice, cucumber, lime juice and chili powder.
Blend until smooth.
Add ice cubes, one at a time, blending until ice is finely crushed. Serve immediately.

i: 1 (12 oz.) can frozen lemonade concentrate, thawed
1 to 2 (32 oz.) bottles lemon-lime carbonated beverage, chilled
d: Select a melon about 20 inches long with a flat base.
The day before serving, cut an 8-inch oval section making a sawtooth edge, out of the top of the melon.
Remove cut portion and reserve. Using a long handled spoon, scoop out pulp; remove seeds.
Place 1/4 of the pulp at a time in blender container or food processor bowl.
Cover, blend or process until pureed.
(Should have 12 cups.)

i: 1 (7 1/2 oz.) can crabmeat, drained and flaked
d: Combine crabmeat, green onion, Swiss cheese, mayonnaise, lemon juice and curry powder.
Separate rolls.
Separate each into 3 layers.
Place on ungreased baking sheet and spoon on mixture. Top each with 1 to 2 water chestnut slices.
Bake at 400° for 10 to 12 minutes or until golden.
Makes 36.
Melt oleo and chocolate chips in a sauce pan, cool and add nuts and marshmallows.
Shape into 3 logs.
Spread coconut on wax paper and roll logs in coconut to coat.
Wrap logs in wax paper and refrigerate over night.
Cut into thin slices.
Heat vinegar until salt and sugar dissolve.
Cool and pour over cucumber slices, onions and spices.
Refrigerate.
Will be ready in seven days.
Stir together the sugar, yeast and salt in a large mixing bowl.
Add hot water; mix and let stand 5 minutes.
Beat in egg and margarine.
Dump all of flour; mix well.
Use electric mixer, if available; do not knead.
Pour into a greased bowl.
Note:
Can top with any topping or sprinkle powdered sugar on top.

t: Orange Pineapple Punch(Make 1 Day In Advance)
d: In a 6-quart container, combine first 7 ingredients.
Place punch in covered container and freeze.
Remove to partially thaw. Break into chunks; add ginger ale and cherries.
Stir until slushy.
Makes 27 (6 ounce) servings.
Clean shrimp and set aside.
Add soy sauce and sugar to stir-fry seasoning; set aside.
Add peanut oil to electric wok set at 350°.
Add broccoli, mushrooms, water chestnuts, snow peas and green onions and cook five minutes, stirring often.
Remove from wok and set aside.
Place oleo in wok and add shrimp.
Dust chicken breasts with seasoned flour.

Heat a large saute pan over medium heat. Add oil and saute chicken to brown. When chicken is browned on one side, turn and brown second side and add seasoning, Dijon and heavy cream. Cover and let simmer until the chicken has finished cooking.
Add a little water if sauce gets too thick. When chicken is done, remove from heat and keep warm.

i: 2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen chopped spinach, thawed
1 (8 oz.) pkg. (2 c.) shredded Mozzarella cheese
d: Drain spinach thoroughly, squeezing out liquid.
In skillet, cook sausage until brown; drain fat.
Stir in tomato sauce; set aside.
In large bowl, combine drained spinach, cottage cheese, Parmesan and egg; spread mixture in bottom of 12 x 7 1/2 x 2-inch baking dish.
Spoon sausage mixture over spinach mixture.
Top with Mozzarella; cover and chill several hours or overnight.

i: 1 c. Minute rice, cooked in 1 qt. water, drain and cool
d: Dissolve Jell-O in 1 1/2 cups hot water, stirring until dissolved.
Add sugar and juice.
Let set until slightly jelled. Add remaining ingredients and fold in Cool Whip.
Refrigerate until firm.
Pour oil into skillet and heat.
Cut okra into thin, round slices.
Crack an egg and mix into okra slices well.
Sprinkle flour over okra and egg until well coated.
Add to hot oil and fry until lightly browned.
Drain on paper towels and salt to taste.
Combine dry ingredients in large mixer bowl.
Heat milk and oil in saucepan over low heat until warm.
Add 1 egg and warm liquid to flour mixture; beat 1/2 minute at low speed and 3 minutes at medium speed.
By hand, gradually stir 2 cups flour, Cheddar cheese and 2 to 3 cups more flour until soft dough.
Knead on floured surface until smooth and elastic.
Cover and let rise until doubled in size.

t: Old Time Molasses Dried Apple Stack Cake
d: Cream sugar, shortening and molasses.
Add egg and buttermilk. Mix well.
Stir in dry ingredients together with vinegar and soda mixture.
Divide dough into 6 balls.
Press each ball into well-greased 8-inch cake pan.
Bake at 350° for 15 minutes or until slightly browned.
Chop walnuts fine.
Sprinkle about 1 cup evenly in 9-inch square pan.
Set remaining walnuts aside.
Combine gelatin and 1/2 cup of the water in measuring cup; set aside to soften.
In 2-quart glass measuring cup with handle, combine remaining 3/4 cup water and sugar.
Microwave at High for 5 minutes.
Cream butter and sugar until fluffy.
Beat in egg.
Stir together flour, salt, baking soda, cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, raisins and pecans.
Add to sugar mixture alternately with applesauce.
Pour into greased 9 x 5 x 3-inch loaf pan.
Bake in 325° oven for 1 hour or until knife inserted in center comes out clean.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Cut top off tomatoes; scoop out pulp. Drain tomatoes upside-down on paper towels 10 minutes.
Heat oil and saute onion, garlic and peppers for 5 to 10 minutes.
Add corn, chicken, seasonings and sour cream.
Heat through.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Brown ground beef and onion; drain.
Mix cream cheese and sour cream in blender; add Ranch and taco mix.
Add ground beef mixture and blend. Put in casserole, 9 x 11 pan.
Chill; cover with cheese.
Can be served with diced tomatoes, ring peppers and salsa.
Make sure you don't forget the Scoops.
Dissolve jello in 1 1/2 cups water; add to juices. Add 1 quart ginger ale at serving time along with 1/2 gallon sherbet.
Dissolve gelatin in hot water; cool.
Add 7-Up, strawberries, pineapple and nuts.
Chill until firm.
Dissolve jello in water.
Add cranberry sauce and stir until blended.
Chill until partially thickened.
Add sliced bananas and pecans.
Spoon into mold; chill until firm.
Serve on greens. Yields 6 to 9 servings.

i: 1 small can diced green chiles or to taste
1 1/2 c. Longhorn or Jack cheese, grated
d: Boil the first 6 ingredients together for 5 minutes.
Line your soup bowls with crushed tortilla chips, tomatoes, green onions and cheese.
Pour hot broth over and serve.
Melt chips, margarine and peanut butter for about 3 minutes in microwave.
Stir in cereal.
Shake in bag with powdered sugar.
Combine juice, sherbet and 1/2 ice cream.
Beat until smooth. Add 7-Up or ginger ale.
Spoon remaining ice cream into punch. Serve immediately.
Yields 14 (1/2 cups).
Combine sugar and cornstarch in saucepan; stir well. Gradually add water, stirring until smooth.
Stir in vinegar and oil; cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until thickened. Add beets, salt and pepper and cook until thoroughly heated, about 10 minutes.

i: 2 Tbsp. wine vinegar or lemon juice
d: Skin tomatoes by plunging them into boiling water. Leave for 1 to 2 minutes.
Then plunge them into cold water and peel off skins.
Then place tomatoes in blender.
Remove seeds from pepper. Chop and add to tomatoes.
Peel and chop cucumber and onion.
Add with the garlic and egg to the ingredients in the blender. Measure remaining ingredients into blender.
Spread meat with mixture of garlic, pepper, salt, chili powder and mustard.
Cut into 1/2-inch wide strips.
Place in slow cooking pot.
Cover with onion, bouillon cube and tomato sauce.
Cover and cook on low for 6 to 8 hours.
Add beans and cook on high, covered for 30 minutes.

t: The Ten Minutes, No Fuss, Healthy Lunch
d: In medium bowl, combine the chickpeas, tomatoes, onion and tuna.
Mix olive oil, vinegar and pepper until blended.
Pour over chickpeas-tuna mixture.
Toss.
Serve with bread.
Serves 3 to 4.
Drain all fruit well.
Sift together dry ingredients.
Mix shortening in with fork. Add water; roll thin and cut into pieces.
Cook about 20 minutes.

i: 1 (19.8 or 21.5 oz.) pkg. fudge brownie mix
1 (14 oz.) can Eagle Brand sweetened condensed milk (not evaporated)
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
Prepare brownie mix as package directs. Stir in nuts.
Spread into greased 13 x 9-inch baking pan.
Bake for 25 to 30 minutes or until center is set.
In heavy saucepan, over medium heat, melt chips and margarine with sweetened condensed milk and vanilla.
Spread evenly over brownie layer. Cool. Chill 2 hours or until topping is firm.
Combine 1/2 cup butter and soups in saucepan.
Heat; stir until smooth.
Add rice.
Spread in 9 x 13-inch baking dish.
Lay chicken breasts on top, skin side up.
Brush with melted butter. Sprinkle with paprika.

i: 8 slices day old bread or toast
d: Preheat oven to 325°.
Cut bread into small cubes.
Put into large mixing bowl.
Melt margarine in large skillet.
Add onions and celery; cook until tender.
Add to bread crumbs.
Wash and cut cucumbers in 1/4-inch sliess.
Soak in water and lime 8 to 10 hours.
Wash several times to get lime out.
Put cucumbers in solution made with rest of ingredients.
Let stand 3 hours.
Boil in same solution for 30 minutes.
Cover bottom of Bundt pan with pecans. Mix sugar and cinnamon and divide into half.
Melt 1 stick butter and pour into half of the cinnamon and sugar mixture to form glaze.
Cut each biscuit into 4 pieces and coat with the sugar and cinnamon mixture.
Layer one can of biscuits in bottom of Bundt pan and pour 1/3 of the glaze over it.
Continue layering with the other two cans of biscuits and finish off with remainder of glaze.
Bake at 350° for 35 minutes.
Combine all ingredients in a 4 quart
Dutch oven; bring to boil.
Reduce heat; cover and simmer 1 to
hours or until peas are tender (add more water as
In mug combine lemonade concentrate and water.
Add cinnamon stick stirrer.
Microwave at Full power for 7 to 8 minutes or until "almost" boiling.
Stir bourbon into mug.
Float 1/2 slice of lemon on top.
Microwave at Full power for 1 to 1 1/2 minutes or until heated through.
Shake ingredients (except pretzels) in a jar. Put pretzels in a large container.
Pour mixture over pretzels; stir occasionally for 1 hour only.
Then freeze.
Put in Ziploc bags, then in freezer.

Wash cucumbers. Cut in strips. Pour boiling water over them. Let stand 4 to 5 hours. Drain and pack solidly in jars. Boil solution 5 minutes. Pour boiling hot over pickles in jars. Cook in hot water bath for 5 minutes. Makes 4 to 5 quarts.
Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
Lightly grease to large baking sheets with butter or vegetable shortening.
Sift together flour, sugar, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, ginger, and nutmeg.
Using a pastry blender or two forks, work the margarine into flour mixture until
thoroughly mixed.
Mix the chopped walnuts with the oatmeal.

t: Green Beans In Olive Oil (Lubiyeh Bi-Zaut)
i: 1 tsp. tomato paste diluted in little water
d: Wash beans in cold water, cut off ends and cut in half.
Chop onions and garlic and saute them in oil until golden.
Add beans, salt and allspice and toss lightly.
Cover and cook for 10 minutes over low heat; add tomato paste.
Slice tomatoes and fold in; simmer for 15 to 20 minutes until it thickens.
Can be served cold or hot.

i: 1 lb. boneless, skinned chicken breast halves, cut into strips
1 (7 oz.) jar roasted red bell peppers (undrained)
1 small can pitted black olives, wedged or sliced
1 pkg. tri-color fusilli, freshly cooked (twisty noodles)
d: Heat
oil in large heavy skillet over medium heat.
Add onion and garlic and cook until translucent, stirring occasionally, about 10 minutes.
Add chicken and stir until cooked through, about 5 minutes.
Mix in artichoke hearts (chopped slightly) with liquid, peppers (chopped into pieces) with liquid and olives and heat through.
Season with salt and pepper.

i: 1 large box lime or cherry Jell-O, dissolved in 2 c. hot water *
1 small bottle lemon or cranberry juice *
d: Mix all together and chill.
Makes 1 1/2 gallons.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Grease 8 x 8-inch pan.
Sift dry ingredients together.
Add shortening, half of buttermilk and 1 egg.
Beat 2 minutes.
Add remaining egg and buttermilk; beat 2 minutes more.
In a small saucepan, combine butter, lemon juice, water, soy sauce, paprika, honey, mustard, garlic, salt and cayenne pepper. Heat until butter melts, stirring well.
Grill chicken over medium coals about 15 minutes per side or until chicken is done, brushing frequently with sauce.
Makes 4 servings.

t: Sue Dexter'S Cuban Black Bean Soup
d: *(I do not use this much water.)
Heat margarine in skillet.
Cook chicken for 10 minutes or until browned.
Remove.
Combine flour, baking powder and salt in large mixing bowl. Add milk, blending thoroughly.
Add warm water in small amounts. Knead dough until soft, but not sticky.
Cover bowl.
Let stand about 15 minutes.
Peel cucumbers.
Slice lengthwise.
Remove and discard seeds. Dice cucumbers and place in bowl.
Add remaining ingredients and mix well.
Chill and serve very cold.
Cook vegetables 5 minutes or until tender.
Put garlic, paprika, olive oil and a little salt and pepper into the container of the blender, cover and start motor.
Remove the cover and gradually pour in the cooked vegetables.
Replace the cover and blend soup for 10 seconds, or until it is smooth.
Stir into the soup 1 cucumber, cut into tiny dice and the black olives; chill. At serving time, put an ice cube in each plate and pour over it the chilled gazpacho.
Sprinkle the soup liberally with chopped parsley and garnish it with tiny croutons.

i: 1 small can sliced pineapple, drained and cut in half
d: On double thick foil, mix all ingredients.
Wrap in foil. Place on grill for 15 minutes.
Just before serving, stir fruit well.

In an open saucepan, heat oil and add onions for 1 minute and add garlic. On medium heat, stir until tender. Add tomato and stir for 3 minutes. Add spices and tomato sauce. Cook for 3 to 4 minutes. Stir in sour cream and on low heat, cook for 3 minutes. Add fish; stir gently for 5 minutes. Cover and cook for an additional 15 minutes. Serve over white rice.

t: French Onion Soup With Garlic Toast
d: Cut onions in half and cut into thin slices.
Brown in Dutch oven with olive oil.
Fill pot with onions and water.
Simmer for 3 hours.
Salt and pepper to taste.
Add bouillon cubes, if desired.
Put shanks in casserole large enough to be in single layer. Add garlic, rosemary, wine, salt and pepper.
Cover casserole with foil and a lid.
Bake at 300° for 2 1/2 hours.
Remove shanks to warm plates.
Cook down liquid until thickened slightly.
Pour over meat and serve.

i: 3 Tbsp. rum or 1 tsp. rum extract
d: Combine applesauce, sugar, rum, grated orange rind and gingersnaps.
Stir until well mixed.
Chill.
Just before serving, fold in whipped cream and pile into sherbet dishes.
If desired, top with additional cream and nuts.
In large mixer bowl, mix ingredients in order.
Form into balls and roll in shredded coconut.
Refrigerate.

i: 1 large (29 oz.) can sliced peaches with syrup
1 pkg. Betty Crocker butter pecan cake mix
d: Heat oven to 325°.
Layer ingredients in order listed in ungreased, oblong 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan.
Bake 55 or 60 minutes.
Let stand at least 15 minutes before serving.
Good with vanilla ice cream on top.

t: Oven Fried Vegetables(A Weight Watcher Recipe)
i: 2 large potatoes (skin on), cut up into slices
1 large onion, cut up into slices
1 green pepper, cut up into slices
1 red pepper, cut up into slices
d: Put vegetables in a bowl and toss with oil.
Arrange in a shallow layer on a nonstick cookie sheet; sprinkle with seasoned salt.
Cook 20 to 30 minutes at 425°.

i: 8 cups fresh peaches, peeled and sliced
Pastry for double-crust pie, you can use refrigerated ready made crust
d: Combine peaches, sugar, flour and spices.
Set aside until syrup forms.
Bring peaches to a boil.
Cook 10 minutes or until tender.
Remove from heat and blend in almond extract and butter.
Mix all ingredients.
Cover bowl and let stand in refrigerator a few hours.
Form in balls.
Brown in oil.
Do not overcrowd. Place in crock-pot with your spaghetti sauce and cook on low for at least 8 hours and serve.
Mix well and pour into unbaked pie crust.
Slice 3/4 stick margarine on top.
Bake in 425 degree oven for 30 minutes or till set.
Heat coals in outdoor grill until hot.
Lightly oil or coat grill rack with nonstick vegetable cooking spray and set aside.
Cream butter and sugars.
Add eggs.
Sift dry ingredients. Mix with creamed mixture.
Add remaining ingredients.
Drop by teaspoon on cookie sheet.
Bake at 350°.

i: 1 pkg. Uncle Ben's chicken flavor rice
1 to 2 green onions and tops, chopped
d: Cook rice according to directions and cool.
Add onions, pepper and olives to rice.
Drain artichokes and save juice. Combine juice and curry powder with mayonnaise.
Pour over rice, sliced artichokes and shrimp.
Chill.
Better done day before.
Mix all ingredients together and store in an airtight container.
Brown sausage in large, deep pot.
Remove and add chicken. Cook chicken until done.
Remove.
Throw in vegetables and ham and cook until tender.
Add spices.
Add sausage and debone chicken. Stir thoroughly.
Sift dry ingredients together.
Add yeast to warm water and let dissolve.
Mix milk, yeast and shortening with dry ingredients until slightly sticky.
Form into rolls and place on ungreased baking pan.
Bake at 450° for 12 to 15 minutes.
Bake immediately or cover and store in refrigerator up to one week.

i: 6 pieces crumbled bacon or bacon bits
d: Layer the first 5 ingredients.
Sprinkle with sugar, cheese, salt and garlic powder.
Just before serving, add bacon or bacon bits.
Cover with plastic wrap.
Can be made the day before needed.
Drain beans.
In a large bowl combine sugar, vinegar and oil. Add beans, onion and pepper.
Stir with rubber spatula.
Keep refrigerated overnight.
Stir again before serving.

i: 5 lb. chicken, disjointed and cut up
d: Fry chicken pieces in oil until brown.
Saute onions in same pan; add sherry (or other liquid).
Stir, scraping brown bits loose and blending into liquid to combine with chicken in pan.
Add crushed pineapple and water or stock and braise 25 minutes.
Add bean sprouts and bamboo shoots.
Thicken with cornstarch.
Blend first 5 ingredients and set aside.
Mix dry ingredients. Mix dry ingredients with wet ingredients until blended.
Optionals can be added if desired.
Pour batter into greased and floured loaf pans and bake at 325° for 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
Yields 2 loaves.
Combine all ingredients.
Place pineapple rings on top.
Pour a little juice over top.
Cover.
Bake at 325° for 2 hours.
Brown hamburger and drain.
Mix with spaghetti sauce, drained mushrooms and water.
Layer in 9 x 13-inch pan: meat sauce, uncooked lasagne noodles, cottage cheese, Mozzarella and Parmesan cheese, beginning and ending with meat sauce.
Sprinkle top with Parmesan.
Cover with heavy duty foil and seal tightly.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
In a saucepan combine olive oil, butter and garlic.
Heat for 3 minutes.
Add clams.
In separate pot, cook linguine to preferred doneness.
Drain.
Place linguine in large bowl and pour clam sauce over it.
Mix together with a whisk.
Brush on meats or poultry last 20 minutes of cooking or grilling.

i: 1 lb. chicken breasts, cut in chunks
d: In a large skillet, over medium heat, cook bacon until crisp. Remove from pan and set aside.
Reserve 1 tablespoon of the fat. In the same skillet, saute the chicken, onion and garlic in the fat until tender.
Stir in the rice, broth, tomatoes, lemon juice, sherry and peppers.
Bring to a boil.
Cover, reduce the heat and simmer 25 minutes or until rice is tender.
Stir in the shrimp and bacon; cook and stir until the shrimp is cooked and pink, about 5 minutes.

i: 1 (28 oz.) can tomatoes with liquid, cut up
1 (4 oz.) can mushroom stems and pieces, drained
1 (2 1/4 oz.) can sliced ripe olives, drained
1 lb. ground beef, browned and drained (optional)
d: In a large skillet, saute onion and green pepper in butter until tender.
Add tomatoes, mushrooms, olives and oregano.
Add ground beef if desired.
Simmer uncovered for 10 minutes.
Place half of the spaghetti in a greased 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish. Top with half of the vegetable mixture.
Sprinkle with 1 cup Cheddar cheese.
Mix the first 4 ingredients together.
When ready to eat, add dressing and mix, then add crushed Fritos.

i: 3 (6 oz.) pkg. (3 c.) semi-sweet chocolate chips
d: Melt chocolate chips over hot, not boiling, water, stirring until smooth.
Spread about 1/3 of the melted chocolate in buttered 8-inch square pan.
Cover with marshmallows mixed with walnuts.
Pour over remaining chocolate, mixing slightly until marshmallows and walnuts are coated.
Let set until firm.
Cut in squares.
Mix meat, crumbs, onion, egg, salt, ginger and milk.
Shape mixture by rounded tablespoonfuls into balls.
Melt shortening in a large skillet.
Brown and cook meatballs.
Remove meatballs; keep warm.
Pour fat from skillet.

t: Grandma'S Moist Chocolate Cake And Frosting
d: Sift dry ingredients together.
Add milk, oil, eggs and vanilla.
Beat well.
Add coffee.
Blend completely.
Batter will be thin.

t: Italian Pasta And Bean Soup(Pasta E Fagioli)
i: 1 (16 oz.) can tomatoes or 2 c. chopped fresh tomatoes
d: In medium saucepan, saute garlic in oil just until it begins to brown.
Add tomatoes, beef broth, parsley, salt, pepper, basil, oregano, and beans.
Simmer uncovered, 20 minutes.
Stir in pasta to heat.
Serve with grated cheese and crusty Italian bread.
Melt butter; add sugar and curry powder.
Drain fruit.
Place in casserole dish.
Spoon sugar mixture over fruit.
Bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes. Stir occasionally.
Good with any meat dish.

i: Cheddar cheese or cheese of choice slices
d: Cook macaroni according to package directions; drain.
Set aside.
Saute onions, garlic and green pepper until tender.
Add ground beef and brown; add spaghetti sauce.
Bring to boil, then pour over macaroni.
Pour into a large baking dish or pan; cover with cheese slices.
In a large bowl, combine flour, sugar, baking powder and salt. Stir in blueberries.
Add milk, eggs and butter; mix just until dry ingredients are moistened.
The batter will be lumpy.
Do not overbeat.
Spoon batter into 12 (2 1/2-inch) greased muffin cups. Bake at 400° for 25 minutes or until tops spring back when lightly touched.
Serve warm.

i: 20 to 24 white large mushrooms, cut off stems and hollow out carefully, reserving stems
d: Fry and chop stems; drain.
Fry and drain sausage.
Mix cheese and bread crumbs and add mushroom stems and cooked sausage.
Mix all ingredients together.
Fill hollowed out mushroom caps with mixture.
Put in shallow baking dish.
Drain peas, pimentos and corn in colander.
Mix vinegar, sugar and oil together thoroughly.
Stir into vegetable mixture. Marinate overnight.
Great side dish.
Mix all together, then baste turkey.
Set 2 days in refrigerator.
Deep fry in peanut oil 3 1/2 minutes/lb. at 375°.
Dissolve Jell-O in water; let slightly thicken.
Add rest of ingredients.
Store in refrigerator.
Combine yeast and 1/2 cup warm milk in 2-cup liquid measuring cup; let stand 5 minutes.
Combine yeast mixture and remaining milk and next 4 ingredients in large mixing bowl.
Gradually add 1 cup flour, stirring until smooth.
Gradually stir in enough remaining flour to make soft dough.
Place in well-greased bowl, turning to grease top.
Cover and let stand in warm place free from drafts for 15 minutes.

Drain and mash the chickpeas well. Puree in blender. Beat oil and lemon juice and add to the chickpea mixture. Add salt, pepper and garlic to taste. Spread thinly on plate and make deep swirls. Dribble olive oil in the ruts. Decorate with chopped parsley, green olives and pickles. Serve with pita bread or crackers as an appetizer.
Mix Jell-O and water and let set until partially jelled.
Mix in other ingredients.
Top with pecan halves and chill until completely jelled.

i: 1 lb. Monterey Jack or Mozzarella cheese, grated
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
Cook spinach and drain well.
Melt margarine in 9 x 13-inch pan.
Mix ingredients in any order.
Pour into pan.
Bake at 350° for 35 minutes.
Sift together flour, baking powder and salt.
Heat oven to 400°.
Work shortening in a bowl until creamy.
Add the 1 cup granulated sugar and beat until well blended.
Add egg; beat thoroughly.
Add sifted dry ingredients alternately with lemon juice to creamed mixture.

i: 1/4 lb. ham or bacon, cut up
d: Brown floured and salted chicken in olive oil.
Set aside in warm place.
Add ham, onion, scallions and saute for few minutes. Add rice and spices.
Blend for few minutes and add chicken broth; salt to taste.
Cook on stove for about 10 minutes.
Place rice mixture and chicken in casserole and cook in 350° oven for 30 minutes or until rice is tender.

i: 1 bag of dole coleslaw mix in bag
1 pkg. Ramen noodles (chicken or oriental flavored)
d: Mix first 5 ingredients together in a large bowl. Set aside. In small bowl mix seasoning from noodles, vinegar, oil and sugar. Mix all together and serve immediately.
Bring butter and cheese to room temperature.
Beat together the butter and cheese until well blended.
Combine flour, oregano, salt and cayenne; add to cheese mixture, gradually.
Add water; mix until well blended.
Form dough into a ball.
Roll out on floured surface to 1/4-inch thickness.
Combine beans and enough liquid to make of dipping consistency in saucepan.
Add remaining ingredients, mixing well.
Cook over low heat until cheese is melted.
Serve hot with tortillas or chill and serve with chips.

i: 1 tsp. vinegar or cream of tartar
d: Beat egg whites until very stiff.
Gradually add sugar, vanilla and vinegar.
Keep beating until no longer grainy.
Pour into ungreased pan of choice (spring-form, Bundt).
Bake at 275° to 300° for about 40 minutes.
Cool and serve with whipped cream and fresh fruit.
Dissolve Jell-O in boiling water.
Add vinegar and salt.
Let start to gel and all 1 cup sour cream and onion and cucumber.
Mix well and gel until solid.

t: Aunt Mamie'S Tomato Relish(Good To Put On Vegetables)
i: 4 c. vinegar (red vinegar, White Oaks Brand best)
1/4 c. sugar to 1/2 c. to taste (varies with vinegar)
2 heaping tsp. each of mustard, cinnamon and ginger
d: In a cloth, put 2 heaping teaspoons of mustard (ground), cinnamon and ginger.
Tie up cloth.
Scald tomatoes and peel.
Cut up and mix all ingredients.
Cook until thick (use rubber gloves when cutting up hot peppers).
Cook about 3 hours until thick. Makes about 5 or 6 pints.
Active yeast (mix with water).
Melt margarine.
Add all other ingredients together with yeast and margarine in mixing bowl with dough hook attachment.
Mix until dough comes off the bowl.
Next proof dough until double in size.
Punch down; separate into 2 ounce balls.
Combine all ingredients and roll 1/2 inch thick.
Cut with 2 inch cutter.
Bake at 450° for 10 to 15 minutes.

i: 1 c. carrots, cut in small rings
d: Begin vegetables cooking in small Dutch oven.
Add just enough water to keep from sticking to bottom.
Let cook all the time while you are peeling tomatoes.
Use the quart of water in bottom of large pot and add peeled tomatoes.
Let cook until they begin to fall apart.
Add other vegetables and water they were cooked in. Add other ingredients, salt, vinegar and sugar.

i: 3/4 lb. boneless beef sirloin or top round steak
1 can Campbell's cream of broccoli soup
d: Slice beef across the grain into thin strips.
Heat oil in skillet; cook beef until browned, stirring often.
Remove beef from skillet.
In remaining 1 tablespoon hot oil, cook broccoli and onion with garlic powder until tender-crisp, stirring often. Add soup, water and soy.
Heat to boiling.
Return beef to skillet.
Put sugar over yeast in lukewarm water and let foam.
Scald milk, sugar, oleo and salt.
When cool, add dissolved yeast.
Fold in egg whites, stir in flour.
Dough should be soft.
Do not add more flour.
Cream the shortening, sugar and egg.
Mix molasses, water and soda.
Add to sugar mixture. Add sifted dry ingredients.
Mix well. Bake in 3 layers in a 275° oven for 50 minutes.
Spread with cooked, mashed dried apples, sweetened to taste.
Rinse and drain rice, sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Pour 1 c. tomato juice over rice, then sprinkle with the onion, pepper, and celery (if wanted).
Then the meat, water, and 1 1/2 c. tomato juice.
Cut the bacon in 1/2 and put on top.
Cover and bake at 350°F for 1 hour.
Uncover and bake for another 1/2 hour or until bacon is crisp.

Mix together Cool Whip, melted butter and sugar. Tear cake into pieces and put into mixture. Crush up the Butterfinger bars and put about 1/2 cup into the mixture. Sprinkle the remaining 1/2 cup onto the top of the mixture. Refrigerate.
In a large skillet, fry bacon until crisp; remove and set aside.
Drain all but 2 to 3 tablespoons of drippings.
Cook onion until tender. Stir in flour; blend well.
Add vinegar and water. Cook and stir until bubbly and slightly thick.
Add sugar and stir until it dissolves.
Crumble bacon; gently stir in bacon and potatoes.
Parboil chicken in water with vinegar (approximately 1/2 hour). Combine all ingredients.
Coat chicken.
Barbecue on grill.
Recoat as necessary.
Grill until golden brown.
Serves approximately 2 to 3 people.

i: 1 lb. ground beef or round steak, cut into small cubes
d: Wash rice in several cold water rinses until water is clear. Put into 3-quart saucepan with water to cover rice, about 1-inch from top of rice. Cook on high until this boils.
Turn on low heat with lid until all water is absorbed and rice is tender and flaky. Turn heat off and let steam with lid on.
Rinse and drain chickpeas and place in blender with other ingredients.
Blend well.
Sprinkle with a little olive oil. Garnish with parsley.

i: 1 lb. corkscrew pasta, cooked and drained
1 1/2 c. cooked chicken, that has been cubed
4 to 5 c. assorted cooked or raw vegetables (such as zucchini, bell peppers, green onions, cherry tomatoes, broccoli, etc.)
d: Toss all ingredients in a bowl.
Chill until ready to serve.
Crush chips in bag.
Toss together all ingredients, except tortilla chips.
Refrigerate.
Before serving, mix in chips and enough dressing to wet.
