Break fish in pieces and place in saucepan with cold water. Gradually heat to boiling and pour off water.
Repeat until fish is no longer salty.
Remove skin and bones and slice fish in very small pieces.
Blend fish with first five ingredients.
Serve on platter and garnish with lettuce, hard-cooked eggs and avocado pear slices.

i: 1 large pkg. fresh spinach, washed and stems removed, dry well with paper towel
8 slices bacon, cooked crisp and crumbled
d: Combine first 4 ingredients; chill well.
Make dressing with remaining ingredients (except croutons); shake well to blend. Pour over greens, a little at a time and toss (it doesn't take all of the dressing for 1 salad).
Add croutons just before serving. Serves 8.
Clean birds, do not split.
For each bird, saute 1/4 pound bulk sausage; add an equal amount of breadcrumbs and stuff birds with mixture.
Rub birds with olive oil and dust with salt and pepper.
Roast in 325° oven.

i: 1 (14 oz.) can Eagle Brand sweetened condensed milk
d: Mix all ingredients.
Pour into pastry shell and bake at 425° for 15 minutes.
Reduce heat to 350° for 35 minutes longer or until knife edge comes out clean.
Cool and serve.
Mix all ingredients together except cabbage.
Roll mixture in cabbage leaves and place in pan.
Pour 2 cups of boiling water over rolled mixture and cover with foil.
Cook for 1 hour at 300° to 325°.
Mix all ingredients thoroughly.

i: 2 lb. chopped veal or combo of veal and turkey
d: In the microwave:
Mix 1/2 of your spaghetti sauce with all your ingredients.
Using a quiche pan (micro-safe) form a loaf in a circle, leaving the center empty.
Pour the rest of spaghetti sauce over the top of the circular meat loaf.
Cook on High for 12 minutes.
Pour off drippings.

i: 1 1/4 cup chicken stock or beef stock
d: Heat oil in pan. Add the onion, red pepper and garlic. Saute until softened. Add the meat and saute
until browned. If desired, transfer the cooked mixture to a food processor and chop for a few seconds on the pulse setting. Transfer the meat
to a saucepan and add the stock, parsley, tomato paste, Worcestershire sauce. Add mushrooms. Cook over medium heat for about 20 minutes.
Fry meat and drain.
Add crunched chips and Tabasco; mix together.
Mix other ingredients in bowl; let meat cool and mix with salad.
Mix together the ground beef, beans, onion, oregano, 1 1/2 tablespoons of seasoning mix and 1 cup of cheese.
With a small spoon or using fingers, press meat mixture into shells.
Arrange in a 12 x 8-inch microwave-safe baking dish.
Combine all ingredients and mix well.
Refrigerate several hours before serving.
Beat eggs.
Salt fish and dip into eggs.
Mix flour, cornmeal and bread crumbs for breading.
Dip fish in breading.
Fry in hot lard for 4 to 5 minutes on each side.
Mix first three ingredients.
Drop by spoonfuls onto an ungreased cookie sheet.
Bake at 375° for 8 to 10 minutes.
When biscuits are still warm, brush them with melted margarine/garlic mixture.
Combine consomme and water in medium saucepan.
Bring to boil. Add remaining ingredients and let stand overnight at room temperature.
Makes about 2 quarts of sauce.
This is used as a basting sauce for barbecued meats.
Apply with small kitchen dishmop or cloth that has been soaked in sauce.
Lightly brown beef, onions and celery; add remaining ingredients and stir well.
Pour into greased casserole dish. Bake for 1 1/2 hours in 350° oven.
Wash and drain cucumbers.
Combine salt and water.
Pour over cucumbers and let stand 5 hours.
Drain; rinse thoroughly.
Combine remaining ingredients.
Add cucumbers.
Chop tomatoes, onions and sweet green peppers and sprinkle with salt.
Let stand 4 to 6 hours in cool place.
Drain well; add hot peppers, vinegar, sugar, salt and spices tied in thin cloth bag.
Simmer 15 minutes.
Remove spice bag.
Pack into hot jars. Close.
Dissolve
jello
in boiling water.
Chill until mixture begins to thicken
and
is
Put in bottom of quart jar the sugar, salt, mustard seed, turmeric, dill heads and garlic cloves.
Put cucumbers in jar. Boil vinegar and water; pour over cukes and seal.
Do not process. Shake them when cold to mix spices.

Cream together butter, sugar and eggs, then add salt and baking powder. Add alternately flour, milk and vanilla. Beat egg whites until stiff and fold into batter at last. Bake at 350° for 25 minutes. Makes three 9-inch layers.

i: 1 can Ocean Spray cranberry sauce (with whole berries)
d: Mix Jell-O according to package directions, then add remaining ingredients. Pour into Jell-O mold or bowl and chill until set.

i: 1 (1 1/2 lb.) top round steak or London broil
d: Place steak in a shallow dish or zip top heavy-duty plastic bag.
Combine remaining ingredients in a small bowl. Stir well. Pour marinade over steak. Cover or seal and chill 8 hours, turning occasionally. Remove steak from marinade.
Grill over medium (300° to 400°) coals for 7 to 9 minutes on each side or to desired degree of doneness.
To serve, slice grilled steak across the grain into thin slices.
Yields about 6 servings.

i: 3 whole large chicken breasts, skinned, boned and halved lengthwise
1 medium tomato, peeled, seeded and chopped
d: Place chicken, boned side up, between two pieces clear plastic wrap.
Working out from the center, pound each lightly with meat mallet to 5 1/2 x 5 1/2 inches.
Remove wrap.
Place a ham slice and a cheese slice on each cutlet, cutting to fit within 1/4 inch of edges.
Top with some tomato; sprinkle lightly with sage.
Fold in sides; roll up jelly roll fashion, pressing to seal well. Combine crumbs, Parmesan and parsley.

Preheat oven to 350°. Beat eggs at medium speed until thick and pale. Gradually add sugar; beat until blended. Add oil and vanilla; beat at low speed until blended. Combine flour, baking soda, salt and cinnamon. Add to beaten mixture and stir until blended. Stir in apples and pecans. Pour into greased and floured 12-cup Bundt pan. Bake 1 hour and 25 minutes or until tester comes out clean. Cool in pan. Remove from pan when completely cool. Glaze, if desired.
Heat brown sugar, corn syrup, margarine and salt.
Stir constantly 2 minutes.
Cool.
Pour over popcorn and stir together.
Preheat oven to 300°.
Butter a cookie sheet and bake for 15 minutes.
Mix all ingredients.
Store in glass or plastic container in deep freezer for 8 to 12 hours before serving.
About 30 minutes before serving, remove from freezer.
Mixture should be icy.
Any portion not used may be frozen again.
You may want to omit one can of pink lemonade and add one 10 ounce package of frozen strawberries.
Boil vinegar, sugar, salt and pepper until sugar melts.
Cool mixture and pour over sliced Daikon and let sit 1 hour and refrigerate.
Preheat oven to 325°.
Spray 2 loaf pans with vegetable spray. In a large bowl, using an electric mixer, beat together sugar, oil and eggs.
In a separate bowl, stir together dry ingredients. Add dry mixture to batter and mix just until blended.
Stir vanilla, apples and pecans into batter.
Pour batter into prepared loaf pans.
Bake for 1 hour or until inserted knife comes out clean.

i: 2 (8 oz.) cans tomato sauce with tomato bits
d: Saute onion, green pepper, ground beef and rice in oil in a large skillet until beef is browned.
Pour off excess fat. Add remaining ingredients and mix well.
Bring to a boil.
Cover and simmer 35 minutes, stirring once or so until rice is done.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.

Cut or slice cucumber, onion and pepper into bite-size pieces. Add olive oil, lemon juice, salt and pepper. Mix well. Place bowl covered with Saran Wrap, together with salad plates and forks, in freezer for 10 to 15 minutes before serving. Serve immediately.
Combine all ingredients except Fritos and lettuce.
Chill. Add Fritos and lettuce just before serving.
Combine milk, lemon juice and vanilla.
Mix until well blended.
Fold in remaining ingredients, one at a time.
Mound mixture into crust.
Freeze until firm, 2 to 3 hours.
Let stand at room temperature 15 minutes before serving.

t: Crazy Aunt Moo'S "I Married A Greek" Orzo Salad(Yield:  10-12 Servings)
d: 1. Cook box of orzo, set aside.
Grind zucchini to make 10 cups.
Combine with ground peppers and onions.
Sprinkle salt over vegetables.
Let stand overnight in fridge.
The next morning, rinse in cold water; press out.
Add turmeric, nutmeg, celery, pepper, cornstarch, vinegar and sugar to vegetables.
In a large bowl stir first five ingredients.
Pour into unbaked pie shell.
Lay pecans on top and bake 50 to 55 minutes.
Combine flour, confectioners sugar and margarine. Press into a buttered 9 x 13-inch pan. Bake at 350° for 20 minutes.

i: 1 lb. frozen fish fillets, cut in 1 1/2-inch cubes
2 medium tomatoes, cut in 1/2-inch cubes (2 c.)
1 large zucchini, cut in 1/2-inch cubes (2 c.)
d: Heat 1 1/2 cups water and wine in large skillet over medium heat.
Add fish; cover and simmer 3 to 4 minutes or until fish flakes easily.
Drain, reserving 1 1/2 cups liquid.
Heat corn oil over medium heat in large skillet.
Add tomatoes, zucchini, onion and garlic.
Cook 5 minutes or until crisp-tender, stirring occasionally.
Soak beans in water to cover in soup pot overnight.
Drain; rinse.
Add water to cover, onion, garlic, bay leaves and chicken bouillon.
Simmer over low heat for 1 1/2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Add tomatoes, green pepper, seasonings, ham chunks and brown sugar.
Remove bay leaves before serving.

i: 2 (3 oz.) pkg. lemon or lime jello
d: Dissolve jello in hot water. Add pineapple juice and sugar. Stir in ginger ale. Freeze until icy. Stir occasionally to keep mushy. Makes 1 gallon.
Blend well together and coat each serving with dressing, then sprinkle with Romano cheese.

i: 2 garlic cloves, finely minced, or 1/4 tsp. garlic powder
d: Drain mushrooms and set aside.
Melt butter and combine olive oil.
Add remaining ingredients and saute for about 5 minutes. Add mushrooms and continue to saute, turning occasionally, for 5 to 8 minutes (until thoroughly heated, but not browned).
Serve promptly with lightly spooned sauce.
Wash and sort beans.
Put in a large pan and cover with cold water.
Bring to boil and boil for 2 minutes.
Turn off heat, cover and let sit 2 hours.
Drain.
Put back into pan, cover with water and bring to boil.
Mix all ingredients and bring to a boil.
Simmer about 30 minutes or longer.

t: Souffle Crackers(Originated At The White House)
d: Preheat oven to 400° and butter 2 cookie sheets.
Put ice water in shallow dish or pie plate.
Using several at a time, put crackers into water in a single layer, pushing to just below surface.
Let stand 30 seconds.
Carefully remove with slotted spatula to cookie sheet about 1/2-inch apart.
When sheets are full, tip and drain off excess water.
Mix all ingredients together and place in an unbaked 9-inch pie shell.
Bake in a 325° oven until filling is set, about 50 to 55 minutes.
Peel and cut potatoes into bite size pieces.
In large pan, put enough water to first cover potatoes.
Cook until done.
Add milk, salt and pepper.
In small skillet, lightly saute onions in melted Parkay.
Add to potato mixture and simmer for about 20 minutes, stirring often.
Drain juice from fruit.
Melt butter; add sugar and curry powder.
Bake in a 9 x 13-inch dish at 350° for 1 hour, uncovered.

i: 1 (20 oz.) can crushed pineapple with juice
d: Combine dry ingredients.
Add pineapple and eggs.
Mix with mixer about 2 minutes.
Add pecans.
Bake in 9 x 13-inch loaf pan greased and floured.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Place rice, sugar and milk in a large saucepan.
Do not stir. Cover and heat over medium low heat until pot begins to steam, about 20 minutes (watch so that it does not boil over).
Uncover pan, stir and lower flame to simmer.
Cover loosely and cook for 45 minutes or until rice is tender, stirring frequently.
Mix beaten eggs with evaporated milk and gradually add to rice during the last 5 minutes of cooking.
(Stir constantly until mixture thickens, but do not let it boil.)
Arrange the chicken in a baking dish.
Heat marinade ingredients.
Cool slightly; pour over wings.
Bake at 325° for at least 1 1/2 hours, turning frequently.
The secret is in slow baking.
Mix the children and dogs together.
Put them in a field, stirring constantly.
Pour the brook over the pebbles.
Sprinkle the field with some flowers.
Spread over all a deep blue sky and bake in a hot sun.
When brown, remove and set to cool in a bathtub.
Mix well in glass baking dish all the ingredients.
Dot with 1/2 stick butter.
Bake at 350° until
set or it barely shakes in middle.
Mix together yeast, 1 cup warm water and 1 tablespoon sugar. Let stand for 5 minutes.
Add yeast mixture to other ingredients. Add more flour until mixture is dough consistency.
Let rise for 3 hours (beating down every 1/2 hour).
Break into buns (roll), let rise until desired size.
Bake at 350° for 15 to 30 minutes or until golden brown.
Heat the oil in a pot; cook the meat until it turns brown. Add everything to the pot except the kidney beans.
Stir well. Don't let stick.
Let it simmer for about 40 minutes.
Add the beans, water and all, if pot will hold it.
Simmer 20 more minutes.
Get 5 bowls ready and enjoy.

t: Italian Chocolate Cookies(A Very Old Recipe)
d: Make enough perked coffee to cover raisins.
Pour over raisins and let cool.
Sift dry ingredients together in mixing bowl.
Add shortening and milk.
Blend other ingredients except cranberries.
Beat 2 minutes.
Stir in cranberries.
Bake in greased 9-inch square pan at 350° for 40 minutes or until tested done.
Preheat oven to 400°.
Grease a 12-cup muffin pan or use paper liners.
Beat together softened margarine and sugar at medium speed until light and fluffy.
Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
Beat in bananas until smooth.
Mix together flour, salt, baking powder and soda.
Place meat and rice in pan; brown.
Put in casserole.
Add remaining ingredients, which have been coarsely chopped, in layers.
Do not stir.
Salt to taste.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Mix pudding and milk until thick, then add Cool Whip and sour cream; mix well.
Put a thin layer of pudding in a bowl, then a layer of wafers, then bananas and continue until you get to the top and end with a layer of pudding.
Blanch asparagus until tender. Put in cold water to stop cooking action and drain. Mix the remaining items together except egg yolk. Pour over asparagus. Add chopped egg yolk over top and serve.
Place butter and chocolate chips in medium heat resistant bowl.
Heat, uncovered, in microwave for 4 minutes, or until melted.
Combine egg and sugar; add slowly to chocolate mixture. Stir in marshmallows to coat well.
Pour onto waxed paper.
Shape into 18-inch log.
Refrigerate.

In skillet, cook beef and onion until tender. Drain. Stir in flour, salt and pepper until blended. Add pizza sauce and corn. Simmer 5 minutes. Transfer to a greased 13 x 9 baking dish. Separate biscuits and arrange over beef mixture. Sprinkle with cheese. Bake, uncovered, at 350° for 20 minutes or until biscuits are golden and cheese is melted. Makes 6 servings.
Mix the flour and soda together.
Roll 1/2 dough on floured board into a rectangle, 1/2-inch thick.
Spread filling all over top (then sprinkle with chopped English walnuts, if desired). Roll like a jelly roll and place in refrigerator.
When firm, slice and bake 10 to 12 minutes at 350° or 10 minutes at 375° (top of cookie may be sprinkled with sugar and top with 1/2 walnut before baking).

i: 1 1/3 c. vegetable oil or olive oil
d: In a small bowl or jar with a tight-fitting lid, combine oil, vinegar, garlic, salt and pepper; set aside.
In a large salad bowl, combine all remaining ingredients.
Chill until ready to serve.

i: 1 pkg. low-calorie Hidden Valley Ranch dressing, made with mayonnaise
d: Mix first 4 ingredients.
Just before serving, add remaining ingredients.

i: 1 lb. skinless chicken breast, cut into 1/2-inch slices
2 c. white mushrooms, cut in half
salt and black pepper as needed
d: Spray large wok with nonstick cooking spray; heat until very hot.
Add chicken and cook it for 4 minutes.
Remove from wok and keep it warm.

t: Zemakove Knedliky A Kysele Zeli(Potato Dumplings With Sauerkraut)
d: Mix potatoes, salt, baking powder and egg together.
Add 1 cup flour and work with hands as for pie crust.
More flour may be needed if egg is large or potatoes are fresh.
Make into a ball and knead.
Cut into 5 or more pieces.
Roll each piece into a ball or press together.

i: 1 oz. fresh ginger root, thinly sliced
d: Marinate lamb in soy sauce, brandy, garlic and cornstarch for one hour or more.
Heat a wok or large skillet.
Add oil to thinly coat bottom of pan.
Add salt and ginger.
Cook until ginger begins to brown and dry up.
Add lamb with marinade, all at once, and saute until lightly browned.
In a mixing bowl, cream shortening and sugar.
Add the eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
Blend in vanilla.
Add bananas and mix well.
Combine flour, baking powder, baking soda and salt; add to the creamed mixture alternately with sour cream, stirring just until combined.
Stir in nuts.

i: 1 can Del Monte seasoned green beans
d: Mix all ingredients together.
Combine lettuce, strawberries and cucumbers.
Toss.
Serve with vanilla yogurt dressing.
Wash
chicken	strips	thoroughly.
Drain.
Mix
Ranch dressing mix and buttermilk.
Soak chicken strips 2 to 3 hours or overnight in refrigerator.

i: 2 ripe plum tomatoes, seeded and cut into 1/4 inch dice
d: Preheat oven to 400°.
Cut eggplants in half lengthwise.
Make deep slits in the flesh (do not pierce the skin) and insert 2 cloves garlic in each half.
Place eggplants in shallow roasting pan, skin side down, and sprinkle tops with salt.
Cover with foil and bake for one hour or until eggplants and garlic are tender. Cool.
With a spoon, scoop eggplants and garlic out of skin into bowl.

i: 2 c. hot water and orange juice
d: Using juice from the mandarin oranges as part of liquid, dissolve gelatin in the hot water.
Add other ingredients and mix well.
Chill.
Miniature marshmallows may be added to mixture.
Cook noodles and drain.
Add red beans, mixed vegetables (drained), bell peppers, celery, onion and pimento.
Cook celery seed, salt, margarine, sugar, vinegar, flour and dry mustard until bubbly and thickened.
Pour over noodle mixture.
Let stand overnight.
Mix these ingredients together and pour dressing on just before you serve.
Brown meat and onion.
Chop tomatoes.
Mix with soup and meat. In baking dish, arrange layers of chips, meat mixture and grated cheese.
Repeat layers, ending with cheese.
Bake 15 to 20 minutes at 350° or until cheese is melted.

i: 1 (12 oz.) can pineapple juice, chilled (1 1/2 c.)
1/2 (6 oz.) can frozen limeade concentrate, thawed (1/3 c.)
d: Combine pineapple juice, water and limeade concentrate. Divide pineapple mixture among 6 tall glasses.
Spoon half of the lime sherbet into the glasses; stir gently.
Carefully pour chilled ginger ale into each glass.
Top with scoops of remaining lime sherbet.
Garnish with mint leaves, if desired.
Makes 6 servings.

i: 1 (6 oz.) can frozen limeade concentrate, thawed
d: Combine condensed milk, limeade concentrate and whipped topping in a bowl; mix well. Spoon into pie shell. Chill for 10 minutes or until serving time. May substitute lemonade concentrate for limeade concentrate, if desired. Makes 8 servings.
Combine all ingredients, but meat.
Mix well.
Pour half in an 8-inch iron skillet that has been heated in the oven with oil. Then put on layer of meat, layer of cheese and then remaining mix. Bake 30 minutes at 450°.
Heat milk in double boiler.
Add marshmallows and stir until melted.
Cool thoroughly.
Combine whipped cream, creme de menthe and creme de cacao.
Fold into cooled marshmallow mixture.
Pour into chilled crust and freeze.

i: 1 lb. sirloin, top round or chicken or both, cut into short thin strips
1 medium onion, cut into 1/4 inch wedges
1 medium green pepper, cut into short strips
d: Combine picante sauce, cornstarch, cumin and salt in small bowl; set aside.
In large skillet or wok over high heat, heat oil until hot but not smoking.
Add meat.
Cook until almost done.
Add onion, pepper and garlic.
Stir-fry until vegetables are crisp tender.
Cook onions and peppers in oil until tender.
Add hamburger and cook until meat falls apart.
Drain fat if necessary.
Add tomatoes and 1 cup uncooked macaroni or spaghetti or 1/2 cup rice.
Pour into buttered casserole and bake for 45 minutes or until tender in 375° oven.
Stir occasionally.
In a medium size baking dish, spread refried beans.
Combine sour cream and taco seasoning mix for next layer.
Mash ripened avocados with guacamole mix and spread.
The other ingredients may be added for the remaining layers.
Dip is better if allowed to chill for a few hours.
Serve with taco chips.

i: confectioners sugar (enough to thicken for spreading)
d: Cook together until it boils hard.
Remove from heat.
Add:

i: 2 c. or more cooked, deboned turkey or chicken, cut into bite size pieces
1 can cream of mushroom or celery soup
8 oz. sour cream (optional; increase milk if not used)
d: Mix all ingredients, except stuffing mix and 1 stick butter, together.
Season and place into lightly greased, medium to large casserole dish.
In another bowl, mix stuffing mix with stick of butter.
Mix well and spread evenly on top of vegetable mixture. Bake at 350° for 30 to 45 minutes; just long enough to heat mixture and lightly brown top.
Can be made ahead of time and frozen until ready to bake.
Serves 6.
Combine.
Bake 10 minutes at 425° and 40 minutes at 325°.
Peel cucumbers; slice thin and chill.
Mix other ingredients together.
Toss all lightly.
May be served on greens.
Makes 4 servings.
Mix all ingredients and roll in powdered sugar.
Bake at 300° for 1 hour.
Serves 5.
Beat eggs; add oil, sugar and flavoring and beat until thick and foamy.
Stir in zucchini, flour, wheat germ, soda, salt, baking powder and nuts.
Divide batter into 2 greased 5 x 9-inch loaf pans.
Sprinkle with sesame seed.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour. The best and mouth watering when eaten hot!

i: 2 or 3 cooked sweet potatoes or 1 can sweet potatoes (1 lb. 2 oz.)
d: Mash drained sweet potatoes.
Mix with beaten egg, margarine, salt, brown sugar and nutmeg.
Arrange slices of pineapple in a lightly greased shallow baking pan.
Mound the sweet potato mixture on each slice.
Brush with additional margarine, if desired.
Bake at 425° for about 15 minutes.

i: 8 oz. mild or hot Italian sausage
d: In a Dutch oven, brown ground beef and sausage; drain excess fat.
Add pureed tomatoes, tomato paste, sugar, 1 tablespoon parsley, basil, garlic and 1 teaspoon salt.
Simmer, uncovered, for 30 minutes.
Mix all ingredients well.
Keep covered 3 days, mixing every day.
On fourth day, roll out in logs.
Bake at 175° for 8 hours. Turn after 4 hours.

t: Creole Seasoning (Make Your Own)(Club Member: Renee Evans)
d: Mix well and use like salt. Store in airtight container.

i: 2 medium apples, peeled, cored, and chopped
d: Blend together eggs, oil, and sugar until creamy.
Add flour, a small amount at a time, blending well after each addition. Batter will be stiff.
Fold in apples, coconut, and pecans.
Place in greased and floured 10 inch tube pan;
bake at 350 degrees about one hour or until wooded pick inserted in center of cake comes out
clean.
Mix rice, soup mix, onion, mushroom soup and water in baking dish.
Sprinkle breasts with salt and pepper; put on top of rice mixture.
Bake uncovered 1 hour and 30 minutes at 325°.
Serves 6.
Soften cream
cheese.
Whip
cream
cheese and lemonade with mixer.
Use a fork to whip in strawberries and Cool Whip. Pour in prebaked pie shells and chill.
Wash squash.
Slice in 1/4-inch slices.
Peel and slice onions.
Mix squash, onions and salt thoroughly.
Cover with ice; let stand 3 hours.
Drain thoroughly.

i: 2 lb. stewing beef, roast or round roast
d: Boil in open pan for 15 minutes with 1/4 cup vinegar and water to cover. Pour off all liquid. Put in pressure and add water, salt, pepper with 1/2 apple and small onion in cavity. Cook about 2 hours on 10 pounds pressure. Take liquid and make dressing if you like it.
Brown bacon, pepper and onion in skillet.
Drain grease and break up bacon.
Add beans, catsup and syrup to skillet.
Let simmer 45 minutes.

i: 2 to 3 lb. chicken wings, cut at the joint, tips discarded
d: Combine sugar, water, soy sauce, pineapple juice, oil, ginger and garlic powder.
Stir until sugar is dissolved.
Pour over chicken wings in bowl small enough so that all are covered.
Cover and refrigerate.
Marinate at least one full day, stirring occasionally.

i: 1 (2 lb.) can Great Northern beans (not drained)
d: Brown sausage and onion.
Add remaining ingredients and bake in casserole at 350°.
Serves 4.
Sausage Casserole plus a salad makes a fine low cholesterol meal.

i: 4 lb. small red potatoes, cooked and cut into 3/4-inch chunks
d: Put potatoes in a large bowl; sprinkle with olive oil and vinegar, tossing lightly to coat.
Mix mayonnaise, yogurt, mustard, salt and pepper in a bowl; stir in onion and dill.
Pour over the potatoes; toss to coat.
Cover and chill.
Best if prepared a day or two before serving.
This always gets rave reviews!!

i: 1 c. light brown sugar, firmly packed
d: Preheat oven to 375°.
Sift flour with soda, salt, ginger and cloves.
Set aside.
In a large bowl, with wooden spoon, or portable electric mixer at medium speed, beat shortening, peanut butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
Add eggs and milk. Beat until smooth.
Stir in flour mixture and peanuts until thoroughly combined. Drop by rounded teaspoonfuls 2-inches apart onto ungreased cookie sheet.
May substitute bean of your choice.

i: 6 oz. sharp Cheddar cheese, softened and shredded
1 1/2 to 2 c. finely chopped green onion
d: In a bowl,
beat
together
cheeses,
curry powder, dry sherry and salt.
Spread

t: Mango With Tomatoes And Scallions Or Leeks
d: Slice mango, chunk tomatoes and slice leek; toss well.
Whisk together the lemon juice and pulp, safflower oil and dry mustard; pour over mango mixture.
Toss well.
Serve chilled or at room temperature.
Bone chicken; remove skin and chop into half-inch pieces. Clean scallions; cut crosswise into quarter-inch pieces.
Add to chicken.
Add sugar, salt, soy sauce, wine, egg white and cornstarch to chicken.
Marinate for 30 minutes.
Peel cukes.
Cut in half lengthwise; scoop out seeds.
Combine first 8 ingredients in a jar.
Cover tightly and shake vigorously.
Add blue cheese; stir mixture before serving.
Dissolve Knox gelatine in cold water.
Dissolve sugar and jello gelatin in the boiling water.
Add juice and Knox gelatine mixture. Pour into 9 x 13-inch pan.
Refrigerate.
Cut in squares and serve as finger gelatin.
Mix the first 3 ingredients together in small bowl.
Set aside.
Put egg whites in a large mixing bowl and beat until it forms peaks.
Slowly add sugar, then vanilla.
Add first bowl of dry ingredients and fold together gently with large spoon.
Pour into 9-inch pie plate.
Mix all ingredients except chocolate milk.
Add milk.
Pour into 4-quart ice cream freezer bucket and freeze according to manufacturer's instructions.
Let harden 1 hour before serving. Yields 1 gallon.

i: 2 c. Nabisco 100% Bran (2 c. boiling water added to bran)
d: Cream shortening and sugar.
Add eggs, buttermilk and soaked Nabisco Bran.
Sift flour, soda and salt and add Kellogg's All-Bran.
Stir.
Store in refrigerator.
Bake for 15 minutes at 400°.

i: 2 (15 oz.) cans baked beans with liquid
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
Brown bacon in skillet; drain and crumble.
Set aside.
Add onion and garlic to bacon drippings in pan; saute until onion is soft.
Combine all ingredients in bean pot or covered ovenproof casserole dish.
Bake 1 1/2 hours.
Combine squash, onions and salt; cover and let set overnight. In morning, drain good.
Combine remaining ingredients; bring to a boil.
Fill jars with squash mixture and hot liquid.
Seal.
Combine softened cream cheese, margarine, sugar and vanilla until well blended.
Add flour and salt.
Mix well.
Chill.
On a lightly floured surface, roll dough to 1/4-inch thickness.
Cut with assorted cookie cutters.
Combine onion, carrots and hot shortening together; cook slowly for 5 minutes.
Add bread cubes, milk, salt, sage and pepper; mix well.
Dissolve Jell-O in 1 cup of boiling water.
Then add remaining ingredients in punch bowl.

i: 6 c. mixed salad greens (romaine, bibb, Boston, radicchio)
d: Combine first 6 ingredients and stir well with a whisk. Combine greens and strawberries in a large bowl. Add vinegar mixture. Toss to coat. Sprinkle with pine nuts (if desired).
Preheat oven to 400°.
Line a 12-cup muffin pan with paper liners.
In a bowl, combine wheat and oat brans, whole wheat flour, baking soda, baking powder, cinnamon and nutmeg.
In a separate bowl, combine all other ingredients.
Pour onto dry mixture and mix until just combined. Scoop by 1/4 cupfuls into prepared muffin cups.
Bake 14 to 16 minutes or until toothpick comes out sticky, but not wet.
Cook lima beans following package directions.
Drain if necessary.
Add vinegar, olive oil, salt, pepper, parsley and garlic to limas in saucepan.
Heat thoroughly and then mix in lemon juice.
Serve immediately.
Serves 4.

i: 2 lb. washed clean, deveined shrimp tails
d: Boil 2 quarts water to which has been added the next 6 ingredients.
Add shrimp tails; cook on medium heat until shrimp turn pink.
Remove shrimp; save stock.
Make a dark roux of next 4 ingredients.
Keep roux soft; add shrimp stock, salt, hot sauce and tomato sauce.
Simmer until tomato color is dark.
Boil or cook in microwave, small red potatoes.
Skewer red potatoes, cut in half, and white onion pieces.
Cook on barbecue or Jennaire or Farberware.
Toss in dressing.
Enjoy.

i: salt, pepper and Tabasco sauce to taste
d: Saute chopped peppers, onions and celery until soft in large pan.
In separate pan, brown hamburger and drain.
Place beans in a 12 x 18-inch baking dish.
Add hamburger and onion mixture. Combine gently.
Cut potatoes lengthwise into slices.
Mix with olive oil; sprinkle with seasonings.
Shake in large storage bag.
Place on nonstick baking pan.
Bake 20 minutes at 450° or until done.
Cut chicken into bite size pieces and saute in skillet using Pam, until brown.
To Dutch oven add juice from 20 oz. can of pineapple chunks, brown sugar, water, soy sauce, vinegar and ginger.
Heat through and thicken sauce with 3 Tbsp. cornstarch in 1/4 c. of water.
Cut green pepper into bite size chunks and add to sauce along with chicken and pineapple chunks.
Simmer until heated through and green pepper is tender.
Serve over rice.
In medium size bowl, combine chicken, apple, celery, nuts and grapes.
In small bowl, combine mayonnaise, lime juice and honey; blend well.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Spoon dressing over chicken salad and toss to coat.
Serve on bed of leaf lettuce with croissants.
Bake cake mix according to instructions on box. Cool completely; break into small pieces. Mix together Jell-O, Dream Whip and milk; beat well. Refrigerate 1 hour.
In casserole dish, layer as follows:
Mix all ingredients together and beat until cheese melts, approximately 20 minutes.
Serve with your favorite cracker.
Melt chips and butter and cool.
Add marshmallows, nuts and vanilla and mix until all marshmallows are covered with the chocolate.
Sprinkle coconut on waxed paper.
Butter hands and put mixture on top of coconut and shape into roll, having coconut on the outside of roll.
Refrigerate until cold.
Makes about 3 rolls.

t: Pasta With Tomatoes And Four Cheeses
i: 2 3/4 lb. tomatoes, cored and cut into 3/4-inch dices
1 c. Ricotta cheese, at room temperature
1/2 c. Fontina cheese, cut into 1/4-inch dices
1/2 c. Mozzarella cheese, cut into 1/4-inch dices
1 lb. rotelle or other short pasta
d: Combine first 4 ingredients in medium bowl.
Let stand at room temperature 1 to 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Fluff Ricotta with fork; thin to creamy consistency with milk.
Season with pepper and nutmeg.
Mix in Fontina and Mozzarella.
Let stand at room temperature while cooking pasta.
Mix first 6 ingredients and pour into greased 9 x 13-inch or two 8 x 8-inch pans.
Sprinkle cake mix over mixture.
Press nuts into cake mix.
Drizzle melted margarine over cake mix.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Can be frozen.
Slice onions about 1/8-inch thick and separate into rings. Roll each ring in flour, then dip in mixture made of the eggs and milk.
Roll in cracker crumbs and fry in a wire basket in furiously boiling cooking oil.
Remove and drain.
Over medium heat add butter, onion, green pepper, saute until tender, about 5 minutes.
Add beef and saute 2 minutes.
Add lemon juice, worcestershire sauce, ketchup, brown sugar, mustard, salt and hot pepper sauce to skillet, mix well.
Beat eggs, adding sugar gradually.
Beat until smooth.
Add cooled margarine and vanilla or anise.
Sift flour and baking powder.
Blend into egg mixture until smooth.
Dough will be sticky enough to be dropped by spoon.
Preheat the oven to 400°.
Lightly spray an 8 x 8 x 2-inch baking pan.

i: 1 medium sweet onion, diced or green onion
d: Tear lettuce into small pieces.
Place in a 9 x 12-inch glass dish.
Layer next 6 ingredients in order given.
Add sugar to mayonnaise and spread over top as you would frosting.
Top with grated cheese.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 8 to 12 hours. Garnish with bacon bits and parsley.
Brown meat and drain. Add all ingredients. Simmer for 20 to 30 minutes. Serve with tostado chips or corn bread.
1. Soak beans overnight. Drain water and place beans in crock pot. Add fresh water until beans are covered.
2. Saute onions, garlic, and peppers in olive oil and add
to crock pot. 3. Add worcestershire sauce and slow cook for 2 hours.
4. Add hot sauce, salt, and pepper to taste. Dice ham and
add before slow cooking for 2 hours or until beans are
at desired consistency.
Pierce eggplants at different points.
Broil for 30 minutes, turning occasionally (the surface should be burned and cracked). Remove skin, drain pulp.
Combine all ingredients in blender; puree until smooth.
Garnish with chopped parsley and olive oil. Eat with fresh pita bread.
Saute shrimp and clams in olive oil until cooked to your liking.
Add clam sauces and garlic.
Simmer for 1/2 hour over low heat; cook linguini until tender.
Add butter.
Pour cooked ingredients over linguini and serve hot.
Brown ground beef with small chopped onion and drain.
Add 1/2 can soup and 1/2 cup water.
Add Velveeta (cubed).
Pour into casserole dish.
Put Tater Tots on top.
Mix 1/2 can soup and 1/2 can water and pour over Tater Tots.

i: 1 (10 oz.) can Pillsbury refrigerated All-Ready pizza crust
d: Heat oven to 425°.
Grease 12-inch pizza pan with oil; sprinkle with cornmeal.
Unroll dough; press in greased pan.
In large bowl, combine chicken, chili powder, cumin and garlic salt. Mix well.
Spoon chicken evenly over dough; top with tomato, olives, onions, green chilies, Monterey Jack and Cheddar cheese. Bake at 425° for 20 to 24 minutes or until golden brown.
Serve topped with avocado slices and salsa.
Cut zucchini into
2 x 1/2-inch strips.
Cut carrots into matchstick strips.
Cut green onions into 2-inch pieces.
Bring to a bubble.
Do not boil.
Strain and put in container to use later.
Keep in refrigerator.
Heat when ready to serve.

i: 5 (1/2 inch) slices day old French bread, cubed
d: Saute garlic in olive oil in a large nonstick skillet until tender.
Add water mixing well.
Add bread crumbs, tossing to coat; remove and discard garlic.
Spread bread cubes evenly on a baking sheet and bake at 325° for 25 minutes or until golden brown.
In a large blender, combine tomato, 1/2 cucumber, 1/2 onion, 1/4 green bell pepper and 1/2 cup tomato juice.
Puree.
Dissolve sugar and Jell-O in 2 cups boiling water, then add 2 cups cold water.
Add fruit juices and stir.
Chill.
Add 1 bottle ginger ale right before serving.
You may also float sherbet on top if desired.
Cook chickpeas in enough water to keep covered.
Cook for approximately 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
Taste peas from time to time until done.
Save water when done.
In a food processor, add the ingredients in "batches."
Add chickpea water to keep it smooth (a thicker than pudding look).
Place 1/2 cup of cornstarch in a small bowl.
Gradually stir in 1/4 cup of water.
If mixture is too dry, add more water.
If too runny, add more cornstarch.
Food coloring may be added, if desired.
Slime will feel dry and solid to the touch, but if picked up, will dribble through your fingers.

i: 1 large can crushed pineapple with juice
d: Mix all ingredients by hand.
Fold in Cool Whip.
Refrigerate all day or overnight.
Will keep for 7 to 10 days in fridge.

i: 2 lb. assorted potatoes (fingerling, yellow, purple; larger ones halved)
3 large carrots, peeled and cut into bite-size pieces
d: Heat oven to 425°.
Grease 13 x 9-inch roasting pan.
Add potatoes, onions, carrots, olive oil, rosemary, salt and pepper; toss to coat all ingredients.
Roast in oven, stirring occasionally, for 45 minutes to 1 hour or until potatoes are pierced easily with a knife.
Makes 6 servings.

t: Cicoria Fina Agliata(Dandelion Sauteed With Garlic)
d: Clean and wash greens, cut in half.
Place in saucepan.
Cook for 10 to 15 minutes or until tender.
Remove and drain.
Heat oil and garlic in frying pan.
Place greens in pan.
Grind the cucumbers, onions and peppers; mix together with the salt and 2 quarts boiling water.
Let stand for 1 hour; drain well.
Add spices, vinegar and sugar.
Boil for 1/2 hour.
Add a few drops of green food coloring.
Seal hot.
Prepare unbaked pie crust. Whisk together eggs and the next 7 ingredients until blended. Stir in pecans and the melted chocolate.
Pour into pie crust. Bake on lowest oven rack at 350° for 1 hour or until set. Cool completely.

i: 1 lb. fresh mushrooms, cleaned and sliced
d: In a large saucepan, combine tomatoes and seasonings.
Add vegetables and stir until thoroughly moistened. Cook over medium-high heat until vegetables are tender, stirring occasionally. Combine cornstarch and water.
Add shrimp.
Heat and stir just until boiling.
Remove bay leaf. Serve over rice.
Makes 6 servings.

i: 1/2 c. finely minced onion or 6 scallions
d: Rinse chickpeas and drain.
Combine all ingredients besides flour in a food processor or bowl.
Mash to a uniform batter.
Add flour, stir thoroughly.

i: 1/3 to 1/2 c. vinegar or lemon juice
d: Combine all ingredients in a bottle.
Cover tightly and shake well.
Chill and remove the garlic.
Shake thoroughly before serving.
Makes 1 1/3 to 1 1/2 cups.
Mix.
Bake
in	three
9-inch pans at 325° for 25 to 30 minutes or until done.
Melt butter, peanut butter and chocolate chips. Pour in box of Rice Chex, then put Rice Chex in bag of powdered sugar.
Combine celery, onion, bay leaf, garlic and water.
Cook for 15 minutes, or until celery is tender.
Remove bay leaf.
Drain; reserve liquid.
Set vegetables aside.
Add water to reserved liquid to make 2 cups.

i: 2 envelopes dry chicken noodle soup mix
d: Brown sausage; pour off grease.
Bring water to boil.
Add soup; boil for 7 minutes.
Add to sausage and vegetables.
Pour into greased casserole pan; sprinkle sliced almonds on top.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Brown onion and meat.
Drain.
Add remaining ingredients and bake 30 to 45 minutes at 325°.

i: 1 head broccoli, cut into large flowerets
1/4 head cauliflower, cut into large flowerets
1/2 c. green onion, chopped real fine
1 medium size tomato, cut into bite size
d: Steam first 3 ingredients crispy.
In a salad bowl, throw first 6 ingredients.
Add remaining ingredients to your taste. Mix well.

i: 2 (7 oz.) cans lemon-lime carbonated beverage, well chilled
d: Dissolve gelatin and dash of salt in 2 cups hot water; cool to room temperature and add lemon juice.
Carefully pour lemon-lime beverage down side of bowl; stir gently with up and down motion. Chill until partially set.
Meanwhile, core apples and slice very thinly.
Add apples and grapes to gelatin mixture, stirring gently with up and down motion.
Pour into 2-quart ring mold.
Chill until firm.

i: 1 small jar sliced mushrooms or 1/2 c. fresh
1 pkg. Good Seasons Italian dressing mix
d: Combine all
vegetables.
You
will
want more broccoli than cauliflower.
Cut onions in wheels and cut up some of the blades in

i: 1 lb. hot links, cut in bite size pieces
2 lb. plus shrimp, peeled and cleaned
d: Start a pound or so of hot, smoked stuffed sausage in a quart of water.
Add shrimp and crabmeat.
Let this mixture simmer while you dice celery, bell peppers and onions.
Fry these in bacon fat over low heat until done but not brown.
(A covered skillet is best for this.)
When done, add to seafood mixture and continue simmering.

i: lettuce, broccoli, tomatoes, cucumbers, walnuts and green peppers
d: Cut chicken into bite size pieces.
Saute in oil until done. Combine soy sauce, oil, garlic and lemon juice.
Add the cooked chicken and cut up lettuce, broccoli, tomatoes, cucumbers, walnuts and peppers.
Add can of water chestnuts (drained).
Toss with chicken mixture.
Marinate overnight.
Brown burger and drain off grease.
Mix tomato paste, Italian seasoning, onion flakes and garlic salt to burger.
Spread over the pita bread and sprinkle cheese over the top.
Saute chopped onions and peppers until tender in shortening. Add ground meat and brown.
Add tomatoes, rice and seasoning.
Put in covered dish and bake at 375° for about 45 minutes.
Serve.
Preheat oven to 350°. Split buns and hotdogs lengthwise. Place layer of cheese on buns.
Add hotdog slices to buns.
Top with sauce.
Sprinkle with parmesan cheese.
Place on broiler pan until cheese melts.
Combine sugar, mustard and oil.
Add mayonnaise and garlic powder. Refrigerate for a few hours and serve with raw vegetables.
Place 5 pats butter over bottom of deep dish casserole.
Place sliced zucchini on bottom of dish, then layer tomato chunks, onions and cheese.
Repeat layers (including butter) to top of dish. Cover and bake at 350° for 45 minutes.
Uncover and pour Chinese noodles over top.
Put back in oven, uncovered, and cook an additional 15 minutes at 425°.
Remove and let stand 15 to 20 minutes before serving.
Cream together and bake in Bundt pan for one hour at 350°.

i: 4 regular size cans buttermilk biscuits cut into quarters
2 tsp. cinnamon in a medium sized plastic bag
10 to 15 maraschino cherries cut in half
d: Put pecan pieces and maraschino cherries into bundt pan.
Add the 1 c. granulated sugar in a plastic bag along with the 2 tsp. cinnamon. Toss a few of the biscuit quarters in the mixture and put into bundt pan. Continue coating all the
biscuit pieces. Heat margarine or butter, brown sugar, and 1 Tbsp. cinnamon. Pour over the biscuits. Bake at 350° for 35 minutes. Cool 15 minutes, then, turn out onto cake dish. Serve warm.

i: 1 oz. part skim Mozzarella cheese, shredded
1 fresh California Bartlett pear, cored and sliced
d: Preheat oven to 400°.
On large baking pan, place pastry. Fold edges under to form ridge.
Sprinkle with Mozzarella.
Top with small dollops of Ricotta.
Sprinkle with raisins, nuts and spices.
Arrange fruit on top.
Peel and seed the avocados.
Mash the avocados in a nonmetallic bowl with a fork.
Don't overdo it.
Add the lime juice, garlic and Tabasco.
Add a dash or two of salt.
Taste and see if you need to add any more lime juice or salt.

i: 4 Tbsp. flour (a little more than level)
d: Cook first 7 ingredients and add margarine.
Shake in some pepper and celery seed.
This makes 1 quart and will keep a long time.

n: green giant american mixtures california style
i: 16 oz. pkg. Green Giant American mixtures California style frozen vegetables
d: In a large skillet combine chicken and 1/3 cup Italian dressing.
Saute until chicken starts to brown.
Add vegetables and remaining dressing.
Cover.
Simmer 7 to 9 minutes until vegetables are crisp-tender, stirring frequently.
Serve over pasta or mix pasta with vegetables.

i: 1 c. cooked, mashed prunes or 1 - 8 oz. jar baby food prunes
1/4 tsp. walnut flavoring or vanilla flavoring
d: Sift together self-rising flour and sugar into a bowl.

In another bowl stir together eggs, olive oil, prunes and flavoring. Use the 2 to 3 tbsp. of flour to dust walnuts (4 oz. of raisins may also be used) mix into a batter; then pour in flour mixture. Grease and dust a tube pan and pour in cake batter.
Bake 1 hour at 350°.
Take 1 cup of cornmeal and 1 cup of flour.
Then mix it up with milk.
Put cooking oil in the pan.
Pour the mix in the pan and you cook it until it gets good and brown on the other side and then you turn it over.
You turn it with a spatula.
Then you eat it.

i: 2 cans diced Ro-Tel tomatoes with chili peppers
d: Brown meat and drain grease well.
Add taco seasoning mix and heat well.
Add Ro-Tel and cheese.
Over medium heat, melt cheese, stirring often.
Serve warm with chips.

i: powdered or granulated sugar to sprinkle over
d: Sift flour, salt and baking powder together.
Stir in beaten egg.
Add water to make soft dough.
Round up on lightly floured surface.
Knead lightly.
Roll or pat out 1/2-inch thick.
Brown lean hamburger and sausage in large skillet.
Drain off fat.
Add Velveeta cheese, cut in cubes.
Stir until cheese melts. Add soup and salsa sauce.
Serve warm with your choice of corn chips.
Other vegetables can be substituted.
In a large frying pan, cook all the chiles until black on each side, then cut chiles down the middle and remove all the seeds. Dice chiles real small.
Put into a large saucepan.
Chop onions and tomatoes real small.
Put into pan with chiles.
Add the Pace salsa to other ingredients in pan.
Also, add tomato sauce, stewed tomatoes and 1 cup of water.
Blend all ingredients in big pot.
Add chicken and cook on medium heat until chicken is fully cooked; stir often.
It takes about 45 minutes.
Serve with rice and tossed salad.
Mix together the vinegar, mustard, salt and pepper, then slowly add the olive oil.
If the sauce is too thick, add some water.
Serve with asparagus, salad or your favorite vegetable.
Brown onion in olive oil.
Brown and drain hamburger.
Add remaining ingredients and simmer for 1 hour.
Rinse scallops and dry with a paper towel.
Roll scallops in flour.
In a wok or large skillet, saute scallops and garlic in olive oil until tender (about 5 to 10 minutes).
Add snow peas, red peppers and mushrooms and mix until heated through.
Serve over rice.
Serves 5.
Combine flour, sugar, oil, egg yolks, pineapple cinnamon and nutmeg in large bowl.
Beat egg whites until stiff peaks form. Fold into batter.
Fold in pecans.
Pour into a 10-inch tube pan. Bake at 325° for 1 hour and 10 minutes, or until cake tests done. Let cool in pan.

i: 4 to 5 Tbsp. balsamic vinegar (or use Paul Newman balsamic vinaigrette)
d: Cook pasta. In a skillet, combine all other ingredients and simmer. Drain pasta and combine with heated tomato mixture. Top with Parmesan cheese.

i: 1/2 to 1 c. cooking Muscatel dry wine
d: Soak chicken in salt water.
Brown chicken in oil or butter. Chop onion and garlic in a little oil.
Place in bottom of Dutch oven.
Place chicken on top.
Add flour, paprika and wine.
Simmer for 1 hour or until chicken is tender.
Brown hamburger meat and chopped onion in pan.
In 9 1/2 x 13-inch cake pan, pour 3 cans soup (no water), 1 small jar picante sauce, the meat and crushed chips.
Mix well together.
Bake at 300° for 1/2 hour.
Shred cheese on top; melt in oven for 5 minutes.
Serves 6.
Combine tuna, carrots, zucchini, apple and onions.
Mix in mayo, mustard and poppy seeds and toss lightly.
Cut pita bread in half to form pocket; fill with tuna mixture and serve.

i: 2 c. fruit, sweetened with sugar to taste
d: Melt butter in 8-inch square pan.
Mix sugar, flour and milk by hand.
Pour batter over melted butter.
Pour fruit into middle of batter in pan.
Bake at 350° until brown on top.
Serve warm with ice cream.
Mix and top with sharp Cheddar cheese.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

i: 2 c. cooked chicken, cut into small pieces
1 1/2 c. seedless grapes, cut in half
d: Combine chicken, celery, grapes, carrots and almonds.
Blend mayonnaise with sour cream and seasonings; pour over chicken and toss lightly.
Chill thoroughly.
Makes 6 servings.
(I think it best if made the day before.)

i: 1 c. mashed or pureed fresh or frozen unsweetened strawberries
d: In a small saucepan, sprinkle gelatin over orange juice.
Let stand 1 minute.
Heat over low heat until gelatin is dissolved and mixture comes to a boil. Remove from heat and stir into strawberries.
Add remaining ingredients and stir to blend. Refrigerate until firm, 3 to 4 hours.
Mix and chill.
May add celery seed or celery salt.
Garnish with dill pickle or celery stalk.

i: 16 oz. pkg. frozen broccoli, carrots, water chestnuts and red peppers
d: In medium saucepan, cook vegetables as directed on package. Drain.
In small saucepan, stir together remaining ingredients. Cook and stir until thickened and bubbly.
Cook and stir for 2 minutes more.
Pour sauce over vegetables.
Toss to coat.
Serves 4 to 6.
Shell and devein shrimp.
Preheat oven to 400°.
Melt butter in 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan. Add salt, garlic, 1 tablespoon parsley and shrimp.
Bake for 5 minutes.
Turn shrimp.
Bake for 5 minutes. Turn shrimp; sprinkle withe lemon peel, lime juice and remaining parsley.

i: 1 lb. boneless chicken, skinned and cut into cubes
1 (14 1/2 oz.) can Del Monte original recipe stewed tomatoes
d: In skillet, brown meat in oil.
Add onion and cook 2 minutes. Stir in tomatoes, marinade from artichokes and rosemary.
Cook over medium heat 10 to 15 minutes or until thickened, stirring frequently.
Stir in artichoke hearts.
Heat through.
Top with cheese.
Boil water; remove from heat.
Add oatmeal and margarine.
Mix other ingredients; add to oatmeal mixture.
Bake in an oblong pan at 350° for 30 minutes.
Cook and cut up potatoes.
Heat stock and pour over warm potatoes.
Add vinegar, mustard, oil, salt and pepper.
Sprinkle with herbs and allow to stand for full flavor.
Serves 6 to 8. Red pepper, chopped, is a pleasant addition.
Brown beef in 12-inch skillet or Dutch oven.
Drain.
Add chicken, broth, mushrooms, rice, onion, green pepper, barbecue sauce and seasoning; bring to a boil.
Reduce heat.
Cover and simmer 30 minutes or until rice is tender.
Add tomatoes; mix lightly.
Put all ingredients, except pecans and vanilla in pan.
When mixture begins to boil, time for 3 minutes.
Remove from heat. Add pecans and vanilla.
Beat by hand until thick enough to drop by teaspoonfuls onto waxed paper.
Mix all ingredients together.
Drop onto baking sheet.
Bake at 325° until golden brown.
Mix altogether in a baking dish.
Bake at 350° for 20 minutes. Spread bacon strips across (optional).

i: 1 lb. bacon, cut in small pieces
d: Boil potatoes; skin and slice into pieces.
Fry bacon; drain off all fat.
Sprinkle flour over bacon; mix it up.
Pour vinegar, sugar and water over bacon.
Simmer until thick.
Pour over potatoes; mix well.

i: 1 c. celery, cut in thick diagonal slices
1 can bean sprouts, rinsed and drained
1 can mushroom soup and/or 1 can cream of chicken soup (no water)
d: Brown beef; drain fat.
Add onion and celery and continue cooking until tender, but not soft.
Add bean sprouts and soup. Heat thoroughly.
Add canned mushrooms if desired.
Serve over chow mein noodles.
Combine meat, rice, egg, parsley, onion, salt, pepper and 1/4 cup tomato soup.
Mix and make balls.
Mix remaining soup, water and Worcestershire sauce.
Pour over meatballs; bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer 40 minutes.
Stir often.
Brown meat; add onions and cook until tender.
Stir in tomatoes, tomato paste, water, garlic and seasonings; cover and simmer 30 minutes.
In a 12 x 8-inch baking dish, layer noodles, meat sauce and cheeses; repeat layers.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Mix cornstarch and 2 tablespoons sugar with water and cook together until clear.
Set aside.
Add a dash of salt to the egg whites.
Beat until foamy and standing in peaks.
Add cornstarch mixture and continue to beat until creamy.
Add 6 tablespoons sugar gradually.
In a mixing bowl, combine flour, brown sugar and butter; blend at low speed until crumbly.
Stir in the nuts.
Press about 2 3/4 cups into the bottom of an ungreased 9 x 13-inch pan.
Combine or mix tortilla flour with warm water.
Slowly swift until it becomes a dough (not too sticky).
Make round balls, then roll out into any shape no bigger than the size of an interior coffee cup.
Preheat pan with vegetable oil, low to medium heat. Lay dough inside pan.
Cook until one side is brown, then flip over and cook the other side until brown.
Combine sugar and cinnamon on another plate; mix with fork.
Brown meat, onion and garlic.
Fill each tortilla with 2 tablespoons of mixture, 1 tablespoon of each cheese and top with onion.
Roll and place in a 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan.
Put remaining meat mixture over top with remaining cheese.
Bake, uncovered, at 350° for 30 minutes.

i: 1 large can pineapple juice (not the frozen kind)
d: Freeze some extra of the same mixture in a ring with some added green food coloring to float in bowl.

i: 1 cup margarine (or substitute equal amount of applesauce)
d: Combine and let stand Bran Buds and boiling water.
Cream sugar, margarine, eggs, then add buttermilk, flour sifted with baking soda, salt and All-Bran cereal.
Add first mixture of bran buds and boiling water and combine with second mixture.
Dissolve jello.
Add cheese; mix well (wire whisk helps).
Add pineapple, juice and all.
Turn into mold.

i: 2 c. tri-color rotini, cooked and drained
1/4 c. sliced red onion, peppercorn, Italian or Ranch dressing to taste.
d: Mix prepared pasta and vegetables in a large bowl.
Add dressing.
Mix lightly.
Refrigerate several hours.
Stir and serve.
Mix flour and water, then add detergent.
Then add food coloring (your choice of color).
Mix well and you're ready for finger painting.

i: 1 box Duncan Hines yellow cake mix
d: Blend to moisten, then beat for 2 minutes.
(Optional: a few drops of green food dye for a richer green color.) Pour about 3/4 batter into a greased and floured Bundt pan.
With remaining batter, add 3/4 cup Hershey's chocolate syrup.
Pour into Bundt pan on top of the green batter.
(The chocolate batter will fall to the bottom.)
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

i: 1/2 c. bottled oil and vinegar dressing
d: Place romaine, tomatoes, olives, cheese and oregano in a large bowl.
Toss with dressing.
Makes 10 servings.

i: 2 c. Aunt Jemima self-rising white cornmeal
d: Combine ingredients; mix well.
Heat vegetable oil (approximately 2-inches deep) to 375°.
Drop mixture by rounded tablespoonful into hot vegetable oil.
Fry a few at a time, until golden brown.
Drain on paper towels.
Makes about 2 dozen.
Combine first 4 ingredients in a small bowl.
Combine remaining ingredients and add to vegetable mixture.
Chill at least 2 hours before serving.
Serve with corn chips.
Makes 2 1/2 cups.

i: 1 lb. sausage, browned and drained well with paper towels
d: Crumble up cornbread and mix with all other ingredients.
Mix all ingredients together.
Store in airtight container for later use.
For each cup of tea, mix 1 teaspoon of tea mix to 1 cup of hot water.
Cut chicken breasts into bite size pieces and place in pan with small amount of butter.
Cover with wax paper and place in 400 degree oven for 6 to 8 minutes. (Chicken is done if it springs back when pressed with finger.)
Combine with other ingredients. Refrigerate several hours or overnight before serving.

i: 1 c. plus 2 Tbsp. self rising flour
d: Cream butter, sugar, brown sugar and vanilla in mixer bowl until light and fluffy. Add egg, mix well, stir in flour, chocolate chips and pecans. Spread on lightly greased pizza pan and bake at 350° until brown. Cool before cutting. Yield: 24 servings.
Combine butter, margarine, cream cheese and flour.
Knead until well blended.
Form into ball, cover with plastic and place in refrigerator for at least 2 hours.
Melt margarine in large casserole dish.
Stir in rice, water and soup.
Add green pepper, onion and pimentos, all chopped. Bake at 350°, covered, for 45 minutes.

i: 3/4 c. catsup, water, onion and green pepper
d: Bake at 350° for 2 hours over meat.
Combine chicken and 1/2 teaspoon salt in Dutch oven.
Add water to cover.
Bring to boil; cover, reduce heat and simmer 40 minutes or until tender.
Bone chicken and cut into bite size pieces.
Combine chicken with next 4 ingredients.
Combine mayonnaise and next 3 ingredients; add to chicken.
Drain cooked macaroni.
Melt margarine in pan and add onion. Cook until golden brown.
Remove from heat and add macaroni and remaining ingredients.
Mix well.
Pour into casserole dish and bake 40 to 45 minutes at 350°.
Soften cream cheese and blend in milk to desired consistency. Add curry to taste.
Place in bowl and cover with chutney (Major Grey's or other).
Dip with crisp gingersnaps.
It really is wonderful.
Saute onion and garlic with ground chuck.
Drain well.
Add bay leaves, tomato paste, sugar and tomato juice.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Add oregano to taste.
Simmer 3 hours.
Cook potatoes with skins on until tender, drain and cool. Peel and slice into 1/4-inch slices or desired size.
Cut bacon into 1-inch pieces and fry until crisp but not burnt.
Remove and drain bacon, saving 1/4 cup bacon drippings.
Mix cornstarch and water together.
Heat sugar, vinegar, bacon drippings, salt and pepper.
Then add cornstarch mixture to thicken.

t: Chicken Soup (Greek Egg-Lemon Soup)(Greek; Heart Healthy.)
d: Cover fowl with water and chicken broth and heat gradually to boiling point with onion and celery. Cook until fowl is tender, about 1 1/2 hours, adding salt when half done. Remove fowl from broth and set aside on warm platter. Strain broth; bring to boiling point. Stir in rice; cover and simmer over moderate heat until rice is cooked. Stir the contents frequently while rice is cooking. Cool soup slightly and blend with the following Egg-Lemon recipe. Serve immediately. Makes 6 servings.

i: 1 (7 oz.) can whole green chili peppers or 2 (4 oz.) cans chopped green chilies
d: Drain peppers (seed whole ones).
If using whole peppers, cut them lengthwise and line sides and bottom of a 9-inch pie plate. Preheat oven to 350°.

Rinse carrots, but do not dry. Combine carrots, salt, pepper and curry in saucepan. Add water. Cover and cook over low heat about 20 minutes or until tender. Shake pan a few times to prevent sticking. Add butter. Serve hot. Serves 6.
Mix all of the ingredients together.
Cut fish into small pieces.
Put in very salty water; soak for 48 hours, then drain. Put fish in clear white vinegar.
Pour brine over fish.
Boil 5 minutes and cool.
Store in refrigerator in glass jars.

i: 1 (15 oz.) can whole kernel corn, drained
d: Cook ground beef with onions until browned and drain well. Spoon into a 13 x 9-inch ungreased baking dish.
Into this stir taco seasoning, drained corn and tomato sauce.
Spread evenly in dish.
Sprinkle 1 cup shredded cheese on top.
Mix Bisquick, milk and eggs until well blended.
Pour over beef mixture.
In large bowl, crumble beef.
Heat in microwave 2 to 3 minutes on High until beef is browned, stirring once.
Drain.
Stir in sauce and water.
Mix first 6 ingredients in a pan over high heat.
Combine cider and brown sugar.
Add apple mixture and salt.
Simmer until reduced to 2 cups.
Cool.
Serve with ham or curried dishes.

i: 1 lb. orange slice candy, cut up
d: Sift together flour and 1/2 teaspoon soda.
Combine orange slice, dates, nuts and coconut.
Add 1/2 cup flour mixture to fruit mixture.
Mix well.
Cream butter until light, then gradually add sugar, beating constantly.
Add eggs, one at a time to creamed mixture, beating well after each addition.

i: onion slices or clove of garlic
d: Soak cucumbers in ice water 3 or 4 hours.
Drain.
Slice or cut into strips.
Place in sterilized jars, along with onion and a generous amount of dill.
Combine sugar, water, vinegar and salt and bring to a boil.
Pour over pickles and seal.
Slice washed, pithed peppers lengthwise.
Heat oil in chafing dish.
Add peppers and sliced onions.
Saute 15 to 18 minutes, until peppers are soft.
Beat eggs.
Add salt and pepper.

i: 1 1/2 c. finely chopped fully cooked smoked ham
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
Lightly grease jelly roll pan.
Mix all ingredients thoroughly.
Shape mixture into 1-inch balls.
Place about 2-inches apart in pan.
Bake 20 to 25 minutes or until brown. Immediately remove from pan.
Dissolve yeast in warm water.
Let stand 5 minutes.
Stir in the buttermilk; set aside.
In a large mixing bowl combine flour, sugar, baking powder, soda and salt.
Cut in shortening with a pastry blender, until mixture resembles coarse meal.
Stir in yeast/buttermilk mixture; mix well.
Dissolve yeast and sugar in warm water in large mixing bowl. Let stand until bubbly, 8 to 10 minutes.
Stir in salt and enough of the flour to make a stiff dough.
Turn dough onto lightly floured surface; knead in additional flour until dough is smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes.

i: 1 (4 oz.) can black olives, small diced
d: Smooth the cream cheese over a dinner plate.
Pour salsa over cream cheese.
Spread black olives over salsa.
Sprinkle cheese on top.
Serve with Fritos or corn chips.

i: 2 c. each: shredded green and red cabbage
1 (8 oz.) can sliced water chestnuts, drained
1 (6 oz.) pkg. frozen Chinese pea pods, thawed and drained
1/4 c. each: julienne carrots and sliced green onion
d: Whisk dressing and soy sauce together and set aside.
In large bowl, combine remaining ingredients (except chow mein noodles). Pour dressing over salad and toss well.
Chill until ready to serve.
Serve topped with Chinese noodles.
(This is really good over hot white rice, too.)

i: 3 Tbsp. chopped fresh chives or green onions
d: Place chicken breast halves between sheets of waxed paper. Pound slightly with mallet.
Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Heat 1 tablespoon of oil and butter in large skillet.
Cook chicken over high heat for 4 minutes on each side.
Transfer to warm serving platter.
Add chives, lemon juice and brandy (if used), parsley and mustard to pan.
Mix all ingredients together until dough is soft.
(Food processor works well.)
Drop by tablespoonful on ungreased cookie sheet.
Bake at 450° for 10 minutes.
Brown meat and onion; drain.
Add soup and ketchup.
Pour into casserole dish.
Top with biscuits.
Bake at 325° for about 45 minutes, or until biscuits are done and lightly browned.

t: Pastel Shower Jello(Great For Bridal Or Baby Showers)
i: 4 small pkg. jello (different flavors or color combinations)
1 c. boiling water per pkg. (do 1 pkg. at a time)
1/2 gal. vanilla ice cream, cut into 4ths
d: Prepare 1 package jello using ice cream in place of cold water.
Pour into 8 x 12-inch glass pan.
When 1st jello is set, spread with 1/3 pint sour cream.
Prepare next package jello and spoon over sour cream gently.
Repeat until all 4 jellos are used. When set, frost with 1 carton Cool Whip.

i: 1 medium green pepper, cored, seeded and chopped
2 tsp. finely chopped marjoram or 1 tsp. dried
d: Preheat oven to 425°.
Trim off the ends of the zucchini and core the tomatoes.
Cut each into equal horizontal slices. Arrange the slices in an oven-proof baking dish with the edges slightly overlapping.
Alternate layers of zucchini and tomatoes.
Combine ingredients and pour over chicken.
Marinate for 2 hours, turning occasionally.
Great broiled or grilled.
Beat egg yolks and sugar in medium saucepan.
Slowly beat in two (2) cups of the milk.
Cook over medium heat, stirring frequently, until thermometer registers 150°F. or mixture coats the back of a metal spoon.
Remove from heat;
strain into pitcher.
Stir in remaining milk, Jim Beam Bourbon, vanilla and nutmeg.
Cook, drain and squeeze spinach.
Mix everything else together.
Chill 2 to 3 hours.
Roll into 1-inch balls.
Freeze on cookie sheet.
Put in plastic bag and use as needed.

i: 2 (1 lb. 2 oz. each) cans sweet potatoes, drained
d: Slice sweet potatoes into 1/2-inch thick pieces.
Combine with pineapple, butter, sugar and cinnamon in 2-quart casserole.
Place in microwave oven and cook on High 4 minutes.
Stir.
Sprinkle top with pecans and brown sugar.
Continue cooking 2 to 4 minutes.

i: 1 3/4 c. Gold Medal flour (sift if lumpy)
7 egg whites (beat with 1/2 tsp. cream of tartar)
d: Baking Temperature: 325° and 350°.
Baking Time: 55 minutes and 10 minutes until done.
Preheat oven to 400°.
In small bowl, combine the flours, sugar and baking powder.
In large bowl, mix cereal, milk and bananas.
Let stand 1 to 2 minutes.
Add egg and oil to cereal mixture and beat well with a spoon.
Stir in flour mixture until just combined.
Combine cabbage and onion; set aside.
Combine rest of ingredients in a small saucepan, except oil.
Bring to a boil, stirring often.
Stir in oil.
Pour over cabbage; toss gently. Cover and chill at least 2 hours.
Will keep in the refrigerator for several days.

i: 1/4 c. chopped onion (or as much as you want)
d: Melt margarine in skillet; saute onions and celery.
Remove from heat.
Stir in flour and curry powder.
Drain mushrooms and keep juice.
Add water to juice to make 1/2 cup liquid.
Stir liquid and soup into flour mixture; add mushrooms.
Cut off tops of green peppers; remove seed.
Cook peppers 5 minutes in boiling water to cover (salt added).
Drain and set aside.
Combine next 7 ingredients.
Mix well.
Fill peppers with meat mixture.
Bake ham according to instructions on package; usually 350° for 20 minutes per pound.
Mix brown sugar, mustard and cloves and pour over top and sides of ham.
Return to oven and bake 20 minutes more.
Let sit 30 minutes before slicing.
Refrigerate leftovers for another meal or to make sandwiches.

i: 1 1/4 lb. fresh ground turkey or other meat
1 1/2 c. shredded Monterey Jack or Cheddar cheese
d: Heat oil in skillet over medium-high heat until hot.
Crumble turkey into skillet, stir in onion.
Cook until turkey is no longer pink and onion is tender, drain.
Stir in seasoning mix, chili powder and water.
Heat to boiling, reduce heat.
Simmer, stirring occasionally, 15 minutes.
You will need 6 small jars.

i: 1 lb. uncooked shrimp, deveined (cooked shrimp works also; shrimp need to be just warmed in olive oil)
d: Cook fettuccine according to package directions.
Meanwhile, in a large skillet, saut the shrimp and garlic in olive oil until shrimp turn pink.
Remove and keep warm.
In the same skillet, combine the cream cheese, milk and Parmesan cheese; cook until cheeses are melted and smooth.
Spread 1 teaspoon of refried beans on each tortilla chip.
Top with 1 teaspoon of cheese and one olive slice.
Place 8 in circle on paper plate.
Heat on full power for 30 to 60 seconds, or until cheese is melted.
Serve warm.
Repeat with remaining chips.
Mix first 5 ingredients to form crumbs.
Put aside 1/2 cup of these crumbs.
Add soda which has been dissolved in the buttermilk and the egg.
Beat well and pour into 9 x 13-inch pan.
Sprinkle 1/2 cup reserved crumbs, nuts and chopped Heath bars on top.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Pound steak.
Cut in thin slices.
Blend cornstarch and spices. Coat the meat slices and brown well in hot oil.
Add onion and cook 3 minutes.
Stir in the beer, pepper sauce and sugar. Bring to boil and simmer on low heat, covered 15 minutes.
Add carrots and simmer until meat and carrots are tender.

i: 2 c. Cap'n Crunch peanut butter cereal
d: Mix the first 5 ingredients together in a large container. Melt the almond bark.
Pour the almond bark over the cereal mixture.
Stir quickly and scoop out onto wax paper by tablespoons.
Will resemble a haystack.

i: 1 c. tomato juice from plum tomatoes
d: Render bacon in
quart saucepan until crisp;
remove with slotted spoon, leave
to

i: 1 lb. ground round, cooked and drained
2 eggs (Egg Beaters may be substituted, 2/3 c.)
d: Grease square baking dish.
Layer cottage cheese and Parmesan cheese in dish.
Mix cooked beef, herbs, tomato paste and 1/2 cup Mozzarella; spoon evenly over top.
Beat milk, eggs, Bisquick, salt and pepper with a wire whisk until smooth.
Pour into dish.
Bake until knife inserted in center comes out clean.
Meatballs:
Mix hamburger, milk, soup mix and 1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce together by hand.
Form into meatballs and place on broiler pan.
Broil 12 to 15 minutes (or until done).
Cook the first 3 ingredients.
When cooked, add the jello, Kool-Aid and sugar.
When cold, add the cut up oranges and put in the sugar overnight.
Pineapple or fruit cocktail added also makes it good!
Cut off the ends of the lime.
Grind up fine and squeeze juice from pulp and use.
Roll in balls and bake 10 minutes.

i: 1 c. Hellmann's mayonnaise (I use Miracle Whip instead)
d: Chop artichoke hearts; mix with other ingredients.
(I mix in the bowl I'm using to bake.)
Bake at 350° for 20 minutes.
Serve with corn chips around baking dish.
Combine first 6 ingredients in a small saucepan; bring to a boil over medium heat.
Cover and simmer 10 minutes.
Remove to a bowl; cover and refrigerate 4 to 8 hours.
Yield: 2 cups.
Combine all the ingredients.
It can be prepared the night before so it's ready to serve first thing in the morning.
It also makes a delicious punch for any occasion.
Makes approximately 2 quarts.

i: 3 Tbsp. low calorie Italian salad dressing
d: Cook potatoes in jackets until fork tender; peel and cut into small pieces.
Add salt, pepper, celery, onion, sweet pickles, pickle juice, Italian dressing and eggs while warm.
Toss lightly and chill.
Combine mustard with mayonnaise and mix carefully with potato mixture.
Chill.
Makes 8 servings, containing about 100 calories each.
Mix well
together;
pour into cookie sheet pan.
Chill in refrigerator until firm, then cut into cubes.
I put all ingredients into my bread maker and set it on "dough". When ready, I form into rolls, let rise until double and bake at 400 degrees for 15-20 minutes.

i: 1/2 c. Monterey Jack cheese, shredded (with or without jalapeno)
d: Combine all ingredients, except tortillas.
Spread 2 tablespoons mixture onto each tortilla; fold in half.
Bake at 400° for 8 minutes or until golden.
Cut each into 4 wedges.
Cover the mushrooms with water containing 3 tablespoons salt. Soak for
minutes; drain, then cover with the marinade containing
all other ingredients.
Heat to boiling, then turn down heat
and

i: 1/4 c. bourbon or suit to taste
d: Mix first five ingredients well.
Add syrup and bourbon.
Mix well.
Roll into one-inch balls and roll in powdered sugar.
Keep refrigerated for storage.

i: 2 1/2 to 3 lb. sweet potatoes, peeled and boiled
d: Cut potatoes into large chunks and put into greased casserole. Mix rest of ingredients, except marshmallows, and pour over potatoes.
Bake for 35 minutes at 325°; top with marshmallows to cover and bake for 5 minutes more.
In bowl, cream the sugars and the shortening.
Add well beaten eggs.
Add sour cream.
Alternate with sifted ingredients of flour, salt, nutmeg, baking soda and baking powder.
Drop on greased and floured cookie sheet.
Bake at 375° for 13 to 15 minutes.

i: 1 small onion, sliced and separated into rings
d: Layer lettuce, onion and peas in 13 x 9-inch dish.
Spread mayonnaise to cover.
Sprinkle with sugar, bacon and cheese. Cover with plastic wrap.
Refrigerate overnight.
Sprinkle chicken with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in heavy skillet.
Brown chicken on all sides.
Push to side of pan; add onion, green pepper, garlic and mushrooms.
Saute a few minutes. Mix tomato paste with remaining ingredients and pour over chicken; stir well.
Cover and simmer gently until tender, about 30 minutes.

i: 1 (10 1/2 oz.) can condensed cream of chicken soup
d: In a sauce pan, heat soup, milk, mayonnaise and corn (with liquid), stirring occasionally.
Garnish with pimiento and parsley.
Makes 5 servings (1 cup each).
Sift flour with baking powder, salt and sugar into a medium bowl.
With rotary beater, beat egg.
Add milk and butter; beat until well mixed.
Pour this into dry ingredients; beat only until combined.
Batter will be lumpy.
Mix all ingredients together and chill.
Melt cheese in a frypan.
In another frypan, saute onions and diced peppers in butter; add clams and their juice.
Cook over medium heat for 10 minutes; add remaining ingredients.

Combine first 8 ingredients in a large shallow dish or zip-top freezer bag; add beef. Cover or seal and chill 8 hours, turning occasionally. Remove beef from marinade and place on rack in roasting pan. Discard marinade. Bake at 425° for 40 to 50 minutes or to desired degree of doneness. Cover loosely with foil and let tenderloin stand 10 minutes before slicing.

i: 3 c. semi-sweet or milk chocolate chips
d: Place all ingredients, except nuts, in large bowl.
Microwave until chocolate chips are melted, 3 to 5 minutes, stirring once or twice.
Stir in nuts.
Pour into well-greased baking dish. Refrigerate.
Mix together the first 5 ingredients, then mix together the rest of ingredients.
Use a 13 x 9-inch casserole dish.
Put a light layer of sauce mixture on bottom of pan, then a layer of rice mixture, then again sauce, rice and so on.
Top with layer of sauce and then Mozzarella cheese and Parmesan cheese.
Bake at 350° until hot and bubbly.
Into large saucepan, dissolve bouillon in water; add rice. Cover and simmer 25 minutes.
In small bowl, beat eggs and lemon juice.
Gradually stir about 1/2 cup hot bouillon mixture into eggs; stir into hot soup.
Serve immediately.

i: 1/2 c. chopped onion, cook in bacon fat
d: Add beans and brown sugar to onion cooked in bacon fat.
Mix all and place slices of bacon on top.
Bake at 325° for 1 hour (approximately).

i: 1 1/2 lb. round steak, cut in thin strips or 1 to 1 1/2 lb. ground beef
d: Brown meat in oil; add onion, rice, beef broth, water and soy sauce.
Bring to boil; reduce heat.
Cover and cook low, until liquid is absorbed (about 25 minutes).
Add green pepper and pimento.
Heat through.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.
1. Pour enough olive oil in a baking dish to cover the bottom.
Saute onion and celery in shortening until soft.
Cook rice and broccoli separately according to directions.
Mix all ingredients together in a 2 quart buttered baking dish.
Bake at 375° for 10 minutes.
May be prepared in advance and stored in refrigerator before baking.
Diced cooked chicken breast may be added for variety.

i: 1 tsp. each: marjoram, thyme and rosemary
d: Crush garlic with salt and pepper.
Mix with oil; spread on lamb.
Sprinkle with marjoram, thyme, rosemary and flour.
Pour wine and 1 cup water into pan.
Roast in a 325° oven for 2 1/2 hours, basting frequently.
Serves 6.
Mix lime and water; add sliced cucumbers.
Let stand 24 hours. Next day, pour water and lime off cucumbers.
Let cucumbers set in ice cold water for 4 hours.
Important: Change water every hour!
Mix vinegar, sugar, celery seed and salt together; put cucumbers in solution.
Leave overnight.
Must prepare night before.
Cook whole box rotini.
While rotini is cooking, use an electric mixer to mix Hellmann's, water, sugar, vinegar and turmeric.
Drain rotini and add hot rotini to mixture; add celery and onion and mix well.
Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Stir before serving and add salt and pepper to taste.
Combine chopped vegetables with salt.
Let stand for 2 hours. Drain well.
Combine sugar and remaining ingredients.
Heat to boiling.
Add drained vegetables and bring back to boiling point. Pack in hot, sterilized jars and seal.
Cut bacon in 1-inch pieces and fry.
Reserve 2 tablespoons bacon grease.
Blend sugar, onion, flour and seasonings into grease in large frying pan. Gradually add water and vinegar. Boil 1 minute. Add potatoes and bacon. Cook 5 minutes. Serves 6.

i: 2 eggs, well beaten or equivalent egg substitute
d: Cook spaghetti (about 3 cups).
Stir olive oil into hot spaghetti.
Stir in Parmesan cheese and eggs.
Form spaghetti mixture into a "crust" in a 10-inch pie plate sprayed with Pam. Spread cottage cheese over bottom of spaghetti crust.
Put zucchini and onion slices through food grinder.
Mix well with 5 tablespoons salt; let stand overnight.
Rinse with cold water.
Drain well in colander or sieve.
In a large kettle, mix vinegar, sugar, dry mustard, nutmeg, cornstarch, turmeric, celery seed and black pepper.
Cook until it starts to thicken.
Mix salt, pepper, oil, egg yolks, crushed garlic cloves, dry mustard, Worcestershire sauce and juice of 1/2 lemon.
Stir until mixed well.
Cut romaine up in small pieces; add sauce and mix. Add 1/4 cup grated Parmesan cheese and croutons and enjoy.
Shred cabbage fine.
Add salt and mix in large bowl.
Set aside for 1 hour.

While waiting, prepare remaining vegetables. Make a syrup of sugar, vinegar, celery seed and mustard seed and boil for 1 minute until sugar is dissolved and cool to lukewarm. Drain cabbage and squeeze to remove water.
Pour syrup over all vegetables and mix thoroughly.
Pack in desired containers.
In a saucepan, combine tomato paste, water, garlic and chili powder; simmer 20 minutes.
In skillet, brown ground beef with half of the onions; drain fat.
Stir in 1 1/2 cups of cheese and 1 cup tomato paste mixture.
Spoon 1/4 cup tomato paste mixture in 3-quart shallow baking dish.
Soften tortillas, one at a time, by dipping in and out of remaining mixture.
Spoon beef mixture down center of each tortilla; roll up.

i: 2 Tbsp. Great San Francisco seafood bouillabaisse seasoner
d: Combine first 10 ingredients in large stock pot and cook over moderate heat until vegetables are softened.
Add clam juice and water and bring to brisk boil.
Add shellfish, monkfish and then squid.
Cover and gently simmer until fish is cooked.
Stir in Pernod and sprinkle with parsley just before serving.
Sift salt and pepper with flour; fold in butter.
Let cheese be at room temperature and work until smooth.
Add to flour; mix until smooth.
Roll thin and cut into strips.
Bake at 400° for 8 to 10 minutes.
Roll graham crackers with rolling pin until very fine.
Melt marshmallows and add milk in double boiler.
Mix all the other ingredients toegther.
Pour melted marshmallows and milk over them.
Mix well and pack in cracker box or roll.
Put in refrigerator 24 hours before cutting.

i: 1 small can tiny shrimp, washed and drained
d: Heat through until thick.
Add pepper and garlic salt to taste.
Cool completely before wrapping mixture in egg roll skins and seal rolls with egg yolk.
Deep-fry in wok.

i: 2 gal. large cucumbers, sliced and cored out (hollow insides out) *
d: *Note:
You can quarter these instead of making rings.
Mix together.
Let set 2 to 3 hours before serving.
Keep refrigerated.
Cream margarine and sugar together.
Sift together flour, baking powder and salt.
Stir into margarine mixture with milk. Beat until smooth.
Pour into greased baking dish.
On top of the batter, place peach slices close together.
Pour about 1/4 cup of peach juice over the peaches and batter.
Cut chicken wings in half.
Discard the tip.
In a bowl, mix orange juice, soy sauce and minced garlic.
Add the chicken wings to the mixture and marinate at least one hour.
It is better to do this the day before and marinate overnight.
Place chicken wings on a cookie sheet and bake at 350° for about 30 to 45 minutes or until very brown.

i: 24 large or jumbo shrimp, peeled and deveined
d: Heat butter, garlic and olive oil in a large skillet.
Add shrimp and saute on both sides until done (about 5 minutes).
Pour off pan drippings into a small saucepan.
Add the remaining ingredients.
Cook over high heat 1 minute.
Pour sauce over shrimp and serve with rice to absorb the juices.

i: 1 small (2 1/4 oz.) can sliced black olives
3/4 c. onion, thinly sliced or chopped
d: Tear lettuce in pieces.
Cook and drain peas.
Layer vegetables in a large salad bowl.
Spread a layer of mayonnaise, sealing the lettuce.
Make a second layer of vegetables.
Seal with mayonnaise.
Put all ingredients in pot. Cover and cook until meat falls off bones. Remove about 1 cup juice.
Thicken with 2 tablespoons flour.
Add to meat and sauerkraut. Add cooked kielbasi.

i: 2 c. shredded Monterey Jack or Cheddar cheese (8 oz.)
d: Rub both sides of each tortilla lightly with oil; place on waxed paper or a paper plate. Microwave each tortilla on 100 %, (high) for 45 seconds.
