Sprinkle cheese on hot tortilla; fold tortilla in half. Microwave on 100 %, (high) for 35 seconds or until cheese melts. Repeat procedure for each tortilla and serve immediately.
Mix together and drop by tablespoonfuls in boiling water or broth.
Cook for about 10 minutes or when dumpling is parted, it is done.

i: 1 unbaked pie shell (9-inch deep will do)
d: Cook 1/2 fresh pumpkin in oven.
Remove seeds to use later in a snack.
Cook until soft.
Scoop out and mash.
Mix sugar and seasonings with mashed pumpkin.
Add eggs, one at a time.
Add liquids to sifted flour and stir.
It will take up all the flour.
Roll out dough and cut out biscuits.
Bake in 450° preheated oven until brown.

Melt butter in saucepan. Cool to room temperature. In a bowl, gently stir the butter in mayonnaise, parsley, curry powder, garlic, salt and pepper. In a large bowl, combine chicken, grapes and almonds. Arrange this mixture on a bed of fancy lettuce leaves. Pour the dressing on top and sprinkle with paprika.
*May substitute apples or pumpkin.
Mix lime juice with condensed milk and Cool Whip.
Pour mixture into cooked pie crust.
(Real easy and good.)

t: Midwestern Scalloped Corn And Sausage Casserole
d: Brown sausage; drain off all fat except about 2 tablespoons. Add flour and stir until blended, then add milk and stir until mixture boils and thickens.
Add corn, salt and pepper to mixture. Place into shallow casserole.
Arrange thick slices of tomato over the top of sausage and corn mixture.
Top with grated cheese. Place under broiler for 4 to 5 minutes, until tomatoes are cooked and cheese is melted.
Yields 4 servings.
Sift together the flour and soda.
Mix all ingredients together and bake at 350° for 45 minutes.
Mix all ingredients and pour over roast.
Turn roast over in sauce and cover all sides.
Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate overnight.
Bake in slow oven at 325°.

i: 4 to 5 lb. loin end pork roast
d: Rub pork with salt and pepper.
Make tiny knife slits in the meat.
Insert garlic slivers in slits.
Broil meat 15 minutes to remove excess fat.
Place 1 sliced onion in the bottom of crock-pot.
Add pork roast and remaining ingredients to crock-pot. Cover and cook on high heat for 5 hours or on low 10 hours.
Combine all ingredients and heat.
Serve with egg rolls.
Blend first 5 ingredients well.
Can use graham cracker crust in 9 x 12-inch pan or cheese cake pan.
Pour into prepared crust. Bake 1 1/2 hours at 325°.
Turn off oven and let sit 20 minutes. Cool completely and refrigerate.
Top with fresh fruit or canned pie filling before serving.
Cook hen , beef and bacon in 1/2 gallon of water for 2 hours. Then add onions, carrots, cabbage, green beans and tomatoes.
Cook 1 1/2 hours.
Then add potatoes, sugar, salt and pepper.
Cook 1 1/2 hours.
Then add corn and cook at least 15 minutes.
Makes 2 1/2 to 3 gallons.
In large skillet cook and stir rice in oil over medium heat until golden brown.
Add salt, water, bouillon cubes and soy sauce. Cover tightly; simmer 20 minutes.
Stir in onions, celery, green peppers and meat.
Cover tightly; simmer 10 minutes or until liquid is absorbed and rice tender.
Combine margarine, honey, mustard, curry and salt in small saucepan.
Heat on low flame.
Place chicken wings in shallow baking pan; sprinkle with curry and a dash of pepper.
Pour sauce mixture over chicken.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes and baste every 15 minutes.

i: 3 whole chicken breasts, cooked, deboned and cut in bite-size pieces
1 can or 2 boxes Stove Top stuffing mix (prepare as directed on package)
d: Combine chicken, sour cream and mushroom soup together.
Put in 9
x 13-inch pan.
Top with stuffing.
Cover with foil and bake at 350° for 45 minutes to 1 hour.
Mix all ingredients and put in your favorite unbaked pie shell.
Sprinkle pecans and sink them slightly so they are moistened.
Bake 45 minutes at 350°.

i: 1 (2 layer size) pkg. white cake mix
d: Prepare cake mix as directed on package, baking in 2 well-greased and floured 9-inch layer pans at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.
Cool in pans 15 minutes.
Poke with fork at 1/2-inch intervals.
Do not remove from pans.
Mix sugar, pecans, coconut.
Add milk, margarine.
Mix and roll into balls.
Refrigerate to harden.
Melt chocolate and wax in double boiler or on
very low heat.
Cut
and
dice	chicken
breasts;
season with seasoned salt, pepper
and
Combine jello and pineapple and microwave in bowl until jello melts.
Let cool.
Add Cool Whip to buttermilk; stir well.
Add jello mix when cooled.
Stir well and refrigerate.
Combine all ingredients except almonds.
Mix lightly.
Chill. When serving, garnish with almonds, if desired.
Makes 4 servings.
Cut two pieces of foil (about 12 x 18-inches).
Split potato in half and put in center of foil.
Place chop on top; stack with other vegetables and salt and pepper.
Fold foil, sealing tightly. Bake at 450° for 1 1/2 hours.
Serve with green salad, tea and dinner rolls and a little dessert later.
Hugs and kisses go a long way.
Combine sugar and cinnamon. Cut biscuits into quarters and roll in sugar and cinnamon.
Layer into well greased Bundt pan.
Dump pineapple and pie filling in bottom of oblong pan.
Mix and spread evenly over bottom.
Sprinkle dry cake mix evenly over those.
Pour melted margarine over cake mix.
Sprinkle evenly with coconut and then pecans.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Heat oil in heavy saucepan.
Add onions and garlic; cook 5 minutes over medium heat until soft.
Add other ingredients. Simmer 1 1/2 to 2 hours, uncovered, stirring occasionally.
Cook and bone chicken.
Place in casserole dish.
Then slice onion on chicken.
Slice Velveeta on chicken and onion.
In a bowl, mix soup and Ro-Tel, 1 cup chicken broth and 1/2 of 10 ounce package Doritos (whole).
Pour mixture over chicken, onion and cheese.
Pour cereal into a very large bowl.
In a 1-quart bowl, combine chips, peanut butter and margarine; microwave on High for 1 to 1 1/2 minutes until smooth.
(Stir after 1 minute.)
Pour chocolate mixture over cereal, stirring all pieces until all are coated.
Then little by little, add the powdered sugar.
Stir well each time the powdered sugar is added.

i: 6 oz. can mushrooms, sauteed in butter
d: Preheat oven to 400°.
Break up beef and cook until no longer pink.
Stir in onion and cook until tender.
Add sauce, mushrooms, water, olives, herbs and salt.
Simmer about 10 minutes.
Spoon into prepared pan.
Flour the pieces of round steak and brown on low heat in preheated vegetable oil.
Stir in 1 cup water and sliced onion. Add garlic salt and soy sauce.
Cook, covered, until tender.
Cut onions into rings. Combine cereal, salt, sugar and paprika.
Divide in half. Beat egg at high speed with electric mixer until soft peaks form. Dip onion in egg.
Dredge in crumb mixture.
Place in single layer on baking sheet coated with vegetable cooking spray.
Bake at 375° for 15 minutes until crispy. Good and low-fat.

i: 1 pkg. Hidden Valley Ranch buttermilk dressing
d: Mix dressing and oil; add seasonings.
Pour over crackers.
Put in warm oven, 15 to 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Mix the first 7 ingredients in bowl; let stand.
Put head of cabbage in boiling water to loosen leaves.
Peel off.
Mix remaining ingredients together.
Fill leaves and fasten with toothpick.
Pack in roaster pan.
Mix ingredients together for 5 minutes.
Scrape bowl often. Last fold in chocolate chip mints.
Bake at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes or until done (cake mix directions).

i: 1 lb. sweet Italian sausage (ground) or hamburger
d: Put English muffins on cookie sheet.
Fry Italian sausage and drain.
Crumble.
Spoon sauce on muffin halves.
Top with sausage, shredded cheese and sprinkling of oregano.
Broil until bubbly.
Saute onion and bell pepper in butter until clear.
Add tuna, water, soup and salt and pepper to taste.
Mix well and then add cooked spaghetti.
Mix part of the cheese in the mixture and reserve part to cover the top.
Cook at 350° for 45 minutes.

i: 1 c. sugar (only if you use unsweetened Kool-Aid)
d: In 2-quart container, mix Kool-Aid, pineapple juice and sugar. Finish filling the container with water, 7-Up or ginger ale.
Stir and serve.
Brown beef; add all remaining ingredients, except parsley; bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer, covered, for 1 hour. Garnish with parsley.
Yields 3 quarts.
Melt butter.
Mix with olive oil, Worcestershire sauce, lemon juice and fresh minced garlic.
Place cleaned, deveined shrimp in baking dish.
Cover with about 1/2 the mixture.
Bake at 350° for 5 to 6 minutes.
Turn the shrimp over.

i: 1 small bag Shoe Peg corn, thawed
d: Soak beans overnight. Drain soaking liquid. Cook 1 hour. Rinse and chill beans.
Mix all ingredients.
Pour over with Italian dressing. Will keep several days.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Mix soup and sour cream together.
Add chicken and lemon juice; mix well.
Pour into casserole dish (9 x 13-inch).
Melt butter.
Mix together with crackers and poppy seed.
Cook eggs 10 minutes, then drain off water.
Cover with cold water to cool eggs some, then peel hard-boiled eggs.
Cut into halves lengthwise
and
scoop
yolks out into bowl.

i: 2 cans chicken or deboned, cooked chicken, cut in strips
d: Warm shells slightly to prevent cracking.
In each shell, place chicken, a sprinkling of cheese and 1 tablespoon picante sauce.
Roll and place in greased baking dish.
Continue with each roll.
Pour remaining picante sauce and spread cheese evenly over dish.
Bake at 400° until cheese is bubbly, about 30 to 40 minutes.

t: Roast Turkey Breast With Garlic And Herbs
d: Rinse the turkey with cold water.
Remove and discard all visible fat.
Pat turkey dry with paper towels.
Place the celery, carrots, onions and thyme in a roasting pan.
Place the turkey on top.
Rub the skin with the oil.

i: 1 1/2 lb. round steak, cut in bite-size pieces
1 (12 oz.) pkg. small egg noodles, crush in pkg.
d: Pressure cook
round
steak with water approximately 15 minutes; set aside.

i: 2 c. Stove Top dressing or cornbread
d: Put water and margarine in 12 x 8-inch pan.
Melt.
Stir in Stove Top and 1/4 cup salsa.
Arrange chicken over dressing.
Pour rest of salsa on top.
Cover with foil.

i: 1 (16 oz.) can whole tomatoes, chopped coarsely
d: Sort and wash lima beans.
Put in a large saucepan with 3 cups water.
Bring to a boil.
Cook 2 minutes.
Remove from heat, cover and let soak 1 hour.
Place beans over medium heat until tender. Add more water if necessary.
In a large bowl, measure 3 cups of the orange juice, the lemon juice, eggs, sugar, cinnamon, ginger and cloves.
Blend with beater.
Stir in remaining orange juice.
Cover and refrigerate. Just before serving pour eggnog mixture into punch bowl.
Spoon ice cream into bowl; add ginger ale.
Blend with beater.
Mix all ingredients together and pour over meat for grill.
Cook cranberries in water on high until all cranberries pop. Add Jell-O and sugar.
Add pineapple, apples and nuts.
Chill.

t: Willie Mae'S Cabbage With Tomato And Bacon
d: Boil the cabbage until tender.
Drain, chop and add salt and pepper.
Simmer the mixture in tomato juice for about 20 minutes. Fry bacon over medium-high heat until crisp.
Crumble the bacon over the cabbage and sprinkle with the chopped parsley just before setting it on the table.
Combine flour, baking powder, salt, brown sugar, nonfat dry milk and oats in medium bowl.
Mix thoroughly, breaking up brown sugar to distribute evenly.
Cut in shortening until mixture is crumbly.
Combine eggs and water; add to flour mixture.
Stir just until blended.
Drop by heaping teaspoonfuls onto cookie sheet. Bake in 400° oven for 8 to 10 minutes, or until lightly browned. Makes 2 dozen drop biscuits.
Combine tomato sauce and spaghetti sauce mix in bowl; mix well.
Alternate layers of noodles, turkey, cheese and sauce in greased casserole.
Top with frankfurters. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Butter a 9 x 13 pan.
Set aside.
Mix cocoa, sugar, and salt in a large, heavy pan.
Stir in both milks.
Cook over med heat, stirring constantly until mixture comes to full boil.
Boil without stirring until mixture reaches a soft ball stage (234° on candy thermometer).

i: 6 hard-boiled eggs (leave whole), pierced with fork
d: In frypan, heat oil.
Saute onion until transparent, about 5 minutes.
Add tomato, curry, salt, water and eggs.
Cover and simmer 10 to 15 minutes.
Serve immediately with sauce spooned over eggs.
Eggs are pierced to absorb sauce.

i: 12 jumbo shrimp, shelled, deveined and tails intact
3 Tbsp. Marsala or dry white wine
d: Soak fish in buttermilk overnight or for 4 to 6 hours.
Wash well, removing buttermilk.
Drain on paper towels.
Squeeze lemon juice over fish.
Roll in mixture of cracker meal, flour and small amount of cornmeal if desired.
Fry in heavy skillet (cast-iron) in enough Crisco plus 1 tablespoon butter to cover bottom of pan well.

i: 1/8 tsp. garlic powder or 1 clove garlic, minced
1 can (10 3/4 oz.) cream of mushroom soup
d: In skillet, in 1 tablespoon hot margarine, cook chicken 10 minutes or until browned on both sides.
Remove.
In remaining 1 tablespoon hot margarine, cook mushrooms and onion with garlic powder until tender and liquid is evaporated (stirring often). Add remaining ingredients.
Heat to boiling.
Return chicken to skillet.
Cover; cook over low heat 5 minutes or until chicken is no longer pink, stirring occasionally.
Place apples in crust.
Sprinkle brown sugar over apples. Dust the flour over apples.
Dust cinnamon and salt over the apples.
Dot butter over apples.
Place crust on top.
Bake at 350°

i: 1/2 c. maraschino cherries, chopped in halves
d: Dissolve jello in boiling water.
You may add Coca-Cola now or later.
Add cherries and pecans.
When at room temperature, place in refrigerator.
After jelling, cut and place on lettuce leaf.
Mix in a plastic bag; seal and put into refrigerator.
Next day, mix again.
Following day, mix and leave bag open in refrigerator.
The next day, spread out on cookie sheets and put into oven.
For electric ovens, cook on warm approximately 12 hours.
For gas ovens, leave in oven with just pilot light for approximately 24 hours.
Spray 12 muffin cups with vegetable spray.
Combine first 5 ingredients.
Mix well.
Add milk, margarine, syrup and egg whites, mixing until just moist.
Fold in apples.
Fill muffin cups 3/4 full.
In skillet, cook bacon until crisp; remove and crumble.
Dust liver with flour; brown lightly in bacon drippings.
Stir in soup. Cover; simmer 5 minutes or until done.
Stir occasionally. Garnish with bacon.
Serve with cooked rice.
Makes 4 servings.
In large bowl, stir together first 6 ingredients.
Add next 4 ingredients and toss to coat well.
Cover and chill for several hours.
If desired, serve in bowl lined with lettuce leaves. Makes about 8 cups.

Cook bacon in saucepan until crisp. Remove from pan and save for later use. Lightly brown onion in bacon fat; add potatoes and water. Boil gently for 10 minutes. Add corn and cook 10 minutes longer. Stir in milk, salt and bacon. Heat just until hot. Makes 6 (1 cup) servings.
Mix together both cans of corn, crackers, egg, 2 teaspoons of melted butter and pepper and place in a buttered baking dish.
Dot with remaining 4 teaspoons of butter.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

Place chicken in 9 x 13 x 2-inch glass baking dish. Melt butter; add honey, mustard, salt and curry powder. Pour over chicken and bake at 325° for 1 hour and 15 minutes. This is good, but all chicken is good.
Melt butterscotch over low heat, stirring.
Pour in noodles and mix.
Spoon out noodles onto cookie sheet lined with wax paper.
Refrigerate to cool and harden together.
(Optional:
Use 1/2 package each of butterscotch and peanut butter chips.)

i: 1 lb. round steak, cut in strips
d: Brown steak strips in olive oil, soy sauce, ginger and garlic until tender.
Add 3/4 cup water to mixture.
Cook on medium to low flame for about 35 minutes.
Add onions, celery and parsley. Cook for ten minutes on low to medium flame.
Add cornstarch to remaining 1/4 cup water.
Stir into skillet and heat until thickened.
Mix all ingredients together and pour into greased and floured pie pan.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.

i: 2 gal. yellow cucumbers or overgrown cucumbers
d: Peel cucumbers and slice about like apple rings.
Cut round center out.
Soak cucumbers in pickling lime with 8 1/2 quarts of water for 24 hours.
Drain and wash with fresh water.
Soak in water for 3 hours.
Pour off and cover with 1 cup white vinegar, alum and red food coloring.

i: 1 box light brown sugar (approximately 2 1/4 to 2 1/2 c.)
1 pkg. biscuit mix (approximately 1 1/4 to 1 1/2 c. if you buy the box type)
d: Pour into greased oblong baking pan.
Bake for about 30 minutes at 350°.
Combine all ingredients, except the blueberries, and mix well. Stir in berries.
Pour in muffin cup pan. Bake at 375° for 20 to 25 minutes.

i: 1/3 c. Cremora (if desired for richer mix)
d: Mix all ingredients together.
Store in covered container. Place 4 tablespoons of mix in cup and fill with boiling water. Add whipped cream or marshmallows for extra treat.
In a saucepan over low heat, melt chocolate chips with milk and salt.
Remove from heat; stir in nuts, caramel topping and vanilla.
Spread evenly in a foil-lined 8 or 9 inch pan.
Chill 2 hours or until firm.
Turn fudge onto cutting board; peel off foil and cut into squares.
Makes about 2 pounds.

i: 3 whole, medium size chicken breasts, skinned, boned and cut into thin strips
1/4 lb. thinly sliced Prosciutto or ham, cut crosswise in half, then into strips
d: Combine flour, salt and pepper.
Coat chicken strips with flour mixture.
In a 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat in hot olive oil, cook chicken until lightly browned, stirring frequently.
Add broth, wine, artichokes and Prosciutto; heat to boiling.
Reduce heat to low, cover and simmer 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in peas; cook 5 minutes more or until chicken is fork-tender.
*Can use 3 cups of the two cheeses.

i: 4 pieces boned chicken breast, skin removed, cut into strips
d: Preheat a 10-inch browning dish 5 minutes at 100% power.
Pour in oil.
Add onions, chicken strips and broccoli.
Stir until sizzling stops; cover.

Microwave at High power for 6 to 9 minutes or until chicken is no longer pink and broccoli is tender-crisp. Add mushrooms and soy sauce mixture, stirring to coat chicken. Microwave 2 to 3 minutes or until sauce thickens slightly, mushrooms are tender and mixture is heated throughout.
Mix first 5 ingredients together in a large container.
Place cucumbers and onions into canning jars.
Pour mixture over to cover them.
Place in refrigerator for two weeks, until they are ready.
Shape like large donut on microwave or glass dish.
Spread barbecue sauce on top.
Cover with wax paper.
Bake in microwave on Medium-high for 15 minutes or in regular oven for approximately 1 hour, covered with foil.
Brown little steaks in skillet on both sides.
Add 1 can Campbell's beef broth soup.
Add chopped onion and green pepper. Cook on low heat 1 hour.
When done, broth can be thickened with cornstarch.
Heat oven to 400°.
Grease bottoms only of 12 medium muffin cups.
Beat egg; stir in milk and oil.
Stir in remaining ingredients all at once just until flour is moistened.
Bake until golden brown, about 20 minutes.
Immediately remove from pan. Makes about 1 dozen muffins.

i: 3 lb. short ribs (if meaty, cut into 3 pieces to bone)
d: Combine toasted sesame seed with oil, shoyu sauce, green onions, garlic, ginger, sugar and ajinomoto.
Mix well, then marinate meat overnight in refrigerator.
Broil over charcoal.

i: 4 lb. leg of lamb, boned and cut in 1 1/2-inch cubes
d: Combine all ingredients and marinate for at least six hours or preferably overnight.
(Pork or sirloin beef may be substituted.) Put meat on oiled wooden or metal skewers and set to grill over charcoal or gas grill, turning to brown evenly.
If regular oven is used, turn on broil; put skewers on rack and broil 6-inches from broiler, turning meat to brown.

i: 1/2 c. toasted pine nuts or slivered almonds
d: Mix first 12 ingredients.
Spread out 1 grape leaf at a time. Put 1 to 2 teaspoons of filling (depending on leaf size) at lower end of leaf (about the bottom 1/4 of leaf).
Fold both sides into center and roll from bottom towards stem.
They should look like short fat cigars.
Place rolls in casserole barely covering with water and 1/3 of lemon juice.
Bake covered at 350° for 40 minutes.
Brown onions, bell pepper and beef in large skillet.
Add tomatoes, basil, rice, salt and pepper.
Cover and simmer for 25 minutes.
Pour into casserole dish and cover with slices of American cheese.
Heat in oven at 350° until cheese melts.
Serve hot.
Place chicken skin side down in pan.
Mix other ingredients and pour/brush over chicken.
Cut orange in pieces and tuck around chicken.
Bake, uncovered, for 30 minutes at 375°.
Turn and cook another 30 minutes.

i: 4 double breasts of chicken, boned, trimmed and split into 8 pieces
6 oz. chilled, unsalted butter, cut into pieces
d: Whisk together mustard, sugar, bourbon, soy sauce and Worcestershire sauce.
Set aside.
Brown hamburger first.
Drain grease from browned hamburger. In a large bowl, crush nacho chips.
Grate cheese, cut up lettuce and chop onions and tomatoes.

i: enough turkey or chicken broth (nonfat) to make soup
d: Simmer about 1 hour or longer.
If you use broth that you have prepared yourself from turkey, put it in the refrigerator until fat has risen to top.
Skim off fat and use only the broth.
Add pineapple (drained), cottage cheese and jello powder together until mixed.
Fold in Cool Whip and refrigerate for several hours.

i: 1 c. frozen hash brown potatoes, thawed
1 c. (4 oz.) shredded sharp Cheddar cheese
d: Cook
sausage
and drain.
Separate rolls into 8 triangles. Place triangles onto pizza pan.
Press to form sides of crust. Seal
perforations.
Mix
all ingredients, pour into greased 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan. Bake at 350° for 25 minutes.
Cut into squares and serve cold.
Beat egg whites and fold in last.
Make small pancakes.

i: 2 (3 oz.) pkg. lime or strawberry jello
d: Mix all ingredients.
Form into balls and roll in powdered sugar.
Let stand until firm.
Cut a thin slice off of each tomato top.
Scoop out pulp. Invert the tomatoes on paper towel to drain.
Cream butter and sugar well.
Add four eggs, one at a time. Mix soda with 1/2 cup buttermilk; add to cake, alternately with flour.
Stir in dates, orange slices and nuts (use a small amount of the flour to dust).
Add coconut.
Bake in greased and floured tube pan.
Bake at 300° for 2 hours.
Brown beef.
Add onions and peppers.
Stir in remaining ingredients and mix well.
Pour into baking dish and cover.
Bake at 350° until lightly brown.

i: 1 large (5 oz.) can chow mein noodles
d: Melt morsels in microwave or on low heat.
Mix noodles into mixture.
Stir.
Drop by spoonfuls onto wax paper to dry.

i: 1 (14 oz.) can Eagle Brand sweetened condensed milk (not evaporated)
d: In heavy saucepan over low heat, melt chips, milk and salt. Remove from heat.
Stir in nuts and vanilla.
Spread evenly into wax paper lined 8 or 9-inch square pan.
Chill 2 hours or until firm.
Turn fudge onto cutting board.
Peel off paper and cut into squares.

i: garlic powder or fresh garlic cloves (1/2 tsp. powder per chicken breast or 2 fresh cloves)
1/2 c. white wine Chablis or Graves
d: Marinate chicken breasts with all ingredients for 6 hours. Better results are obtained if every chicken breast is cut in two halves and pounded, to thin the breast to no more than 1 centimeter thickness.
Blend all ingredients until smooth.
Refrigerate in tightly covered container.
Yields 1 cup.
Boil until thick.
Put everything in food processor.
Put in jars and seal.
Brown beef and garlic in oil; add tomato sauce, tomatoes and seasonings.
Simmer, covered for 15 minutes.
Fill casserole with alternating layers of noodles, Mozzarella cheese, Ricotta cheese, meat sauce and Parmesan cheese, ending with sauce.
Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
Bake at 375° for 15 to 20 minutes.
Combine chopped vegetables; sprinkles with salt. Let stand 4 to 6 hours in a cool place. Drain well.
Tie nutmeg and other spices in a small cloth bag.
Combine apple cider and sugar in large kettle.
Place bag in cider; bring to boil.
Boil for 5 minutes; serve hot.
Serves 40.
Saute garlic in cooking oil, add onions, pork, carrot and cabbage.
Season with soy sauce and fry for 2 minutes.
Add broth and simmer, add Kintsay.
When vegetables are cooked, mix in soaked and drained bijon and season with salt and ajinomoto. Garnish with chorizo canton and spring onions.
Serve with calamansi.
Layer lettuce, onion, peas and eggs.
Sprinkle with bacon bits. Ice top with mayonnaise.
Sprinkle top with cheese.

i: 1 chicken, cooked and deboned with broth
8 chicken bouillon cubes dissolved in 2 qt. boiling water
d: In a large pot bring chicken and broth to boil.
Break biscuits in quarters and add to broth mixture.
Cook on low heat 10 minutes, uncovered, then 10 minutes, covered. Do not stir until cooking is complete.

i: 1 can kidney or pinto beans with juice
d: Brown hamburger and drain.
Mix taco seasoning, creamed corn and beans with juice into hamburger.
Crumble chips in bottom of long casserole dish (any amount you like).
Pour meat mixture over chips and bake at 325° for 20 minutes.
Sprinkle shredded cheese over top and heat until cheese melts.
Serves 4 to 6 people.
Combine all ingredients. Bake at 250° for 45 minutes.
This dip is put in layers; either on a glass plate or in a glass bowl.
Divide Basic Sweet Dough into 2 portions.
Press into 2 buttered 8 x 8-inch baking pans.
Press apples, rounded side up, into dough.
Sprinkle with mixture of sugar and cinnamon; drizzle with butter.
Let rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk. Bake at 350° for 40 to 50 minutes, or until golden brown.
Cool on wire rack.
Dissolve jello in boiling water.
Add strawberries and stir until thawed.
Add bananas and nuts.
Pour 1/2 mixture into long dish and put in refrigerator until firm.
Combine pineapple and Cool Whip and spread on layer.
Top with remaining mixture. Refrigerate until firm.
In bowl, combine cornstarch with next 4 ingredients.
Blend until smooth.
Add meat; stir to coat.
Cover and let stand at room temperature for 1 hour.
Stir 1 or 2 times.
Drain well; reserve marinade.
Cut up 1 cup strawberries.
Add water.
Cook for 2 minutes. Mix sugar and starch and add to fruit mixture.
Cook until thick and clear.
Add butter, lemon juice and salt; cool.
Arrange berries in prepared pie crust.
You will need a punch bowl, cups, potato masher and whisk.
Combine all ingredients, except flour and oil.
Shape meat into small balls.
Roll in flour.
Brown meatballs on all sides in oil over medium heat.
Yields 8 servings.
Mix first four ingredients.
Spread on buns.
Add ham and cheese.
Wrap sandwiches in foil and bake 20 minutes at 350°.
Mix fruit together in a large bowl.
In another bowl, beat real whipping cream and sugar to sweeten until fluffy.
Fold into prepared fruit and serve.
Mix all ingredients in greased casserole dish.
Bake 1 hour at 325°.

i: 4 oz. Kraft natural Cheddar cheese, sliced
d: Brown meat and onion in a large skillet on medium-high heat. Drain.
Stir in water, catsup and mustard; season with salt and pepper.
Bring to a boil.
Stir in rice; cover.
Remove from heat. Let stand 5 minutes.
Fluff with fork.

i: 1 lb. boneless lean pork, cut into 1-inch cubes (may substitute 1 lb. top sirloin or bottom round steak)
d: Combine flour, paprika, salt and pepper.
Toss with meat.
In skillet, brown meat in hot oil.
Stir in tomatoes.
Cook, uncovered, over medium heat 20 minutes or until meat is tender, stirring frequently.
Remove pan from heat.
Grind tomatoes, onions, peppers and cucumbers with fine blade of chopper.
Mix together with salt; let stand overnight.
Sift dry ingredients together and blend in liquid.
Beat vigorously 1 minute. Shape into balls and drop into deep fat heated to 350°.
Cook until golden brown and enjoy while hot.
Take 1 pound of bacon and cut into cubes; brown.
Add 1 cup sliced mushrooms, garlic and green pepper; saute with bacon.
Set aside and save fat in pan.
In a spaghetti bowl, beat 2 eggs.
Add parsley and grated cheese.
Cook 1 pound of spaghetti.

i: 1 lb. shrimp, cleaned, peeled and deveined
salt, pepper and crushed red pepper to taste
d: In a saucepan, place oil; heat to medium.
When oil is warm, put in onions, garlic and green pepper.
When onions are transparent, put in sauce.
Add 1 can water and sugar.
As it starts to bubble, throw in the salt, pepper, crushed pepper and Vigo yellow seasoning mix.
Stir well.

Combine honey, oil, vinegar, garlic, herbs, salt and pepper in shallow pan. Add pork and turn to coat well. Cover and refrigerate 2 to 4 hours. Turn pork occasionally. Remove pan from the refrigerator 30 minutes before grilling.

i: 2 large fryers, skinned, cooked and deboned
d: Cook spaghetti in chicken broth after cooking fryers.
Saute onion and peppers in margarine.
Mix in chicken, peas, pimento, Ro-Tel tomatoes and cheese; cut into chunks.
Heat until cheese melts.
Stir in spaghetti and broth mixture.
Pour into three 13 x 9 x 2-inch pans.
Place peas in boiling water and boil 1 minute, drain and cool.
Combine tuna, celery, pecans and pineapple; stir well. Combine mayonnaise and curry powder.
Stir into tuna mixture. Cover and chill mixture.
Serves 3.

i: 2 Tbsp. plus 2 tsp. reduced-calorie tub margarine
1 medium onion, thinly sliced and separated into rings
1 Tbsp. firmly packed light brown sugar
2 small Granny Smith apples, cored and cut into 16 wedges
d: In a medium saucepan of boiling water, cook carrots for 8 to 10 minutes, until just tender; drain.
In a large nonstick skillet, melt margarine.
Add onion; cook, stirring frequently, for 8 to 10 minutes, until golden.
Add carrots, sugar and syrup; stir to coat.
Cover and simmer for 5 to 10 minutes, until glazed. Stir apples, cinnamon and nutmeg into skillet.
Cover and cook 5 minutes longer, until apples and carrots are tender.

i: 9 oz. pkg. rice pilaf, cooked according to pkg. directions and cooled slightly
d: Toss rice, mushrooms and onion together in large bowl. Combine remaining ingredients in small bowl or jar with tight fitting lid and blend well.

i: 4 (4 oz.) skinned, boned chicken breast halves
d: Combine first 4 ingredients in large non-aluminum skillet, bring to a boil.
Add chicken and cover.
Reduce heat and simmer 10 minutes or until chicken is done.
Remove chicken from skillet and keep warm.
Bring cooking liquid to a boil; cook 7 minutes or until reduced to 3/4 cup, stirring occasionally.
Combine strawberry spread and cornstarch; stir into cooking liquid and cook 1 minute or until syrupy.
Mix cooked spaghetti and any combination of vegetables you want.
Add bacon and seasoning.
Add the Italian dressing, a little at a time, until the spaghetti is coated.

i: 10 oz. spinach, washed, cooked and drained
d: Saute zucchini, onion and mushrooms in oil.
In a bowl, mix Ricotta and eggs, one at a time.
Add sauteed vegetables and 1/2 cup of Mozzarella to egg mixture.
Then add spinach, salt and pepper.
Place in an 8-inch spring-form pan, lightly oiled.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour or until lightly browned.
Preheat oven to 350°.
In a heavy saucepan on low heat, melt margarine and chocolate square.
Cool.
Add Eagle Brand milk. Stir in eggs, hot water and vanilla.
Mix well.
Pour in crust. Top with pecans and coconut.

t: Marinated Cucumber And Sweet Onion Salad
d: Slice cucumbers and onions.
Combine and sprinkle with 1/2 of salt.
Set aside.
Combine other ingredients in a saucepan and heat (hot but not boiling).
Pour over vegetables; let cool and refrigerate until ready to serve.

i: 4 Snickers bars, cut into 1/2 inch pieces
d: Combine all ingredients in a large bowl. Cover and chill. Store in refrigerator.
Beat eggs with sugar; add remaining ingredients (except marshmallows) to eggs.
Mix well and pour into greased and floured sheet cake pan.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
While hot, cover with marshmallows.
I return cake to oven until marshmallows lose their shape.
Let cool.
Layer.
Grate cheese over top.
Bake at 350° until heated through.
May be microwaved.
Mix all ingredients.
Put in a loaf pan.
Bake at 400° for 30 to 40 minutes.
Combine 1 cup flour, yeast and salt.
Add water and Crisco. Mix until smooth.
Add the rest of the flour to make a stiff dough.
Let rest 15 minutes.
Press into 2 well-greased pans (12 to 14-inch).
Add topping of cheese, sauce and meat.
Brown hamburger and drain.
Add sauce and simmer 5 minutes. Cut pita breads in half.
Sprinkle cheese in each pocket.
Add Sloppy Joes.
Top with more cheese.
Yields 6 servings.
Press crescent rolls in 13 x 9-inch pan, forming crust 1/2-inch up edge.
Brown hamburger in skillet with onion.
Drain grease.
Remove from heat and stir in remaining ingredients. Spread over crescent rolls.
Bake 20 to 25 minutes at 350°.
Kids love this!
Trim fat from chops and put in baking dish.
Mix other ingredients and pour over chops. Bake in a 400° oven about 45 minutes or until tender.
Put all ingredients into blender and puree.
May substitute mint chocolate chips for plain chocolate chips.
In large skillet over medium heat, cook dove meat and onion in 1/2 the butter; season with lemon pepper.
Cook for 10 minutes; set aside.
In same skillet melt remaining butter and fry potatoes adding lemon pepper
and garlic salt to taste.
Place meat in with potatoes; adding salsa and chili peppers.
Simmer 15 minutes; stirring frequently.

i: 5 oz. extra thin spaghetti or vermicelli
d: Saute ground beef, pepperoni, onion and chopped green pepper until vegetables are tender and onion is transparent.
Melt butter and place it in a 2 1/2-quart baking dish or an 11 x 13-inch baking dish.
Toss cooked spaghetti with the butter and cover with one can of tomato soup.
Add half the cheese and half the meat mixture and all of the mushrooms.
Sprinkle with oregano and basil.
Add remaining cheese and meat and top with the second can of tomato sauce.

i: 4 oz. frozen nondairy whipped topping, thawed
d: Beat cream cheese until smooth. Gradually beat in sugar. Add sour cream and vanilla. Fold in whipped topping. Spread thin layer of glaze over bottom of crust. Place strawberry slices on glaze and cover with remaining glaze. Gently spoon cream cheese mixture over glazed berries. Cover with inverted dome and chill until set, at least 4 hours. Store leftover pie in the refrigerator.
Melt butter in a large skillet.
Add onion and saute until soft.
Add cabbage to skillet.
Cook for 5 minutes.
Stir in caraway seed, salt and pepper.
Microwave Velveeta until smooth.
Add the browned drained beef.
Add tomatoes, drained.
Keep warm and serve as dip with corn chips.
Saute onion and garlic in oil in a medium saucepan until tender.
Add zucchini and peppers; cook for 3 minutes over medium heat.
Stir in remaining ingredients; cover and simmer 10 minutes. Discard bay leaf.
Store relish in airtight containers in refrigerator up to 5 days.
Yields 3 cups.
Mince
the
garlic and chop scallion.
Squeeze 2 tablespoons juice
from the lemon.
Heat butter and oil in a large frying
Brown venison and onion in skillet.
In pan put rest of ingredients.
Bring to boil and then add meat.
Cook until done. Add 2 tablespoons brown sugar.

i: 1 medium onion, minced fine or 4 green onions
d: Place wheat in bowl; cover with warm water and set aside until cool.
Squeeze moisture out of wheat with hands.
Toss with chopped vegetables, salt, lemon juice and olive oil.
Refrigerate for at least 4 hours before serving.

i: 1 (14 oz.) can Eagle Brand sweetened condensed milk
2 c. (12 oz. pkg.) semi-sweet chocolate chips
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
Combine flour, sugar and cocoa; cut in margarine until crumbly.
Add eggs; mix well.
Reserve 1 1/2 cups crumb mixture.
Press remainder evenly on bottom of greased 13 x 9-inch baking pan.
Bake 10 minutes.
Combine in 2-quart saucepan over medium heat sugar, corn syrup and water.
Heat until hard crack stage.
Remove from heat.
Add optional ingredients if desired.
Let stand 1 minute.
Pour into greased miniature muffin pan.
Beat eggs first.
Add remaining ingredients.
Bake in preheated oven at 425° for 15 minutes and then at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes.
Makes 2 (9-inch) pies.
Beat eggs with sugar and salt.
Add Karo, butter and vanilla. Add pecan pieces.
Pour into uncooked pie shell.
Bake at 350° about 50 minutes or until knife comes out clean.
Clean
octopus
by
soaking
in salt water and pounding octopus against side of bowl.
This helps remove any sand that may be
Preheat oven to 375°.
Lightly oil a baking tray and a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Peel and cut eggplants into slices.
Combine bread crumbs and herbs.
Place milk in a shallow dish.
Dip eggplant in milk and then crumb mixture.
Combine beef and garlic in a Dutch oven.
Cook over medium heat until browned, stirring to crumble meat.
Drain meat in colander, pat with paper towel.
Wipe pan drippings from skillet with paper towel.
Return meat to Dutch oven, add tomato juice and next 9 ingredients.
Bring to a boil.
Make roux with flour and oil.
Add onions and chopped celery to roux.
Cook until onions are wilted; add water and garlic. Cook in heavy pot for 1 hour.
Season to taste with salt, black and red peppers.
Add shrimp, parsley and fish.
Cook another 10 minutes.

i: 1/4 c. each: diced celery and red bell pepper
1 tsp. olive oil or vegetable oil
1 c. canned Italian tomatoes with liquid, pureed
4 oz. rinsed and drained canned white kidney beans (cannellini beans)
d: In a 1-quart casserole, combine onion, celery, bell pepper, garlic and oil.
Cover; vent top and microwave on High 3 minutes until softened.
Stir halfway through cooking.
Add remaining ingredients and stir.
Cover and microwave on High 1 minute until thoroughly heated.
Serves two 1-cup servings.
Wash okra thoroughly and cut off the stems.
Blanch pods by submerging in a pot of rapidly boiling water for 1 to 2 minutes. Drain and pat dry.
Slice okra crosswise into 1-inch pieces. Heat the butter and garlic (if used) in a large skillet over medium heat and saute the okra.
Stir frequently for 5 minutes or until nice and brown.
Season with salt and pepper to taste and serve.

i: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. light cream cheese, softened
d: Line bottom and sides of 9-inch spring-form pan with ladyfingers.

In large bowl, combine Ricotta cheese, cream cheese, sugar, Espresso powder, vanilla and 3 teaspoons cocoa powder. Beat at medium speed with electric mixer until well blended. Carefully spoon mix into prepared pan.
Refrigerate at least 3 hours or overnight.

i: small green pepper, cut in strips
d: Mix beef, onion, egg, bread crumbs, salt, pepper and tomato sauce.
Make into patties.
Melt butter in pan.
Add patties and green pepper strips.
Brown slowly until done.
Serve with rice or mashed potatoes.

i: 1 (8 oz.) box small elbow macaroni, cooked and drained
d: Brown beef and onion in skillet.
Pour off grease.
Spread beef mixture in large casserole dish.
Add cooked and drained macaroni.
Add tomato sauce.
Mix seasonings and water and add. Top with shredded cheese and pepperoni.

i: 2 avocados, mashed or 2 cans avocado dip
d: Layer in 9 x
13-inch pan bean dip, avocados mixed with garlic and Tabasco, sour cream, mayonnaise and taco seasoning (mixed), then
top
with
remaining
ingredients.

i: 6 to 8 medium to large potatoes, cooked
d: Slice potatoes in casserole or bowl.
Heat the oil in bacon pan.
Add vinegar, lemon juice, onion and salt.
Pour over potatoes.
Sprinkle on parsley, bacon and pepper.
Heat until warm. Toss and serve.

i: 1 1/2 c. small shrimp, cooked or canned
d: Mix all ingredients, except lettuce, thoroughly.
Arrange nests of lettuce on 6 plates and fill with mixture.

i: 1 (8 oz.) can water chestnuts, drained and chopped
1 (8 oz.) can pineapple tidbits, drained and chopped
d: Combine all ingredients; blend well.
Chill.
Serve on lettuce cups or use for sandwiches.

i: 1 bag spinach, washed and drained well
d: Trim spinach; tear in small pieces and refrigerate.
Sprinkle strawberries with 1 teaspoon of sugar or artificial sweetener and refrigerate.
Brown ground beef; drain and add taco seasoning mix.
Set aside.
Warm refried beans in bottom of a medium size rectangular pan.
Cover beans with sour cream, then spoon ground beef mixture over the sour cream.
Melt Velveeta cheese with Ro-Tel tomatoes; pour over the entire pan.
Serve with taco chips and watch it disappear.
Brown ground beef; add salt and pepper.
Add vegetables in layers without mixing.
Last, sprinkle rice on top (do not stir). Simmer until done (about 30 to 40 minutes).
Mix before serving. Serves 6.

i: 1 (8 oz.) can chicken gumbo or cream of chicken soup
d: Brown meat and onions until lightly brown in fat.
Add other items.
Simmer for 30 minutes.
Combine 2 tablespoons juice drained from pineapple with mayonnaise, mustard, curry powder and salt.
Set aside.
Cut chicken into small slivers and mix with remaining ingredients. Add flavored mayonnaise and blend well.
Serve with lettuce, pineapple and green grapes, if desired.
(Can be made ahead.) Serves 4.
Brown the ground beef in a skillet and pour off excess fat. Add taco seasoning and water.
Simmer until reduced.
Place equal amounts of taco meat on each tortilla.
Add some refried beans, if desired.
Put some salsa in bottom of a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Roll up tortillas and place seam down in pan.

Combine all filling ingredients in mixing bowl. Mix well. On each tortilla, spread about 1/2 cup mixture. Roll up tightly (jelly roll fashion). Wrap in Saran Wrap. Refrigerate at least 2 hours. Cut each tortilla into 12 slices. Makes 48.
Heat oven to 350° (375° for high altitude).
Brown meat and onion.
Drain.
Spoon into ungreased 13 x 9-inch baking dish. Stir in taco seasoning, tomato sauce and corn.
Sprinkle cheese over top.
Stir remaining ingredients until blended.

i: 1 (5 oz.) can cooked white meat chicken, drained
3 slices Armour lower salt bacon, cooked crisp and crumbled
d: Combine chicken, celery, onion, yogurt and pepper in small bowl.
If desired, place lettuce leaf on bottom of each bun; place pineapple ring on lettuce.
Top each with one third of the chicken mixture and bacon.
Makes 3 servings.
Cook peas in boiling water for 1 minute.
Cool.
Cream together butter, Crisco, brown and white sugar.
Add flavoring.
Add eggs, one at a time, mix well after each.
Mix flour, baking powder and salt.
Add dry mix and milk to egg mix, start with dry mix and add milk, end with dry.
Stir in walnuts. Pour into 10-inch well-greased and floured pan.
Cook sliced onions in butter until soft.
Add next 6 ingredients and cook for 1 hour over low heat.
Next, add mushrooms, 1 teaspoon chili powder and kidney beans and simmer for 15 minutes more.
Preheat oven to 500° (preheating is essential).
Separate broccoli into serving size spears.
Wash and pat thoroughly. Place on a cookie sheet that has been sprayed with olive oil spray.
(If you are not counting calories, use regular olive oil.)
Spray spears lightly.
Cook 10-12 minutes.

Coat a 9 X 13 baking dish with cooking spray. Heat over to 425°. Whisk together oil, mustard, paprika, thyme, salt and pepper in a large bowl. Add potatoes and stir until they are coated. Transfer potatoes to the baking dish and roast 15 minutes. Stir and roast another 15 minutes.
Mix together coffee, water and sugar and let cool.
Combine with vanilla bean and alcohol in a glass jar or bottle.
Let sit in a dark place at least 4 weeks, the longer it sits, the better it gets.
Shake now and then.
Remove the vanilla bean and store in sealed glass containers.
Enjoy over ice cream or in coffee.

i: 1 pkg. shaped taco shells or 1 pkg. soft tortillas
d: Brown chopped meat, stirring often.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Drain off fat.
Put ground meat in a bowl.
Everyone puts their favorite things on their own tacos.
Eat and enjoy.

i: 2 (19 oz.) Cans Chickpeas (Garbanzo Beans) Rinsed & Drained
d: Place first 3 ingredients in food processor, process until smooth. Add juice and remaining ingredients; process until smooth scraping sides occasionally.

i: 1 to 1 1/2 lb. round steak, cut into strips
d: Brown meat; drain grease.
Add broth to meat; simmer until tender.
Add all other ingredients, except cornstarch, and simmer for 10 to 15 more minutes.
Mix together cream cheese, mayonnaise and lemon juice.
Finely chop onions, beef, olives and bell pepper. Add to cheese mixture and mix well. Chill at least 6 hours.
Shape into a ball and roll in chopped nuts. Serve with Ritz crackers.

i: chicken, cut up in 1 x 1-inch pieces
beef, cut up in thin 2-inch long slices
d: Cook chicken and beef in soy sauce until brown.
Add flour and water to cover meat.
Add ginger and garlic cloves to your taste. Before adding watercress, take garlic and ginger out.
Add sugar to your taste.
If you like bamboo shoots, add some sliced pieces. Serve over rice.

i: large overripe cucumbers (enough to make 2 gallons after cored and sliced round-wise about 1/4-inch thick)
d: Soak cucumbers for 24 hours in lime and enough water to cover. Drain and soak for 3 hours in clean clear water.
Drain.
Simmer for 2 hours in 1 cup white vinegar, red food coloring and enough water to cover.
Drain.

i: 4 whole chicken breasts, halved and boned
d: Place chicken in greased baking pan and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Place Krrrrisp kraut over chicken; top with Swiss cheese. Pour dressing evenly over cheese.
Cover with foil and bake at 325° for about 1 1/2 hours.
Sprinkle with chopped parsley.
Makes 4 servings.
Mix all ingredients and place in airtight jars. Heat boiling water and add 1 tablespoon of mixture to 1 cup of water.
Beat margarine and sugar together until creamy.
Add egg and beat well.
Combine remaining dry ingredients.
Mix well.
Add to egg mixture.
Mix applesauce and apple juice.
Prepare orange, grape and lime jello and let set. Combine 1 cup pineapple juice --finish the cup with water and add another 1/2 cup cold water and sugar.
Heat until sugar is dissolved, then dissolve the strawberry jello.
Let set until it is thick like syrup.
Then add Cool Whip, crushed pineapple.
Cut the 3 flavors of jello into cubes.
Mix all together and put on a graham cracker crust.
Bake in a 375° oven for 45 minutes.
Serves 8.
Chop onion and ham.
Mix together with cream cheese.
Make sure cream cheese is left out to room temperature.
Makes 1 medium cheese ball.
Put chicken in large pot and add about 1/2 gallon water; cook until tender.
Remove chicken from broth and let cool; remove from bone.
Reserve broth.
Mix milk, Crisco and flour.
If not thick enough, add a little more flour.
Put broth on stove and add cans of chicken broth; bring to boil.

i: 4 or 5 c. chopped mushrooms (leave small ones whole)
d: Saute mushrooms and onions in sesame oil.
Add garlic, thyme, sesame seeds, tamari, and yeast.
When mushrooms are done, add flour, stirring until brown.
Pour in the liquid and let it simmer for one hour.
Then 15 minutes before serving, add soy milk.
If you cannot get soy milk, combine a cup of the soup with half a block of tofu in a blender.
Pour juices into punch bowl. Stir in water and ginger ale. Makes 20 servings.
In a small bowl, heat together the cream cheese, sour cream and spices.
Spread in a 10-inch dish or a pie plate.
Spread pizza sauce over it.
Sprinkle with pepperoni or other toppings. Bake at 350° for 10 minutes.
Top with cheese and bake 5 more minutes.
Serve with chips or nachos.
Blend Eagle Brand, Cool Whip, lemon juice, crushed pineapple, cherry jello and almond flavoring. Add bananas, cherry pie filling and pecans. Fold in. Chill.
Cook instant rice; set aside.
Cut green onions and saute in skillet with small amount of shortening.
Scramble eggs in same skillet with sauteed green onions; mix together.
In large bowl, mix and add as follows:
layer of rice, sprinkle pepper and salt lightly, sprinkle La Choy soy sauce evenly.
Pour lightly
Blend well and chill before serving with cucumbers, carrots, cauliflower, celery, etc.

i: 1 jar (4 oz.) chopped green chilies, drained
d: In bowl, mix shrimp and salsa.
Cover and chill 1 hour.
Cook rice and add chilies, scallions, salt and pepper. Mix well. Skewer salsa-coated shrimp; broil or grill.
Place on rice.
Serves 4.
Cook bacon, saute onions in bacon grease.
Add to beans in a crock-pot or bean pot.
Bake in 350° oven for 1/2 hour or on low in crock-pot 4 to 5 hours.
Use some of the canned bean liquid.
Even better the next day.
Heat chicken, 4 cups of water, onion, bouillon and pepper to boiling in Dutch oven and reduce heat.
Simmer, uncovered, 30 minutes.
Add carrots and simmer 10 minutes.
Mix flour and 1/2 cup water. Stir flour mixture and celery into chicken. Cook, stirring constantly, until thickened.
Combine Bisquick, milk and parsley.
Drop by spoonfuls onto boiling stew. Cook, uncovered, over low heat 10 minutes.
Cream together the butter and sugar for 20 minutes.
Add eggs, one at a time.
Add flour and lemon extract.
Fold in 7-Up.
Pour into well greased 12 cup Bundt pan.
Bake at 325°.

i: 1 pound ground beef, browned and drained
1 large bell pepper, cut in strips
d: In a bowl combine browned and drained beef, onion and tomatoes with liquid, Worcestershire sauce and salt. Spoon into a greased 3 quart casserole. Layer the potatoes, flour, corn, lima beans and green peppers on top. Cover and bake at 375
for 45 minutes. Sprinkle with cheese and continue baking, uncovered, for 30 minutes longer.
Mix thoroughly making sure sugar is dissolved.
Serve cold. Makes 1 gallon.

i: 1 (14-1/2 oz.) can whole or stewed tomatoes, undrained
d: Heat bacon drippings in a large skillet.
Add flour.
Cook and stir over medium-low heat until a very dark roux forms.
Add tomatoes and mash with fork until smooth, stirring constantly.
Add Worcestershire sauce.
Add a bit of V-8 or tomato juice if more liquid is needed.

i: 2 to 4 lb. imitation or real crab meat
milk (1 can to each can of soup)
d: Chop onion and saute in butter until tender, then slowly add crab meat.
Stir on low heat so it won't stick.
Add white pepper, then slowly add 2 cans of soup, then 2 cans of milk, stirring slowly.
Do the same with the other 4 cans of soup.
Let cook on low heat until ready to serve.
Feeds 6 to 8 people.
You will also need 4 (18 x 12-inch) pieces heavy aluminum foil.
Spread on top.
Bake 5 minutes at 450°.
Gradually mix the ingredients, except cracker crumbs and melted margarine, together into a large bowl, then pour into a casserole bowl.
Add the crushed crackers evenly on top, then spread the melted margarine evenly on top.
Bake at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes.
Brown ground beef, onion and green pepper.
Drain off fat. Combine beef mixture with remaining ingredients.
Place in casserole dish and bake in 350° oven for 1 hour.

t: Harold'S Hot As You Can Handle Chili
i: habanero peppers, chopped or sauce (1 oz. - 1/4 c. to taste)
d: Brown ground beef then pour off grease.
Add beans and stir until well mixed.
Add Ro-Tel tomatoes and stir.
Add 2 cans chopped green chilies and stir.
Add 2 cups of water and 2 packages of chili seasoning then stir until well mixed.
Bring to a boil then let simmer on low heat for approximately 2 hours.

i: 1 c. finely shredded "meat" (e.g. Fri Chik or favorite vegetarian meat)
dash of salt or McKay's chicken style seasoning
d: Fry beaten eggs and cut in shreds.
Return eggs to frying pan with cut meat and onions.
Cook 3 to 4 minutes, then add rice.
Mix soy sauce, sugar and oil together.
Dribble over rice.
Drain most liquid from beans.
Blend all ingredients in food processor until smooth.
Sprinkle with paprika before serving. Serve with pita points.
Preheat oven to 300°.
Spread pecans on large baking sheet. Melt butter and pour over pecan halves.
Sprinkle with salt and bake 20 to 30 minutes.
Combine flour, corn meal, sugar, cornstarch, baking powder, baking soda and salt in large bowl.
Beat egg in smaller bowl and whisk in butter slowly until well mixed.
Whisk in buttermilk. Pour egg mixture into dry ingredients, mixing until just blended. Gently spoon into greased 9 x 5-inch loaf pan.
Bake at 375° for 45 minutes or until wooden pick inserted in center of loaf comes out clean.
Makes 1 loaf.

i: 1 qt. (32 oz.) apple cider (apple juice okay)
2 c. cranberry juice cocktail or cranapple
d: Put cinnamon sticks, whole cloves, allspice and brown sugar in basket in top of an electric 8 to 10-cup percolator.
Mix juices in bottom and perk like coffee.
Double recipe if necessary.
May substitute apple cider with orange Hawaiian Punch.

i: 1/4 lb. mild or sharp Cheddar cheese, cut into small cubes
d: In a large mixing bowl with a wire whisk or fork, beat eggs until frothy.
Stir in milk; mix well.
Add cheese.
Season with salt and pepper.
Pour into buttered 2-quart baking dish.
Bake in a preheated 350° oven for 30 minutes.

i: 2 Cornish game hens (1 lb. 5 oz. each), halved
d: For 2 hours, refrigerate game hens in a marinade of soy sauce, ginger and pineapple juice, covered.
Preheat broiler.
Preheat oven to 350°; remove hens from marinade.
Put marinade aside. Place hens in skillet; boil 5 minutes on each side.
Bake hens, uncovered, 30 minutes until done.
Pour crushed pineapple & juice in a mixing bowl. Dissolve Jell-o in pineapple. Mix in sour cream, then cool whip, and nuts. Pour into dish and refrigerate overnight.
Cook beef in large pot. Add taco seasoning and onion soup mix. Add remaining ingredients. Cook on medium heat for 1 hour.
In a large bowl, combine the flour, sugar, baking soda, cinnamon and salt.
In another bowl, beat eggs.
Add carrots, oil, pineapple, nuts and vanilla.
Stir into dry ingredients just until moistened.
Spoon into 2 greased and floured loaf pans.
Bake at 350° for 65 to 70 minutes or until loaves test done.
Break lettuce into large bowl, add green onions.
Fry bacon, drain and crumble.
Pour off drippings from skillet.
Combine rest of ingredients, beat well.
Add bacon and pour all into skillet.
Cook until thick, stirring constantly.
Brown hamburger.
Add pepper and ringed onions.
Mix in chili powder, tomatoes and rice.
Bake in covered dish at 350° for 1 hour.
Cook ground beef until brown, drain. Put beans, tomato paste and 4 cans water.
Add other ingredients.
Cook for about an hour on low.
Cut unpeeled eggplant, squash and zucchini into 3/4-inch pieces.
Heat oil in large skillet over medium heat.
Add squash, eggplant, zucchini and onion.
Cook 5 to 7 minutes or until vegetables are almost tender, stirring frequently.
Add seasonings.
Continue cooking, stirring frequently, 1 minute or until vegetables are tender.
Mix and serve.
This can be stored in refrigerator for a few days.
Combine all ingredients except brown sugar and oil.
Boil for 20 minutes, then add brown sugar and oil.
Cook for another 20 minutes.
Mix butter, curry powder, Tabasco sauce, Worcestershire sauce and
garlic
salt.	Add remaining ingredients and stir. Bake at 250° for 1 1/2 hours.

i: 1 c. sliced green onions with tops
d: Cut round steak into strips and brown in butter.
Add garlic and beef broth.
Cover and simmer 30 minutes.
Add green onion and green peppers.
Cover and cook 5 minutes.
Blend cornstarch, water and soy sauce.
Beat together ingredients with rotary beater just until smooth.
Pour into well-greased deep muffin cup (3/4 full) or oven glass cups (1/2 full). Bake at 425° for 35 to 45 minutes.

i: 1 pkg. American cheese and Parmesan cheese
d: Cook noodles in salted water until tender.
Brown beef and minced onion and garlic in salad oil (about 2 tablespoons).
Add tomato sauce, tomatoes, salt, pepper and oregano; let simmer 35 minutes.
Combine all ingredients.
Mix well, roll out and cut with "bone" shaped cookie cutter.
Bake at 300° until very hard.
Fido will love these!
Dissolve jello in hot water.
Combine cold water and ice cubes to make 1 1/4 cups.
Add to jello, stirring until slightly thickened.
Stir in strawberries and bananas.
Place cake cubes in large bowl.
Sprinkle with orange juice.

i: 2 1/2 lb. Aunt Jemima self-rising white corn meal
d: Mix all ingredients well; drop by spoonfuls into hot oil. Submerge, if possible, until brown.
Enjoy!
Cream margarine and eggs. Add next 5 ingredients to the margarine egg mixture and blend together. Put into 2 unbaked pie shells. Bake 1 hour at 325°.
Saute pork steak; salt and pepper to taste.
Brown hamburger; salt and pepper to taste.
Put Ro-Tel and tomatoes in blender and blend about 30 seconds.
Cut jalapenos into small pieces.
Put all ingredients into large soup pot, add 1/2 jar of minced garlic and cook (simmer) for about 1 hour or so.
Slice pork into 1/8-inch strips.
Marinate in soy sauce, vinegar, sesame oil, garlic and ginger for 15 minutes.
Heat oil in skillet until hot.
Add pork and stir-fry for 3 to 5 minutes. Add vegetables
(unthawed) and the chicken broth or water.
Stir all together.

i: liquid red pepper seasoning to taste
d: Combine vinegar and sugar in small saucepan.
Cook over low heat just until sugar dissolves.
Stir in barbecue sauce, water, mayonnaise, mustard powder and red pepper seasoning.
Combine cabbage and carrots in large bowl.
Pour dressing over; toss to combine.

Place butter in a shallow dish. In another dish, combine bread crumbs, Parmesan cheese and seasonings. Dip fillets in butter, then coat with crumb mixture. Bake at 350° for 20 minutes, uncovered, or until fish flakes easily with a fork.
Saute onions in butter.
Add broccoli and simmer until tender. Add remaining ingredients and heat until smooth.
Pour into a greased 2-quart casserole dish and bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Crush vanilla wafers.
Add the remaining ingredients.
Mix well and form into balls.
Roll in additional powdered sugar. Makes about 2 dozen small balls.

i: 2 1/2 c. turkey or chicken breast meat
d: Cut meat in 1/4 to 1/2-inch cubes and season with salt and pepper.
Blend all other ingredients into the mayonnaise.
Toss meat with mayonnaise and chill for at least 1 hour in refrigerator. Serves 4.

i: 2 or 3 fresh young cucumbers, sliced very thin
1 Tbsp. each olive oil and vinegar
d: In a bowl, arrange the sliced cucumbers, salting them in layers.
Cover with a saucer and weight down with something heavy. Let stand for several hours, or until juices run out.
Pour off the cucumber juice, which will take care of most of the salt. Combine all other ingredients and add to the cucumbers.
Mix and chill.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.
Combine first six ingredients.
Mix dressing with curry powder.
Pour over salad and toss well.
May prepare ahead of time but do not add dressing until just before serving.
In large skillet brown 1 green pepper, sliced fine, and 1 large onion, cut fine.
Then add ground beef, salt and pepper to taste.
Brown meat slowly, crumbling with a fork.
When meat is completely browned, pour off any grease that has formed.
Add mustard, vinegar, sugar, water, ketchup, salt and pepper.
Mix all ingredients with meat.
Melt 1 stick margarine.
Add other ingredients and mix well. Bake at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes in an unbaked pie shell.
Dissolve Jell-O in boiling water and add sugar and lemon juice.
Chill until thick (like pudding).
Whip and add to whipped chilled Pet milk.
Combine graham cracker crumbs and melted butter.
Press in bottom of 2 (9 x 13-inch) pans.
Pour Jell-O mixture over crumbs and chill.

Cook noodles according to package. Saute onions and bell pepper in margarine. Stir in flour slowly until thick. Add seasonings and cheese; stir until melted. Add 2 cups milk, soups, chicken (chopped), and noodles. Turn into shallow baking dish and cover with bread crumbs. Bake at 400° for 15 to 20 minutes or until browned on top.
Mix sugar, milk and oleo; cook to soft ball stage.
Add peanut butter and beat.
Pour onto greased plate.
Thicken boiling milk with flour (mix flour with milk and cook until smooth).
Mix all other ingredients, except egg whites.
When thoroughly mixed, add slightly beaten egg whites.
Bake until brown on top; well done.
Bake in 425° oven first 15 minutes, then 325° for 30 plus minutes.
Brown hamburger and onions in a large pot.
Stir in tomato paste and simmer.
Add remaining ingredients and cook on low heat for 30 minutes.
Makes a complete meal served with green salad. Serves 8 to 10.
Boil chicken; bone and cut up meat.
To the meat, add onion and green pepper.
Mix soups, tomatoes, chili powder and garlic salt.
Dip each tortilla in chicken broth, then in a buttered 9 x 12-inch baking dish.
Place a layer of tortilla, 1/2 the chicken, 1/2 the soup mixture and 1/2 cheese.
Repeat layers; bake 30 to 40 minutes in 350° oven until cheese melts.
Cook chicken in unsalted water to cover until tender.
Remove from broth, reserving 2 tablespoons broth.
Cool.
Bone chicken; chop fine.
Combine cheese and mayonnaise, beating until smooth. Add onion, pickle juice, celery, curry powder, salt, pepper, pimiento, hot sauce and garlic powder; mix well.
Stir in chicken and broth.

t: Chicken And Penne Pasta With Kalamata Olives
i: about 5 Tbsp. extra virgin olive oil
4 to 6 garlic cloves, chopped or sliced
freshly ground black pepper to taste
d: Cut white meat chicken up into 1-inch pieces.
Pit the kalamata olives and cut each olive into 3 or 4 pieces.
Chop or slice garlic.
Cut the basil leaves into strips.
Begin heating a large pot of salted water to boiling.
When water begins to boil, add pasta.
Cook beef and onions, drain.
Add other ingredients except cheese. Cook for 20 minutes. Pour in crock-pot, add grated cheese. Do not stir.
Simmer on low.
Serve with chips.
Sift together flour, sugar, baking powder, soda and salt.
Stir in shortening.
Dissolve yeast in 2 tablespoons warm water and add to buttermilk.
Add to flour mixture and mix well.
Roll out on floured board 1/4-inch thick.
Cut with a biscuit cutter.
Mix Ragu sauce and Cheddar cheese soup with 2 cups of Mozzarella cheese in baking dish.
Drain grease off ground beef. Mix ground beef in sauce.
Add cooked noodles; mix well.
Top with 2 cups of Mozzarella cheese.
Bake at 350° for 25 minutes.
Mix bourbon, catsup, sugar and onion in crock-pot.
Cut hot dogs in bite-size pieces.
Simmer in crock-pot on low for 4 to 6 hours.

i: 1 (10 oz.) can Campbell's French onion soup (not creamy)
d: Blend the French onion soup in blender on high for about 5 seconds.
Pour over ground beef in skillet.
Cook until done.
Mix all ingredients, including ground beef, in a big pan.
Cook on low for about 25 minutes.
Cut bread diagonally into 1-inch slices. Blend butter and other ingredients.
Butter bread slices and wrap in aluminum foil. Heat in hot oven at 400° for 10 minutes.
Also in a 250° oven for 1/2 hour or on coals.

i: 1 1/2 c. Corn Chex cereal, crushed to 1/2 c.
d: Toast Chex crumbs in a 300° oven for 10 minutes; set aside. Combine egg, seasoned salt and taco sauce.
Stir in ground beef and Chex.
Shape into 4 oval patties to fit bread loaf.
Broil or grill for 10 to 12 minutes, or until done.
Turn midway.
Place, end to end, on split French bread.
Pour the first 6 ingredients over remaining ingredients. Make up a day before serving. Will keep 3 weeks. Keep refrigerated.
Measure pecans in a small bowl.
Mix vinegar and sugar; pour over pecans and stir to coat thoroughly.
Let stand 15 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes.
Place pecans in a shallow baking dish and bake in a 300° oven for 10 minutes.
Add melted butter to pecans, stir and allow to bake for another 5 minutes.

i: 2 qt. peppers (salt down; let stand a while)
d: Cook all of the ingredients, adding salt to cook.
You can add cauliflower if you like but steam it a little.
Makes 16 quarts. You can double if you like.
Combine ingredients and pour into casserole dish.
Let jello start to jell; mix in drained pineapple.
Then add banana slices.
Mix well.
Let jell, then top with Cool Whip.

i: salt, pepper, garlic salt and onion salt
d: Wash the
potatoes well and dice.
In a large cake pan, pour the
Crisco
and
add potatoes. Coat potatoes in oil. Add salt, pepper,

i: 3 Tbsp. melted bacon fat or vegetable oil
d: Heat water, beans, onion and garlic to boiling in 2-quart saucepan.
Boil 2 minutes.
Remove from heat, cover and let stand 1 hour.
Add just enough water to cover beans and add salt.
Heat to boiling; reduce heat and simmer 1 1/2 hours.
(Add water if necessary!)
Heat and stir dates and water.
Cook until smooth.
Put dates, egg white, squash, spices and milk into blender and process until smooth.
Pour into unbaked 9-inch pie pastry and bake at 400° for 15 minutes, then at 350° for 20 minutes.
Sprinkle chicken with salt and pepper.
Put in frying pan. Mix Coca-Cola, catsup and mustard; pour over chicken.
Cover and simmer 45 minutes or 1 hour before removing cover, to let sauce thicken.

i: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen cut okra, thawed
1/4 lb. andouille or other smoked sausage, sliced
2 whole boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut into cubes
1 small green pepper, cut into strips
d: Heat oil in a large skillet on medium-high heat. Add sausage; cook and stir until lightly browned. Add chicken, onion, pepper, celery and garlic; cook and stir until chicken is cooked through. Stir in tomatoes, okra, tomato sauce and seasoning. Bring to boil. Stir in rice; cover. Remove from heat. Let stand 5 minutes or until liquid is absorbed. Stir.

i: 1 small can crushed pineapple (do not drain)
d: Mix all ingredients together.
Cook until thick (approximately 7 minutes in microwave).
Pour over 6 medium-size apples, cubed, and 1 cup of marshmallows.
Let cool.
Add nuts or raisins, if desired.
Mix together and put in greased muffin pans.
Bake at 425° for 18 to 20 minutes.

i: 5 or 6 biscuits or 5 or 6 slices bread, toasted
salt, pepper and garlic powder to taste
d: Brown sausage.
Remove from pan.
Put celery and onions in same pan and saute until tender.
Set aside.
Crumble cornbread and bread in large bowl.
Add sauteed vegetables and sausage.

i: 1 (16 oz.) can red kidney beans, drained
d: Combine all ingredients in a food processor or blender; mix until blended.
Taste to determine amount of hot sauce you wish to add.
If it's too thick for dipping, add a little water.
Make ahead so flavors mingle.
Serve with low calorie tortilla chips. Contains 62 calories in 1/4 cup.
Chop garlic and brown in skillet with olive oil.
Shuck clams, reserving the juice.
Chop clams and add to garlic and oil.
Cook for 3 minutes.
Add parsley and pepper; simmer for 5 minutes. Serve over freshly cooked linguini.

i: 1 ready crust graham cracker pie crust (6 oz.)
7 oz. (1/2 14 oz. can) evaporated milk, well chilled
d: Pour chilled evaporated milk into a medium size bowl and whip with an electric mixer until soft peaks form, about 1-2 minutes. Mix water, lemon juice and gelatin in microwave bowl and heat on high for 1 minute.
Stir to dissolve gelatin.
Mix whipped milk, sugar, food coloring and gelatin mixture with an electric mixer until throughly blended.
Pour into pie crust.
Cover with inverted plastic dome and chill until fully set, at least 1 hour. Garnish with kiwi fruit, if desired.

i: 2 lb. boneless pork loin, trimmed of excess fat or boneless chicken breasts
d: Quarter the pork lengthwise into 4 large strips.
Pierce the meat with a fork.
Whisk the other ingredients together in a bowl. Line a roasting pan with heavy-duty aluminum foil.
Arrange the pork or chicken in a single layer.
Pour the marinade sauce over the meat and allow it to marinate for at least 4 hours, or overnight, in the refrigerator.

i: 2 c. cooked and cubed ham, turkey or chicken
1 c. Miracle Whip or your favorite dressing
d: Chop broccoli, cauliflower, lettuce and tomatoes. Place the vegetables in a large mixing bowl.
Grate carrots and add to vegetable mixture.
Stir in cheese and meat.
If adding the hard-boiled eggs, slice them and mix into vegetables. Mix with Miracle Whip and refrigerate 1 hour.
Serve with crackers or toast.
Layer in pan:
refried beans, taco mix and sour cream (mixed together), lettuce, tomatoes, salsa and cheese.
Serve with nacho chips.
Cut steak 6 oblong pieces (or have done at the butcher's). Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Pound thin with edge of plate.
Top each with half strip bacon.
Mix crumbs, onion, parsley and cheese. Spread over bacon.
Roll up and secure with toothpicks or string. Brown gently on all sides in hot oil.
Mix tomato paste and remaining ingredients, except pasta.
Brown hamburger; drain.
Add spices and tomatoes.
Heat. Cover bottom and sides of a 13 x 9-inch baking dish with tortillas.
Pour beef mixture over tortillas.
Place a layer of tortillas over meat mixture.
Combine cottage cheese, Monterey Jack and egg together and pour over tortillas.

i: 1 lb. of extra lean ground beef
1 1/2 c (6 oz.) shredded Cheddar cheese, divided
12 sliced bacon, cooked, crumbled and divided
d: Wash potatoes; prick several times with a fork.
Microwave them with a wet paper towel covering the potatoes on high for 6-8 minutes.
The potatoes should be soft, ready to eat.
Let them cool enough to handle.
Cut in half lengthwise; scoop out pulp and reserve.
Discard shells.
Mix together the first
ingredients in a medium-size bowl. In a small bowl
beat egg whites, orange juice, oil and vanilla; add to dry ingredients until just moistened.
Fold in blueberries. Fill muffin
cups (sprayed with nonstick cooking spray) about half full.

i: 3 1/2 lb. Hamburger meat (give or take according to cut of meat)
4 (15 oz.) cans Luck's Chili Hot Beans
d: Brown meat, drain grease.
Add onion and cook till onion almost done.
Add remaining ingredients.
Simmer on medium to low heat until done.

i: 6 whole wheat pitas, cut in half
d: Cover eggs in pan with cold water by 1 inch.
Bring to a boil; turn off heat.
Let sit covered for 15 minutes.
Drain. Rinse under cold water.
Mix eggs, onion, mayonnaise, curry, carrot, salt and pepper.
Divide lettuce, tomato and egg mixture into pitas.

i: 1 c. chopped roast beef, cut fine and cooked
2 Tbsp. chopped Mario salad olives with pimiento strips
d: Combine all ingredients and chill overnight.
Roll into balls. Place on serving tray.
Sprinkle with paprika.
Makes 2 dozen.
Combine meat, egg, onion and pepper.
Make 1-inch balls. Place in greased 8 x 8-inch pan.
Spoon cottage cheese around meatballs.
Pour sauce over all; sprinkle cheese on top.
Bake at 350° for 50 minutes, or until done.

i: 5 large onions, peeled and thinly sliced
d: Heat oil in a large skillet; saute onions until golden brown. Sprinkle on sugar and continue to saute until onions are well browned and crisp.
Drain on paper towel to remove excess oil; set aside.

i: 1 pkg. (8 oz.) Velveeta cheese, regular or hot
1 pkg. (12 or 16 oz.) spaghetti, cooked and drained
d: Boil spaghetti until cheese is cooked.
Melt Velveeta cheese in 1 cup milk.
Blend mixture of cheese and milk and well drained spaghetti together.
Pour into casserole dish.
Sprinkle American cheese on top and bake at 350° until the cheese becomes brown.
Serve hot.
Mix all ingredients except ginger ale. Chill. Just before serving, add ginger ale (as desired). Serves 50.

i: 1 top, bottom, or eye of round roast (5 lb.)
2 c. pitted ripe black olives, quartered
d: Brown beef on all sides in oil.
Add wine and water to meat. Cover and simmer 2 1/2 to 3 hours until meat is fork-tender.

i: 2 medium onions, thinly sliced and separated into rings
d: Scrub potatoes and slice 1/8 inch thick.
Coat.
Put in shallow 2-quart casserole with Pam.
Combine potatoes and salad dressing in casserole; toss gently.
Cover and microwave it on High for 10 to 12 minutes, stirring at 4-minute intervals.
Stir in remaining ingredients.

i: 1 c. whole wheat pie and pastry flour
d: Combine the ingredients and roll or press in pie pan. Bake for 15 minutes at 425 degrees.

i: 1 round steak (top or bottom, 10 or 12-inches long and 6 or 7-inches wide; meat should be 1/2 to 3/4-inches thick)
d: You will need a 3 to 4-quart pot (Dutch oven) and 1 empty Coke bottle.

t: Linguine With Shrimp Scampi And Broccoli
d: Melt 1/2 butter in oil and saute shrimp for 2 minutes.
Put aside.
Put 1/2 butter, 2 cloves garlic and olive oil in.
Add broccoli, wine, parsley and pepper.
Mix together, then add shrimp.
Serve over cooked linguine.
Prick the sausages with a sharp fork, cover with cold water and bring to a boil.
Simmer, uncovered, about 10 minutes; drain and dry.
Slice in rounds.
Heat the oil in a skillet and add the sausage, onion, garlic and parsley.
Cook, stirring often, until meat and vegetables are golden brown, then add tomatoes and tomato sauce.
Simmer covered while you prepare pasta.

i: 2 to 3 celery stalks with leaves, chopped and crushed
2 Tbsp. olive oil or corn oil
2 bay leaves, broken into small pieces
d: Rinse artichokes thoroughly in running cold water; drain on paper towel.
Snap off small bottom leaves, slice off stem and immediately dip into 3 tablespoons of lemon juice to prevent discoloration.
Trim the thorn tip off each leaf (about 1/2-inch) and slice off top of artichoke to eliminate thorns on leaves not easily trimmed.
Dip and roll in lemon juice.

i: 1 (16 oz.) can jellied cranberry sauce (can use whole berry sauce)
d: Combine first 7 ingredients in a medium saucepan.
Stir together flour and water until smooth.
Add to cranberry mixture. Bring mixture to a boil, stirring constantly.
Reduce heat and simmer 5 minutes or until thickened and bubbly.
Remove from heat and cool.
Store ketchup in an airtight container in refrigerator. Serve with cream cheese and crackers.
Peel onion and chop fine.
Seed green pepper and chop.
Chop celery and carrot.
Place fish fillets on large plate.
Sprinkle with lemon juice and salt.
Let stand 30 minutes.
Wash cucumbers and cut in sticks.
Cover with boiling water and let stand 4 to 5 hours.
Drain and pack solidly into pint jars.
Mix remaining ingredients in pan, bring to a boil and boil 5 minutes.
Pour over cukes, filling within 1/2-inch of top.
Put on lids and rings.
Pita croutons:
Bake pita bread for 4 hours at 200° or until crisp.
Break into small pieces.
Do not brown pita bread.
Cut up lettuce.
Chop cauliflower and onion.
Fry bacon until crisp.
Crumble into bits.
Sprinkle over lettuce mixture.
Line a 13 x 10-inch pan with 1 large sheet of foil to cover meat completely later.
Put in brisket, season beef and arrange onion slices on top.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
When onions brown slightly, then cover (seal) beef tightly with foil.
Add 1 cup of water to pan (may have to add more water).
Reduce heat to 300°. Cook 2 more hours.

i: 6 small cans artichoke hearts, drained and quartered
2 green peppers, seeded and cut into thin strips
2 red onions, peeled and thinly sliced
d: Toss together the artichokes, tomatoes, peppers and onions. In a separate bowl, whisk together the remaining ingredients and pour over the vegetables.
Serve over a bed of greens.
Yield:
25 portions.
Cook ham and potatoes until potatoes are almost done.
Add noodles and cook for 12 to 15 minutes.
Then add tomato juice and sauce and simmer until done.

i: 2 Tbsp. hot pepper sauce (Tabasco sauce) or to taste
d: Using poultry shears, cut the bird open by cutting down the backbone.
Flatten a bit by pounding with your hand so that the chicken is "butterflied".
Mix the remaining ingredients in a large bowl; marinate the bird for 2 hours.
Cook
hamburger
in pot; drain.
Pour in other ingredients; cook
minutes.
In a saucepan combine sugar, vinegar, turmeric and mustard. Bring to a boil.
Remove from heat and cool (sugar should be dissolved).
Drain sauerkraut, bean sprouts and water chestnuts. Slice chestnuts.
Mix kraut, bean sprouts, sliced chestnuts, chopped celery, sliced onion rings and mix in cooled liquid.
Keep in refrigerator.
Mix taco seasoning into refried beans.
Spread into small casserole dish.
Spoon sour cream on top of and sprinkle with cheese.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes or microwave on High for 5 to 8 minutes.
Wash cucumbers thoroughly and drain.
Slice, unpeeled, into 1/8-inch slices.
Add onions and garlic.
Add salt and mix thoroughly.
Cover with crushed ice or cubes.
Let stand 3 hours.

i: 2 cans celery, onion, chicken or mushroom soup
d: Put raw hamburger in bottom of 9 x 13-inch pan.
Cover with a layer of stuffing mix (not prepared).
Cover stuffing with onion. Pour soup over stuffing and onion.
Then pour milk over soups. Top with frozen Tater Tots; cover with foil.
Bake at 350° for 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
Remove foil the last 15 minutes to brown the Tater Tots.
My Mother is horrified at my method, but I don't want to fuss over a lot of frying, so I put all the ingredients and the ground beef into the saucepan and stir the whole concoction together.
I add about 1/2 to 3/4 cup water and then let the curry cook on low heat until the hamburger is done.
I also add fresh cut potatoes, carrots, peas and cauliflower to the curry and presto!
It's all there and done!
Bring to a boil for one minute, longer if desired thicker.
Brown meat and drain.
Mix in cheese and spices until cheese is melted.
Spoon meat mixture on bread and broil just until meat browns.
Can be frozen before baking.

i: 1 small can frozen lemonade concentrate, thawed
d: Chill all liquid ingredients. Pour all over ice mold, adding Cold Duck last. It is a light alcoholic punch for a ladies special function.

i: 1 bunch fresh or frozen broccoli spears
d: Cool rice; spread in flat casserole dish.
Parboil broccoli; arrange on top of rice.
Combine mushrooms, mayonnaise and seasonings.
(For thinner sauce, add 1 cup milk.)
Top rice with sauce mixture.
Sprinkle almonds on top.

i: 1 head lettuce, washed, dried and torn into small pieces
4 strips crisp bacon, cooked and crumbled
d: Layer the first 5 ingredients in order given.
Top with mayonnaise, bacon and cheese.
Garnish with tomato wedges and cucumber slices. Refrigerate at least 6 hours before serving.

i: 4 packets sugar substitute or to taste
d: Mix all ingredients well.
Makes 6 servings, 33 calories per 1 cup serving.

i: 1 c. prepared Hidden Valley Ranch Original salad dressing
d: Wash and dry chicken pieces.
Dip into prepared salad dressing.
Spoon crumbs over the dressing to cover.
Place in foil lined pan.
Sprinkle with salt, pepper and paprika to taste. Bake uncovered at 375° for 1 1/4 hours.
Wash eggplants, cut in half lengthwise and scoop out center, leaving 1/2-inch wall.
Cut center into pieces and set aside. Place small amount of olive oil in skillet and saute hamburg, parsley, onions, salt, pepper and mushrooms together.
Cook until hamburg browns.
Toss with fork.
Add tomato sauce and cook 15 minutes.
Add cut up pieces of eggplant and cook 15 minutes more, stirring.
Arrange marshmallows in 8-inch square pan.
Combine chocolate, peanut butter and butter in saucepan.
Melt over low heat, stirring constantly.
Pour mixture over marshmallows and smooth into the cracks.
Refrigerate.
Cut into small pieces about 30 minutes before serving.

i: 1 (40 oz.) can sweet potatoes, mashed and drained
d: Mix well and spread in Pam sprayed 12 x 9-inch pan.
In large bowl, beat together sugar and shortening; add molasses.
Mix well.
Add beaten egg.
Stir together all remaining ingredients.
Mix into molasses mixture until thoroughly combined. Cover and chill at least 1 hour or overnight.
Roll dough into 1-inch balls, then place balls into granulated

i: 4 boneless center cut pork loin chops (1 1/2-inches thick)
d: Combine spices; mix well.
Coat chops with spice mixture. Grill over medium-high heat 7 to 8 minutes each side, turning once.

i: 3/4 c. shredded carrots, green olives, green peppers, green onions, broccoli, cauliflower, mushrooms and black olives
d: Mix softened cream cheese, mayo and seasonings together, then let stand.
Spread crescent rolls in cookie sheet, jelly roll size pan.
Bake 10 to 15 minutes at 350°.
Let cool.
Spread cream cheese mixture on crust.
Add veggies and top with cheese.

i: 2 tsp. green bell pepper, finely minced
d: Melt butter and slowly fry the bacon in a Dutch oven until golden.
Remove and save. Add the onion, celery, bell pepper and garlic.
Saute until onion is transparent. Add potatoes, celery, bell pepper, bay leaf and boiling water.
Simmer for 10 minutes. Add tomatoes and catsup. Simmer until potatoes are soft.
Add clams, thyme, cayenne pepper, parsley and bacon. Simmer 3 minutes. Remove bay leaf.
Adjust seasoning and serve.
Place fish and ginger in pot of boiling water (water should cover fish).
