Turn flame off and cover with tight fitting lid. Let stand for 30 minutes or until done.
Place fish on platter. Sprinkle with finely chopped green onion.
Cook pork in lightly salted water over low heat 2 to 2 1/2 hours.
Drain, reserving 1 cup broth.
Cut pork into thin slices and place in a large shallow baking pan.
Combine salt, reserved broth and remaining ingredients.
Mix well and pour over pork slices.
Bake at 325° for 30 to 45 minutes.
Make a cream sauce of the oleo, flour, salt and milk.
Add cheese; stir until melted.
Cook until thick.
Cook macaroni until nearly done; drain and put in a baking dish.
Pour cream sauce over macaroni; sprinkle cheese on top.
Bake at 350° for about 30 minutes, or until brown.
One day in advance before serving, mix well all ingredients and refrigerate.
Preheat oven to 375°.
Beat egg whites until stiff.
Gradually beat sugar into stiff egg whites.
Fold in vanilla, salt and chocolate chips.
Drop by teaspoonfuls onto greased cookie sheet. Put cookies into the oven.
Turn the oven off and leave cookies in overnight.
Dissolve yeast in tepid water.
Combine the rest of the ingredients in a large bowl.
Add the yeast mixture to ingredients and beat at high speed until a smooth dough is formed.
Cover and place in warm place until batter rises 2 to 3 times original amount.
When dough has risen, moisten 1 hand with water and take a handful of dough.
Squeeze your fist until a small ball is formed and with a spoon, ease it into the hot oil.

i: 2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen spinach, thawed and drained (you can also use fresh)
d: Cook sausage meat over medium-heat in skillet until browned, 10 minutes.
Stir often; spoon off fat.
Reserve 1 egg yolk.
In a large bowl, combine remaining eggs, sausage meat, spinach, Mozzarella cheese, Ricotta cheese, salt, pepper and garlic. Prepare pie crust in a 9 or 10-inch pie pan.
Spoon meat mixture into shell.
Roll out top crust and put over filling in pie shell. Make steam vent in top shell.

i: 6 large whole chicken breasts (on the bone)
1 large onion, peeled and cut in quarters
d: Put chicken
in
large
pot with peppercorns and onion. Add enough water
to cover and bring to boil.
Reduce heat and cook for 10 minutes.

i: 2 Tbsp. plus 1 tsp. pure olive oil, divided
salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
d: * To roast bell pepper, halve, core and seed them.
Flatten with your hand and place, skin side up, on foil on a baking sheet. Broil about 3-inches from the heat until skins blacken and are charred.
Remove to a paper bag and close up for 15 minutes to steam.
Rub off the charred skin.

i: 1 1/2 c. cracker crumbs or cereal crumbs
d: Mix meat, eggs, crumbs, onion, parsley flakes and Worcestershire sauce together.
Season to taste.
Shape into meatballs and bake at 400° for 15 minutes.
Drop into sauce and heat thoroughly.
Mix cornstarch and sugar.
Stir in reserved pineapple syrup, vinegar and soy sauce until smooth.

i: 1 1/2 lb. ground beef (get good lean beef)
d: Add chopped onion, pepper and celery to beef. Add eggs, bread, Tabasco, Worcestershire, salt, pepper and garlic. Mix well. Pack into a lightly greased 9 x 5 x 3-inch loaf pan.

i: 2 Tbsp. each oregano and chili powder
d: Put noodles on to boil in large pot. Salt and add 1 tablespoon oil. Let water boil before adding noodles. In a large pot, cook onions and green peppers in 2 tablespoons oil for 3 minutes. Add hamburger and stir until browned. Add spaghetti sauce and spices. Cook over low heat, stirring often, about 15 minutes. In very large Pyrex baking pan, put in a little sauce and spread over bottom. Add one layer of noodles. Spread 1/3 Ricotta cheese on noodles. Next, spread about 1/3 sauce. Then sprinkle a thin layer of Mozzarella cheese. Repeat twice, ending with heavier layer of Mozzarella cheese on top.
In a large baking dish (9 x 13-inch), melt butter. Stir in honey, pineapple juice, mustard, salt and curry powder. Coat chicken well and arrange in baking dish. Bake about 1 hour at 375° (uncovered).
Turn at least once during baking. Serves 4.
Preheat oven
to
Grease
a
12 cup (2 1/2 cups) muffin pan.
Mix all ingredients.
Pour into a greased baking pan or an iron skillet.
Bake in a hot oven at 435° for 30 to 35 minutes or until done.
Very good!
Blend together 2 to 3 minutes cucumbers, tomatoes, consomme or water and pimiento.
Add and blend for a shorter time the olive oil.
Add, but do not blend, as the flavor would be too strong the chopped chives.
Season to taste.
Serve by pouring the broth over 2 ice cubes.
Makes about 1 cup.

i: 1 (8 oz.) can whole kernel corn, drained
2 fresh tomatoes, peeled and chopped (in summer)
d: Cut chicken into 1/2-inch cubes.
Cut peppers into strips. Cook pasta according to package; drain.
Meanwhile, add cooking oil and preheat wok over high heat.
Stir-fry chicken 3 minutes or until lightly browned.
Add green peppers, onion, cumin and red pepper.
Stir-fry 3 minutes (adding more oil if needed).
Layer bottom of pan with vanilla wafers; put slice of banana on each wafer.
Mix Eagle Brand milk and water; add pudding and beat with fork until lumps are gone.
Add sour cream; mix until well blended.
Add Cool Whip.
Cover layer of wafers and bananas with 1/2 of pudding.
Add another layer of wafers and bananas; cover with other half of pudding.
One and one-fourth hours before serving, cook yams in boiling, salted water for 25 minutes or until tender.
Drain and cool.
In a 1-quart saucepan, mix cornstarch, nutmeg or cinnamon, salt and granulated sugar.
Stir in water.
Cook over low heat until sauce is clear.
Stir in lemon juice and brandy.
Cut chicken into small pieces (bite-size).
Cut grapes in half.
Add mayonnaise for desired consistency.
Grapes should be one-third amount of chicken.
Add curry powder to taste (sprinkle first).
Decorate with slivered almonds.

i: 1/2 c. crushed bran or whole wheat crackers (about 17-1 1/2)
2 whole eggs and 4 egg whites, beaten
10 oz. pkg. frozen, chopped spinach, drained
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
Spray 9-inch pie pan with nonstick spray.
Mix crumbs with small amount of egg mixture, just to moisten and spread in bottom of pan.
Beat together eggs, milk, yogurt, cottage cheese, Mozzarella cheese and spinach.
Pour into pie pan with prepared crust.
Bake at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes or until knife inserted comes out clean.

i: 1 pkg. lime gelatin, dissolved in 1 c. boiling water
1 c. fruit juice or cold water
d: Dissolve gelatin in boiling water and add fruit juice or cold water.
Add fruit cocktail.
Pour in a 9 x 13-inch dish and let set.
Grease a 13 x 9-inch pan.
Cut up cabbage in medium pieces. Spread on bottom of pan.
Brown meat and onion.
Stir in rice, salt and pepper.
Spoon over cabbage.
Heat tomato soup and water to boiling.
Beat together sugar, eggs, oil and carrots.
Add in the flour, soda, salt and cinnamon, then pour into a 9 x 13-inch plus an 8 x 8-inch pan or 15 1/2 x 11-inch pan and bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.
Melt butter; add brown sugar and curry powder.
When melted, add the fruit and bake in a 325° oven for 1 hour.
Stir two or three times.
Refrigerate overnight.
The next day warm again in oven.
Stir well together the sugar, syrup and water.
Allow to boil without stirring until it forms a hard ball in cold water.
Pour this over stiffly beaten egg whites, beating constantly.
Cool; add vanilla and nuts.
Beat until cool and thick enough to set. Pour into a buttered platter or drop by teaspoons on waxed paper.

i: 1 Tbsp. onion powder or small dried onion
d: In a 3-quart pot with cover, cook beef in 1 cup water for 1/2 hour, then add chicken breast with spinach and other ingredients. Add water as needed.
Cook for 45 minutes.
Usually served over rice.
Serves 4.

i: 1 lb. ground meat (beef, pork or chicken)
1 medium onion or 1/2 c. green onion
d: Cook meat. Chop remaining ingredients very fine or run through a food processor.
Return all ingredients to large skillet and cook until tender.
Place 1 teaspoonful in wonton wraps and deep-fry.
Serve hot with Sweet and Sour Sauce.
Sift flour, baking powder and salt.
Cream butter and sugar. Add egg and almond extract and beat well.
Blend in the dry ingredients gradually.
Mix thoroughly.
Add cherries, coconut and pecans.
Mix well.

i: 1 lb. jumbo shrimp, shelled and deveined
d: In a large bowl combine oil, garlic, parsley, bread crumbs, salt and pepper.
Add shrimp and scallops to mixture; mix well until coated.
Let stand 1 hour.

i: 1 c. chopped pecans (walnuts may be substituted)
water (enough to make a soft dough)
d: To standard muffin mix, add apple pie filling (chop up the apple slices, but use all of the can contents, including the thickening, which makes the muffins moist), cinnamon and pecans. Mix with enough water to make a soft dough.
Divide into muffin tins.
Will make 12 extra-large muffins or 24 medium.
Bake at 375° until done.
Heat oven to 400°.
Grease pans.
Mix all ingredients together and bake 20 minutes.
Yield: 2 dozen.
Mix cottage cheese, mayonnaise and Pet milk well.
Mix all together.
Put in refrigerator to chill.
Serve on lettuce leaf with crackers or Ritz.
Use 2 teaspoons of mixture for each cup of hot water to make hot tea.
Less sugar may be used in mixture, if desired.
Keep stored in airtight jar or canister.
Cook mushrooms, onion, green pepper and garlic in olive oil in a large, nonstick skillet over medium high heat, stirring constantly until tender.
Remove from heat; drain.
Combine cooked vegetables, drained kidney beans, olives and pepper.
Spoon about 1/2 cup bean mixture evenly down center of each tortilla. Top with 1 tablespoon sour cream, 1 tablespoon salsa and 1 tablespoon cheese; fold opposite sides over filling.
Coat a large nonstick skillet or griddle with cooking spray.
Heat over medium-high heat until hot.

i: 1/2 lb. whole candied red and green cherries
2 to 4 Tbsp. rum or brandy or 2 to 4 tsp. rum extract*
d: *I use 6 tablespoons brandy.
Bring 8 cups water and 1 tablespoon salt to boil.
Add rice. Cover.
Bring back to full boil.
Turn off heat.
Leave covered on burner 30 minutes.
Saute meat.

i: 1 large can pitted black olives, drained and chopped
d: Mix ingredients and spread on English muffins.
Bake at 400° until bubbles appear, about 15 minutes.

i: 2 c. cheese, shredded (your choice - Mozzarella, Monterey Jack, Cheddar, etc.)
d: Mix avocados with lemon juice to a spreading consistency.
In a separate bowl, mix sour cream, mayonnaise and taco mix.
Set aside.
Spread all of the avocado mix on the bottom of a 13 x 9-inch pan or large plate.
Spread 1 layer of refried beans on top of avocados.
Spread 1 layer of sour cream mix.
Dissolve yeast in warm water in a large bowl.
Stir in sugar, shortening, beaten egg, salt and 3 cups of the flour.
Beat until smooth.
Stir in enough flour to make dough easy to handle.
Place dough in greased bowl, then turn greased side up.
Cover and refrigerate at least 2 hours or until ready to use.
Combine spaghetti, eggs and Parmesan cheese.
Place half of mixture in greased 8 x 8-inch baking dish.
Add half of Mozzarella cheese.
Repeat with remaining spaghetti and Mozzarella.
Bake at 350° for 20 minutes.
Cut into squares and serve with spaghetti sauce.
Brown meat in 2 tbsp. fat in frying pan. Add vegetables, rice on top. When steam appears cover and cook on low for 1 hour.
Cut bananas in half lengthwise.
Carefully roll them in confectioners sugar and then in flour.
Beat the egg in wide soup dish.
Dip the banana strips in the egg and then into the sugar/flour mix.
Melt butter in large frying pan and cook bananas over medium heat.
Turn them carefully so they don't fall apart. When done, place them gently into a large serving dish.
Make a large patty for each person.
Place each patty separately in foil.
Sprinkle 1/2 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce over meat; dash with salt and pepper.
Place slice of onion on top of meat.
Place sliced potato on top of onion.
Place sliced carrot around meat.

i: cherry tomatoes, leaf lettuce and lime slices (garnish)
d: Combine oil, teriyaki sauce, lime juice, ginger, garlic powder and pepper.
Place tuna in a shallow dish.
Add marinade.
Cover and refrigerate 2 hours to 1 day.
Grill tuna over medium hot coals about 5 minutes on each side.
Serve on a bed of leaf lettuce.
Combine first 9 ingredients and place in a casserole. Sprinkle cracker meal over top.
Cover with catsup.
Dot with butter.
Bake at 375° for 1/2 hour.
Uncover and continue baking 15 minutes longer.

t: Marinated London Broil Or Flank Steak
d: Prick meat with
fork
and marinate in Italian dressing overnight or 5
to
6 hours.
Charbroil steak on grill.

i: 1 1/2 c. quick oats or bread crumbs
d: Mix first 6 ingredients and form into small balls.
Bake 10 minutes at 400°; turn and bake 5 more minutes.
Combine and heat brown sugar, vinegar, seasoning, green pepper and pineapple.
When sugar dissolves, stir in bouillon and cornstarch.
Cook, stirring constantly until thick and clear.
Stir in meatballs and heat thoroughly.
Combine dry ingredients. Add eggs, butter and milk, stirring until moistened. Spoon into greased muffin tins. Bake at 400° for 15 minutes. Makes 12 muffins.
In heavy skillet over medium heat, cook kitchen spoon of sugar until carmelized and medium brown color.
In large saucepan, cook 2 cups sugar and milk until it boils; remove from heat and add soda.
Stir thoroughly (mixture will be frothy).
Return to heat and again bring to rolling boil.
Remove from heat and stir in carmelized sugar; stir well.
Return to heat and cook to soft ball stage, stirring frequently.
Cook the lasagna noodles according to directions.
Drain rinse and separate.
brown the beef and sprinkle the mix over the meat.
Add tomatoes and blend well.
Whip the cream cheese. In a buttered 1 1/2 quart casserole.
Make 2 layers each of noodles, meat & dollops of cream cheese. Bake at 350 degree for 20 minutes or until bubbly.
Cut in shortening.
Stir in milk quickly and knead lightly for a few seconds, using as little flour as possible on board.
Roll to 1/4-inch thickness and cut with floured cutter.
Cook in lightly greased hot skillet over very low heat until biscuits are brown on underside and raised to height of 1-inch.
Turn and brown on other side.
Spread refried beans in bottom of 9 x 13-inch pan.
Brown meat and remove from heat.
Add taco mix, enchilada sauce and taco sauce.
Mix well and pour over beans.
Spread cheese on top.
Heat in 400° oven 15 to 20 minutes or until bubbly.
Place fillets in greased baking dish.
In saucepan melt butter and add soy sauce, garlic and herbs.
Pour over fish and bake at 375° for 10 minutes per inch of thickness at thickest part.
When almost done, remove fish and sprinkle with mushrooms and scallions.
Arrange lemon slices on top and make sure the topping is well basted with juices.
Return to oven and bake 5 minutes more or until fish flakes easily and topping is heated through. Serves 4 to 6.

i: 2 or 3 onions, peeled and sliced thin
cucumbers, sliced thin (enough for 1 gal.; wash cucumbers)
d: Mix sugar, vinegar and spices together; pour over cucumbers. Put in refrigerator.
Do not heat vinegar and spices.
Takes from 4 to 6 weeks to be good.
Keep in refrigerator.
Pack pickles and onions in gallon jar.
This is enough for 1 gallon.
Brown hamburger and onion together. Add remaining ingredients; mix well. Simmer for 1 hour.

i: 3 c. plain flour (cake flour may be substituted for plain flour)
1 to 2 Tbsp. flavoring (lemon, vanilla or almond or a combination of the 3)
d: Have all ingredients at room temperature. Hand stir all dry ingredients together. Add eggs and stir. Add butter and Crisco and stir. Add milk and stir. Put batter in

mixer and beat at high speed until well blended. Add 1 to 2 teaspoons of your favorite flavoring and mix well. Pour batter into greased and floured tube pan. Place in cold oven. Bake at 325° for 1 hour and 20 to 30 minutes.
Mix together garlic powder, parsley flakes, Parmesan cheese and oregano.
Cut bread in half lengthwise.
Spread oleo or butter mixture over both halves of bread.
Cut halves into 1-inch slices. Don't cut all the way through the crust.
Wrap each half in foil and bake in a preheated 350° oven for about 15 minutes.
Serve at once.
Pick full beans.
Snap as if to cook.
Place them on a scaffle out in the sun.
Stir them once or twice a day to dry quickly. When they are real dry, place them in cloth bags and hang them up. Or, you can place them in the freezer.
To cook them, soak overnight.
Next morning, rinse them good.

i: 6 to 8 chicken thighs, legs or breast pieces
d: Boil chicken until done; save broth.
Fry onion in oil until clear.
Add chicken pieces, green mango, curry and flour until browned.
Add broth and tomatoes.
Simmer 45 minutes.
Serve over rice, if desired.
Mix sour cream and taco seasoning together; set aside. Layer in order: bean dip, guacamole, sour cream mixture, picante sauce, chopped tomatoes, and sliced black olives.
Cover with cheddar cheese and top with jalapenos to taste.
Refrigerate.

t: White Pear Cake(Quick, Easy And Delicious!)
d: High altitude:
Add 1 tbsp flour and 1/4 C sugar.
Mix banana, pineapple, sugar and sour cream.
Add pecans and cherries.
Freeze in individual molds or cut in squares.
Mix ingredients together.
Chill and serve over crushed ice.
Melt cheese and stir in other ingredients.
Simmer for a few minutes.
Serve with plain tortilla chips.
In saucepan, melt oleo; add cocoa.
Combine remaining ingredients and add oleo-cocoa mixture.
Bake in 9 x 13-inch pan for 30 minutes at 350°.
While still hot, punch holes in cake. Spread on cake, 1/2 cup peanut butter mixed with 1 1/2 tsp. oil.
Thaw shells 10 minutes before baking 10 minutes at 400°. Reduce heat to 350°.
Brown beef; drain.
Add seasonings and fix as package directs.
Mix refried beans and half of salsa.
Layer crust with half beef, bean mixture and cheese.
Repeat layer. Bake 25 minutes.

i: salt, pepper and garlic powder to taste
d: Cut potato into quarters lengthwise; mix well with olive oil. Sprinkle with garlic powder, salt and pepper.
Place in nonstick baking dish and bake 20 minutes at 450° or until done.

i: 1 (11 oz.) can Green Giant Mexicorn, drained
d: In a small saucepan, combine sugar, onion, mustard, celery seed and vinegar; bring to a boil.
Stir in corn.
Reduce heat and simmer 10 minutes.
Place in a nonmetal bowl; cool.
Cover. Refrigerate 8 to 10 hours.
Melt margarine in a 9-inch pie pan.
Beat eggs, sugar and flour, then add milk and stir.
Add vanilla and melted margarine. Pour in pie pan.
Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.
Cook the beef, bell pepper and onion. Drain the beef.
Add the enchilada sauce, taco sauce and salt and pepper to taste.
Layer 6 tortillas in a 13 x 9-inch pan with half of the meat mixture and half of the grated cheese. Finish with second layer of each.
Pour the Pet milk over layers.
Bake at 350° for 35 minutes.
Wash and break up broccoli.
Toss in peanuts, bacon (fried and crumbed) and onion.
Mix sugar, mayonnaise and curry.
Right before serving, toss in dressing.
Cook spaghetti according to package directions; drain.
Add bottle of salad supreme; mix well.
Add dressing and chopped vegetables.
Mix well and chill.

i: 1 c. sorghum (1/3 c. molasses and 2/3 c. Karo)
d: Cream sugar and shortening.
Add eggs and sorghum and beat. Sift together dry ingredients.
Add dry ingredients alternately with water to creamed mixture.
Beat.
Pour into a 9 x 13-inch pan and bake at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes.
Mix all ingredients, except ginger ale and soda water; stir well.
Add ice cubes.
Just before serving, add ginger ale and soda water.
Top with frozen fruit ring, if desired.

i: 2 lb. lean sirloin, cut into paper-thin strips
6 baking potatoes, baked until easily pierced
d: Heat olive oil and saute onion and mushrooms until tender and wilted.
Add beef and stir until beef loses its red color. Sprinkle with flour.

i: 1 large Vidalia or other sweet onion
d: Peel onion, leaving root end intact.
Cut onion vertically into quarters, cutting to within 1/2-inch of root end.
Cut each quarter vertically into thirds.
Place onion in boiling water 1 minute; remove and place in ice water 5 minutes.
Loosen petals if necessary.
Drain onion, cut side down.

i: 2/3 c. cooked, mashed pumpkin (or canned pumpkin)
d: Beat eggs at high speed for 5 minutes.
Gradually beat in sugar.
Stir in pumpkin and lemon juice.
Combine flour, salt, baking powder and spices.
Fold in flour mixture.
Pour into a greased and floured (or Pam sprayed) jelly roll pan.

i: 2 Tbsp. salt or less to taste
1/2 fresh 9 lb. ham, shank or butt
d: In a bowl, mix all ingredients together except ham.
Remove rind from ham. Score fat with a knife.
Rub mixture over ham. Wrap ham in foil or plastic and refrigerate overnight.
Heat oven to 425°.
Unwrap ham.
Place in a 13 x 9-inch cake pan.

i: 1 (10 count) pkg. beef and bean frozen burritos, thaw and cut into thirds
15 oz. can beef chili without beans
d: Spray 9 x 13 baking pan with nonstick spray and line bottom with burritos, which have been cut into thirds. Mix chili and salsa together and pour over burritos. Sprinkle cheese on top and bake at 350° until hot and bubbly.
Mix all ingredients and refrigerate.
Serve with crackers.
Mix and spread on tortillas.
Roll up tortilla and wrap in Saran Wrap overnight or at least 6 hours.
Cut off in 3/4-inch slices.
Serve with salsa sauce.

i: 1/2 lb. cheese (your choice; Cheddar or Jack, etc.)
d: Cut up ham in small pieces and mix in with the potatoes, eggs and cheese that have been cut into chunks.
Add the mayonnaise and mix well.
Put in baking dish and bake, covered, 30 minutes at 350°.

t: Pumpkin Pie Crunch(Even Those Who Don'T Like Pumpkin Pie Like This!)
d: Grease 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan.
Combine everything but cake mix, nuts and margarine.
Pour into baking dish.
Sprinkle cake mix on top.
Add nuts and drizzle with melted margarine.
Bake at 350° for 50 to 55 minutes or until golden brown.

i: 6 ripe plum tomatoes, cut into 1/4-inch cubes
1/2 c. coarsely chopped pitted black olives
3 scallions (with 2-inch of green onion), cut thinly
d: Combine all ingredients except pasta.
In a large bowl, let rest covered for at least 2 hours to develop flavor.
Toss cooked pasta into sauce and serve at room temperature.
Mix first four ingredients.
Leave 1 hour, then drain for 3 hours.
Heat remaining ingredients to boiling.
Put the vegetables in jars, pour vinegar mix over vegetables and cover tightly.
Can put jalapeno peppers and red pepper to taste. Great for eating with beans and rice.

i: 1 lb. small new potatoes, scrubbed and patted dry
2 large cloves garlic, peeled and crushed
d: Place potatoes in a 1 1/2-quart dish.
Add remaining ingredients and stir to coat potatoes.
Cover tightly with microwave plastic wrap.
Cook on High for 10 minutes in a 600 to 700 watt oven or 15 minutes in a 400 to 500 watt oven, shaking dish to redistribute potatoes.
Potatoes are done when the tip of a small knife easily pierces the potato in the center.
Serves 4 as a side dish.

i: 3 medium kiwi fruit, peeled and quartered
1 (6 oz.) can frozen orange juice concentrate, thawed (undiluted)
d: Mix all ingredients in electric blender or food processor until smooth (make sure the
top is on the blender or food processor).
Pour into 1
gallon
ice cream freezer and freeze according to
manufacturer's

i: 1/2 c. red, green and/or yellow pepper strips
1 boneless, skinless chicken breast half, cut into strips
d: Spray a large skillet with no-stick cooking spray.
Add pepper and onion.
Cook and stir until tender.
Remove from skillet. Spray skillet with no-stick cooking spray.
Add chicken.
Cook and stir until cooked through.

i: 2 Tbsp. olive oil or vegetable oil
1 lb. boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut in 1-inch pieces
d: In a large skillet over medium heat, heat oil.
Add chicken, onion and garlic.
Cook 5 minutes, stirring frequently or until chicken is no longer pink.
Stir in tomato sauce, wine, sugar and seasonings.
Heat to boiling.
Reduce heat.

t: Robbie'S Favorite Graham Cracker Fruit Cake
i: 1 lb. unshelled walnuts, chopped or shelled 2 1/4 c.
1 lb. unshelled pecans, chopped or shelled 2 1/4 c.
14 oz. jar green cherries, chopped (save juice)
14 oz. jar red cherries, chopped (save juice)
1 (10 oz.) or 1 lb. pkg. large marshmallows
1 lb. box honey graham crackers, crumbled
1/2 box each: red and blonde raisins
d: Mix nuts, cherries and crackers together.
Pour juices into double boiler and melt marshmallows.
Pour over mixture and pack back into cracker box.

i: 1 (1 lb.) pkg. raw bacon, cut up
d: Mix all ingredients together.
Bake at 325° for 3 hours.
Boil chicken in water.
Cool and cut into pieces.
Put in a 9 x 13-inch dish.
Place on top of chicken in following order: chopped onion, grated cheese and crushed Doritos.
Whip cream to soft peaks.
Add sugar and vanilla.
Fold in marshmallows and pineapple.
Store in refrigerator.
Tastes best made one day in advance.
Really good served on plain cake.
Blend eggs slightly.
Add syrup, sugar, salt, margarine and vanilla.
Add nuts.
Pour into unbaked pie shell and bake for 12 minutes in a 375° oven, then reduce heat to 325° and bake for 40 minutes or until pie is set.
Dab fish with butter.
Cover with chopped vegetables.
Sprinkle with salt.
Cover and bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
A quick, easy, nutritious meal.
Add 1 c. water to large jar of Ragu.
Place small amount in 9 x 13 pan.
Fill uncooked noodles and place in pan.
Cover with remaining sauce.
Cover with foil and bake at 350° for about 1 hr. If using glass pan, cook longer.

i: 1 (10 oz.) can Ro-Tel tomatoes and chilies
d: Brown beef and add onions.
When onions are sauteed, add the Ro-Tel and beans.
Cook 30 minutes or until ready to serve.
Heat oil in a large saucepan and saute the onion for 5 minutes.
Add garlic and saute 1 more minute.
Add remaining ingredients and bring to boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Drain fruit (liquid makes good jello with lemon, oranges or lime).
Put in Dutch oven.
Cover with mixture of butter, brown sugar and curry powder.
Bake, uncovered, at 325° for 30 minutes. Serve in place of vegetable.
Wash and cut up lettuce and put in salad bowl.
Sprinkle Parmesan cheese and croutons over lettuce.
Pour desired amount of dressing over that and toss.
Melt oleo in 8-inch square pan.
Stir in flour and pecans and beat in bottom of pan to form crust.
Bake at 350° until lightly brown.
Mix all ingredients well and chill.
Combine in medium saucepan the cranberries, pears, sugar and lemon peel.
Heat to boil, stirring constantly.
Cover and cook 5 minutes longer.
Skim off foam with spoon.
Simmer, uncovered, 15 minutes, until fruits are tender.
Pour into bowl and allow to cool.

t: Gloop(Gloop Can Be Pulled To And Fro For A Snap-And-Crackly Tug-Of-War!)
i: 1 tsp. Borax powder (located in the detergent aisle in grocery stores)
d: Pour the white glue into a clean, empty peanut butter jar. Add 1/2 cup of water and 10 drops of food coloring and stir. Combine the Borax and a cup of water in a medium size plastic bowl.
Slowly pour the glue mixture into the Borax solution and stir until well mixed.
The Borax causes the mixture to congeal. Scoop the gloop out of the water and knead it in your hands until it becomes dry enough to play with.
If stored in a sealed container or plastic bag, it should keep for several weeks.
Gloop dries out after a lot of handling.

t: Easy Grilled Yellow Fin Tuna Steaks For 4
i: 2 one lb. yellow fin tuna steaks
d: Turn grill on and bring temp to medium high (approx. 20 minutes). Meanwhile, mix Stubbs pork marinade, molasses, soy sauce and ground pepper in a bowl or large cup. Set aside. Wash tuna steak with tepid tap water and place in flat bottom dish with sides (will be holding marinade, too). Fresh tuna should not smell fishy. Add marinade. Cover and set aside for 15 minutes or so. Turn tuna with tongs and let set for another 15 minutes. When grill is ready, place marinated tuna steaks on grill to sear side one. Pour part of the marinade on tuna. Cook on this side approximately 2 or 3 minutes with grill covered. Turn tuna over using flat spatula by quickly shoving under tuna while pressing down on grilling surface. This will help keep tuna intact. Reduce heat to low, pour on the rest of marinade and close lid. Tuna will continue to sear on higher heat and cook while temp. goes down. Turn once more after 4 or 5 minutes. Check tuna often during this stage. Remove from grill and let set for 5 minutes or so. Slice in half and serve.
Combine all ingredients in a jar.
Cover tightly and shake vigorously.
Makes 1 cup.
Drain fruits.
Place in greased casserole.
Melt butter; blend in sugar, cornstarch and curry powder.
Spoon curry mixture over fruits.
Bake in preheated 325° oven for 45 minutes.
Yields 8 servings.
Place caramels and water in double boiler and stir to dissolve.
Pur over pecans.
Make in flat round balls.
Put on buttered platter and refrigerate for several hours.
Mix all together.
Let set for awhile to blend.
Spread on crackers.

i: 5 to 6 qt. popped corn (salted or unsalted)
d: Heat the margarine, sugar and syrup in a heavy saucepan over medium heat.
Boil for 5 minutes. Remove from heat.
Add salt, vanilla and baking soda.
Pour over corn and bake at 250° for 1 hour.
Stir every 15 minutes.
Blend
in punch bowl and serve.
Makes about 1 1/3 punch bowl.

i: 1 small jar whole pimentos with juice, chopped
d: Mix with heavy-duty mixer until well blended.
Refrigerate.

i: 2 medium zucchini, halved lengthwise and cut into 1/4-inch slices
2 medium ripe tomatoes, seeded and diced
d: In a large nonstick skillet, saut the zucchini, corn, green pepper, leek and seasoned salt in oil until vegetables are tender. Add tomatoes; heat through. Yield: 4 servings.

t: Sweet And Sour Tofu And Vegies
i: 1/3 can (2/3 c.) pineapple chunks with juice
1/2 Tbsp. malt or molasses or 1 tsp. honey
d: Dissolve cornstarch in 2 tablespoons of water; add 1/4 cup pineapple juice, 1/4 cup water, vinegar, honey, soy sauce, ginger, and pepper; set aside.
Heat oil in a wok or large skillet on high heat.
Add onion and stir until strong smell starts to go away. Add carrot and stir a little more.
Add tofu and stir until lightly browned.
Add green pepper and salt.
Stir a minute more, then reduce heat to medium and cook, uncovered.

i: pickled jalapeno peppers, halved and seeded
d: Mix tuna, mayonnaise and Cheddar cheese in a bowl.
Fill jalapeno pepper halves with mixture. Keep refrigerated until served.
* Four small to medium-size breast halves on a plate covered with plastic lid will cook in 5 minutes in microwave.
Combine all ingredients; chill.

i: 2/3 c. Italian or buttermilk salad dressing
d: Combine all ingredients except avocado and tomato.
Chill several hours.
Add rest and serve.
Place lasagna in a 12 x 7-inch glass baking dish. Cover with water and set aside.
In 1-quart glass dish, combine onion, oil and flour. Cook on High power 1 minute. Stir to dissolve flour. Stir in vegetables and milk.
Cook on High for 4 minutes, stirring once. Combine cheese, cottage cheese, egg and pepper.
Drain lasagna.
In the same dish, layer half each lasagna, vegetables, cheese and tuna.
Repeat layers and top with remaining cheese. Cover and cook on High power 15 minutes or until hot.
Grate cheeses together.
Mix eggs and picante sauce together. Put 1/2 of cheeses in bottom of Pyrex dish (oblong).
Put the egg and picante mixture on top of cheese.
Cover with the remaining cheese.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Cut in squares and place each piece on Doritos or tortilla chips.
Prepare pie crust as directed on box for 9-inch pie.
Line plate bottom with crust.
Mix sugar and flour.
Mix with apples. Fill apples in bottom of crust; add the blueberry filling on top of apples.
Cover with top crust.
Slit vent in top; flute edges high to prevent spill-over in oven.

i: 1 pkg. dry chicken noodle soup mix
d: Cook chicken in the water until tender (20 to 25 minutes). Remove meat in strips; sprinkle with lemon juice, salt and pepper and toss, then set aside.
Wash chicken and dry pieces between towels.
Saute chicken and onions in fat until chicken is golden brown on all sides.
Combine salt, paprika, curry powder and catsup.
Add catsup mixture and hot water to chicken.
Cover and simmer 30 minutes or until tender.

i: 1 gal. green tomatoes, chopped (measure after chopped)
d: Soak tomatoes, peppers and onions 2 hours in salt; add no water.
Wash 3 times.
Tie the spices in a bag.
Put all together and cook 1 hour.
Remove spice bag and relish is ready for cans.
Prepare outdoor grill for barbecuing.
Meanwhile, peel carrots; cut each carrot lengthwise in half.
Cut each unpeeled potato lengthwise into 6 wedges.
Cut each onion lengthwise in half.
In 12-inch skillet over high heat, in 3/4-inch boiling water, heat carrots, potatoes and onions to boiling.
Reduce heat to low; cover and simmer 10 to 15 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp; drain.
Place asparagus and water in 1-quart casserole; cover. Microwave at High 4 1/2 to 5 1/2 minutes, or until asparagus is hot, stirring after half the cooking time to break apart.
Drain. Cover and set aside.
In small bowl or 1 cup measure, combine lemon juice, Tabasco sauce and seasonings.
Pour mixture over asparagus.
Toss to coat.
Sprinkle with sunflower seeds.

i: 1 (16 oz.) can tomatoes, drained and chopped (reserve liquid)
d: Mix all ingredients.
If too dry, add some of reserved tomato juice.
Refrigerate overnight.
Serve with Doritos or Fritos. Yields 2 cups.

i: 12 oz. salmon fillet, cut into 3/4-inch pieces
1/4 c. chopped, fresh dill or 1 Tbsp. dried dill weed
d: Heat oil in heavy, large skillet over medium-high heat. Season salmon with salt and pepper.
Saute in skillet until just cooked through, about 3 minutes.
Using slotted spoon, transfer salmon to plate.
Add garlic to same skillet; saute 30 seconds. Add mushrooms; saute 2 minutes.
Add green onions, tomato, wine and capers; cook until mushrooms are tender, about 5 minutes.
Add linguine, dill, lemon juice and salmon and toss.
The day before serving, prepare tomatoes. Wash, remove stems and cut tomatoes in halves crosswise.
Rinse snails with cool water and drain.
Melt butter in frying pan and add
garlic.
Saute
lightly
over
Mix and pour into a tube pan and bake at 350° for 1 1/2 hours.
Cook rice according to package directions and cool.
Drain artichokes (save juice) and chop.
Add chopped vegetables and chopped artichokes to cold rice.
Mix juice from artichokes, mayonnaise and curry; pour over rice/vegetable mixture.
Marinate for 20 minutes.

i: 1/2 c. whipped cream or Cool Whip
d: In a mixing bowl, combine milk and lemon juice. Mix well.
Add yogurt.
Mix well.
Spoon mixture into cones.
Set upright in glasses or jars and place in freezer.
Freeze 3 hours.

i: 1 can Eagle Brand sweetened condensed milk
d: Make white or yellow cake mix and bake according to package directions in a 9 x 13-inch sheet pan.
Immediately pour the Eagle Brand milk over hot cake.
Let sit until cake is warm.
Pour the cream of coconut over cake.
Sprinkle with coconut.
Mix all together and chill.
In large bowl, combine 3 cups flour, yeast, sugar, salt and baking soda; set aside.
In medium saucepan, combine milk and water and heat until very warm (115° to 120°).
Add to flour mixture gradually, beating until batter is smooth.
Gradually add the remaining flour.
Mix ingredients.
Make into balls and brown in heavy skillet. Add tomato juice and simmer for 1 to 2 hours.
Add water if it gets dry.
In large saucepan, cut Polish sausage into 1/2-inch slices. Then add remaining ingredients.
Cook on medium for about 30 minutes, then simmer for 15 minutes.
In a large skillet, fry bacon crisp.
Drain; crumble.
Discard all but 3 tablespoons of drippings in pan.
This lamb can be
roasted
on
an open spit for a great party. We usually do this at Easter.

i: 1 pt. chopped onions or 1/2 box dehydrated onions
d: Simmer for 1/2 hour.
Combine tomato sauce, brown sugar, vinegar, onion, salt, pepper and garlic powder.
Simmer on medium heat 5 to 8 minutes.
Sprinkle salt over squash, onions and peppers.
Let stand overnight.
Rinse and drain well.
Bring remaining ingredients to a full boil.
Pack in sterile jars.
Seal.
Sift flour, nutmeg and baking powder.
Chop fruit and nuts. Mix into flour until all fruit is separated and flour coated. Cream sugar and butter, then add eggs.
Beat well.
Pour sugar mixture into flour mixture and mix well.
Grease and flour tube pan, then place brown paper in the bottom.
Bake 2 1/2 to 3 hours at 250°.
Brown meat; drain.
Add onion, chili powder and pepper sauce and cook until onion is tender.
Add 1/4 pound cheese spread (cubed) and sour cream.
Mix well.
Dip tortillas into hot oil and remove quickly to drain.
Fill tortillas with meat mixture.

i: hot sauce to taste or hot sausage
d: Mix all ingredients together and spoon onto cookie sheet. Bake at 400° until done.

i: 2 medium size cucumbers, peeled, seeded and chopped
1 c. mayonnaise with 1 to 3 cloves garlic, minced
1/2 c. plain yogurt or sour cream
d: In a large bowl, combine chicken, cucumbers, Feta cheese, olives and parsley; set aside.
In a small bowl, combine mayonnaise, yogurt and oregano.
Add to chicken mixture.
Toss lightly until coated.
Cover and chill.
Serve on Boston lettuce or whole wheat pita.
Boil chicken until tender; cool.
Debone and set aside.
In mixing bowl, blend flour, baking powder and salt.
Cut in shortening.
Add enough chicken broth to form dough.
Roll out on floured surface (thin).
Cut cabbage in large pieces, stalk and all.
Put in 4-quart cooker.
Add bacon grease, salt, pepper and water.
Cook until done, adding more water if necessary.
Mix together in large bowl.
Mix all ingredients together well.
Chill several hours before serving.
Mix together and add 1/2 cup lemon juice before pouring into graham cracker crust.

i: 6 oz. bag corn or tortilla chips
1 can kidney or Ranch Style beans
d: Toss 1 cup grated cheese, 1/2 cup French or Thousand Island dressing and hot sauce to taste.
Crunch and add one (6 ounce) bag of chips.
Slice and add 1 avocado.

i: 4 (15 oz.) cans Great Northern white beans
3/4 lb. bacon, cooked and cut in pieces
d: Combine all ingredients well in a casserole dish.
Bake for 1 hour at 325°.

i: 1 to 2 lb. boneless chicken breast, skinned and cut into 1/2 inch pieces
4 to 8 drops of hot pepper sauce
2 c. kidney or red beans, rinsed and drained
d: Heat oil in large saucepan over medium heat. Add chicken; cook 5 to 10 minutes or until lightly browned, stirring frequently. Add onion; cook 3 to 5 minutes. Stir in remaining ingredients. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium; cover and cook 30 to 40 minutes or until rice is tender. Stir occasionally so rice does not stick. Yields 9 to 10 cups.

i: finely diced jalapeno pepper to taste (optional)
d: Heat chili.
Add Velveeta and peppers.
Stir until cheese is melted.
Serve hot with tortilla chips.
Put bulgur wheat in bowl.
Add boiling water; let stand 1 hour.
Drain and return to bowl.
Add all other ingredients and refrigerate.

Preheat oven to 400°. In resealable plastic bag, combine cheese, crumbs and paprika; shake well. Transfer mixture to plate; dip each chicken breast in cheese mixture, turning to coat all sides. Arrange on nonstick baking sheet. Bake until chicken is cooked through, 20 to 25 minutes.

t: Robert Fraga'S Cuban Black Bean Soup
d: Preparation Time:
Soak beans overnight, cook for 3 hours.
Mix sugar, egg yolks and shortening.
Mix well.
Add flour and baking powder alternately with milk.
Beat egg whites last.
Fold into batter.
Bake at 350°
Combine and mix all ingredients thoroughly.
Chill for 2 hours.
Shape into cakes.
Melt 3 tablespoons of butter in a skillet over a quick fire.
Brown the cakes on both sides. Yield: 6 cakes.
Prepare 12 hours before serving and keep refrigerated.
Mix oil, sugar and eggs in bowl.
Beat until creamy.
Add a little at a time the flour, salt, soda and baking powder.
Add apples, nuts, coconut and vanilla.
Bake for 1 hour in angel food pan at 350°

i: 1 egg, beaten or 1/4 c. Egg Beaters
3 Tbsp. flour (slightly more if needed to hold patties together)
8 or 9 crushed crackers (to roll patties in)
d: Mix salmon, onion, green pepper (if desired), basil, thyme, salt, pepper, egg, flour and cracker crumbs (if desired).
Form patties and roll in additional cracker crumbs.
Fry in small amount of Crisco.
Makes 5 to 6 patties.

i: dash of pepper, oregano and green pepper
d: Parboil cabbage leaves 5 minutes.
Mix all other ingredients and place half of mixture on each leaf.
Roll tightly and place in greased baking dish.
Pour tomato sauce over rolls and bake at 350° for
1 hour.
Yields 1

i: 1 lb. ground beef, browned and drained
brick of Monterey Jack cheese with jalapenos, cubed
d: Melt cheeses (with salsa) with ground beef. Serve with Doritos.
Grease tube pan.
Cut each biscuit into fourths.
Shake in a bag with sugar and cinnamon.
Put a layer of pecans (1 cup) and 1/2 biscuit mixture in pan and repeat this.
Pour mixture of brown sugar and margarine over all of this.
Bake at 350° for about 30 to 40 minutes.
In large heavy pot, brown turkey; pour off any excess fat. Add onion, green pepper and garlic; cook 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add beans, water, tomatoes, tomato paste, chili powder, oregano, cumin and red pepper; cover.
Simmer for 1 1/2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Serve hot with crackers.
Grind and boil for 30 minutes.
When thick, pour into jars and seal.

i: 3 c. leftover cubed cooked roast beef
d: In a large saucepan, combine all ingredients.
Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer 30 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
If necessary, add 1/2 to 1 cup water to thin soup. Yield:
6 to 8 servings.

In small ovenproof bowl, spread gelatin over water. Set bowl in a saucepan of hot water. Let stand until gelatin dissolves. Remove from saucepan. Let cool 1 minute. Whip cream until almost stiff. Add gelatin, salt and vanilla. Continue to whip until stiff. Makes 2 1/2 cups.

i: 2 chicken cutlets or 2 large whole breasts without skin
d: Cut chicken cutlets (or whole breasts) in half.
Microwave rice per directions.
Slice mushrooms into thick slices.
Mince the garlic and saute in skillet with warm olive oil until soft. Add the chicken and cook quickly on both sides but don't brown the meat.
Cut scallions in 1/2-inch pieces and add to pan, stirring until soft.
Meanwhile, in a mixing bowl, stir together the cranberry sauce and wine and add mixture to the skillet.
Brown meat, onions and garlic.
Add salt, pepper and molasses or brown sugar. Add this to beans in baking dish.
Stir.
Cover and cook 30 minutes at 325°.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.
Add Fri Chik, pineapple and garlic powder into sauteed carrot and onions.
Mix pineapple juice, soy sauce, cornstarch, lemon juice and brown sugar.
Stir into hot mixture.
Cook until thickened.
Add water chestnuts and snow peas.
Serve onto rice.

i: 1 lb. frozen bread dough, thawed or 1 lb. pizza dough
d: Preheat oven to 450°.
Combine onion and garlic with an equal amount of water; set aside 10 minutes to soften.
Fit dough into a greased 12-inch round pizza pan.
Brush dough with 2 tablespoons of oil.
Sprinkle dough with salt and reserved onion and garlic. Dribble remaining 2 tablespoons oil over top of dough.
Bake until dough and onion is browned, 15 to 20 minutes.

i: 4 oz. pkg. candied cherries, cut up
d: In mixing bowl, combine cherries, dates, raisins, pecans and 1 cup flour.
In large bowl, mix applesauce, margarine and molasses. Place in sifter 2 cups flour, sugar, soda, salt and spices.
Sift into applesauce mixture and mix well.
Add fruit and goodie mixture.
Pour into greased and floured tube pan.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour and 30 minutes.

i: 2 (3 oz.) pkg. Jell-O (mixed fruit or strawberry-banana)
d: Mix together cottage cheese, dry Jell-O and drained fruit. Fold in Cool Whip and all the pecans you can afford.
This can be made 1 or 2 days ahead and kept in the refrigerator.
Serves 6 to 8.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Massage creme de menthe into the flesh of the lamb.
Place in a roaster.
Sprinkle top with salt, pepper and mint.
In a small mixing bowl, beat olive oil, Worcestershire sauce, wine and mustard.
Pour into bottom of roaster around the meat.
In a 2 quart pyrex dish or pan, smooth cherry pie filling, then pineapple on top of that. Then sprinkle sugar. Smooth dry cake mix on top, then pour melted margarine, then chopped pecans.

Brown hamburger and stir in the seasoning. In large round casserole, layer meat, then cheese, then tortilla. Repeat until all is used. Put beans and sauce on top. Cover with more cheese. Bake in oven until bubbly and hot, for 20 to 30 minutes. Serve with sauce and sour cream.
Slice onions thin and soak for 2 to 4 hours in sugar, vinegar and water.
Drain well.
Toss with mayonnaise and celery salt. Serve on saltine crackers, hot dogs or hamburgers.
Place white rice in casserole dish.
Put in the can of beef broth and consomme.
Add salt, pepper and pats of margarine.
Mix together.
Put in oven at 350° for 1 hour.

t: Spinach, Red Pepper, And Feta Quiche
i: 1 1/2 Tbsp. ice water, plus additional if necessary
2 c. packed, fresh spinach leaves, trimmed and washed thoroughly (about 5 oz.)
d: Preheat oven to 425°.

i: 3 Tbsp. milk (may take a little more)
d: Mix Crisco or oleo, powdered sweetener, milk, almond, vanilla and eggs together and add flour, baking powder and nuts.
Make into logs and refrigerate overnight.
Slice and bake at 350° until light brown.
They do not brown like cookies with sugar.
Can also add coconut and substitute unsweetened orange juice for the milk.
Keep in refrigerator.
Combine all.
Roll in 7 1/2-inch log; roll in nuts.
Serve with crackers.
Fry rice, onions and pepper in small amount of shortening until it is brown, then add cabbage; cover it with water and add beef and salt to taste.
Add tomato paste.
Let simmer for about 30 to 35 minutes, then serve.
Combine all ingredients in order given and mix well.
Serve with lettuce or vegetable salad.
Combine eggs, honey, cilantro, cumin, lime juice, lime zest, cayenne pepper and salt; reserve.
Combine beans, rice and cheese; spread in pie shells.
Top with reserved egg mixture.
In large mixer bowl, combine cake mix and farina.
Add milk, eggs and oil.
Beat at medium speed of electric mixer for length of time given on cake mix package.
Stir in 3/4 cup nuts.
Divide batter evenly between two greased 9 x 9 x 2-inch baking pans. Bake in 350°
oven for 30 to 35 minutes or until cake tests done. Place on wire racks, not removing cakes from pans.

i: 3 lb. watermelon, rind and seeds discarded and the flesh cut into pieces, plus thin slices of the watermelon for garnish
d: In a blender, puree the watermelon pieces and strain the puree through a very fine sieve set over a pitcher, discarding the solids.
(There should be about 2 1/2 cups juice.)
Stir in the vodka, the Triple Sec and the lime juice; pour the cooler into long-stemmed glasses filled with ice and garnish each drink with a watermelon slice.
Makes 4 to 6 drinks.
Boil chicken in a pot of water.

Remove chicken when cooked, reserving broth. Cook green pepper and onion in a skillet sprayed with nonstick cooking spray. Add tomatoes with juice and cheese. Debone chicken and add to cheese mixture. Cook spaghetti in reserved chicken broth. Drain cooked spaghetti and mix with cheese and chicken.
Pour into a casserole dish.
Bake at 325° for 1 hour.

i: 2 (4 1/2 oz.) cans drained tiny, small or broken shrimp
d: Mix all ingredients together; serve with crackers.
Dissolve yeast in water.
Mix flour, sugar and corn meal together.
Add yeast and enough water to make a sticky dough.
Drop by teaspoons into hot lard (deep fat fry).
Fry until crispy brown on both sides.
Remove to a paper towel covered pan.
Have cheese and oleo at room temperature.
Mix well together with your hands, then add other ingredients.
Roll in 1-inch balls. Bake at 400° for 15 to 20 minutes on ungreased cookie sheet.
Cook bacon, hamburger, onion, green pepper and mushrooms until meat loses its color.
Add remaining ingredients.
Let simmer. Cook 1 pound spaghetti according to package directions.
Add to sauce and simmer for 30 minutes.
Serve with grated Parmesan cheese.
Serves 8.
Mix cheese, onion, pecans and mayonnaise together.
Mold mixture with a spoon or hands into a ring.
Fill the center with strawberry preserves. Serve with Ritz crackers.
Refrigerate.

i: spaghetti sauce from scratch or Ragu
d: Make spaghetti sauce; simmer.
Boil noodles.
Mix together in casserole.
Sprinkle Mozzarella cheese or layer.
Bake 30 minutes or until cheese is melted.

t: Fish In Garlic Sauce(Garlicky, But Good!)
i: 2 lb. red snapper, sea bass or other firm whitefish
d: Arrange fillets side by side in greased baking dish.
Mix 3 cloves garlic with 3 tablespoons olive oil and spread over fillets.
Squeeze juice of 1 orange over all and sprinkle with pepper.
Bake in hot oven, 450°, for about 20 minutes or until fish flakes easily.
Fry the rest of garlic in remaining olive oil.
Remove the garlic and fry parsley 3 minutes.

i: 2 c. whole kernel corn and chopped carrots
d: Combine cucumbers, onions, cabbage, garlic, green pepper and salt.
Cover with ice; let stand for 3 hours.
Drain.
Bring mustard seed, celery seed, sugar, vinegar and turmeric to boil; pour over the drained vegetables and return to a boil.
Add corn and chopped carrots (2 cups total); put into sterilized jars. Process in water bath for 20 minutes.
Makes 9 pints.
Combine all ingredients in an oiled baking dish.
Bake in moderate oven (350°), uncovered, for about 1 hour or until rice is soft.

i: 1/2 c. each sliced mushrooms and chopped onions
2 oz. each Swiss and Parmesan cheese
10 oz. each fresh or frozen deveined shrimp and scallops
d: In 10-inch nonstick skillet stir 1 tablespoon margarine until bubbles and hot; add broccoli, mushrooms and onion and cook stirring quickly and frequently until vegetables are tender crisp. Using a slotted spoon remove vegetables from skillet and set aside.
In same skillet heat remaining tablespoon margarine.
When bubbly and hot stir in flour stirring for about 1 minute. Gradually stir in milk and Tabasco at medium heat, stirring constantly, until mixture is smooth and thickened.
Add cheese and cook, stirring frequently until melted.
Return sauteed vegetables to skillet and add seafood and linguine and toss to combine. Reduce heat to low and cook until thoroughly heated.
Sprinkle with salt and pepper and stir to combine.

i: 1/3 c. lemon concentrate or juice of 2 lemons
d: Reserve the liquid from chickpeas.
Cover peas with water and boil 5 minutes.
Drain water.
Place garlic and lemon juice in blender.
Process until garlic is liquid.
Add tahini.
Heat 1 cup water.
Add sugar, stirring until sugar is dissolved. Transfer to blender and add rest of ingredients, blending until ice cubes are crushed.
Serve immediately.
Mix together all ingredients.
Pour into a greased and floured 9x13-inch pan.
Mix milk, 2 cups flour, baking powder and salt, then add enough flour to make soft dough.
Roll out and cut in strips.
Fry in deep, hot fat until golden brown.
Drain on paper towels. Sprinkle with confectioners sugar and eat while still hot (or can dip "sticks" into warm honey or syrup as you eat).
Grind vegetables coarsely.
Add salt and let set overnight. Rinse and drain.
Combine remaining ingredients and pour over vegetable mixture.
Heat to boiling and simmer for 3 minutes. Seal in hot, sterilized jars.
May substitute cucumbers for cabbage or tomatoes.
Makes 8 pints.

i: 2 slices bread made into buttered crumbs
d: In saucepan, cook onions in margarine. Stir in soup, milk and 3 slices of cheese.
Heat until cheese melts.
Add macaroni.
Pour into 1-quart casserole.
Top with remaining cheese and crumbs. Bake at 350°
for 30 minutes.
Cook rice according to directions, adding butter, bouillon cubes and cumin, but omitting salt.
Peel, seed and coarsely chop avocado.
Stir in rice with picante sauce and green onions.
Serve cold with additional picante sauce.

i: 4 flavors (such as oil of peppermint, cloves, cinnamon, wintergreen, etc.)
d: To make this candy, you will first need a large slab of marble on which to pour the hot candy.
Possibly a large cookie sheet would suffice, but I've always used marble.
You will also need several pairs of scissors and at least 3 additional people to help cut the candy before it sets up.
Saute ground turkey, onion and celery in oil in nonstick frypans sprayed with Pam.
Add cooked rice, spices, bouillon, V-8 and Worcestershire.
Mix well.
Spoon into pepper halves.
Garnish with a tomato slice and 1 teaspoon grated cheese.
Bake at 350°, covered, 1 hour.

i: 4 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves (about 1 1/2 lb.)
1 c. dry white wine or apple juice
d: Cut each chicken breast horizontally to make 2 thin slices. Coat with flour. Heat butter in 12-inch skillet over medium heat. Cook chicken and garlic in butter for 4 to 6 minutes, turning once until chicken is brown.
Add wine and lemon juice.
Sprinkle with pepper.
Heat until hot.
Sprinkle with capers.
Spread shallots on fillets.
Drizzle oil on fillets.
Put 3 slices tomato on each and 3 slices lemon on each fillet.
Place wine over fillets and sprinkle with parsley.
Heat oven to 475°. In greased pan, cook fillets uncovered for 6 minutes.

i: 1 lb. fresh, raw, peeled and cleaned shrimp
1 qt. fresh tomatoes, peeled and chopped or 2 (16 oz.) cans chopped tomatoes
d: Cook all ingredients, except spices and shrimp, in oil for 5 minutes.
Add 1 cup of water and cook down for 30 minutes.
Add remaining ingredients, except shrimp; cover and simmer for 30 to 40 minutes.
Add water, if needed.
Add shrimp and cook 20 minutes longer.
Serve over cooked long grain rice.
Heat oven to 400°.
Beat egg with fork.
Add remaining ingredients except blueberries and stir until flour is moistened. Batter should be lumpy.
Add blueberries, if desired.
Fill muffin cups 2/3 full.
Bake 20 to 25 minutes.
Cream shortening, add sugar and mix thoroughly.
Add egg and molasses; mix.
Add dry ingredients and mix thoroughly.
Chill dough.
Roll in small balls.
Roll in sugar.

i: 3 corn tortillas, cut in strips and fried
d: Cook chicken in 8 cups water 30 to 40 minutes.
Set aside; cool and debone.
Add tomatoes, bell pepper, onion and cilantro to chicken broth.
Bring to boil; reduce heat and simmer 20 to 30 minutes, then cool to room temperature.
Remove tomatoes, bell pepper, onion and cilantro from liquid and puree.
Return pureed mixture and broth to heat.

i: 1 1/2 lb. sirloin steak, cut into 1/8-inch thick strips
1 c. sliced green onions with tops
d: Sprinkle steak with paprika and set aside while preparing other ingredients.
Brown garlic in margarine and add steak, onions and green peppers.
Continue cooking until vegetables are wilted.
Add tomatoes and broth.
Cover and simmer on low heat about 15 minutes.
Blend water with cornstarch and soy sauce. Stir into steak.

i: 1 1/2 lb. ground beef, round or chuck
d: Melt the butter in a skillet and add the mushrooms. Cook, stirring, until mushrooms are golden. Add the meat, salt, pepper, curry powder, coriander and pepper flakes. Break up the meat with a wooden spoon and stir all ingredients together in the skillet. Cook for 10 minutes. Add the tomato puree. Simmer, covered, for 20 minutes. Add the peas. Cover again and simmer until peas are tender, 5 to 10 minutes. Serve with rice. Yields: Four to six servings.

i: 1 lb. bacon, fried and crumbled up
d: Chop the onions, tomatoes, green peppers and pickles and mix with the cornbread and bacon.
Add pickle juice and mayonnaise to taste for the dressing.

i: 1 (28 oz.) can whole tomatoes, chopped and undrained
d: Brown the beef in a skillet; crumble.
Drain off fat.
Add remaining ingredients except rice, stir to mix well; bring to a boil.
Add the instant rice; cover and remove from heat.
Let stand for 5 minutes.
Place meaty spareribs in a pan under your broiler until brown on both sides.
Transfer the ribs to a deeper pan and cover with barbecue sauce.
Cover with a tight lid and bake at 350° for 2 to 2 1/2 hours or until tender.

i: 1 (16 oz.) pkg. Italian bread shell or ready-to-serve pizza crust (12 to 14-inches in diameter)
1 small jalapeno chili, seeded and finely chopped
d: Gas grill. Sprinkle

cheese evenly over bread shell. Top with remaining ingredients. Cover and grill pizza 4 to 6-inches from medium heat 8 to 10 minutes or until crust is crisp and cheese is melted (if crust browns too quickly, place a piece of aluminum foil between crust and grill). Makes 4 servings.

i: 1 c. rice or 3 c. cold cooked rice
1 c. frozen peas, thawed and drained
d: Cook rice.
With fork, beat eggs and salt slightly and set aside.

i: 1 can cream of chicken or mushroom soup
d: Brown ground beef and drain well; mix sour cream and soup into ground beef.
Dip tortillas in hot water.
Put ground beef mixture on tortillas (not too much).
Sprinkle on cheese and roll tortillas.
Then place, seam side down, in a greased baking dish (9 x 13-inch).
Spread the remaining cheese over rolls.
Chop ice cream and sherbet in punch bowl.
Add juice.
Stir. Add ginger ale last.
Serves 25 people.
Dissolve gelatin in hot water.
Add sugar, lemon juice and pineapple juice. Stir to dissolve.
Chill until partially set. Add remaining ingredients.
Chill in oiled pan or in individual molds until firm.
Brown and drain the meat.
Do not drain any juice from the can.
Add everything to the pot.
Turn on high, then low to simmer for about 45 minutes.
Serve with shredded cheese and guacamole.
Brown ground chuck and drain.
Combine remaining ingredients and add to ground chuck.
Simmer until hot.

i: 22 medium sized ears of corn (10 cups kernels)
1 large red bell pepper, chopped (1 cup)
1 large green bell pepper, chopped (1 cup)
d: Cook ears in boiling water for 3 to 5 minutes.
Plunge into cold water and drain.
Cut kernels from cob.
Kernels should measure 10 cups.
Combine corn with remaining ingredients in large pot.
Simmer 15 minutes.
Combine all ingredients in jar with tight fitting lid; shake well.
Chill 3 hours.
Strain to remove onion and garlic. Rechill.
Makes 3 1/2 cups.

i: 2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen sliced strawberries, thawed and drained
d: Combine flour, salt, soda, cinnamon, and sugar. Make a well in center of dry ingredients. Add eggs and oil, stirring only until dry ingredients are moistened. Stir in strawberries and pecans. Spoon batter into 2 lightly greased 8 inch loaf pans. Bake at 350° for 1 hour or until bread tests done.

i: 1 small pkg. Jell-O instant chocolate pudding
1 can Betty Crocker milk chocolate frosting (to frost)
d: In one bowl, combine all ingredients except frosting.
Beat with mixer at high speed for 2 minutes.
Grease and flour medium size (9 cup) Bundt pan and pour in batter.
(This will give a high cake; larger pans will give a flatter cake.)
Bake at 350°.
Test at 45 minutes and then every 5 minutes until done.

i: 1 lb. fresh mushrooms, wash, trim off ends and slice lengthwise
d: Combine all ingredients; mix well.
Marinate at least 3 hours or overnight.
Cover the bottom of a 13 x 9-inch pan with coconut.
Sprinkle pecans over coconut.
Mix the cake as box directs and pour over coconut and pecans.
Mix cream cheese, melted butter and powdered sugar.
Mixture should be slightly thick (if more powdered sugar is needed, add gradually until you feel mixture is thick enough). Drop over cake and bake until done at 350°.
Cake will crack open. Frost with Coca-Cola Icing.

i: 2 1/2 c. peeled, cored and diced apples
d: Blend flour, baking powder, salt, sugar and shortening like pie crust.
Add milk and mix thoroughly until forms a soft dough. On a floured surface, pat dough, then roll out to a rectangular shape with 1/4-inch to 1/2-inch thickness.
Spread softened margarine over the dough.
Sprinkle cinnamon, sugar, apples, and more cinnamon and sugar.
Roll up like a jelly roll.
Cut in 2-inch slices and place in 9 x 13-inch pan (sprayed).

i: 1 lemon, washed and cut into 8 slices
d: Put cranberry juice and ginger ale into a large punch bowl. Add ice.
Decorate with lemon slices with a cherry placed in the center of each slice.

i: 32 oz. jar dry pinto beans, soaked overnight
d: Cook soaked beans several hours until tender.
Add ingredients and continue to simmer.
Make sure beans are always covered with enough water.

i: assorted fresh vegetables or potato chips
d: In a bowl, combine the first 8 ingredients; mix well. Refrigerate until serving. Yield: About 1 cup.
Mix together sugar and cocoa.
Stir in eggs.
Add milk, vanilla, melted margarine, nuts; mixing well.
Put in pie crust.

i: 1 large can tomato puree (1 lb. 15 oz.)
1 bag frozen, chopped onions (12 to 16 oz.)
1 (8 oz.) pkg. Neufchatel or cream cheese
d: Two days before using, make sauce:
Brown onions in bottom of a large Dutch oven.
Add meat and brown.
Stir occasionally to break it up well.
Add salt, pepper, lots of oregano, garlic powder, 3 cans of tomato paste and can of puree.
Add 1 teaspoon of sugar.
Mix all ingredients except the ketchup.
Put mixture in a large iron skillet.
Spoon ketchup over top.
Bake, uncovered, in a 350° oven for 1 hour and 15 minutes.

i: 2 c. fresh thyme leaves, stripped of twiggy part
d: Puree all ingredients in blender until creamy.
Marinade is best if made 24 hours ahead to marry flavors.
In a large mixing bowl, thoroughly combine applesauce, sugar, oil, eggs and milk.
Sift together flour, baking soda, baking powder, the first 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon, salt, nutmeg and allspice.
Stir in applesauce mixture; beat well.
Fold in the 1/2 cup pecans.
Turn into a well-greased loaf pan.

i: 8 oz. can tomatoes, drained and chopped
16 oz. can black or pinto beans, rinsed and drained
8 oz. can whole kernel corn, drained
d: Brown
ground beef with
minced
garlic.
Add

i: (Or in place of the above, 3 large cans of fruit pieces)
d: Drain fruit and arrange in 1 1/2 quart casserole.
Melt butter, sugar and curry together in microwave.
Spoon over fruit. Bake at 325 degrees for 1 hour.
Mix all ingredients, except nuts, together with mixer until smooth.
Add nuts.
Pour into greased loaf pan.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes, or until done.

i: 2 pkg. (6) skinless and boneless chicken thighs or 4 to 6 skinless and boneless chicken breasts
d: Mix plain yogurt (small cup) with garlic powder to taste.
Cut several chicken breasts into cubes.
Dip in yogurt mix and in wheat germ or oat bran that has poultry seasoning and 1 teaspoon salt. Bake on a cookie sheet at 425° for 25 minutes.
Dip in honey mustard or barbecue sauce or yogurt with curry powder and sugar-onion powder.
Mix sausage, rice and salt.
Make into patties or small balls and brown. Place in oven dish and pour over these the tomatoes and the chili powder.
Cook in moderate oven until the rice is done. This makes a delicious dish.
Bake at 300° for 15 to 20 minutes.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Layer apples in the bottom 8 by 8 inch square of baking dish.
Combine water, cinnamon and salt, sprinkle mixture over apples.
Work together the sugar, flour and butter until crumbly. Spread crumb mixture over apples.
Bake uncovered about 40 minutes.
Serve warm.
Place pork chops in a large food storage bag and pour salad dressing into it.
Refrigerate for 1 to 2 hours.
Spread bread crumbs on a plate and generously coat each pork chop.
Place chops in a glass cooking dish, cover with foil and cook at 375° for 40 minutes.
Remove foil and cook for additional 10 minutes.
Serve with angel hair pasta with butter and Parmesan cheese.

i: 4 small (6 oz.) or 2 large (12 oz.) pkg. chocolate chips
d: Mix first 7 ingredients in large bowl and freeze.
Roll into balls or small thin logs.
Stick toothpicks into balls and freeze.
Melt oleo in pan.
Mix together in a large bowl all dry ingredients.
Pour mixture into hot pan where oleo has been melted.
Lay peaches very carefully on top.
Measure 1 cup of the peach juice and pour over the peaches.
Bake at 350° for 35 to 40 minutes.

i: 6 skinless, boneless chicken breast halves, pounded to 1/4-inch thickness
1 tsp. cornstarch, dissolved in 1 Tbsp. water
d: Place chicken in a large shallow baking dish.
Combine lemon juice, mustard, garlic and pepper in a small bowl.
Beat in oil. Pour over chicken. Marinate 1 hour at room temperature.

i: 1 container guacamole, thinned with lemon juice or 2 ripe avocados, mashed, with lemon juice
d: Mix sour cream with taco seasoning mix.
On round plate, layer the following: refried beans, guacamole, sour cream with seasoning mix, grated cheese and chopped tomato.
Chill and serve with chips.
Heat electric wok or skillet at 325° for 2 to 3 minutes; add oil and heat 1 minute.
Add celery, onion and broccoli; stir-fry 2 minutes.
Add cabbages and stir-fry 3 minutes.
Cover and cook vegetables over low heat 3 minutes.
Stir in salt, pepper and Worcestershire sauce.
Serve immediately.
Place alternate layers of cabbage and onions in a large bowl. Cover with sugar and celery seed.
Boil together vinegar, salt and mustard.
Add oil; heat again to boiling.
Pour over cabbage and onions.
Do not mix.
Refrigerate at least 4 hours.
In a saucepan, cook raisins, water and shortening for 5 minutes over medium heat.
In a large bowl, combine remaining ingredients and mix with the raisins.
Pour in a 9 x 13-inch pan and bake at 350° for about 35 minutes. This makes a good moist cake that keeps well.
Mix Jell-O and waters.
Add remaining ingredients.
Pour in 9 x 13-inch pan.
Refrigerate until firm.
Combine the first 5 ingredients and bring to a boil.
Boil 1 minute and remove from heat.
Add the peanut butter and stir to dissolve.
Add oats and vanilla.
Stir well.
Drop by teaspoon onto waxed paper.
With large spoon, beat eggs.
Add sugar, syrup and margarine, beating each time an ingredient is added.
Stir in pecans.
Pour into pie shells and bake at 300° for 1 hour.
Wash chicken; pat dry and remove skin (if you want).
Melt butter in shallow baking pan; stir in remaining ingredients.
Roll chicken pieces in mixture to coat all over.
Arrange meaty side up in a single layer.
Bake at 375° for 1 hour or until chicken is tender and richly glazed.
Mix together.
Chill and serve in 2 hours.

i: 1 (8 1/2 oz.) can crushed pineapple and juice
d: Take 1 large ball field, half a dozen children, 2 or 3 small dogs, a pinch of brook and some pebbles.

Mix the children and dogs well together; put them on the field, stirring constantly. Pour the brook over the pebbles, sprinkle the field with flowers, spread over all a deep blue sky and bake in the sun.
When brown, set away to cool in the bath tub.
Place the pita bread in the oven at 350° to crisp. Cut into bite-size pieces.
Place in a large salad bowl.
Place half the noodles in a greased baking dish.
Add half the chicken and mushrooms.
Repeat layer.
Add salt, pepper and margarine to hot broth. Pour over chicken.
Cover with bread crumbs and bake uncovered at 350° for about 20 minutes or until brown.
In a 3-quart rectangular baking dish, combine honey, water, mustard, margarine, Cajun seasoning, curry powder, lemon juice and garlic; mix well.
Add chicken breast halves, turning to coat. Arrange in a single layer.
Bake, uncovered, in a 350° oven for 30 minutes or until chicken is tender and no longer pink.
Serve chicken and pan drippings with hot cooked rice.
Each serving contains 438 calories and 2 grams saturated fat.
Mix ground beef, bread crumbs, egg and poultry seasoning. Shape into about 2 dozen 1 1/2-inch balls. Brown balls slowly in a lightly oiled skillet over medium-high heat. Pour off excess fat.

i: ground turkey or extra lean ground beef
d: Brown and drain meat.
Add spaghetti sauce, onions and green pepper.
Add enough spaghetti sauce to make thinner than usual. Cook slowly until well blended.
Combine grated Mozzarella cheese with cottage cheese and Egg Beaters.
In a 9 x 13-inch pan spread a thin layer of the meat mixture, then the uncooked lasagna noodles.
Top with meat mixture and a layer of cheese mixture. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.

i: 1/2 k. pork, cut into 1 to 2-inch pieces
d: In a saucepan, combine vinegar, garlic, pepper, bay leaf, rock salt and soy sauce.
Put in chicken and pork.
Let soak 20 to 30 minutes.
Add water and simmer uncovered until tender.
Strain sauce.
Set aside.

i: 1 (15 oz.) can white cannellini or Great Northern beans, drained
1/4 tsp. each: thyme, basil and oregano
d: Cut sausage into 1/2-inch pieces; brown in large heavy saucepan over medium heat.
Drain off all but 1 tablespoon fat. Stir in onions; cook 5 to 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until soft, but not browned.
Stir in garlic and celery; cook until soft, 5 to 8 minutes.
Stir in cabbage, tomatoes, beans, chicken broth, thyme, basil, oregano and bay leaf.
Cover and bring to a boil.
Simmer 30 minutes.
Mix sugar, milk and butter and cook until it forms a firm ball in cold water.
Fold in the dates and bring to a boil.
Remove from heat and beat thoroughly.
Add vanilla and nuts and stir. Pour onto a damp tea towel and roll lengthwise.
Let harden.
Cut in 1/4 inch slices.

i: 1 pt. (16 oz.) yellow and/or red grape tomatoes
d: Prepare couscous according to package directions.
Fluff with a fork.
Halve tomatoes and dice cucumber.
In large bowl, toss together couscous, halved tomatoes, diced cucumber, chopped mint and citrus vinaigrette.
Season with salt, pepper, extra lemon juice and hot pepper sauce to taste, if desired.
Chill.
Cream butter; add sugar and blend well.
Add flour and mix thoroughly.
Blend in pecans and vanilla.
Chill dough.
Shape 1 teaspoon dough into small balls and bake on lightly greased cookie sheets at 300° for 20 minutes.
Roll in powdered sugar while hot.

t: Orange Wild Rice With Raisins And Apples
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
In mixing bowl, combine cooked rices. Gently toss together raisins, almonds, parsley, zest, apple, oil, orange juice, salt and pepper with combined rices.
Place mixture in casserole.
Cover with foil; heat thoroughly for 20 minutes.

i: 4 pork chops (1 inch thick) or 4 tenderloins
d: Brown pork chops.
Remove from pan.
Add onions and cook until soft.
Return chops to pan with remaining ingredients.
Salt and pepper to taste.
Cover.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Beat egg whites until very stiff; add sugar gradually and continue to beat on high.
Add vanilla.
Fold in nuts and chocolate chips with a spatula.
Drop 1/2 teaspoon on cookie sheet covered with a brown paper bag.
Shut off oven and close; open in 8 to 10 hours.

i: 1 avocado, peeled and cut into wedges
d: Drain pineapple, reserving juice.
Set aside.
Combine juice, sugar, cornstarch and lemon juice in heavy saucepan.
Cook until thickened over low flame, stirring constantly.
Cool.
Combine pineapple and remaining ingredients, except avocado.

t: Corn Bread Or Mexican Corn Bread
d: Mix the dry ingredients.
Beat eggs. Add milk and stir into dry mixture.
Add the Crisco and mix thoroughly.
Pour into "hot" cast-iron skillet or corn bread stick pan with about 1 tablespoon oil. Bake in preheated oven at 450° or until golden brown.
Mix mustard and jelly.
Place in double boiler.
Diagonally slice franks 1/2-inch thick.
Add to sauce and cook.
Refrigerate or freeze.
When ready to serve, place in chafing dish and heat. Serve with cocktail picks.

i: 3 tomatoes, cut up and seeds removed
d: Spread the first 3 ingredients on pizza plate.
Add remaining ingredients.
Use Ritz crackers to dip.
Brown flour in Wesson oil.
Add salt and pepper.
Brown real good, then add water and tomatoes.
Cook until thick.
Real good.

i: 1/2 c. olive oil or vegetable oil
1 Tbsp. chopped fresh herb (tarragon, rosemary, basil, oregano, thyme or marjoram)
d: Shake all ingredients in tightly covered container.
About 1 cup dressing; 60 calories per tablespoon.

i: 3 Tbsp. hot or mild tomato salsa
d: Preheat oven to 400°.
Over medium heat, cook sausage, mushrooms and onion.
Drain well.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Combine eggs and sour cream in blender.
Pour into greased 9 x 12-inch baking dish (greased).
Sift together flour, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, salt, allspice and cloves; set aside.
Combine oil and sugar. Beat until well blended.
Beginning and ending with dry ingredients, add dry ingredients alternately with applesauce to sugar mixture.
Mix well after each addition.
Fold in nuts, raisins and coconut.
Combine brown sugar, butter and water in 1-quart casserole. Microwave on Roast for 2 minutes or until butter is melted.
Stir in nuts.
Separate biscuits, cut into quarters, add biscuits to sugar mixture, stir and coat each piece.
Drain pineapple reserving juice.
Dissolve gelatin in boiling water; add reserved juice.
Cool until slightly thickened; beat until frothy.
Fold in pineapple, cottage cheese, mayonnaise and nuts.
Place in 13 x 9-inch pan or mold.
Refrigerate until firm. Yield:
Mix sugar, water, butter and salt.
Come to a boil without stirring.
When mixture has reached the rolling ball stage, drop in pecans and continue to cook until when a few drops are dropped into a cup of cold water and a hard balls is formed.
Remove from stove and beat until slightly cool.
Drop on wax paper.

i: 3 fresh tomatoes, peeled, seeded and diced
1 Tbsp. olive oil (not cooking or vegetable oil)
d: Mix all together; it is awesome!

i: 1 (28 oz.) can whole tomatoes, coarsely chopped
d: Brown ground beef in oil in a large skillet, stirring to crumble.
Drain off pan dripping.
Stir in tomatoes, tomato paste and seasonings into meat.
Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer 40 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Make 4 cheese sandwiches (with 2 slices of American cheese in each sandwich).
Remove crusts and arrange in baking dish.
Mix eggs, milk and dry mustard and pour over sandwiches.
Bake in 400° oven for 20 minutes or until brown on top.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Brown ground beef and onion; pour off fat.
Blend in pizza sauce, garlic salt and oregano.
Put mixture in greased 9 x 13-inch pan and sprinkle with Mozzarella cheese. In small bowl, mix eggs, milk, oil, salt and flour.
Pour over meat mixture and sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
Bake for 30 minutes.

Combine all ingredients and bring to soft-ball stage,* stirring constantly. Remove from heat. Stir until mixture thickens, becomes creamy and cloudy and pecans stay suspended in mixture. Spoon out on buttered wax paper. When using wax paper, be sure to place newspaper underneath as hot wax will transfer to whatever is beneath.
Combine all ingredients; stir well.
Chill 3 hours.
Serve with fresh vegetables.
Yield: 2 cups.
Toast sesame seed under broiler until brown.
Cream butter and brown sugar.
Add egg and vanilla; mix well.
Sift flour with spices; add to creamed mixture and blend.
Stir in nuts.
Spread 1/4 inch thick in 13 x 15-inch greased pan.
Preheat oven to 250°.
Place apples in a single layer in a large, flat baking pan. Bake 20 minutes.
Remove from oven, and stir in popcorn and pecans. Mix remaining ingredients in a cup and drizzle over apples, popcorn, and pecans. Return to oven, and bake for 30 minutes, stirring every 10 minutes.

i: 1 c. sugar and 1 Tbsp. flour, mixed
d: Grease sides of 9 x 13-inch cake pan.
Put in fruit.
Pour in water.
Mix sugar and flour; sprinkle evenly over fruit. Sprinkle dry cake mix over this.
Add margarine patties over cake mix.
Sprinkle chopped nuts over the top.
Spread bean dip over plate.
Mix taco mix and salad dressing together.
Spread over bean dip.
Put shredded lettuce on top, then put tomatoes.
Sprinkle shredded cheese over the top.
Serve the dip with chips.

i: 1 (3 to 3 1/2 lb.) chicken, cut into eighths
d: Heat oil in a large skillet. Season the chicken with salt and pepper.

Brown on both sides in the heated oil. Remove chicken from pan and set aside. Add green pepper, onion and garlic to the skillet and cook, stirring frequently, until softened, about 5 minutes. Return chicken to pan. Cover and cook on medium-low for about 45 to 50 minutes, stirring often. Gently stir in frozen peas and cook for another 5 minutes. Serve with potatoes, rice or noodles. Makes 4 servings.
Mix all together and slowly whisk into dry ingredients.
Place mixture over medium-high heat and cook, stirring constantly until mixture thickens and loses its wet look.
Pour out on countertop or board and knead until of even consistency.
Bake potatoes.
Let cool.
Peel and cube.
In large skillet, fry bacon.
Remove bacon and allow to cool, then crumble.
Saute onions and peppers in bacon drippings.
Preheat oven to 325°.
Cook eggplant until mushy; drain.
Add all ingredients, reserving 1/2 cup cheese, 1/2 cup crumbs and small amount of butter.
Mix well.
Pour into baking dish; sprinkle with reserved cheese, cracker crumbs and melted butter. Bake at 325° for 25 to 30 minutes.
Combine cabbage, onion, green pepper and sugar.
In saucepan, combine 1 tablespoon sugar, salt, celery seed, dry mustard, water, vinegar and oil.
Bring to a boil; pour over vegetables.
Cool in refrigerator for 24 hours.
Keeps for 3 weeks.

i: 2 lb. green beans, drained and washed if canned and cooked until barely done if fresh
d: Mix all ingredients; marinate overnight.
Serves 8.
Mix ingredients in the order written.
Refrigerate.

i: 2 c. (5 1/2 oz.) tricolor rotini, cooked and drained
parsley, dill weed, seasoned salt in dressing
d: Mix pasta and vegetables in large bowl.
In separate bowl, mix dressing, parsley, dill and seasoned salt to taste.
Pour over vegetables and mix well.
Refrigerate several hours before serving.
Serves 8.
Combine flour, nuts and melted margarine and mix well.
Press into bottom of 11 3/4 x 7 1/2-inch baking dish.
Bake at 350° for 15 minutes.
Cool.
Combine softened cream cheese and powdered sugar and mix well until blended.
Fold in whipped topping. Spread over crust.

i: 1 small jar red or green cherries, drained
d: Preheat oven to 350°. Combine cake mix, pudding mix, club soda, eggs and oil, beat with mixer. Stir in cherries, pineapple and nuts with spoon. Pour into 3 cake pans (9 inch) and bake at 350° for 20 minutes.
Combine 1/2 cup cheese and biscuit mix in a mixing bowl.
Add milk; stir just until flour is moistened.
Pat dough over bottom of a greased 8 or 9-inch pie plate.
Combine remaining cheese, egg and onion.
Spread over biscuit dough.
Sprinkle with poppy seed. Bake at 425° for 15 to 20 minutes.

i: 1 lb. Velveeta cheese, cut in cubes
d: Brown meat and drain.
Stir in cheese. Then add tomatoes. Serve with tortilla chips.

i: 1 1/2 lb. boneless lamb, cut into 1-inch cubes
d: Soak beans, drain and rinse.
Add 6 cups fresh water; cover and simmer about 1 hour or until nearly tender.
Drain.
In Dutch oven or heavy kettle, saute onion in olive oil.
Add lamb and brown on all sides.
Add beans, 1 1/4 cups water and seasonings. Simmer, covered, for 45 minutes.
Peel and slice cucumbers and let stand overnight in brine made with 1/2 cup salt to 3 quarts water.
Slice thin or chop fine the onions.
Mix sugar, spices, flour and vinegar.
Add cucumbers, onions and pimentos.
Cook 10 minutes.
Pack in jars while hot and seal at once.
Saute garlic and onion in olive oil until translucent.
Add salt, pepper, basil, oregano, tarragon, rosemary, carrots and wine.
Cover and cook 5 to 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add green pepper, stock, puree and beans.
Cover and simmer 30 minutes.
Add chopped tomatoes, cooked pasta and zucchini and simmer for another 15 minutes.

t: Southern Nut Torte With Ice Cream
d: Combine vanilla, sugar, Ritz crackers, eggs and 1 cup crushed nuts.
Pour into a well-greased (Pam okay) pie plate and bake at 350° for 25 minutes.
Top will be crisp and dry.

t: Pumpkin Shell Casserole(Serves  8 To 10, Depending On Size Of Pumpkin)
i: lemon juice, cinnamon and nutmeg to taste
d: Wash and dry pumpkin; slice off top and reserve for top. Scrape out seeds.
Mix remainder of ingredients and fill pumpkin. Place lid on pumpkin; place on a cookie sheet.
Bake at 350° until apples are tender, checking for doneness at 40 minutes, although it may take up to 1 hour and 45 minutes, depending on size of the pumpkin.
Casserole may be served hot or cold with roast pork, ham or poultry.
Serve from the shell, spooning some pumpkin with each portion.
Top with sour cream if desired.
