Combine cooked rice, tuna, celery, green pepper, lemon juice and onion in a mixing bowl.
Blend the seasonings with the sour cream.
Stir into the tuna/rice mixture.
Chill for 1 hour. Garnish with tomatoes and serve with corn chips.
Brown onion, garlic, pepper and beef in oil.
Add remaining ingredients.
Simmer over low heat for 45 minutes.

i: 2 (8 oz.) cans crushed pineapple with juice
2 (6 oz.) pkg. shredded or flaked coconut
2 (8 oz.) c. frozen whipped topping, thawed
d: Prepare cake mix by package directions.
Bake into layers. Cool layers.
Prepare 1 box pudding mix.
Place 1 cake layer (cut into cubes) in bottom of punch bowl.
Spoon the prepared pudding mix evenly over cake.
Top with half of fruit cocktail, 1 can pineapple, 1/2 of fruit cocktail, 1/2 of cherry pie filling, 1 cup nuts and 1 package coconut.
Raita is a yogurt dish served mainly with rice dishes.
Grate or dice the cucumber.
Combine everything.
Mix well and serve chilled.
May add diced tomatoes and onions.

i: 1 small can light red kidney beans
1 small bottle Heinz hot ketchup or 1 small jar salsa
1 c. grated Cheddar cheese (1/4 c. reserved)
d: Mix all ingredients.
Bake at 325° for 30 minutes.
Sprinkle 1/4 cup cheese on top.
This is too thick to use as a dip.
It is best to serve in a bowl over crumbled tortilla chips or Doritos after baking.
Mix Cool Whip and Eagle Brand milk.
Sprinkle dry Jell-O over and mix well.
Add peaches, then lemon juice.
Mix well.
Pour into graham cracker crust.
Chill.

i: 1 1/2 c. shreds of wheat bran cereal
d: Heat oven to 400°.
Grease bottoms only of 12 medium 2 1/2 x 1 1/4-inch muffin cups.

i: 1 1/3 Tbsp. each: chopped pepper, onion, stuffed olives and sweet pickles
d: Mix all ingredients except buns.
Fill buns and wrap buns together with foil.
Bake at 250° for 30 minutes or until cheese melts.
In a buttered 2-quart casserole mix tomatoes and 2 cups stuffing together.
Stir in onion, sugar and seasonings.
Dot with oleo.
Sprinkle remaining stuffing on top.
Bake at 375° for 45 minutes.
Mix all dry ingredients together.
Add onion, then milk and last the beaten egg.

Drop by small tablespoons into hot deep fat. They will sink toward bottom of fat and when done on one side, the almost perfectly formed balls will pop to the top and continue to brown on the other side.
Drain on paper towels and serve warm.
Combine all ingredients.
Bake at 350° until firm.

i: 6 oz. pkg. semi-sweet chocolate chips (1 c.)
1 c. cut up pecans or another kind of nut
d: Mix the sugar, milk and corn syrup in heavy 2-quart saucepan. Cook, stirring constantly, over medium heat to a boil (mixture will be bubbling all over top). Boil and stir another 2 minutes more. Take off heat. Stir in the chocolate chips until completely melted. Cool about 15 minutes.
Stir in the pecans and marshmallows. Drop by 2 teaspoons onto wax paper.
Chill until firm or put in a buttered pan and cut into pieces.
Brown onions lightly in shortening.
Add meat.
Break up particles so meat mixture is very fine.
When brown, drain and add peppers, tomato juice and water.
Add rice. Season to taste, stirring well.
Bring to a boil.
Dissolve jello in hot water.
Add ginger ale and chill. When slightly
thickened,
add
fruits and nuts.
Chill until firm.
Dissolve yeast in warm water. Add rest of ingredients and stir until smooth. Fill greased muffin tin 2/3 cup full. Bake at 350 degrees for 20 minutes.
Dissolve salt in the 2 quarts of water in enameled or stainless steel pan.
Add squash and onions and soak 12 to 18 hours.
Drain for an hour but do not wash or rinse.
Combine remaining ingredients in a kettle or pan and boil for 5 minutes. Put in hot jars and seal.
Makes 5 pints.
Mix Jell-O in boiling water.
Add strawberries, pineapple, crushed bananas and nuts.
Spread 1/2 mixture in 13 x 6 1/2-inch pan.
Allow to gel in freezer 30 minutes.
Add layer of sour cream, then remaining mixture on top.
Gel and serve.
Brown hamburger and onion in skillet.
Drain grease.
In a greased casserole, layer hamburger, potatoes, rice, celery, tomatoes, kidney beans and Cheddar cheese.
Make at least 2 layers, then pour water over top.
Bake at 350° for 1 1/2 hours.

i: 1 lb. bacon, crisply fried and broken up
d: Crisply fry 1 pound of bacon.
Drain, break up and set aside. Break up lettuce in bottom of very large bowl.
Cut up cauliflower and layer on top of lettuce.
Sprinkle onion over top if you desire.
Pour on mayo and seal to edges of bowl.
Sprinkle sugar on top of mayo and then shake cheese over top of the sugar.

i: 1 medium size can green chili salsa
d: Bake the chicken ahead of time so skin and bones can be removed.
Cut chicken into cubes.
Cut about 7 tortillas into strips.
Make gravy of the first 6 ingredients.

i: 3 lb. medium-size red potatoes (unpeeled and sliced)
d: Combine first 5 ingredients; toss to coat well.
Layer half of mixture in a lightly greased 12 x 8 x 2-inch dish.
Sprinkle with half of parsley.
Layer remaining mixture.
Cover and bake at 350° for 45 minutes or until tender.
Uncover; sprinkle with remaining parsley.

i: 1 (5 oz.) can water chestnuts, drained and thinly sliced
d: In large skillet, cook and stir onion and green pepper in oil until onion is tender.
Stir in rice, water chestnuts, mushrooms and soy sauce.
Cook over low heat 10 minutes, stirring frequently.
Stir in beaten eggs.
Cook and stir 2 to 3 minutes longer.
Makes 4 to 5 servings (about 1/2 cup each).
Drain liquid from olives. Mince garlic and add to olives. Fill to top with olive oil. Marinate in refrigerator for a couple of days or until ready to use.
Put squash, onion and peppers through food grinder (coarse blade).
Add salt and enough water to cover; let stand for 1 hour. Then rinse good under cold water and drain well.
Boil sugar, vinegar, water and spices for 3 minutes; then add vegetables and cook after it boils another 15 minutes.
Stir often.
Pack in hot sterilized jars and seal.
To seal boil jar lids for 10 minutes and top jars with rings.
Drizzle jello in boiling water.
Add juice.
Chill until consistency of unbeaten egg whites.
Add coconut, carrots, pineapple and pecans.
Chill in refrigerator.
Combine zucchini, onion and salt; let set overnight.
Drain and rinse well.
Place in large pot along with remaining ingredients.
Cook until mixture thickens, stirring constantly. Do not overcook.
Pack hot into hot sterilized jars, leaving 1 inch space.
Adjust caps.

i: 32 oz. can tomato sauce or spaghetti sauce
d: Mix sauce together:
meat, oregano, pepper, parsley, onion, garlic, pepperoni, tomato sauce, egg and mushrooms.
(Best when making mix 1 day before eating.)
Put uncooked noodles into a greased 9 x 13-inch pan, then add sauce mix, then cheese.
Repeat layers.
Cook at 350° for 30 minutes.

i: 9 oz. thin spaghetti or vermicelli, broken, cooked and drained
2 (10 oz.) frozen chopped spinach, thawed and squeezed dry
d: Saute butter, mushrooms and onions.
Add to rest of ingredients and gently mix together.
Bake in a 9 x 13-inch greased casserole at 350° for 45 minutes.

i: 2 cooked chicken breasts, cut into strips
d: Place all ingredients into saucepan, except spaghetti.
Heat and simmer about 10 minutes.
Stir thoroughly.
Boil spaghetti and drain.
Serve on top of spaghetti.
Cream oleo and sugar.
Add eggs, one at a time.
Add vanilla wafers.
Add coconut, vanilla and pecans.
Bake at 325° in tube pan for
1 1/2 hours (grease and flour pan).
Mix well.
Spoon batter into greased muffin pans.
Cook 25 minutes at 275°.

i: 1 Can (11 ounces) condensed Cheddar Cheese Soup
1 Cup Frozen Peas and Carrots, if Desired
d: Heat oven to 400.
Grease rectangular baking dish, 13x9x2 inches.
Cook beef and onion in 10 inch skillet over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until beef is brown.
Drain.
Stir in soup, vegetables and milk.
Remove from heat.
Melt margarine.
Add eggs and sugar to margarine and beat. Add buttermilk and mix thoroughly.
Add vanilla and coconut. Mix thoroughly.
Pour into 2 deep dish pie shells.
Bake at 300° for 1 hour.

i: 5 (15 1/2 to 16 oz.) cans pork and beans
d: Brown ground beef. Drain.
Add beans and remaining ingredients. Mix well.
Bake at 370° for about 1 hour or until nice and bubbly, stirring some.
Cut the bottom part of the eggplant with stalk to discard it. Wash the eggplant and boil it in water by cooking in microwave at High energy for 9 minutes.
Drain the water by a colander.
Peel the eggplant and mash it.
Cut the tomato into small pieces, onion and the clove of garlic should be minced and kept ready.

i: 2 (16 oz.) cans Shoe Peg white corn
1/4 c. plain yogurt or substitute 1/2 c. mayonnaise
d: Slice green onions and their tops.
Stir together with corn, tomato, mayonnaise, yogurt, salt and pepper in a large bowl. Cover and refrigerate for 4 to 24 hours.
Makes 8 to 10 servings.
Broil eggplant until soft, turning every 5 minutes.
Peel and smash inner part.
Add garlic, salt and olive oil.
Mix well. Spread out.
Sprinkle tomatoes and Feta on top.
(Goes great with bread.)

i: 2 1/2 c. leftover coffee or prepared instant
d: You will need 3 coffee cans.
Heat oil and saut the onion.
Add the chicken, tomatoes and taco seasoning.
Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
Layer 4 corn tortillas on the bottom of a 9 x 13-inch glass pan.
Layer 1/3 chicken mixture, olives and cheese.

i: 1 each diced bell peppers (green and red)
d: Fry onions, bacon and mushrooms together, turning frequently until bacon is sufficiently cooked (not crispy).
In a separate frypan, heat vegetable oil on medium-high temperature for 2 to 3 minutes.
Season sliced potatoes with paprika, pepper and salt as listed in large bowl.
Reduce temperature to low-medium; add sliced potatoes slowly to hot oil; turn potatoes frequently.
Fry until light brown color is achieved.
Combine all ingredients in one frypan and cook an extra 10 to 15 minutes and serve.
Mix ingredients all together well.
Pour over raw roast. Cover with foil.
Bake at 350° for 3 to 4 hours.
Remove from roaster and shred with fork.
Enjoy!

i: 2 Tbsp. each: brown sugar, vinegar and Worcestershire sauce
d: Mix together the beef, salt, pepper, bread crumbs and 3/4 cup water.
Shape into balls, brown and pour off the fat.
Add all but beans.
Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add beans and bake 25 minutes at 350°.

i: 2 c. small curd cottage cheese, drained
d: Brown ground beef; drain.
Add cumin, chili powder, garlic powder, salt, peppers and tomatoes.
Heat through.
Cover bottom and sides of 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish with tortillas.
Pour beef mixture over tortillas.
Place a layer of tortillas over meat mixture and set aside.
Combine all ingredients except Parmesan cheese.
Mix thoroughly.
Pack lightly into an 8-inch square baking pan.
Bake at 350° for 20 minutes. Sprinkle top with cheese.
Bake 10 minutes longer.
Let stand for 5 minutes. Cut into squares.
Cook ground beef, onions and drain off grease.
Add tomato juice, tomato paste, kidney beans, salt, pepper, paprika and chili powder.
Simmer in pot for several hours.

i: 2 tomatoes, diced and very well drained
d: On large round cookie tray, layer in order listed.
Should be made a day ahead.

i: 1 c. Cheddar cheese, grated, or Velveeta
d: Fry sausage; drain and crumble into a greased 9 x 13-inch casserole dish. Sprinkle cheese over sausage. Mix well the remaining ingredients; pour over sausage mixture. Bake at 400° for 25 to 30 minutes, until set and browned on top. Cool 5 minutes before slicing.
Grind the first 7 ingredients.
Add 1/2 cup salt and a little salt.
Let stand 2 hours.
Drain.
Add the vinegar; cook for 20 minutes.
Add the remaining ingredients; mix.

i: 1 chicken, cooked, deboned and sliced or chopped
d: In double boiler, melt butter with bouillon cubes.
Mix in shallots, cream, mushrooms, pepper and Parmesan cheese.
Simmer until mushrooms are soft and cheese is melted.
Add cooked chicken; heat until warm.
Pour sauce over noodles, tossing gently. Garnish with additional Parmesan cheese and parsley.
Soak beans in cold water overnight.
Drain, wash and cover with about 2 inches of water.
Add salt and boil over moderate heat for about 1 hour, adding water if needed.
Add diced onion, diced garlic, pepper, cumin seed, chopped chilies, taco sauce and tomatoes.
Cook over medium heat for an hour or longer, until beans are tender.
For spicier beans, add 1 or 2 teaspoons of red chili powder.

i: 1 corned ham or 15 to 25 lb. Mashes low-salt ham
d: Chop vegetables (cabbage, kale and onions); mix with other ingredients.
Cut slits in ham.
Stuff ingredients in slits and if any are left over, put on top of ham.
Wrap ham in heavy-duty aluminum foil.
Put in a roasting pan; add 2 small saucepans of water.
Cook in oven at 350° for 4 1/2 to 5 hours.

i: 1 large can crushed pineapple, not drained
d: Mix jello with hot water and sugar. Allow to come to a boil. Take off stove and add peaches and pineapple.
Wisk ingredients together until mixed well and chill.
Fry and drain bacon.
Mix the chopped onions, bacon and A.1. sauce into the green beans.
Heat 10 minutes.

i: 1 1/2 tsp. pumpkin pie spice (stir into flour)
d: Cream sugar and eggs until thick and lemon colored.
Slowly add oil while beating at medium speed.
Add flour slowly, scraping down sides of bowl.
As you beat, add vanilla.
Stir in by hand nuts and carrots.
Pour batter into 3 greased and floured 8-inch pans.

i: 1 1/2 c. cooked shrimp, hen or chicken
d: Fry bacon. Remove from fat. Add onions and green pepper. Fry until slightly browned. Pour in soy sauce. Stir. Combine rice and meat. Add to onion mixture. Salt and pepper to taste. Heat. Add unbeaten egg and stir until blended well with rice. Crumble bacon over top.
Trim excess fat from roast.
In Dutch oven or large skillet, slowly brown meat on all sides in hot shortening.
Add next 7 ingredients.
Cover.
Cook slowly 2 1/2 hours or until meat is tender.
Place biscuits on meat. Sprinkle with parsley.
Mix all ingredients together and let set in refrigerator 3/4 hour.
With a large serving spoon, make mini meat loaves in a 10 x 12-inch pan.
Bake at 325° for 45 minutes.
Beat egg yolks until light; add milk and beat well.
Fold in lime juice and rind.
Beat egg whites until stiff; fold into lime filling.
Pour into graham cracker or chocolate crust and freeze. Sprinkle crust crumbs on top or make meringue or eliminate meringue and freeze.
Brown meat with onions and peppers and drain.
Add sauce and simmer.
Cook noodles and drain.
Layer meat, noodles, mushrooms, cheese and pepperoni in crock pot.
Cook on low for 90 minutes.
Sift together flour, soda, baking powder and salt.
Sift again. Cream butter with sugar until light and fluffy. Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition.
Blend in vanilla. Add flour mixture and buttermilk alternately and beat until smooth after each addition.
Pour into greased and floured wax papered loaf pan. Bake in moderate oven at 350° for about 1 hour and 10 minutes or until cake tester comes out clean.
Remove from pan and cool. Dust with powdered sugar.
Combine all of the ingredients in a large pan and cover with water.
Bring to a boil.
Cook for one hour.
Put in jars to seal.
When ready to cook for your meal, drain and rinse twice before cooking.
This standard recipe has been in use in Ireland for many years.

i: 1 c. red maraschino cherry halves, drained
1 (8 oz.) pkg. pitted dates, cut up
1/2 c. green maraschino cherry halves, drained
d: Mix all ingredients thoroughly.
Place baking cups in a muffin pan.
Spoon batter into
each cup.	Bake at 325° for 25 minutes or line 6 miniature loaf
pans,

i: 1 small jar Kraft garlic cheese spread
d: Let cheese soften to room temperature, then blend both cheeses together until thoroughly mixed.
Shape into one big ball.
Chill. Roll in crushed pecans.
Serve with any type crackers.
Wash greens in cold water to remove grit or any dirt.
In a large pot filled 3/4 full of water, add the bacon grease and salt. Bring to a boil.
Add the greens by the handful to the pot, letting them have a
little time to start to cook down before adding more.
When pot is full, you will need to poke the greens down for a few minutes as they like to come to the top of the pot and run over.
Add 1/4 teaspoon of baking soda to the greens to take the bitterness out.

i: 4 1/2 lbs. ripe tomatoes, scalded, peeled, and chopped
d: Combine all ingredients in a large pot. Bring to boil; cut down to medium. Cook for 70 min. Stir occasionally, until thicker. Makes 5 pints.
Combine all ingredients in saucepan.
Bring to a boil.
Add to any meat.
In a tall glass combine the cocoa powder and sugar.
Add 2 tablespoons of the buttermilk and stir until no lumps remain.
Mix in the remaining buttermilk.
Stir well again and serve.
Serves 1 (make sure that you purchase plain unsweetened cocoa).
Preheat oven to 350°.
You will need three 9-inch round pans. Bring raisins and water to a boil in saucepan.
Let stand until cool; drain.
Cream butter and brown sugar in mixer bowl until light and fluffy.
Blend in eggs.
Add mixture of soda and buttermilk; mix well. Add sifted flour and spices; mix well.
Remove string from around roast and discard.
Place roast in roasting pan.
Pour liquid from jar of peppers over roast.
Remove stems from peppers and discard.
Do not discard seeds.
Chop peppers and sprinkle over roast.

i: 1 lb. beef filet, cut into 1/2-inch strips
1/2 lb. carrots, peeled and cut into 4 pieces
d: Heat a Dutch oven or large cooking pot with lid; add oil. Brown diced onion; remove onion.
Saute ground beef with onion and garlic in skillet until brown; drain.
Add next 4 ingredients, mixing well.
Simmer for 10 minutes.
Add Parmesan cheese, mixing well.
Mix in cream cheese. Pour into chafing dish.
Serve warm with taco chips.

i: 1 c. sharp American cheese, shredded (about 4 oz.)
d: Cook spinach according to package directions; drain well.
In saucepan, melt butter.
Blend in flour and salt.
Add the 1 cup of milk all at once.
Cook and stir until thickened and bubbly. Remove from heat.
Add cheese.

i: 2 c. cooking oil for deep frying
d: Mix flour, cornmeal, baking soda, pepper, garlic salt and egg. Slowly add milk to make a thick, smooth batter.
Try not to make it too runny.
Use less milk if you need to.
Cut onions into rings.
Prepare oil by heating to frying temperature.
Dip onions into batter and put immediately into oil.

i: 1 lb. leeks, rinsed well and julienned
d: Steam carrots and leeks in large saucepan.
Transfer to serving platter and keep warm in oven.
Meanwhile, heat oil in 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat.
Sprinkle both sides of cutlets with salt and pepper.
Add to skillet and saute until fully cooked (about 1 1/2 minutes per side). Arrange chicken on top of vegetables and return to oven.
Add thyme and shallots to skillet; cook 2 minutes.
Line cookie sheet, 12 x 16-inch, with Waverly crackers and sprinkle about 1/2 cup pecans on top of crackers.
Boil sugar and oleo for 2 minutes.
Remove from heat and add vanilla.
Pour over crackers.
Bake at 350° for 10 minutes.
Do not overbake.
Combine sugar, buttermilk, soda, oleo and Karo.
Cook over low heat until candy makes hard ball in cold water, about 45 to 55 minutes.
Add pecans and vanilla.
Start beating by hand until it gets thick and loses its sheen.
Drop on wax paper and cool.

i: 1 (6 oz.) can frozen lemonade concentrate, thawed
d: Pour into large pitcher and mix together.
Pour mixture into 7 paper cups.
Freeze about 1 hour or until the mixture is thick and slushy.
Place a wooden stick in the center of each pop.
Freeze about 7 hours longer or until pops are solid.
Peel the paper cups from the frozen pops before eating.
Chop apples and set aside.
Measure oil into large bowl; add sugar, egg and vanilla.
Beat until creamy.
Sift flour, salt, soda and baking powder together.
Add small amounts at a time to creamed mixture.
Fold in pecans and apples.
Pour pineapple over marshmallows and let stand until mushy. Mix all together and let set.
Refrigerate in covered dish overnight.
Very good with ham.
You can also add maraschino cherries.
Bake at 350° for 20 to 30 minutes.
Makes a 9-inch square pan. Delicious with spaghetti or chili.

i: 1 can refrigerator biscuits (Pillsbury Big Country biscuits)
d: Arrange biscuits in ungreased 9 x 13-inch pan.
Combine ingredients.
Pour over top of biscuits.
Bake for 25 to 30 minutes at 400°.
Biscuits will come up to top through the tuna mix, browned and puffy.
Mix packages of jello with 3 cups hot water to dissolve and add 3 cups cold water.
Set aside.
Stir-fry chicken in oil for 4 minutes.
Combine chicken and vegetables; stir-fry 4 to 5 minutes.
Add 1/2 cup mayonnaise and soy sauce to taste.

i: 1 can kidney beans, rinsed and drained
d: Brown beef and onions.
Drain.
Add all ingredients.
Cover and simmer 45 minutes.
Add cheese just before serving while hot.

t: French Toast With Bananas 'N Brandy Coulis
d: Slice baguettes diagonally into 3/4-inch thick slices. Mix buttermilk, milk, eggs, vanilla and cinnamon thoroughly. Soak slices in mixture for 10 minutes or so. Lightly spray griddle with nonstick vegetable coating. Over medium heat, cook slices on griddle until golden brown. Keep in warming oven (covered with a sheet of aluminum foil) at 200° until just ready to serve.
Boil chicken with skin on until done when it is done remove from broth and take off bone.
Cut up onion put into broth.
Add minced garlic; cook for 5 minutes.
Add rice and
cook until done. Add the chicken to the ingredients add salt to taste.
Serve with pears or bread.
Fry bacon and crumble.
Cook ground meat and drain.
Mix all ingredients together and bake for 1 hour at 350° or slow cook 6 to 8 hours.
Heat oven to 450°.
Grease round pan, 9 x 1 1/2-inches, square pan, 8 x 8 x 2-inches or 10-inch ovenproof skillet.
Mix all ingredients.
Beat vigorously 30 seconds.
Pour into pan.
Bake round or square pan 25 to 30 minutes, skillet about 20 minutes or until golden brown.
Steam green beans seven minutes only.
Run cold water over them to stop the cooking.
Add remaining ingredients.

i: 1 lb. boneless chicken, cut up into cubes
d: In greased casserole dish, mix chicken, broccoli, soup, sour cream, 1/2 cheese and 1 cup bread crumbs.
Spread evenly.
Top with remainder of cheese and bread crumbs.
Bake at 350° for approximately 1 hour.
Cut peppers in halves; remove stalks and seeds and cut into thin slices.
Place onion and oil in skillet and simmer until transparent.
Add tomatoes and cook 5 minutes.
Add peppers, salt and pepper; cover and cook slowly 30 minutes, adding water if necessary, stirring frequently.
Serves 4.

i: 1 garlic bulb, separated into cloves and peeled
d: In a blender, blend garlic, ginger root and chili pepper with 1/4 cup water to a fine paste, nice and thick.
Remove skin from chicken and cut into desirable sizes (each into several pieces, including bone).
Wash rice 3 to 4 times, draining the water completely.
Heat a 5-quart nonstick pan; add oil and fry onion and bay leaves on a medium heat until they turn light brown.
Add the ground paste in the pan and cook until it dries and has a good smell.

i: 2 Tbsp. balsamic vinegar (red wine vinegar can be substituted but balsamic has a better flavor)
d: Place first 3 ingredients in a frying pan and simmer until the veal is tender.
Add remaining ingredients.
Simmer another 5 minutes.
Serve over noodles or rice.
Cook roast and spices until tender.
Drain & slice.
Add gravy and onion soup mix.
Simmer 2 hours until done.
Brown meat
with
onions, salt and pepper.
Drain.
Add tomato paste and
sauce,
Cook spaghetti and drain.
Brown hamburger and drain grease from meat.
Stir in barbecue sauce and add water and sugar.
Stir all together.
Combine dry ingredients in large bowl, then mix together.
Make a well in middle of bowl.
Place eggs and water/milk gradually into well.
When mixing, include dry ingredients from sides.
Place flour on flat surface to roll out and cut-out round cookies.
Bake in 325° oven for 10 to 12 minutes.

i: 1 tsp. mixed pickle spices tied in cheesecloth bag
d: Peel and core pears. Grind pears, onions and peppers in food chopper.
Place in saucepan.
Add remaining ingredients.
Bring to a boil.
Cook 30 minutes.
Stir frequently.
Mix oil, eggs, sugar and vanilla well.
Sift dry ingredients and blend with first mixture (mixture will be thick).
Add apples and pecans.
Grease and flour Bundt pan.
Put in cold oven.
bake at 350° for 1 hour and 10 minutes.

i: 1/2 lb. ground beef, browned and drained
1/2 lb. bacon, cooked, drained and crumbled
1 (15 oz.) can kidney beans, rinsed and drained
d: Combine all ingredients and mix well. Bake at 350° for 1 hour or cook in crock-pot.

i: 1 c. chopped cauliflower or 1 c. chopped carrots (optional)
d: Rinse peas in hot water and drain.
Combine vegetables, nuts and bacon with sour cream.
Mix dressing and mustard; pour over salad.
Toss gently and chill.
Can make the day ahead and refrigerate.
Serves 6 to 8.
Cook rice according to package directions.
Cool.
Combine rice and vegetables, tossing lightly.
Combine mayo, mustard and garlic dressing.
Pour over rice-vegetable mixture.
Toss lightly. Chill overnight.

i: 1 lb. round steak, cut into strips
1 (8 oz.) can sliced water chestnuts, drained
1 medium red or green pepper, cut in strips
5 green onions, cut diagonally into 1-inch pieces
d: Saute beef in oil in large skillet until browned, about 5 minutes. Add cornstarch and blend well. Add broth, water chestnuts, red pepper, green onions, soy sauce and pepper. Bring to a full boil, stirring frequently.
Stir in rice. Cover; remove from heat. Let stand 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork. Yield: 4 servings.

i: 10 oz. or 2 c. grated sharp Cheddar cheese
d: Mix all together and put in greased 9 x 13-inch casserole dish.
Top with 2 cups corn flakes (crushed) and mixed with 1/2 cup melted margarine.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.
Sift dry ingredients together.
Blend in vegetable oil.
Add enough milk to make a stiff dough.
Thinly roll out dough (1/4-inch thick) on a lightly floured board into two 10 x 18-inch rectangles.
Spread on sausage; roll up lengthwise.
Chill well and slice 1/4-inch thick.
In bowl, combine flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Cut in Crisco until mixture is coarse crumbs.
Stir in buttermilk. Knead dough gently.
Roll out to 1/2 inch thick.
Cut with 2 1/2 inch biscuit cutter and place on lightly greased baking sheet. Bake 450 degrees for 10 - 15 minutes or until golden brown.
Makes 10 biscuits.

t: Curried Baked Fruit(Prepare The Day Before)
i: 1 (1 lb.) can pineapple chunks or spears
d: Drain all fruit.
Add sugar and curry to melted butter. Arrange fruit and nuts in layers in casserole; pour butter mixture over all and bake at 325° for 1 hour.
Refrigerate overnight. Reheat at 350° before serving.
Yields 10 to 12 servings.

t: Chicken And Andouille Smoked Sausage Gumbo
i: 1 c. finely chopped green bell peppers
d: Remove excess fat from chicken.
Rub with salt, garlic powder and red pepper.
Let stand 30 minutes.
Combine onions, bell peppers and celery in a medium bowl; set aside.
Combine flour, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder and 1/2 teaspoon red pepper in paper or plastic bag.
Add chicken pieces and shake until chicken is coated.
Combine first 4 ingredients in large bowl.
In blender, whiz bananas, dates and water.
Mix all ingredients well.
Spread on jelly roll pan 1/2-inch thick.
Bake at 200° for 90 minutes, stirring every 30 minutes until golden and almost dry.
Turn oven off and leave pans in oven to complete drying.
Brown meat; drain.
Stir in remaining ingredients.
Add 4 cups of water.
Cook until vegetables are done.
Combine in a Dutch oven or saucepan the lentils, bay leaf, water and salt.
Bring to a boil.
Cover tightly; reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes.
Do not drain.
Discard bay leaf.
Let margarine set at room temperature for a while.
Cream until soft.
Add Mazola and whip more.
Add buttermilk and continue beating.
Fill 4 (1-cup) margarine containers or 2 large containers.
Soak beans in water 4 to 6 hours or overnight.
In large pot, add olive oil, onions, sliced kielbasi and garlic.
Saute; do not brown.
Add chicken stock and navy beans; let cook 15 minutes. Add kale, crushed red pepper and sugar.
Let cook 20 minutes.
Add potatoes.

t: Baked Beans With Green Pepper And Carrots
d: Wash beans, cover with water and cook until soft.
Drain, not too dry.
Add salt and bacon drippings.
Mix in baking dish the beans, brown sugar, onions, green pepper and grated carrots.
Add catsup.
Bake in 325° oven for one hour or until as dry as desired.

i: 1 large can artichoke hearts, chopped (do not use marinated ones)
d: Mix together and heat at 350° for 1/2 hour. Serve with pumpernickel bread or crackers.
Mix unflavored gelatin into cold water.
Mix Jell-O into hot water.
Then mix both together.
Pour into Pam sprayed glass 9 x 12-inch pan.
Set until firm.

i: 1 sliced onion (sliced as you would for onion rings)
d: Mix sugar, vinegar and oil.
Pour over vegetables.
Cover and place in refrigerator.
This salad will keep for weeks and improves with age.
Preheat oven to 400°.
Generously grease fluted tube pan. Combine the brown sugar, nuts, margarine and water.
Heat until the butter melts.
Separate the biscuits; cut each biscuit into quarters.
Place in mixing bowl.
Pour brown sugar mixture lightly over the biscuits.
Cook grits in water and salt.
Melt cheese and margarine together.
Beat eggs.
Finish cup with milk.
Mix all ingredients together.
Pour in large casserole.
Mash potatoes.
Add 2 beaten eggs, Parmesan cheese and seasonings.
Layer with Mozzarella cheese.
Place in greased casserole dish.
Top with seasoned, buttered bread crumbs.
Bake 30 minutes at 350°.

t: Pier 4 Mushrooms("A Favorite From The '76 Cookbook.")
i: 2/3 c. olive oil or salad oil
d: Slice mushrooms after cleaning.
Combine mushrooms with all other ingredients and marinate overnight in refrigerator.
Drain and serve.
Heat milk. Add 1/2 cup sugar, salt and farina.
Mix and cook until thick. Add butter and stir until melted.
Beat eggs and sugar.
Add to mixture.
Pour into a greased 9 x 12 x 2-inch pan. Bake in a 350° oven for about 30 minutes.

i: 1 pkg. berries (frozen mixed berries are the best)
d: Place all ingredients in a blender.
Blend until smooth and pour into plastic container.
Freeze for several hours or overnight before serving.
Beat eggs and shortening.
Add dry ingredients and enough milk to make a stiff dough.
Knead dough on floured surface.
Roll out very thin, cut in strips and drop into boiling broth with a teaspoon of Accent seasoning. When dumplings are almost done, add a few drops yellow food coloring and 1 cup of cream.

i: 2 Tbsp. soy sauce or lemon soy sauce
d: Combine all ingredients, except nuts and cherry.
Form into ball.
Roll in enough nuts to cover.
(If too soft, chill a little first, then roll in nuts.)
Can garnish with cherry on top.
Put pears, onions and peppers through meat chopper.
Drain part of pear juice off or it will be soupy.
Add other ingredients and cook 30 minutes after it begins to boil.
Put hot relish into hot jars and seal.
Peel and seed cantaloupe; slice into 1/4-inch wedges. Peel cucumbers.
Slice into half lengthwise and slice thinly.
Combine all ingredients into mixing bowl.
Mix together.
Chill one hour. Ready to serve.
Yields 8 servings.
In skillet, combine oil, onions, bell pepper and garlic. Saute until vegetables are tender.
Add tomatoes, oregano, salt and pepper.
Bring to a boil for 1 minute.
Reduce heat; simmer for 10 minutes.
Add scallops and simmer for 3 minutes.
Serve over warm pasta.
Mix all ingredients together.
Put in 2 greased and floured cake pans.
Bake at 375° for 25 to 30 minutes.

i: 1/3 c. sunflower seeds, unsalted, if possible
d: Wash and cut broccoli flowerets into bite-sized pieces.
Add raisins, sunflower seeds and onions.
Mix mayonnaise, sugar and vinegar.
Pour over broccoli mixture.
Mix lightly.
Top with bacon bits.
Mix Italian dressing, brown sugar, soy sauce and ground ginger.
Pour over flank steak and marinate 3 to 24 hours.
Grill steak, uncovered, over medium coals or broil 3-inches from heat for 12 to 14 minutes, turning once.
Serves 4 to 6 people.

i: 4 Tbsp. Crisco oil or melted Crisco
d: Dissolve yeast in water with wire whip.
Add buttermilk, sugar, salt, melted shortening or oil and wheat germ.
Mix with wire whip well.
Add flour; blend with scraper.
Cover and let double in size in warm area.
Punch down and roll on floured pastry sheet.

i: 8 slices boiled ham (rectangular shape is best)
1/4 tsp. curry powder or to taste
1/4 c. half salad dressing and half sour cream
d: Mix last 4 ingredients.
Place a portion on each slice of ham and roll up jelly roll fashion.
Secure and chill.
Serve with garnish of green grapes or fruit in season.
Combine buttermilk and soda and let stand for a few minutes. Cream together sugar, margarine, and shortening.
Add egg yolks one at a time.
Add flour alternately with buttermilk mixture.
Fold in vanilla, eggs whites, pecans and coconut.
Bake at 325 degrees for approximately 25-30 in 3 greased pans.

i: 2 or 3 bell peppers (red or green or combination)
d: Heat oven to 350°.
Slice onions and bell peppers.
Mince garlic.
Drain tomatoes.
Cook beef in a large frying pan until no longer pink.
Remove with a slotted spoon.

i: 4 chicken breasts, cooked, skinned, boned and chopped
d: Mix all ingredients
in large crock-pot and cook on low for about 8 hours.
Serve over rice.
In a saucepan, combine pineapple and cornstarch. Stir. Add honey, soy sauce and cider vinegar.
Heat ingredients for 5 minutes until thickened.
This sauce is poured over sliced ham when it is served.
Do not pour over ham when it is baking.

i: 1 (8 oz.) and 1 (3 oz.) pkg. Philadelphia cream cheese
d: Mix the cream cheese, wine and garlic powder.
Add the crabmeat.
Bake in a casserole at 350° for about 20 minutes. Serve with crackers or toast rounds.
Cut beef into desired sizes.
Marinate for an hour or two with: garlic, vinegar, soy sauce, peppercorn or powder, salt to taste, Pirim's sauce (squeeze of liquid from meat and fry with just enough oil until brown).
Then add a little water (enough to cook), sliced onion, pickles, olives, tomato sauce or paste, bay leaf, red pepper (sliced), the liquid off the meat and a little sugar.
Cook until tender (pressure cooker only 15 minutes).
This is a Philippine food.
Place pineapple in saucepan, bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
Remove from heat, add gelatin, stirring until dissolved.
Stir in buttermilk and nuts.
Cool.
Fold in Cool Whip. Pour in dish.
Chill.
Cut cold butter into 1/4-inch pieces.
Toss baking mix and butter with fork until coated.
Add milk; stir just until milk is absorbed (do not overstir).
Rinse chicken well.
In a bag, combine flour, salt and pepper. Shake chicken (few pieces at a time) in flour mixture to coat.
In a large skillet, brown chicken in oil.
Remove from skillet.
Add mushrooms, parsley, garlic and thyme.
Cook until mushrooms are tender.

i: 1 pkg. Hidden Valley Ranch original party dip
d: Break pretzels into bite size pieces.
Then place pretzels in a large mixing bowl minus any crumbs which will soak up the dressing. Mix ingredients in a separate bowl and pour over the pretzels.
Mix pretzels and ingredients well.
After mixing, put pretzels in a pan, approximately 11 x 9 1/2-inch.
Put in preheated oven at 350° for 5 minutes.
Then take out.

i: 1 (1 lb.) can whole berry cranberry sauce
d: Dissolve jello in boiling water.
Add remaining ingredients. Pour into container and chill until firm.
Cut each biscuit into 4 pieces.
Place sugar and cinnamon in plastic bag.
Add biscuit pieces a few at a time and shake to coat.
Place pieces into a well-greased tube or Bundt pan until all are used.
Sprinkle layers with pecans, raisins or coconut. Bring brown sugar and margarine to a boil in saucepan.
Pour over top of biscuits.
Combine first 3 ingredients.
Fold stiffly beaten egg white into first 3 ingredients.

i: 1 large or 2 small cans mandarin oranges
d: Mix gelatin and salt thoroughly in small pan.
Add water and stir constantly over medium-low heat until gelatin is dissolved. Remove from heat and stir in lemon juice and rind.
Chill in refrigerator.
While gelatin is chilling, beat egg whites, gradually adding sugar and beating after each addition.
Set aside.
Remove chilled gelatin from fridge and beat it up a little until fluffy if too firm.
Combine oil, onion and pepper in shallow 2-quart microwave proof casserole.
Cover and microwave on High 2 minutes, stirring once.
Stir in cabbage; microwave covered 3 minutes more.
Combine mustard, honey and vinegar in a cup.
Place ham steak over cabbage; spread with the mixture.
Cover and microwave on Medium for 7 minutes or until ham is heated through.
Add eggs and sugar to molasses and melted shortening.
Add flour, sifted with
other dry ingredients.
Add hot water last. Bake at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes.
Serve warm with Cool Whip or applesauce.
Great anytime.
Slice cooked beets and combine with orange slices or sections and minced Vidalia onions.
Arrange on garden lettuce.
Pour Honey Curry Dressing on at last minute.
Mix all ingredients and top with cheese. Bake 1 hour at 350°.
Brown the ground beef; drain grease.
Saute the onion slightly and add the salt, pork and beans, barbecue sauce and brown sugar. Heat until bubbly.
Place in a 2-quart casserole.
Cut the biscuits in half (moon shape) and place cut side down over mixture.
Sprinkle with Cheddar cheese.
Bake 25 minutes or until biscuits are browned at 375°.

i: 8 oz. drained and rinsed black-eyed peas
d: Combine first three ingredients. Salt and pepper to taste.

i: 1 small onion, sliced or 4 scallions, chopped
1/4 c. parsley, finely cut with scissors
d: Break lettuce into small pieces.
Place in large bowl with remaining vegetables.
Pour enough dressing to lightly coat each piece.
Garnish with Feta cheese, olives and anchovies.

t: Microwave Tomato Shells Stuffed With Curried Chicken
d: Cut a slice from stem end of each tomato.
Scoop out pulp and invert tomatoes to drain.
Melt butter in a 1 1/2-quart glass casserole in microwave oven (30 seconds at High).
Blend in flour, onion and curry powder.
Stir in milk.
Cook uncovered for 2 1/2 to 3 1/2 minutes at High or until thickened and smooth.
Layer 1 pound hamburger, onion, potatoes and 1 pound hamburger.
Cook 35 to 45 minutes at 350°.
Drain and cover with a 10 ounce package of Cheddar cheese.
Brown hamburger in pan. Add beans, tomatoes, chopped pepper and onion. Cook 20 to 30 minutes.
Brown 1 lb. ground meat in skillet.
Drain all grease from pan.
Add BBQ sauce, simmer for a few minutes, stirring well.
Add Ketchup, stir and simmer again then soup (no water).
Simmer on low heat 15 - 20 minutes. Serve on hamburger buns.
In large bowl mix milk (Eagle Brand), lime peel, juice and salt.
Add yolks.
Use mixer at high speed.
Beat 1 or 2 minutes or until thick.
Pour into crust.
Chill at least 2 hours or until set.
In saucepan, melt butter.
Stir in flour, salt and pepper.
Add chicken broth; stir constantly until mixture boils.
Cook for 1 minute.
Remove from heat; set aside.
In skillet, brown pork chops; season with additional salt and pepper.

i: 2 c. fresh or frozen green beans, cooked and drained
8 oz. Mozzarella cheese, cut into 1/2-inch cubes
d: Mix all ingredients together in large bowl.
Cover and refrigerate 1 hour before serving.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.
Place half of the sliced apples in a buttered casserole dish. Cover with half of the carrot slices.
Combine dry ingredients; sprinkle half of the mix over carrots.
Repeat the layers; pour orange juice over the top.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.
Serves 6.

t: Smoked Salmon And Penne(Or Chicken, Or Shrimp)
i: 1 c. fish stock OR chicken stock OR clam juice
d: In a large skillet, brown the garlic and shallots in the olive oil.
Add the diced tomatoes.
Add the 1 c. liquid you've chosen; stir well.
Add the salmon, lemon juice, chives and pasta; stir well.
Heat through and add 1/2 c. sour cream.
Serve immediately.
Fry meat, onion and pepper; drain.
Cook noodles; drain.
Add tomatoes, meat and corn.
Pour in a baking dish.
Cover with cheese.
Bake at 350° until cheese melts.
Crumble ground chuck in large pot.
Cover with water, salt and pepper to taste.
Start cooking on high.
Chop up onion and celery.
Skim off excess grease.
Take tomato sauce and dump in. Rinse can with water and add to meat mixture.
Mix and pour into well-greased iron skillet sprinkled with cornmeal. Bake at 450° for 15 to 20 minutes.
Mix flour, granulated sugar, brown sugar, eggs, salt, vanilla, margarine and baking soda together.
Then add chocolate chips, pecans and cherries.
Toast nuts at 325° for 15 minutes.
Beat egg whites until stiff.
Fold in sugar and salt.
Slowly fold nuts into mixture and mix until coated.
Boil sugar, milk, butter and salt to soft-ball stage.
Add vanilla.
Remove from heat.
Beat vigorously until mixture loses gloss.
Stir in pecans.
Drop by spoonfuls on waxed paper.
Mix sugar and Jell-O together.
Add boiling water; mix well. Add pineapple and orange juice.
Freeze in plastic 1 gallon container or 1 gallon Ziploc bag.
When ready to serve, add ginger ale.
Cook flour and milk until thick, stirring constantly. Let cool and add remaining ingredients.
Beat until smooth.
In a bowl, mix hamburg, bread crumbs, cheese, eggs, onion and parsley.
Form into patties about 2 inches thick and 3 inches in diameter.

i: 1/4 c. sugar, mixed with 1/4 tsp. cinnamon
d: Add flour to the first 6 ingredients and mix well.
Form dough into 1-inch balls.
Roll in cinnamon-sugar.
Lightly flatten cookies before baking.
Bake 10 to 12 minutes or until golden brown.

i: 1 c. finely chopped lightly salted cashews
d: In a small saucepan, combine apricot preserves, mustard and curry powder.
Cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until preserves are melted.
Dip chicken in apricot mixture, then roll in cashews.
Place chicken in a greased 15 x 10 x 2-inch baking pan.
Bake in a preheated 375° oven for 15 to 20 minutes, or until chicken is done.
Bring remaining sauce to a boil.
Preheat oven to 400°.
Cut up chicken and place in a pan. Melt butter and squeeze fresh lemon juice into butter.
Baste chicken.
Sprinkle with oregano and bake at 400° for 1 hour.
Noodles: Combine eggs, flour, oil and salt. Add small amounts of water to moisten. Roll thinly on floured surface. Cut into strips and separate to dry.
Mix ingredients together, then add carrots, nuts and pineapple.
Bake at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes.
Oil and flour pan.
Prepare jello using pineapple juice for cold water.
After thickened, add other ingredients.

i: 2 Tbsp. butter or margarine (at room temperature)
d: Drain all fruits and turn out into 1 1/2-quart casserole and mix together lightly.
Blend together the soft butter, brown sugar, curry, cornstarch and lemon peel.
Sprinkle over fruit mixture.
Let mixture stand for several hours to draw out juices. Bake uncovered about 1 hour at 325°.
Spoon some of the juices over fruit several times during baking.
Serve while hot. Serves 6.
Grind vegetables.
Sprinkle salt; allow to stand overnight. Rinse and drain.
Combine remaining ingredients; pour over vegetables.
Heat to boiling; simmer for 3 minutes.
Seal in jars. Makes 8 pints.
Grind all vegetables through food chopper.
Mix well.
Put into a crock or plastic container (not metal).
Sprinkle 1/2 cup salt over this.
Cover and let stand overnight.
Drain and rinse. The best way to rinse without losing part of it is to put it into a cloth sack.

i: 2 Tbsp. chopped fresh or 1 Tbsp. dried tarragon
d: In a medium mixing bowl, combine tuna, feta cheese, celery, apples, nuts and tarragon.
Mix well.
Stir in salt and pepper. Serve over assorted lettuces.
Serves 4.
Brown vegeburger with taco sauce mix.
Set aside 1/3 of the vegeburger, olives, tomatoes and onion.
Take all of the beans and the 2/3 of the rest of ingredients and mix in large bowl.
Spread into casserole dish and garnish with ingredients that were set aside.
Bake at 375° for 25 to 30 minutes or until edges boil. Serve with warmed chips and green salad!
Mix butter and graham cracker crumbs together.
Press into 9 x 13-inch pan.
Layer other ingredients as listed.
Sprinkle a few nuts on top.
Bake in greased pan at 375° for 30 minutes.
Boil potatoes, cool and peel; cut into quarters.
Put in baking dish and add oil, oregano, salt and pepper.
Bake in 350° oven until lightly browned, about 30 minutes.
Garnish with parsley. Serves 4.
Boil corn 5 minutes.
Cut from cob.
Measure.
Chop and measure cabbage and peppers.
Chop onions.
Add sugar, seasonings, vinegar and water.

i: 4 trays ice cubes or 3 juice cans water
7 qt. ginger ale (2 at a time)
d: Mix pineapple juice, orange juice, ice cubes, fruit cocktail and cherries.
You can refrigerate overnight before needed or freeze.
When ready to use, pour into punch bowl.
Add 2 quarts of the ginger ale.
Mix.
Top with sherbet.
Saute onions in large kettle.
Fry hamburger in saucepan. When hamburger is half browned, add 1/4 cup water, 1/2 cup catsup, onion salt, garlic salt, turmeric, curry powder and salt.
After onions are browned, add 3 cups rice. Add whole cloves to hamburger mixture; add bay leaves to rice.
Add peas and juice to hamburger mixture.
Add 1 quart of more water to rice mixture; boil.
Add more water if it becomes too thick.
Mix together and refrigerate.
Great with vegetables.
To prepare caramel:
In round
metal mold, cook 2/3 cup sugar on medium heat until golden brown (watch it doesn't burn).
Stir constantly until smooth.
Remove from heat and move it around until bottom and all sides are covered with caramel.
Let it cool.
Cut melon into thin slices; remove seeds and skin.
Sprinkle with powdered sugar and liqueur.
Let stand for at least 1 hour. Mix together flour, wine, 1/2 teaspoon oil and a little salt. Blend in stiff egg white.
Dip melon slices into batter and fry in hot olive oil only until light gold in color.
Sprinkle with confectioners sugar.
Serve.
Cover wheat with boiling water.
Let sit for 2 hours (quickie: boil the wheat) until all the water is absorbed.
Mix all ingredients.
Refrigerate about 1 hour.

i: 2 lb. apples, peeled and cut into eighths
d: Unroll crescent rolls and separate into 8 triangles.
Cut each in half lengthwise to make 16 triangles.
Place an apple slice at wide end of each triangle and roll up.
Arrange in lightly greased 9 x 13-inch pan.
Drizzle with melted butter.
Combine sugar and cinnamon and sprinkle over rolls.
Combine all dry ingredients including raisins.
Beat eggs. Combine eggs, milk and flavoring.
Stir liquid into dry ingredients.
Fold in oil and mix thoroughly.
Spoon into 2 greased loaf pans.
Bake in 350° oven for 55 to 60 minutes.
In skillet, crumble sausage.
Add onions, bell pepper and celery.
Cook until sausage is brown.
Put water in saucepan and bring to a boil.
Add rice and soup mix.
Cook 10 minutes. Combine all ingredients in a 2-quart casserole dish and bake at 325° for 1 hour.

i: 1/2 c. each: peanuts or mixed nuts, walnuts, pecans, almonds, raisins, mixed dried fruit or anything else you like
d: Preheat oven to 350°.
Sift together flour, baking powder and salt.
Set aside.
Cream butter and sugar.
Add eggs to creamed mixture.
Combine milk, vanilla and oil in a measuring cup. Alternate flour mixture and milk mixture until batter is blended. Process the fruit/nut mixture until small pieces.

i: 1 lb. fillo dough or struedel sheets, thawed
2 (10 oz.) pkg. frozen spinach, thawed, drained and squeezed dry
d: Beat eggs; add spinach, onion, leeks, pepper, baking powder, cheeses and 1/4 cup butter and olive oil.
Mix with a tablespoon. Brush a 13 x 9-inch baking pan with melted butter.
Layer with 6 sheets of dough, brushing butter between sheets.
Start spreading 1/2 cup mixture and alternate with one sheet; cover.
Spread mixture evenly.
Do this until you use all of mixture.
Coat chops with flour; set aside remaining flour.
Brown chops on both sides in fat in an ovenproof frypan.
Remove pan from heat. Remove excess fat.
Cover chops with mushrooms.
Boil 2 cups water with 2 tea bags.
Let cool.
Boil 7 cups water and add 1 cup sugar.
Let cool.
Combine both into large container and add orange juice and brandy.
Freeze mixture and when ready to serve, spoon frozen mixture into glass and top with ginger ale.
Cook broccoli and rice as directed on box.
Combine all ingredients; put in casserole dish.
Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.
Brown ground beef; add onion and cook until onion is tender. Drain; add remaining ingredients except tortillas and heat until cheese is melted.
Fill tortilla shells; roll up and warm in microwave.
Serve with shredded lettuce, chopped tomatoes, sour cream and salsa or picante sauce.
Grate cheese.
Mix all ingredients.
Roll and pinch off marble size. Bake at 350° for 8 to 10 minutes (do not overbake).
Boil turnips and peel; cut in slices.
Add chopped onion, salt, oregano, vinegar and oil.
Toss gently and top with sliced eggs.
Brown onion, green pepper, garlic and meat.
Add beans, tomatoes, tomato sauce, chili powder, salt, bay leaves, paprika, cayenne pepper and Tabasco sauce.
Cover and simmer for 1 1/2 hours.
Serves 6.

i: 28 oz. can enchilada sauce (green is preferred)
d: Sauce:
Drain broth from chicken rice soup. Add tomato sauce and enchilada sauce to drained chicken and rice.
Simmer.
Add 1 or 2 teaspoons of cornstarch to thicken.
Thaw out in bread wrapper 3 loaves of frozen dough (Dakato Brand is best).
Let rise until double in size, about 8 hours. Grease 2 pizza pans (13 x 18-inch) with Crisco.
Cut dough in half.
Press 1/2 dough into each pan.
(Make sure dough doesn't shrink.)
Cover with towel and let rise for 1 hour.

Clean, peel and cut carrots into 3-inch sticks. Place in microwave-safe dish and sprinkle with 2 tablespoons water. Cover and microwave on High power for 4 minutes. Drain. Combine spices with lemon juice, honey and oil. Pour over carrots. Chill until serving. Will serve 8 people.

t: Sue Bee Honey Mustard Sauce And Dip
d: Melt margarine in small saucepan; remove from heat.
Add cornstarch and turmeric; mix well.
Stir in remaining ingredients and mix well.
Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring constantly.
Boil for 1 minute.
Makes about 1 cup.
Mix all ingredients.
Pull off a small piece of dough to roll into the size of a walnut.
Bake on a cookie sheet at 325° for 8 to 10 minutes, watching them so that they do not burn.
The nuts, raisins or chocolate chips are optional as you desire.
When cool, frost.
Combine first 6 ingredients in a 4-quart casserole, stirring well.
Microwave at High 12 to 13 minutes, stirring every 4 minutes.
Stir in soda.
Microwave at High 1 to 1 1/2 minutes. Stir in vanilla.
Beat with a wooden spoon just until mixture begins to thicken.
Working rapidly, drop by tablespoons onto greased wax paper.

i: tahini (found in refrigerated gourmet cheese area)
d: Zap bread in microwave for 1/2 minute.
It makes taco shells or pita bread easier to work with.
Put tahini on first, then add other ingredients.
Serve immediately.
Combine the sugar, milk, butter and cocoa in a saucepan and bring to a boil, cooking 1 minute.
Remove from heat and add the remaining ingredients.
Stir and mix well.
Drop by teaspoons onto wax paper, letting them stand for 30 minutes before serving.
Mix all ingredients.
Form into small balls with hands.
Store in refrigerator in closed container.
Melt chocolate in double boiler; add butter and stir.
Stir in nuts.
Drop by teaspoons on waxed paper.
Refrigerate until hard.

i: 1 bunch broccoli, cut into small pieces
1/2 lb. bacon, fried crispy and broken into small pieces
d: Combine the broccoli, bacon, walnuts (if desired), onion, salt and pepper to taste.
Blend the mayonnaise, sugar and wine vinegar.
Pour over the broccoli mixture. Stir until all pieces are coated.
*You may use boneless chicken breasts instead of the veal!
Combine scallops and mushrooms in a shallow casserole. Combine wine, soup, half and half and curry powder.
Pour over scallops and bake at
350° for 40 minutes.
Serve over rice. Serves 6 to 8.
Combine all ingredients.
Raise once; shape (two hours first, one hour second).
Bake at 350° for 20 to 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 400°.
Cook noodles; drain.
Brown meat and onion; drain.
Add tomatoes, tomato sauce, Worcestershire, mustard, salt and pepper.
When mixture is hot, add cooked macaroni and grated cheese (leave enough cheese to go on top). Put mixture into a large casserole dish (5 to 6-quart).
Add remaining cheese.
Dissolve together the first 3 ingredients.
Boil together sugar and water.
Add sugar and water mixture to dissolved jello mixture.
Stir well.
Pour into pan; refrigerate until set.
Cut into shapes.
Heat oven to 350°.
Grease and flour a 9 x 13-inch baking pan. Combine chocolate and shortening in saucepan over low heat until melted.
Remove from heat.
Stir in sugar, eggs and vanilla.
Mix in remaining ingredients. Spread in pan.
Bake 25 to 30 minutes, until brownies begin to pull away from the side.
Scald milk and cool.
Beat eggs until frothy.
Add sugar, spices and milk.
Mix.
Add cooked rice.
If to be served with fruit, cook it plain, otherwise may add raisins, dates or a small can of drained fruit.

i: 2 (29 oz.) cans sweet potatoes, drained and mashed
d: Combine first 5 ingredients, mixing well.
Spoon mixture into a lightly greased 1 1/2-quart casserole.
Cover and refrigerate for 8 hours or overnight.
Remove from refrigerator; let stand 30 minutes.
Bake, uncovered, at 350° for 25 minutes.
Remove from oven; top with marshmallows.
Mix all ingredients, except butter, and place in rice cooker. Pour melted butter over all.
Do not add water.
Cook as you would rice.
Do not open and stir.
After chime sounds on cooker, continue steaming 30 minutes.
Yields 8 servings.
First combine butter, sugar and beaten egg yolks in a bowl, then beat the egg whites in another bowl.
Add the remaining ingredients together and mix with the butter, sugar and beaten egg yolks.
Then fold in the egg whites.
Put in a 9 x 13-inch cake pan and sprinkle sugar on top.
Bake at 350° for 35 to 45 minutes.

i: 1 lb. sweet potatoes, peeled and cut into 1/2-inch thick slices
d: Place potatoes in a 1-quart casserole, coated with cooking spray.
Combine cornstarch and brown sugar in a small bowl.
Add orange and lemon juices, stirring well.
Pour over potatoes. Cover and bake at 425° for 40 minutes or just until potatoes are tender and glaze is thickened.

i: 2 stems green onion (single stalks) or 1/2 medium whole onion
d: Mix broccoli, onion, sunflower seeds, raisins and bacon.

i: 1 envelope Lipton Recipe Secrets savory herb with garlic recipe soup mix
1/3 c. olive oil or vegetable oil
d: Preheat oven to 450°.
Cut potatoes into large chunks.
In large bowl, add ingredients.
Mix well until potatoes are evenly coated.
Bake in a 9 x 13-inch baking dish or roasting pan, stirring occasionally.
Cook about 40 minutes or until potatoes are tender and golden brown.
Chop and saute the first 4 ingredients. Remove from pan.
Make thin chicken cutlets. Dredge in mixture of flour, salt and pepper. Fry cutlets in mixture of 1/2 olive oil and 1/2 butter on medium-high heat. Remove from pan.
Pour the wine in hot pan to glaze. Add the chicken broth, lemon juice and capers.
Stir while heating.
Replace cutlets and onion mixture. This should thicken gravy.
If too thick, add chicken broth to thin it.
Mix cranberry and pineapple juice with sugar and chill.
When ready to serve, add chilled ginger ale.
Serves approximately 35.
Combine all ingredients, form
a
ball	and knead until smooth.
Pat out dough and cut out biscuits.
Bake at 425° for 10 to 12 minutes or until
golden brown.
In a saucepan, saute onion in olive oil and add 1 cup rice, stirring occasionally for about 20 minutes.
Add 1/2 cup water and cook on low heat until rice is almost done.
Wash and chop fresh or canned spinach and add to rice mixture.
Also to this mixture add 1 can stewed tomatoes.
Cover and cook for another 10-15 minutes.
Before starting your bread, rinse a 2 1/2-quart saucepan with 1 teaspoon soda in 1 cup boiling water.

i: 1/3 c. chopped onion or 1 tsp. onion powder
d: Cut off tops of peppers, remove seeds and membranes.
Precook peppers (unless you want them crisp) in salted water, about 5 minutes.
Drain and sprinkle inside with salt.
Soak beans 4 to 8 hours, then cook by bringing to a boil. Simmer until done.
Cook rice by instructions on bag.
Cook beans with coriander, cumin and garlic.
Place beans in a large casserole dish.
Add rice, then chopped tomatoes with juice.
Add cheese and olives.
Slice mushrooms. Dredge chicken with flour; shake off excess. Heat oil in large saucepan. Add chicken and brown well. Remove chicken.
To oil remaining, add onion, green pepper and garlic. Saute 3 minutes. Add mushrooms and saute 4 minutes. Stir in tomatoes, water, salt, Italian dressing and red pepper.
Bring to a boil.
Stir in rice.
Return chicken to saucepan.
Cover, reduce heat and simmer for 45 minutes or until chicken is tender.
In a large skillet, cook ground beef and onion.
Drain fat. Stir in undrained tomatoes, uncooked rice, green pepper, olives, chili powder, garlic salt, salt and water.
Bring to a boil. Reduce heat.
Cover and simmer for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Top with cheese.
Cover and heat for about 3 minutes or until cheese melts.
Fry onions in oil until tender.
Add meat and cooked rice and stir until well mixed.
Add Worcestershire sauce, curry powder, salt and pepper; stir until hot.
Make well in center of rice and break eggs into it.
Stir eggs until partly scrambled; then mix rice into it.
Stir until well blended.

i: 2 whole medium chicken breasts, skinned, halved lengthwise, boned and cut into julienne strips
2 medium tomatoes, peeled, seeded and chopped
d: For pesto sauce, in blender or food processor container, combine spinach, parsley, chicken broth, egg yolk, Parmesan cheese, basil and garlic.
Cover and blend until mixture is pureed.
Cook pasta according to package directions; drain.
Keep hot.

i: 2 cans beans (1 can navy, garbanzo, pea, pinto, kidney or lima beans)
2 Tbsp. chili powder or to taste
d: Combine all ingredients in large skillet.
Heat on medium heat for 10 minutes.
Mix cucumber, onion and salt.
Drain after 1 hour.
Mix sugar, vinegar, black
pepper
and
turmeric.

i: 1 can Campbell's cream of broccoli soup
1 pkg. (20 oz.) frozen broccoli cuts, cooked and drained
d: In 1 1/2-quart casserole, combine soup, milk, soy sauce and pepper.
Stir in broccoli and 1/2 can of onions.
Bake, uncovered, at 350° for 25 minutes.
Top with remaining onions. Bake 5 minutes more.
Mix thoroughly all but nuts; then add them.
Pour into unbaked pie shell.
Bake 1 hour at 325°.

i: 1 (No. 1) can salmon, remove skin and bones
d: Make cream sauce with melted butter, blending flour and adding milk.
Cook for 2 to 3 minutes.
Remove from heat.
Add yolks of eggs and salmon.
Stir well and fold in stiffly beaten egg whites. Put in buttered casserole with straight sides.
Bake at 375° for 35 minutes.

i: 1/2 tsp. finely chopped onion (you may substitute dehydrated)
d: Blend thoroughly and chill.
Excellent for vegies.
Every time I use this dip, the recipe is requested.

i: 3 lb. lamb shanks, cut into thirds
1 lb. fresh or frozen green beans
d: Remove as much fat as possible from meat. Wash thoroughly. Braise meat in a large kettle. Add 1/2 cup water; add onion and green pepper and braise until onion and green pepper are transparent. Braise until meat sizzles, but is not burned. Add 2 tablespoons paprika; blend thoroughly. Add tomato sauce and peeled tomatoes and boiling water to cover mixture. Let simmer 2 hours. Add green beans, cut into 1-inch pieces and allow to simmer another 1/2 hour. Serve with rice.
Mix to a stiff smooth dough.
Drop by 1/2 teaspoon into boiling water, dipping the spoon into water between each dumpling to keep dough from sticking.
Boil about 5 minutes; drain.
Serve with goulash and stew.
Cream
together
sugar	and oleo.
Blend in sour cream. Add 3 eggs;
blend.
Add remaining 2 eggs and blend.

i: 4 c. cooked chicken, cut into small pieces with scissors
1 pkg. Planters slivered almonds, toasted (1/3 c.)
d: Combine all ingredients except lettuce.
Mix thoroughly. Serve over lettuce, if desired.
Makes 6 servings.

i: 1 1/2 lb. round steak, cut into thin strips
d: Brown meat in a small amount of oil.
Add chopped onions, water and salt.
Cover and simmer until tender, about 20 minutes. Add green peppers and cook 5 minutes.
Blend cornstarch with a little water and add to meat mixture.
Cook, stirring, until thick.
Just before serving, add tomatoes and soy sauce; heat. Serve over rice.
Saute ingredients in a little olive oil.
Add some juice from artichoke jar.
Serve hot in chafing dish or pan with toothpicks.

i: 2 (8 oz. each) tubes refrigerated crescent roll dough
1/4 lb. fully cooked ham, diced (about 1/2 c.)
d: Unroll crescent roll dough and place triangles on a 12-inch pizza pan, forming a ring with pointed ends facing outer edge of pan and wide ends overlapping.
Lightly press wide ends together. Combine the remaining ingredients; spoon over wide ends of rolls. Fold points over filling and tuck under wide ends (filling will be visible).
Bake at 375° for 20 to 25 minutes or until golden brown.
Yields 6 to 8 servings.
Beat eggs.
Add all ingredients to beaten eggs and blend well. Spread in a well-greased and floured jelly roll pan.
Bake at 325° for 25 to 30 minutes.
They freeze well.
Mix all the chopped vegetables and vinegar; put in shallow pan.
Sprinkle sugar over mixture.
Let stand overnight.

i: 1/2 c. chopped nuts, pecans or walnuts
d: Mix cream cheese, onion, green peppers and pineapple together and shape into a ball.
Cover ball with nuts.
Serve with your favorite crackers.
Wash and cut chicken into serving size pieces.
Dredge chicken in flour in shallow bowl.
Heat olive oil in large frying pan and place chicken cutlets in pan immediately.
Brown both sides of cutlets.
Add onions and cook until onions are soft.
Add chicken broth and Marsala wine.
Pour all ingredients over noodles and let marinate in refrigerator for 3 to 4 hours.
Wash cucumbers and leave skins on. Cut cucumbers in half lengthwise, then slice.

i: 1/4 c. olive oil or vegetable oil
1 tsp. minced garlic or 1 clove garlic
d: In a 2 cup measure, put drained clams and enough water to make two cups. Cook garlic in oil until lightly browned. Discard garlic. Add butter and clam juice. Heat to near boil. Add clams and heat through. Do not boil. Add salt to taste and 2 Tbsp. chopped parsley. Serve over spaghetti or noodles.
Soak water chestnuts in soy sauce for 4 hours.
Wrap each chestnut in a 1/2 strip of bacon and secure with a toothpick. Broil until brown.
Mix all ingredients together except tortellini and vegetables. Cook tortellini following directions.
Combine with your choice of (bite size) cooked potato, carrots and green beans, plus peas, mushroom caps, black olives and garbanzo beans.
Add oil/spice dressing to cooked tortellini and vegetables.
Stir so pieces are well coated.
Let stand in refrigerator for 2 hours or more.
Pick through and rinse beans.
Place in a 4 quart pot and cover with at least 2 inches of water.
Bring to a boil for 2 to 3 minutes.
Cover, and let stand in the refrigerator overnight. Drain and rinse beans.
Place beans in crockpot with canned tomatoes, broth, wine, and vegetables.
Cover and cook on low for 7 to 8 hours.

i: 8 Roma tomatoes, peeled and diced (about 2 1/2 c.)
d: Cook pasta to al dente and drain.
In large skillet, melt butter over medium-high heat and saute shrimp for 2 to 3 minutes or until shrimp are firm and opaque.
Remove shrimp; set aside. Add tomatoes and mushrooms to skillet and saute for 5 minutes. Stir in brandy, basil, salt and pepper.
Add cream and cook until sauce is reduced by 1/3, about 5 to 7 minutes.
Stir in Parmesan. Reduce heat to low and add shrimp to sauce.
Stir for 1 to 2 minutes to heat shrimp.
Cook over medium heat until thickened.
Pour into a greased 9-inch pan.
Blend together and serve with sesame crackers.
Bone chicken and chip up meat.
Boil spaghetti in chicken broth.
Cook vegetables in margarine until onions are transparent. Mix chicken, spaghetti and vegetables with mushroom soup.
Cook around 40 minutes in a 300° oven.

i: 8 chicken breasts, skinned, boned and pounded flat
d: Dip each breast in a combination of eggs and milk, then bread crumbs. Saute in a heavy frying pan in hot olive oil. Pour 1/2 spaghetti sauce into 15 x 10 pan. Top with sauteed chicken. In same skillet, saute the mushrooms. Spoon over the chicken along with any pan juices. Top each piece of chicken with a slice of Mozzarella and a slice of Swiss cheese, wrapping them slightly around meat. Pour remaining sauce over the chicken. Combine Parmesan cheese with leftover bread crumbs and sprinkle over top. Dish can be refrigerated, but bring to room temperature before baking. Bake at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes.

i: 1 1/2 to 2 lb. steak (i.e. London broil), thinly sliced in strips
1 clove garlic, crushed or 1/4 tsp. garlic powder
d: Brown steak in frypan with 1 teaspoon butter (margarine or Pam), garlic and ginger.
Put into crock-pot with all other ingredients except green peppers.
Cover and cook on high 3 to 4 hours or low 6 to 8 hours.
Add peppers and cook for 1 more hour.

i: 1 dash Tabasco sauce or season to taste
1 clove garlic, pressed and chopped fine
d: Mix ingredients.
This is a good appetizer for dipping carrot sticks, cauliflower, cucumbers and radishes.

Separate garlic into cloves and peel. Place in small ramekin and cover with olive oil. Wrap with foil. Bake at 400° for 30 minutes. Place garlic and 1 tablespoon of oil from baked garlic in food processor. Add butter and puree; season with salt and fresh ground pepper.
Soak
beans
in
broth.
Cook down with garlic that has been minced.
Add
Place spices in a spice bag.
Combine all juices and simmer with spice bag.
Wipe mushrooms clean.
Combine all other ingredients except garlic and parsley.
Boil for 5 minutes.
Pour over mushrooms and add the garlic.
Stir well to combine and coat.
Place the marinating mushrooms in a container with tight lid.

i: 1 lb. pasteurized processed cheese cut into pieces
2 c. butter or margarine cut into pieces
d: Sift confectioners sugar and cocoa into a very large bowl; stir in pecans.
Stirring frequently, melt cheese in the top of a large double boiler over simmering water.
Add butter to melted cheese, stirring until butter melts; stir in vanilla.
Add melted cheese mixture to sugar mixture.
Stir until well blended.
Press into a 10 1/2x15 1/2 jellyroll pan.

i: 1 c. fresh tomato, peeled and finely chopped
1/2 c. green bell pepper, finely chopped
d: Combine all ingredients except sour cream and chives. Chill thoroughly and serve in chilled cups or bowls.
Top with sour cream and sprinkle with chives.

i: 2 c. (8 oz.) crawfish tails, coarsely chopped
d: Sift together dry ingredients.
Beat egg yolks, milk and vegetable oil until blended.
Add to dry ingredients and beat until blended.
Stir in crawfish, rice and green peppers.
Beat egg whites until stiff, but not dry, then fold into batter.
Add Tabasco sauce to taste.
Mix first nine ingredients.
Saute 5 minutes butter, almonds and corn flakes.
Bake at 350° for 35 minutes.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.

i: 1/2 lb. salt pork, cut in chunks
d: In a tall, narrow ware pot (Mexicans call it a jarrow), the beans nestle closely together and pack while cooking and it means the heat seeps gently through the jarrow as it should in cooking beans.
In order that the beans pack right, you should use enough to almost fill the pot when they swell, never less than 2 pounds. Use Mexican pinto beans.
Wash the beans; put them in the pot and fill the pot about half full of water.
Never soak pinto beans.
It causes them to be mushy.
Using a medium flame, bring the water to a boil while stirring the beans to keep them from burning.
Pour salt water over cabbage, onions, cucumbers, tomatoes and green peppers.
Let stand 2 hours and then drain.

Grind together peppers, garlic and onion. Put in crock pan (not aluminum). Add salt and add just enough water to cover. Let set 2 hours and drain. Add oil, vinegar and water. Cook 30 minutes. Seal in hot jars. Makes 12 pints.
In a large bowl, combine the ground beef, celery seed, onion, milk, bread crumbs, egg and Worcestershire sauce.
Shape into a ball and place in a baking pan.
Combine the brown sugar and catsup and pour over the top of the meat loaf.
Bake in a 350° oven for 45 minutes.

i: 2 lb. can pineapple, crushed and drained
d: Mix cracker crumbs and 1 stick melted margarine.
Press into 13 x 9-inch pan. In mixing bowl, cream eggs, 2 sticks margarine (melted), powdered sugar and vanilla.
Beat with mixer 15 minutes, no less.
Spread over crumbs.
Slice bananas thin, and layer pineapple.
Top with Cool Whip.
Cook beets and put in jars.
Pour combined remaining ingredients over beets.
Cold-pack 5 minutes.
Brown ground beef and onion in a large skillet. Put rest of ingredients in a large saucepan and heat on medium. Stir occasionally. Drain ground beef and onion. Add to other ingredients and stir well.
Heat through.
Put into crock-pot on low temperature to serve warm. Cover top with shredded cheese.
Mix all ingredients together.
Makes enough topping for 2 pizzas.
Mix chopped broccoli, red onion, cheese and crumbled bacon in bowl.
Brown sausage and drain off fat.
Beat eggs.
Add in cheeses and sausage.
Pour into pie shells.
Bake 30 minutes at 350°.

i: 4 (1-inch thick) pork chops (about 2 lb. in all)
1 green bell pepper, cut into chunks
d: Brown chops in oil over medium-high heat for about 8 minutes. Remove.
Add onions and green pepper; cover and cook 3 minutes. Add tomato, garlic, spices, salt and rice; stir 1 minute.
Add stock; cover and simmer 5 minutes.
Add chops and any accumulated juices.
Cover and cook until rice is done and chops are cooked through, about 20 minutes.
Sprinkle with olives and parsley.

i: 1 1/2 tsp. chopped fresh tarragon or 1/2 tsp. dried tarragon
1 c. shelled fresh peas (frozen green peas may be substituted, but reduce cooking time to 5 minutes)
d: Sprinkle chicken on both sides with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add chicken and cook for 6 minutes, turning once, or until lightly browned but not cooked through.
With slotted spoon, remove to a plate.
Melt butter.
Add sugar and curry powder.
Drain fruit well. Arrange in a 1 1/2-quart casserole.
Add butter mixture.
Bake for 1 hour at 325°.
Serves 12.
Mix together.
Put on top of coffee cake batter.
Refrigerate 20 minutes before putting into oven preheated to 350°.
Bake 30 to 40 minutes.
Freezes well.
If frozen, remove from freezer the night before.
Mix and pat into 9 x 13-inch glass loaf dish.
Bake at 350° for 20 minutes.
Cool.

i: 2 c. Quaker 100% natural oat cereal (fruit and nut)
1 lb. white chocolate or almond bark
d: Melt white chocolate in a large bowl in microwave. Add cereal and nuts.
Mix well. Drop spoonfuls on wax paper.
Refrigerate.
Blend 3 fruits in blender.
Add ginger ale right before serving.

i: 1 pkg. Hidden Valley Ranch salad dressing mix
d: Brown ground beef with onion and drain.
Drain beans and rise.
Add all ingredients to cooked beef and simmer for 30 minutes.
Sprinkle top with crushed tortillas and grated cheese.
"Grind" each of the vegetable ingredients in food processor before measurements or chop by hand, then measure.

i: enough cucumbers that 16 c. water will cover
d: Slice, peel and cut centers out of cucumbers.
Soak overnight in lime water.
Drain and soak overnight in ice water; leave in icebox the next day for 4 hours.
Next, mix sugar, vinegar, alum, salt, red hots, food coloring and cinnamon sticks; bring to boil. Reduce heat and boil gently for 30 minutes.
Can in jars.
Mix hamburger meat and soy sauce in large bowl.
Sprinkle with cinnamon and sugar and mix through meat thoroughly.
Form into patties and grill.

i: 1/4 c. or more curacao liqueur or any orange liqueur
d: Season pork loin.
Place fat side up in roasting pan. Bake at 325° to 350° until 1/2 hour before the roast is done.
Combine all remaining ingredients except cornstarch and liqueur.
Cook over low heat 5 minutes. Blend cornstarch with some of the sauce and stir until smooth.
Add to the rest of the sauce and stir until slightly thickened. Add the liqueur and continue stirring for 5 to 10 minutes.
Brown bacon.
Remove from pan, drain off grease and set aside. (Leave small amount of grease in pan.)
Put remaining ingredients into pan.
Cover and stir often over medium heat. Takes about 1 hour.
Add bacon last 15 minutes.

i: 1 envelope Good Seasons Italian Salad Dressing mix
1 envelope Good Seasons Italian Salad Dressing
1 pkg. (16 oz.) Birds Eye Farm Fresh Broccoli, Green Beans, Pearl Onions and Red Peppers
d: Prepare salad dressing mix with wesson oil, vinegar, and water as directed on envelope.
Set aside.
Cook pasta as directed on package; drain.
Rinse with cold water and drain.
Run cold tap water over vegetables in a strainer to thaw completely; drain. Combine pasta, vegetables, chicken and cheese in a large bowl. Toss with salad dressing.
Chill.
Sift the flour and measure.
Add soda and salt to flour.
Beat oil, sour cream, eggs and sugar together.
Add flour mixture 1/2 cup at a time.
Beat well.
Add vanilla.

i: 1 tube refrigerator enriched sugar cookie dough
assorted canned or fresh fruits, sliced
d: Press out cookie dough flat on a 13-inch pizza pan or cookie sheet.
Bake according to package directions.
Cream together cream cheese and powdered sugar.
Spread on cooled crust.
Top with chopped or sliced fruits and sprinkle with coconut.
Yields 1 pizza.
Combine first nine ingredients.
Stir well.
Put in two quart casserole.
Combine butter and bread crumbs an put on top.
Cover and refrigerate overnight (good, but not necessary).
Bake at 350 degrees for 40 to 45 minutes.
Mix all ingredients
well.
Bake
in
oven in 9 x 5-inch loaf pan for 1 1/2 hours.

i: fresh spinach, washed and torn apart
d: Layer ingredients in order given.
"Ice" with 1 cup Miracle Whip mixed with 1 cup mayonnaise.
Top with shredded Swiss cheese. Toss together before serving.

i: 6 c. potatoes, cooked with skins on
d: Boil potatoes whole with skins on.
Cool.
Peel and slice. Set aside.
Fry bacon and save 1/4 cup grease.
Cook onion in bacon grease.
Add seasonings, vinegar and 1 cup water.
Trim off the green and pink parts of the rind and cut into cubes.
When you have 3 1/2 quarts prepared, cover with water and cook until just tender, about 20 minutes.
Add alum to the water and let stand overnight.
(If the weather is extremely hot and humid, keep the pan in the refrigerator.)

i: 1 small red sweet pepper, cut in strips
1 lb. spaghetti or other pasta, cooked and drained
d: In a large skillet, saute broccoli, zucchini, red pepper and snow peas in butter until crisp-tender. Add half and half and black pepper.
Cook briefly until slightly reduced. Serve over pasta. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
Makes 4 servings.

i: 1 large can pineapple chunks, drained (save juice!!)
d: Place juice from pineapple into double boiler.
When hot, add sugar, flour, salt and eggs.
Cook until thick and let cool.
Fold Cool Whip into pudding mixture.
Add fruit and nuts.
Mix well. Cover and let stand in refrigerator for at least 2 hours.

i: 1 soup can milk (use soup can to measure out milk)
d: Brown beef in frying pan.
Put potatoes and onion in a greased 9 x 11-inch baking pan.
Spoon beef on top.
Stir soup and milk together.
Pour over beef.
Stir tomato sauce and seasonings together.
In a covered jar, shake all ingredients together well.
Makes 2 1/2 cups dressing.
Beat milk and sugar in mixer large bowl.
Add remaining ingredients and beat.
Pour into large container and freeze.
Will keep several weeks.
Leave in freezer until ready to serve.

i: 2 c. cornmeal (baked until lightly brown in 225~ oven)
d: Mix well, refrigerate 1 hour.
Roll into small balls and serve or store in refrigerator.

i: 1 c. hot pepper rings, chopped fine and drained well
d: Combine Ranch dressing, mayo, milk and sour cream.
Pour into a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Layer cheeses, peppers, black olives and tomatoes.
Refrigerate 1 hour.
Serve with nacho chips.
Simmer beans in 6 cups of water for 1 hour.
Allow to stand, covered, for 2 hours or until softened.
Drain.
Put all ingredients in a crock-pot.
Add 1 cup of water.
Stir to blend. Cover pot and cook on low for 10 to 12 hours or on high 5 to 6 hours.
Preparation Time: 5 minutes plus refrigeration.
Cooking Time: 24 minutes.
Melt butter in a 15 x 10 x 1-inch jellyroll pan.
Spread pecans evenly in pan.
Bake at 300° for 30 minutes, stirring every 10 minutes.

i: sugar or Sweet 'N Low to taste
d: Roll out biscuits real thin and put fruit in side.
Cut around edges.
Fry in oil.
Drain on paper towels.
In a 3 to 4-quart pan, combine oil and onion.
Stir over medium heat until onion is limp.
Add curry powder and flour; stir about 30 seconds.
Add broth and carrots.
Cover and simmer until carrots are tender when pierced, 15 to 20 minutes.
Add parsley.
Bring water, soy sauce and seasoning to a boil.
Turn down heat.
Add onion and oats.
Form into patties.
Bake until nicely browned at 350° for about 45 minutes.
Turn after 20 minutes.
Put fat in skillet. Add onions and tomatoes. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and sugar. Add okra and cover; simmer until okra is tender, about 5 minutes. Stir all together. Serve hot.

i: 12 oz. pkg. regular Jimmy Dean sausage
2 loaves Pepperidge Farm party rye bread
d: Crumble ground meat and sausage; brown and drain.
Add Velveeta in chunks.
Add oregano, Worcestershire sauce and garlic powder.
Cook until cheese melts.
Spread on rye bread.
Bake frozen at 400° for 10 minutes.
Make large hamburger patties and place each patty on foil. On top of each patty, place 1 slice of potato, 1 slice of onion, 1 slice of green pepper and 1 pat of margarine.
Salt and pepper on top.
Fold foil down over burgers and seal.
Bake 45 minutes at 350°.
Mix Bisquick, milk and cheese until soft dough forms.
Beat well for 30 seconds.
Drop dough (teaspoon) on ungreased cookie sheet.
Bake for 8 to 10 minutes at 450°, until golden brown. Brush butter-garlic powder mix over tops of warm biscuits.
Serve. Yields 10 to 12.
Mix in order given.
Let mixture stand 10 minutes.
Drop by teaspoon on greased cookie sheet.
Bake in a 350° oven for 8 to 10 minutes.
Yield: 3 dozen cookies.
Mash together the garlic and salt.
Beat in the mustard and vinegar, then beat in the oil and yogurt.
Stir in the herbs. Season with pepper.
Makes about 1 1/4 cups.

i: 12 oz. boneless beef sirloin steak, cut 1/2 to 3/4-inch thick
1 medium onion, cut into thin wedges
1 medium green pepper, cut into 1-inch pieces
2 medium tomatoes, cut into thin wedges
d: Trim fat from beef.
For sauce, mix together the water, soy sauce, cornstarch, bouillon granules and pepper.
Set the mixture aside.
Cook fettuccine according to package directions, except omit the cooking oil and salt.
Drain well.
You will need 1 large pan or pot (about 5 or 6-quart size).

i: 3/4 c. salted peanuts or cashew halves
d: Place chicken and celery in a large bowl.
Combine mayonnaise and curry powder.
Add to chicken mixture and mix well.
Cover and chill for at least 30 minutes.
Before serving, add the pineapple, bananas, oranges and coconut.
Toss gently.
Combine all ingredients and fold into
Cool Whip. Let set and chill 4 to 5 hours or overnight.
Combine the brown sugar, white sugar and shortening. Add the well beaten eggs, dry ingredients, raisins and nuts.
Roll in balls.
Flatten with fork and bake for 12 minutes, until golden brown in a 350° oven.

i: 1 head cabbage, mixed with green onions and any other veggies you normally use
d: Cook dressing until blended and smooth.
Toss with cabbage mixture at any time, but mix in 1 package Top Ramen noodles just before serving.
Mix dry ingredients together.
Mix wet ingredients in separate bowl.
Mix both together and bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes.
Boil vermicelli, rinse and cut up into bite sizes.
Mix olive oil, lemon juice and mayo.
Pour over cold vermicelli.
Add other ingredients and mix well.
Chill before serving.
Tastes best when served 24 hours after making.
