Soak whole ears with husks in water for 2 to 3 hours before grilling.
At any time, place corn on grill, either during cooking or after.
Grill corn for 15 to 20 minutes on high heat, turning often.
Do not be alarmed if husks burn.
When corn is cooked, using mittens, remove husks as silk will fall off.
Season with salt and butter, if needed.

i: 1 qt. pure apple cider vinegar (found in health food stores)
d: Stir all ingredients together.
Let age 3 weeks.
Two tablespoonfuls of this will flavor and color a stew or gravy.

i: 1 head lettuce, torn in small pieces
8 to 10 strips bacon, crisped and broken
1 pkg. dry Hidden Valley ranch dressing
d: Layer first six ingredients in large salad bowl.
Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Add cheese and toss before serving.

t: Lite Ranch Dressing Mix(Lo-Fat Salad Dressing)
d: Combine all ingredients in a small jar.
Store in dry place.
Blend curry powder into Kraft mayonnaise.
Gradually blend in the evaporated milk.
Add the rest of the ingredients.
Heat, stirring occasionally.
Serve over hot cooked rice.
Serves 4.
Saute meat, onions and garlic; drain.
Add tomato sauce, salt, thyme and pepper to meat; mix well.
Place half of cabbage in greased 2-quart casserole dish.
Top with half of meat sauce. Repeat layers.
Cover and bake at 350° for 45 minutes.
Top with grated cheese.

i: 1/2 medium bell pepper, sliced in rings or strips
1/2 medium brown onion, sliced in rings or strips
d: In large skillet, heat oil over medium heat.
Add all vegetables, except onion, mushrooms and bell pepper.
Cook until vegetables are almost to the desired tenderness, then add the onion, mushrooms and bell pepper, adding as much soy sauce as desired to taste.
Cook up to 1/2 hour or until done to taste. Serve with rice, chow mein noodles or both.
Brown pork chops on both sides in skillet over medium heat and pour off excess fat.
Sprinkle chops with salt and pepper.
Drain peaches, reserving 1/4 cup syrup.

i: 1/2 c. fresh whole wheat bread crumbs
1 c. (4 oz.) Kraft natural shredded sharp Cheddar cheese
d: Mix meat, egg, bread crumbs, cheese, onion, Worcestershire sauce, parsley, basil and pepper.
Shape mixture into 12 meatballs. Brown on all sides in oil in a large skillet about 5 minutes. Remove meatballs from skillet.
Cook and stir mushrooms in same skillet 2 to 3 minutes or until tender.
Add curry powder to juice. Stir in Minute rice straight from the box. Cover; remove from heat.
Let stand 5 minutes while rice takes in all the flavors.
Fluff with a fork.
Mix with remaining ingredients except mayonnaise. Chill.
Add mayonnaise to rice mixture.
Mix lightly with a fork.
Mix ingredients together and place in canister.
Use two rounded teaspoons per cup of boiling water.
Add more sugar as needed.
Use whatever brand tea you like.

i: 4 chicken breasts, cooked, diced and chilled
d: Mix chicken, onions and lettuce. Add sesame seeds, almonds and noodles.

i: 1 large and 1 small can pork and beans
d: Chop onion and bell pepper very fine and cook in small amount of water in frying pan (large).
Add brown sugar, ketchup, mustard, bacon and beans.
Bring mixture to boil.
Pour mixture in baking pan and cook in oven at 350° for 45 minutes to one hour.

i: 6 chocolate-covered round mint cookies or patties
d: For each hamburger cookie, spread 1/2 teaspoon of frosting on flat side of 1 vanilla wafer.
Place 1 teaspoon coconut and 1 mint cookie on frosting.

Spread flat side of second vanilla wafer with 1/2 teaspoon frosting; place frosting side down on mint cookie. Brush top of "hamburger" with honey; sprinkle with sesame seed. Repeat to make 5 more cookies.
Makes 6 cookies.
Fill apple cavities with 1/2 cup sugar and 1 teaspoon cinnamon mixture.
Dot with butter.
Roll out pastry and cut into 7-inch squares.
Place apple on each pastry square.
Boil sugar, water, butter and cinnamon.
Place dumplings in pan; pour hot syrup over dumplings.
Cut asparagus into 2-inch length and microwave for about 5 minutes or steam.

i: 1 box German chocolate cake mix (Duncan Hines)
d: Mix cake mix as instructed on box.
Pour 1/2 of batter in a greased and floured 9 x 13-inch sheet cake pan.
Bake 15 minutes at 350°.
Melt caramels, oleo and evaporated milk in a double boiler.
Pour over warm cake.
Sprinkle chocolate chips and pecans over cake.
Place the pork chops in a baking pan; season with salt and pepper.
Mix catsup and Coca-Cola and pour over pork chops. Sprinkle with brown sugar.
Bake in 350° oven for 1 hour.
Heat oven to 350°.
Grease a 4 1/2 x 6 1/2-inch loaf pan.
Mix flour and sugar.
Stir in pecans.
Blend eggs, potatoes, milk and shortening.
Add liquid mixture to flour mixture.

i: 1 large can (No. 2) pineapple, crushed and drained
d: Mix all together and pour in crusts.
Chill overnight.

i: 1 lb. spareribs, cut into small pieces
d: Pour spices over meat and marinate for 15 minutes.
Broil over a low flame for 10 minutes on each side.
Serve hot.
Makes 10 to 12 servings.
In a jar, combine all the ingredients and shake well. Dressing will keep in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.
Makes about 3/4 cup.
Mix dry ingredients.
Add salad oil and milk.
Spoon onto greased cookie sheet.
Bake at 425° for 18 minutes or until golden brown.

i: 1 small chili pepper or 1 tsp. dried crushed chilies (hot red pepper flakes)
d: Heat the oil and gently fry the chopped onion and garlic until softened.
Add the chili pepper and fry gently for 1 minute.
Add the tomatoes with their juice, sugar, salt and pepper to taste and cook on a high flame, uncovered, for about 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
When the sauce is reduced and thick, check the season.
If you like a smoother sauce, you can pass the sauce through the medium disk of a food mill.
I usually just chop up the tomatoes with a fork and leave the sauce a bit chunky.

i: 2 (16 oz.) pkg. Birds Eye cut green beans
1 (16 oz.) jar Cheez Whiz pasteurized process cheese spread
d: Cook bacon until crisp.
Drain bacon, reserving 1/4 cup drippings.
Crumble bacon; reserve 2 tablespoons.
Microwave beans as directed on package; drain well.
Add mushrooms and onion to reserved 1/4 cup drippings in skillet.
Cook and stir on medium-high heat 10 minutes; drain.
Brown beef and drain grease.
Sprinkle soup mix over meat. Stir in water. Simmer uncovered 10 minutes.
Cool.

i: 1/2 to 1 lb. beef stew meat or hamburger
d: In 10-inch skillet, brown meat, adding salt, pepper and soy sauce to taste. Drain excess grease. Add frozen veggies and as much water as the veggie bag recommends. Simmer with lid on. When veggies are nearly done, add jelly and cornstarch mixed in cold water. Stir regularly until sauce starts to gel. Serve over noodles or rice.
Bring to a boil the sugar and water until sugar is dissolved. Add cranberry, pineapple and orange juices.
When ready to serve, add ginger ale.
Cook ground beef in skillet.
Add onions and green pepper; saute.
Add other ingredients.
Put in casserole dish.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Mix all dry ingredients.
Make 3 holes.
Put vinegar in one, vanilla in one and oil in the third, then add water over all.
Mix with a fork until lumps are gone.
Do not beat.
Bake in a 9 x 13-inch pan in a 350° oven for 40 or 50 minutes.
Mix flour, baking soda, salt and ghee together.
Add yogurt and water and knead the dough until it's smooth and soft.
Leave in a warm place 6 to 8 hours (overnight is best).
Break off balls of dough and roll out so that it is thin and about 5-inches wide. Deep fat fry.
As you fry the bread, keep pushing it to the bottom of the pan as it tends to float in the oil.
Bread will puff up and get crispy.

i: 3 oz. bottle Colgins hickory liquid smoke
d: Trim all fat from brisket.
Generously cover both sides of brisket with garlic salt, celery salt and onion salt. Pour all of hickory liquid smoke; place in 9 x 13-inch baking pan.
Cover tightly with aluminum foil and put in refrigerator overnight.
Slice cucumbers lengthwise.
Pack in jars with dill (lots). Combine remaining ingredients.
Heat to boil.
Let cool to lukewarm and pour over cukes.
Put in cold pack.
Add cold water to cover jars.
Mix all ingredients and pour in pie shell.
Freeze 4 hours. Take out and let thaw until ready to serve.
Mix sugar, milk, margarine and cocoa.
Bring to boil.
Let boil over medium heat for 2 minutes.
Remove from heat, then add peanut butter, nuts, oats and vanilla.
Drop by teaspoon onto waxed paper.
Let cool.
Mix all ingredients until dough forms, about 20 strokes.
Turn into a floured board. Shape into a ball and knead 10 times.
Roll to 1/2-inch thickness. Cut with 2 1/2-inch cutter.
Bake on a greased cookie sheet at 450° for 8 to 10 minutes.
Spread with Vanilla Glaze while warm (see Miscellaneous for Vanilla Glaze).
Preheat oven to 375°.
Brown beef and drain fat.
Add 1/2 cup tomato sauce and the taco seasoning.
Mix well.
Line bottom of 2-quart flat casserole with 2 cups corn chips.
Crush the remaining chips and set aside.
Beat egg until fluffy.
Beat in remaining ingredients until smooth.
For thinner pancakes, add 1/4 cup milk.
Cook on griddle or skillet until golden brown.
Makes about 9 pancakes.

i: 2 pkg. frozen chopped spinach or 2 pkg. fresh spinach, cooked and drained well
2 c. Pepperidge Farms herb seasoned stuffing mix
d: Mix all ingredients together and refrigerate 2 hours.
Roll into small balls.
If too messy, add more stuffing (a little at a time).
Grease bottom of pan with oleo.
Bake in a 325° oven for 30 minutes.
Turn over and bake another 15 minutes.
Roast bread; cube it and set aside.
Wash all vegetables. Break parsley and mint leaves off stems.
Slice carrots and cucumbers.
Cut tomatoes into chunks.
Tear lettuce into pieces and chop onions.
Mix all together.
*Use if you want them a little spicy.
Cook beans until done.
Drain.
Add tomato juice and tomatoes. Cook ground beef, onion and pepper until done.
Drain.
Add to the bean mixture.
Add chili hot beans and chili powder, salt and pepper to taste.
Brown ground chuck.
Cook ingredients together, bringing to boil then simmer for 1 1/2 hours.
Combine all ingredients in bowl and mix thoroughly.
Bake in 350° oven for 45 minutes or until done.

i: 2 (16 oz.) cans ready-to-serve reduced-sodium fat free chicken broth
d: Cook onion and celery in olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat, stirring constantly, until tender.
Add chicken broth and dried thyme; bring mixture to a boil.
Rinse wild rice in 3 changes of hot water; drain.
Add wild rice to broth mixture. Cover.
Reduce heat and cook 30 minutes.
Add black-eyed peas and next 7 ingredients; cover and cook 20 minutes or until rice is tender.

i: 1 qt. cooked, peeled and mashed tomatoes
d: Mix and serve with chips.

i: 1 Tbsp. dry instant Lipton onion soup
d: Mix all ingredients and refrigerate.
Serve with variety of fresh vegetables.

i: 1 (10 1/2 oz.) can Healthy Request mushroom soup
d: Remove all visible fat from chicken breasts.
Place in casserole which has been sprayed with Pam.
Top with condensed mushroom soup and add pepper to taste.
Cover and bake at 350° for 1 hour or until tender.
(No additional salt is needed.
Can substitute Healthy Request condensed chicken soup if desired.)
In large pan, simmer pork chop cubes in salted water (enough to cover) 45 to 60 minutes.
Add more hot water; enough to make as much curry as you want; then put in onions, carrots and potatoes. Salt and pepper to taste.
Bring to boil, lower heat and simmer 2 hours, stirring occasionally, covered.
Add the green pepper and simmer another 15 minutes.
Add flour to water to make sauce for thickening.
Stir into vegetables.
Mix in all ingredients; beat with electric mixer until thick. Keep refrigerated, but do not freeze.
Good on tossed salad or fruit salad.
Put lemon juice on both sides of fish.
Put in baking pan. Heat in skillet; add tomatoes, wine, garlic and sugar.
Simmer 15 minutes.
Add salt and pepper.
Put parsley, bread crumbs and sauce on fish.
End up with bread crumbs and parsley.
Mix all these together, chill overnight and serve.

i: 2 1/2 to 3 lb. ground beef or turkey
3 (10 1/2 oz.) cans meatless Italian sauce (4 c.)
d: Cook lasagna according to package directions.
Cook beef, garlic and onions in large saucepan for 10 minutes.
Add next 3 ingredients; mix well and let simmer 15 minutes.
In bowl, combine cottage cheese and eggs.
Grease a 15 1/2 x 10 1/2 x 2 1/2-inch baking pan.
Arrange several alternate layers of cooked and drained lasagna, sliced cheese, meat sauce and cottage cheese mixture, beginning with lasagna and ending with meat sauce. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.

i: 3 (15-16 oz.) cans beans (kidney, pinto or your choice)
d: Cook beef until done (does not need to be brown).
Add tomato juice, salt, sugar, chili seasoning and beans.
Continue cooking about 20 minutes.
Add garlic and cook 5 more minutes.
Add cocoa and oatmeal; cook until oatmeal disappears (about 10 to 15 minutes).
Mix sugar and flour.
Then add to milk and egg yolks.
Cook in top of double boiler until mixture coats the spoon.
Add vanilla and chill.
When cool, beat the egg whites and add to other mixture (just stir this in).

i: 2 to 2 1/2 lb. flank steak, cut in 1-inch strips
1/4 c. chopped scallions with tops (or use onion salt)
1 clove garlic, minced or garlic salt
d: Combine all ingredients
(except flank steak).	Marinate flank steak overnight.
Grill.
Fry together onion, garlic, salt, pepper, hamburger and sausage.
Combine meat mixture into rice.
Put a large spoonful in each cabbage leaf that has been partly boiled.
Fold leaf into square.
Lay in pan.
Pour tomato mixture over top and sprinkle caraway seed over top.
Cook shrimp about 3 minutes.
Set aside.
Combine spices*, set aside.
Slowly add water to flour; mix into paste, set aside.
Heat oil in wok or large skillet until hot.
Stir in onion, pepper, celery, garlic and cook until soft.
In large bowl, mix Bisquick, cheese and sausage meat; add salt and pepper.
Mix well and roll in small ball.
Place in refrigerator half a day or night.
Cut bacon in three strips and wrap each ball with a strip of bacon.
Lap ends and stick toothpick in to hold in place.
Place on cookie sheet and bake at 350° for 15 to 20 minutes.
Sear together the celery and onions, not too brown.
Add the remaining ingredients.
Cook to right consistency, slowly.
Serve on hamburger buns.
Serves 10 to 12.

i: 1/2 c. plus 2 Tbsp. plus 2 tsp. reduced-calorie mayonnaise
d: Place cabbage and carrot in a large bowl.
Sprinkle with sweetener, salt and celery seed on top.
Let stand 5 minutes.
Mix vinegar and mayonnaise together.
Spoon over cabbage.
Toss to blend.

i: 3 c. boiling chicken broth or bouillon
d: Combine all ingredients except the chicken pieces in a casserole or roaster.
Mix thoroughly to blend spices.
Add chicken.
Cover and bake at 350° for one hour or until chicken and rice are tender and liquid is absorbed.

Combine flour, soda, spices and salt. Beat shortening for 30 seconds. Add sugar; beat until fluffy. Add molasses and egg. Beat until combined. Add dry ingredients and beat on low speed until well combined. Shape into balls; dip into a small bowl of water, then a bowl of sugar. Put sugar-coated balls 3-inches apart on ungreased cookie sheets. Bake at 350° in oven for 15 minutes. Cool on wire racks. Enjoy.
Drain well, reserving syrup from fruit cocktail and pineapple. Combine in saucepan the cornstarch and 1/4 cup sugar.
Mix well. Combine and add the egg yolks and 1 cup reserved syrup.
Blend well.
Cover over medium heat, stirring constantly, until thickened.
Cool thoroughly.
Fold in Cool Whip, chopped nuts, marshmallows, pineapple, fruit cocktail and mandarin oranges. Cover and chill several hours or overnight.

i: 1 (6 oz.) pkg. frozen pea pods, thawed
d: Cut chicken and sirloin into thin strips.
Bias slice the celery, green onions and water chestnuts.
In an electric skillet or wok, quickly cook and stir meats and onions in hot oil.
Add pea pods.
Add chicken broth and soy sauce.
Bring to boiling point, cover and cook 2 to 3 minutes.
Sift together flour, baking powder, baking soda, allspice, cinnamon and salt.
Set aside.
Cream together sugar and shortening.
Add eggs, bananas and vanilla.
Add dry ingredients. Blend well.
Add pecans.

i: 1 tsp. or more ground red pepper
d: Note:
Chicken sausage can be found at delicatessen.
Turkey sausage and ground turkey may be substituted.
Dissolve gelatin and candies in 3 cups boiling water.
Stir in applesauce, lemon juice and salt.
Pour into 8 x 8-inch pan or gelatin mold.
Chill until set.
Combine all ingredients in container of an electric blender; top with cover and blend well.
Cover and chill dressing thoroughly.
Serve over salad greens.
Shake all together in a jar or put all together in a blender and blend.
Store in a refrigerator.
Dissolve gelatin in water.
Stir in blueberries and pineapple. Chill until has consistency of an unbeaten egg white.
Fold in Cool Whip and nuts.
Chill until firm.
Sprinkle chops lightly with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in large skillet.
Cook pork chops on high heat 4 to 5 minutes each side. Transfer to plate.
Cover.
Keep warm.
Add shallots and thyme to skillet.
Cut cabbage in wedges or shred and cook in boiling water until tender.
Drain and place in casserole dish.
For sauce, melt butter in saucepan; blend in flour, salt and pepper.
Cook over low heat; stir until smooth.
Stir in milk; boil for 1 minute until thick.
Spread sauce over cabbage.
Brown ground beef and onions; drain.
Add tomatoes, tomato sauce and seasonings.
Add the two cheeses.
Let simmer while you cook and drain macaroni; add to other ingredients and let simmer 10 minutes.
Pour into two oblong; greased baking dishes and cook at
350° for 20 minutes.
Mix all in large roaster and bake in slow 250° oven for 2 hours.
Stir every 15 minutes; cool.
For marinade, combine all ingredients.
Pour over beef and marinate overnight.
Cook beef on grill, using marinade as a basting sauce.
Bring to a boil and continue boiling and stirring until all water is gone.
Spread on cookie sheet.
Bake in 200° oven until peanuts are crisp inside, not soggy.
Cut shortening into flour.
Add milk gradually to make a soft dough.
Turn out on floured board and knead lightly, about 30 seconds.
Roll 1/3-inch thick.
Cut with biscuit cutter.
Bake on greased biscuit pan at 450° for about 10 minutes.

i: 2 pkg. chopped broccoli, cooked and drained
d: Mix all together and bake in buttered casserole. Crumble Ritz crackers on top.
Bake at 350° for 10 to 15 minutes.
Mix together ingredients and pour into pie crust.
Chill for 5 or 6 hours.
Cook 1 1/2 hours, then add 1 cup flour to water and add to other ingredients.
Cook another 1/2 hour.
Freeze or can.
Add squash to boiling water and cook until tender.
Mix sugar and cinnamon in a bowl.
Whip egg white in a separate bowl, put nuts into whipped egg white, then roll in sugar and cinnamon mixture.
Place on cookies sheet and bake at 300 degrees for 1 hour.
Be sure and stir pecans every 15 minutes.
Mix Cool Whip, Eagle Brand milk and the lemon or limeade. Pour into pie crust. Chill and serve.
Cut sirloin into thin strips.
In a large skillet, quickly brown steak, adding A. 1. sauce and Worcestershire sauce to taste. Add peppers and onions.
Cook until tender, about 10 to 15 minutes.
Serve alone or over rice.

i: 4 (4 oz.) skinned, boned chicken breast halves
d: Combine chicken, onion, celery and bay leaf in Dutch oven. Add water and cover.
Simmer 15 minutes after boiling.
Remove chicken and chop in bite-size pieces.
Set aside.
Discard vegetables and water.
Combine egg white, tarragon, salt, garlic, pepper and lemon juice in blender.
Mix cabbage and onions.
Put in Ziploc bag and refrigerate. Saute butter, noodles, sunflower seeds, almonds and sesame seed. Pour on paper towels and cool.
Keep in Ziploc bag until ready to use.

i: 10 oz. frozen chopped broccoli, thawed and drained
d: Combine thawed broccoli, olives, onion, eggs and water chestnuts.
Coat with mayonnaise.
Serve chilled in bowl or in hollowed out honeydew melon shell.
Follow the directions on all the packages except for 1 lemonade package.
Add the contents of this one package (lemonade) dry to the other mixture.
Using less water (since one package will be included "dry") will make the whole drink more zestful and delicious.
Add boiling water to All-Bran; set aside.
Mix sugar, oil and beaten eggs.
Add buttermilk and flour mixed with soda and salt. Mix in over All-Bran, then fold in 40% Bran Flakes.
Bake in greased muffin pan at 350° for 18 to 20 minutes.
Mix keeps up to 6 weeks in refrigerator.
Mix all together and cook for 1/2 hour.
Makes one gallon.
Remove outside leaves from cabbage.
Wash cabbage.
Shred to measure 5 cups.
In saucepan, heat 1/2-inch salted water (1/2 teaspoon salt to 1 cup water) and 2 tablespoons vinegar to boiling.
Add cabbage. Cover and heat to boiling. Cook about 5 minutes or until crisp-tender. Drain.

Trim fat from steak. Cut into serving size pieces. Heat oil in skillet. Add steak and brown on both sides. Place steak in baking dish. Combine all other ingredients and pour over steak. Bake at 325° for 1 1/2 hours.
Mix the mayonnaise and sugar and pour over the vegetables, seeds and raisin mixture.
Toss well and add 6 strips of crumbled bacon to top before serving, if desired.

i: 1 pkg. Uncle Ben's wild rice mix
d: Cook mushrooms in butter about 4 minutes.
Cook rice according to directions on box.
Mix all ingredients in buttered 2-quart casserole.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Makes 8 servings.
Mix all ingredients in an airtight container.
Use 1/3 cup (more or less to taste) per cup of hot water.
Combine all ingredients well and refrigerate.

i: 1 (3 to 3 1/2 lb.) beef chuck pot roast
d: Trim excess fat from meat; reserve trimmings.
Coat meat with flour.
In a 4 1/2-quart Dutch oven, heat trimmings until about 2 tablespoons of fat accumulate.
Discard trimmings.
Brown meat on both sides in hot fat.
Add bouillon granules and 1 1/4 cups hot water.
Saute
onion in butter in a saucepan until tender.
Add water and
bouillon cube; bring to a boil.
Stir in remaining ingredients.
Allow
Cut broccoli; do not cook.
Mix in bacon, onion and cheese. Mix together mayo, sugar and vinegar and pour over broccoli. Chill.
Serves 6 to 8.

i: dash of freshly ground black pepper
1/4 tsp. minced fresh or dried tarragon
d: If mushrooms are not clean, gently rinse and dry on paper towels.
Slice in half.
In a bowl, combine other ingredients, except endive, and toss well with mushrooms.
Let stand at room temperature for 1 to 2 hours.
Mix everything together, except shrimp.
Pour over shrimp and marinate in refrigerator for at least 1 hour.

i: 2 cans whole green chiles, split and seeded
d: In a well-buttered, 9 x 13-inch casserole dish, beginning and ending with cheese, layer cheese and chiles.
Mix eggs with salt, pepper and garlic powder to taste.
Pour egg mixture over layered cheese and chiles.
Mix yeast with warm water.
Mix all other ingredients.
Leave in refrigerator and use as needed.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Cut each shell into 8 pie-shaped wedges.
Bake on ungreased baking sheets for 7 to 9 minutes or until golden brown.
Remove shells and place on waxed paper.
Place caramels and milk in microwave-safe bowl.
Microwave on Medium power for 3 minutes.
Grease 8 x 8 x 2-inch baking pan.
In 2-quart saucepan, melt margarine over low heat.
Stir in cocoa, corn syrup, water and vanilla until well blended.
Remove from heat.
Gradually stir in confectioners sugar until well blended and smooth.
Stir in marshmallows and nuts.

i: 1/4 c. cream or 1/4 c. sour cream
d: Blend together the curry powder, salt, mayonnaise, cream and onion.
Add to all other ingredients, except almonds.
Chill for 4 or 5 hours.
Sprinkle almonds on top before serving.

t: Red, White And Green Vegetable Melange
d: Cut red pepper into 1 inch squares.
Heat oil in large skillet.
Add red pepper, cauliflower, peas (unthawed), water, salt, pepper and cumin.
Cover tightly and cook over high heat, shaking the pan often until cauliflower is just tender; about 8 minutes.
Combine caramels and milk in top of a double boiler.
Heat over boiling water until smooth and creamy.
Stir occasionally; remove from heat.
Drizzle over popcorn.
Use your microwave to saute the vegetables right in the serving dish.
In one-quart casserole combine first five ingredients listed until vegetables are tender; about 4 minutes.
Then stir in rest of the ingredients.
Microwave 3 or 4 minutes, stirring once.
Then serve over your burgers, pork chops, chicken, etc.
Makes 3 cups.
In Dutch oven, brown ground meat.
Add chopped onion, garlic and green pepper; cook with meat until tender.
Add seasonings, tomato sauce and Ro-Tel tomatoes.
Cook mixture over medium heat about 15 minutes; stir as needed.
Add flour and water mixture. Stir often to prevent sticking or burning.
Cook 15 or 20 minutes. Add a little water, if needed.
Brown hamburger and drain.
Add Ragu and mashed tomatoes. Simmer for about 3 hours.
Pour over noodles.
Mix sugar, margarine and Crisco.
Add eggs, one at a time. Beat on low.
Add flour, sour cream and vanilla.
Bake at 325° for about 1 hour and 15 minutes.

i: hot peppers to taste (yellow Hungarian work best)
d: Pack cucumbers, dill, garlic and pepper in gallon jar.
Heat brine; pour over cucumbers.
Cover and place in refrigerator. Ready to eat in 1 week.
Will stay crisp indefinitely.

i: 3 c. (24 oz.) canned, crushed low sodium tomatoes with juice
d: Spray a large saucepan with olive oil spray.
Add onions, celery and green pepper and saute until tender.
Drain clams, reserving juice.
Add juice, water, potatoes, sage and thyme to vegetable mixture.
Cook until potatoes are tender, about 20 minutes.
Add clams, tomatoes and Tabasco.
Combine first five ingredients in a large bowl.
Add eggs and oil.
Stir until dry ingredients are moistened.
Do not beat. Stir in vanilla, pineapple, pecans and bananas.
Spoon batter into 3 greased and floured 9-inch pans.
Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes.
Mix soy sauce, garlic, ginger, brown sugar, m.s.g. and pepper in large bowl with tight seal.
Add chicken wings; refrigerate for 12 to 16 hours.
Turn bowl upside-down after 6 hours.
After 12 hours, place chicken onto a sheet pan and place in oven at 375° for 45 minutes.

i: 1 individual tea bag or 1 tsp. of tea leaves
d: Make the tea as instructed on the package. Add ginger slices. Let them remain for a few minutes. Add sugar if needed and serve.
Brown meat and seasoning in skillet.
Drain off fat.
Add tomato paste; blend.
Turn mixture out on crust.
Top with cheese. Bake at 375° for 10 to 15 minutes.

i: 3/4 c. quick-cooking couscous (many supermarkets carry Far East brand)
3 oz. Feta cheese, cut into 1/4-inch dices
3 Tbsp. minced arugula or 1/4 c. minced watercress
d: Bring stock to boil in small saucepan.
Mix couscous.
Cover and remove from heat.
Let stand 5 minutes.
Fluff couscous with fork.
Turn into medium bowl.
Sift 1 cup flour and powdered sugar.
Dot in butter.
Press into 11 x 17-inch pan.
Bake 10 minutes at 350°.
Mix anchovy and garlic to a paste.
Add mustard and lemon juice; mix.
Add egg yolks, olive oil and Worcestershire sauce; mix well.
Gradually add cheese.
Add lettuce; toss well.
Add pepper, croutons, extra cheese, bacon bits and mushrooms, if desired.
Brown hamburger, onions and garlic, drain.
Add tomato sauce and paste.
Add 2 cans water with tomato paste can.
Add Italian seasonings and salt and pepper to taste.
Add mushrooms and cheese.
Simmer for 2 to 3 hours.

t: Jim'S Favorite Zesty Bell Pepper Slaw(This Recipe Can Be Prepared In 45 Minutes Or Less.)
i: a pinch of cayenne or to taste
d: Coarsely chop cabbage, bell peppers and scallions. In a bowl, whisk together mayonnaise, yogurt, vinegar and cayenne. Add vegetables, basil and salt and pepper to taste; toss well. Chill slaw, covered, 20 minutes.

i: 1/4 c. lemon juice and grated rind
d: Chill the evaporated milk, unopened, in refrigerator until icy cold.
Cook bacon until crisp; drain and reserve 1/4 cup fat from bacon.
Cook onion in the fat until tender.
Blend flour, sugar, salt and celery seed; add vinegar and 1 cup water and cook until thickened.
Add bacon, potatoes and eggs.

i: 1 (16 oz.) can no-salt-added green beans, drained
d: Combine green beans, wax beans, kidney beans, bell pepper and green onions. Combine apple juice, cider vinegar, sugar, pepper, dry mustard, paprika and dried oregano in a small jar; cover tightly and shake vigorously. Pour vinaigrette over salad; toss gently. Cover and chill at least 2 hours. Yield: 12 servings.
Melt oleo. Add milk and sugar. Boil until firm ball forms when dropped in cold water. Add marshmallows, chips, nuts and vanilla. Pour in greased pan. Cool and cut.

i: 1 (8 oz.) pkg. spaghetti (your choice), cooked in slightly salted water
3 tomatoes (or more), cut in bite size
d: Drain spaghetti well.
Add vegetables and dressings.
Sprinkle with Salad Supreme.
Toss and refrigerate.
Measure blackberries that have been cleaned and washed.
Crush well and add an equal amount of sugar in a large kettle.
Mix well.
Bring to a quick boil and cook rapidly, stirring often. Cook until large bubbles appear.
Ladle into jars and seal.
Mix together the first 5 ingredients.
Combine remaining dry ingredients together and add to molasses mixture.
Mix well.
Let set overnight in refrigerator.
Roll out and bake at 350° for 12 minutes.

i: cooking oil for deep fat frying
d: Peel and cut onions into 1/2-inch thick slices.
Separate into rings and set aside.
In a medium bowl, combine beaten egg, milk and oil.
Blend in flour and salt just until moistened.
Fill deep fat fryer or a 2 to 3-quart saucepan with cooking oil.
Heat oil to 375°.
Combine all ingredients, except fruit pectin, in a Dutch oven; bring to a boil.
Boil for 6 minutes, stirring frequently.
Stir in fruit pectin; continue boiling for 3 additional minutes, stirring frequently.
Remove from heat and skim off foam with a metal spoon.
Quickly pour jelly into sterilized jars, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
Cover at once with a 1/8-inch layer of paraffin.
Bring jelly and Sure-Jell to a hard boil.
Add sugar, Jell-O and lemon juice.
Boil 8 minutes.
Mix ingredients.
Let come to room temperature before covering and then refrigerate.
Serve only on rainy summer days, preferably holidays and vacations.
Combine all dry ingredients.
Combine milk, egg and oil.
Add to dry ingredients; stir quickly.
Fill greased muffin pans 2/3 full.
Bake at 425° for 12 to 15 minutes.
Roll chicken livers in flour and saute in shortening until brown.
Arrange asparagus in baking dish.
Top with sauce made from mayonnaise, minced onion and chicken livers.
Sprinkle with cheese.
Brown lightly under the broiler.
The chicken livers and sauce may be prepared in advance, if desired.

i: 2 lb. squash (mix varieties, zucchini, crookneck and patty pan)
1 Tbsp. minced onion (or 1 small onion chopped fine)
d: Cut squash into chunks and steam until tender.
Drain well and set aside.
Beat eggs and blend in mayonnaise; add seasonings. Place squash in 2 1/2-quart casserole and pour mix over.
Put Parmesan cheese evenly on top.
Bake, uncovered, at 375°
for 25 minutes, or 35 minutes if cold.

i: 1 to 3 garlic buds (adjust for size and taste)
1 to 3 Tbsp. tamari sauce (use smaller amount for low sodium diet)
1 (16 oz.) can cooked beans (navy, pinto, kidney or pork and beans)
d: Brown meat over medium heat in a large skillet.
Remove meat from pan and drain off fat.
Stir 1 to 3 tablespoons tamari sauce into meat.
Saute onion, garlic and tomatoes.
Add spaghetti sauce and heat.
Add cooked beans and meat mixture.
Combine sugars, corn syrup and water in buttered large glass mixing bowl.
Microwave for 15 to 17 minutes on roast or until candy forms a hard ball in cold water (250°).
Meanwhile, beat egg whites in large bowl until stiff peaks.
Pour candy syrup in thin stream into egg whites; beat constantly with mixer.
Add maple flavoring and salt; beat until mixture is stiff and dull.
Fold in nuts.

i: 1 c. red or green peppers, cut into 1-inch sq.
d: Cut beef cross grain into thin strips.
Combine soy sauce and ginger; add beef, then set aside while preparing vegetables.
Heat oil in large frying pan or wok.
Add beef and
toss over high heat until browned.
Over medium heat, add vegetables and toss until tender-crisp.

i: 5 c. water or enough to make 1 gal.
d: Dissolve jello in 1 cup water.
Add remaining ingredients and stir well.
(Optional:
1 quart ginger ale when ready to serve.)

i: 1 (8 oz.) jar Kraft Zesty Italian dressing
d: Cook noodles as directed.
Drain and rinse with cold water. In a large bowl, mix noodles and Italian dressing.
Add veggies and Salad Supreme seasoning.
Refrigerate 4 hours.
Cream together the margarine, sugar and egg.
Sift together and add the flour, salt, baking soda and baking powder.
Add rolled oats and coconut.
Drop by teaspoon; press flat.
Bake at 350° for 15 to 20 minutes.

i: 6 c. chopped, cubed chicken breasts, cooked
1 c. mayo (reduced or no-fat works well)
d: Mix all ingredients together in any kind of baking dish (Pyrex, Correll).
This is a one pan dinner.
Bake, covered, for 30 to 40 minutes at 350°.
Serve alone or over rice.
The kids will love it.

i: 2 c. boiled chicken, cut into bite sizes
d: Place the chicken in the bottom of a greased baking dish. Spread the onion over the chicken, then spread the eggs over the onion.
Mix together the vegetables, soup and broth and then pour over the top.
Mix together the mayonnaise, milk and flour and pour over all other ingredients.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes or until golden brown.
Melt chocolate and butter over low heat.
Cool.
Add marshmallows and nuts to chocolate mixture.
Shape into 3 rolls, 1 1/2 to 2 inches in diameter.
Place on wax paper.
Roll each in coconut.
Cook noodles as directed on package.
Drain.
Brown beef and onion.
Stir in 1 can pizza sauce, tomato paste, garlic salt and oregano. Simmer 10 minutes.
Mix plaster, water and paint well.
Add to container and allow to dry.
Boil okra and tomatoes.
Brown onions and celery in bacon fat. Add all other ingredients.
Boil 5 to 10 minutes.
Shape in loaf and bake at 300° for 1 1/2 hours.

i: 4 large boneless chicken breast halves or 8 small
d: Place chicken breast halves between sheets of waxed paper; pound slightly with mallet.
Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Heat 1 1/2 tablespoons each of oil and margarine in large skillet. Cook chicken over high heat for 3 minutes on each side; transfer to warm serving platter.
Add green onions, lemon juice, brandy, parsley and the Dijon mustard to pan.
Cook for 20 seconds, whisking constantly.
Whisk in broth.
In a small mixing bowl, cream the margarine.
Add cocoa and powdered sugar alternately with milk.
Beat to spreading consistency.
Additional milk may be needed.
Blend in vanilla.
Preheat oven to 400°.
Mix sugar, flour, baking soda, cinnamon and Raisin Bran.
Add the eggs, Crisco and buttermilk and mix well. Bake in muffin pans 15 to 20 minutes.
Dough will keep 6 weeks in the refrigerator.

i: 1 (2.8 oz.) can French-fried onions, coarsely crumbled
4 thinly sliced beef top round steaks (about 1 lb.)
d: Put broiler pan 8 to 10-inches from heat and preheat broiler. In a shallow bowl, combine oil and mustard. Dip steaks into mixture, then into the onions, coating completely. Place steaks on a rimmed baking sheet and broil for 3 to 4 minutes on each side.
*May vary amounts as desired.
Combine first 5 ingredients in a jar; cover tightly and shake vigorously.
Rinse endive lightly; pat dry and trim ends.
Arrange on salad plates; top with watercress.
Drizzle dressing over top and sprinkle with toasted walnuts.

i: 1 Tbsp. plus 1 tsp. white wine vinegar
d: Place potatoes in saucepan.
Add water to cover.
Bring to a boil.
Cover, reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes, or until potatoes are tender.
Drain.
Place potatoes and onion in a bowl. Combine vinegar and remaining ingredients; stir well.
Dice tomatoes and peppers.
Combine all ingredients in top of double boiler for 30 minutes.
Serve hot over corn chips.
Mix egg yolk slightly.
Add oil, 1 drop at a time, until it begins to thicken, then add slowly and steadily.
Add lemon juice and honey.
Store in fridge.
Mix both packages of Jell-O in the hot water.
Let cool.
Add pineapple, milk, salad dressing, cheese and pecans.
Pour into a greased 9 x 13-inch pan.
Refrigerate until congealed.
Cut in squares.

i: 5 c. flour (can be 2 c. whole wheat and 3 c. white, if desired)
d: Sift and measure and then set aside the flour, baking soda and salt.
Mix eggs, vanilla and sugar plus honey.
Add 1 cup shortening.
Add alternately with flour mixture the buttermilk and then add Raisin Bran cereal.
Mix all ingredients well and pour into pie crusts.
Let stand overnight.
Layer cucumbers, onions, squash and bell peppers to fill wide-mouth gallon jar.
Mix rest of ingredients together.
Stir syrup until dissolved, then pour over veggies layered in jar. Refrigerate 2 weeks, then ready to eat.

Dissolve the gelatin in boiling water. Stir in the cranberry sauce, pineapple and pears. Use potato masher to mash and mix all together. Pour into a Tupperware pan of 9 x 9 x 3-inch or larger. Makes about 16 servings.
Mix all ingredients except onion.
Put into skillet, top with onion and simmer for 10 minutes.

Blend as for pie dough. Roll into a 13-inch circle and place in 12 inch pizza pan. Crimp edges. Prick dough all over w/fork to prevent bubbling. Bake at 425° 12-15 min. (For sweeter pizza, you may use prepared or homemade sugar cookie dough.) Cool completely.
In saucepan, melt butter; set aside.
In bowl, mix sugar, cinnamon, cloves and pecans; set aside.
In large 13 x 9-inch pan, layer filo (3 or 4 sheets) and brush with butter.
Sprinkle with pecan mixture.
Layer more filo brushed with butter, pecan mixture, etc., ending with filo.
Cut into 4-inch square and cut 4-inch squares into 2-inch triangles.
Sift dry ingredients together.
Cut in butter.
Add milk to make a soft dough.
Turn onto floured board and roll 1/2 inch thick.
Cut into small squares.
Drop into hot liquid.
Bring to a boil ginger ale and catsup.
Pack and roll meat mixture into firm balls.
Add to boiling mixture.
Cook about 3 hours.
Chill and remove fat from top; reheat.
This makes a good dish to freeze or prepare ahead.

i: 1 (12 oz.) pkg. mint flavored chocolate chips
1 1/3 c. shortening (part oleo may be used)
d: Melt chocolate chips with corn syrup.
Remove from heat and cool.
Combine shortening, sugar and eggs.
Blend until creamy. Sift the flour, soda, cinnamon and salt.
Blend chocolate mixture with shortening mix.
Add flour gradually.

i: cooked, fresh or frozen asparagus, chilled
d: Mix the first 5 ingredients in a bowl.
Add the radish slices and marinate for 3 hours.
Place the asparagus on the salad greens.
Drain the radishes, keeping the marinade.
Place the radishes on the asparagus and serve with the marinade.

t: Copy Cat Cheese Biscuits(Similar To Those Served By A Popular Seafood Restaurant)
d: Preheat oven to 450°.
Mix baking mix, cheese and water.
Drop by large spoonfuls onto greased baking sheet.
Bake for 8 to 10 minutes.
After baking, while hot, brush with melted butter, mixed with the parsley, garlic and Italian seasoning.
Serve hot.
Peel and thinly slice potatoes and onions.
Heat oil in a nonstick pan.
Add potatoes and cook on medium heat.
After a few minutes, add the onions.
Saute until potatoes are tender and onions are transparent.
Add salt and pepper.
Cream the sugar and oleo.
Add meal, flour and nutmeg.
Mix. Add well beaten egg yolks.
Mix. Add milk and vanilla.
Mix until smooth.
Pour into an unbaked pie shell and bake approximately 45 minutes at 350° or until fairly jelled.
Slice zucchini into pickle type slices.
Layer in microwave-safe baking dish.
Top layers with prepared spaghetti sauce.
Microwave zucchini and shift sauce until zucchini is tender, approximately 10 minutes.
Sprinkle top with Mozzarella cheese and microwave until melted.
Serve hot!
Mix all ingredients and 2 tablespoons water in blender until smooth.

i: 2/3 c. coarsely chopped green pepper, divided
d: Combine chicken, water, broth, half of the celery, carrots, onions, green pepper, pepper and bay leaf; bring to a boil. Simmer 2 1/2 hours or until chicken is tender. Remove chicken. When cool enough to handle, remove meat from bones and cut up. Set chicken aside. Strain broth and skim fat. Return to kettle. Add salt and remaining ingredients. Simmer 10 to 12 minutes. Add noodles and chicken. Simmer 12 to 15 minutes.

i: 1 Vidalia (or Peru Sweet) onion, cut in rings
1/2 Hass avocado, cut in small pieces
1 lb. medium steamed shrimp, cooled and peeled
d: Using a clear 3-quart salad bowl, layer lettuce, onion, tomatoes, avocado and olives.
Top with shrimp and cheese.

i: 1 c. uncooked whole wheat shells or spirals (1 1/2 c. cooked)
1 (6 1/2 oz.) can light tuna (packed in water), drained and flaked
d: Cook pasta in boiling water 10 to 12 minutes to taste.
Drain and rinse with cold water.
In a large bowl, combine yogurt, honey, lemon juice, vinegar, dill, celery seed, paprika and white pepper.
Stir in pasta, tuna, peas, celery and red pepper.
Mix gently, but thoroughly, until evenly coated with dressing. Sprinkle green onion on top.
Serve at room temperature.

i: 1 (1.8 oz.) pkg. dry onion and mushroom soup mix
d: Rub roast with vinegar. Cut 8 to 10 slits in roast; sprinkle garlic salt into slits. Dredge roast in flour. Brown roast on all sides in hot oil. Stir together soup mix, sherry, coffee, 1 cup water and mushrooms; pour over roast. Heat until warmed. Bake, covered, at 350° for 3 hours or until roast is fork-tender.
Position rack in center of oven and preheat to 400°.
Line 10 muffin cups with muffin papers.
Mix together; makes 2 cups.
Note:
As many as you wish boneless chicken breasts.
Combine all ingredients in a casserole dish.
Bake, uncovered, at 350° for 1 hour.
Makes 6 servings.
Marinate steaks overnight. Turn several times. Roll in seasoned cracker crumbs (to taste) and roast in oven at 350°. Turn every 20 minutes.

i: 1 lb. beef stew meat, cut in 1-inch cubes
d: In 10-inch skillet, brown beef cubes in hot oil.
Stir in wine, undrained tomatoes, onion, green pepper, raisins, apricots, salt, pepper and garlic.
In a cheesecloth bag, combine basil, thyme, tarragon and bay leaf.
Add to meat mixture and simmer, covered, for 1 hour.
Stir mushrooms and olives into stew. Simmer, covered, 30 minutes more.
Discard herb bag.
Cook macaroni according to directions on label.
Saute ground beef until well done.
Add tomatoes, green peppers, steak sauce and ketchup.
Simmer for 30 minutes.
Pour into baking dish.
Add cooked macaroni and mushroom soup.
Slice chicken breasts.
Combine honey, lime juice and mustard. (I use a combination of Kosciusko and a stone-ground mustard.) Place chicken in a baking dish.
Pour marinade over chicken, turning to coat.
Let stand for 15 to 20 minutes, turning once.
Roll each pepper in hand 8 or 9 times hard, then dice.
Put all ingredients in blender and blend.
Serve with chips, veggies, etc.

i: 4 c. diced white meat of chicken
d: Toss all ingredients together.
Season individual portions into lettuce cups.
Makes approximately 12 servings.
Sift together flour, baking powder, baking soda and salt. Beat eggs.
Add sugar, vanilla, lemon rind and shortening.
Mix into bowl with flour to form a soft dough.

i: 2 1/2 c. all-purpose * or whole wheat flour
d: Dissolve yeast in warm water.
Stir in remaining ingredients; beat vigorously 20 strokes.
Let rest about 5 minutes.

i: green tomatoes, peeled and ground (need 2 c. pulp)
d: Boil tomato pulp and sugar for 5 minutes.
Add jello.
Put in jar and let set.
Chop onion; add garlic to oil in a large pot.
Saute until soft (do not burn).
Strain tomatoes.
Add this to the onions and garlic, plus puree, salt, black pepper, water and sugar.
Cook this for 1 hour, low to medium heat.

i: 2 c. chicken stock or 2 c. water and 2 bouillon cubes
salad herbs, salt, pepper to taste
d: Cook the rice in the liquid and cool. Add corn and peppers. Make a dressing of the remaining ingredients; mix thoroughly with the rice and vegetables. May be served at room temperature or chilled. Excellent with grilled steak, grilled or fried chicken and fish.
Melt chocolate and butterscotch chips over hot water.
When completely melted, add noodles and peanuts.
Drop by rounded teaspoonfuls on wax paper.

i: 1/2 c. old fashioned or quick oats (uncooked)
1 or 2 c. fresh cranberries to taste
d: Combine flour, brown sugar, oats and cinnamon and cut in margarine until the mixture resembles coarse crumb mixture.
Toss 1 cup of crumb mixture with sweet potatoes (drained) and the cranberries.
Place in 1 1/2-quart casserole.
Top with remaining crumbs.
Bake at 350° for 35 minutes.
Sprinkle marshmallows over top and broil until lightly brown.

i: 1 lb. fresh spinach or 2 pkg. frozen spinach
1/4 c. olive oil or salad oil
d: Rinse and drain spinach or defrost frozen spinach.
Brown onions and garlic in hot oil until golden brown.
Remove onions and garlic with slotted spoon; set aside for garnishing.
Add spinach to oil; cover and cook for 10 minutes on low heat. Garnish with oil and garlic.
Serve with lemon wedges.
Serves 4.

i: 1 pkg. Ranch or Italian dressing mix
d: Layer potatoes in casserole dish.
Pour margarine and dressing mix over potatoes.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.

i: 2 Tbsp. sesame oil and rest salad oil to equal 1/2 c. total
1/2 c. pimiento or marinated red pepper
d: Cook spinach and broccoli as directed.
Drain well.
Mix rest of ingredients, except onion rings, with vegetables.
Bake 15 minutes in a 350° oven.
Add 1/2 can of onion rings.
Mix well and add rest of can on top of casserole.
Heat molasses.
Remove from heat and add margarine and soda. Add flour, baking powder, salt and ginger gradually.
Chill. Roll very thin on floured board.
Bake at 350° for about 8 to 10 minutes.
Don't overbake.
Store in stone jar and they will become very soft.
Grind the following ingredients: onions, cabbage, green tomatoes and green peppers.
Pour salt over mixture and let stand 2 hours.
Pour in greased and floured 9 x 13-inch pan.
Bake at 325° for 40 to 50 minutes.
Frost with cream cheese frosting.

i: 1 lb. boned chicken, cut into bite-size pieces
d: Saute chicken in butter.
Add 2 cups chicken bouillon.
Add apples and onions.
Add curry powder.
Simmer about 1 hour or until good consistency to serve over rice.
Serve over white rice. Salted peanuts or chutney are nice, served on the side.

i: 2 to 3 lb. chicken parts or cutlets
d: Skin chicken, combine all ingredients and marinate chicken for as little as 15 minutes to as much as overnight.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes to 1 hour.
Buy two salmon steaks from the fresh seafood section of the supermarket.
Rinse with cold water and pat dry.
Preheat broiler (if using Jenn-Air, spray grill with Pam).
Lightly sprinkle both sides with garlic salt and fresh lime 15 minutes before cooking. Use more lime during cooking to prevent drying.
Turn twice at least.
Broil for a total of 10 to 12 minutes.

i: 2/3 c. thick and chunky salsa or picante sauce
1 (8 oz.) can dark red kidney beans, rinsed and drained
avocado slices, sour cream and chopped cilantro (optional toppings)
d: Cook sausage in half lengthwise.
Cut crosswise into 1/4-inch slices.
In a 10-inch skillet, combine rice, sausage and remaining ingredients except optional toppings.
Mix well, breaking up tomatoes with large spoon.
Bring to boil.
Reduce heat.

i: 1 pkg. (2.3 oz.) Wyler's sweetened dry lemonade
d: Mix above ingredients together and store in airtight container.
Skin and bone the chicken.
Cut into bite size pieces.
Mix all other ingredients, in a blender if you wish, until smooth. Put the chicken in a shallow pan and pour the mixture of spices over the top.
Marinate, covered, in a cool place (refrigerator) for no less than 2 hours, the longer the better.
Place the chicken on skewers and cook over grill or under the broiler until cooked through.
Enjoy!
Unroll crescent roll dough and separate into 4 rectangles, pinch edges to seal.
Stir together sugar, powdered sugar, and cinnamon.
Sprinkle evenly over rectangles.
Roll up, jelly roll fashion, starting with long side.
Press edges to seal.
Cut each log into 5 slices and place in lightly greased 8 in. round cake pan.
Mix well, refrigerate and serve with vegetables.
You will need a large serving bowl.

i: 1 can Ro-Tel tomatoes and green chilies
d: Brown meat; drain.
Add seasoning and water.
Simmer for 5 to 10 minutes.
Prepare rice.
Melt cheese and Ro-Tel tomatoes; mix well.
Combine meat, rice and cheese dip.
Place chicken in pan skin down.
Combine juice with soy sauce, and pour over chicken.
Put in oven at 350 degrees for 30 minutes.
Turn chicken
over and bake 30 minutes.
Save peaches for a cobbler.

i: 1 (28 oz.) can tomatoes, cut up and undrained
d: In Dutch oven, cook beef, sausage, onion and garlic.
Drain. Add next 7 ingredients.
Simmer 30 minutes.
Prepare spaghetti as package directs.
Drain.
Serve sauce over hot spaghetti.
Combine flour, salt and soda.
Cream the butter and sugar. Beat in egg and vanilla. Stir in nuts and chips (dough is stiff). Make small balls and flatten either with fingers or fork.
Bake at 350° about 13 minutes or until golden in color. Do not overbake. They will start to flatten out a little.

i: 2 cans (16 oz. each) whole tomatoes, undrained and coarsely chopped
1 can (15 oz.) kidney beans, rinsed and drained
d: In a large saucepan brown hamburger with onion, green pepper and garlic; drain.
Add remaining ingredients and bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover and simmer 30 minutes, stirring occasionally. Makes 8 servings.
Place Brie on large serving plate.
Melt butter and brown sugar in saucepan; add corn syrup and flour, stirring well. Bring to boil; reduce heat and simmer 5 minutes, stirring constantly.
Remove from heat; cool to lukewarm.
Gradually stir in milk.
Pour caramel over Brie.
Sprinkle with pecans.
Preheat oven to 425°.
Brush meat with olive oil.
Rub salt and pepper over meat.
Pour wine over meat.
Bake at 425° for 15 minutes, then reduce heat to 350° for 1 1/4 hours.
Day before, cook rice, water and salt.
Cool.
Cut chicken or turkey into chunks.
Refrigerate.
Brown ground beef and drain.
Finely chop yellow onion (possibly 1/2 the onion) and add to ground beef.
Dice black olives, green onion and tomatoes.
Grate the cheese.
Heat up refried beans on medium heat.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Put all ingredients into a liquidiser and switch on!
When light and frothy, chill in fridge until ready to eat.
Put first 5 ingredients in saucepan.
Cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until sugar is dissolved.
Cook until a soft ball is formed when dropped in cup of cold water.
Remove from heat and cool until lukewarm.
Beat until mixture loses its gloss. Stir in nuts and vanilla.
Pour in buttered 8-inch pan.

Cut 1-inch thick slice from top of pineapple, leaving green leaves on top. Reserve top. With curved knife, cut out fruit from shell, leaving 1/2-inch wall. Cut fruit into cubes, removing any fibrous core.
Mix the ingredients with beater.
Pour into well-greased and floured 9 x 13-inch pan.
Dissolve Jell-O in boiling water; mix Kool-Aid with water and sugar.
Stir well.
Add pineapple juice and Jell-O.
Refrigerate until ready to serve, then mix in 7-Up and sherbet just before serving.
Yield: 35 servings.
Soak cucumbers (covered with water) and salt for 3 hours. Rinse and drain.
Chop cucumbers, peppers and onions.
Add remaining ingredients.
Boil for 20 minutes.
Seal in pint jars which have been sterilized.
Great on hot dogs and in salads.
Boil the sugar and water until thread stage.
Beat egg whites until stiff.
Pour boiling mixture into egg whites and continue beating.
May brown in oven slightly.

i: 1/2 c. drained canned chickpeas, kidney beans or pinto beans
1 (10 1/2 oz.) can Campbell's condensed beef broth
d: In a 2-quart saucepan over medium high heat cook bacon until crisp.
Add garlic, coriander and cumin.
Cook 1 minute, stirring constantly.

i: 1 (8 oz.) jar Pace picante sauce or salsa
d: Stir Velveeta and salsa in saucepan over low heat until Velveeta is melted.
Stir in cilantro.
Serve hot with tortilla chips or vegetable dippers.
Makes 3 cups.
Place beans in a saucepan and cover with water; bring to a boil.
Cook, uncovered, for 8 to 10 minutes or until crisp-tender. Meanwhile, in a skillet, saute the sesame seeds and garlic in oil.
Drain beans; stir into garlic mixture.
Season with salt. Makes 6 servings.
In a greased 2-quart baking dish, arrange potatoes in overlapping rows.
Sprinkle with cheese; spoon soup mixture over top.
Cover with foil.
Bake at 400° for 45 minutes.
Uncover. Bake 10 minutes or until potatoes are fork-tender.
Blend by shaking in a jar and letting stand for several hours before use. Always shake before using. For extra tang, add 1 teaspoon of Dijon mustard. Contains 33 calories per tablespoon.

i: 1/2 lb. smoked sausage, cut into 1/4-inch slices and halved
1/2 c. green or red pepper strips
d: Mix together salad dressing, mustard and milk until well blended; set aside.
Combine sausage, peppers and onions in a medium bowl.
Microwave on High for 2 minutes.
Stir in squash. Continue microwaving on High for 2 minutes or until vegetables are crisp-tender.
Add salad dressing mixture; mix lightly.
Microwave on Medium (50%) for 2 to 3 minutes or until thoroughly heated, stirring after 2 minutes (do not overcook).
Mix all
ingredients well.
Pour into unbaked pie shells and cook until
done;
approximately
1 hour in oven at 350° or 375°.
Combine all ingredients in large nonmetallic bowl and chill overnight.
Serve soup cold with dollop of sour cream on top of each service.
Pass croutons.
Serves approximately 6 to 8.

i: 1 Tbsp. chopped, fresh rosemary or 1 tsp. dried rosemary
d: Whisk together until well combined.
Use with halibut dish or use to make garlic bread.
Cook macaroni until tender.
Drain.
Put into dish and mix in 1 small carton cottage cheese.
Brown butter in skillet and pour over mixture.
Salt and pepper to taste.
Serve.
Grind vegetables; sprinkle with 1/2 cup salt.
Let stand at least 4 hours.
Rinse and drain; then add the remaining ingredients to the ground vegetable mixture.
Simmer about 10 minutes.
Put in jars and seal.
In bowl, combine cornmeal, salt and soda.
In medium bowl, beat eggs; chop onions and jalapeno peppers.
Grate cheese and add beaten eggs, onions, peppers, cheese, oil, corn and crawfish tails. Combine this mixture with cornmeal mix and mix well.
Pour into greased 12 x 14-inch baking dish.
Bake at 375° for 55 minutes or until golden brown.
Fry bacon in a 9-inch skillet until crisp; remove bacon and add all the mentioned ingredients.
Cover and cook approximately 9 minutes.
Remove from skillet and crumble crisp bacon on top. Serves 4.
Saute onion, add beef and brown.
Cook bacon.
Mix all ingredients together and bake for 1 hour at 350°.

i: enough milk to make a little thinner than pancake batter
d: Preheat oven to 450°.
Heat oil in skillet.
Mix corn meal, flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, egg and milk.
Pour oil into corn meal batter and stir.
Pour back into skillet.
Bake about 20 minutes or until golden brown.

i: 6 slices bacon, cooked and cut up
d: Mix all together and bake at 325° for 2 1/2 hours in a casserole dish, removing cover last 30 minutes.
Peel and core apples.
Cut into quarters or eighths.
Slice one gallon of apples.
Put in a large churn.
Put 2 tablespoons sulphur in a teacup and heat on live coals.
Drop it on the sulphur that is in a cup, that's burning good or has a good blaze.
Combine zucchini, onions and salt and let stand overnight. Next day, drain the mixture and rinse twice.
Add remaining ingredients; cook for 30 minutes.
Seal in hot jars.
Use a boiling hot water bath for 5 minutes.
Makes 6 pints.
Mix all together and simmer 1 hour or until thickened.
Seal in jars.
Beat all together.
Spoon in muffin pan.
Bake at 375° for 20 to 25 minutes.
Cook beans until partially tender; drain and saute in olive oil, 1 clove garlic and oregano.
Add salt and pepper.
Cook until tender.
Mix well and chill.
Can be served with cashews on top.

i: a big Tbsp. glop of tahini
d: Blend in the blender, pushing down with a spatula.
Turn into a bowl.
Pour a thick layer of olive oil over the top, 1/4 inch deep, then a layer of lemon juice.
Sprinkle with chopped parsley. Serve with pita bread as a dip or drop into falafels inside the pita bread with chopped tomatoes and alfalfa sprouts.
In a greased 7 x 11-inch deep dish, combine tomatoes, 1/2 can onions, green peppers and pepper sauce.
Break eggs over sauce. Top with cheese and olives.
Bake, uncovered, for 15 to 20 minutes at 400° or until eggs are poached.
Bake a minute or so longer until onions are golden. Serve with tortilla chips.
Fry some of the fat from bacon.
Roll chestnuts with bacon. Anchor with toothpick.
Cover with sugar, chili sauce and mayonnaise.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Boil until syrup reaches 300° on candy thermometer.
Take from heat.
Add 1 tablespoon soda.
Pour on a buttered cookie sheet. When hard, break in pieces and dip.

i: 1 c. Bisquick or Jiffy mix (self-rising flour)
d: Mix all together.
Spread into a greased 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish.
Bake at 350° for 30 to 35 minutes, until brown.
Let cool, cut into squares.

i: 1 can cherry or blueberry pie filling
d: Grease baking dish.
Add crushed pineapple, pie filling, yellow cake mix and nuts in layers.
Pour melted margarine over top.
Bake at 350° for 1 hour.
Mix all ingredients together well.
Place in baking pan.
Bake at 350° until well done.
Combine ingredients.
Add scallops and toss to coat.
Cover and refrigerate 1 hour (stir occasionally).
Remove scallops from marinade.
Reserve.
Thread scallops on 4 skewers.
*Add to own tastes.
Saute meat and drain on paper towel.
Combine all ingredients in large stewpot.
Cook until okra turns color.
Cube apple.
Blend with milk on medium setting.
Add ice cubes, one at a time, with blender on liquify setting.
When in slush form, add sugar substitute and blend for 15 seconds on high speed.
Makes 1 serving.
Combine all ingredients except meat with enough water to cover. Bring to a rolling boil, then simmer for about 3 hours, adding choice of meat in last 20 minutes.
(The use of fresh herbs enhances flavor.)

i: 1 (10 oz.) pkg. chopped spinach, thawed and drained
d: In a large bowl, combine flour, salt and baking powder.
Stir in beaten eggs, milk and butter.
Add remaining ingredients and stir until moistened.
Pour into lightly greased 9 x 13-inch pan. Bake at 350° for 35 minutes.
Cut into 1-inch squares.
Makes 2 dozen.
Mix ground beef with egg.
Add remaining ingredients except flour in order listed; mix well.
Form into loaf.
Place in baking pan. Rub small amount of flour on top of loaf. Bake in preheated 350° oven for about 45 minutes or until done.
Yields 6 servings.
Mix the butter until smooth; slowly add the sugar, eggs and the mixture of flour, cornstarch and baking powder.
Add the lemon extract.
Pour batter into greased loaf pan.
Bake 60 to 75 minutes in 350° oven.
Prepare eggs as you
desire
for omelette.
You may add crumbled Feta cheese to eggs
and
mix

i: 2 cans clams with juice, chopped or minced
d: Soften garlic in oil.
Add all other ingredients; saute over low flame for approximately 15 to 20 minutes.
Mix with linguini and serve.
Mix all dry ingredients.
Cut in shortening.
Add milk.
Knead until smooth.
Roll out to 1/2-inch thickness on well-floured surface.
Cut biscuits out with a jar lid or floured glass.
Mix well, store in airtight container.

i: 1 (15 oz.) box Mueselix, Fruit and Fibre or Basic Four
d: Mix cereal, sugar, flour, soda and salt.
Add eggs or Egg Beater equivalent, buttermilk and oil.
Mix in large bowl.
Batter lasts 6 weeks or so in refrigerator.
Use whatever amount you need to make muffins for any specific time.
Bake at 400° for 15 minutes.
Combine first 4 ingredients.
Stir in beaten egg whites and almond extract; mix well.
Lightly grease cookie sheet.
Cut cherries in quarters.
Drop by teaspoonful onto cookie sheet about 1-inch apart. Top with cherry.
Bake at 325° for 20 to 25 minutes. Take out when edges are golden.

i: 1 bunch green onions, chopped finely (about 1 c. total)
d: Mash avocados.
Add lemon juice, salt and pepper.
In separate bowl, mix sour cream, mayonnaise and taco seasoning.
Refrigerate both mixtures.
Mix first 3 ingredients together and roll out to fit a cookie sheet.
Peel and slice apples; crush cornflakes and sprinkle on pastry.
Place apples in row until sheet is filled, then make a mixture of 3/4 cup sugar and 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon and sprinkle on apples.
Dot with butter.
Bake at 400° for approximately 15 minutes.
Beat egg, milk, vanilla and 2 tablespoons sugar; pour over apples.

i: 1 jalapeno (1/3 for mild, 2/3 for medium and all for hot and spicy)
d: Put all of the ingredients in your food processor and mix to a consistency desired.
Medium thickness is best.
Serve with tortilla chips.
Mix all ingredients together.
Let sit in refrigerator several hours before serving.
Serve with raw cauliflower, broccoli, carrots, celery, mushrooms and cherry tomatoes.
Blend all ingredients in blender until smooth.
Sprinkle with nutmeg.
Add ice cream.

i: 3 (10 oz.) cans tomatoes and green chiles
d: Dissolve jello in water; add tomatoes and mold.
Dissolve yeast with sugar in 1/4 cup water.
Let set for 5 minutes to proof.
In large bowl, mix 2 cups flour with yeast mixture together; add remaining water and flour as needed.
Mix by hand or electric mixer with dough hook.
Knead until smooth and elastic.
Put in greased bowl to rise.
Soak chicken in mixture of remaining ingredients for several hours or overnight.
Cook or grill.
Bring to boil the last 5 ingredients.
Pour mixture over fruit.
Put in oven for 20 minutes at 350°.
Serve warm.

i: 1/2 c. coarsely chopped walnuts or pecans
d: Cream butter and sugar, then beat until light and lemon colored.
Beat in eggs.
Sift together dry ingredients.
Add alternately to the creamed mixture with milk.
Stir in apples, nuts and vanilla.
Pour into greased 9 x 5 x 3-inch loaf pan. Bake at 350° for 50 to 60 minutes until knife inserted comes out clean.
Saut onion; add broth and remaining ingredients. Bring to a boil. Cover. Reduce heat. Simmer 20 minutes.
Chop onion, celery and eggs together.
Add beans, mayonnaise, relish and other seasonings.
Refrigerate.
Serves 6.

i: 1 tsp. salt or chicken bouillon cube
d: Heat oil and garlic on high.
Stir in rice to coat with oil. Add water (it will sizzle).
Cook on high to boil off water (about 8 to 10 minutes).
Uncover and stir the liquid.
It should be almost gone.
Turn off heat and let stand.
Melt butter in heavy saucepan.
Add brown sugar and cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until sugar dissolves.
Do not boil. Remove from heat.
Stir in whipping cream.
Add powdered sugar and vanilla; beat at high speed until smooth.
Frost 1 (10-inch) cake.

i: 2 whole chicken breasts, skinned, boned and cut into strips
d: Drain oranges, reserving 2 tablespoons syrup.
Blend syrup with stir-fry sauce.
Heat 2 tablespoons oil in wok or skillet (high heat).
Add chicken, cashews and garlic; stir-fry for 3 minutes.
Remove from pan.
Heat remaining oil in same pan.
In a shallow bowl, beat the egg white until foamy; add milk and mix well.
In another shallow bowl, combine the cornmeal, salt, garlic powder, cayenne and pepper.
Dip fillets in milk mixture, then coat with cornmeal mixture.
Heat 1/4-inch of oil in a large skillet; fry fish over medium-high for 3 to 4 minutes per side or until it flakes easily with a fork.
Yield: 4 servings.
Melt butterscotch morsels. Add nuts and stir. Add noodles and stir.
Drop small spoonfuls onto waxed paper.
Slice cucumbers and onions. Layer cucumbers and onions.
Boil vinegar, sugar, salt, mustard seed, celery seed and turmeric. Pour over cucumbers.
When cool, refrigerate.

t: Spinach Salad With Oranges And Warm Goat Cheese
i: 6 oz. fresh mild goat cheese (preferably in log form)
1 small red onion, sliced into thin rings
d: Preheat oven to 350°. Cut peel and white pith from oranges with a sharp small knife. Working over a sieve set over a bowl, cut orange sections free from membranes, letting sections drop into sieve. Cut goat cheese into fourths and gently form into a disk. Dip into pecans all over each disk to coat. Bake on a small baking sheet in middle of oven until heated through, about 5 minutes. Measure out 1 tablespoon orange juice from bowl and whisk together with mustard, vinegar, salt and sugar in a large bowl. Add oil and whisk until blended. Add spinach, onion and orange sections to dressing and toss well. Season with pepper. Divide among 4 salad plates and carefully transfer 1 goat-cheese disk to each salad. Yield: 4 (first-course) servings. Prep Time: 15 minutes. Cook Time: 10 minutes.
Mix dry pudding mix, pineapple with juice, marshmallows and pecans. Add Cool Whip and refrigerate 1 to 2 hours before serving.

i: 4 boneless pork loin chops, each 3/4-inch thick
d: On waxed paper, combine flour, curry powder, salt and pepper. Use to coat both sides of pork chops.
In 10-inch skillet over medium-high heat, in hot salad oil, cook pork chops 7 to 8 minutes until tender and golden brown on both sides, turning pork chops once.
Makes 4 main dish servings.
Brown meat in oil in electric skillet or large frying pan. Add celery and onion; cover and cook slowly 10 minutes.
Add tomato paste, water, noodles, cheese, olives, salt and pepper; stir to combine.
Cook slowly until noodles are tender, about 30 minutes.
Run nuts and raisins through food chopper.
Cream sugar, margarine, eggs and milk.
Add dry ingredients.
Add jam, nuts and raisins.
Pour in 3 or 4 cake pans that have been sprayed.
Bake at 350°.

i: 1 can (14.5 oz.) diced tomatoes with basil, garlic, and oregano
2 cups (8 oz.) shredded mozzarella cheese, divided
d: Preheat oven to 425°.
Coat a 9x13 baking dish with nonstick cooking spray.
In a medium skillet, brown the ground beef over medium-high heat, stirring and crumbling the beef until no pink remains; drain.
Add the tomatoes to the beef; cook until heated through.
Meanwhile, unroll the pizza crust; press over the bottom and halfway up the side of the baking dish.
Sprinkle 1 cup mozzarella cheese over the crust then top with the meat mixture. Bake uncovered for 12 minutes.
Chop cabbage fine.
Add salt.
Let stand 10 to 15 minutes, then squeeze liquid out.
Cook noodles in salted water.
Drain and rinse in cold water.
In a large skillet, saute cabbage in butter until golden and transparent.
Shell
and,
if necessary, devein shrimp.
Slice garlic and ginger
into
thin	slices;

i: your favorite barbecue sauce (we like Heinz 57)
d: Layer chicken pieces in a 9 x 13-inch baking dish.
Sprinkle salt, pepper, garlic and onion powder to your taste.
Place in microwave oven and cook chicken until done (around 30 to 40 minutes depending on your oven).
Remove chicken and place on heated outdoor grill.
Baste barbecue sauce over it and put lid down.
It only has to stay on the grill for a few minutes to get the smoked flavor without all of the time and trouble of regular grilled chicken.
Combine first 6 ingredients in a large bowl.
Rub into ribs and let stand 15 minutes.
Place onion, celery and parsley in roaster.
Add bay leaf, ribs, and rest of seasoning.
Add chicken broth, wine and Worcestershire sauce.
Cover; roast at 350° for 2 1/2 hours.

i: 1 (6 oz.) can spicy tomato juice or V-8
d: In a large skillet, cook ground beef, onion and garlic.
Drain. Meanwhile in a crock-pot combine remaining ingredients except buns and cheese.
Stir in meat mixture; cover and cook on low heat for 10 to 12 hours or on high setting for 3 to 5 hours.
Toast buns and spread meat mixture over buns.
Sprinkle with cheese.
Mix all ingredients.
Drop in muffin pan.
Bake in 350 degree oven 10 to 12 minutes.
Mix sugar and Jell-O together.
Add boiling water; mix well. Add pineapple and orange juice.
Freeze in plastic 1 gallon containers or 1 gallon plastic bags.
When ready to serve, add ginger ale.

i: 2 Tbsp. white wine with tarragon vinegar
d: Will marinate up to 2 pounds salmon.
Marinate two or more hours before grilling.
Grill on Reynolds Wrap on medium heat 20 minutes on one side.
Brush on marinade while cooking.
Turn over and grill other side 20 minutes or until "flaky."
Serve immediately.

i: 1 (No. 2) can sliced or diced carrots, drained
d: Combine carrots, peppers, onion, sugar and garlic salt; mix well.
Add vinegar and olive oil; let stand overnight.
Serve as a salad or a vegetable.
Combine first 6 ingredients in medium saucepan. Simmer 15 minutes.
Combine next 6 ingredients; mix well.
Shape into 10 patties about 3/4 inch thick.
Cook patties over medium heat in a large skillet until browned, turning once.
Arrange patties in a 13 x 9-inch baking dish.
Pour ketchup mixture over patties.

i: 1 (4 oz.) pkg. Jell-O instant pudding (any flavor)
d: Combine pudding mix, sugar and milk. Beat slowly with electric mixer until well blended. Fold in Cool Whip. Spread on cake at once. Makes about 4 cups or enough for two 9-inch layer cakes.

i: 2 to 3 Tbsp. cornstarch, added to cold water to dissolve
d: In wok, fry chicken breasts in Wesson oil.
Sprinkle with garlic powder.
Remove excess oil when done with paper towels.
In separate pan, cook Knorr soup and bouillon cubes about 20 minutes. Thicken with cornstarch.
Cut up vegetables and add with the soup to chicken in wok.
Heat together while stirring.

i: 1 lb. beef round or cubed steak
d: Combine flour, celery salt and pepper.
Pound into meat.
Cook in hot oil until brown.
Mix sliced onion, undrained tomatoes and remaining ingredients.
Put meat in baking dish.
Add tomato mixture.
Mix together and baste chicken while cooking.
Peel and devein the shrimp.
Mince the garlic and chop the scallion.
Squeeze 2 tablespoons of juice from the lemon.
Heat the butter and oil in a large frying pan over medium heat.
Add the shrimp, garlic and 1 teaspoon salt.
Saute, stirring, until the shrimp turns pink, 3 to 4 minutes.

i: 1 (12 oz.) can pet milk (can use fat free)
d: In 9 X 13 dish spree fritos or doritos to cover well.
Brown and mix hamburger and onion.
Add rotel, soups and milk.
Pour over chips, cover with cheese and bake at 350 degrees until bubbly (25 to 30 minutes).

i: 2 tsp. each: fresh lemon juice and white wine vinegar
8 oz. frozen cooked tiny shrimp, thawed and well drained
d: Bring 1 inch of water to a boil in large skillet.
Slice scallops in and simmer 1 1/2 minutes or until opaque halfway through.
Drain and spread out on a large plate to cool quickly. Put oil, lemon juice, vinegar, mustard, salt, tarragon, pepper and sugar in small bowl or jar with tight-fitting lid.
Whisk or shake until well blended.
Toss scallops, shrimp and half each of the chives and parsley with half the dressing in a medium size bowl to coat well.
Chill at least 2 hours or up to 24 hours.

i: 6 oz. can frozen orange juice, thawed
d: Mix
all
ingredients except coconut.
Shape into small balls and roll in coconut.
Makes about 8 dozen.

i: 5 c. tomatoes, skinned and chopped in food processor
d: Brown ground beef and drain thoroughly.
Combine remaining ingredients, except cheese, in crock-pot.
Add beef.
Add water until crock-pot is 2/3 full.
Cook on low at least 5 hours.
Top with grated cheese and serve with corn muffins.
Combine all ingredients and spread in a 9 x 13 inch greased baking dish.
Bake in a 325° oven for about 25 to 30 minutes or until slightly golden brown.
Serve warm with Fritos, chips of choice, or crackers.

i: 1 large or small size pkg. peach flavored gelatin
1 (20 oz.) can crushed pineapple (do not drain)
d: Mix dry gelatin and undrained pineapple in saucepan.
Heat until gelatin melts.
Cool slightly.
Add buttermilk and fold in whipped topping.
Pour into prepared mold and refrigerate.
Whisk together and refrigerate for 2 hours.
Cook all ingredients for
minutes,
stirring at all times. Put in jars and seal.

i: 2 c. olive oil (the amount of oil needed will vary depending on whether you use a frying pan or a frier and on their sizes)
d: Coat the olive with egg and flour.
Fry them using plenty of hot oil until golden brown.
Drain them on paper towels.
Serve warm.
Serves 4.
Separate each wedge into chips.
Place in ice water.
Beat together the milk and egg.
Remove chips from water; dip in flour, then egg mixture.
Dip in bread crumbs and seasoning.
Fry in hot oil until brown; drain.
Mix first 4 ingredients together.
Place chicken on top.
Put a pat of butter on each piece of chicken; season to taste.
Bake for 1 hour at 375°.

i: 4 to 5 c. chicken broth (use water from boiling chicken)
d: In big de'pot, melt butter.
Saute onions, green pepper, garlic and seasonings.
Add broth and tomatoes.
Simmer 20 minutes.
Add chicken (cubed) and sausage (chunked).
Simmer 20 to 30 minutes.
Mix sugar and flour.
Beat together eggs and juice, add to dry mixture and cook in double boiler until thick.
Stir constantly. Cool.

t: Willie'S Homemade Boiled Chicken And Rice
d: Boil chicken thighs for 1 hour and 45 minutes.
Stir in 2 cups rice.
Cook, covered, for 20 minutes longer.
Ready to serve.

i: 1/2 lb. carrots, peeled, cut into 4 x 1/2-inch strips
1/2 lb. turnips, peeled, cut into 4 x 1/2-inch strips
d: In skillet, boil 1/4 cup broth with garlic, ginger and pepper flakes until almost all liquid evaporates.
Add vegetables and 3/4 cup chicken broth; cover and cook 4 to 6 minutes or just until vegetables are crisp-tender.

i: 5 oz. water chestnuts, drained and chopped
d: Combine all ingredients; shape into very small balls (scant teaspoonfuls).
Brown in shortening.

i: 1/2 c. each: green pepper, celery and cucumber
d: Finely chop all vegetables.
Combine all ingredients in a stainless steel or glass bowl.
Cover.
Chill at least 4 hours. Serve in chilled bowls.
Serves 6.
Bring the apple cider, cloves, allspice, cinnamon and sugar to a boil.
Lower heat.
Cover and simmer for 20 minutes. Strain punch and pour into heatproof bowl. Add orange slices.
Makes 16 cups.
Mix all together. Bake in Bundt pan for 45 to 55 minutes at 350° until done.
Dissolve gelatin in white wine over hot water. Combine with remaining ingredients.
Place in a lightly oiled loaf tin, pressing down well. Cover with foil and allow to set overnight in refrigerator.

i: 1 c. Karo light or dark corn syrup
2 Tbsp. Mazola margarine or butter, melted
d: In large bowl, stir together first 5 ingredients until well blended.
Stir in nuts.
Pour into pastry shell.
Bake at 350° for 50 to 55 minutes or until knife inserted halfway between center and edge comes out clean.
Cool.
Serves 8.
Mix cornmeal, flour, salt and soda.
Add buttermilk, eggs, oil and corn.
Beat and mix well.
Stir in peppers and green onions.

i: 1 can undrained pork and beans in sauce
d: Cook bacon until crisp.
Remove and drain, leave grease in skillet and add brown sugar, dry mustard, salt and vinegar. Slice and ring onions.
Cook all together in pan for 20 minutes. Drain all beans except pork and beans.
Mix onion mixture with beans.
Place in 3 quart casserole dish, crumble bacon on top. Bake 1 hour at 350°.
Serves 12.

t: Mexican Pork Or Beef Chile Verde(Microwave)
d: In 2-quart casserole dish, combine onion and garlic.
Cover. Microwave High 2 minutes.
Stir in meat in cubes, flour, salt and pepper.
Add tomatoes and green chiles.
Stir to break up tomatoes.
Cover.

i: 1 (12 oz.) pkg. Nestles chocolate chips or butterscotch
1/3 c. crushed chow mein noodles (leave chunky)
d: Melt chocolate pieces in double boiler until it melts, add peanuts and stir in noodles.
Mix until well covered.
Drop by teaspoonfuls onto a waxed paper lined cookie sheet.
Chill in refrigerator 15 to 30 minutes.
Place bagel halves on baking sheet.
Top with remaining ingredients.
Broil until cheese melts.
Dissolve Jell-O in boiling water and let cool.
Stir in next 7 ingredients and spoon into lightly oiled 4-cup mold.
Cover and chill until firm.
Unmold on lettuce leaves and garnish with fresh cranberries and orange rind strips, if desired.

i: 3 6 1/2 oz. cans minced or chopped clams
d: Rinse clams under running water.
Put clams and reserved liquid in medium saucepan.
Add 1/4 c. water or clam juice.
Add parsley, garlic and olive oil and stir.
Bring to a boil and then simmer over medium heat for 10 minutes.
Put linguine in large pasta bowl.

i: 28 oz. bottle ginger ale or lemon-lime soda, chilled
d: *Mix each with proper amount of water and chill.
Melt
butter
until soft; add curry powder and paprika. Remove from
heat
and blend well.
Stir in mayonnaise and mix well. Serve over asparagus.
Take 2 fish, cooked, and 5 loaves of bread.
Bless before God in the name of Jesus.
Break into pieces and serve.
Feeds about 5 thousand!
St.
Stem and wash grapes.
To each 5 pounds grapes, add 2 cups water.
Cover and let come slowly to a boil; simmer until well cooked.
Remove from heat and place in cloth sack to drain.

i: 3/4 c. crushed juice pack pineapple, drained
d: Scrub potatoes and prick with fork.
Bake in 400° oven 1 hour or microwave 8 minutes until tender.
Halve potatoes lengthwise and scoop out pulp.
Mash and combine with a mixture of the remaining ingredients.
Pile into shells.
Bake in 350° oven 15 minutes until potatoes are heated through.
Brown ground meat; drain.
Heat tortillas until soft, if you want to roll enchiladas.
The fast way is to layer in a greased casserole dish, tortillas, meat, onions and cheese until used up. Finish with tortillas.
Mix and heat remaining ingredients.
Pour over casserole.
Bake at 350° for about 30 minutes.

t: Asparagus And Yellow Bell Pepper With Balsamic Vinaigrette(6 Servings)
i: 1/4 c. balsamic vinegar or 3 Tbsp. red wine vinegar
1 large yellow bell pepper, finely chopped
d: Cook asparagus in large pot of boiling salted water until tender, but still firm to bite, about 3 minutes.
Drain.
Transfer asparagus to bowl of ice water and cool.
Drain well.

i: 1/2 c. lime juice (key lime if possible)
d: Preheat oven to 250°.
Beat egg yolks; add condensed milk and lime juice and beat until thick.
Pour into baked pie shell.
Mix together.
Make patties and brown.
Place patties in baking dish.
In large skillet crush garlic with salt to a paste.
Add butter and oil; heat.
Add shrimp and fry gently, stirring often, for 5 to 10 minutes (until done).
Remove from heat; add lemon juice and garnish with parsley.
Serves 3 to 4.
Let the first 4 ingredients sit for about 1 hour, then drain off real good.
Mix and pour cucumber mixture with the
last 4 ingredients.
Cook for 45 minutes.
Put in jars and seal.
Preheat oven to 400°.
Grease baking dish with 1 tablespoon of oil.
Toss chicken and potatoes with the remaining 2 tablespoons of oil.
Arrange in dish in an even layer.
Sprinkle with garlic, rosemary and 4 tablespoons lemon juice.
Cover loosely with foil. Bake 30 minutes.
Brown hamburger.
Add all ingredients.
Simmer 3 to 4 hours. Remove garlic clove and bay leaves.
Serves 5 ways:
Place garlic under the skin of the chicken.
Salt and pepper to taste.
Drizzle with a little olive oil.
Bake at 450° to 500° for one hour in covered casserole dish.
Serve with French or sourdough bread.
Mix and roll into two rolls.
Wrap in Saran Wrap and refrigerate 24 hours.
Remove from wrap and place on broiler pan; cook at 300° for 1 1/2 hours.

i: 1 (16 oz.) can kidney beans, rinsed and drained
1 (15 oz.) can black beans, rinsed and drained
d: In a bowl, combine the first six ingredients; mix well.
Add the beans, corn and onion; stir to coat.
Cover and refrigerate until serving.
Yield: 6 servings.

i: 36 Doritos corn chips, plus 1 c. corn chip crumbs
d: Take 4 tablespoons shortening and melt in skillet.
Then add chopped onions and 1 pound ground beef.
Cook until meat loses red color.
Then add 36 Dorito corn chips, 1 cup grated cheese, 1 can enchilada sauce, 2 cans tomato sauce and 1 tomato sauce can of water.
Add salt and pepper to taste and 1 tablespoons comino spice.
Pour into baking dish (8 x 12-inch), greased.
Melt 3/4 cup sugar in a pan until golden brown; let cool.
Season chicken with salt and pepper.
Brown in oil.
Remove chicken and saute onions and garlic until soft.
Add chicken and 20 cups of water.
