Bring to a boil and add salt, pepper and Tabasco.
Add rice.
Mix ingredients together.
Cut and fry in hot grease at 375°. Cook about 1 minute on each side.

i: 1 c. sour cream or fat-free sour cream
d: Mix until well blended.
Serve with fresh, raw vegetables or chips.
Makes 2 1/4 cups.
Put all ingredients in roasting pan in order given.
Cover and bake at 325° to 350° from 8:30 a.m. until you get home from church.
I skin my chicken to remove as much fat as possible. Serve with salad or applesauce.
In a large heavy skillet, heat the oil. Add onion and cook until soft.
Add chicken pieces and brown. Sprinkle with paprika to taste and cover chicken with broth.
Cook, covered, until tender (about 45 minutes).
Remove chicken to heated platter and keep warm. Thicken remaining broth with milk, sour cream and flour, stirring constantly.
Arrange Spoon Dumplings around chicken on platter and serve with prepared gravy. Serves 4.
Skin and bone chicken breast; cut into 1 x 3-inch strips.
In a 10-inch nonstick skillet, combine oil and margarine and heat over medium heat until bubbly and hot.
Dredge chicken in flour; cook in skillet until browned on all sides (3 to 4 minutes). Remove chicken from pan; set aside.
Add shallots and garlic to skillet; saute until softened.
Add water, wine, broth mix and seasonings.
Using a wooden spoon, stir well.
Add water, oil, salt and pepper to beaten eggs.
Mix well. Add meal and stir thoroughly.
Refrigerate one hour.
Form into balls and drop into soup or 1 1/2 quarts of boiling water to which salt has been added.
Cook 20 minutes with lid on.

i: 1 lb. calf liver, thinly sliced, cut in serving pieces
d: Coat liver in mixture of flour, salt and pepper.
Set aside. Saute onion in butter until tender.
Add liver and brown lightly on both sides.
Dissolve bouillon in boiling water.
Add mushrooms, bouillon, wine and basil.
Cover and simmer 15
Quarter and separate onion pieces.
Quarter and then halve green peppers.
Halve tomatoes; divide lamb into 8 portions. Thread each portion on a skewer alternating lamb cubes with vegetables.
Combine remaining ingredients to marinade.
Cover pan that has the skewered lamb.
Refrigerate overnight.
Melt oleo in 9 x 13-inch cake pan.
Mix flour, sugar and milk well.
Pour mixture over melted oleo; add peaches.
Bake at 350° for approximately 30 minutes or until brown on top.
This is very good served hot with ice cream.

i: 6 oz. pkg. butterscotch chips (I substitute one 12 oz. pkg. milk chocolate chips)
d: Combine chips and milk and microwave for 2 1/2 minutes.
Stir and microwave 2 1/2 minutes more.
Add vanilla and pecans.
Pour into greased glass pan and refrigerate.

i: 2 c. (16 to 17 oz. can) canned pumpkin
1 1/3 c. (15 oz. can) Eagle Brand milk
d: In large mixing bowl, blend together all ingredients.
Turn mixture into pie shell.
Bake in 375° oven until sharp bladed knife inserted near center comes out clean, 50 to 55 minutes. Cool.
Refrigerate at least one hour.
Cut bacon strips into 1/2-inch pieces. Fry until done and drain off grease. Add tomatoes, tomato paste and 1 cup water. Cook to a thick sauce.
Boil noodles according to directions. Drain.
Pour sauce over noodles. Top individual servings with generous sprinkling of grated Cheddar cheese.
In a skillet, cook beef and onion until the meat is browned and onion is tender.
Drain.
Stir in the remaining ingredients; bring to boil.
Reduce heat; cover and simmer until macaroni is tender, about 20 minutes.
Stir occasionally, adding additional water if needed.
Yields 4 to 6 servings.
Cook noodles according to package directions; rinse and drain. Melt butter in skillet.
Stir in chuck and cook until it loses its pink color.
Add tomato sauce, salt, garlic salt and pepper. Cover and simmer 5 minutes.
Fold together noodles, cottage cheese, sour cream and onions.

i: 1 large 2 1/2 lb. can pumpkin or 3 c. fresh
1 large yellow or white cake mix
d: Mix first 8 ingredients.
Spread in greased 9 x 13-inch pan. Sprinkle one large white or yellow cake mix "dry" evenly over pumpkin.
Drizzle melted oleo over dry cake.
Bake at 425° for 15 minutes.
Reduce heat to 350° and bake for 45 minutes.
Bake until knife comes out clean.

i: 1 (4 3/4 oz.) jar strained plum baby food
d: Trim any excess fat
from chops.
Brown in 1 tablespoon cooking oil, season meat
with
salt to taste.
Combine plums, coconut, water, vinegar, soy sauce, 1/2 teaspoon salt, ginger, lemon peel and pepper, pour
Mix in the order listed above; pour into the two pie shells and refrigerate. Makes a very rich dessert.

i: 10 slices white bread, cut in 1-inch cubes
d: Grease a 9 x 13-inch baking dish.
In dish, layer bread, bacon and cheese.
Beat together eggs, milk and salt; pour over bread, bacon and cheese layers.
Refrigerate several hours or overnight. Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Cut in squares.

i: 1 large ripe tomato, cored and diced
1 Ds. Tabasco sauce or to taste
d: Cut up stewed tomatoes and combine in saucepan with onions, fresh tomato, salt and pepper. Bring just to a boil. Boil hard 1 minute and remove at once from heat. Put half of mixture through blender just to mince fine, but not to puree. Return to remaining half of mixture. Cool and refrigerate in tightly covered container to use within a few weeks. Freezes well to use within 6 months.
Melt chocolate in microwave, stirring often to avoid scorching.

Add the pretzels and peanuts and stir to coat well. Cover a cookie sheet with foil and spread the mixture on foil. Let cool in refrigerator for at least 30 minutes or until hardened. Lift foil from cookie sheet and peel away from hardened candy. Break candy into bite-sized pieces. Store in air-tight container in a cool, dry place.
Mix zucchini, onions and salt all together and set overnight. Rinse and drain the next day.
Mix remaining ingredients in pan. Cook for 30 minutes, stirring a lot.
Pour into hot jars, then seal.
Make gelatin as directed on package.
Add rest of ingredients except ginger ale and mix thoroughly.
Add ginger ale just before serving.
Sherbet may also be added when ready to serve.

i: sliced fresh mushrooms, drained (canned mushrooms are okay)
d: Layer ingredients in a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan.
Pour envelope of dry onion soup and 2 cups of water over top.
(For richer broth, add another envelope of onion soup and one more cup of water.)
Lay foil over top and bake at 350° for 2 hours.

i: 2 Tbsp. lemon juice or wine vinegar
d: Place in blender until smooth.
Mix and knead until doughy.
*Margarine may be substituted but the flavor is not quite the same.
Cut cauliflower and put in bowl with 2 tablespoons salt.
Do the same with carrots; let stand 2 hours.
Rinse and drain. Combine vinegar, sugar, onion flakes, mustard seed, turmeric and peppercorns.
Bring to a boil; add carrots and return to boil. Simmer 5 minutes.
Add cauliflower; return to boil and simmer 8 minutes.
Put in jars and seal.
Prepare and bake brownie mix according to package directions. Meanwhile, in small saucepan over low heat, melt caramels with milk.
Stir until smooth.
Immediately after removing brownies from oven, sprinkle with chocolate chips and chopped pecans. Drizzle with caramel mixture.
Cool completely before cutting. Makes 24 bars.
Heat oil and cook onions until transparent. Add tomatoes; cook 10 minutes. Add all spices and simmer for 15 minutes.
Crumble beef into sauce, stirring. Cook, covered, for 20 minutes. Add potatoes and cook until almost tender, about 15 minutes. Add frozen peas. Cook for 10 more minutes.
Serve over rice.

i: 1 fryer, cut-up or your favorite pieces of chicken
d: Wash and
clean
chicken.
Salt
and pepper chicken and soak in milk overnight in refrigerator.	Flour and deep fry in Crisco until done.
Mix in order given and bake in loaf pan in moderate oven, 350°.
Break eggs lightly, then add condensed milk to the yolk and beat again.
Add lime juice and beat until smooth.
Pour mixture into a 9-inch baked pastry shell.
Top with meringue and bake in a slow oven until meringue is brown.

i: cornstarch, mixed with water to thicken sauce
1 Tbsp. chicken base or 1 (No. 2) can chicken stock
d: Season hens with salt and pepper.
In saucepan, boil wine until alcohol is vaporized.
Add other ingredients and thicken with cornstarch to desired thickness.
Bake hens 1/2 hour at 400°. Baste hens with sauce to prevent drying out.
Serve hens with sauce.
Serves 2.
Heat oven to 425°.
Grease bottoms of 12 medium muffin cups. Blend all ingredients about 20 seconds.
Beat 1 minute.
Fill muffin cups 2/3 full.
Bake 15 minutes; immediately remove from pan.
Makes 12 muffins.
Combine bread crumbs, garlic and salt in a large zip-top plastic bag, shaking well to combine.
Combine lemon juice and oil. Brush over both sides of chicken.
Place chicken, one piece at a time, in the bag, shaking to coat.
Remove chicken from bag and place in 12 x 8 x 2-inch baking dish coated with vegetable cooking spray.
Sprinkle remaining crumbs over chicken.
Bake, uncovered, at 375° for 45 minutes or until done.
Put all ingredients in layers except for mayonnaise, Miracle Whip and cheese.
Take mayonnaise and Miracle Whip and spread over top and sprinkle with cheese.
Let set 12 to 24 hours.
Mix together and serve.

t: Pork Cutlets With Curried Cream Sauce
i: 1 1/2 tsp. curry powder or to taste
1 tsp. cornstarch, dissolved in 1 Tbsp. cold water
d: Pat the pork cutlets dry and sprinkle both sides with the curry powder and salt and pepper.
In a skillet, cook the onions in 1/2 stick of the butter over moderately low heat, stirring occasionally, for 20 minutes.
In a large ovenproof skillet, brown the cutlets in the remaining 1/2 stick butter over moderately high heat for 3 minutes on each side and stir in the onions, the chutney and the cream.
Bake the mixture, covered, in a preheated 350° oven for 30 minutes.
Bring the sauce to a boil; swirl in the cornstarch mixture and cook the sauce over moderate heat, stirring, until it has the desired consistency.
Serve the dish with rice.

i: 1 pound fresh or frozen haddock fillets
d: Thaw fish if frozen.
Cut fish into 1-inch pieces.
To make marinade:
combine oil, soy sauce, scallions, garlic, brown sugar, dried mustard and black pepper.
Place fish pieces in marinade. Cover and let stand at room temperature for 1 hour.

i: 3 fully ripe bananas, peeled and crushed
d: Cream shortening, sugar and eggs thoroughly.
Add bananas. Beat well.
Blend flour, soda, salt and cinnamon.
Beat into banana mixture until blended.
Mix in pecans (if using).
Spread each tortilla with 2 tablespoons of horseradish sauce. Place 2 slices of roast beef and one slice of cheese on each tortilla.
Divide onion, tomato and greens among tortillas.
Roll up; cut into 1-inch rolls.
Cut bell peppers in half lengthwise; remove core and seeds.
In a medium bowl mix beef, rice, pepper, oregano, garlic powder, tomato sauce, egg and half of the 8 ounce can of tomato sauce; blend well.
Spoon beef mixture into bell pepper halves; top with remaining tomato sauce.
Cover with waxed paper.
Heat in microwave on Roast for 19 to 22 minutes.
Allow to stand 4 minutes, covered, before serving.
Dissolve yeast in warm water; let stand 5 minutes.
Combine flour and next 4 ingredients; make a well in center of mixture. Add yeast mixture, oil and buttermilk; stir until dry ingredients are moistened.
Turn dough out onto a lightly floured surface; roll to 1/2-inch thickness.
Cut with a 2-inch biscuit cutter; place on ungreased baking sheets.
Cover; let rise in a warm place (85°) free from drafts, 1 hour.
Bake at 400° for 15 minutes or until lightly browned.
Mix all ingredients except chicken in a saucepan; bring to boil.
Place chicken in a baking pan; pour mixture over chicken and bake for 1 hour at 325°.

i: 1 can sour cherries, drained and cut in half
d: Mix all ingredients except crusts.
Pour into 2 pie crusts and chill.
Top with additional Cool Whip.
Brown chicken (unfloured) in shortening until golden brown. Remove chicken from skillet and make gravy in small amount of shortening.
In same skillet return chicken and lay peppers and onions over gravy covered chicken.	Simmer for 1 hour to 1 1/2 hours.
Brown chuck and onions.
Drain off grease.
Add remaining ingredients.
Cover and cook 30 minutes to 1 hour.

i: 2/3 c. each: cherry, apricot and pineapple preserves
d: Cream together butter, sugar and eggs.
Sift together flour and spices.
Add flour mixture alternately with buttermilk to which the soda has been added.
Fold in gently the preserves, vanilla and nuts.
Pour into a greased and floured angel food cake pan.
Bake for 1 1/2 hours or until done at 325°.
Mix together and top with crushed Hi Ho crackers. Cook in oven at 350° until bubbly.
Brown beef and drain.
Add 1/2 cup tomato sauce and taco seasoning.
Stir.
Line bottom of 9 x 11 x 2-inch pan with 2 cups corn chips.
Crush remaining chips and set aside.
Spoon meat mixture over corn chips.
Stir-fry chicken in small amount of oil in wok or skillet. Remove.
Stir-fry raw vegetables until transparent.
Mix together rest of ingredients.
Put chicken pieces, vegetables and liquid together in skillet and thicken.
Serve on rice and Chinese noodles.
Mix together and let stand overnight.
Serve with carrots, cauliflower, bell pepper, radishes, celery and tiny green onions.
Beat eggs and sugar together.
Add oleo and mix.
Add other ingredients.
Add vinegar last.
Pour into unbaked pie shell and bake at 300° for 45 minutes.
Heat and let come to boil.
Salt and arrange chicken in Pyrex, greased well.
Pour sauce over chicken.
Sprinkle heavily with flour and dot with butter and pepper.
Cook at 400° until bubbly. Then turn to 300° to 325° for 1 hour to 1 1/2 hours, according to size of chicken pieces.
Turn good side up first and turn over about halfway through.
In saucepan, melt
butter.
When
butter foams, stir in flour, salt and pepper.
Cook and stir 2 minutes until mixture bubbles. Add milk all at once.
Stir constantly while mixture thickens. Makes 2 1/2 cups sauce.
Cook until the berries pop the cranberries, raisins, sugar, water and spices.
Add celery and apple and continue to cook until mixture thickens and apples are cooked.
Makes about one quart.
Fry seasoned pork chops in iron skillet, using the 1 tablespoon of olive oil, until golden brown.
Cook rice separately.
Slice onion, bell pepper and tomato.
Place pork chops in iron skillet.
Place onion, bell pepper and tomato on top of pork chops.
Cover with rice.
Drain and combine all 3 kinds of beans.
Add all other ingredients; mix.
Refrigerate several hours or overnight, stirring 3 or 4 times.
Drain before serving.
Makes 10 servings.
Mix ginger ale, juices, pop and sugar.
Add sherbet by spoonfuls.

i: 1 Tbsp. plus 10 tsp. peanut or vegetable oil
5 tsp. chopped fresh oregano or 2 1/2 tsp. dried oregano
d: Preheat the broiler.
Core the tomatoes and slice them in half.
Grease a baking dish (large enough to hold the tomatoes) with 1 tablespoon of oil.
Arrange the tomato halves in it, cut side up.
Sprinkle each with about 1/2 teaspoon oregano and salt and pepper.
Sprinkle about 1 teaspoon oil over each half.
Fry bacon until crisp.
Let cool.
Chop broccoli and cauliflower into bite-size pieces.
Add chopped onion.
Mix Miracle Whip, vinegar and sugar; add to mixture.
Stir together and add cheese and bacon.
Melt butter; add honey, mustard and curry powder.
Pour over chicken parts.
Cover and bake at 400° for 1/2 hour, covered, and 1/2 hour, uncovered.
Baste often.
Dissolve Jell-O and sugar in boiling water.
Then add cold water, lemonade, orange juice and pineapple juice.
Mix all ingredients together.
Just before serving, add ginger ale. Makes 1 gallon.
Float fresh strawberries or make an ice ring.

i: 1 head lettuce, washed and cut into bite-sized pieces
3 tomatoes, washed and cut into bite-sized slices
3 or 4 green onions, washed, ends cut off and sliced diagonally
1/2 lb. Greek Feta cheese, crumbled or cut into small pieces
d: Add ingredients in the order given, toss gently and serve with your favorite crackers.
I like to serve it with Wheatsworth stone ground wheat crackers.
Recipe yields 6 large salads or 10 small salads.
Enjoy!
In a large mixing bowl, mix all dry ingredients together.
In a separate bowl, mix buttermilk, oil and egg yolks and beat well. Slowly stir buttermilk mixture into dry ingredients until thoroughly mixed.
Beat egg whites and fold into batter.
Spoon dip batter into preheated waffle iron.
Drain all fruits; combine fruits* in
a 12 x 8 x 2-inch baking dish.
Combine brown sugar, melted butter and curry; stir well and spoon over fruit.
Cover and bake at 350° for 35 minutes or until thoroughly heated.
Good served with any entree.
We serve this on Christmas morning with Laura's Eggs and Grits Souffle.
Bring to a boil; simmer for 15 minutes.
Can and seal.
Have your jars and lids good and hot.

i: 2 jars marinated artichokes (drain and reserve juice)
1 chicken, cooked and chopped or use 4 chicken breasts
d: Cook rice.
Combine
artichoke,
onion,
pepper, olives and chicken in	a
large
Double recipe for more chicken.

i: 2 pkg. frozen chopped spinach, cooked and drained
1 pt. Ricotta cheese (whole or part skim)
d: Grease a 10-inch pie plate with margarine.
Pour the above mixture into pie dish.
Bake at 350° for 35 to 45 minutes or until knife inserted in center comes out clean.

i: seasoned salt or any other combination of preferred spices
d: Peel yams and cut into 1/2-inch thick circles.
Spray glass baking dish with Pam.
Add remaining ingredients.
Bake for 1 hour at 375°, tossing every 15 to 20 minutes.
Serve with turkey and stuffing meat loaf.
Add first 6 ingredients and simmer 10 minutes.
Mix remaining ingredients, except Monterey Jack cheese and tortillas, together. Layer 1/2 meat mixture and top with 1/2 shells.
Spread cheese mixture.
Add 1/2 Monterey Jack cheese.
Repeat layers.
Bake uncovered 20 to 30 minutes at 350° in 9 x 13-inch pan.
Roll crescent rolls out flat in bottom of ungreased 13 x 9-inch pan.
Pour beaten eggs over dough.
Spread fried sausage over eggs.
Add hash browns (uncooked) over sausage.
Sprinkle cheese over all.
Bake in 350° oven for 15 to 20 minutes, or until cheese is melted and browned.
Drain clams.
Heat olive oil in skillet.
Add garlic; brown lightly.
Add parsley and seasoning.
Heat 2 minutes.
Serve over cooked spaghetti.
Mix all ingredients; put in a shallow dish.
Bake at 325° for 20 minutes.
Serve with crackers.
Delicious!
Serves 10 to 12.

i: salt, pepper and garlic salt to taste
d: Mix well.
Can be mixed in blender, but don't process too much or it will be too runny.
Makes 5 cups.

t: Chicken Breast Baked In Lemon And Garlic
d: Heat small skillet; add olive oil.
Heat and add garlic. Saute 30 seconds.
Stir in lemon juice, salt and pepper.
Bring to boil; set aside.
Arrange chicken in lightly buttered shallow baking dish, enough to hold chicken in single layer.
Pour garlic mix over chicken; sprinkle with grated lemon peel.
Add salt and ice cubes to cucumbers.
Let stand 3 hours; drain.
Mix sugar, vinegar, mustard seed, celery seed and turmeric together.
Bring to boil, add cucumbers.
Bring to boil once more and put in pint jars and seal.
Heat oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat.
Add onion and cook until tender, about 5 minutes.
Reduce heat to low, add cabbage.
In small bowl, combine water, tomato puree, Italian seasoning and 1/2 of garlic powder.
In another bowl, combine cottage cheese, onion powder and rest of garlic powder.
Spray a 9 x 9 x 2-inch casserole dish with cooking spray.
Spread 1/3 of tomato mix in dish.
Layer 1/2 of macaroni, all of cheese mixture, and 1/3 of the tomato mix.
Add rest of macaroni and cover with tomato mix.
Combine sour cream, lemon juice, sugar, salt, pineapple, bananas and coloring.
Fold in nuts.
Spoon into paper muffin cup liners in muffin tins and freeze.
When frozen remove from tins. Put in plastic bags or airtight container and return to freezer. Will keep for several months.
Take out of freezer for 15 minutes before serving.
Peel off liners and serve on lettuce.

i: 5 slices bacon, cut into 1 inch pieces
2 medium potatoes, peeled and cut into chunks
d: Cook bacon and onion in a skillet until bacon is crisp, stir constantly.
Stir in sauerkraut and brown sugar, add potatoes and apples.
In a cheese cloth bag tie peppercorns, juniper berries, cloves, parsley and bay leaves; add to sauerkraut.
Add pork chops and smoked kielbasa pieces, pour chicken broth in, stir well.
Heat to boiling, then reduce heat, cover and simmer until meat is done, potatoes are tender for about 45 minutes.
When done remove spice bag.

i: 1 (12 oz.) pkg. jumbo shells (1 c., Ronzini)
d: Cook shells according to package directions.
Pour marinara sauce into saucepan and heat.
Spoon 1 cup of sauce into bottom of a large roasting pan or 1/2 cup sauce into each of two 2-quart baking pans.
Reserve remaining sauce; keep warm.
Slice onions in a large saucepan.
Stir together vinegar, hot sauce, sugar, chili powder and salt.
Add franks and onions; bring to a boil.
Boil 3 minutes.
Cover and refrigerate 2 to 3 days before serving.
Makes about 64.
Clean chicken and marinate several hours in dressing.
Preheat oven to 400°.
Put bread crumbs in plastic bag and shake chicken generously in crumbs.
Spray a 13 x 9 pan with Pam.
Place chicken in pan.
Cook for 1 hour, uncovered, turning once.

i: 1/4 lb. lean ham or Canadian bacon, diced
1 small can pimentos, drained and diced
d: Separate breasts, wings, drumsticks and liver.
Place remaining parts of chicken in saucepan with 3 cups water, peeled onion, saffron and 1 1/2 teaspoons salt.
Boil 30 minutes; strain to make 2 1/2 cups.

i: 1 c. boiling salted water (1 1/2 tsp. salt)
1/2 c. dry bread crumbs or cracker crumbs
d: Combine the ground beef, salt, pepper and onion together.
Add the tomato sauce and bread crumbs to the ground beef.
After cooking the meat until brown, put the meat into the green peppers. Boil the green peppers for about 2 minutes before you put the meat into them.
Cut corn off the cob.
Chop rest of the vegetables fine.
Mix all together and boil 25 minutes.
Thick with flour, mixed in water, to make paste.
Continue to boil 15 minutes longer.
Seal in sterilized jars.
Place vinegar, salt and pepper in salad bowl.
Mix well to dissolve salt.
Add olive oil and mix well.
Stir flours together.
Toast in heavy frying pan (no oil is needed) until well browned. Do not burn.
Place in large mixing bowl.
Add wheat germ, coconut, sesame seed and sunflower seeds which have been toasted individually.
Cut biscuits into fourths. Coat with mixture of sugar and cinnamon.
Layer biscuits in well-greased Bundt pan.
Combine melted margarine and brown sugar in bowl; mix well.
Drizzle over biscuits.
Sprinkle with pecans.
Place in cold oven.
Mix all ingredients and pour into graham cracker crusts. Makes 2 or more pies!
Cook and stir onions and garlic in oil over medium heat until onions are tender. Stir in water, lentils and salt.
Heat to boiling. Reduce heat. Cover and simmer 1 hour.
Stir in spinach, lemon peel and lemon juice. Simmer about 5 more minutes. Serves 4.

i: 1 (1 lb. 1 oz.) can peach halves, drained
1 (1 lb.) can pineapple chunks with 1/4 c. syrup
d: Preheat oven to 325°.
Melt butter in skillet.
Add sugar and curry powder.
Add pineapple and syrup to skillet.
Mix and simmer until sugar is melted.
Put fruits in a buttered shallow pan or baking dish (1 1/2-quart).
Mix all together.
Spread it evenly over bottom of 8 x 8-inch pan.
Bake at 350° for 25 minutes.
Cook potatoes, celery and onion until tender.
Add beans, tomatoes, soup, chili powder and smoked sausage.
Simmer and serve.
Beat eggs.
Add cake mix, pudding, water and oil.
Beat at medium speed for 10 minutes.
Pour into an ungreased 10-inch tube pan with removable bottom.
Bake at 350° about 50 minutes.
Cool slightly and remove from pan.
Boil for 30 minutes.
Put in jars and seal.
Make dough according to Bisquick directions on box.
Roll out until about 1 inch thick.
(Shape, as you roll out into an oblong shape.)
Thinly spread with a knife, mustard on top of dough. Then, salt and pepper lightly or to taste.
Spread corned beef hash on top of the mustard, salt and pepper.
Then, lengthwise, roll up securely and pan freeze.
Cream sugar and oleo together. Add eggs, salt and vanilla. Beat until fluffy.
Spoon into bottom of crust.
Chill well. Mix Cool Whip, pecans and pineapple together.
Spoon on top of pies. Chill well.
Pour into well greased 9x5x4 loaf pan.
Let stand 20 minutes before baking.
Bake until tests done, about 50 - 60 minutes at 350 degrees.
Ketchup spice may be used and others omitted.

i: fresh ground black pepper to taste
8 large green onions (green part only), cut into large pieces
d: Make sauce by placing lemon juice, salt, pepper (as desired), green onions and olive oil in a blender or food processor; process until completely smooth.
Set aside.

i: 1 lb. or more large fresh mushrooms
d: Remove stems from mushrooms and wash carefully.
Save stems for another dish.
Combine drained crab meat and grated sharp cheese.
Add mayonnaise and mix well with a fork.
Stuff this mixture into each mushroom cap.
Sprinkle each with crushed crackers.

i: 1 small pimento, chopped or container of pimento
d: Mix all together.
Let stand for 24 hours in refrigerator before serving.
Scoop out the apple cores.
Mix cinnamon and sweetener and put in the apples.
Bake in small baking dish with 1/2 cup water in microwave for 2 1/2 to 3 minutes.
Add raisins and serve.
Has 96 calories each, 0 fat grams, 1 fruit exchange.
Combine cabbage, pepper and onion.
Pour sugar over and set aside.
Combine salt, dry mustard, celery seed and vinegar in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Pour over cabbage and stir.
Let refrigerate overnight.
Serves 12 and will keep a week.

t: Texas State Fair Blue Ribbon Chili
d: Simmer for 1 hour.
Drain pineapple and reserve juice.
Cut bananas in chunks like pineapple.

Lightly coat an 8-inch square baking pan with cooking spray; set aside. Blot cherries with paper towel to dry. Combine chocolates in heavy saucepan and place over very low heat; stir until melted and smooth. Remove from heat; add sweetened condensed milk and cherry juice. Stir until smooth and add pecans. Spoon mixture into prepared pan. Immediately press cherries into fudge, leaving top of each cherry and stem exposed. Cover and chill 2 hours or until firm. Cut into squares, a cherry in each square. Store in airtight container in the refrigerator.

t: Spinach Salad(If You Like Spinach, You Will Love This Spinach Salad!)
d: Wash spinach and break into small pieces in a large bowl.
Add bacon, eggs and bean sprouts and lightly toss.
Top with croutons and this dressing:

i: 2 (19 oz.) cans chickpeas, drained and rinsed
1/2 c. black olives, chopped or sliced
d: Combine the first 5 ingredients in a bowl.
In a small jar, shake together the vinegar, salt and pepper. Add the olive oil and shake again.
Pour dressing over chickpea mixture and toss.
Cook hamburger with onion until brown, then drain.
Stir in brown sugar, salt, barbecue sauce and pork and beans and heat through.
Place in casserole.
Separate biscuits and cut in half. Sprinkle with cheese, then arrange over hamburger mixture.
Bake in preheated 425° oven until biscuits are brown.

t: Pasta With Vegetables And Goat Cheese
i: 1 small yellow squash, halved lengthwise and sliced
8 oz. light or fat free cream cheese
d: Combine broth, wine, shallots and black pepper in large saucepan.

Bring to a boil, add broccoli, breaking florets apart. Add squash and mushrooms, reduce heat, cover and simmer 5 minutes. Add cream cheese, goat cheese and lemon juice, cook on low heat, stirring constantly until cheeses are melted.
Set aside, keeping warm.
Cook pasta according to package directions, drain.
Place in large serving bowl, add vegetable/cheese mixture, toss gently. Garnish with red pepper strips.
Serves 6 to 8.
Measure crushed blackberries into a kettle.
Add sweetener; stir well.
Boil rapidly, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens.
Fill and seal containers.

i: small can sliced mushrooms (or use fresh)
d: Brown ground beef.
Saute onion and bell pepper with meat (also mushrooms if fresh).
Drain excess grease.
Season with garlic salt and black pepper.
Add tomato sauce and mushrooms (drained if canned) to skillet.
Simmer, covered, about 10 minutes.
Beat together first three ingredients.
Mix together in separate bowl all dry ingredients.
Add oil mixture to dry mixture.
Puree apple pie filling and add to mixture along with chopped pecans.
Bake at 350° in greased and floured tube pan for 1 hour and 15 minutes.
Freezes well.

i: 1 large can cooked chicken breasts, drained
4 oz. shredded Swiss or Cheddar cheese
d: In a large nonstick skillet, heat margarine over medium heat until bubbly; add zucchini and onion and saute, stirring constantly, until zucchini is tender, 2 to 3 minutes.
Add chicken and cook, stirring constantly until heated through, about 1 minute.
Add eggs and cook, stirring frequently, just until eggs are set, 1 to 2 minutes.
Stir in cheese and cook until cheese melts.
Cut pita in half crosswise, forming 2 pockets.
Stuff each pocket with 1/8 of the egg mixture and 1 tomato slice.
Mix all ingredients together and refrigerate a couple of hours.

i: 1 can hearts of palm, drained and cut into 1-inch pieces
9 oz. jar marinated artichoke hearts, drained and halved
d: Make dressing as directed.
Marinate all in dressing for at least 4 hours.
Alternate all on 20 to 25 wooden skewers to serve.
Combine cornstarch, ginger, chicken broth and soy sauce in a 2-cup glass measure.
Stir well.
Microwave, uncovered, at High for 2 to 3 minutes or until thickened, stirring every 30 seconds. Set aside.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Trim off each garlic head to expose the tops of the cloves.
Place heads in shallow baking dish, such as a 9-inch pie plate.
Drizzle garlic with oil and sprinkle with pepper, salt and thyme.
Roast garlic for 30 minutes.
Reduce heat to 250° and cook for 1 hour longer.
Mix yeast, sugar, milk, meal and salt in bowl.
Heat the water and oleo to very warm.
Not hot!
Add the liquid to the dry ingredients beating for about 2 minutes.
Add 1/2 cup flour.
Add the rest of the flour, 1 cup at a time.
Cream the first three ingredients together until smooth.
Add the next two ingredients into mixture.
Stir well.
Grease and flour an oblong cake pan.
Place 2 cups broken pecan pieces on bottom.
Wet hands and smooth cream mixture over the nuts.

t: Pasta With Broccoli Di Rape And Sausage
i: 2 Tbsp. olive oil or vegetable oil
1 lb. medium pasta shells or any small size pasta of choice
d: Remove the long stems and tough outer leaves from the Broccoli di Rape.
Keep florets and small leaves, cut up, place in colander and wash several times.
Pour oil into pan on medium heat.
Lightly crush garlic, place in heated oil and allow to turn golden, then remove garlic if you wish.
Remove casing from sausage, place meat in pan and cook thoroughly, breaking the pieces of meat apart as they continue to cook.
If you still have not removed the garlic, do so now.
Brown meat, pepper and onion in 2 tablespoons oil with garlic. Add tomatoes.
Drain all liquid so mixture is very dry.
Flatten out crescent rolls into 1 sheet, mush seams together so there are no holes.
Place meat and vegetable mixture on one half and bring other side over.
Press edges together.
Cook 35 minutes at 350° until crust is golden brown.

i: 12 fresh clams in their shells, soaked in cold water
d: Saute onion, tomato and garlic in olive oil.
Add turmeric, clams, clam juice and white wine.
Cover and cook 5 minutes.
Add shrimp, crab and cayenne.
Cover and cook on high until clams open, plus 2 extra minutes.

i: 3 to 5 lb. boneless pork roast (or 1 small beef roast and 1 small pork roast)
d: Cover all ingredients with water.
Cook 6 hours, covered. Stir and add water as needed.
Remove lid; cook 1 more hour.
Meat will fall apart.
Serve over tortilla chips.
Add grated cheese, lettuce, tomato, onion and avocado.
Saute onion and celery in butter until tender.
Sprinkle in curry powder; cook about 1 minute.
Soak gelatin in 1/4 cup chicken broth; add to remainder of broth (which you have heated in a microwave) to dissolve the gelatin.
Set aside about 1/4 cup to decorate the mold.
Stir into curry mixture and bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
Cool.
Melt chocolate and add milk.
Stir in coconut and walnuts.
Chop cabbage and leave in bowl.
Dice green peppers and onions.
Add to chopped cabbage.
Stir in sugar, salt, oil and vinegar.
Let stand 30 minutes.
Refrigerate for crispness.

i: 3 pkg. McCormick brown gravy mix or substitute
d: Fry 2 pounds of hamburger with chopped onions, salt and pepper to taste. Drain off grease. Mix 3 cups of cold water and 3 packages of gravy mix in a container, and stir well.
Add the water and gravy mix to the hamburger, cover, and bring to a low boil.
Turn down to simmer, and simmer for 10 - 15 minutes, stirring frequently.
If gravy is too thick, add water.
While this is cooking, make your mashed potatoes.

Dish the hamburger and gravy together, and the mashed potatoes separately. Let everyone serve themselves.	Place on table and let everyone serve themselves individual helpings of potatoes, and top with hamburger and gravy mix.

i: 5 green onions, sliced or 1/4 c. chopped red onion
d: Trim off large leaves of broccoli and remove the tough ends of lower stalks. Wash the broccoli thoroughly. Cut the florets and stems into bite-size pieces. Place in a large bowl. Add bacon, onions and raisins. Combine remaining ingredients, stirring well. Add dressing to broccoli mixture and toss gently. Cover and refrigerate 2 to 3 hours. Makes about 6 servings.
Combine all ingredients.
Baste on ham last 45 minutes of baking, basting frequently.
Cut catfish in pieces, 2 x 1-inch.
In first pan, put 1 cup flour and 1 1/2 teaspoons seasoning mix.
In 2nd pan, combine milk, eggs and brown mustard.
In third pan, combine 2 tablespoons plus 1 teaspoon seasoning mix with 1 1/2 cups flour and cornmeal.

i: 1 (29 oz.) can Bush's cut green and shelly beans
1 (15 oz.) can Bush's whole white potatoes, drained
d: Empty beans into crock-pot.
Add drained potatoes, onion and bacon.
Cook on high for 30 minutes.

i: 1 can crushed pineapple with the juice
d: Mix pudding mix and Cool Whip together.
Add the rest of the ingredients and stir well.
Refrigerate.
May be used immediately.

t: Triple Bean Casserole Or Crock-Pot Casserole
d: Cook beef and onions until meat loses pink color; drain.
Add remaining ingredients.
Cook at 325° for 1 to 1 1/2 hours or cook on low heat in slow cooker (crock-pot) for 8 to 10 hours.
Mix butter, milk, powdered sugar, coconut and pecans together and roll into a ball about the size of a walnut.
Chill overnight. In double boiler, melt the chocolate chips and the paraffin. Then dip each ball into the chocolate mixture with a toothpick and place on wax paper to dry.
Makes about 60 to 80 balls.

i: boiling water (just to cover peppers and onions)
cold water (to cover peppers and onions)
d: Chop peppers and onions.
Pour boiling water over peppers and onions, then drain.
Cover with cold water.
Bring to boiling point, then drain again.
Put vinegar, salt, sugar and mustard seed in another pan and bring to boil.
Add to peppers and onions and boil 1 minute.

i: 1 chicken, cooked and cut into small pieces
d: Layer chicken in bottom of casserole dish and pour melted butter or margarine over chicken.
Next, mix flour with milk and pour over chicken.
Mix soup and broth and pour over top of casserole and gently stir across top; smooth out.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes or until golden brown and done in center.
Dissolve yeast in warm water.
Mix with other ingredients.
To bake, spoon batter into well-greased muffin tin about 2/3 full. Bake at 425° for 20 minutes or until golden brown. Will keep in refrigerator for 2 weeks if sealed in Tupperware bowl.

t: Cocido Castellano(Castillian Bean Soup; One Pot Meal)
i: 1 chorizo (Spanish sausage) or 3-inch beef smoked sausage
1 piece smoked bacon (2 x 2 x 3-inch cube)
d: Soak beans overnight (8 to 10 hours) in saltwater 3-inches over beans.
When ready to cook, drain off saltwater.
Put garbanzo beans, ham hock, sausage, soup bone, bacon and onion in boiling water and cook about 4 hours.
When it has cooked about 3 1/2 hours, taste.
Add salt if needed.
Saute onions in oil.
Preheat oven to 350°.
Cook entire package of noodles.
Let sit in water for 1 hour or overnight.
Separate eggs.
Beat egg yolks, sugar and vanilla until thick and lemon colored.
Beat egg whites until stiff.
Bring water to boil.
Place fish in water; bring to boil again.
Turn burner off, cover pot and let fish stand for 15 to 20 minutes (1 pound=15 minutes, 2 pounds=20 minutes).
Toothpick goes through fish easily if done.
Remove fish from water and place in platter.
Garnish with finely chopped ginger, green onions and Chinese parsley.
Mix all liquids together.
Mix all dry ingredients together, then mix together.
Bake at 350° for 45 minutes in 13 x 9-inch pan.
Grease and flour pans.
(Makes three (9-inch) layers.) Mix all ingredients together and bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Bake
at
275° uncovered 2 hours in shallow pan.
Serve on warm buns.
Wash cabbage and chop fine, then chop onions.
Heat ground beef and onions in skillet.
Put half of cabbage in baking dish. Cover with beef and onions.
Put remainder of cabbage on top. Pour undiluted tomato soup over mixture.
Bake 1 hour or longer, covered with foil, at 325°.

i: 1 (16 oz.) pkg. California Blend vegetables, cooked
d: Spread Rice-A-Roni in a 9 x 13-inch pan.
Place chicken on rice.
Put vegetables on chicken.
Heat soup, milk and margarine until margarine is melted.
Pour soup mixture over everything. Sprinkle cheese on top.
Cover with foil and bake at 350° for 45 minutes.
Mix all ingredients, except oil, and rub into steaks.
Splash on olive oil. Coat all sides. Cover and refrigerate, turning occasionally, 6 to 8 hours or overnight. Grill as desired.
Sift flour and cut lard into mixture.
Add sugar and buttermilk; mix well with hands.
Roll out and place apart on baking sheet that has been greased with margarine.
Bake at 450° for about 18 minutes, or until brown on the bottom, then broil tops until golden brown.
Brush margarine over the tops while hot.

t: Sweet Pickle Made From Fresh Cukes
d: Mix cucumber slices with lime.
Cover with water for 24 hours.
Pour off water and wash cucumber slices 3 or 4 times. (May be necessary to wash each slice.)
Cream shortening and sugar together.
Add rest of ingredients, adding one beaten egg at a time. Pour into unbaked pie shell. Bake at 425° for 10 minutes.
Reduce heat to 350° and bake for 25 minutes.

i: 10 oz. Hungry Jack biscuits, pressed into 4-inch circles
1 garlic clove, minced or 1/8 tsp. garlic powder
d: Press Hungry Jack biscuits into circles.
Mix well mayo, Parmesan cheese, tarragon, basil, green onions and garlic clove. Spread onto biscuit circles to 1/4-inch of edge.
Bake at 400° for 10 to 13 minutes.
Drain and rinse beans.
Brown ground beef.
Drain excess fat. Add beans, tomatoes, tomato paste, onion, pepper, chili powder and water.
Simmer 45 minutes.
Brown beef and onions in skillet; drain.
Add Worcestershire sauce.
Stir in flour and mix beef well.
In large pot, cook egg noodles and drain.
Add to noodles, ground beef, mixed vegetables (drained) and 1 1/2 cups tomato juice.
Cook and drain ground beef.
Add remaining ingredients and simmer until cheese is melted.
Serve with Tostitos chips.

i: 4 tubes Pillsbury crescent rolls (may use frozen dough for roll)
d: Cut
sausages
in
inch pieces or smaller to make 32 pieces.
Remove
In a blender or food processor fitted with the metal blade, combine 1/4 cup bean liquid and remaining ingredients.
Process until mixture is smooth paste.
Add more liquid if needed to make desired consistency.
Cover and refrigerate until chilled.
Serve with crackers or pita bread.
Use as a dip for crudites or use as a stuffing for celery.

i: 1 Fennel Bulb, trimmed and thinly sliced
3/4 lb. chicken breast cut into bite sized pieces or chicken tenders (no breading)
1/4 c. dry white wine or a good splash (optional)
d: In a large skillet, heat the olive oil and butter.
Saute the fennel, leeks and shallots until golden brown, about 10 minutes. Transfer to a platter.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until done.
Arrange over the vegetables.
Keep warm.
In the skillet, combine the broth, wine, mustard, salt and pepper; bring to a boil.

t: No Fat-No Cholesterol-No Trouble Apple Pie
i: 1 tsp. apple pie spice or cinnamon
d: Mix all ingredients and place in pie plate sprayed with vegetable spray.
Bake 30 minutes at 350°.
Serve with low-fat whipped topping.

i: 1 c. drained tomatoes or 2 c. chopped fresh ones
d: Combine ingredients in large skillet.
Cover and cook 3 minutes.
Stir and cook 3 more minutes.
Yields about 6 servings.

i: 1 1/2 c. drained canned tomatoes or 1/4 tomato paste
4 lb. fish heads or 1 qt. clam juice and reduce water to 1 1/2 qt.
d: Cook onions and
leeks	slowly in the oil until tender, but not browned.
Add
other
ingredients
to onions and cook, uncovered, at a
Mix dry ingredients in mixer bowl.
Add buttermilk, margarine and eggs.
Mix well and beat at medium speed for 2 to 3 minutes. Bake in heated waffle iron.

i: 1 (18 oz.) can sweet potatoes or yams, drained, cut crosswise into 1/2-inch slices
12 frozen apricot halves, cut into quarters
d: Preheat oven to 375°.
Grease 1 to 1 1/2 quart casserole. Arrange potatoes in casserole.
Mix sugar, cornstarch, salt, cinnamon and orange peel in heavy quart saucepan.
Add apricot nectar and water.
Cook on high until mixture comes to boil, stirring constantly.
Remove from heat.
Dissolve yeast in warm water.
Scald milk and cool to lukewarm.
Add dissolved yeast, milk, sugar, salt and shortening. Add 4 cups flour and beat by hand until it makes a medium-soft dough.
Add remainder of the flour and work into dough.
Let dough rise in a bowl, greased with oil and covered (you can use a wet cloth to cover).
Let rise until the size doubles, then roll out on a floured board.
Combine white wine, lemon juice and chopped garlic.
Pound thin about 1 pound chicken cutlets.
Coat with seasoned bread crumbs and saute chicken in olive oil until lightly browned and tender.
Add wine mixture and simmer uncovered a
few minutes until liquid is somewhat reduced.
Sprinkle with parsley.
Let pears, onions and peppers stand in separate containers for 1 hour. Pour boiling water over each; drain well.

Combine pears, onion, peppers, vinegar, sugar and seasonings in a large kettle. Bring to a boil, reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 30 minutes. Pour hot mixture into hot sterilized jars, leaving 1/4 inch headspace.
Remove air bubbles and wipe jar rims.
Cover at once with metal lids and screw on bands.
Process in boiling water bath for 20 minutes.
Let stay in a cool place for 1 month before using.
Mix first 7 ingredients in a large bowl, using your hands. Divide mixture into 12 even sized balls.
Place 1 ball into center of each cabbage leaf.
Wrap leaf around mixture.
Place each wrapped ball into a crock-pot generously sprayed with Pam.
Pour 1 quart tomato juice over cabbage rolls.
Cook 2 hours on high, then cook 4 hours on low, then serve.

t: Cucumbers And Onions In Sour Cream
d: Slice cucumbers, peeled.
Cut Vidalia onion in quarters and slice.
Sprinkle with salt and put in refrigerator for at least 1 hour.
Drain.
If less salt desired, rinse under cold water.
Wad up a couple of paper towels and stir through cucumbers and onions to remove excess moisture.
Melt oleo.
Saute onion and bell pepper in oleo until tender. Empty the 2 cans of hominy, draining one and draining 1/2 of the other can.
Mix in with the onion and bell pepper mixture.
Stir in Cheez Whiz and pimento until melted and mixed well.
Pour into casserole dish.
Bake until bubbly.
Brown hamburger meat and drain.
Then add taco seasoning and water according to package and bring to a boil.
Then add the beans.
Take shells and fill with meat and roll up.
Place rolled tortilla in a pan and make one layer of tortillas, then add some juice from meat over the layer.
Make another layer of tortillas and then pour the rest of the juice over the layers.

i: 3 Tbsp. olive oil or any oil
d: In a heavy kettle or pot, heat 2 cups water with 2 teaspoons salt.
When water is at a rolling boil, add rice.
Stir and bring to a second boil.
Let it cook, uncovered, at high heat until water is absorbed (about 5 to 8 minutes).
Add 3 tablespoons olive oil.
Stir and simmer, covered, on low heat for 10 minutes more or until tender.
Saute chicken in oil.
Add peas, mushrooms and veggies with 2 cups water.
Cook 5 minutes.
Cook 5 minutes.
Cover and simmer until vegetables are tender.
Combine soy sauce and cornstarch with 2 tablespoons water.
Combine ingredients, except shrimp; beat.
Peel shell from shrimp, leaving last section and tail intact (optional). Butterfly shrimp by cutting almost through at center of back without severing ends.
Remove black vein.
Dry shrimp thoroughly between paper towels.
Dip into batter.
Fry in deep, hot fat (375°) until golden brown.
Cook beef, garlic, onion and green pepper in
a heavy skillet over medium-high heat, stirring frequently to break up meat.
Cook until onion is soft and meat has lost its pink color.
Add remaining ingredients.
Bring to a boil.
Cover and reduce heat. Simmer the chili for 45 minutes, stirring frequently.
Brown ground beef; drain.
Add package of taco seasoning mix and about half of taco sauce.
Simmer.
Put tortilla chips on bottom of 9 x 13-inch pan, then ground beef mixture.
Layer cheese on meat.
Then add lettuce, onion and tomato.

i: 1 medium eggplant (1 1/4 lb.), peeled and cut into 1/4-inch slices
1 green pepper, halved, seeded and cut into thin strips
2 medium tomatoes, cored, seeded and cut into eighths
d: Preheat oven to 425°.
Place eggplant between several thicknesses of paper towels.
Sprinkle with a teaspoon of salt; weigh down with breadboard or several recipe books and let stand 30 minutes.
Cut into 1/4-inch cubes.
Heat 1/2 cup olive oil in a large Teflon skillet; add the eggplant and quickly toss to coat with oil.
Cook, stirring constantly, until eggplant is tender, about 5 minutes.
Bring first 6 ingredients to a boil; let cool.
Pour into gallon jar and add cucumbers and onions.
Garlic and hot peppers may be added if desired.
Put into refrigerator.
Add new cucumbers as needed.
Peel and slice boiled potatoes.
Pour Italian dressing over potatoes and allow to sit 1 hour.
Pour off excess dressing and add eggs, onion and spices.
Add mayonnaise until desired consistency is obtained.
Serve immediately or chilled.
Clean cabbage and wilt in warm water.
Mix all ingredients, except some of the sauce.
Roll mixture into cabbage leaf tightly. Put in a pan.
Pour remaining tomato sauce.
Cover with foil. Bake for about 1 hour at 350°.
Cream cheese and add pineapple.
Slice in marshmallows and nuts.
Fold in whipped cream.
Put in cupcake holders in muffin tins.
Freeze.
Will keep up to 6 months.

i: 2 lb. fresh, shelled and deveined shrimp
d: Heat oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add shrimp, garlic and parsley; saute until shrimp turns pink, about 2 minutes.
Turn shrimp.
Season with pepper.
Stir in lemon juice and wine. Simmer about 2 more minutes.
Don't overcook.
In a large saucepan, combine potatoes, water, celery, onion, parsley, bouillon cube and seasonings.
Cover; simmer for 15 to 20 minutes, or until vegetables are tender.
Gradually add milk to flour, mixing well.
Add milk mixture to vegetables; cook until thickened.
Add broccoli and bacon, if desired.
Add cheese; stir until melted.
Brown sausage, set aside and cut into thirds.
Brown ground meat, onion and green pepper; drain.
Mix all ingredients and cook over low heat for 2 to 3 hours.
Drain other fat from top of sauce as it cooks.

i: 1 lb. bacon, cut into small pieces and fried
4 (4 oz.) cans mushroom stems and pieces
d: Fry bacon.
Add peppers and onions; saute.
Add all ingredients to beans.
Bake at 350° for 1 1/2 to 2 hours.
This makes a large quantity.
Can be made in smaller amounts.
Pick the meat
off the bones of a larger fryer or young hen. Cut the
meat
into
small cubes, put it into heavy iron pot.
Add 1

i: 1 lb. can salmon, flaked, remove bone and dark meat
d: Combine cheese, juice, onion and seasoning.
Then add salmon. Chill 2 hours.
Shape mixture into ball.
Roll in nuts and parsley.
Serve with crackers.
Mix all ingredients and pour over wings.
Marinate overnight. Bake at 350° for 15 minutes on each side.
Recipe will make 2 batches of punch.
Put in small cake pan in layers:
Bean dip, guacamole (avocados mixed with the mayonnaise), sour cream mixed with taco seasoning, shredded cheese, tomatoes, olives, onions and shredded cheese. Serve with chips.

Microwave chocolate and milk in large bowl on High 2 to 3 minutes or until chocolate is almost melted, stirring halfway through heating time. Remove from microwave. Stir in vanilla and walnuts. Spread in greased 8-inch square pan. Refrigerate until firm. Cut into squares. Makes 4 dozen.
Blend eggs, sugar, flour, margarine, salt and spices; then mix in vanilla.
Add or mix in grated raw sweet potatoes.
Pour into unbaked pie shell and bake at 450° for 10 minutes, then 350° for 40 more minutes.
In a large mixing bowl, cream shortening and sugar until light fluffy.
Beat in egg and molasses.
Add flour, soda, salt and spices.
Stir until a stiff dough is formed.
Wrap dough and refrigerate for 3 hours.

t: Broccoli With Oil And Lemon Vinegar
d: Cook broccoli until tender-crisp.
Drain and put in serving dish.
Combine all remaining ingredients and pour over broccoli. Serve hot or cold.
Bring 2 cups water to a boil.
Pour over oil, sugar and salt. Cook
until
only
lukewarm.
Dissolve
Place beans in pie pan or casserole dish; add a layer of lettuce, tomatoes, onion, olives, taco sauce, sour cream and top with cheese.
Serve with nachos or large tortilla chips.

i: 2 Tbsp. dried marjoram, thyme or tarragon, crumbled
d: Trim fat from chops.
Preheat broiler.
Rub the chops with the garlic which has been cut in two.
Brush chops with the olive oil; sprinkle with marjoram and pepper.
Broil 4 to 5 inches from heat for 4 to 5 minutes on each side, for medium cooked chops.
Serve with lemon wedges, if desired, and oven baked French fries. Serves 4.
Reserve 2 tablespoons syrup from oranges.
Blend reserved syrup with stir-fry sauce.
Set aside.

i: 1 pkg. (2 piece) turkey link sausage, diced
1 (20 oz.) can peel whole tomatoes, undrained
2 (15 1/2 oz.) cans Green Giant red beans, undrained
d: Saute diced turkey sausage, peppers and onion in just enough olive oil to coat the bottom of the pan. Add squash, mushrooms and creole seasoning to taste. Add tomatoes and red beans, including juice from both. Add Sea Legs and rice. Let simmer until juice is absorbed.
Combine corn, water and salt.
Cook for four minutes.
Remove from heat.
Add butter.
Combine milk and flour in shaker.
Shake until blended.
Liquify all ingredients, except turkey, in blender.
Inject liquified ingredients into turkey.
Marinate turkey in refrigerator 24 hours.
Deep fry turkey in pot 4 minutes per pound at 350°.

i: 2 ripe tomatoes, peeled and cut in wedges
d: Marinate artichoke hearts for 2 to 3 hours in olive oil and lemon
juice.
Tear
lettuce
into	bite-size
pieces and combine with
Mix thoroughly.
Knead 10 times.
Cut and bake at 450° for 12 minutes.

i: 3 to 4 lb. country style or spareribs
d: Combine soy sauce, marmalade, catsup and garlic.
Brush both sides of ribs.
Place in crock-pot.
Pour in remaining sauce. Cover and cook on low, 8 to 10 hours.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.
Sift together three times the flour and 1 cup of sugar and set aside.
Beat egg whites, water and vanilla until frothy, then add cream of tartar and continue beating until mixture stands in peaks but not dry.
Stir in 1 cup of sugar a little at a time.
Gradually add and fold in sifted flour and sugar mixture.
Bake in angel food cake pan at 325° for about 35 or 40 minutes.
Brown ground steak in large skillet.
Add garlic and onion. When tender, add all other seasonings.
Continue to simmer for 1 minute; add tomato sauce, spaghetti sauce and water.
Stir until meat is completely mixed with sauce; simmer for 20 minutes.
Stir to keep from sticking. Serve sauce over pasta with garlic bread. Makes 4 to 6 servings.
Adjust oven rack to lowest position.
Preheat oven to 350°. In food processor with steel blade, combine flour, salt and Crisco; pulse about 6 seconds, until mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Add milk and process until dough forms a ball.
Continue processing 2 minutes.
On floured surface, roll dough into rectangle 1/4-inch thick.
Fold dough in half, pressing lightly. With a 1 1/2-inch biscuit cutter, cut out dough and place on cookie sheet.

Combine 2 cups sugar, flour, cocoa and salt in medium saucepan. Stir in milk and butter; cook over medium heat. Separate eggs; beat yolks and stir into mixture. Continue to stir until thick. Pour into baked crust.
Layer in 9 x 13-inch pan: beans, avocados, chiles, sour cream, taco seasoning (sprinkle over the sour cream), olives and tomatoes.

i: 2 c. rotini pasta, cooked and drained
d: Mix Miracle Whip, Italian dressing and cheese until well blended.
Stir in other ingredients.
Refrigerate overnight.
Combine all ingredients together in large bowl.
This is a great salad and easy to make.
Mix those thoroughly and add tears of joy, sorrow and sympathy for others.
Flavor with little gifts of love and kindly service. Fold in four cups of prayer and faith to lighten all other ingredients and raise the texture to great heights of character and Christian living.
After pouring all this into your daily life, bake well with the heat of human kindness.
Serve with a smile at any time and it will satisfy the hunger of starved souls.
Drain juice from pineapple into 1-cup glass measure.
Stir in brown sugar and lemon juice.
Microwave on High, uncovered, 1 to 1 1/2 minutes or until mixture boils. Stir in flavoring. Cool. Quarter apples and cut into very thin slices.
Peel oranges. Cut into thin slices.
Cut slices in half.
In glass serving bowl, layer half the apples, oranges and grapes. Slice banana and arrange over grapes.

Melt butter in 9 x 13 glass pan in oven at 350°. Pour 1 cup graham cracker crumbs over melted butter. Then sprinkle chocolate morsels and butterscotch, then coconut and Eagle Brand milk, then pecans. Bake for 30 minutes. Let cool. Cut into bars.
You may use any combination of Jell-O and fruit you want. (Lime Jell-O and pineapple, orange Jell-O and mandarin oranges.)

i: 1 1/2 lb. lean beef and pork (1/2 each)
d: Cube meat, flour and brown.
Add small amount of water and simmer until done.
Cook onions and celery in salted water until done.
Add celery, onions, vegetables and bean sprouts to meat. Add soy sauce and brown gravy sauce; thicken with cornstarch.
Salt and pepper to taste.
Saute shallots and garlic in olive oil until soft (not brown). Add wine and tomatoes.
Bring to boil over medium-high heat and reduce heat.
Simmer 5 minutes.
Preheat oven to 400°: spray 8 inch square, 7x11, or 12 cup muffin tin with nonstick cooking spray.
In a large bowl, combine the cornmeal, flour, sugar, baking powder, and the salt.
In a small bowl, beat the buttermilk over the flour mixture; stir just until blended (do not overmix).
Transfer to the pan.
Bake until golden brown and a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, 20-25 minutes.
Cool pan on a rack 10 minutes; serve warm. Makes 12 servings.

i: 1 tsp. olive oil or vegetable oil
d: Heat all ingredients except couscous and cheese to boiling in a 2-quart saucepan.
Serve over couscous.
Sprinkle with cheese. Makes 6 servings.
Mix all ingredients in a large bowl until well blended.
Roll the dough out on a floured board.
Cut out treats with a dog bone shaped cookie cutter.
Bake at 350° for 15 to 20 minutes, or until dry.
Ask butcher to "crack" the pork loin roast as that the roast is cut through at the bone.
Coat with salt and pepper.
Put fresh or dried rosemary between each "crack." Coat outside with rosemary.
Bake at 350° after first broiling for a few minutes to brown the roast.
Cook about 1 1/2 hours until done.
Serve with cooked couscous which has been flavored with 1/4 cup olive oil and chopped, fresh mint.

i: 2 c. cooked and chopped chicken breasts
d: Mix vinegar, oil and curry powder together as a dressing. Toss in onion and rice.
Refrigerate overnight or 24 hours.
To rice mixture, add all other ingredients and mix well.
Chill at least 1 hour.
Serves 5 to 6.
Mix together and pat into 9 x 13-inch pan.
Bake 8 minutes in 400° oven.
Set aside to cool.
Mix first 5 ingredients.
Pour into casserole and top with crackers mixed with margarine.
Bake at 350° for 30 minutes.
Put water and lentils in medium-sized pot.
Bring to boil. Cover and cook over medium heat for 20 minutes.
Slice onions. Heat oil in skillet and add onions.
Stir until golden brown.
Add rice, salt and pepper to lentils and water and cook until rice is done, approximately 20 minutes.
Add oil and onions.
Melt Crisco; add flour and mix well.
Add milk gradually and bring to the boiling point, stirring constantly.
Add corn, sugar, salt and pepper and heat thoroughly.
Remove from fire; add well beaten eggs and pour into a greased baking dish.
Bake in oven at 350° for 25 minutes, or until corn is firm.
Mix everything in saucepan and cook until mixture thickens. Cool and store in refrigerator.
In chafing dish or saucepan, combine jam, sugar and water. Simmer over low heat until syrupy, about 5 minutes.
Add peaches; cook over low heat until almost tender, about 3 minutes.
Stir in lemon juice.
Heat brandy just until warm; pour slowly over peaches and ignite immediately.
Do not stir.
Spoon sauce and peaches over ice cream.
Spread bean dip on shallow serving tray (make sure the sides are at least 1-inch high).
Mash avocados and add lemon juice, salt and pepper.
Spread on top of bean dip.
Mix sour cream, mayonnaise and taco seasoning mix.
Spread on top of avocado layer (or bean dip layer if avocado layer is omitted).
Layer in order: chopped green onions, chopped black olives and tomatoes.
Cook pasta as directed and cool.
Chop up vegetables, any size you please.
Mix pasta, vegetables, Italian dressing and Salad Supreme (1/2 to 3/4 jar) to taste.
Melt butter in a saucepan.
Add flour and stir until bubbly. Do not brown.
Immediately add whipping cream and stir well. Mixture should have a fairly thin consistency.
Add Provolone; cut into small cubes, a few at a time and Parmesan, a little at a time.
Season with spices.
If sauce is too thick, thin with milk. When sauce has a smooth, creamy consistency, serve over hot noodles.

i: 1 small green bell pepper, cut into chunks
3/4 c. sweet and sour French dressing
d: In medium skillet, heat oil and cook carrots, snow peas and green bell pepper over medium heat, stirring frequently 5 minutes or until crisp-tender.
Add water chestnuts, tomato, cucumber and sweet 'n sour spicy French dressing blended with brown sugar and soy sauce.
Simmer, covered, 5 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
Top, if desired, with sesame seeds.
Yields 6 servings.

i: 2 lb. new potatoes, each 1 1/2-inch diameter, scrubbed but not peeled
1 fresh hot chili pepper, cut crosswise paper thin
d: Boil potatoes in water for 10 minutes.
Drain and peel.
Pour 2 cups vegetable oil into deep skillet with oil for deep frying. Heat until 350° and fry potatoes for 2 minutes in 3 batches.
Brown all sides.
Put on paper towel and stub with toothpick.
Heat oil and in other skillet, add ginger and chili and fry.
Cook the cabbage in 1 cup water and 1 teaspoon salt for 7 minutes.
Remove from heat, drain well and place in a 1-quart casserole.
Fry the chopped onion until crisp, remove from pan and set aside.
Blend the flour and brown sugar into the bacon drippings in the pan.
Stir in the beer and vinegar.
Add pepper, 1/4 teaspoon salt and the onion; cook lightly until onion is limp.
Heat oven to 350°.
Brown meat and drain.
Add green pepper and onion.
Cook until tender.
Stir in tomatoes, tomato sauce, water and seasonings.
In a 2-quart casserole dish, layer half the noodles, half the meat sauce and half the Velveeta cheese.
Brown and drain sausage and hamburger.
Mix meats with remaining ingredients.
Pour in a 9 x 13-inch baking pan.
Bake in a 350° oven for 1 hour.
Peel and cut each apple into 8 pieces and place in shallow dish. Cook in microwave for 1 minute. Wrap each piece of apple in a crescent roll. Place in greased 9 x 13 pan.
Combine all ingredients in a glass jar.
Cover; shake well.
Mix ingredients with hands. Roll into balls.
Place on cookie sheet. Bake at 450° for about 10 minutes.
May be frozen and cooked later.

i: 1 c. brown sugar (1/2 light and 1/2 dark)
d: Mix sugar, salt, milk and Karo syrup in heavy, 3-quart pan and cook to soft ball stage.
Add butter and vanilla.
Beat until cool.
Add pecans and drop by tablespoon onto wax paper.
When firm, store in closed container.
Makes approximately 2 dozen pralines.

i: 1 1/2 lb. pork steak, cut in strips
d: Brown meat; remove fat.
Drain on paper towels.
Put all ingredients in slow cooker.
Cook 8 hours on low or 6 hours on high.
Serve over fluffy rice or chow mein noodles.
Place all ingredients in bowl, mix well and shape in desired shape.
Bake at 350° for 15 to 20 minutes.
Marinate chicken breasts in eggs and garlic salt for 3 hours. Coat chicken in bread crumbs and brown on top of stove.
Lay flat in a 15 x 10-inch pan.
Mix bouillon cubes in 1 cup of water and pour over chicken.
Spread shredded cheeses over chicken.
Bake at 350° for 30 to 40 minutes.
Fry bacon until crisp in a wide 6-quart saucepot.
Remove bacon and all but 2 tablespoons of fat.
Save both bacon and extra fat.
Add ground beef, breaking it up with a spoon and cook until brown, stirring occasionally.
Stir in onion, green pepper and garlic and cook for 10 minutes.
Add more bacon fat if needed. Mash plum tomatoes with a spoon.

i: 4 apples, peeled and sliced (a tart, cooking apple)
d: Bring water and sugar to a boil. Add fruit and boil for 5 minutes or until berries pop. Add nuts. Serve for breakfast, hot or serve cold. Serves 8 to 10.
Brown beef in oil.
Add onion and pepper.
Saute until onion is transparent.
Add remaining ingredients.
Cover pan and bring to a quick boil.
Lower heat and simmer slowly for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
In greased baking dish, place croutons and Velveeta.
Mix eggs, milk, mustard, salt, onion powder and pepper.
Pour on top of cheese.
Place crumbled bacon on top.
Bake at 325° for 50 minutes.
Cook onion and garlic in oil until tender.
Add remaining ingredients.
Simmer, uncovered, for 30 minutes.
Makes 4 cups.
Dip chicken in Italian dressing, then into bread crumbs. Place in baking dish.
Cover and bake at 450° for 15 minutes. Uncover; bake 10 minutes more.
Serves 4.
(I prefer skinless chicken.)
Mix first 4 ingredients; let stand overnight.
Add lemon jello, dissolved in hot water.
Add cold water, nuts, celery and plain gelatin dissolved in 1/4 cup cold water and 1/2 cup hot water.

i: 1 (9 oz.) pkg. frozen mixed vegetables, thawed
d: Brown meat and onion in large skillet; drain.
Stir in water, pepper, tomato sauce and paste.
Simmer 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat; stir in vegetables and 1 1/2 cups cheese.
Spoon mixture into greased 12 x 8 (2-quart) baking dish. Separate refrigerated biscuits into 10 biscuits.
Separate each biscuit into 2 layers.
Pour boiling water over shortening, sugar and salt.
Blend and cool.
Add eggs.
Dissolve yeast in warm water and add to mixture. Add flour and blend well.
Cover and place in refrigerator at least 4 hours.
About 3 hours before using, roll out and cut.

i: 1 small jar black olives, pitted and sliced
d: Mix lettuce, tomatoes, onion, red cabbage, olives and cheese in large non-metallic container.
Mix well; chill 1 day ahead.
Use on all fresh vegetables.
Slice potatoes and onion.
Put on bottom of pan.
Drain green beans and put on top.
Layer hamburger over beans.
Pour celery over hamburger.
Place biscuits on top.
First Mixture:
One cup of salt to 1 gallon water.
Bring salt water mixture to a boil.
Remove from heat and pour hot liquid over cucumber slices until they are covered.
Let stand 24 hours.
Pour old water mixture out.
Cover chicken with
water.
Salt and pepper.
Boil over medium heat for 30 minutes.
Remove from broth.
Blend all ingredients.
Serve over cream cheese. Serve with crackers.
Mix together.
Add oil and honey, then enough water to make stiff.
Knead thoroughly.
Roll very thin.
Prick with a fork and mark off in squares.
Bake in slow or moderate oven at 300° until golden brown, approximately 20 minutes.
Mix together the ketchup, lime juice, scallions, celery, Worcestershire sauce and Tabasco sauce.
Fold in the bananas. Chill for 30 minutes.
Serve in lettuce cups or sherbet cups. Serves 6 to 8.
Brown meat and onion.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
Add rice and beans; heat to boiling and put in casserole.
Top with cheese and serve with Doritos and a salad.
Great for camping.
Dissolve gelatin and salt in hot water; add cold water, vinegar, onion and pepper.
Chill until slightly thickened. Combine vegetables and fold into gelatin mixture.
Chill until firm.
To fill a 9 x 13 dish, double this recipe.
Combine soy sauce, honey, oil, garlic and Five Spice powder. Pour over chicken.
Marinate 4 hours.
Remove wings and place on a broiler rack.
Bake at 375° for 45 minutes, turning and basting every 15 minutes.
Tear up cabbage and place in greased casserole.
Mix next 5 ingredients and pour over cabbage.
Cover with bacon slices (bacon can be omitted).
Bake covered at 350° for 50 minutes.
Uncover and bake for 20 minutes.

i: 1 1/2 lb. skinned, boneless chicken breasts, cut into strips
1 small red pepper, cut into thin strips
3 c. cooked rice, cooked in chicken broth
d: Season chicken with salt and pepper.
Cook chicken, asparagus and red pepper in olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat for 2 to 3 minutes.
Add wine and tarragon.
Simmer 1 1/2 minutes. Heat thoroughly.
Serve over rice.
Yields 4 servings.
Cut 1-inch thick slices from top of pineapple, leaving green leaves on top.
Reserve top.
With curved knife, cut out fruit from shell, leaving 1/2-inch wall.
Cut fruit into cubes, removing any fibrous core.
Place olive in curve of each shrimp; skewer with plastic pick.
Place pineapple cube on pick.
Layer ingredients in order given in a shallow serving dish. Vary amounts and size of dish to suit your needs.
Sliced black olives can be added.

t: Indian Pickle(Splendid Way To Salvage Green Tomatoes)
d: Chop coarsely
all the vegetables.
Sprinkle with salt and let stand 12 hours or overnight.

i: 1 1/3 c. (2.8 oz. can) French fried onions or fried onion rings
d: Mix beef, 2/3 cup French fried onions and seasonings; shape into 6 burgers.
Grill or broil 10 minutes or until no longer pink in center, turning twice.
Serve on rolls and top with 2/3 cup onions.
Makes 6 servings.
Debone cooked chicken.
Cook noodles.
Combine all ingredients. Serve while hot.
Stir rice and margarine into boiling water in a saucepan.
Add cinnamon stick, turmeric, salt and bay leaf.
Bring to a boil. Reduce to a simmer.
Simmer, covered, 20 minutes or until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed.
Discard cinnamon stick and bay leaf.
Stir in raisins.

i: 1 (5 oz.) can or pkg. chow mein noodles
d: In a large bowl, combine cooked chicken, drained pineapple, celery, oranges, olives, green pepper and onion.
Blend salad dressing and mustard; toss gently with chicken mixture.
Cover and chill for several hours.
Just before serving, mix in chow mein noodles.
Put salad on lettuce lined serving bowl.
Layer first four items.
Decorate with green onions, chopped tomato and black olives.
Finish with teaspoon of sour cream and avocado.
Serve chilled with tortilla chips.
Cut shortening into flour and salt until crumbly.
Gently stir in cold water until pastry holds together.
Divide into 2 parts. Roll out on floured surface.
Cut beef in 8 pieces.
Combine flour, cracker crumbs, garlic, onion, salt and pepper.
Mix egg and Worcestershire sauce together.
Dip steak into egg and Worcestershire sauce mixture, then in crumbs mixture.
Fry in skillet in oil, then place in 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish and put on onion slices.
Combine tomato sauce, chili sauce, brown sugar, chili powder, dry mustard and vinegar.
In skillet, brown meat; pour off fat.
Add chilies.
In small bowl, combine sour cream and taco seasoning.
In 8-inch spring-form pan or on large plate, spread beans.
Top with meat mixture, sour cream mixture and avocado dip.
Cover; chill.

i: 1 c. kidney beans, cooked and drained
1 sm. can mild green chilies, chopped (opt.)
d: Combine all ingredients in large pot; simmer 30-45 minutes or until onion and bell peppers become soft.
This recipe triples and freezes well.
Serve with cornbread.
Cut beef into 1-inch squares.
Add 1 1/2 teaspoons salt.
Chop the onion fine and brown in oil.
Add beef and paprika.
Let beef simmer in its own juice for 1 hour.
Add sliced carrots and chopped parsley.
Mix salt, sugar and mayonnaise and pour over the vegetables. Sprinkle with cheese and crumbled bacon.
Refrigerate overnight.
Mix and put in punch bowl.
Top with sherbert.
Cream together shortening or margarine, sugar and eggs until smooth.
Sift together flour, baking powder and salt and stir into the shortening/sugar/egg mixture.
Add vanilla.
Roll into balls. Then roll in brown sugar and cinnamon.
Crisscross with fork. Bake at 400° for 8 to 10 minutes or until lightly browned.
Beat the first 4 ingredients together.
In another bowl, mix the remaining ingredients.
Combine the two mixtures and bake for about an hour at 350°.
(This is a very old recipe.)
Toast cracked wheat and coconut by stirring constantly in a dry pan over medium heat for several minutes, until lightly browned.
Place in a saucepan andd remaining ingredients.
Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 45 minutes.
Sprinkle with chopped nut, if desired.

i: 1 to 2 tomatoes, peeled, seeded and chopped
d: Combine all ingredients and let set overnight.
Serve with king size chips.
Preheat oven to 400°.
Mix grated cheese, grated onion and mayonnaise.
Put in a small ovenproof dish (like a soup crock). Bake for about 20 minutes in a 400° oven.
Serve with crackers while bubbly and hot.

i: 2 large cooking apples, peeled, cored and grated
d: Combine flour, soda, salt, cinnamon and sugar in large bowl. Add grated carrots, apples, coconut, raisins and pecans.
Stir well.
Combine oil, eggs and vanilla.
Make a well in center of dry mixture and combine the oil, egg and vanilla mixture.
Spoon into greased muffin pans, 3/4 full.
Bake at 375° for 18 to 10 minutes or until golden brown.

i: 1 1/4 c. chicken, finely chopped and cooked
d: Heat oven to 375°.
In a medium bowl combine chicken, parsley, 2 tablespoons crushed croutons, curry powder, mayonnaise and onion.
Separate dough into 4 rectangles; firmly press perforations to seal.
Press or roll each to form 10 x 5-inch rectangle.
Spread 1/4 of the chicken mixture on each rectangle within 1/2 inch of edge.
Starting at longest side, roll up; pinch seams to seal.

i: 2 (7 1/2 oz.) jars large or jumbo, pitted, ripe olives (undrained)
1 (4 oz.) jar pimientos, drained and chopped
d: Drain olives; save liquid.
In small saucepan, combine 1 cup olive liquid, chili powder, garlic, bay leaves, olive oil, pepper and onion.
Heat to boiling.
Reduce heat; simmer 5 minutes, uncovered.
Stir in pimientos.
Place olives in a large jar; pour seasoning mixture over.
Mix meat, rice, water, onion, salt, garlic salt and celery salt in bowl.
Shape into balls.
Place into a 9 x 9-inch or larger baking dish.
Mix tomato sauce, 1 cup water and Worcestershire sauce.
Pour over meatballs.
Cover with foil. Bake at 350° for 45 minutes.
Drain fruit for one hour.
Arrange in baking dish.
Melt together butter with brown sugar and curry.
Spoon over fruit and bake at 325° for about 20 minutes.
Serves about 6.

t: Pasta, Sugar Peas, Asparagus And Parmesan
i: 1/2 lb. sugar or snow peas, trimmed
d: Add asparagus to boiling salted water.
Cook until tender. Transfer to bowl of cold water and cool this.
Drain.
Add pasta to the same water and boil until tender, but still firm.
Add peas and boil 2 minutes.
Add asparagus and heat.

i: 4 oz. crumbled cheese (Blue cheese is good)
3 large apples, cored and cut into cubes
2 ribs celery, cut in medium size pieces
d: In a medium size bowl, whisk together vinegar, lemon juice, honey, salt and pepper. Gradually drizzle in olive oil, continuing to whisk until thick and well blended.
Add 1 cup
water
to
jello.
Add 7 ice cubes and stir. Put in refrigerator
until

i: all the clam juice you can get
d: Heat oil in pan; chop garlic and cook in olive oil until golden brown.
Add clam juice and pepper; let simmer very slowly. Add clams.
A few minutes before serving, add parsley.
Pour over linguine and serve.
Wash the cranberries and discard the few that are too soft or too hard.

In a large saucepan, bring to a boil water and sugar. Boil 5 minutes. Put the cranberries in the boiling water. Bring to a rapid boil so they will pop. When they begin to pop, reduce heat to medium and cook 5 minutes, stirring a few times. Remove from heat. When cool, refrigerate. Can be frozen.

i: 1 (1 lb.) can cut green beans, drained
d: Preheat oven to 350°. Place alternate layers of sauerkraut and beans in a buttered 8-quart casserole, ending with sauerkraut. Combine water and salt. Mix until dissolved. Add to casserole. Slice sausage roll. Place slices on top of sauerkraut. Bake 40 minutes. Remove from oven. Increase temperature to 400°. Arrange biscuits on top. Bake 10 to 15 minutes until browned.
In a large bowl combine cottage and cream cheese.
Beat until smooth.
Add tuna, one can at a time, blending until smooth after each addition.
Add remaining ingredients.
Chill and serve with crackers.
Yields 4 cups.
Stuff eggs into tomatoes.
Stuff tomatoes into chickens. Stuff chickens into lambs.
Stuff lambs into camel.
Roast until tender.
Serves 150 people.

i: 3 c. sweet potatoes, cooked and mashed
d: Mix all ingredients and spread in a 13 x 9 inch casserole dish.
Top with topping and cook in 350 degree oven for 30 to 35 minutes.

i: 1 large can El Paso refried beans
d: Mix beans, seasoning mix and salsa together.
Put in a large pan.
This is the bottom layer. Top with sour cream, then avocado dip, then layer cheese and tomato on top.
Let set in refrigerator for about 1 hour before serving.
Serve with chips.
Combine first 9 ingredients.
Put in shallow 2 quart casserole and sprinkle with cheese.
Bake at 350° for 25 minutes.
Makes 6 servings.
Dice and fry bacon crisp.
Combine all ingredients except bacon.
Heat in 400° oven until cheese melts.
Remove and stir in bacon.
Spread on crackers or bread.

Put into a heavy 2-quart saucepan the sugar, Pet milk and corn syrup. Stir over medium heat until mixture boils and is bubbly all over top. Boil and stir over medium heat 2 minutes more. Remove from heat; stir in the chocolate morsels until completely melted. Add pecans. Drop on wax paper.
Dissolve jello in boiling water.
Cool slightly, then add milk.
Chill until thick, but not set.
Stir in remaining ingredients.
Refrigerate.
Serves 16.

i: 3 medium chicken breasts, cut in half lengthwise
d: Sprinkle chicken with seasoned salt and paprika.
Place in 11 x 7 x 1 1/2-inch baking pan.
Dissolve chicken bouillon cube in boiling water.
Add wine, instant minced onion, curry powder and pepper.
Pour over chicken.

t: Chicken And Vegetables In One Pan
i: 2 c. whole wheat flakes cereal, slightly crushed
d: Grate 1 tablespoon
of onion, then chop remaining onion in pie plate.
Mix
grated onion, yogurt, curry powder and 1/2 teaspoon of salt on	wax
paper.
Mix cereal and chopped parsley.
Prepare tomatoes, onions, peppers, celery and cabbage.
Mix together with salt.
Let mixture stand for 2 hours, then drain. Mix the remaining ingredients with the vegetables.
Cook for 30 minutes.
Put in jars and seal.
Blend first 6 ingredients 5 seconds.
While blending, pour in oil very slowly.
Place one tortilla on a microwave-safe plate.
Spread spaghetti sauce evenly over tortilla.
Add pepperoni.
Cover with cheese.
Put the pizza in the microwave.
Cook until cheese is melted, approximately 1 minute on medium heat.
In a blender, combine all ingredients except chicken.
Puree until smooth.
Place chicken in shallow dish.
Pour marinade over and turn to coat.
Cover and let stand at least 15 minutes. Preheat grill or large cast-iron skillet over medium heat. Spray cooking surface. Cook 4 minutes per side.

i: 1 small (8 oz.) can crushed pineapple, drained
d: Mix jello according to directions and let chill until it begins to thicken.
When thickened, take out of refrigerator.
Add the cabbage, carrots and pineapple.
Return to refrigerator to jell until served.
Combine and beat 2 minutes the flour, baking powder, salt, butter, milk, egg and pudding.
Pour into 9-inch greased pie plate.
Drain peaches; place on batter.
Beat eggs in a small bowl.
Gradually add sugar; mix well. Add melted margarine and bourbon.
Blend in cornstarch.
Stir in pecans and chocolate chips.
Pour into unbaked pie crust.
Bake in a 350° oven for 45 to 50 minutes.

i: 1 large bag tortilla chips (your choice of brands)
d: On large microwavable platter, start layering as follows: tortilla chips, Cheddar cheese, refried beans, hamburger, sour cream, tomatoes, green onions and black olives.
After completing first layer of everything, begin with second layer in same order. When ingredients are gone, top with guacamole and jalapeno peppers.
Entire platter can be heated in microwave.
Serve immediately.
Great party snack (but a little messy)!
Mix first 3 ingredients in pan and heat until sugar is dissolved.
Cool, then add mayonnaise, turmeric, celery salt, salt and pepper.
Cook macaroni until tender.
Drain and pour cold water over it again and drain.
Mix with dressing (it will be soupy). Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Next day, add celery, onion, pepper and eggs.
Cut tomatoes into 1/4-inch slices.
Combine cornmeal, flour, salt and pepper.
Dip tomato slices into cornmeal mixture.
Heat bacon drippings until melted.
Add tomato slices; cook, turning once, until golden brown.
Makes 4 servings.

t: Hotel Juana'S Roasted Monkfish With Thyme Flowers, Spinach And Red(Ingredients For 4 Persons)
d: Remove the filets from the monkfish and brush with olive oil and fresh thyme flowers.
Peel the 3 half pepper and cut in large julienne.
Cook in boiling salted water; refresh in ice water and drain.
Reheat just before serving in a saucepan with olive oil. Make the red pepper coulis.
Season the monkfish filets with salt and pepper.
In a Teflon frying pan, add olive oil, 2 cloves garlic and lightly brown the filets, finishing the cooking in the oven. When done, set aside covered with aluminum foil.

i: 2/3 c. milk (more if batter is too thick)
d: Sift first 5 ingredients together in a bowl.
Mix egg and milk together; add to dry ingredients.
Beat until smooth.
Hold finger over bottom of a funnel; pour in some batter.
Remove finger to let batter drop.
Drop batter in a spiral motion into pan filled with 1 inch of hot oil (375°).
Preheat oven to 400°.
Grease and flour a 13 x 9-inch baking pan.
Combine sugar and flour; set aside.
In a medium saucepan, melt butter or margarine with cocoa and water; bring to a boil. Pour mixture over sugar and flour.
Mix well.
Add buttermilk, eggs, cinnamon, baking soda and vanilla; mix until smooth.
Mix together in large punch bowl just before serving. Beautiful pink color.
Beat eggs slightly.
Add sugar, salt and lemon juice; cook until thick in double boiler, stirring constantly.
Cool.
Whip the cream and fold into cooled egg mixture.
Combine fruits, pecans and celery; fold into dressing.
Serves 10.
Saute ground beef, pepper, onion, parsley and garlic.
In large deep pot, place tomatoes, water and bouillon cubes.
Add the sauteed mixture and simmer for 20 minutes.
Cook tiny bow noodles; drain.
Add to mixture.
Just before serving, add fresh sauteed mushrooms.
Mix well.
Put in 9-inch pastry.
Cook for 1 hour at 325°.
Cook sugar and milk until it boils.
Add dates and salt. Cook to soft ball stage.
Add oleo, nuts and vanilla.
Beat until firm and holds shape on spoon.
Pour candy in row on wet cup towel.
Roll.

i: 1 (8 oz.) can refrigerated quick crescent rolls
d: Heat oven to 375°.
In small bowl combine cheese, pepperoni, oregano, pizza sauce and pepper; mix well.
Separate dough onto 4 rectangles.
Firmly press perforations to seal.
Spread each rectangle with about 3 tablespoons mixture.
Starting at shortest side, roll up each rectangle; pinch edges to seal.
Brown beef in 3-quart saucepan.
Drain excess fat.
Add sauce and water.
Simmer 10 minutes.
Combine rest of ingredients, except lasagne for filling.
Pour about 1 cup of sauce onto bottom of a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan.
Add cut-up margarine to flour.
Make well in flour; add egg yolks and sugar that's been mixed together.
Mix with fingers; sprinkle with 2 tablespoons cold water.
Continue to work with fingers.
Make into a ball, wrap in wax paper and refrigerate until chilled.
Preheat oven to 325°.
